PN CN213312652-U
TI High-frequency spraying ventilation tracheal catheter for use in narrow position of trachea, has negative pressure pipeline, high frequency injection ventilation pipeline and oxygen supply pipeline that are connected with deep part.
AU PENG J
   SHAN L
   LV J
AE PENG J (PENG-Individual)
GA 202163632N
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a high-frequency spraying ventilation tracheal catheter through the narrow part of the trachea, relating to the technical field of medical appliance, comprising a high-frequency spraying ventilation tracheal catheter through the narrow part of the trachea, comprising a deep part, a tube body and an interface part connected in turn; the pipe body is provided with a negative pressure pipeline with the same outer diameter; a high frequency spraying ventilation pipeline and an oxygen supply pipeline; one end of the negative pressure pipeline passes through the interface part and is connected with the negative pressure suction port; one end of the high frequency injection ventilation pipeline passes through the interface part and is connected with the vent port; one end of the oxygen supply pipeline passes through the interface part and is connected with the oxygen supply port; it effectively improves the oxygen supply efficiency of the high-frequency injection ventilation; at the same time, it reduces the carbon dioxide concentration in the air channel, which not only ensures the oxygen supply, but also solves the carbon dioxide retention problem; the ventilation safety of the patient is greatly improved; it can establish stable air channel for the medical staff, such as tracheotomy, placing air pipe bracket and so on, striving for more abundant operation time.

PT P
PN TW202120194-A; TW778308-B1
TI Nozzle device for connecting outer tube to            conveying compressed gas and inner tube for conveying            carbon dioxide gas, has outer cover connected to body            and surrounding guide section and part of cone section            of nozzle to define communicating with orifice.
AU XU W
   TSAO K J
   CHEN J
   LIU Q
AE HABOR PRECISION INC (HABO-Non-standard)
GA 2021A3314E
AB    NOVELTY - A nozzle device includes a body connected to                an outer tube, a nozzle connected to the body, and                an outer cover connected to the body and                surrounding the nozzle and defining an annular                chamber. The body includes a surrounding wall                defining a passage. This channel is suitable for                inserting an inner tube. The surrounding wall has a                hole communicating with the channel and the                outside. The nozzle includes a large-diameter hole                and a small-diameter hole communicating with the                channel, a tapered section connecting the end                surface of the adjacent small-diameter hole, and a                guiding section connecting the end surface of the                adjacent large-diameter hole. The large diameter                hole is sleeved with the inner tube. The small                diameter hole is used to generate particles due to                the phase change of the passing carbon dioxide gas.                When the compressed gas enters the annulus through                the orifice, a spiral airflow is generated in the                annulus through the orifice, and flows along the                cone section, and entrains the external airflow and                particles to be ejected forward. In this way, the                annular chamber restricts the air flow to flow only                along the tapered surface section, which can not                only increase the flow rate, but also improve the                collimation during spraying.
DC P42 (Spraying, atomising (B05).)
MC P42-A01; P42-T01A; P42-T05C
IP B05B-001/00; B05B-009/04
PD TW202120194-A   01 Jun 2021   B05B-001/00   202173   Pages: 19   Chinese

PN CN112882062-A
TI Space-based carbon dioxide (CO2) flux laser            detection device has solution module that is configured            to calculate CO2 flux data according to wind field            profile information and CO2 concentration profile            information.
AU YAO W
   WANG L
   YANG H
   MAO Y
   ZHANG Z
   GAO Y
   YU Z
   MA R
   ZHANG S
   HU L
AE CHINA ACAD SPACE TECHNOLOGY (CAER-C)
GA 202163972W
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a first single-frequency laser                (102) whose output wavelength is obtained by                performing a doppler shift compensation. An output                wavelength of a second single-frequency laser (103)                is far away from a CO2 absorption peak wavelength.                An optical switch (105) controls an output laser of                the first and second single-frequency lasers. A                transceiving module includes an optical transceiver                (201) and a servo system (202). The transceiver                emits the output laser to a measurement area and                receives an echo signal of the area. The servo                system controls an exit angle of the optical                transceiver. A solution module (301) performs a                signal processing and solution according to the                echo signal to obtain wind field profile                information and CO2 concentration profile                information of the measurement area. A CO2 flux                data is calculated according to the wind field                profile information and the CO2 concentration                profile information.
   USE - Space-based CO2 flux laser detection                device.
   ADVANTAGE - The sky-based CO2 flux laser detection device                is arranged on the sky-based platform to high                precision the carbon dioxide collection amount of                the ground surface with high reliability.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a                space-based CO2 flux laser detection device.                (Drawing includes non-English language text)

   Second single-frequency laser (103)

   Servo system (202)

   LIU D
   GU X
   ZOU M
   WANG S
   WANG Y
   GE X
   LIU J
AE UNIV SHENYANG TECHNOLOGY (UYTE-C)
GA 202156667P
AB    NOVELTY - A pre-mixed flow state solidified soil doped with high-silicon iron tailings comprises 1-2 pts. wt. lime, 2-5 pts. wt. iron tailings powder, 12-15 pts. wt. graded soil, 2-3 pts. wt. water, and 0.02-0.05 pt. wt. auxiliary grinding agent.
   USE - The pre-mixed flow state solidified soil is used for road foundation, building foundation reinforcing treatment field, and reducing carbon dioxide emission.
   ADVANTAGE - The pre-mixed flow state solidified soil doped with high-silicon iron tailings effectively reduces the use of high energy consumption resource such as cement lime, reduces energy consumption and carbon dioxide emission, applies a lot of industrial solid waste and reduces the storage of solid waste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the solidified soil comprising taking iron tailings, grinding for 1-2 hours, adding grinding aid, and continuously grinding for 1-2 hours; mixing lime and high silicon iron tailings, calculating quality of water by water gel ratio, mixing and stirring, slowly adding water, and stirring to obtain curing agent; calculating quality of the graded soil; and mixing curing agent and graded soil and stirring.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the pre-mixed flow state solidified soil (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The iron tailings powder is high-silicon iron tailings comprising 60-75 wt.% silica, 6-0 wt.% magnesium oxide, 5-8 wt.% calcium oxide, 4-7 wt.% alumina, and8-12 wt.% iron oxide. The graded soil is gravel soil, coal gangue and/or various granular slag. The auxiliary grinding agent is sodium polyphosphate, lignin sulfonate, graphite powder, anhydrous sodium carbonate, calcium sulfate dihydrate, and/or ammonium chloride.

AU XU Y
   XU A
AE XU Y (XUYY-Individual)
GA 202152983T
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a box type filling machine, comprising a containing tank for placing the container, a first pipeline for conveying the liquid, a second pipeline for conveying the carbon dioxide gas and an air escape hole for discharging the gas in the containing tank; the first pipeline and the second pipeline are connected with the inner part of the containing tank; the air outlet hole is connected with the inner part of the containing tank. in the filling process, the container is placed in the containing tank; the carbon dioxide gas enters the containing tank through the second pipeline; the air in the sealing range of the containing tank is discharged from the air discharging hole; even the air in the container is discharged; at the same time, the air pressure in the containing tank rises, until the air pressure of the containing tank is equal to the air pressure in the bottle liquid the second liquid continuously flows into the container in the containing tank; at the same time, the pressure releasing hole continuously discharges the gas, so that the air pressure of the containing tank and the air pressure in the bottle liquid dynamically balanced, so that the air in the container is completely discharged; the filling device has simple structure and low manufacturing cost.

The calcium hydroxide is provided (506) to a marine vessel ballasting system by the carbon negative energy storage system. The input is comprised with calcium metal, magnesium oxide, and magnesium metal.

AU SUO H
AE QINHUANGDAO GAHA MEDICAL ELECTRONIC (QINH-Non-standard)
GA 202145614P
AB    NOVELTY - The cloth has a skull top air inlet pipe (5) hermetically connected with an external air supply device such that air flow enters a mask air chamber (100) from a cranial top air inlet pipe. A cranial top exhaust passage (6) enters a body air chamber (200). An air inlet pipe head of a crotch exhaust pipe (11) is located at a front section of crotch of the cloth body. An external air suction device is hermetically connected with the body air chamber and discharges moist and heat gas, and enhances moist and heat in the cloth body.

GA 2020622864
AB    NOVELTY - The natural exothermal emitting property evaluation method involves obtaining a moisture air flow from a mixed gas that is obtained by mixing oxygen gas, and nitrogen gas. A heating of the coal is performed with, which a reactor (17) is filled to a predetermined temperature under the moisture inactive gas airflow. A generation rate of the carbon dioxide is quantified by measuring the time of the moisture oxidation. A balance value of the generation rate of the carbon dioxide in each temperature is computed. The moisture inactive gas airflow is acquired from a no less than one sort of inactive gas. The moisture oxidation of the coal is carried out under the switched moisture air flow.
   USE - Natural exothermal emitting property evaluation method for coal, such as a bituminous coal, a sub-bituminous coal, and a wood coal.
   ADVANTAGE - Natural exothermal emitting property evaluation method accurately evaluate the spontaneous exothermic property of coal in an environment close to an actual coal storage pile. The moisture oxidation of the coal is suppressed that is carried out before an evaluation start.

TI Method for detecting vehicle exhaust synchronous diffusion model based on large light spot, involves obtaining linear fitting degrees of carbon monoxide and Carbon dioxide in standard gas under different diameter detection beams.
AU YU X
   HUANG Z
   WANG K
AE ZHEJIANG DUOPULE ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTIO (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020153495
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves using a laser beam coupling technology to combine beams of a corresponding component target absorption wavelengths emitted from a transmitting end to obtain a narrow-diameter coupled beam. A large-beam diffusely reflected beam is obtained. Detection of an exhaust target is realized. An emission end is adjusted to an exhaust emission height. A narrow-diameter coupled beam that passes through the exhaust target is obtained when the vehicle passes. Multiple sets of exhaust information samples are measured during exhaust diffusion process. Linear fitting degrees of Carbon monoxide, Nitric oxide and Carbon dioxide in standard gas under different diameter detection beams are obtained by referring to exhaust gas fitting operation.
   USE - Method for detecting a vehicle exhaust synchronous diffusion model based on a large light spot.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables avoiding the problem that specular reflection is very sensitive to changes in the optical path, which is extremely likely to cause beam shifts and bring great inconvenience to the stable operation of the equipment and the problem of maintenance of the mirror surface, so that the diffuse reflection of the beam with a large light spot can enhance the anti-interference.

PT P
PN KR2019100715-A
TI Cultivating ginseng sprout involves cultivating ginseng sprout in hydroponic culture, solar, LED and carbon dioxide atmosphere in hydroponic cultivation system.
AU JUNG J B
AE JUNG J B (JUNG-Individual)
GA 201975350V
AB    NOVELTY - Cultivating ginseng sprout involves cultivating ginseng sprout in hydroponic culture, solar, LED and carbon dioxide atmosphere in the hydroponic cultivation system.
   USE - Method for cultivating ginseng sprout.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to cultivate ginseng sprout, which has high saponin content and can contribute significantly to farm income.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for producing a ginseng composition, which involves washing the leaves, stems and roots of the hydroponic cultivated shoot ginseng, first drying at 55-65 degrees C for 12-18 hours, crushing, and classifying the crushed ginseng into 50-120 mesh and roasting sprouts ginseng powder secondary dried at 55-65 degrees C for 6-12 hours, then at 110-115 degrees C for 1-3 minutes to make sprouts ginseng powder, hydroponic cultivation sprouts by mixing excipients, binders and sugars with the sprouts ginseng powder; and
   (2) an artificial sweetener, which is a bud ginseng powder composition including 0.1-0.3 pts. wt. any one or two selected from sucralose, aspartame and acesulfame K, 5-10 pts. wt. any one or two selected from maltodextrin, potato starch and corn starch, and the binder is 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. any one or two of HPMC and CMC, Sugar is 10-20 pts. wt. any one or two selected from sugar, powdered syrup, powdered sugar and honey powder, sugar alcohol is 3-5 pts. wt. any one or two selected from xylitol, sorbitol and erythritol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Conditions: The temperature of the hydroponic cultivation system is photosynthesized by shading light at 20-25 degrees C for 10-14 hours during the day and 6,000-10,000 lux of light emitting diodes at 1,000-1,500 ppm atmosphere at night. The carbon dioxide gas is supplied in an open or closed form to hydroponicly grow sprout ginseng. Preferred Conditions: The method for cultivating ginseng sprout further involves mixing the binder, sugar and purified water, mixed with granules, granulated with a granulator, and dried with a hot air dryer to remove unpleasant odorless sprout tea ginseng granular composition of health functional powder tea, pills, capsules, and tablets.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)
MC A03-A04A; A12-W04B; P13-A01C; P13-A07; P13-A99; P13-E02; P13-E03
IP A01G-031/00; A01G-007/02; A01G-007/04

PT P
PN EP3527538-A1; EP3527538-B1; ES2884087-T3
TI Bioelectrochemical system for simultaneous production of water disinfection agents and carbon-neutral compounds, comprises anode chamber, cathode chamber where cathode electrode comprises biocompatible conductive material, and membrane.
AU BATLLE VILANOVA P
   MONSALVO GARCIA V M
   COLPRIM GALCERAN J
   BALAGUER CONDOM M D
   PUIG BROCH S
   ICARAN LOPEZ P
   ROGALLA F
AE FCC AQUALIA SA (FCCA-Non-standard)
   FCC AQUALIA SA (FCCA-Non-standard)
GA 201971766J
AB    NOVELTY - Bioelectrochemical system for simultaneous production of water disinfection agents and carbon-neutral compounds, comprises: (a) an anode chamber (1) where a chloride-containing solution (2) is oxidized to chlorine compounds (3) in presence of an electrochemical catalyst (4); (b) a cathode chamber (5) where the cathode electrode (6) comprises biocompatible conductive material which allows the bioelectrochemical conversion of carbon dioxide (CO2) dissolved in an aqueous stream (7) to carbon-neutral compounds (8) and clarified water (11) with the action of bioelectroactive microorganisms added and/or developed in the cathode chamber; (c) a power source (9) that provides the electric energy to carry out the process; (d) at least one membrane (10) separating the anode chamber and the cathode chamber, and a central chamber can be placed in between.
   USE - The bioelectrochemical system is useful for simultaneous production of water disinfection agents and carbon-neutral compounds.
   ADVANTAGE - The system provides a combination of electrochemical and bioelectrochemical reactions that results in an economically attractive process that overcomes current cost limitations.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of electrochemical catalyst in the anode, and biocompatible conductive material and carbon dioxide-reducing microorganisms in the cathode.
   Anode chamber (1)
   Chloride-containing solution (2)
   Chlorine compounds (3)
   Electrochemical catalyst (4)
   Cathode chamber (5)
   Cathode electrode (6)
   Aqueous stream (7)
   Carbon-neutral compounds (8)
   Power source (9)
   Mmebrane (10)
   Clarified water (11)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The electrochemical catalyst in the anode and/or biocompatible conductive material in the cathode (6) are carbon-based electrodes, such as carbon foam, carbon paper, carbon cloth, carbon felt, carbon wool, carbon nanotubes, carbon fibers, activated carbon, vitreous carbon or graphite (in any form); graphene, conductive polymers, dimensionally stable electrodes (DSA), electric conductive diamond (ECD), such as boron-doped diamond (BDD); cobalt, copper, gold, iridium, iron, nickel, palladium, platinum, ruthenium, silver, stainless steel, tin, titanium, tungsten, and its combinations. Preferred Microorganism: The bioelectroactive microorganisms in the cathode chamber are suspended in the solution and/or in the form of attached biofilm on the cathode electrode. The bioelectroatractive microorganisms are selected from Acetitomalacum, Acetoanaerobium, Acetobacterium, Azospira, Blautia, Butyribacterium, Bacteroides, Clostridium, Desulfobacterium, Eubacterium, Geobacter, Geovibrio, Megasphaera, Methanobacterium, Metanobrevibacter, Methanococcus, Morella, Rhodobacter, Sporomusa, Sulfospirillium, Thermoanaerobacter and/or any other CO2-reducing genus. Preferred Composition: The chloride-containing solution can be obtained from different sources and contains different chloride salts, or its combinations, with a chloride concentration of 0.1-150 g/L.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Process: The CO2 dissolved in an aqueous stream is fed into the cathode chamber by direct injection in the cathode chamber or in a previous absorption unit where CO2 is dissolved in an aqueous stream. The carbon-neutral compounds are produced from CO2 either by direct reduction of electrons and/or using hydrogen as intermediate. Preferred Components: The aqueous stream is mineral water, groundwater, seawater, brackish water, reject water, wastewater, treated wastewater, or other water streams. The membranes is selected from anion exchange and/or cation exchange membrane and are installed in a 2-chamber or 3-chamber reactor.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC D04-A01; D04-A02; D05-A03A; A12-E14; A12-W11A; A12-W11E; A12-W11J; A12-W11K; A12-W11L

PN CN209131514-U
TI Carbon dioxide blasting device, has spiral sleeve whose top part is fixedly connected with guide rod, connecting rod whose end is fixedly connected with supporting plate, and screw rod whose end is fixedly connected with drill bit.
AU WANG D
   YANG Z
   ZHANG D
AE CCCC FIRST HIGHWAY ENG CO LTD (CCCC-C)
GA 2019647751
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a carbon dioxide blasting device and blasting system, comprising a top part of the blasting device main body, said explode device main body is fixedly connected with a connecting spring, top of the connecting spring is fixedly connected with a V-shaped baffle, the left and right sides of the bottom of the baffle is fixedly connected with a push rod, the blasting device main body left and right two sides of the top part are provided with two pushing rod corresponding to the groove, the bottom groove wall of the clamping groove is fixedly connected with a transverse plate. The utility model has reasonable structure, through reduction of the baffle with the push rod can push the inclined panel of the two sides and the connecting rod move towards the opposite direction, the inner wall of the supporting plate and the hole is contacted to move can drive the screw rod in the spiral sleeve to rotate so as to drive the drill bit is screwed in the inner wall of the hole so that the blasting device main body can be firmly fixed to the effect realized in the hole, avoid blasting device main body slides out from the hole, the blasting, and safe.

PT P
PN KR2019001830-U; KR491928-Y1
TI Multipurpose nipper used for carbon dioxide gas welding, has small projections formed along inner peripheral surface of side of each half groove, and large projections formed along other inner surface circumference.
AU BANGYOUNGGYUN
AE BANGYOUNGGYUN (BANG-Individual)
GA 2019644710
AB    NOVELTY - The nipper has a cutting portion with the cutting blade, the coupling portion formed with pin hole, pressing hole, and the handle which are formed from one end in the longitudinal direction to the other end. The first main portion and the second main portion are pin-coupled by a coupling pin passing through each pin hole while crossing in the coupling portion. The pressing holes are provided with a half groove facing each other by engaging the first and second bodies to form a pressing hole. The small projections are formed along the inner peripheral surface of one side of each half groove and the large projections are formed along the other inner surface circumference.
   USE - Multipurpose nipper used for carbon dioxide gas welding.
   ADVANTAGE - The nipper is easy to disassemble and assemble regardless of the size and shape of the tip of the welding gun and the gas diffuser, since the pressure hole of the multi-purpose nipper can firmly hold elements of various sizes and shapes. The nipper is used for disassembly and assembly of other elements in addition to the tip of the welding gun and the gas diffuser. The element in the pressure hole portion is grasped more stably, when the extension is formed to protrude around the pressing hole portion of the small protrusion and the length of the large protrusion is extended.
DC P62 (Hand tools, cutting (B25, B26).)
MC P62-A02; P62-A05; P62-B08
IP B25B-013/02; B25B-013/48; B25B-027/14; B26B-017/00

PT P
PN KR2019076625-A; KR2158977-B1
TI Sterilizer for sterilizing e.g. cutting board, has control unit electrically connected to sensor, where control unit converts disinfection unit into standby state in asepsis when rate of change of concentration reaches predetermined rate.
AU BONG J K
AE BONG J K (BONG-Individual)
GA 2019602017
AB    NOVELTY - The sterilizer has a chamber formed with a sterilization space. A disinfection unit supplies oxidizing agent within the chamber and oxidizes contaminant existing in an inner side of the chamber. A sensor is installed at the inner side of the chamber, and detects concentration of carbon dioxide. A control unit is electrically connected to the sensor, and converts the disinfection unit into a standby state in asepsis when rate of change of the concentration reaches predetermined rate. The disinfection unit is provided with an optical catalyst plate in which photocatalyst coating agent is coated. A UV ray lamp irradiates UV ray to the optical catalyst plate.
   USE - Sterilizer for sterilizing a product. Uses include but are not limited to a cutting board, a kitchen knife, a tableware, a dish cloth, a loofah, a nursing bottle, a spoon and chopsticks.
   ADVANTAGE - The control unit is electrically connected to the sensor, and converts the disinfection unit into the standby state in asepsis when rate of change of the concentration reaches the predetermined rate so as to oxidize the contaminant through oxidizing agent and display powerful sterilizing power. The sterilizer measures concentration of the carbon dioxide generated in oxidation of the contaminant so as to allow a user to confirm normal completion of sterilization.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a sterilizer. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'
DC P34 (Sterilising, syringes, electrotherapy (A61L, M, N).)
MC P34-A01
IP A61L-002/08; A61L-002/26; A61L-002/28

PT P
PN JP2019084464-A; JP6960825-B2
TI Gas separation membrane for separating and recovering olefin gas and carbon dioxide comprises porous hollow fiber support body which has gas-separation active substance impregnation layer, and gas-separation active layer.
AU MIKAWA M
   KURISHITA Y
   OHASHI H
AE ASAHI KASEI CORP (ASAH-C)
GA 201950278D
AB    NOVELTY - A gas separation membrane (100) comprises porous hollow fiber support body (10) which has gas-separation active substance impregnation layer (12) which impregnates gas-separation active substance in thickness direction from inner surface, and gas-separation active layer (20) which is arranged on inner surface of porous hollow fiber support body and contains a gas-separation active substance.
   USE - A gas separation membrane for separating and recovering olefin gas and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas separation membrane has high durability, high gas-separation performance and high gas-permeability, and has long-time operation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacturing gas separation membrane (100) which involves coating process which circulates coating liquid containing a gas-separation active substance inside a porous hollow fiber support body (10), and forming a coating surface, gas-separation active substance impregnation layer formation process which provides a pressure difference in inner side and outer side of porous hollow fiber support body, and forms a gas-separation active substance impregnation layer (12), and gas-separation active layer formation process which dries coating surface and forms a gas-separation active layer (20).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic sectional view of the gas separation membrane. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Porous hollow fiber support body (10)
   Hole (11)
   Gas-separation active substance impregnation layer (12)
   Gas-separation active layer (20)
   Gas separation membrane (100)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gas-separation active layer contains metal salt containing greater than or equal to 1 metal ion selected from silver ion and copper ion. Preferred Parameters: The permeation side gas flow rate is 50 cm/minute and supply side gas flow rate is 600 cm/minute. The transmission rate of carbon dioxide (CO2) gas measured by humidification atmosphere isobar in 30 degrees C is Q1. The transmission rate of nitrogen gas (N2) gas measured by humidification atmosphere isobar in 30 degrees C is Q2. The ratio of Q1 to Q2 is alpha 20-100 when Q1 is 1-100 GPU.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Parameters: The internal diameter of porous hollow fiber support body is 500-1500 mu m. The gas-separation active substance impregnation layer has thickness of 0.1-50 mu m. Ratio with respect to thickness of porous hollow fiber support body of the thickness of gas-separation active substance impregnation layer is less than or equal to 1/5. Average hole diameter in inner surface of porous hollow fiber support body is 0.005-1 mu m. Inner surface of porous hollow fiber body has surface hole area ratio of 5-50%. Transmission rate of propylene gas is 15-1500 GPU and separation factor of propylene/propane is alpha 50-1000. Preferred Components: The gas-separation active substance contains polymer gel which is a polymer containing amino group, pyridyl group, imidazolyl group, indolyl group, hydroxyl group, phenol group, ether group, carboxyl group, ester group, amide group, carbonyl group, thiol group, thioether group, sulfone group, sulfonyl group, and structure of formula -R-S(=O)2-NH2. The polymer gel is a polymer containing greater than or equal to 1 functional group selected from amino group, sulfone group, and hydroxyl group. Polymer is polyamine, such as chitosan. The mixed gas contains 60 mass% propane and 40 mass% propylene.R=methylene group or 2-5C alkylene group.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC A08-R01; A11-B09A1; A12-S08D; A12-W11A; E10-J02C3; E11-Q01B; E12-A04; E31-N05C; E35-A; E35-B; J01-E03E

PT P
PN CN109645393-A
TI Compound extract prepared by taking jujube powder, glutinous powder, koji powder, Chrysanthemum powder and ginger powder used as raw materials, mixing and extracting.
AU LI X
   MENG J
   WANG H
   LI D
AE QIANJIN PHARM CO LTD ZHUZHOU (QJPM-C)
GA 201939518H
AB    NOVELTY - Compound extract comprises (i) taking 3-10 pts. wt. jujube powder, 3-8 pts. wt. glutinous powder, 0.4-8 pts. wt. koji powder, 2-10 pts. wt. Chrysanthemum powder and 2-14 pts. wt. ginger powder used as raw materials, mixing and extracting the raw materials by supercritical carbon dioxide, and (ii) extracting for 3 times at 45-55 degrees C under the first extraction pressure of 25-30 MPa and the second extraction pressure is 15-20 MPa, the extraction temperature is 30-40 degrees C and the third extraction pressure is 8-15 MPa, and the extraction temperature is 25-30 degrees C.
   USE - The compound extract is useful in health product (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical; has high extraction rate; and improves utilization rate of raw material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of the compound extract, comprising (i) taking brown sugar or sugar, ginger powder, Chrysanthemum powder, medlar powder, jujube powder, xylooligosaccharide powder, Ampelopsis grossedentata leaf powder, crushing respectively, and screening; (ii) adding ginger , Chrysanthemum powder, medlar powder, jujube powder, Ampelopsis grossedentata leaf powder into supercritical carbon dioxide extracting device, adding cosolvent, setting extraction pressure and extracting to obtain extracting solution; (iii) heating the brown sugar powder or brown sugar and melting, adding to the extract, and adding the xylooligosaccharide powder and stirring at a high speed to obtain mixed liquid; and (iv) spray-drying the mixture, sterilizing, qualifying the water content, and sieving.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the three extractions have an extraction time of 35-60 minutes and a carbon dioxide flow rate of 30-40 l/hour. In the step (i), the sieve screen is 70-90 mesh. In the step (iv), the spray drying head has a rotation speed of 350-380 revolutions/minute, an inlet air temperature of 100-120 degrees C, an outlet air temperature of 60-75 degrees C, a feed flow rate of 30-40 revolutions/minute, and a fan frequency of 20-50 Hz. In the step (iv), the sterilization temperature is 90-100 degrees C, the sterilization time is 10-30 minutes, the moisture content is less than or equal to 8%, and the sieve mesh is 30-50 mesh. Preferred Components: The health care product comprises 7.4-43 pts. wt. compound extract, 50-63 pts. wt. sugar, and 1-5 pts. wt. xylooligosaccharide powder. . In the step (ii), the weight ratio of ginger powder and cosolvent is 7:1. In the step (iii), the weight ratio of brown sugar to extract is 1.15-7:1 and the cosolvent is water, ethanol, ethanol or water. In the step (iii), the heating temperature is 90-120 degrees C, the high speed stirring speed is 1500-1800 revolutions/minute, and the stirring time is 15-30 minutes.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PT P
PN WO2019040737-A1
TI Producing malonate ester involves fermenting recombinant host cell to produce malonic acid, contacting malonic acid with alkali metal base or ammonium base to produce malonic acid salt, isolating malonic acid salt.
AU VAN WALSEM J
AE LYGOS INC (LYGO-Non-standard)
GA 201919791X
AB    NOVELTY - Producing malonate ester involves fermenting a recombinant host cell to produce malonic acid, contacting the malonic acid with an alkali metal base or ammonium base to produce a malonic acid salt, isolating the malonic acid salt, esterifying the isolated malonic acid salt with an alcohol using supercritical carbon dioxide (sCC) or sulfuric acid as a catalyst, and distilling the malonate ester.
   USE - Method for producing malonate ester.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce malonate ester, which ensures that fermentation is both substantially pure of contaminating microbes and that the concentration of unconsumed carbon source at the completion of the fermentation is measured.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing diethyl malonate, which involves:
   (A) fermenting a recombinant host cell comprising a heterologous nucleic acid encoding a mutated malonyl-CoA hydrolase in fermentation broth and producing malonic acid;
   (B) contacting the malonic acid with an ammonium base to produce a malonic acid salt;
   (C) isolating the malonic acid salt from the fermentation broth by separating a liquid fraction comprising the malonic acid salt from cells, cell debris and soluble impurities; crystallizing the malonic acid salt;
   (D) isolating the crystalline malonic acid salt;
   (E) esterifying the crystalline malonic acid salt with an alcohol using supercritical carbon dioxide (sCC) or sulfuric acid as a catalyst to produce diethyl malonate; and
   (F) distilling the diethyl malonate; where the ammonium base controls the pH of fermentation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The recombinant host cell comprises one or more heterologous nucleic acids encoding a malonyl-CoA hydrolase. The recombinant host cell comprises one or more heterologous nucleic acids encoding a mutated 3-hydroxyisobutyryl-CoA hydrolase derived from Pseudomonas fulva. The mutations are selected from E95N, Q348A, F304R and combinations. The fermentation is performed in a broth, which comprises a carbon source and a nitrogen source. The carbon source is selected from monosaccharides, disaccharides, polysaccharides, glycerol, acetate, ethanol, methanol, methane and combinations. The fermentation is performed under aerobic conditions. The malonic acid is contacted with an alkali metal base selected from sodium bicarbonate, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide and combinations. The malonic acid salt is selected from Na2M, K2M and Na2M- monohydrate. The esterifying produces an alkali metal carbonate that neutralizes malonic acid in the culture. The malonic acid is contacted with an ammonium base selected from ammonia, ammonium hydroxide, ammonium carbonate and combinations. The ammonium base is ammonium hydroxide. The salt is (NH4)2M. The ammonium base neutralizes malonic acid in the culturing step. The recombinant host cell produces 20%, 30% or 40% of malonic acid by weight of the carbon source. The malonic acid titer is 80 g/l at the conclusion of the fermentation. The separating comprises centrifugation, microfiltration, ultrafiltration or combinations. The separation further comprises nanofiltration through a material selected from a ceramic membrane, metal membrane, polymer membrane, and composite membrane. The material comprises pores ranging in size from 0.0005-0.005 micrometer, equating to a molecular weight cut-off of about 100-2,000 Daltons. The host cell further comprises an integrating plasmid comprising an expression cassette with an AOXl promoter operably linked to the heterologous nucleic acid. The hydrolase comprises the amino acid mutation E95N. The hydrolase comprises the amino acid mutations E95N and Q348A. The hydrolase comprises the amino acid mutations E95N and F304R. The hydrolase comprises the amino acid mutations E95N, Q348A and F304R. The hydrolase is derived from Pseudomonas fulva. The fermentation is performed under aerobic conditions. The ammonium base is selected from ammonia, ammonium hydroxide, ammonium carbonate and combinations. The ammonium base is ammonium hydroxide and the malonic acid salt is diammonium malonate. The crystalline malonic acid salt is dried prior to esterification. Preferred Process: The isolating involves separating a liquid fraction comprising the malonic acid salt from cells, cell debris and soluble impurities, crystallizing the malonic acid salt, and isolating the crystalline malonic acid salt. The method further involves hydrolyzing the diethyl malonate to produce malonic acid.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PN CN109192991-A; CN109192991-B
TI Method for preparing graphene composite metal air            battery, involves quickly releasing pressure to form            protective film of magnesium carbonate on surface of            magnesium ball, to form protected metal magnesium            nanopowder material.
AU ZHANG L
   XIE W
   XIN W
   CHEN X
AE GUANGZHOU LIWEN ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGZHOU LIWEN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201910215N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves heating and melting the                metal magnesium, and adding a small amount of an                organic protective agent and a mixture of graphene.                High-speed stirring is performed to form a                nano-magnesium ball. A polymer film is formed on a                surface of the nano-magnesium ball by                polymerization by organic protective agent. The                graphene is coated on a surface of the                nano-magnesium ball. A pre-protected metal                magnesium ball is formed. The pre-protected metal                magnesium ball is poured into a supercritical                carbon dioxide, and subjected to secondary ball                milling. The pressure is quickly released to form a                protective film of magnesium carbonate on the                surface of the magnesium ball, to form a protected                metal magnesium nanopowder material. The protected                metal magnesium nanopowder material is tableted to                prepare a desired magnesium metal sheet.
   USE - Method for preparing graphene composite metal                air battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The protective film of magnesium carbonate is                formed on the surface of the metal magnesium by the                carbon dioxide supercritical method, and the metal                magnesium powder is effectively prevented from                contacting the air explosion, and the polymer film                only serves as a pre-protective film. The                electrical conductivity is improved by mixing                graphene on the surface of the magnesium                powder.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a graphical view of the air                stability test for protecting a treated magnesium                powder. (Drawing includes non-English language                text)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The argon gas is protected during the                  heating process. The organic protective agent is                  hydrocarbon polymer.

PT P
PN US10052581-B1; WO2019060153-A1; CN111247118-A; IN202017011054-A; RU2736090-C1; IN370724-B
TI Removing light gases from hydrocarbon feed stream e.g. ethane comprises absorbing carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide and carbonyl sulfide from the stream, hydrogenating acetylene, and removing and demethanizing heavier hydrocarbon feed stream.
AU MONTALBANO J A
   LECHNICK W J
   ZHU X X
   MONTALBANO J
   LECHNICK J W
AE UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
   UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
GA 201864814R
AB    NOVELTY - Removing (I) light gases from a hydrocarbon feed stream including ethylene, ethane and heavier hydrocarbons, comprises: (a) absorbing carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide and carbonyl sulfide from the hydrocarbon feed stream by contact with a solvent to provide an absorbed hydrocarbon feed stream; (b) hydrogenating acetylene in the absorbed hydrocarbon feed stream to ethane and ethylene to provide a hydrogenated hydrocarbon feed stream; (c) removing a heavier hydrocarbon feed stream from light gases comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide, nitrogen and methane in the hydrogenated hydrocarbon feed stream; and (d) demethanizing the heavier hydrocarbon feed stream to provide an overhead stream comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide, nitrogen and methane and a net bottoms stream comprising ethylene, ethane and the heavier hydrocarbons.
   USE - The method is useful for removing light gases from a hydrocarbon feed stream including ethylene, ethane and heavier hydrocarbons.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for removing (II) light gases from a hydrocarbon feed stream comprising steps (a), and (b) as above per se, (b1) selectively removing a heavier hydrocarbon feed stream from light gases comprising hydrogen, nitrogen, carbon monoxide and methane from the hydrogenated hydrocarbon feed stream in an adsorption zone to provide the heavier hydrocarbon feed stream, step (d) as above per se and (e) steam cracking at least a portion of the net bottoms stream to produce olefins.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: . The process further comprises drying the hydrogenated hydrocarbon feed stream before the selective adsorption by contacting the hydrogenated hydrocarbon feed stream with a hydrophilic adsorbent, chilling and condensing water from the hydrogenated hydrocarbon feed stream before contact with the hydrophilic adsorbent, washing the hydrocarbon feed stream with water to absorb chlorides and ammonia from the hydrocarbon feed stream before the absorption step, recycling the demethanizing overhead stream to the removal step, where the absorption step comprises absorbing carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide and carbonyl sulfide from the hydrocarbon feed stream by contact with a first solvent to remove carbon dioxide and most of the hydrogen sulfide and then by contact with a second solvent to remove carbon dioxide, carbonyl sulfide and hydrogen sulfide to low levels and provide the absorbed hydrocarbon feed stream, acid gas adsorbing hydrogen sulfide, carbonyl sulfide and carbon dioxide from the net bottoms stream by contacting the net bottoms stream with an acid gas adsorbent to provide an acid gas adsorbed net bottoms stream, regenerating a second acid gas adsorbent bed with a desorption gas while acid gas adsorbing hydrogen sulfide, carbonyl sulfide and carbon dioxide from the net bottoms stream by contacting the net bottoms stream with a first acid gas adsorbent bed to provide the acid gas adsorbed net bottoms stream, sweet adsorbing mercaptans and arsine from the acid gas adsorbed net bottoms stream by contacting the net bottoms stream with a sweetening adsorbent to provide a sweetened net bottoms stream, delivering the net bottoms stream to a steam cracking unit to produce olefins, and adding a hydrogenation sulfiding agent to attenuate catalyst activity and to reduce the potential for a temperature runaway, where the demethanizer column operates at a bottoms temperature of -30 to -50 degrees C. The heavier hydrocarbon feed stream is compressed prior to being passed to the demethanizer column.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

A control unit (12) is provided to process global positioning system information, which is received from the global positioning system receiver. An application program is provided to process data necessary for the processing of the control unit, and for storing data generated by the control unit. The control unit calculates an inclination angle of a road, on which the vehicle travels from the global positioning system information based on real-time reception of global positioning system data. The speed and acceleration of the vehicle is calculated from the received global positioning system information. A traveling resistance of the vehicle is calculated.

PN CN108098175-A
TI Method for welding sheet metal, involves grinding low carbon steel material, applying flux mixture powder, introducing argon, helium and/or carbon dioxide, preheating alloy steel material, welding, cooling, washing and air-drying.
AU YANG A
AE NINGBO JUNMING SHEET METAL CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
GA 201845449W
AB    NOVELTY - The method for welding sheet metal, involves (1) providing alloy steel material and low carbon steel material as sheet metal portions, (2) grinding low carbon steel material, applying flux mixture powder, cold air drying, adding solvent to form paste, (3) providing a boss at an end of alloy steel material, placing alloy steel material and low carbon steel material according to design requirements, (4) introducing argon, helium and/or carbon dioxide into elongated conduit to form gas mixture, using flow corrector to correct the flow of the gas mixture, forming ternary gas mixture as a welding shielding gas, (5) preheating alloy steel material at 400-420 degrees C, heating with to-be-welded electrode at 200-220 degrees C, welding at a contact between the boss and the low carbon steel material, (6) placing the weldment in oven or heat-preserving device at 180-220 degrees C and slowly cooling to room temperature, after the welding is finished, washing with warm water at 50-70 degrees C and then air-drying.
   USE - Method for welding sheet metal.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables welding of sheet metal with increased welding heat input, improved arc stability, excellent weld bead wetting performance and improved welding fluidity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The particle size of flux mixture powder is 150-250 mesh.

     hydrogen gas, entering into the deaerator to remove                oxygen and releases heat, passing the heated argon                gas through the second cooler, cooling and                purification in a cooling and argon pre-cooling                purification system, (iii) entering the argon gas                cooled and purified into the rectification cold                box, refined argon column, (iv) throttling the                gas-liquid mixed fluid partially liquefied by the                argon column reboiler and sending into the upper                part of the rectification column through                a pure liquid argon section, where the flow valve                is throttled and depressurized, entering the                external replenishing liquid argon into the                evaporation side of the condensing evaporator to                evaporate to argon gas, reheating the argon gas                through the main heat exchanger to recover the                cooling amount, sending the rectifying cold box and                entering the pure argon user pipeline, (vi)                accumulating the argon gas generated by the                rectification of the refined argon column on the                top of the refined argon column, entering a part of                the argon gas at the top of the refined argon                column on the gas side condensation of the                condensing evaporator a liquid, flowing into the                top of the refined argon column and condensing a                portion of the gas entering the auxiliary                condensing evaporator into a liquid, (vii)                compressing the air by the air compressor, cooling                the air pre-cooling purification of the                main heat exchanger, entering the expander, sending                into the fine nitrogen tower to participate in the                rectification, cooling the other part after the air                passes through the nitrogen, liquefying the tower                reboiler and allowing to enters the gas-liquid                separator for gas-liquid separation, (ix) dividing TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (v), the pure                  liquid argon extracted from the bottom of the                  rectification column is throttled and                  depressurized by the pure liquid argon throttle                  valve and evaporated into the evaporation side of                  the condensing evaporator together with the                  external replenishing liquid argon. The                  temperature difference outside is 0.5-1.5 degrees                  C in the argon tower reboiler.

AE UNIV TIANJIN CHENGJIAN (UTCJ-C)
GA 201901597L
AB    NOVELTY - Flame retardant halloysite-silica composite aerogel material, is claimed. The preparation of flame retardant halloysite-silica composite aerogel material comprises (i) adding halloysite nanotubes to a mixture of deionized water and ethanol, uniformly dispersing, adding sodium styrene sulfonate, polydivinylsiloxane, initiator and hexabromocyclododecane to the mixture of deionized water and ethanol to obtain mixed solution, adding mixed solution to halloysite dispersion, ultrasonically dispersing, vacuuming, washing product, heating and polymerizing; (ii) washing step (i) product, dispersing in water, ethyl orthosilicate, hydrochloric acid, initiator and bisphenol bis(diphenyl phosphate), stirring, heating, adding sodium hydroxide, stirring and polymerizing; and (iii) adding methacryloyloxydodecyl trimethyl ammonium chloride to flame retardant halloysite-silica composite wet gel, placing product in supercritical high pressure extraction device containing carbon dioxide and drying.
   USE - Used as flame retardant halloysite-silica composite aerogel material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material has synergistic effect. The method utilizes different properties of flame retardant in combination.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Flame retardant halloysite-silica composite aerogel material is claimed. The average specific surface area of the halloysite-silica composite aerogel material is 600-604 m2/g and containing both nanoscale and micrometer-scale pores, the average nano-scale pores size is 10-11 nm and the average micron-scale pore size is 20-21 mu m. The preparation of flame retardant halloysite-silica composite aerogel material comprises (i) adding 0.6-16 pts. wt. halloysite nanotubes to a mixture of 50 pts. wt. deionized water and 50 pts. wt. ethanol, uniformly dispersing to obtain a halloysite dispersion, adding 0.6-16 pts. wt. sodium styrene sulfonate, 0.06-4 pts. wt. polydivinylsiloxane, 0.01-0.4 pts. wt. initiator and 0.05-12 pts. wt. hexabromocyclododecane to the mixture of 50 pts. wt. deionized water and 50 pts. wt. ethanol to obtain mixed solution, adding the mixed solution to the halloysite dispersion, ultrasonically dispersing, vacuuming, repeating the vacuuming step three times, washing the product, dispersing in 100 pts. wt. water, heating in a water bath to 60-120 degrees C to initiate polymerization and polymerizing for 48 hours to obtain step (i) product, where the average molecular weight of polydivinylsiloxane is 500-5000, preferably 1000-300 and 0.1-5% vinyl content mole percentage amino mono-terminated polydimethyldivinylsiloxane or an amino-terminated polydimethyldivinylsiloxane; (ii) washing the step (i) product, dispersing in 100 pts. wt. water, 74-154 pts. wt. ethyl orthosilicate, 0.07-1.5 pts. wt. 12 mol/l hydrochloric acid, 0.01-1.8 pts. wt. initiator and 0.08-16 pts. wt. bisphenol bis(diphenyl phosphate), uniformly stirring, heating in water bath to 30-75 degrees C to initiate polymerization, adding 0.01-0.4 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide, stirring to dissolve completely, polymerizing for 48 hours to obtain a flame retardant halloysite-silica composite wet gel; and (iii) adding 0.08-1.2 pts. wt. methacryloyloxydodecyl trimethyl ammonium chloride to the flame retardant halloysite-silica composite wet gel, allowing to stand, placing the product in supercritical high pressure extraction device containing carbon dioxide and drying with carbon dioxide as medium at 32-58 degrees C and the pressure is 6-12 MPa for 1 hour. The inner side of the wall of the halloysite nanotube has a positive charge, and the outside of the tube wall has a negative charge. The sodium styrene sulfonate added in the step 1 has a negative charge, and the sodium styrene sulfonate is adsorbed on the halloysite nanometer by electrostatic action on the inner wall of the tube, the polydivinylsiloxane, initiator and hexabromocyclododecane added in step 1 are also dispersed in the hollow structure of the halloysite nanotube. The hollow structure of the halloysite nanotube is flame retardant. The halloysite-silica composite aerogel material provides a microporous structure. The polydivinylsiloxane is copolymerized with sodium styrene sulfonate to form an interior of the halloysite nanotube after vacuuming and washing and hexabromocyclododecane structure is supported in an halloysite nanotube. The outside of the halloysite nano-tube hollow structure of poly-vinyl siloxane of step (ii) is vinyl functional group is polymerized under the action of evocating agent. The oxane co-forms a three-dimensional network structure and the ethyl orthosilicate, hydrochloric acid and sodium hydroxide added in the step (ii) react to form silicon dioxide particles and silicon dioxide particles are constructed as a network pore structure, and the three-dimensional network structure and the silicon dioxide particles are constructed. The network pore structure together forms a three-dimensional network pore structure. The three-dimensional network pore structure provides a mesoporous structure for the flame retardant halloysite-silica composite aerogel material, and simultaneously loads bisphenol bis(diphenyl phosphate) in the mesoporous structure. AN INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing flame retardant halloysite-silica composite aerogel material, comprising the steps as mentioned above.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The initiator is dibenzoyl peroxide (BPO) or azobisisobutyronitrile (ABIN). In the step (iii), the 0.1-1 pts. wt. methacryloyloxydodecyltrimethylammonium chloride is added, incubated for 1-6 hours, drying is carried out at 33-50 degrees C for 2-4 hours, preferably 3 hours with gas pressure of 7-10 MPa. Preferred Method: In the step (i), adding 1-15 pts. wt. halloysite nanotubes to a mixture of deionized water and ethanol, ultrasonically dispersing for 1-2 hours, adding 1-15 pts. wt. sodium styrene sulfonate, 0.1-2 pts. wt. polydivinylsiloxane, 0.01-0.1 pts. wt. initiator and 0.1-10 pts. wt. hexabromocyclododecane to the mixture of deionized water and ethanol, stirring for 15-65 minutes, adding the mixed solution to the halloysite dispersion, ultrasonically dispersing for 20-40 minutes, vacuuming for 1-2 hours, polymerizing at 60-120 degrees C for 10-60 minutes, cooling to 15-50 degrees C for 12-24 hours and polymerizing at 80-90 degrees C for 2-8 hours. In the step (ii), adding 80-150 pts. wt. ethyl orthosilicate, 0.1-1 pts. wt. 12 mol/l hydrochloric acid, 0.01-1 pts. wt. initiator and 0.1-15 pts. wt. bisphenol bis(diphenyl phosphate), uniformly stirring for 10-70 minutes, polymerizing at 30-75 degrees C for 10-40 minutes, adding 0.01-0.2 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide, stirring to dissolve completely, cooling to 20-50 degrees C, polymerizing for 20-35 hours and polymerizing at 80 degrees C, 90 degrees C and 100 degrees C for 2-4 hours.

AB    NOVELTY - Microbial fermenting device comprises a progressively-disposed scraper, a mixer, a pre-fermenter, a screed, and a final fermenter. The scraper mechanism includes a hopper (1), a feed hopper (101), a control arm (2), a revolution shaft, a revolution frame, a rotation shaft, an autorotation screw reamer, an auto-rotator shaft two, an autorotation screw reamer two, and the tail end of the feed hopper is connected to the upper part of the hopper. A revolution rack is provided, and a revolution shaft and a rotation shaft are respectively arranged on the revolution rack, and an autorotation is set on the rotation shaft. A screw reamer, an autorotation screw reamer is arranged on the rotation shaft.
   USE - Used as microbial fermenting device of liquid fertilizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The device accelerates fermentation process; and improves yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Microbial fermenting device comprises a progressively-disposed scraper, a mixer, a pre-fermenter, a screed, and a final fermenter. The scraper mechanism includes a hopper (1), a feed hopper (101), a control arm (2), a revolution shaft, a revolution frame, a rotation shaft, an autorotation screw reamer, an auto-rotator shaft two, an autorotation screw reamer two, and the tail end of the feed hopper is connected to the upper part of the hopper. The control arm is disposed above the hopper, and the revolution shaft is disposed on the control arm and is located inside the hopper on the revolution shaft. A revolution rack is provided, and a revolution shaft and a rotation shaft are respectively arranged on the revolution rack, and an autorotation is set on the rotation shaft. A screw reamer, an autorotation screw reamer is arranged on the rotation shaft. The mixing mechanism includes a mixing tank, a support arm, a turret, a rotating shaft, a mixing knife, a control arm two, and a collecting hopper. The mixing tank is connected to the bottom end of the hopper. The upper side wall of the mixing tank is provided with multiple support arms. The support arms rotate the frame connected with a rotating shaft, and multiple of mixing knives are arranged on the rotating shaft, and the control arm two is arranged on the side of the mixing tank. The junction hopper is connected to the bottom of the mixing tank. The pre-fermentation mechanism includes a rotating arm, a rotating shaft, a rotating plate, a pre-fed tank, a feed inlet, a temperature control component, and a classification plate. The rotating shaft is disposed on the turning arm, and the rotating shaft is connected with the rotating plate, and multiple pre-fed boxes are arranged on the rotating plate. A feed inlet is provided at the top of the pre-fed tank. The feed inlet corresponds to the size of the bottom outlet of the collecting hopper. The temperature control unit is set at on the pre-fermentation box, multiple classification boards are set in the pre-fed box. The sizing mechanism includes a collecting hopper, a sizing tube, a rotating shaft, a slicing reamer and a motor. The collecting hopper is disposed on the rotating disk at the bottom; the screed pipe is connected with the bottom of the collection hopper. The screed pipe is provided with a rotating shaft. A reamer (a reamer) is arranged on the rotating shaft. The motor is disposed outside the sizing tube and is connected with the rotating shaft. The multiple mesh holes are arranged at the bottom of the sizing tube. The final fermentation mechanism includes a final fermentation tank, a sealing chute, a sealing door, a layered board, a opening and closing door, a temperature control tube, and a thermostat, trachea, carbon dioxide filter, water pipe, atomizer and the final fermentation tank is disposed directly below the sizing tube. A sealing chute is arranged on both sides of the upper end of the fermentation tank, a sealing door is arranged on the sealing chute, and multiple of layered boards are arranged in the final fermentation tank, a closing door is disposed on one side of the final fermentation tank, and a temperature control box and a carbon dioxide filter are disposed on the other side of the final fermentation tank. An atomizer is provided with multiple temperature control tubes on the temperature control box, and the temperature control tube extends inside the final fermentation tank. The carbon dioxide filter connected to the final fermentation tank via a gas line, which is connected to the final fermentation tank via a water line.

AB    NOVELTY - The factory (100) has a plant (10) which                produces the exhaust gas which contains CO2 during                working. A CO2 capture processing system has an                introducing unit which takes in mixture gas                containing a CO2 which includes the exhaust gas of                the plant. A separation membrane raises the CO2                levels in the mixture gas, and generates CO2                concentration mixture gas. A concentration mixture                gas discharge unit discharges the CO2 concentration                mixture gas. A residual mixture gas discharge unit                discharges the residual mixture gas containing the                low concentration CO2 which remains after the CO2                concentration mixture gas is generated. The CO2                concentration mixture gas generation apparatus                connects the CO2 in concentration mixture gas to                the concentration mixture gas discharge unit of the                concentration mixture gas generation                apparatus.
   USE - Carbon-dioxide (CO2) negative emission factory                for making negative value emission of CO2 from                factory to exterior.
   ADVANTAGE - The CO2 negative emission factory is                constructed such that the CO2 negative emission is                achieved. The separation membrane prevents the                transmission.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The CO2 converter discharges the last residual                gas from a discharge unit. A final-treatment                apparatus discharges the gas which does not contain                CO2. The CO2 direct acquisition apparatus (30)                takes in the air contained in ambient environment                and supplies the air to an upstream side from the                final-treatment apparatus of the CO2 capture                processing system. The concentration mixture gas                discharge unit is in a reduced pressure state. The                exhaust gas from the plant is introduced into the                introducing unit of the CO2 concentration mixture                gas generation apparatus in the CO2 capture                processing system. The                CO2 direct acquisition apparatus is arranged to                distribute the air contained in the taken-in                ambient environment to the final-treatment                apparatus of the CO2 capture processing system. The                exhaust gas containing the CO2 generated in the                plant is in the range of 1.5 wt% or more in CO2                levels. The final-treatment apparatus is equipped                with a collection receptacle. The structure                collects the CO2s. An installation utilizes the                exhaust heat from the product manufacturing plant                for the heating at the time of the collection when                a heating is performed. The CO2 converter converts                the CO2 in received concentration mixture gas into                carbonate. The residual mixture gas is discharged                from a discharge unit. The carbonate is a calcium                carbonate. The CO2 capture processing system has a                structure which drives with natural energy. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a CO2 capture                processing system for carrying out acquisition                process of CO2, has an introducing unit which takes                in mixture gas containing a CO2. The separation                membrane raises the CO2 levels in the mixture gas,                and generates CO2 concentration mixture gas. A                first power generation device (40) receives the                hydrogen-containing compound from the conversion                installation, generates electric power, and                supplies the generated electric power to the plant.                A second power generation device (50) generates                electric power with natural energy and supplies the                generated electric power to the plant. The CO2                capture installation captures the CO2 contained in                ambient environment, and supplies the captured CO2                to the conversion installation.

PN KR2021111637-A
TI Obtaining pearl protein solubilized product and Grateloupia elliptica extract useful in cosmetic material for preventing hair damage comprises preparing pearl powder, mixing the pearl powder with alkali agent, and performing hydrolysis.
AU LEE E
AE STANLEY SMART COVERING (STAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021A7815E
AB    NOVELTY - Obtaining pearl protein solubilized product and Grateloupia elliptica extract comprises preparing pearl powder, mixing the pearl powder with alkali agent having greater than or equal to 20 wt.% of the protein content in the pearl powder, heating, stirring at a temperature of greater than or equal to 100 degrees C for greater than or equal to 2 hours to hydrolyze to the constituent units of peptides and amino acids an alkali solubilization step in which basic nitrogen is generated during the hydrolysis, adding an acidic aqueous solution containing hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, acetic acid, and citric acid to the solubilized product reacted in the alkali solubilization step to neutralize it in a range of pH 8-9 to prevent precipitation of oligomeric or higher polymer peptides, in the stabilization step, in order to remove the base nitrogen generated from the solubilized product, placing solubilized product and water into high-pressure reactor in an environment with carbon dioxide and concentrating.
   USE - The product is useful in cosmetic material for preventing hair damage.
   ADVANTAGE - The product improves elasticity, gloss and smoothness of the hair.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Obtaining pearl protein solubilized product and Grateloupia elliptica extract comprises (a) preparing pearl powder, (b) mixing the pearl powder with alkali agent having greater than or equal to 20 wt.% of the protein content in the pearl powder, heating, stirring at a temperature of greater than or equal to 100 degrees C for greater than or equal to 2 hours to hydrolyze to the constituent units of peptides and amino acids an alkali solubilization step in which basic nitrogen is generated during the hydrolysis, (c) adding an acidic aqueous solution containing hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, acetic acid, and citric acid to the solubilized product reacted in the alkali solubilization step to neutralize it in a range of pH 8-9 to prevent precipitation of oligomeric or higher polymer peptides, (d) in the stabilization step, in order to remove the base nitrogen generated from the solubilized product, placing solubilized product and water into high-pressure reactor in an environment with carbon dioxide and concentrating under the conditions of 30 atmospheres and 0-4 degrees C, forming lattice structure, hydrating in which gas containing carbon dioxide in the lattice structure is collected, and water surrounds the gas containing carbon dioxide and basic nitrogen to form a hydrate, so that the basic nitrogen contained in the solubilizer is concentration of the solubilized product using clathrate hydrate to separate, (e) transferring the concentrated solubilizer to a separator in the concentration step of the solubilization using clathrate hydrate to separate the hydrate and the base nitrogen from which the hydrate is formed to form a clathrate hydrate, (f) obtaining a pearl solubilized product isolated in the filtration and separation, (g) dry pulverizing red algae, Grateloupia elliptica, to prepare a powder, (h) performing hydrolysis for 1-3 hours in the range of 1 atm at 80-100 degrees C under an acid catalyst containing 10-30% of citric acid based on the weight of gambling powder, (i) removing unreacted residues remaining in the product hydrolyzed in step (h), (j) adding 80-100 wt.% acid catalyst with the resultant hydrolyzed under the acid catalyst of step (h) and an inorganic salt catalyst comprising sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, sodium hydroxide, and potassium hydroxide Neutralizing the pH within the range of 5-9, (k) concentrating the hydrolysis neutralized product neutralized in step (j) within the range of 5-30 Brix, and (l) after step (k), obtaining Grateloupia elliptica extract.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises (m) mixing pearl protein solubilized product and Grateloupia elliptica extract.
   ACTIVITY - Endocrine-Gen. Test details are described but no results given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of obtaining Grateloupia elliptica extract chemical entities that do not support the claims.

A rotary cutter (8) is provided on one side of the shear thinning section, and a console (19) is provided on the side of the top of the base (20) close to the motor.
   USE - Device for continuously preparing small particles of reclaimed rubber with low Mooney rebound (Claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device realizes the continuous automation of reclaimed rubber refining production, realizes the entire operation process in a closed environment, and is environmentally friendly and saves labor, and by adding supercritical carbon dioxide fluid in the compression and refining section, the Mooney viscosity of the desulfurized rubber powder is greatly reduced and the broken cross-linked network is homogenized, and the torque of the extruder is reduced, which greatly reduces the difficulty of equipment manufacturing and energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for continuously preparing low-viscosity small particle reclaimed rubber, which involves:
   (A) completing the desulfurization process, the desulfurized rubber powder with a Mooney viscosity of 120-200 enters a co-rotating twin-screw refining extruder through the feeding device;
   (B) controlling the temperature of the first extrusion conveying section to 15-45 degrees C, the temperature of the compression refining section to 80-120 degrees C, and the pressure to be 7.0-11.0 megapascal, temperature of the shear thinning section is 15-25 degrees C, the pressure is 2.0-5.0 megapascal, and the temperature of the steady flow cooling conveying section is 8-12 degrees C;
   (C) adding 0.05-1.0 wt.% carbon dioxide fluid by mass to the compression refining section of the extruder under the cooperation of the carbon dioxide storage tank and the injection pump, it is in the supercritical carbon dioxide state; and
   (D) adding 0.2-2 wt.% of the release agent in the steady-flow cooling conveying section of the extruder, and control the screw speed at 100-200 revolutions/minute to react for 1-4 minutes, and small particles of reclaimed rubber with a Mooney viscosity of less than 60 with a temperature of 30-60 degrees C and a particle diameter of 5-30 mm with a Mooney viscosity of less than 60 are prepared by the extrusion of the extruder head.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the front cross-sectional view of device for continuously preparing small particles of reclaimed rubber with low Mooney rebound.
   Motor (1)
   Reduction box (2)
   Twin screw extrusion mechanism (4)
   Toothed disk (7)
   Rotary cutter (8)
   Steady flow cooling and conveying section (9)
   Mounting seat (10)
   Shear refinement section (14)
   Compression refining section (15)

PT P
PN CN110233004-A; CN110233004-B
TI Method for recycling conductive paste, involves placing mixture in mixing tank, passing heating medium into heating medium chamber, stopping stirring, vacuuming barrel followed by stopping vacuuming and introducing hydrogen and nitrogen.
AU LI Y
AE GAO J (GAOJ-Individual)
   LI Y (LIYY-Individual)
   CHEN J (CHEN-Individual)
   WANG H (WANG-Individual)
   LIU X (LIUX-Individual)
   WANG Z (WANG-Individual)
GA 201981249S
AB    NOVELTY - A conductive paste recycling method involves placing a mixture in a mixing tank and passing a heating medium into the heating medium chamber. The heating medium and the planetary agitator is stopped to stir and the mixing barrel is vacuumed. A volume ratio of 5:95 hydrogen and nitrogen is introduced into the mixing tank to quickly return the pressure in the mixing tank to 1 atmosphere. The heating medium is introduced into a heating medium chamber. The content of carbon dioxide gas in the gas discharged from the mixing drum is detected that no carbon dioxide gas is volatilized, the heating medium is stopped and stirred by the planetary agitator to obtain a mixed powder of the metal powder and the ceramic powder. The mixed powder is analyzed by an intracranial pressure device to obtain a ratio of the metal powder and the ceramic powder. The mixed powder is re-feeded to prepare a new conductive paste.
   USE - Method for recycling conductive paste.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reusing waste products and reducing environmental pollution and production cost, ensuring that the re-acquired raw materials can be directly used for production and powder is less prone to agglomeration, which is more conducive to dispersion during production and processing as the moisture in the mixed powder is completely volatilized.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A conductive paste recycling method involves placing a mixture in a mixing tank, after the preliminary filtration of the conductive slurry to be recovered, where the side wall of the lower barrel of the mixing barrel and the bottom barrel wall has a heating medium chamber and the upper barrel of the mixing barrel has a planetary agitator extending into the lower barrel of the mixing drum. A heating medium is passed into a heating medium chamber, where the heating temperature is 140-180 degrees C, the speed of the planetary agitator is 80-120 rpm/minute and the heating and stirring time is 0.8-1.2 hours. The heating medium and the planetary agitator is stopped to stir, the mixing barrel is vacuumed and vacuuming is stopped when the vacuum degree reaches 0.08--0.11 MPa. A volume ratio of 5:95 hydrogen and nitrogen is introduced into the mixing tank to quickly return the pressure in the mixing tank to 1 atmosphere, when the pressure in the mixing tank returns to 1 atmosphere, the volume ratio of the inlet is 5:95, and the flow rate of hydrogen and nitrogen is controlled to be 180-220 ml/minute. The heating medium is introduced into the heating medium chamber, where the heating temperature is 280-320 degrees C and the speed of the planetary agitator is 180-220 rpm/minute. The content of carbon dioxide gas in the gas discharged from the mixing drum is detected that no carbon dioxide gas is volatilized, the heating medium is stopped and stirred by the planetary agitator to obtain a mixed powder of the metal powder and the ceramic powder. The mixed powder is analyzed by an intracranial pressure device to obtain a ratio of the metal powder and the ceramic powder. The mixed powder is re-feeded to prepare a new conductive paste on the basis of the ratio of the metal powder to the ceramic powder in the mixed powder, metal powder or ceramic powder is added to satisfy the ratio of the metal powder and the ceramic powder in the new conductive paste.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The heating medium is a silicone oil.
