PN CN108794931-A
TI Modification of nano-calcium carbonate used for preparing polyvinyl chloride sheet, involves carbonizing lime emulsion, ultrasonically stirring obtained slurry with surface treatment agent, drying obtained slurry, and crushing.
AU SHI B
AE QINZHOU BAOYE NIXING POTTERY FACTORY (QINZ-Non-standard)
GA 2018927156
AB    NOVELTY - Modification of nano-calcium carbonate involves adding quicklime and hot water to a digestion reactor, digesting, removing impurities, aging, diluting, obtaining a lime emulsion, adding the lime emulsion to a carbonation reaction tower, introducing carbon dioxide, carbonizing, adding a crystal-form controlling agent and a dispersing agent during the carbonization process, carbonizing to pH of 7-8, terminating introduction of carbon dioxide, controlling calcium carbonate specific surface area of 25-40 m2/g, obtaining nano-calcium carbonate slurry, adding nano-calcium carbonate slurry to an ultrasonic stirrer, heating to 60-80 degrees C, adding a surface treatment agent comprising vegetable oil fatty acid alkanolamide borate and dodecafluoroheptylpropyl cage silsesquioxane, ultrasonically stirring for 20-30 minutes, obtaining a modified nano-calcium carbonate slurry, drying the modified nano-calcium carbonate slurry, and crushing.
   USE - Modification of nano-calcium carbonate used for preparing polyvinyl chloride sheet (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The modified nano-calcium carbonate has high dispersibility, and excellent flame retardance, weather resistance and mechanical property. The PVC sheet prepared using the modified nano-calcium carbonate has high strength, and excellent impact resistance, heat resistance and durability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Modification of nano-calcium carbonate involves adding quicklime and hot water to a digestion reactor, digesting, removing impurities, aging, diluting, obtaining a lime emulsion, adding the lime emulsion to a carbonation reaction tower, introducing carbon dioxide, carbonizing, adding a crystal-form controlling agent and a dispersing agent during the carbonization process, carbonizing to pH of 7-8, terminating introduction of carbon dioxide, controlling calcium carbonate specific surface area of 25-40 m2/g, obtaining nano-calcium carbonate slurry, adding nano-calcium carbonate slurry to an ultrasonic stirrer, heating to 60-80 degrees C, adding a surface treatment agent comprising vegetable oil fatty acid alkanolamide borate and dodecafluoroheptylpropyl cage silsesquioxane, ultrasonically stirring for 20-30 minutes, obtaining a modified nano-calcium carbonate slurry, drying the modified nano-calcium carbonate slurry, and crushing. The crystal-form controlling agent comprises phytic acid and tartaric acid. The dispersing agent comprises polyoxyethylene glyceryl ether monostearate and succinate sulfonated hydrogenated castor oil. The addition amount of crystal-form controlling agent and dispersing agent is 1-2 wt.% and 1.5-2.5 wt.%, respectively with respect to the dry basis of calcium carbonate. The addition amount of surface treatment agent is 1.5-3 wt.% with respect to the dry basis of calcium carbonate. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) usage of nano-calcium carbonate in polyvinyl chloride (PVC) sheet material. The PVC sheet material comprises 100 pts. wt. PVC resin, 5-10 pts. wt. ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer, 50-100 pts. wt. wood powder, 40-70 pts. wt. modified nano-calcium carbonate, 3-5 pts. wt. boron nitride, 3-5 pts. wt. glass fiber, 4-6 pts. wt. shell powder, 2-4 pts. wt. sodium dodecylbenzene sulfonate, 1-3 pts. wt. polyether-modified polydimethylsiloxane, 4-6 pts. wt. composite foaming agent, 1-3 pts. wt. coupling agent, 2-4 pts. wt. stabilizer, 1-3 pts. wt. lubricant, 0.5-1.5 pt. wt. antibacterial agent and 3-5 pts. wt. processing additive; and
   (2) preparation of PVC sheet, which involves mixing wood powder, modified nano-calcium carbonate, boron nitride, glass fiber, shell powder, sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate and polyether-modified polydimethylsiloxane, obtaining modified mixture, adding PVC resin and ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer resin to a high-speed mixer, heating, adding modified mixture, composite foaming agent, coupling agent, stabilizer, lubricant, antibacterial agent and processing additive, stirring, extruding obtained mixture in a conical twin-screw extruder, cooling, and molding.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of quicklime and hot water is 1:4-6.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of phytic acid and tartaric acid in the crystal-form controlling agent is 1:1-3. The mass ratio of polyoxyethylene glyceryl ether monostearate and succinate sulfonated hydrogenated castor oil in the dispersing agent is 1:1-3. The composite foaming agent comprises AC foaming agent and sucrose rosinate in a mass ratio of 1-3:1. The antibacterial agent comprises chitosan and cerium oxide in a mass ratio of 1-3:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The processing additive comprises nonylphenol polyoxyethylene ether and polyaminopropylmethylsilsesquioxane in a mass ratio of 1-3:1.

PN US2022306499-A1
TI Redox-active compound for electrochemical cells            used for generating pH gradient in fluid stream, such            as seawater, comprises product obtained by reaction of            electron-withdrawing monomer comprising imidazolium,            pyridinium and pyrazolium, with cross-linker and            ionomer or polymer redox-active moiety.
AU BEH E S
AE PALO ALTO RES CENT INC (PRCA-C)
GA 2022C4625C
AB    NOVELTY - A redox-active compound comprises a product                obtained by the reaction of an electron-withdrawing                monomer with a cross-linker and a redox-active                moiety.
   USE - Redox-active compound is used in                electrochemical cells (claimed) used for generating                pH gradient in fluid stream, such as                seawater.
   ADVANTAGE - The redox-active compounds when used in                electrochemical cells, form a pH gradient, at low                energy, to enable efficient extraction of carbon                dioxide from seawater and increase the carbon                dioxide absorption capacity of seawater.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   a)) redox-active composition which comprises                the redox-active compound, a binder and a current                collector;
   b) membraneless electrochemical cell (400)                which comprises: i) a fluid stream input to the                membraneless electrochemical cell; ii) a first                electrode (402) containing a first current                collector and a first redox-active material which                are provided to enable a proton-coupled oxidation                reaction with a first portion of the fluid stream                input and form a pH-modified first portion, the                first redox-active material contains a first                electron-withdrawing monomer, a first cross-linker                and a first redox-active moiety; iii) a second                electrode (404) containing a second current                collector and a second redox-active material                provided to enable a proton-coupled reduction                reaction with a second portion of the fluid stream                input and form a pH-modified second portion, the                second redox-active material contains a second                electron-withdrawing monomer, a second cross-linker                and a second redox-active moiety; iv) a first                effluent stream (416) containing pH-modified first                portion and a first pH; and v) a second effluent                stream (418) comprising pH-modified second portion                and a second pH which is different from the first                pH; and
   c) method which involves:
   i) flowing an aqueous electrolyte feed stream                into the fluid stream input of the membraneless                electrochemical cell, followed by directing a first                portion of the feed stream to contact the first                electrode, then directing a second portion of the                feed stream to contact the second electrode and                applying an electrical potential to the first and                second electrodes.
   ii) The redox-active material on the first                electrode is oxidized in contact with the first                portion of the feed stream, in response to applying                the electrical potential, forms the pH-modified                first portion and the redox-active material on the                second electrode, contacting the second portion of                the feed stream, is reduced, to form the                pH-modified second portion.
   iii) The pH-modified first portion from the                electrochemical cell, is removed as the first                effluent stream and the pH-modified second portion                from the electrochemical cell, is removed as the                second effluent stream.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of                membraneless electrochemical cell containing                oxygen-stable redox-active compound.
   400Electrochemical cell
   402First electrode
   404Second electrode
   416First effluent stream
   418Second effluent stream
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: 1) The cross-linker of                  the redox-active compound, is connected to the                  redox-active moiety through the                  electron-withdrawing functional group.2) The redox-active compound has a reduced                  form and an oxidized form, which are not                  decomposed by oxygen.3) The redox-active compound undergoes a                  proton-coupled redox reaction at a predetermined                  pH.4) The first redox-active material and the                  second redox-active material contained in the                  membraneless electrochemical cell, have the same                  chemical composition.5) At least one of the first and second                  redox-active materials of the cell, is grafted                  onto at least one of the first or second                  electrodes.Preferred Components: The                  electron-withdrawing monomer of the redox-active                  compound, comprises imidazolium, pyridinium                  and/or pyrazolium.The redox-active moiety present in the                  compound, is an ionomer or a polymer containing a                  quinone, phenazine, pyrazine, quinoxaline and/or                  their derivatives.Preferred Composition: The redox-active                  compound is a polymer containing                  electron-withdrawing monomer and cross-linker, in                  a weight ratio 1-10: 1.Preferred Method: The method of using the                  redox-active material, further involves reacting                  the first effluent stream with a carbonate-rich                  mineral and releasing a target molecule or solute                  from the first effluent stream.

TI Herbal atomizing essential oil used for promoting            blood circulation, comprises red jujube, longan, tsaoko            amomum fruit, cinnamon, raspberry, clove, dried ginger,            malt, tuckahoe, white hyacinth bean flower, black            jujube and fermented soybean.
AU YANG Z
   YANG J
AE WUHUADA HEALTH IND GROUP HAINAN CO LTD (WUHU-Non-standard)
GA 2022C65317
AB    NOVELTY - Herbal atomizing essential oil, comprises                15-35 pts. wt. red jujube, 10-20 pts. wt. longan,                7-25 pts. wt. tsaoko amomum fruit, 5-20 pts. wt.                cinnamon, 3-15 pts. wt. raspberry, 3-15 pts. wt.                clove, 4-15 pts. wt. dried ginger, 3-10 pts. wt.                malt, 4-10 pts. wt. tuckahoe, 4-12 pts. wt. white                hyacinth bean flower, 2-8 pts. wt. black jujube,                and 1-8 pts. wt. fermented soybean. The processing                method of the red jujube and black jujube all                comprises (a) taking proper amount of raw                materials, washing the surface impurities and                controlling the moisture; (b) placing the raw                material under ultra-low temperature to rapidly                cool for 8-15 minutes, taking out and breaking the                wall into powder A. The processing method of tsaoko                amomum fruit, cinnamon, dried ginger, white                hyacinth bean flower, and clove comprises taking                proper amount of raw material and drying, making                into small pieces, crushing, and passing through a                120-meshes sieve to obtain powder B.
   USE - The herbal atomizing essential oil is useful                for promoting blood circulation.
   ADVANTAGE - The essential oil does not contain toxic and                irritating substances, will not cause side effects                to the human body when used for a long time, has no                addiction, is safe and effective, and is safe and                effective, has the functions of warming the palace,                meridian circulation, smooth ventilation and blood,                and achieves the purpose of cold hands and feet due                to poor blood flow.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparation method of herbal atomizing essential                oil, comprising (1) taking the processed powder A,                powder B, longan pulp, raspberry pulp, malt powder,                tuckahoe powder, fermented soybean powder, white                hyacinth bean flower, and clove; (2) mixing powder                A, powder B, malt powder, tuckahoe powder, and                fermented soybean powder, placing into the                extraction kettle of the supercritical carbon                dioxide extraction apparatus, and extracting for                3-10 hours under the conditions of a pressure of                10-25 MPa and a temperature of 40-60 &#176;C to                obtain semi-finished oil A; (3) placing the longan                pulp and raspberry pulp in a distiller for                distillation treatment, collecting the pulp steam,                and utilizing supercritical carbon dioxide                extraction instrument to carry out desorption                treatment to pulp steam to obtain semi-finished oil                B; (4) mixing the semi-finished oil A and the                semi-finished oil B, and allowing to stand for                0.5-1.5 hours to separate oil and water to obtain                essential oil I and mixed water; (5) using forward                osmosis to concentrate mixed water to obtain oil                layer and concentrated solution; (6) separating oil                and water concentrated solution to obtain essential                oil two and aqueous phase; and (7) mixing the first                essential oil and the second essential oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The processing method of                  longan and raspberry comprises (i) removing the                  shell and core of longan to obtain longan fruit,                  and cleaning the surface of raspberry; and (ii)                  crushing fruit, and collecting juice and pulp.                  The processing method of malt comprises removing                  impurities, stir frying until brown, stirring for                  3-5 minutes, cooling naturally, sieving, removing                  husks, removing crumbs, crushing the malt kernels                  into powder, passing through a 120-meshes sieve,                  and collecting the malt powder. The processing                  method of the tuckahoe comprises soaking the raw                  materials for 30-60 minutes, washing the surface,                  steaming for 3-10 minutes, taking out, manually                  peeling, cutting into 0.5&#215;0.5 cm blocks,                  drying, crushing, and passing through a 120-mesh                  sieve to obtain tuckahoe powder. The processing                  method of the fermented soybean comprises (A)                  drying the raw material to a moisture content of                  30-35 wt.% at 50-60&#8451;; (B) naturally                  cooling and allowing to stand for 2-5 hours, and                  vacuum drying for 3-15 minutes under the                  conditions of 0.5-1.5 MPa and 90-120&#8451;;                  (C) returning to normal pressure, and vacuum                  drying at 55-80&#8451; and 1-5 MPa for 1-5                  hours; and (D) taking out the dried raw materials                  and grinding, passing through a 120-meshes sieve.                  The carbon dioxide flow rate of the supercritical                  carbon dioxide extractor is 300-450 l/hour. The                  working pressure of the distiller is 30-65 Mpa,                  and the distillation time is 3-20 hours. The                  working conditions of the forward osmosis                  concentration are 0-30&#8451; and pressure                  0.1-0.2 MPa.

PN CN114777118-A
TI Coal-water slurry supercritical hydrothermal            combustion equipment, has lower pressure-bearing            cylinder whose top is sealed and fixedly connected to            bottom of upper pressure-bearing cylinder, and reactant            outlet is brought into communication with reactant            inlet.
AU WU H
   WU J
   ZHANG K
AE CHENGDU K & H ENVIROTECH CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2022986647
AB    NOVELTY - The equipment has an injection burner (2)                whose outlet end extends into a supercritical                hydrothermal combustion chamber (11) through a                nozzle fixing cylinder (12), and sealed and fixed                by the nozzle fixing cylinder. The inlet end of the                injection burner located outside an upper pressure                bearing cylinder (1) comprises a coal-water slurry                feed port and a fuel inlet. The inner cavity of a                lower pressure-bearing cylinder (3) is used as a                reactant accommodating cavity. The top opening                serves as a reactant inlet (31), and bottom opening                serves as a slag discharge port (32). A reaction                gas outlet (33) and a grey water outlet (34) which                are communicated with the reactant accommodating                cavity are sequentially opened on the side wall                from top to bottom. The top of the lower                pressure-bearing cylinder is sealed and fixedly                connected to the bottom of the upper                pressure-bearing cylinder. A reactant outlet (13)                is brought into communication with the reactant                inlet.
   USE - Coal-water slurry supercritical hydrothermal                combustion equipment for clean utilization of coal                for industrial production.
   ADVANTAGE - The oxidation process of the coal-water slurry                is carried out in supercritical water, in the                combustion equipment and the combustion treatment                method and the secondary pollution and a large                amount of exhaust gas are avoided. The carbon                dioxide separated by gas and liquid is easy to                capture and the capture cost is low, which realizes                zero emission of carbon dioxide. The cooling water                circulation technology is adopted, in the                coal-water slurry supercritical hydrothermal                combustion equipment and the combustion treatment                method. The cooling water circulates on the outer                wall of the inner cylinder of the jacket through                the annular gap. The reaction temperature in the                hydrothermal combustion chamber is adjusted by                controlling the water flow rate, and effectively                avoids the problem of uneven reaction temperature                distribution and excessive local temperature, which                leads to the easy burn-through of the inner jacket                of the jacket, and improves the utilization rate of                thermal energy in the reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for supercritical hydrothermal combustion of                coal-water slurry.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross sectional view of                coal-water slurry supercritical hydrothermal                combustion equipment.

PT P
PN WO2022035964-A1
TI Steam reforming hydrocarbon feedstock, comprises e.g. passing mixture of hydrocarbon feedstock and steam through catalyst bed comprising particulate and structured nickel steam reforming catalyst.
AU HSU S
   PATAKAMURI G
AE UNIV WASHINGTON GEORGE (WAOR-C)
GA 202226596G
AB    NOVELTY - Steam reforming hydrocarbon feedstock, comprises passing mixture of hydrocarbon feedstock and steam through catalyst bed comprising particulate nickel steam reforming catalyst and structured nickel steam reforming catalyst disposed within externally heated tubes in a tubular steam reformer, where each tube has an inlet to which the mixture of hydrocarbon and steam is fed, an outlet from which a reformed gas containing hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, steam and methane is recovered, and the steam reforming catalyst at the outlet of the tubes is the structured steam reforming catalyst, the particulate steam reforming catalyst comprises 5-30 wt.% nickel, expressed as nickel(II) oxide, has a largest dimension that is at most 15% of the internal diameter of the many tubes, and the structured steam reforming catalyst comprises nickel dispersed over the surface of a porous metal oxide present as a coating on a non-porous metal or ceramic structure.
   USE - The method is useful for steam reforming hydrocarbon feedstock.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides optimal balance of hydrogen production and efficiency; and utilizes particulate catalyst to reduce carbon formation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Steam reforming a hydrocarbon feedstock, comprises passing a mixture of hydrocarbon feedstock and steam through a catalyst bed comprising a particulate nickel steam reforming catalyst and a structured nickel steam reforming catalyst disposed within a plurality of externally heated tubes in a tubular steam reformer, where each tube has an inlet to which the mixture of hydrocarbon and steam is fed, an outlet from which a reformed gas containing hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, steam and methane is recovered, and the steam reforming catalyst at the outlet of the tubes is the structured steam reforming catalyst, the particulate steam reforming catalyst comprises 5-30 wt.% nickel, expressed as nickel(II) oxide, and has a largest dimension that is at most 15% of the internal diameter of the many tubes, and the structured steam reforming catalyst comprises nickel dispersed over the surface of a porous metal oxide present as a coating on a non-porous metal or ceramic structure, the nickel content of the metal oxide coating is 5-50 wt.% and the thickness of the coating is 5-150 mu m. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of combination of non-structured, particulate steam reforming catalyst comprising 5-30 wt.% nickel, expressed as nickel(II) oxide, having a largest dimension that is at most 15% of the internal diameter of the plurality of tubes, and a structured steam reforming catalyst comprising nickel dispersed over the surface of a porous metal oxide, present as a coating on a non-porous metal or ceramic structure, where the nickel content of the metal oxide coating is 5-50 wt.% and the coating thickness is 5-150 mu m for suppression of carbon formation on the catalyst during catalytic steam reforming of a hydrocarbon feedstock.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The hydrocarbon feedstock comprises methane and is preferably pre-reformed gas, associated gas or natural gas. The tubular steam reformer contains many tubes through which the mixture of the hydrocarbon feedstock and steam is passed, and to which heat is transferred by means of a hot gas comprising a combustion gas or a synthesis gas, flowing around the tubes. The structured steam reforming catalyst comprises metal or ceramic structures having many passages through which a process fluid may pass in ordered, non-random directions. The structured steam reforming catalyst comprises cylindrical units with a diameter complimentary to the tubes in which they are placed, comprising many passages through which a process fluid may pass in ordered, non-random directions. Preferred Components: The particulate steam reforming catalyst comprises alkali metal oxide, preferably potassium oxide, in content of 0.5-7 wt.% on the particulate catalyst. The largest dimension of the particulate catalyst is 3-15% of the internal diameter of many tubes in which it is placed. The particulate steam reforming catalyst is a pelleted steam reforming catalyst. The particulate steam reforming catalyst is cylindrical with a diameter of 3-10 mm or 5-25 mm and a length of 2-15 mm or 5-25 mm and an aspect ratio of 1:0.5-2:1. The particulate steam reforming catalyst comprises 3-10 through holes and optionally 3 to 12 flutes or lobes around the periphery of the pellet. The particulate catalyst is cylindrical with an aspect ratio of 0.75-1 and the ratio of the ratio of the internal diameter of many tubes to the diameter of the catalyst particle (Dt/dp) of 5:1-50:1, preferably 8:1-50:1. The particulate catalyst has an equivalent spherical diameter, expressed as a sphere of the same specific surface area, in the range of 3 to 5 mm. The particulate catalyst has an external geometric surface area of 200-2000 m2/m3, preferably 500-2000 m2/m3. The catalyst bed consists of two, three or more layers of steam reforming catalyst where in each case the layer of steam reforming catalyst adjacent the outlets of the tubes is the structured catalyst. The catalyst tubes consist of two layers of nickel steam reforming catalyst where the catalyst layer adjacent the outlets of the tubes is the structured nickel steam reforming catalyst and the catalyst layer adjacent the inlets of the tubes is a particulate nickel steam reforming catalyst. The catalyst tubes consist of three layers of nickel steam reforming catalyst, a structured catalyst layer at the inlets of the tubes, a particulate catalyst layer at a middle or second region of the tubes, and a structured catalyst layer at the outlets of the tubes. The particulate catalyst is contained within a gas permeable container or containers located between the structured catalyst layers. There are at least two layers of steam reforming catalyst within the tubes and the structured catalyst layer comprises 5-95% of the volume of the bed, preferably 25-75% of the volume of the bed.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of steam reformer modelling software was used to examine the difference between various catalyst configurations in order to quantify benefits of using commercially available pelleted steam reforming catalyst over CATACEL SSR (RTM: Reforming catalyst) structured steam reforming catalysts in a steam reformer comprising a plurality of tubes each having an internal diameter of 127 mm that do not support the claims.

PT P
PN CN113830776-A
TI Recycling polymetallic crystal co-produced water            glass from copper-nickel sulfide ore tailings, by            mixing active clinker prepared using tailings and            additive with water, filtering, carbonizing,            decomposing, separating to obtain silica gel, mixing            with sodium hydroxide, filtering, and            crystallizing.
AU MA R
   SUN Y
   MA Y
   PAN A
AE PAN A (PANA-Individual)
   BEIJING RUNJIE HAODA TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202208640D
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling polymetallic crystal co-produced                water glass from copper-nickel sulfide ore tailings                involves: (S1) mixing ore tailings and additive,                grinding, calcining to obtain an active clinker,                and collecting furnace gas during calcining; (S2)                mixing active clinker with water, stirring,                leaching, and filtering to obtain water leaching                residue and leaching solution; (S3) introducing                leaching solution into carbon dioxide for                carbonizing and decomposing, separating to obtain                silica gel and a filtrate, and concentrating the                filtrate; (S4) oxidizing collected furnace gas,                dissolving in water to obtain sulfuric acid                solution, mixing with the leaching residue,                stirring and filtering to obtain a filtrate (A);                (S5) heating and aging filtrate (A), and separating                to obtain silica gel and a filtrate (B); (S6)                combining the silica gels, mixing with sodium                hydroxide solution, and filtering to obtain water                glass; and (S7) evaporating, concentrating and                crystallizing the filtrate (B).
   USE - Recycling polymetallic crystal co-produced                water glass from copper-nickel sulfide ore                tailings.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures efficient resource                utilization of copper-nickel sulfide ore tailings,                and makes full use of sulfur dioxide generated by                calcining tailings to prepare sulfuric acid to                reduce acid leaching reagent cost and avoid the                discharge pollution problem of sulfur.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recycling polymetallic crystal co-produced                water glass from copper-nickel sulfide ore tailings                involves: (S1) mixing copper-nickel sulfide ore                tailings and an additive according to the mass                ratio of 1:(0.5-2), grinding to 200-300 mesh,                aerobically calcining to obtain an active clinker,                and collecting furnace gas produced by calcining;                (S2) mixing the active clinker with water,                stirring, leaching, filtering to obtain a water                leaching residue and a water leaching solution                containing sodium silicate; (S3) introducing the                water leaching solution into carbon dioxide for                carbonizing and decomposing,                filtering/centrifuging, solid-liquid separating to                obtain silica gel and sodium carbonate filtrate,                concentrating the sodium carbonate filtrate and                then applying to the step (S1) as additive for                batching; (S4) fully oxidizing the collected                furnace gas after dust removing and purifying,                collecting the oxidized product, dissolving in                water to obtain a sulfuric acid solution, adjusting                the concentration of the sulfuric acid solution to                1-6 mol/L, mixing with the water leaching residue,                stirring, dissolving, and filtering to obtain an                acid leaching residue and a filtrate (A); (S5)                heating and aging the filtrate (A), and                solid-liquid separating to obtain silica gel and a                filtrate (B), which is a desiliconized metal salt                solution; (S6) combining the silica gel obtained in                steps (S3) and (S5), mixing with sodium hydroxide                solution, stirring under heating conditions, and                filtering to obtain water glass; and (S7)                evaporating, concentrating and crystallizing the                filtrate (B) to obtain product. The additive is                sodium hydroxide or sodium carbonate. The filtrate                (A) contains metal salts soluble in sulfuric acid                solution, and silicates that are insoluble in                alkaline solution but soluble in sulfuric acid                solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The solid-to-liquid                  mass ratio of active clinker and water in step                  (S2) is 1:(5-15). The solid-to-liquid mass ratio                  of the sulfuric acid solution and the water                  leaching residue in step (S4) is 1:(5-10). The                  concentration of the sodium hydroxide solution in                  step (S6) is 10-40 %mass. The solid-to-liquid                  mass ratio of silica gel and the sodium hydroxide                  solution in step (S6) is 1:(1-5).

PN CN113818842-A
TI High-efficient exploitation of shale gas, low temperature hydrogen production, waste gas utilization integrated method in oil and gas field development field., involves selecting shale gas well, extracting shale gas of the shale reservoir to the ground.
AU JIN Y
   CHEN C
   YANG H
   XU Y
   MENG Z
   LI X
   TAN X
   LI Y
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 2022067938
AB    NOVELTY - High-efficient exploitation of shale gas, low temperature hydrogen production, waste gas utilization integrated method involves selecting shale gas well, where the shale gas of the shale reservoir is extracted to the ground by hydraulic fracturing technology, separated the shale gas well product by a gas-liquid separator at the wellhead, the methane steam reforming hydrogen production device is connected behind the gas-liquid separator, the shale gas main component is methane, through methane reforming hydrogen production reaction formula, and the needed reaction temperature is 200-500degrees Celsius, determined the shale gas amount as hydrogen raw material, water amount and providing heat source of the shale gas amount according to the raw material gas shale gas and water ratio of 1:2-1:2.5 to the methane steam reforming reaction container conveying shale gas and yielded water, introduced oxygen.
   USE - High-efficient exploitation of shale gas, low temperature hydrogen production, waste gas utilization integrated method used in oil and gas field development field and for preparing ammonia, preparing methanol, metallurgy, fuel cell and rocket propellant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves recovery rate and utilization rate of shale gas, and realizes energy saving and emission reduction, reducing development cost and improving economic benefit. The method utilizes the shale gas well product at the wellhead, reducing the processing transportation cost, effectively increasing the economic benefit, and using low temperature hydrogen-waste gas using integrated shale gas low temperature direct conversion utilization technology.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - High-efficient exploitation of shale gas, low temperature hydrogen production, waste gas utilization integrated method involves selecting shale gas well, where the shale gas of the shale reservoir is extracted to the ground by hydraulic fracturing technology, separated the shale gas well product by a gas-liquid separator at the wellhead, the methane steam reforming hydrogen production device is connected behind the gas-liquid separator, the shale gas main component is methane, through methane reforming hydrogen production reaction formula, and the needed reaction temperature is 200-500degrees Celsius, determined the shale gas amount as hydrogen raw material, water amount and providing heat source of the shale gas amount according to the raw material gas shale gas and water ratio of 1:2-1:2.5 to the methane steam reforming reaction container conveying shale gas and yielded water, introduced oxygen, adding catalyst ruthenium/carbon, ignited the fuel gas, performed hydrogen production reaction, fully separated and purified the hydrogen production carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen, the un-reacted carbon monoxide gas is recycled to the methane steam reforming reaction container to continue to participate in the reaction, the purified hydrogen is stored by a gas storage tank, the exhaust gas carbon dioxide back to the shale reservoir, supplemental formation energy, displaced the replacement shale gas, or directly buried in the shale reservoir.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The shale gas is efficiently produced by pumping the water body which exceeds shale gas reservoir bearing capacity into the constructed shale gas well after drilling well engineering, the shale gas reservoir is cracked to generate crack, communication shale gas flow channel in the well, the shale gas is collected. The shale gas well product is separated by gas-liquid separator, the shale gas in the product is used as heat source and raw material gas of methane reforming hydrogen production; the gas field produced water and fracturing fluid return drainage water are used as raw material water of methane reforming hydrogen production, the redundant water is continuously configured fracturing fluid. The method involves reacting methane with water to generate synthesis gas containing hydrogen, carbon monoxide and water vapor, then under the action of water vapor, the carbon monoxide in the synthesis gas is converted into carbon dioxide, the final methane is converted into synthesis gas mainly containing carbon dioxide and hydrogen through chemical reaction balance and reaction temperature is 200-500degrees Celsius, the pressure is 1.5-3.0 megaPascal, water-carbon ratio is 3-3.5, determining the ratio of shale gas to water is 1:2-1:2.5. The method involves adopting ruthenium/carbon catalyst to realize methane hydrogen production process under the condition of 200-500degrees Celsius.

PT P
PN WO2021224317-A1
TI Sheet-like element for used in sheet-like element supply device for food packaging, has coating layer comprising particulate filler having surface-reacted calcium carbonate, dispersant and polymeric binder, and substrate layer.
AU MONNARD F W
   WALLER S
   WEIHS J P
   RUEEGG N
   FALLER A V
   BECK B M
   YILDIRIM S
   MENARD R
AE OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
GA 2021C58096
AB    NOVELTY - Sheet-like element comprises: (a) a coating layer comprising (a.1) 100 pts. wt. a particulate filler comprising at least 50 wt.% surface-reacted calcium carbonate, where the surface-reacted calcium carbonate is a reaction product of natural ground calcium carbonate or precipitated calcium carbonate with carbon dioxide and at least one hydronium ions (H3Q+) donors, and the carbon dioxide is formed in situ by the H3q+ ion donors treatment and/or is supplied from an external source, and the surface-reacted calcium carbonate has a specific surface area of 20-200 m2/g, as measured by the Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) method, (a.2) 0.1-10 pts. wt. a dispersant and (a.3) 5-30 pts. wt. a polymeric binder; and (b) a substrate layer, where the coating layer has a total intruded specific pore volume of 0.25-2 cm3/g, as measured by mercury intrusion porosimetry.
   USE - The element is useful in: a sheet-like element supply device; and for achieving an antimicrobial and/or antifungal and/or anti-mold and/or antifouling effect in food packaging (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The element: provides a food packaging allowing for an efficient protection of the food from microbial contamination by antimicrobial active agents; absorbs suitably high amounts of antimicrobial active compositions; can be easily dispensed and provided at the point of use; and prevents or retards the growth of pathogenic microorganisms in the food packaging.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a coating layer loaded with the composition;
   (2) manufacturing the sheet-like element comprising (a) providing the particulate filler, (b) providing the dispersant, (c) providing the polymeric binder, (d) providing the substrate layer comprising at least one individual substrate layer, (e) mixing 100 pts. wt. the particulate filler of step (a), 0.1-10 pts. wt. the dispersant of step (b) and 5-30 pts. wt. the polymeric binder of step (c) to obtain a coating composition, (f) applying the composition of step (e) onto the substrate layer of step (d) to form a composite, (g) drying the composite obtained in step (f) to obtain a sheet-like element, (h) optionally adding the composition comprising at least one antimicrobial active ingredient, preferably having a vapor pressure of 1-500 Pa at 25 degrees C, during any at least one of steps (a)-(g) or after step (g) 1-50 wt.%; and
   (3) a sheet-like element supply device comprising the sheet-like element, where the supply device preferably comprises a roll or a magazine comprising the sheet-like elements; and
   (4) a food packaging comprising the sheet-like element, where the sheet-like element is located within the food packaging, or the coating layer and the coating layer is present within the food packaging.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The coating layer has a total intruded specific pore volume of 0.4-1.5 cm3/g, preferably 0.5-1 cm3/g, as measured by mercury intrusion porosimetry, and/or has a total intra particle intruded specific pore volume of 0.05-1 cm3/g, preferably 0.1-0.4 cm3/g, as measured by mercury intrusion porosimetry, and/or has a total inter particle intruded specific pore volume of 0.05-0.5 cm3/g, preferably 0.1-0.3 cm3/g, as measured by mercury intrusion porosimetry, and/or has a total occlusion intruded specific pore volume of 0.05-0.4 cm3/g, preferably 0.1-0.2 cm3/g, as measured by mercury intrusion porosimetry, and/or has a fluid receptivity of 1-50 wt.%, preferably 10-45 wt.%, based on the total weight of the coating layer, and/or is present on the substrate layer 1-70 g/m2, preferably 2-50 g/m2. The particulate filler comprises the surface-reacted calcium carbonate comprising at least 70 wt.%, preferably at least 90 wt.%, based on the total weight of the at least one particulate filler, and most preferably the particulate filler consists of the surface-reacted calcium carbonate, and any optionally present further particulate filler material comprising dolomite, ground calcium carbonate, precipitated calcium carbonate, magnesium hydroxide, talc, gypsum, titanium dioxide, kaolin, silicate, mica, barium sulfate, calcined clay, non-calcined (hydrous) clay and/r bentonite, and preferably comprising calcium carbonate and/or precipitated calcium carbonate, the particulate filler consists of the optionally present further particulate filler material and the surface-reacted calcium carbonate. The surface-reacted calcium carbonate has a specific surface area of 50-120 m2/g, as measured by the BET method, and/or has a total intra particle intruded specific pore volume of 0.1-2.5 cm3/g, as measured by mercury intrusion porosimetry. The dispersant comprises polyacrylic acid having a molecular weight of 1000-15000 g/mol or it salts or derivatives, starch and/or carboxymethyl cellulose, preferably the dispersant is a polyacrylic acid being partially or fully neutralized by alkali metal ions, preferably lithium, sodium and/or potassium, and having a molecular weight of 1500-6000 g/mol, preferably the dispersant is a polyacrylic acid being partially or fully neutralized by sodium ions and having a molecular weight of 1500-6000 g/mol. The polymeric binder comprises polyacrylic acid or it salts or derivatives, starch, proteins, styrene butadiene latices, polyvinyl alcohol and/or polyvinyl acetate. The substrate layer comprises at least one individual substrate layer comprising polymer material layers, preferably made from polyethylene, polypropylene, polyethylene terephthalate, polylactic acid, poly hydroxy butyrate, polyethylene-2,5-furandicarboxylate and/or polystyrene, fibrous material layers, preferably made from cellulose acetate, viscose, polypropylene, polyethylene terephthalate and/or polylactic acid, paper layers, cardboard layers, textile layers, nonwoven layers, layers made from bio-based materials, wood layers, bamboo layers, metal foil layers, aluminum layers and/or print receptive coating layers, where the individual substrate layers optionally have been subjected to a corona treatment, and where preferably the individual substrate layers comprising polymer material layers. The element further comprises at least one adhesive layer, being located on the substrate layer on the opposite side of the coating layer and/or between the individual substrate layers, where the adhesive layer preferably is comprising adhesives, sealants, rubber coatings and/or pressure-sensitive layers; and/or at least one primer layer, being located between the substrate layer and the coating layer, and/or at least one breathable covering layer to permanently cover the coating layer, preferably comprising breathable film layer, fibrous material layer and nonwoven fabric layers and/or at least one protective layer to temporarily seal the coating layer, and/or the adhesive layer, preferably polyethylene, polypropylene and/or coated paper. The sheet-like element further comprises an antimicrobial active composition comprising at least one antimicrobial active ingredient preferably having a vapor pressure of 1-500 Pa at 25 degrees C, preferably 25-300 Pa at 25 degrees C, where the sheet-like element comprises 1-50 wt.% the composition. The composition comprises at least one essential oil comprising cinnamon essential oil, thyme essential oil, clove essential oil, rosemary essential oil, oregano essential oil, orange essential oil, carrot seed essential oil, ginger essential oil, lemongrass essential oil, bay leaf essential oil, marjoram essential oil and/ mustard essential oil and/or further comprises at least one viscosity modifier, preferably comprising guar gum, starch, cellulose, carboxymethyl cellulose, locust bean gum, xanthan gum, pectin, carrageenan and/or agar or it salts or derivatives. Preferred Method: The mixing step (e) is performed in the presence of a solvent, preferably water, and/or application step (f) is performed by means of roller coating, dip coating, grooved rod coating, curtain coating, stiff blade coating, applicator roll coating, fountain coating, jet coating, short dwell coating, slotted die coating, bent blade coating, bevel blade coating, air knife coating, bar coating, gravure coating, conventional or metering size press coating, spray application techniques, and/or wet stack coating, preferably roller coating, and/or drying step (g) is performed at a temperature of 50-150 degrees C at ambient pressure, or at reduced pressure, preferably by hot air drying, infrared radiation drying or UV radiation drying, and/or addition step (h) is performed by inkjet printing, spraying, coating, vapor deposition and/or dripping, on at least a part of the surface of the coating layer.

PT P
PN CN113493528-A
TI Preparing chitosan by fungal fermentation coupling of oxidized squid fan-shaped bone by preparing filamentous fungi using fermentation bacterial solution for plate culture, picking single colonies, culturing, performing squid fan-shaped bone fermentation, obtaining crude chitosan and purifying.
AU BI J
   WU W
   YANG J
AE RONGCHENG WANYING AQUATIC TECHNOLOGY CO (RONG-Non-standard)
GA 2021C0320W
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing chitosan by fungal fermentation coupling of oxidized squid fan-shaped bone involves (1) preparing filamentous fungi using a fermentation bacterial solution for plate culture, picking single colonies, purifying using a slant scribing method, preparing a liquid culture medium, performing secondary strain culturing and oscillation culturing at 25-40degrees Celsius and 150-180 rpm for 1-2 days, centrifuging and precipitating, washing with purified water, freeze-drying, and superfine grinding, (2) performing squid fan-shaped bone fermentation by (a) taking the squid fan-shaped bone, washing with clear water to remove impurities and crushing using a crusher; and (b) adding the squid fan-shaped bone meal obtained in step (a), adding purified water, and culturing and obtaining crude chitosan and (4) purifying the crude chitosan in vacuum, putting the dried crude chitosan into a supercritical carbon dioxide extractor for treatment.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing chitosan by fungal fermentation coupling of oxidized squid fan-shaped bone.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problems that an existing chitosan chemical method that is easy to pollute the environment, long in enzymatic reaction time and poor in physical method yield, obtains the product with excellent water solubility and biocompatibility, adopts fermentation and oxidative degradation coupling technologies, simple and efficient process flow, mild reaction conditions and easy control, realizes the clean preparation of chitosan and is beneficial to large-scale popularization and production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing chitosan by fungal fermentation coupling of oxidized squid fan-shaped bone involves (1) preparing filamentous fungi using a fermentation bacterial solution for plate culture, picking single colonies, purifying using a slant scribing method, preparing a liquid culture medium, performing secondary strain culturing and oscillation culturing at 25-40degrees Celsius and 150-180 rpm for 1-2 days, centrifuging and precipitating, washing with purified water, freeze-drying, and superfine grinding, (2) performing squid fan-shaped bone fermentation by (a) taking the squid fan-shaped bone, washing with clear water to remove impurities and crushing using a crusher; and (b) adding the squid fan-shaped bone meal obtained in step (a), adding purified water, and carrying out shaking constant-temperature culture for 2 -3 days at 30-37degrees Celsius, pH of 6-7 and 120-150 rpm, completing the fermentation, heating at 90-95degrees Celsius and maintaining the constant temperature for 20-30 minutes and centrifuging to obtain a supernatant, (3) adding hydrogen peroxide to the supernatant obtained in step (b), heating at 35-40degrees Celsius, maintaining the constant temperature for 6-8 hours, centrifuging to obtain a supernatant, adding ethanol, centrifuging and precipitating, washing with purified water to obtain crude chitosan and (4) purifying the crude chitosan in vacuum, putting the dried crude chitosan into a supercritical carbon dioxide extractor for treatment for 2 -5 hours and performing extraction to obtain a residue, that is, chitosan.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (1) involves adopting the culture medium is lysogeny broth culture medium. The step (2) involves adopting the squid fan-shaped bone meal to the bacterial powder in a weight ratio of (15):1, where the added purified water is 10-15 times the total weight of the squid fan-shaped bone meal and the bacterial powder. The step (3) involves adopting hydrogen peroxide in a concentration of 30-35%, where the amount of hydrogen peroxide added is 25-30% based on the weight of the supernatant obtained in step (b). The step (4) involves adopting processing conditions of the supercritical carbon dioxide such as pressure of 15-20MPa, temperature of 20-30degrees Celsius, carbon dioxide flow rate of 10-15 kg/hour and time of 10-20 minutes. The step (b) involves adding 35% mineral dissolving bacteria before fermentation, where the mineral dissolving bacteria are composed of two kinds of bacteria among Lactobacillus, Diplococcus, Thiobacillus ferrooxidans, Bacillus polymyxa, and Pseudomonas aerobic, with a mass ratio of 1:1.Preferred Components: The filamentous fungi is one or two of Aspergillus niger, Mucor, Rhizopus oryzae and Absidia coerulea, where the particle size of the bacterial powder obtained by ultrafine pulverization is 200-500nm.
DC A11 (Polysaccharides; natural rubber; other natural polymers (only a restricted range of (modified) natural polymers are included. Thus starch would be excluded, but chemically modified starch included).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN KR2021114583-A; KR2364033-B1
TI Producing natural red pigment derived from safflower useful for cotton fabric or silk fabric dyed comprises e.g. extracting red pigment from harvested safflower petals, drying in shade with good air circulation, and soaking in water.
AU KIM D W
   PARK Y K
   PARK J H
AE KOE SAN COUNTY (KOES-Non-standard)
GA 2021B3666Q
AB    NOVELTY - Producing natural red pigment derived from safflower comprises e.g. extracting red pigment from safflower petals harvested from fully mature organically grown safflowers and drying in shade with good air circulation so that the moisture content of the safflower petals is 15-25%, soaking dried safflower in water at room temperature for a week, squeezing safflower in clear running water until safflower red pigment does not come out, washing with hot water to remove the yellow pigment, suspending safflower petals from which the yellow pigment is prepared, removing water, using an ultrasonic extractor, adding plant cell decomposition complex enzyme and mixing, hydrolysing, heat treating to inactivate the plant cell decomposition complex enzyme and pulverizing safflower treated with decomposing enzymes, using supercritical carbon dioxide as an extraction solvent and extracting to complete the safflower supercritical extract.
   USE - The natural red pigment derived from safflower is useful for cotton fabric or silk fabric dyed (claimed) and in organic processed foods and organic cosmetics.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally friendly, promotes national health by preventing atopy and various skin troubles that can occur with dyed textiles or cosmetics, and can increase the income of farmers by cultivating safflower as a specialized crop on idle farmland.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing natural red pigment derived from safflower comprises (i) extracting red pigment from safflower petals harvested from fully mature organically grown safflowers and drying for 72 hours in shade with good air circulation so that the moisture content of the safflower petals is 15-25%; (ii) soaking dried safflower in water at room temperature for a week for 48 hours, squeezing the safflower in clear running water 5 times a day or more until safflower red pigment does not come out and washing with hot water at 50 degrees C to remove the yellow pigment; (iii) suspending safflower petals from which the yellow pigment prepared in the above step, removing water, using an ultrasonic extractor, generating 20 kHz ultrasound at 20-35 degrees C for 10-20 minutes under the condition of 750 watt, 20 amplitude; (iv) adding 0.5-10 unit wt.% plant cell decomposition complex enzyme and mixing in the same ratio (w/w/w) to 100 wt.% safflower based on the weight of the sonicated safflower treated in the above step, hydrolysing at 35-45 degrees C with pH 4.5-6 for 24 hours, heat treating at 90-120 degrees C for 5-15 minutes to inactivate the plant cell decomposition complex enzyme; and (v) pulverizing the safflower treated with decomposing enzymes, using 250 bar of supercritical carbon dioxide as an extraction solvent at a flow rate of 5-20 ml/minutes at 30-40 degrees C and extracting for 1-2 hours to complete the safflower supercritical extract.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The complex enzyme is cellulase, xylanase, pectinase each 1:1:1 (w/w/w) or 2:1:1 (w/w/w).
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of extraction of organic red pigment that do not support the claims.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PN CN112972764-A; CN112972764-B
TI Preparing polyether-ether-ketone bone repair            material with multi-scale pores, by hot pressing            polyether ether ketone pellet, mixing concentrated            sulfuric acid with methane sulfonic acid, performing            sulfonation treatment, and vacuum drying.
AU ZHANG S
   MEI X
   WU T
   LIANG L
   PENG X
   WANG G
AE UNIV JILIN (UYJI-C)
GA 202172269B
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing polyether-ether-ketone                bone repair material with multi-scale pores,                involves (a) hot pressing polyether ether ketone                pellet, (b) sequentially performing ultrasonic                cleaning process on polyether ether ketone plate in                distilled water, ethanol and acetone for 2-4 times,                and vacuum drying, (c) placing obtained polyether                ether ketone plate in supercritical carbon dioxide                foaming device, performing foaming and pressure                relief process, (d) uniformly mixing concentrated                sulfuric acid with methane sulfonic acid, soaking                partially foamed polyether ether ketone plate                attached to polyether ether ketone plate in mixed                acid solution, vigorously stirring for performing                sulfonation treatment, and adding to distilled                water to terminate sulfonation reaction, performing                sulfonation treatment on partially foamed polyether                ether ketone plate, and (e) thermally treating                sulfonated polyether-ether-ketone foam, and vacuum                drying.
   USE - The method is used for preparing                polyether-ether-ketone bone repair material with                multi-scale pores (claimed) used in biomedicine                application.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not have problem of incomplete                removal of pore-forming agent, which ensures                possibility of material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing polyether-ether-ketone                bone repair material with multi-scale pores,                involves (a) hot pressing polyether ether ketone                pellet having melt index of 16-25 g/10 minutes at                370-390 degrees C under pressure of 2-5 MPa to                1.5-3 mm thick polyether ether ketone plate, (b)                sequentially performing ultrasonic cleaning process                on polyether ether ketone plate in distilled water,                ethanol and acetone for 2-4 times, performing each                process for 5-15 minutes, and vacuum drying at                70-90 degrees C, (c) placing obtained polyether                ether ketone plate in supercritical carbon dioxide                foaming device, performing foaming process at                330-340 degrees C for 20-30 minutes under pressure                of 7.5-10 MPa, and performing pressure relief for                20-30 seconds to obtain partially foamed polyether                ether ketone plate, (d) uniformly mixing 98 %mass                concentrated sulfuric acid with methane sulfonic                acid in a mass ratio of 1:(8-12) to form mixed acid                solution, soaking partially foamed polyether ether                ketone plate attached to polyether ether ketone                plate in mixed acid solution, vigorously stirring                for 3-5 minutes for performing sulfonation                treatment, and adding to distilled water to                terminate sulfonation reaction, performing                sulfonation treatment on partially foamed polyether                ether ketone plate, taking out, ultrasonically                cleaning distilled water and acetone for 2-4 times,                performing each time for 5-15 minutes, removing                concentrated sulfuric acid and methane sulfonic                acid remaining on surface, and drying under vacuum                at 70-90 degrees C, and (e) thermally treating                sulfonated polyether-ether-ketone foam at 110-130                degrees C for 4-6 hours, and vacuum drying at                90-110 degrees C to obtain polyether-ether-ketone                bone repair material with multi-scale hole.

PT P
PN CN112357962-A; CN112357962-B
TI Supercritical assisted coating of manganese oxide on lithium-rich manganese surface comprises e.g. preparing lithium-manganese-cobalt-nickel-oxygen complex i.e. LMCN, mixing and ball milling and calcining.
AU HAN J
   JI X
   CHEN Y
   XU M
AE WANXIANG 123 STOCK CO (WXJT-C)
   WANXIANG GROUP CORP (WXJT-C)
   WANXIANG A123 SYSTEMS CORP (WXJT-C)
GA 2021182310
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical assisted coating of manganese oxide on lithium-rich manganese surface comprises (i) dissolving manganese(II) acetate tetrahydrate, cobalt(II) acetate tetrahydrate and nickel(II) acetate tetrahydrate in distilled water to obtain metal salt solution A; adding aqueous ammonia into sodium hydroxide solution to obtain solution B; pouring distilled water into co-precipitation reactor, introducing argon gas, simultaneously injecting solutions A and B into the reactor, filtering, washing, drying to obtain precursor, mixing the precursor with lithium carbonate by ball milling, calcining, cooling to obtain lithium-manganese-cobalt-nickel-oxygen complex i.e. LMCN, (ii) mixing LMCN with ethanol, stirring, adding manganese(II) acetate tetrahydrate and lithium acetate dihydrate into ethanol, stirring, vacuumizing, filling with carbon dioxide, allowing to stand, ball milling and (iii) filtering the product, drying, calcining to obtain the product manganese(IV) oxide-LMCN.
   USE - The method is useful for supercritical assisted coating of manganese oxide on lithium-rich manganese surface.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: adopts the product prepared by the supercritical method to have uniform particle size, excellent dispersibility and high purity, and because the supercritical fluid has excellent fluidity, the coating agent is dissolved in the entrainer ethanol to react and uniformly coat the material surface; adopts manganese oxide coating, which can reduce the thermodynamic equilibrium potential of the cathode material and provide lithium vacancies during the first discharge; produces manganese(IV) oxide-LMCN, which shows excellent cycle stability and rate performance and the capacity retention rate reached 73.5% after 100 cycles of charge and discharge at a current density of 30 mA/g.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Supercritical assisted coating of manganese oxide on lithium-rich manganese surface comprises (i) dissolving manganese(II) acetate tetrahydrate, cobalt(II) acetate tetrahydrate and nickel(II) acetate tetrahydrate in distilled water, configuring it as metal salt solution A, adding aqueous ammonia into the sodium hydroxide solution to make solution B, pouring distilled water into the co-precipitation reactor, adding aqueous ammonia to adjust the pH to 11-11.4, introducing the argon gas, simultaneously injecting solutions A and B into the reactor using a peristaltic pump, filtering after completing reaction, washing and drying to obtain precursor, mixing the precursor with lithium carbonate by ball milling and calcining, cooling to obtain the product lithium-manganese-cobalt-nickel-oxygen complex ( Li(Li0.2Mn0.54Co0.13Ni0.13)O2)), which is marked as LMCN, (ii) mixing LMCN with ethanol, uniformly stirring, then adding manganese(II) acetate tetrahydrate and lithium acetate dihydrate into the ethanol, stirring thoroughly until the solid and liquid uniformly mix, moving the liquid to the ball milling tank for vacuumizing, then filling with carbon dioxide to 8-10 MPa, allowing to stand for 40-60 minutes and placing it on a ball mill for ball milling and (iii) filtering the product obtained by ball milling, drying, calcining in an argon atmosphere to obtain the product manganese(IV) oxide-LMCN.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i): the ratio between manganese(II) acetate tetrahydrate, cobalt(II) acetate tetrahydrate, nickel(II) acetate tetrahydrate and the distilled water injected into the reactor is 4: 0.8-1.2:0.8-1.2:0.8-1.2 (mol:mol:mol:litre); the concentration of the salt solution A is 1.8-2.2 mol/litre; the concentration of sodium hydroxide solution is 4.8-5.2 mol/litre; the volume ratio of sodium hydroxide solution and aqueous ammonia is 1:1-1.5; and the volume ratio of metal salt solution A and solution B is 1:1-1.2. In step (i): the molar ratio of the precursor to lithium carbonate is 2:1.5-1.8. In step (ii): the mixing ratio of LMCN, ethanol, manganese(II) acetate tetrahydrate and lithium acetate dihydrate is 0.8-1.2:28-32: 0.0348-0.1816:0.0145-0.0756 (g:ml:g:g). Preferred Method: In step (i): the reaction process in the reactor comprises the rotating speed of the stirring blade is 230-250 revolutions/minute, carrying out the whole reaction under an argon protective atmosphere; the reaction time is 22-24 hours. In step (i): the ball milling process is ball milling at 500-550 revolutions/minute for 12-14 hours; and the calcination process is at 480-520 degrees C for 4-6 hours after pre-sintering, heating to 880-920 degrees C for 11-13 hours. In step (ii): the stirring time after mixing LMCN and ethanol is 30-40 minutes. In step (ii): during the ball milling process heating to 30-35 degrees C and ball milling at 350-400 revolutions/minute for 12-14 hours. In step (ii): no ball is added during the ball milling. In step (iii): calcining at 500-600 degrees C for 4-6 hours.

PN CN112322286-A
TI Nano-calcium carbonate luminescent material with            different morphology doped with rare earth ions in            optical application is prepared by using natural            limestone without modification treatment as calcium            source and introducing rare earth ions.
AU CHEN F
   ZHANG Q
   ZHANG H
   ZHANG Y
   KUANG Z
AE UNIV HUBEI POLYTECHNIC (UHBP-C)
   HUANGSHI MINERAL PROCESSING RES INST (HUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202116853V
AB    NOVELTY - Nano-calcium carbonate luminescent material                with different morphologies doped with rare earth                ions is prepared by using natural limestone without                any modification treatment as calcium source, and                introducing rare earth ions into the crystal                lattice of synthesized nano-calcium carbonate                during carbonization reaction process, where the                mass ratio of the rare earth ion and the calcium                (2+) ion substance in the luminescent material is                (0.001-0.06):1, the natural limestone comprises                55.10 %mass calcium oxide, 0.07 %mass silicon                dioxide, 0.02 %mass aluminum oxide, 0.03 %mass                ferric oxide, 0.08 %mass magnesium oxide and                remaining of ignition loss, the rare earth ion must                contain terbium (3+) ion or europium (3+) ion, and                the rare earth ions are provided by a rare earth                ion solution formed by dissolving rare earth                nitrate, rare earth oxide, rare earth hydroxide                and/or rare earth carbonate in water.
   USE - The nano-calcium carbonate luminescent                material with different morphologies doped with                rare earth ions (claimed) is used in optical                application with near-UV LED chips to prepare high                luminous performance white light LEDs.
   ADVANTAGE - The nano-calcium carbonate luminescent                material with different morphologies doped with                rare earth ions reduces costs, and has excellent                luminescence characteristics and stable light                conversion material. The method involves simple                process and low energy consumption, and is suitable                for industrialized production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing the rare earth ion-doped nano calcium                carbonate luminescent materials with different                morphologies, which involves (i) performing                decomposition of natural limestone by washing raw                limestone ore, crushing, passing through a 40 mesh                sieve, and calcining in a muffle furnace at                950-1100 degrees C for 2-4 hours to obtain calcium                oxide powder, (ii) preparing calcium hydroxide                emulsion by taking the calcium oxide powder and                adding to deionized water at 80-90 degrees C,                stirring at constant temperature for 10-30 minutes,                after the reaction is completed, diluting with                deionized water to obtain a calcium hydroxide                emulsion with 5-10 %mass, and aging in isolation                for 12-24 hours, (iii) performing carbonization                reaction by adding ammonium citrate and rare earth                ion solution for morphology control to the calcium                hydroxide emulsion, where the amount of the                ammonium citrate is 2-6 %mass of the amount of                calcium hydroxide, placing the emulsion in a water                bath at 25-35 degrees C and stirring at a speed of                700-3000 rpm, introducing carbon dioxide gas at a                flow rate of 40-60 ml/minute to monitor the pH                value of the calcium hydroxide emulsion, and when                the pH is 7, stopping the introduction of carbon                dioxide to obtain a mixture of nano-calcium                carbonate, and (iv) drying the nano calcium                carbonate powder, filtering the mixture by suction,                washing and drying at 60-80 degrees C for 2-6 hours                to obtain the nano-calcium carbonate powder with                different morphology.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The rare earth nitrate                  is europium (III) nitrate hexahydrate or terbium                  (III) nitrate hexahydrate. The rare earth oxide                  is europium (III) oxide or terbium(III,IV) oxide.                  The rare earth carbonate is europium (III)                  carbonate or terbium (III) carbonate. Preferred                  Conditions: The calcination of the limestone is                  carried out at 1000-1100 degrees C for 2-4 hours.                  Preferred Properties: The luminescent material is                  fibrous, elliptical or spindle-shaped with an                  average particle size of 100-600 nm, an aspect                  ratio of 1-6, and has luminescence performance,                  which can be absorbed by near UV light at 330 nm,                  343 nm, 380 nm and 397 nm, where the emission                  spectrum comprises multiple sets of peaks between                  450 and 650 nm, the terbium (3+) ion doped                  luminescent materials have the highest luminous                  intensity at 545 nm, and the europium (3+) ion                  doped luminescent materials have the highest                  luminous intensity at 578 nm.

PN CN111551689-A
TI Uniform sampling inspection for production of            high-alumina powder by mixing high-alumina fly ash with            sodium hydroxide, heating, desiliconizing, roasting,            carbonizing, low-temperature Bayer process dissolving,            seeding and calcining.
AU LI C
   CUI J
AE GUCHENG HONGYE REFRACTORIES CO LTD (GUCH-Non-standard)
GA 202082553A
AB    NOVELTY - Uniform sampling inspection for production of                high-alumina powder involves mixing high-alumina                fly ash samples with sodium hydroxide solution,                heating, performing pre-desiliconization reaction,                and separating; adding fly ash filter cake to                slurry prepared by sodium carbonate, limestone or                quicklime, and grinding; roasting, and mixing                clinker with adjustment solution for dissolution;                pouring gas containing carbon dioxide into crude                sodium aluminate solution, carbonizing, separating,                and collecting carbon fraction mother liquor and                crude aluminum hydroxide; adding crude aluminum                hydroxide to lye for low-temperature Bayer process                dissolution, and controlling dissolution                temperature; using second lye to dilute and                desiliconize dissolution slurry, separating,                returning silicon slag to slurry, and filtering;                seeding desiliconized semen with aluminum hydroxide                seed crystals; and calcining aluminum hydroxide to                obtain alumina.
   USE - The method is used for uniform sampling                inspection for production of high-alumina                powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The low-temperature Bayer process is carried                out, which overcomes lengthy one-stage and                two-stage desiliconization process, and reduces                difficult operation of high pressure                desiliconization tank and deep desiliconization                tank. The equipment reduces equipment cost and                operation difficulty.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Uniform sampling inspection for production of                high-alumina powder comprises weighing samples from                high-alumina fly ash raw materials at different                locations, labeling samples with serial numbers,                mixing samples with sodium hydroxide solution one                by one, heating, introducing into container for                pre-desiliconization reaction, and carrying out                solid-liquid separation to obtain fly ash filter                cake and desiliconization liquid; adding fly ash                filter cake to slurry prepared by sodium carbonate,                limestone or quicklime to obtain mixed slurry,                grinding mixed slurry and preparing into slurry;                roasting mature material of slurry, mixing clinker                with adjustment solution for dissolution, and                performing solid-liquid separation after                dissolution to obtain dissolution crude residue and                sodium aluminate crude solution, where adjustment                solution includes 10-60 g/L sodium oxide potassium,                and 10-60 g/L sodium oxide carbide; pouring gas                containing carbon dioxide into crude sodium                aluminate solution to carry out carbonation                decomposition reaction, separating solid-liquid                reaction system, and collecting carbon fraction                mother liquor and crude aluminum hydroxide; adding                crude aluminum hydroxide to lye for low-temperature                Bayer process dissolution, performing solid-liquid                separation to obtain separated filter cake and                dissolution slurry, where low-temperature Bayer                process is dissolved according to 1 gram of                aluminum hydroxide crude product, adding alkaline                solution to lye, and controlling dissolution                temperature at 100-160 degrees C for 0.5-1 hours;                using second lye to dilute and desiliconize                dissolution slurry obtained in fifth step,                performing solid-liquid separation to silicon slag                and crude desiliconization liquid, returning                silicon slag to slurry, and filtering crude                desiliconization liquid; seeding desiliconized                semen obtained with aluminum hydroxide seed                crystals to obtain aluminum hydroxide and seed                decomposition mother liquor; and calcining aluminum                hydroxide to obtain alumina, and measuring weight                of refined alumina in each sample according to                serial number.
