PN US2020164455-A1
TI Method of forming a weld deposit on a zinc-coated workpiece involves feeding a tubular welding wire toward a surface of a galvanized workpiece; and directing a flow of shielding gas toward the surface of the galvanized workpiece.
AU BARHORST S E
   BUNDY J C
   AMATA M A
   HEFLIN-KING T D
AE HOBART BROS LLC (ILLT-C)
GA 2020454994
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves feeding a tubular welding wire toward a surface of a galvanized workpiece; directing a flow of shielding gas toward the surface of the galvanized workpiece near the tubular welding wire, where the flow of shielding gas consists essentially of argon (Ar) or a mixture of Ar and carbon dioxide (CO2) that is at least 90 wt.% argon; and forming the weld deposit on the zinc-coated workpiece using the tubular welding wire while providing the flow of shielding gas near the weld deposit, where the tubular welding wire comprises a granular core set inside of a metallic sheath. The granular core comprises an organic stabilizer component comprising one or more organic molecules or organic polymers bound to one or more Group I or Group II metals; a carbon component comprising graphite, graphene, carbon black, lamp black, carbon nanotubes, diamond, or a combination, and an agglomerate comprising Group I or Group II metal oxides, titanium oxide, and manganese oxides.
   USE - Method of forming a weld deposit on a zinc-coated workpiece.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to improve the welding process (e.g., improve arc stability and/or improve weld quality) for a number of different power configuration and the improved stability of the arc generally enable and improve the welding of coated metal workpieces and thinner workpieces. The tubular welding wire exhibits good gap handling and provides excellent weld properties (e.g., strength, ductility and appearance) using a wide operating process window. The tubular welding wire generally produces less smoke and spatter than other solid-cored, metal-cored, or flux-cored welding wires.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method involves feeding a tubular welding wire toward a surface of a galvanized workpiece; directing a flow of shielding gas toward the surface of the galvanized workpiece near the tubular welding wire, where the flow of shielding gas consists essentially of argon (Ar) or a mixture of Ar and carbon dioxide (CO2) that is at least 90 wt.% argon; and forming the weld deposit on the zinc-coated workpiece using the tubular welding wire while providing the flow of shielding gas near the weld deposit, where the tubular welding wire comprises a granular core set inside of a metallic sheath. The granular core comprises an organic stabilizer component comprising one or more organic molecules or organic polymers bound to one or more Group I or Group II metals; a carbon component comprising graphite, graphene, carbon black, lamp black, carbon nanotubes, diamond, or a combination, and an agglomerate comprising Group I or Group II metal oxides, titanium oxide, and manganese oxides; and where less than approximately 10 wt.% of the tubular welding wire is converted to spatter while forming the weld deposit on the zinc-coated workpiece.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of a process by which the tubular welding wire is used to weld a workpiece.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Conditions: The tubular welding wire is electrified with direct current electrode positive (DCEP) or direct current electrode negative (DCEN). The weld deposit has a length porosity less than approximately 10% and an area porosity less than approximately 4%. The flow of shielding gas consists essentially of Ar, or at least 90 wt.% Ar and 10 wt.% or less CO2. The weld deposit has a Charpy-V-Notch toughness greater than approximately 20 foot pounds at approximately - 20 degrees C and at approximately -?40 degrees C. The weld deposit has an ultimate tensile strength (UTS) of at least 70 kilopounds per square inch (kpsi), a yield strength (YS) of at least 70 kpsi, and a percent elongation of at least 18%. The tubular welding wire is a metal-cored carbon steel tubular welding wire or metal-cored low-alloy steel tubular welding wire. The granular core further comprises one or more filler metals; one or more alloying components; one or more carbon components; and one or more alkali metal or alkali earth metal compounds. The one or more organic stabilizer components comprise between approximately 0.5-1 wt.% granular core of the tubular welding wire.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN110438393-A
TI Producing mud mold moire antirust cast iron wok, by mixing crushed cast iron with cerium and iridium, smelting, shaping, carrying out nitrogen-carbon co-permeation treatment, soaking in sodium hydroxide, heat-treating and installing handle.
AU YANG D
   SHE W
AE SICHUAN JINCHENGXIANG KITCHENWARE CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 201996821P
AB    NOVELTY - Process for producing mud mold moire antirust cast iron wok, involves (i) mixing the crushed cast iron with metal cerium and metal iridium and smelting into molten iron, (ii) adding the molten iron into a slag remover, pouring into a mud mold for shaping, taking out from the mold and radiating and cooling, grinding and processing flash edges and burrs of the pot, and grinding, (iii) carrying out manual clean water scrubbing, ultrasonically cleaning, and washing the pot, (iv) placing the washed pot in a nitriding furnace, carrying out nitrogen-carbon co-permeation treatment, dividing into two sections of co-permeation, starting ammonia gas, controlling the ammonia decomposition rate, introducing mixed gas of ammonia and carbon dioxide, and controlling the ammonia decomposition rate to 45-60%, and (v) soaking the discharged pot in a sodium hydroxide solution, drying, carrying out heat treatment, cooling, polishing the surface of the pot, and installing a pot handle.
   USE - The process is useful for producing mud mold moire antirust cast iron wok.
   ADVANTAGE - The process improves the surface hardness and the anti-oxidation ability of the wok by performing nitrogen-carbon co-permeation treatment on the pot, so that the pot does not easily rust, and is not easily scratched by hard objects, and the maintenance is simpler.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Process for producing mud mold moire antirust cast iron wok, involves (i) mixing the crushed cast iron with metal cerium and metal iridium and adding into a smelting furnace for smelting into molten iron, (ii) adding the molten iron into a slag remover to remove impurities, pouring into a mud mold for shaping for 10-15 minutes, taking out the primary finished product from the mold and radiating and cooling, grinding and processing flash edges and burrs of the pot, and placing the pot into an automatic polishing machine for grinding for 5-10 minutes, (iii) carrying out manual clean water scrubbing on the polished pot, ultrasonically cleaning, and washing the pot by using a high-pressure cleaning machine, (iv) placing the washed pot in a nitriding furnace, carrying out nitrogen-carbon co-permeation treatment, dividing into two sections of co-permeation, starting ammonia gas when the furnace temperature is 300-350 degrees C, controlling the ammonia decomposition rate to 24-32% when the furnace temperature reaches 500-530 degrees C, introducing mixed gas of ammonia and carbon dioxide when the furnace temperature reaches 550-580 degrees C, where the co-permeation is carried out for 3-4 hours, and controlling the ammonia decomposition rate to 45-60%, and (v) soaking the discharged pot in a sodium hydroxide solution for 1-2 hours, drying, carrying out heat treatment, cooling the heat-treated cast iron pot, polishing the surface of the pot by using a polishing machine, and installing a pot handle to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The slag remover comprises 60-80 pts. wt. silica, 20-30 pts. wt. expanded perlite, 10-20 pts. wt. nano-aluminum oxide, 5-10 pts. wt. calcium oxide and 3-8 pts. wt. borax.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - MECHANICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Method: The step (v) involves, heating the pot at 400-580 degrees C in stages after soaking and drying, maintaining the temperature for 0.2-1 hour, and cooling down to room temperature in stages. Preferred Conditions: In step (i), the mass ratio of the cast iron, cerium and iridium is 100:(0.06-0.3):(0.01-0.06). In step (iv), the high pressure is 10-20 MPa. In step (v), the carbon dioxide accounts for 4-8% of the total volume of the mixed gas. In step (v), the sodium hydroxide solution has a mass concentration of 8-12% and a temperature of 40-65 degrees C.

PT P
PN CN110437884-A; CN110437884-B
TI Generation of electricity involves pyrolyzing biomass, introducing pyrolysis gas into steam boiler, heating, introducing steam into rotary furnace, adding pyrolytic carbon, reacting, and introducing hydrogen-rich gas into generator.
AU YANG X
   WANG X
   ZHU Y
   TIAN Y
   WANG Z
AE UNIV JILIN (UYJI-C)
GA 2019968334
AB    NOVELTY - Generation of electricity from biomass, involves pyrolyzing biomass, such that biomass is decomposed into pyrolysis gas and pyrolytic carbon, introducing pyrolysis gas into a medium-temperature steam boiler which indirectly heats medium-temperature steam, introducing the pyrolysis gas and the medium-temperature steam into a high-temperature steam boiler, heating, adding obtained high-temperature steam into the bottom of a water-carbon reaction rotary furnace, mixing the pyrolytic carbon and a catalyst, adding obtained mixture into the top of the rotary furnace, performing water-carbon reaction, passing product gas to the upper portion of the rotary furnace, heating the pyrolysis carbon and catalyst, introducing the product gas into a heat exchanger to recover the heat, purifying, introducing resultant product into a spray tower to remove carbon dioxide, producing produce hydrogen-rich gas, introducing hydrogen-rich gas into a generator to burn and generate electricity.
   USE - Generation of electricity from biomass.
   ADVANTAGE - The method transforms low-density biomass energy into high-density hydrogen energy, and economically generates electricity from biomass.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Generation of electricity from biomass charcoal, involves crushing biomass to a particle size of 30-100 mm, adding biomass particles to a pyrolysis tower, adjusting oxygen content, pyrolyzing at 600-700 degrees C for 0.5-1 hours, such that biomass is decomposed into pyrolysis gas and pyrolytic carbon, introducing pyrolysis gas into a medium-temperature steam boiler which indirectly heats medium-temperature steam to 150-180 degrees C, introducing the pyrolysis gas and the medium-temperature steam into a high-temperature steam boiler, heating to 800-950 degrees C, obtaining high-temperature steam, adding the high-temperature steam into the bottom of a water-carbon reaction rotary furnace with an inclination angle, flowing upward to a reaction zone, mixing the pyrolytic carbon and a catalyst, adding obtained mixture into the top of the water-carbon reaction rotary furnace, flowing downward to the reaction zone, performing water-carbon reaction at 800-950 degrees C, passing product gas through a reactant addition zone of the upper portion of the water-carbon reaction rotary furnace, heating the pyrolysis carbon and catalyst while cooling the product gas temperature, introducing the product gas into a heat exchanger to recover the heat, reducing temperature to 100-150 degrees C, removing dust in a water spray tower, reducing temperature to 95-100 degrees C, introducing resultant product into a calcium hydroxide aqueous solution spray tower to remove carbon dioxide, producing produce hydrogen-rich gas, and nano-calcium carbonate as a by-product, introducing hydrogen-rich gas into a generator to burn and generate electricity, discharging remaining solids of the water-carbon reaction from the bottom of the water-carbon reaction rotary furnace, cooling, crushing in a rolling kneader, classifying by air, collecting nano-carbon/silica powder, and collecting fine powder as a component of the catalyst for the water-carbon reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The catalyst is made of oxide of potassium, sodium, calcium, magnesium or iron, or their salt. When the water-carbon reaction temperature is 900-950 degrees C, the mass ratio of high-temperature steam and pyrolytic carbon is more than 5. The mass ratio of catalyst and pyrolytic carbon is 1-3:100. The content of hydrogen in the water-carbon reaction product is 50-56 vol.%. When the water-carbon reaction temperature is 850-900 degrees C, the mass ratio of high-temperature steam and pyrolytic carbon is 3-5:1, and the mass ratio of catalyst and pyrolytic carbon is 10-15:100. When the water-carbon reaction temperature is 800-850 degrees C, the mass ratio of high-temperature steam and pyrolytic carbon is 1-3:1, the mass ratio of catalyst and pyrolytic carbon is 5-9:100, and the hydrogen-rich gas has calorific value of 5000 kcal/m3. Preferred Process: Stearic acid phosphate and 15% calcium hydroxide solution are added to a reactor, and stirred, to form modified calcium hydroxide aqueous solution. The mass ratio of calcium hydroxide and stearic acid phosphate is 100:2-3. The modified calcium hydroxide aqueous solution is introduced into a top of the spray tower, carbonized with carbon dioxide, nano-calcium carbonate is precipitated, and filtered. The filter cake is transferred to the reactor, 15 wt.% deionized water is added, stirred, and aged for 4 hours. A mixed gas containing 30 vol.% carbon dioxide into the reactor, carbonized, the reaction is terminated when the pH value is 7, filtered, and dried at 120 degrees C, to obtain an active nano-calcium carbonate.

PN CN110241077-A
TI Extracting trophic factors from human mesenchymal stem cell medium used e.g. beauty product, by immersing umbilical cords in physiological saline containing antibiotics, cutting, culturing, passaging and amplifying, thawing and centrifuging.
AU WANG B
   WEI S
   XIE Y
   GAO T
   HUANG F
   WANG L
AE NANJING DRUM TOWER HOSPITAL (GLYY-C)
GA 201982185U
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting trophic factors from human mesenchymal stem cell medium involves aseptically collecting fetal umbilical cords produced in full months, immersing in physiological saline containing antibiotics, and washing residual blood in umbilical artery and umbilical vein with phosphate buffered saline (PBS); cutting umbilical cord, transferring small tissue blocks to bottom of ventilated cap culture bottle, stacking into small piles, inverting into carbon dioxide incubator, adding Dulbecco's Modified Eagle Medium (DMEM) medium containing fetal calf serum to culture bottle, continuously placing in incubator for culturing, replacing medium, and passaging and amplifying; thawing culture supernatant, centrifuging to remove cell debris, collecting supernatant, and desalting and purifying supernatant using PD-10 pre-packed desalting column; and filtering and sterilizing, determining protein content by bicinchoninic acid (BCA) method, transferring to ampoule, freeze-drying, and removing water.
   USE - The method is used for extracting trophic factors from human mesenchymal stem cell medium used as additive for skin care and beauty product and preparing anti-aging cosmetic (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The trophic factor avoids immune rejection reaction and potential infection risk caused by cell injection. The method is in good manufacturing practices (GMP) level environment, and undergoes strict detection of quality control and safety evaluation system of mesenchymal stem cells. The stem cell meets clinical production requirements, and ensures that the subsequent extraction of growth factors is free of infectious virus and bacterial contamination.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting trophic factors from human mesenchymal stem cell medium involves:
   (A) aseptically collecting fetal umbilical cords produced in full months, immersing in physiological saline containing antibiotics, and washing residual blood in umbilical artery and umbilical vein with phosphate buffered saline;
   (B) cutting umbilical cord into small tissue blocks, transferring small tissue blocks to bottom of 75 cm2 ventilated cap culture bottle, stacking into small piles, inverting into carbon dioxide incubator for 4 hours, adding DMEM medium containing 10% fetal calf serum to culture bottle, continuously placing in incubator for culturing, climbing out cells around the tissue block after 7 days, replacing medium, and passaging and amplifying according to ratio of 3 bottles passaging 1 bottle when the cell fusion degree reaching to 90%;
   (C) thawing culture supernatant at 4 degrees C overnight, placing in centrifuge tube, centrifuging for 10 minutes at speed of 3000 revolutions/minute (rpm) to remove cell debris, collecting supernatant in centrifuge tube, and desalting and purifying supernatant using PD-10 pre-packed desalting column to obtain high protein concentration purification liquid; and
   (D) filtering and sterilizing by filter with 0.22 mu m pore size, determining protein content by BCA method, transferring corresponding volume of purified liquid to ampoule according to amount of 2 mg of total protein, placing in freeze dryer, freeze-drying, and removing water to obtain protein powder enriched in mesenchymal stem cell trophic factors.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The antibiotic is 1 wt.% penicillin streptomycin solution. The carbon dioxide culture box has temperature of 37 degrees C and carbon dioxide concentration of 5%. Preferred Method: Desalting and purifying is done by returning supernatant to column top, adding PBS to elute at natural flow rate from top, and starting calculation.

PN CN110184522-A; CN110184522-B
TI Producing sulfur-containing gear steel used in wind power gear comprises carrying out converter smelting, ladle furnace refining and Ruhrstahl Heraeus refining, continuous casting, high temperature diffusion, billeting, heating and rolling.
AU ZHU T
   WANG L
   LI Y
   XU B
   XIAO G
   XU H
   XIAO Z
   LU X
AE HANDAN IRON & STEEL GROUP CO LTD (HBIS-C)
   HBIS CO LTD HANDAN BRANCH (HBIS-C)
GA 2019760068
AB    NOVELTY - Producing sulfur-containing gear steel comprises carrying out converter smelting by blowing to control the carbon dioxide product, combining high-strength carbon process to reduce the peroxidation of molten steel, adding high alkalinity aluminum-containing modifier to modify the ladle slag, controlling iron oxide content in large slag, carrying out ladle furnace refining process by controlling the binary alkalinity of final slag, operating Ruhrstahl Heraeus high vacuum and large circulation, feeding the sulfur line after calcium treatment, carrying out soft blowing, where the continuous casting process protects the casting using dynamic soft pressing, heating the rectangular billet, rolling into a billet, coating the slow-cooled billet with four high-temperature anti-oxidation coatings after four inspections, heating the billet coated with anti-high temperature anti-oxidation coating, subjecting to high-pressure water dephosphorization, and rolling into finished bar product.
   USE - The steel is useful in wind power gear.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can effectively solves the problems of high oxygen content, unstable content and uniformity, difficulty in controlling sulfide precipitation, and high band structure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing sulfur-containing gear steel comprises carrying out converter smelting by blowing to control the carbon dioxide product at the end point of 0.0026% or less, combining the high-strength carbon process to reduce the peroxidation of molten steel, reducing the amount of slag under large-package process during the tapping process, adding high alkalinity aluminum-containing modifier to modify the ladle slag, controlling iron oxide content in the large slag to be less than 1.5 wt.%, carrying out ladle furnace refining process by operating the final slag of refining by high alkalinity low calcium aluminum ratio, controlling the binary alkalinity of final slag at 4-5, and controlling the calcium to aluminum ratio to be 1.5-2, operating Ruhrstahl Heraeus (RH) by high vacuum and large circulation, feeding the sulfur line at intervals of greater than or equal to 8 minutes after calcium treatment, carrying out soft blowing for greater than or equal to 10 minutes, where the continuous casting process protects the casting in the whole process, and using dynamic soft pressing, controlling the required speed stability within the range of standard drawing speed of 0.6 plus minus 0.025 m/minute, controlling the second cooling ratio by 0.45-0.50, after completing the continuous casting, heating the rectangular billet, opening, and rolling into a billet, then slowly cooling, coating the slow-cooled billet with four high-temperature anti-oxidation coatings after four inspections, carrying out heating and rolling process by heating the billet coated with anti-high temperature anti-oxidation coating into a heating furnace, subjecting the surface of billet to high-pressure water dephosphorization, then rolling into a finished bar product according to conventional process, and controlling the temperature at 800-830 degrees C after the end rolling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The continuous casting billet comprises 0.18-0.20 wt.% carbon, 0.25-0.30 wt.% silicon, 0.95-1 wt.% manganese, 1.20-1.28 wt.% chromium, 0.20-0.25 wt.% molybdenum, 0.025-0.035 wt.% sulfur, less than or equal to 0.020 wt.% phosphorus, 0.020-0.035 wt.% aluminum sulfide, less than or equal to 0.10 wt.% nickel, less than or equal to 0.10 wt.% copper, less than or equal to 8 ppm oxygen, and remaining amount of iron and unavoidable impurities, where the center segregation of slab is controlled below 1, and carbon offset coefficient of the slab is below 1.10. The anti-high temperature anti-oxidation coating uses 25 wt.% calcium oxide in general-purpose chromium-molybdenum based carbon steel anti-high temperature anti-oxidation coating. The continuous casting process uses steady drawing speed of 0.6 m/minute, where the second cooling ratio water quantity is controlled at 0.45-0.50 in the standard drawing speed adjustment range, ratio of the second cold water distribution in Zone 1 to Zone 3 is 48:23:29 mode, which effectively promotes the fine dispersion of sulfide in the solidification front, and cast billet is slowly cooled after being placed off the line. Preferred Method: The method comprises performing converter smelting process by using binary alkalinity of the high alkalinity aluminum-containing modifier of 7-8. The RH high vacuum large circulation is carried out by using the ultimate vacuum degree of less than or equal to 20 Pa. The method comprises fully protecting continuous casting and casting, using low-oxygen ferrous oxide low-alkalinity covering agent and low-carbon slag-absorbing agent to reduce the oxygen in the package and carbonation in the process, where the inlet reduction rate under dynamic soft pressing is 0.512-0.538 mm/mute, and outlet reduction rate is 0.175-0.198 mm/minute, placing the cast billet in the pit, slowly cooling, after completing the continuous casting, heating the slab in a heating furnace at 1200-1230 degrees C for 200-240 minutes, carrying out high-pressure water dephosphorization, then rolling the billet into a slow cooling pit for greater than or equal to 24 hours, slow cooling, coating the billet with four high-temperature anti-oxidation coatings after the inspection, heating the billet in a heating furnace at 1160-1190 degrees C, controlling the overall heating time for 180-220 minutes, performing high-pressure water dephosphorization under a pressure of greater than or equal to 18 MPa, where the continuous casting process is carried out by using low-oxygen ferrous oxide low-alkalinity covering agent with ferrous content of less than or equal to 0.1 wt.%, alkalinity of 1.5-2, and low-carbon slag absorbing agent with carbon content of less than or equal to 0.02 wt.% to reduce oxygenation and process carbonation, and stirring is performed by using crystallizer under an electricity of 320A plus minus 10A/2.5Hz plus minus 0.5Hz. The continuous casting process is carried out by using low superheat degree, controlling the first furnace superheat degree at 35-50 degrees C, and controlling superheating degree of the continuous casting furnace at 15-30 degrees C, after completing the continuous casting, heating the slab, using high-pressure water for phosphorus removal under a pressure of greater than or equal to 18 MPa.

PN JP2019084489-A
TI Gas-separation apparatus for separating water vapor and carbon dioxide from exhaust gas, has first adsorbent which is provided to adsorb first gas, and second adsorbent which is provided to adsorb second gas.
AU KUNITOMI S
   YAMAMOTO S
AE TOYOTA CHUO KENKYUSHO KK (TOYW-C)
GA 2019502780
AB    NOVELTY - The gas-separation apparatus (10) has a first adsorbent which is attached to first cylindrical element (20) and is provided to adsorb first gas. A second adsorbent is attached to second cylindrical element (22) and is provided to adsorb second gas. A second flow unit is provided to make a first medium for desorbing the first gas adsorbed to the first adsorbent to flow into and out of a container (12), so as to flow in one direction inside the container in a region different from the region in which the mixed gas is flowed in the circumferential direction. The region in which the mixed gas is flowed and the region in which the first medium is flowed are in a circumferential direction different from the region in which the mixed gas is flowed, so as to flow in one direction inside the container. A third flow unit is provided to make a second medium for desorbing the second gas adsorbed to the second adsorbent to flow into and out of the container.
   USE - Gas-separation apparatus for separating water vapor and carbon dioxide from exhaust gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The first gas and second gas are separated from the mixed gas flowing in one direction through the container.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The gas-separation apparatus has a first cylindrical element which is arranged on first closing lid (16) side and moved to one side in a circumferential direction. A second cylindrical element is arranged on the second closing lid (18) side and moved to other side in the circumferential direction. A first flow unit is provided make a mixed gas containing a first gas and a second gas to flow into and out of the container, so as to flow unidirectionally through a partial region in the circumferential direction inside the container. The first flow unit is provided to flow the mixed gas into the container, so as to flow in the container from one end of the one direction to the other end. The second flow unit is provided to flow first medium of ordinary temperature into and out of the container and to flow from other end of one direction to the other end in the inside of the container. The third flow unit is provided to flow the high temperature second medium into the container, so as to flow from the one end of the one direction to the other end inside the container. First cooling unit is configured to cool the first adsorbent of a portion arranged in a region in which the mixed gas flows. A second cooling unit is configured to cool the second adsorbent of a portion arranged in a region in which the mixed gas flows.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the gas-separation apparatus.
   Gas-separation apparatus (10)
   Cylinder (12)
   First and second closing lids (16,18)
   First and second cylindrical elements (20,22)
   Cooling block (58)

PN CN109809835-A; CN109809835-B
TI Preparing anti-reinforcing steel concrete carbide coating comprises e.g. setting coating template outside the reinforced concrete, injecting fully stirred liquid polyurethane into coating template and discharging saturated lime water.
AU WANG S
   JIANG H
   ZHU J
   LU L
   MA Q
   LIU Q
   WANG Y
   ZHANG Z
   ZHAO Z
AE UNIV XUZHOU TECHNOLOGY (XUZH-C)
   UNIV XUZHOU TECHNOLOGY (XUZH-C)
GA 2019488120
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing anti-reinforcing steel concrete carbide coating comprises e.g. (i) setting coating template outside the reinforced concrete to fix the ellipsoidal capsule in coating template, connecting the upper part of the ellipsoidal capsule with liquid injection line, connecting lower part to drain line, injecting saturated lime water into ellipsoidal capsule through until the liquid pressure in the ellipsoidal capsule reaches 0.16-0.21 MPa, (ii) injecting fully stirred liquid polyurethane into coating template and solidifying liquid polyurethane in coating template to obtain polyurethane initial layer, (iii) injecting stirred liquid polyurethane into coating template, solidifying liquid polyurethane in coating template to obtain polyurethane sealing layer, and (iv) opening drain line, discharging saturated lime water inside ellipsoidal capsule, and injecting saturated lime water into ellipsoidal capsule until the liquid pressure in the ellipsoidal capsule is 0.16-0.21 MPa.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing anti-reinforcing steel concrete carbide coating.
   ADVANTAGE - The anti-reinforcing steel concrete carbide coating: has ellipsoidal capsule filled with saturated lime water in the coating layer which blocks and absorbs intrusive carbon dioxide, prevents carbon dioxide from entering the reinforced concrete, and improves the durability of the reinforced concrete.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing anti-reinforcing steel concrete carbide coating comprises (i) setting coating template outside the reinforced concrete to fix the ellipsoidal capsule in the coating template, connecting the upper part of the ellipsoidal capsule with liquid injection line, and connecting the lower part to drain line, injecting saturated lime water into the ellipsoidal capsule through the liquid injection pipeline until the liquid pressure in the ellipsoidal capsule reaches 0.16-0.21 MPa, and stop injecting, where the distance between the left and right ends of the ellipsoidal capsule is 2-3 mm from both sides of the coating template, and the injection line is provided with pressure gauge, (ii) injecting fully stirred liquid polyurethane into the coating template and spreading the upper surface of the reinforced concrete, solidifying the liquid polyurethane in the coating template to obtain polyurethane initial layer, where the highest position of polyurethane initial layer is 0.6-0.8 times the polar radius of the ellipsoidal capsule, (iii) injecting fully stirred liquid polyurethane into coating template again, solidifying the liquid polyurethane in the coating template to obtain polyurethane sealing layer, where the highest position of the polyurethane sealing layer is higher than the highest position of ellipsoidal capsule by 3-5 mm, and (iv) opening the drain line every 3 months, discharging the saturated lime water inside the ellipsoidal capsule, closing the drain line after completely draining, opening the injection pipe, and injecting saturated lime water into the ellipsoidal capsule until the liquid pressure in the ellipsoidal capsule is 0.16-0.21 MPa to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ellipsoidal capsule is made of Hypalon (RTM: Chlorosulfonated polyethylene synthetic rubber) rubber cloth material. The foaming ratio between polyurethane used in polyurethane initial layer and the polyurethane sealing layer is 2.5-3.5 times, and the minimum thickness of polyurethane initial layer between the ellipsoidal capsule and the reinforced concrete is 2-3 mm.

PN CN109680289-A; CN109680289-B
TI Coupling type bio-electrochemical hydrogen production and purification device comprises e.g. electrolytic chamber, anode and cathode, electrolysis chamber for containing electrolyte and anode used for supplying electrons and hydrogen ions.
AU ZHANG Z
   ZHANG J
   WANG Z
   XIA S
AE UNIV TONGJI (UYTJ-C)
GA 201941090P
AB    NOVELTY - Coupling type bio-electrochemical hydrogen production and purification device comprises electrolytic chamber (1), anode (2) and cathode (3). The electrolysis chamber is used for containing electrolyte. The anode is used for supplying electrons and hydrogen ions and cathode is used for generating hydrogen gas. The device further comprises gas collecting port (4), hydrophobic air-permeable film (5), gas collecting pipe, absorption cell, gas pipe and air bag. The back side of cathode is closely attached to hydrophobic gas permeable membrane. The gas collecting port is set on electrolysis chamber and gas collecting port is connected to gas collecting pipe for collecting hydrogen gas generated by the cathode. The absorption tank absorbs carbon dioxide attached to hydrogen at the gas collection port through the gas collection pipe. The air bag is connected to the absorption pool through gas pipe for collecting and storing hydrogen gas. The methanation inhibitor is added to the electrolyte.
   USE - Used as coupling type bio-electrochemical hydrogen production and purification device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: reduces internal resistance of system, improves coulombic efficiency and system has the purpose of achieving efficient and continuous hydrogen production performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing hydrogen by coupled bioelectrochemical hydrogen production and purification system, comprising (i) culturing anode electroforming bacteria in a microbial fuel cell mode, mixing medium and inoculum in the volume ratio of 1:1, removing dissolved oxygen and feeding same into microbial fuel cell, connecting resistor in the closed circuit system and running it in a static batch mode, no need to use inoculum when voltage across the resistor exceeds 0.1V, directly feeding culture medium into the microbial fuel cell, repeating for at least three cycles until the microbial fuel cell stably outputs the maximum voltage and considering that the anode electrogenerated bacteria has been enriched; and (ii) domesticating hydrogen anode biofilm to start the microbial electrolysis cell in microbial cell mode, replacing cathode of microbial fuel cell with cathode of the microbial cell, transferring to microbial cell mode at an applied voltage of 0.3-1.8V, connecting anode to the anode of external power source through a wire, connecting cathode to cathode of the external power source through a wire, anode releases electrons, hydrogen ion and carbon dioxide during the degradation of organic matter and electrons reach cathode via external circuit, combining cathode with hydrogen ions to generate hydrogen gas, adding methanation inhibitor to electrolyte of the electrolytic cell and stirring electrolyte to operate in a static batch mode, when current in the microbial cell is less than 0.1 mA, replacing fresh electrolyte, recording as operating cycle and repeating for many cycles until microbial cell begins to produce hydrogen.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the coupling type bio-electrochemical hydrogen production and purification device.
   Electrolytic chamber (1)
   Anode (2)
   Cathode (3)
   Gas collecting port (4)
   Hydrophobic air-permeable film (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkali solution is placed in absorption tank and alkali liquid is hydroxide solution, preferably, hydroxide in hydroxide solution is at least one of sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide and calcium hydroxide. The hydrophobic gas permeable membrane is at least one of polytetrafluoroethylene film, polyvinylidene fluoride film and polyethylene film, preferably, methanation inhibitor is 3-nitroester-1-propanol at a concentration of 5x 106 to 5x 103 mol/l. The electrolyte is mixture of low molecular organic acids, preferably, mixed liquid containing low molecular organic acid is at least one of organic waste anaerobic fermentation liquid, organic wastewater anaerobic fermentation liquid and low molecular organic acid mixed liquids having a carbon chain number less than twelve. The anode is at least one of carbon brush, carbon felt, graphite felt, and a carbon cloth, preferably, cathode is at least one of stainless steel felt, graphene electrode, carbon nanotube electrode, palladium-modified electrode and platinum-modified electrode. The medium comprises sodium acetate, phosphate buffer, vitamins and trace elements, preferably, inoculum is at least one of excess sludge and anaerobic sludge, preferably, agitation mode includes turbine agitation, impeller agitation, paddle agitation, anchor agitation, pusher agitation and/or magnetic agitation.

PT P
PN CN109052967-A; CN109052967-B
TI Preparing glass-ceramics involves using potassium feldspar tailings, preparing potassium feldspar tailings powder by washing potassium feldspar tailings and crushing once to a particle size, and carrying out washing process.
AU DENG P
AE HEZHOU JUNXIN MINERAL PROD CO LTD (HEZH-Non-standard)
   HEZHOU JUNXIN MINERAL PROD CO LTD (HEZH-Non-standard)
GA 201900923S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing glass-ceramics involves using potassium feldspar tailings, preparing potassium feldspar tailings powder by washing potassium feldspar tailings and crushing once to a particle size of 1-2 centimeter. The washing process is carried out through the spiral groove to remove the sludge and then dehydrated. The above washed ore-treated feldspar is ball-milled by a ball mill to particle size of 40-50 mesh to obtain potassium feldspar pellets. The high pressure treatment includes mixing above potassium feldspar particles, water and trapping agent into high pressure reactor, and charging the high pressure reactor with carbon dioxide having purity of 99 wt.% or more until high pressure reactor at pressure of 5-10 mega Pascal, and raising temperature at 55-60 degrees C and stirring with stirring rod for 40-50 minutes, and continuously passing carbon dioxide during the stirring process to maintain pressure of the high pressure reactor at 5-10 mega Pascal, after the stirring is completed.
   USE - Method for preparing glass-ceramics.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare glass-ceramics which has good performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The pressure is reduced and above-mentioned K-feldspar particles are treated by microwave for 20-30 minutes, and then irradiated by X-ray, after the above potassium feldspar particles are dried at 100-110 degrees C to obtain potassium feldspar powder. The iron content and titanium content of the potassium feldspar powder are 0.35-0.68 wt.% and 0.28-0.37 wt.%. The raw material equipment includes adding zinc sulfide, calcium oxide and zinc oxide to the above K-feldspar tailings powder to mixed to obtain a raw material powder. The main component of the raw material powder comprises 60-65 silica, 10-15% alumina, 5-10% potassium oxide, 10-16% calcium oxide, 2-3% sodium oxide, 0.2-0.4% titanium oxide, 0.3-0.5% iron oxide, 1-2% zinc sulfide and 1-2% zinc oxide. The above raw material powder is placed in a kiln and melted at 1220 degrees C to obtain a raw material melt. The raw material melt is water quenched and dried to obtain glass granules. The above glass particles are heated from room temperature to 680 degrees C at a heating rate of 15 degrees C per minute, and nucleate the heat for 1-1.5 h, then raise the temperature to 1050 degrees C at a heating rate of 10 degrees C per minute. , crystallization to maintain 1-1.5h; further reduce the temperature from 1050 degrees C to 500 degrees C at a cooling rate of 25 degrees C per minute, and finally the glass from 500 degrees C at a rate of 10 degrees C per minute Cool down to room temperature. The glass frit obtained after the above heat treatment is polished and polished to obtain first glass ceramic.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The ratio of the potassium feldspar particles, water and capturing agent is 1 kilogram:8-10 kilogram: 0.4-0.5 gram. The scavenger is mainly prepared by mixing sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate, linolenic acid and dodecylamine in a mass ratio of 5:1:2. The frequency of the microwave is 500-700 mega hertz. the amount of x-rayirradiation is 8-10 kilo gray. The iron content and the titanium content of potassium feldspar powder are respectively 0.44 wt.% and 0.42 wt.%.
DC L01 (Glass - includes chemical compositions, batch treatment, furnaces, flat glass forming, hollow-ware forming, postforming and glass/ceramics, but not lens designs, bottling, bottle-washing, closures for containers, glazing designs, glass cutting, chamfering edges, printing on glass, disposing of used glass or the production of pure sodium silicate. Chemical aspects of optical fibres (C03).)

PN CN108948796-A
TI Preparing nano calcium carbonate comprises e.g.            calcining natural limestone and coke, adding hot water,            aging, filtering, carrying out carbonization reaction,            adding sodium hydroxide, mixed fatty acid and            dispersant.
AU WANG D
   LI Q
AE NANOMATERIALS TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANO-Non-standard)
GA 2018A05079
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano calcium carbonate comprises                e.g. calcining natural limestone and coke at a high                temperature in a shaft kiln, adding hot water,                allowing to stand, aging, filtering, adding into                the reaction kettle, adding crystal form                controlling agent A, carrying out carbonization                reaction, carrying out surface treatment, adding                hot water, sodium hydroxide, mixed fatty acid,                liquid rosin and dispersant, stirring for 1 hour,                adding into the storage tank, dehydrating,                stirring, drying, reacting, and subjecting to                pressure filtration dehydration, drying, and                crushing to obtain nano calcium carbonate                product.
   USE - The nano calcium carbonate is useful for high                transparent high-grade offset printing ink                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the quality of nano                calcium carbonate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing nano calcium carbonate comprises (i)                calcining natural limestone and coke at a high                temperature in a shaft kiln to form calcium oxide,                and where the ratio between limestone and coke is                9:1-10:1, (ii) adding hot water into digest calcium                oxide to obtain calcium hydroxide slurry, allowing                to stand, aging for greater than 24 hours and                filtering, the temperature of the hot water during                digestion is 60-80 degrees C, (iii) adding calcium                hydroxide slurry is in suspension state into the                reaction kettle, adding 70% of the crystal form                controlling agent A, carrying out carbonization                reaction for 10 minutes, controlling the initial                temperature of the carbonization temperature at                18-25 degrees C, and controlling the process                temperature below 40 degrees C for 15 minutes, and                adding 30% crystal form controlling agent B, adding                the slurry of one compound into the secondary                compounding device and allowing to stand for 2                hours, adding carbon dioxide (4.5 m3/minutes) while                stirring, and reducing the pH value to 10, start of                ripening (circulation, stirring) for 12 hours from                the start of the second compound to the end of the                ripening process, requiring pH adjustment during                the ripening period (2-3 times), at the end of the                ripening, the BET is 20-21, viscosity 10                revolutions per minute, above 500 Pas, air ratio                58000, passing the test, and adding carbon dioxide                to reduce the pH to 7, and raising the temperature                to 59 degrees C, and then carrying out surface                treatment, injecting hot water into the emulsifier                at 80 degrees C, injecting sodium hydroxide, the                specific gravity is 1.27, and the injection amount                is 27.2 l, injecting 66.1 l mixed fatty acid,                adding 22.3 l liquid rosin (specific gravity 1.05),                adding dispersant, stirring for 1 hour, adding into                the storage tank together with the finished liquid,                dehydrating, stirring for one hour, drying,                reacting the temperature of the emulsifier at 80                degrees C, the surface treatment agent should be                transparent after being completely emulsified, and                no precipitation occurs, and (v) subjecting the                surface treated nano calcium carbonate to pressure                filtration dehydration, drying, and crushing to                obtain nano calcium carbonate product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The filter cake moisture                  is 40-45%, and the drying temperature of the                  filter cake is 85-110 degrees C. The process time                  for lowering the pH to 7 in the step (i) is 1                  hour, and the carbon dioxide flow rate is 4.5                  m3/minutes. Preferred Components: The crystal                  form controlling agents A and B are citrate,                  sucrose or phosphate. The dispersant is sulfate                  or phosphate. The rosin is deuterated rosin,                  hydrogenated rosin or rosin in an amount of                  3.5-4.5% of calcium carbonate. The calcium                  hydroxide slurry suspension is configured to have                  a concentration of 6-12% suspension. The carbon                  dioxide concentration in the carbonization is                  26-30%. The primary compound has a specific                  gravity of 1.085, conductivity is 150 ws/cm, when                  the specific gravity of calcium hydroxide is                  diluted to 1.077 before compounding, 6660 l pairs                  of cold water 2340 l, when the specific gravity                  is 1.078, 6580 l pairs of cold water 2420 l, the                  amount of the crystal form controlling agent is                  0.5-3% by weight of calcium carbonate, and the                  temperature of the cold water is below 20 degrees                  C.

PN CN108753461-A
TI Preparing biopeptide composite antioxidant by mixing prepared rapeseed oleic acid, enzymatic hydrolyzate and rosemary extract, followed by placing in shearing machine, adding ascorbic acid, shearing and emulsifying, steaming and drying.
AU LIU M
   DENG B
   ZHU C
AE FOSHAN WANYANG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 201890593W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing biopeptide composite antioxidant involves: (1) taking fresh Agastache rugosa leaves, pulverizing, sieving and collecting; (2) dispersing the dried A.rugosa leaf powder in absolute ethanol, stirring, heating, stirring and dispersing to obtain an extract, centrifuging, separating and removing the lower layer precipitate and collecting the supernatant liquid; (3) placing the above supernatant liquid in a distillation flask, heating and refluxing to obtain a rosemary extract, and carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide on the rosemary extract; (4) preparing rapeseed oleic acid; (5) preparing an enzymatic hydrolyzate; and (6) mixing the hydrolyzate, rapeseed oleic acid and rosemary extract, followed by placing in a shearing machine, adding ascorbic acid, shearing and emulsifying to obtain a mixed emulsion, then placing in a rotary steamer, steaming and placing the rotary steaming bottle in a vacuum drying oven and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing biopeptide composite antioxidant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the biopeptide composite antioxidant with high stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing biopeptide composite antioxidant involves: (1) taking fresh Agastache rugosa leaves, freezing in a freeze dryer, removing the A.rugosa leaves from the freeze dryer, pulverizing the A.rugosa leaves, sieving and collecting the dried A.rugosa leaf powder; (2) dispersing the dried A.rugosa leaf powder in absolute ethanol, stirring at a speed of 200-250 rpm, heating, stirring and dispersing for 2-4 hours to obtain an extract, centrifuging the extract in a high-speed centrifuge, separating and removing the lower layer precipitate, collecting the supernatant liquid; (3) placing the above supernatant liquid in a distillation flask, heating and refluxing to obtain a rosemary extract, carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide on the rosemary extract, combining and collecting the rosemary extract in first-stage separation kettle and second-stage separation kettle; (4) mixing rapeseed oil, sodium hydroxide and ethanol solution for 15-20 minutes to obtain a mixed solution, placing the mixed solution in a separatory funnel, adding hydrochloric acid to the separatory funnel, adjusting the pH-value of the mixture, removing the liquid layer by static liquid separation to obtain an oil layer, and washing the oil layer 3-5 times with distilled water to obtain rapeseed oleic acid; (5) adding an active dry yeast to water to prepare yeast milk, adjusting the pH-value of the yeast milk with sodium citrate solution, heating, adding mixed protease and carrying out enzymolysis to obtain an enzymatic hydrolyzate; and (6) mixing 40-50 pts. wt. above hydrolyzate, 20-25 pts. wt. rapeseed oleic acid and 30-35 pts. wt. rosemary extract, followed by placing in a high-speed shearing machine, adding 4-5 pts. wt. ascorbic acid, shearing and emulsifying to obtain a mixed emulsion, placing the mixed emulsion in a rotary steamer, steaming for 10-15 minutes, and placing the rotary steaming bottle in a vacuum drying oven and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Condition: In step (1), the freeze-drying temperature is -30 degrees C to -20 degrees C, the freezing time is 4-5 hours and the freeze-drying pressure is 10-15 MPa. In step (2), the centrifuging speed is 3000-3500 rpm, and the centrifuging time is 20-30 minutes. In step (3), the supercritical extraction pressure is controlled at 20-22 MPa, the extraction temperature is 48-50 degrees C, the primary separation pressure is 8.5-9 MPa, the secondary separation pressure is 5-6 MPa, and the extraction time is 4-5 hours.

PN CN108745288-A
TI Preparation of magnetic oil tea shell biomass carbon, involves pyrolyzing oil tea shell, reacting biomass carbon with sulfuric acid solution, washing, mixing pretreated biological carbon and inorganic iron salt solution, washing, drying.
AU LIU R
   WU S
   SONG J
   HUANG J
AE UNIV TONGREN (UTRE-C)
GA 201888711S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of magnetic oil tea shell biomass carbon, involves (1) pyrolyzing oil tea shell, introducing nitrogen, heating at 550-600 degrees C, switching the nitrogen to carbon dioxide and maintaining for 0.8-1.5 hours and cooling to obtain biomass carbon, (2) reacting biomass carbon with sulfuric acid solution, performing solid-liquid separation, obtaining a solid substance, washing solid substance until the pH is neutral and dry, obtaining pretreated biological carbon, (3) mixing pretreated biological carbon and inorganic iron salt solution, reacting under urea solution conditions, performing solid-liquid separation, washing until the pH is neutral, drying resulting solid and separating the product. The solid to liquid ratio of biomass carbon and sulfuric acid solution is 1 g:(6-10) mL, preferably 1 g:8 mL. The concentration of sulfuric acid in sulfuric acid solution is 0.5-1.5 mol/L. Before the activated carbon is magnetized, the activated carbon is passed through a sieve of 140-150 mu m.
   USE - Preparation of magnetic oil tea shell biomass carbon.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of magnetic oil tea shell biomass carbon, involves (1) pyrolyzing oil tea shell, introducing nitrogen, heating at 550-600 degrees C, switching the nitrogen to carbon dioxide and maintaining for 0.8-1.5 hours and cooling to obtain biomass carbon, (2) reacting biomass carbon with sulfuric acid solution, performing solid-liquid separation, obtaining a solid substance, washing solid substance until the pH is neutral and dry, obtaining pretreated biological carbon, (3) mixing pretreated biological carbon and inorganic iron salt solution, reacting under urea solution conditions, performing solid-liquid separation, washing until the pH is neutral, drying resulting solid and separating the product. The method involves heating at 400 degrees C for 5-10 minutes, heating at 500 degrees C for 15-20 minutes and heating at 600 degrees C for 25-30 minutes, after the flow of the nitrogen gas is introduced. The solid to liquid ratio of biomass carbon and sulfuric acid solution is 1 g:(6-10) mL, preferably 1 g:8 mL. The concentration of sulfuric acid in sulfuric acid solution is 0.5-1.5 mol/L. The activated carbon is prepared by mixing activated carbon, ferric chloride hexahydrate and deionized water, heating at 70-80 degrees C under stirring, then adding ferrous chloride tetrahydrate to cool to below 35 degrees C, adding urea to precipitate, leaving still for more than 12 hours, separating the solid solution by pressure filtration to obtain precipitate and washing the precipitate. The urea is dripped to a solution with a pH of 10-12. The precipitate is washed with ethanol and deionized water. Before the activated carbon is magnetized, the activated carbon is passed through a sieve of 140-150 mu m.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

PN CN108689388-A
TI Co-producing whitening agent useful for a ceramic body of phosphogypsum and red mud acid, comprises e.g. mixing and grinding phosphogypsum, red mud, additive and modifier raw material and sending to kiln for roasting and obtaining clinker.
AU CHEN X
   ZHENG K
   WANG L
   WANG Q
   LI Z
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 201885082P
AB    NOVELTY - Co-producing a whitening agent comprises e.g. mixing and grinding a phosphogypsum, a red mud, an additive and a modifier raw material and sending to the kiln for roasting, and obtaining the clinker, and subjecting the clinker to water grinding and dissolution, and carrying out solid-liquid separation, subjecting the precipitate obtained by the solid-liquid separation to flotation, separating the sulfide, calcining the separated sulfide, and generating the flue gas by calcination and catalytically reacting with vanadium pentoxide, absorbing with concentrated sulfuric acid to produce sulfuric acid, adding carbon dioxide (CO2) to the liquid obtained by the solid-liquid separation until the precipitate is no longer generated, and then drying the precipitate until the water content is less than 0.5%, obtaining crude aluminum hydroxide, adding decolorizing agent and a dispersing agent to the crude aluminum hydroxide for pulverization.
   USE - The co-produced whitening agent is useful for a ceramic body of phosphogypsum and red mud acid (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The whitening agent has characteristics of high comprehensive utilization ratio of phosphorus gypsum and red mud, low treatment cost and large additional output value, and the obtained aluminum hydroxide: is a good raw material for preparing the white body brightener for the ceramic body, and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Co-producing a whitening agent comprises (i) mixing and grinding a phosphogypsum, a red mud, an additive and a modifier raw material and sending to the kiln for roasting, and obtaining the clinker, and subjecting the clinker to water grinding and dissolution, and carrying out solid-liquid separation; (ii) subjecting the precipitate obtained by the solid-liquid separation in the step (i) to flotation, separating the sulfide, calcining the separated sulfide, and generating the flue gas by calcination and catalytically reacting with vanadium pentoxide, absorbing with concentrated sulfuric acid to produce sulfuric acid; (iii) adding carbon dioxide (CO2) to the liquid obtained by the solid-liquid separation in the step (i) until the precipitate is no longer generated, and then drying the precipitate at 95-125 degrees C until the water content is less than 0.5%, obtaining crude aluminum hydroxide, adding 0.1-1 wt.% decolorizing agent and a dispersing agent to the crude aluminum hydroxide for pulverization, where the weight ratio of decolorizing agent and dispersing agent is 1:0.1-2.5 to obtain aluminum hydroxide; and (iv) taking aluminum hydroxide obtained in the step (iii), taking the activated silicon micropowder, quartz powder, calcium carbonate, kaolin and magnesia as raw materials, and adding water to each of the raw materials to prepare aluminum hydroxide slurry, activated silicon micropowder slurry, quartz powder slurry, calcium carbonate slurry, kaolin slurry and magnesite slurry, and then placing the above slurry in a stirred vessel and uniformly mixing, drying, and heating in a kiln, and dry-grinding.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the red mud is produced by the Bayer process to produce alumina red mud, where the additive is sodium carbonate, sodium sulfate or caustic soda, the modifier is anthracite, and carbon or coal gangue. In the step (i), the kiln is an industrial rotary kiln, an industrial tunnel kiln or industrial shaft kiln. In the step (iv), the whitening agent comprises 70-100 pts. wt. aluminum hydroxide, 10-25 pts. wt. activated silicon micro-powder, 10-15 pts. wt. quartz powder, 5-15 pts. wt. calcium carbonate, 15-25 pts. wt. kaolin and 2-5 pts. wt. magnesite. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the weight ratio of phosphogypsum and red mud is 1-1.3:1-1.5, the additive addition ratio is 1:1 according to the molecular ratio of the sum of sodium oxide (Na2O) and aluminum +ferrous oxide (Al2O3+Fe2O3) contained in the raw material, where the mixing ratio of modifier is 18-25% of the total weight of the raw material, the baking is performed at 1200-1300 degrees C for 2-3 hours and the ratio of liquid to solid during dissolution is 2.5-5:1. In the step (ii), the calcining condition is: placing the sulfide in an oxygen-rich environment of 35-50% and baking at 900-1200 degrees C for 3-5 hours. In the step (iii), the decolorizing agent is sodium percarbonate or sodium perborate, or sodium persulfate, the dispersing agent is ethanol or polyethylene glycol, the grinding is performed by an alumina-lined ball mill, grinding medium is an alumina ball, and the ball-to-batch ratio is 7-10:1. In the step (iv), the particle diameter after the polishing is 0.5-2 mu m. The temperature at which the kiln is fired is 450-550 degrees C. The fineness after dry-grinding is 0.1-2 mu m.

PN CN107999937-A
TI Fully-automatic welding process of long-distance pipelines involves performing groove machining on end face of pipe orifice of welding pipeline before welding.
AU LIU T
   XU Z
   FAN C
   ZHONG J
   NIU G
   SUN Z
AE SINOPEC GAS CO (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC ZHONGYUAN OILFIELD CONSTR ENG CO (SNPC-C)
GA 201837572M
AB    NOVELTY - Fully-automatic welding process of long-distance pipelines involves performing groove machining on an end face of pipe orifice of a welding pipeline before welding. The parameters of groove are such that the angle a and angle b of the upper and outer groove is 5-8 degrees , the angle c of the inner angle of the upper and outer groove is 90 degrees , the angle d of the lower inner groove angle is 75 plus minus 1.5 degrees , the height of the blunt edge is 1.27-0.15 mm, the lower inner groove depth is 1.27 plus minus 0.15 mm, the length of slope at the edge of the lower slope to the variable bevel is 5.08 mm, and the distance between the two blunt edges of the groove is 0-0.5 mm. The welding process involves inner root welding, thermal welding, filler welding and cover surface welding.
   USE - Fully-automatic welding process of long-distance pipelines.
   ADVANTAGE - The fully-automatic welding process improves the welding efficiency and quality, and greatly reduces the labor intensity of the worker.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Fully-automatic welding process of long-distance pipelines involves performing groove machining on an end face of pipe orifice of a welding pipeline before welding. The parameters of groove are such that the angle a and angle b of the upper and outer groove is 5-8 degrees , the angle c of the inner angle of the upper and outer groove is 90 degrees , the angle d of the lower inner groove angle is 75 plus minus 1.5 degrees , the height of the blunt edge is 1.27-0.15 mm, the lower inner groove depth is 1.27 plus minus 0.15 mm, the length of slope at the edge of the lower slope to the variable bevel is 5.08 mm, and the distance between the two blunt edges of the groove is 0-0.5 mm. The welding process involves inner root welding, thermal welding, filler welding and cover surface welding. In the inner root welding stage, the wire polarity is a direct current positive connection, the welding current is 250-280 A, the welding voltage is 25-27 V, the wire feeding speed is 10-13.5m/minute, the welding speed is 95-110 cm/minute, the lateral swing amplitude is 0.5-0.7 cm and the proportion of shielding gas is 80% argon and 20% carbon dioxide. In the thermal welding stage, the filler welding and cover welding use a welding wire of 1 mm, the polarity of filling welding stage is a direct current positive connection, the welding current is 230-255 A, the welding voltage is 25-27 V, the wire feeding speed is 9-11 m/minute, the welding speed is 60-70 cm/minute, the lateral swing amplitude is 1.6-2.1 cm and the proportion of shielding gas is 50% argon and 50% carbon dioxide.

   HU H
   CHEN Q
AE UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
GA 202298332U
AB    NOVELTY - Enhancing and sealing up carbon dioxide in the                exhausted shale oil-gas reservoir, involves                selecting shale oil and gas reservoirs with                nanopores rich in barium ions and strontium ions,                and framework rich in calcite minerals (CaCO3). The                carbon dioxide gas is injected into the nanopores                of depleted shale oil and gas reservoirs through                injection wells and fractures. When all the carbon                dioxide is injected into the nanopore, inject an                acidic aqueous solution rich in sulfate ions into                the depleted shale oil and gas reservoir through                injection wells and fractures, and closes the                injection well, so that the acidic aqueous solution                entered the nanopore near the fracture surface by                forced imbibition and spontaneous imbibition. The                sulfate ions entered the nanopore combine with the                barium ions and strontium ions in the nanopore to                form insoluble barium sulfate and strontium sulfate                precipitation, which causes the nanopores near the                crack surface to be gradually blocked.
   USE - Method for enhancing and sealing up carbon                dioxide in exhausted shale oil-gas reservoir.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to enter sulfate ions,                barium ions and strontium ions in the nanopore to                form barium sulfate and strontium sulfate                precipitation, which induces volume expansion of                the framework, resulting in the compression and                closure of the nanopores, and finally reduces the                flow capacity of the nanopores near the fracture                surface, and also avoid leakage of carbon dioxide                in nanopores from returning into the cracks, so as                to achieve the purpose of strengthening carbon                dioxide sequestration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Enhancing and sealing up carbon dioxide in the                exhausted shale oil-gas reservoir, involves                selecting shale oil and gas reservoirs with                nanopores rich in barium ions and strontium ions,                and framework rich in calcite minerals (CaCO3). The                carbon dioxide gas is injected into the nanopores                of depleted shale oil and gas reservoirs through                injection wells and fractures. When all the carbon                dioxide is injected into the nanopore, inject an                acidic aqueous solution rich in sulfate ions into                the depleted shale oil and gas reservoir through                injection wells and fractures, and closes the                injection well, so that the acidic aqueous solution                entered the nanopore near the fracture surface by                forced imbibition and spontaneous imbibition. The                sulfate ions entered the nanopore combine with the                barium ions and strontium ions in the nanopore to                form insoluble barium sulfate and strontium sulfate                precipitation, which causes the nanopores near the                crack surface to be gradually blocked. The                dissolution and in-situ precipitation reactions are                performed, after sulfate radicals and hydrogen ions                in the nano holes are contacted with calcite                minerals, and then converted calcite in the                framework into gypsum (calcium sulfate dihydrate)                and anhydrite (calcium sulfate) with larger molar                volume in situ to precipitate. The expansion of the                framework volume is induced, the nano holes are                compressed and closed, and finally reduced flowing                capacity of the nano holes near the crack surface.                The carbon dioxide in the nano holes are returned                to enter the cracks to avoid cause leakage, and to                achieve purpose of strengthening carbon dioxide                sealing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The concentration of                  barium ions in nanopores of the depleted shale                  oil-gas reservoir is not less than 100 mg/L, and                  the concentration of strontium ions in nanopores                  is not less than 100 mg/L. The volume fraction of                  calcite minerals of the depleted shale oil-gas                  reservoir is 5-20 vol.%. The injection well is a                  shale oil and gas reservoir exploitation well,                  and the fractures are hydraulic fractures and                  natural fractures. The concentration of the                  sulfate ions is not less than 1000 mg/L, and the                  pH value of the acidic aqueous solution is not                  more than 3.

PN CN109054893-A; CN109054893-B
TI System for hydrogenation of wax oil and hydrogen during production of coal, has carbon dioxide separation unit, hydrogen purification unit, wax oil hydrotreating unit, and oil and gas separation unit.
AU XIAO W
   HUANG W
   JIANG X
   RUAN X
   HE G
   LI X
   WU X
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 201900876X
AB    NOVELTY - A hydrogenation system has carbon dioxide separation unit, hydrogen purification unit, wax oil hydrotreating unit, and oil and gas separation unit. The carbon dioxide separation unit includes precision filter, buffer tank (I), carbon membrane separator (I), carbon dioxide product compressor (I), cooler (I), carbon membrane separator (II) and carbon dioxide product compressor (II). The hydrogen purification unit comprises buffer tank (II), cooler (II), a hydrogen membrane separator (12), dehydrogenation stripper (I), and hydrogen product compressor. The wax oil hydrotreating unit includes hydrogen product compressor, hydrogen heater, hydraulic pump, heating furnace, hydrotreating reactor and hydrodesulfurization reactor. The wax oil is sequentially passed through hydraulic pump and heating furnace. The oil and gas separation unit comprises deaerator stripper, hot high-pressure separator, cold high-pressure separator, and hot low-pressure separator.
   USE - System for hydrogenation of wax oil and hydrogen during production of coal.
   ADVANTAGE - The system ensures simple and efficient hydrogenation of wax oil and hydrogen during production of coal. The operation cost of hydrogen purification unit and hydrogen partial pressure can be reduced. The hydrogenation efficiency of wax oil can be improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A hydrogenation system has a carbon dioxide separation unit, a hydrogen purification unit, a wax oil hydrotreating unit, and an oil and gas separation unit. The carbon dioxide separation unit includes precision filter, buffer tank (I), carbon membrane separator (I), carbon dioxide product compressor (I), cooler (I), carbon membrane separator (II) and carbon dioxide product compressor (II). The carbon dioxide separation unit is located at the beginning of the system to perform carbon dioxide separation on the reformed gas. The hydrogen purification unit comprises buffer tank (II), cooler (II), a hydrogen membrane separator (12), dehydrogenation stripper (I), and hydrogen product compressor. The hydrogen purification unit is located after the carbon dioxide separation unit. The recirculated gas produced by the carbon membrane separator (I) of the carbon dioxide separation unit and the circulating hydrogen separated from a cold high-pressure separator of the oil separation unit are mixed in the buffer tank (II). The wax oil hydrotreating unit includes a hydrogen product compressor, a hydrogen heater, a hydraulic pump, a heating furnace, a hydrotreating reactor and a hydrodesulfurization reactor, and is located after the hydrogen purification unit. The wax oil is sequentially passed through hydraulic pump and a heating furnace at 300-400 degrees C and mixed with hydrogen from a hydrogen purification unit and then introduced into a hydrotreating reactor for hydrogenation reaction. The oil and gas separation unit comprises a deaerator stripper, a hot high-pressure separator, a cold high-pressure separator, and a hot low-pressure separator. The oil and gas separation unit is located at the end of the system and is closely connected with the hydrogen purification unit. The cold high-pressure separator separates the fluid catalytic cracking raw oil. The cold high-pressure gas is circulating hydrogen having a molar concentration of 95.48%, and is returned to the hydrogen purification unit. The hot low-pressure oil separated from the hot low-pressure separator is used to separate the crude petrol from the fractionation tower.

Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

The process is carried out with an isocyanate                  index of 75-120.

PN US2022243229-A1
TI Producing fermentation product from            lignocellulosic biomass such as ethanol, isopropanol,            acetone, n-butanol, isobutanol, 1,4-butanediol,            succinic acid, lactic acid, carbon dioxide, or            combinations, involves introducing lignocellulosic            biomass feedstock to single-stage digestor.
AU MONCLIN J
   RUTHERFORD S R
   PYLKKANEN V
   RETSINA T
AE GRANBIO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY HOLDINGS (GRAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022972798
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a fermentation product from                lignocellulosic biomass involves introducing a                lignocellulosic biomass feedstock to a single-stage                digestor, where the feedstock contains cellulose,                hemicellulose, and lignin. The feedstock is exposed                to a reaction solution comprising steam or liquid                hot water within the digestor, to solubilize a                portion of the hemicellulose in a liquid phase and                provide a cellulose-rich solid phase. The                cellulose-rich solid phase is enzymatically                hydrolyzed in a hydrolysis reactor with cellulase                enzymes, to generate a mixture comprising                fermentable sugars from the cellulose-rich solid                phase, where hydrolysis reactor include one or more                hydrolysis stages. The fermentable sugars are                fermented in a fermentor to produce a fermentation                product, where there is no separation of the liquid                phase from the cellulose-rich solid phase.
   USE - Method for producing a fermentation product                from lignocellulosic biomass such as ethanol,                isopropanol, acetone, n-butanol, isobutanol,                1,4-butanediol, succinic acid, lactic acid, carbon                dioxide, or combinations (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provide release of hemicelluloses                from the biomass solid structure, which increases                cellulose accessibility to enzymes, even when the                lignin content remains high and provide optimum                mechanical refining conditions for enhanced                cellulose accessibility to enzymes. The method is                carried out in a cost-effective manner. The                calculated yield of ethanol is this experiment is                57 gallons ethanol per dry ton of starting biomass                (corn stover).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The lignocellulosic                  biomass feedstock is pretreated using one or more                  techniques selected from cleaning, washing,                  presteaming, drying, milling, particle                  size-classifying, or combinations. A portion of                  the reaction solution is introduced to the                  feedstock in a pre-impregnator prior exposure to                  feedstock to a reaction solution. A mechanical                  refiner is utilized to reduce average particle                  size of the cellulose-rich solid phase, before or                  during enzymatically hydrolyzing said                  cellulose-rich solid phase. A mechanical refining                  of the mixture is carried out by first stage of                  enzymatic hydrolysis and utilizing multiple                  mechanical refiners at different portions of the                  process. The mechanical refiners include a first                  mechanical refiner and a second mechanical                  refiner, where the second mechanical refiner is                  operated at a lower refining pressure compared to                  that of the first mechanical refiner. The process                  utilizes a mechanical refiner selected from                  hot-blow refiner, hot-stock refiner, blow-line                  refiner, disk refiner, conical refiner,                  cylindrical refiner, in-line defibrator,                  extruder, homogenizer, or combinations. The                  process employs a blow tank configured for                  receiving the cellulose-rich solid phase at a                  pressure lower than digestor pressure. The vapor                  is separated from the blow tank, and heat is                  recovered from the vapor. The vapor is condensed                  or compressed and returned to the digestor. The                  step of enzymatically hydrolyzing said                  cellulose-rich solid phase utilizes single-stage                  enzymatic hydrolysis configured for cellulose                  liquefaction and saccharification, where the                  single-stage enzymatic hydrolysis includes one or                  more tanks or vessels. A self-cleaning filter is                  configured downstream of the hydrolysis reactor                  to remove cellulosic fiber strands, and the                  cellulosic fiber strands are recycled back to the                  hydrolysis reactor. Preferred Components: The                  reaction solution further comprises an acid. The                  first mechanical refiner is a pressurized refiner                  and the second mechanical refiner is an                  atmospheric refiner.
