PN JP2022066731-A
TI Metal-based fixed catalyst used in manufacture of carboxylic acid and/or carboxylic acid ester, comprises site in which metal complex is immobilized on inorganic oxide via linker and ligand.
AU OKADA M
   ISHIDA S
   OKU T
   CUI Z Z
AE NIPPON SHOKUBAI CO LTD (JAPC-C)
   NAT INST ADVANCED IND SCI & TECHNOLOGY (NIIT-C)
GA 202263098V
AB    NOVELTY - A metal-based fixed catalyst comprises a site in which a metal complex is immobilized on an inorganic oxide via a linker and a ligand.
   USE - Metal-based fixed catalyst is used in manufacture of carboxylic acid and/or carboxylic acid ester (claimed) used as raw materials for various industrial products and daily necessities.
   ADVANTAGE - The metal-based fixed catalyst enables easy, economical and large-scale manufacture of carboxylic acid and/or carboxylic acid ester using carbon dioxide or formic acid as a raw material, with high yield and productivity, and without using triphenylphosphine.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) manufacture (P1) of the metal-based fixed catalyst, which involves reacting a metal complex of formula: L1MXm (I) with a carrier comprising linker and ligand to fix the metal complex; and
   (2) manufacture (P2) of carboxylic acid and/or carboxylic acid ester of formula (III), which involves reacting an alkene compound of formula (II) with formic acid and/or formic acid ester in the presence of the metal-based fixed catalyst, or reacting compound (II) with hydrogen and carbon dioxide in the presence of the metal-based fixed catalyst.
   L = ligand containing atoms of groups 14-16 of the periodic table;
   l,m = 0-8;
   M = metal atom;
   X = H, halo, alkyl, or hydroxy;and
   R1-R3 = H or 1-24C organic group.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The inorganic oxide is an oxide of any one of silicon, aluminum, titanium, and zirconium, or a composite oxide containing two or more of oxides. The metal complex contains atom(s) chosen from group 8-12 in the periodic table and group including chromium. The number of moles of metal atoms contained in the fixed metal complex is 5-250 mmol with respect to a weight of 100 g of carrier.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The linker is an alkylene group, an arylene group, or a group formed by combining these groups, preferably methylene group, ethylene group, propylene group, phenylene group or group formed by combining these groups. The ligand comprises a phosphorus atom, a nitrogen atom, an oxygen atom and/or a sulfur atom. Preferred Process: In the process (P2), the reaction is performed by flow reaction method using a fixed-bed flow reactor.
   EXAMPLE - 10 g 2-Diphenylphosphinoethyl-functionalized silica gel having a phosphorus atom content of 1.18 mmol-phosphorus/g and a particle size of 38-75 mu m as immobilized carrier and 250 g dehydrated toluene were mixed and stirred to obtain a homogeneous suspension. While stirring the suspension, 0.765 g rhodium carbonyl chloride (Rh2Cl2(CO)4) and 70 g dehydrated toluene were slowly added, and the obtained solution was stirred at room temperature for 15 hours, reacted and aged. While stirring the aged suspension, reaction was performed at 80 degrees C for 3 hours. The obtained product was separated into solid and liquid by vacuum filtration, and the obtained solid was washed with 100 g dehydrated toluene. The washed solid was dried under reduced pressure at room temperature for 15 hours, and dried under reduced pressure at 80 degrees C for 3 hours to obtain a rhodium-based catalyst. 0.33 g Cyclohexene, 0.65 g formic acid, 0.13 g p-toluenesulfonic acid monohydrate and 4.46 g acetic acid were mixed and stirred. The obtained mixed solution and the rhodium-based catalyst were added to the pressure-resistant reactor, and the reactor was sealed. The sealed reactor was heated at 180 degrees C in an aluminum block type electric furnace, and the reaction was performed for 2.5 hours. The reaction product was cooled to obtain cyclohexanecarboxylic acid as main product, and cyclohexane, iodocyclohexane and acetoxycyclohexane as by-products.

The mixed gas is passed into the catalytic material purification unit, ozone is catalyzed into superoxide free radical under the action of the catalyst in the catalytic material purification unit, and the remaining organic waste gas is combined with superoxide radicals to be oxidized into carbon dioxide and water. AB    NOVELTY - A carbon-coated low-melting-point metal                nanomaterial preparing method involves taking                carbonate molten salt as electrolyte, under the                inert atmosphere containing carbon dioxide, taking                low melting point metal as electrode, applying                oxidation current, applying reduction current,                washing and drying to obtain the powder of                carbon-coated low melting point metal ball core,                where the carbonate is lithium carbonate, sodium                carbonate, and/or potassium carbonate, the inert                atmosphere is argon, nitrogen or helium, and the                method adopts two electrodes, one of which is                low-melting-point metal, and other is graphite,                metal, alloy, stannic oxide, perovskite ceramic,                platinum or gold, and the low melting point metal                is tin, bismuth, antimony, lead, indium, and                gallium.

   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple steps and mild reaction                conditions, the morphology and structure of the                product can be adjusted by parameters e.g. reaction                time, temperature and electrolysis current, avoids                the difficult control of the process when the                organic matter is violently pyrolyzed in the                existing method and the additional process required                to prepare the organic matter, and realizes the                modification and value-added of low melting point                metals and the emission reduction and value-added                conversion of the greenhouse gas carbon                dioxide.

PN CN111632614-A
TI Photocatalyst used for photocatalytic degrading water pollutants, photocatalysis of carbon dioxide and in greenhouse gas treatment, comprises nickel aluminide-layered double hydroxides/titanium carbide powder nanosheets.
AU ZHANG H
   HE J
   ZOU Y
   HUANG J
   YANG Y
   SHEN J
AE HUBEI ZHENRUN ENVIRONMENTAL SCI & TECHNO (HUBE-Non-standard)
   UNIV NANJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNSC-C)
GA 202088353H
AB    NOVELTY - Photocatalyst comprises nickel aluminide-layered double hydroxides (NiAl-LDH)/titanium carbide powder nanosheets, where the NiAl-LDH constitutes three-dimensional (3D) structure framework, and two-dimensional (2D) titanium carbide nanosheets, which are supported on the surface of the 3D NiAl-LDH sheet to form 3D petal-like hierarchical structure.
   USE - The photocatalyst is useful for photocatalytic degrading water pollutants, in photocatalysis of carbon dioxide (all claimed) and in greenhouse gas treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst has high specific surface area, photon absorption utilization rate and photogenerated electron and hole separation efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing photocatalyst involving (i) dispersing the titanium carbide in deionized water, ultrasonically treating for 1-3 hours, centrifuging and taking the supernatant to obtain two-dimensional titanium carbide suspension, (ii) dissolving nickel nitrate and aluminum nitrate in deionized water, and adding ammonium fluoride and urea to stir and dissolve to obtain uniform mixed precursor solution, and (iii) dripping the two-dimensional titanium carbide suspension obtained in the step (i) to the mixed precursor solution obtained in the step (ii) under vigorous stirring, and stirring at room temperature for 0.5-1 hours, hydrothermally reacting the resulting mixed solution at 120 plus minus 10 degrees C for 18-24 hours, naturally cooling to normal temperature, centrifugally separating, washing, and vacuum drying to obtain the composite photocatalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The size of the composite photocatalyst is 3-7 mu m. In step (i), the mass concentration of the two-dimensional titanium carbide suspension is 15 mg/ml. In step (ii), the molar concentration of nickel nitrate in the mixed precursor solution is 0.05-0.1 mol/l, the molar ratio of aluminum nitrate to nickel nitrate is 1:3, the molar ratio of ammonium fluoride to nickel nitrate is 5:1-10:1, and the molar ratio of urea to nickel nitrate is 10:1-15:1. The mass ratio of titanium carbide to nickel nitrate is 1:2-1:5. Preferred Condition: In step (iii), the vacuum drying process is carried out at 60 degrees C for 6-12 hours.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing polycarbonates/acrylonitrile butadiene styrene alloy material, which involves forming other materials than plasticizer in raw materials, placing into premixer to mix uniformly, heating to 60-80 degrees C and holding for 30-120 minutes to obtain premix, adding premix obtained to the twin screw extruder, and injecting the plasticizer through the gear pump, injecting water/carbon dioxide/nitrogen in the middle section of the extruder, pumping out vacuum suction section water/carbon dioxide/nitrogen, vacuum pressure in the vacuum pumping section is (-)0.05-(-)0.1 megapascal, cutting pellets to obtain PC/ABS pellets, screw speed of the twin screw extruder is 200-400 revolution per minute, barrel temperature is 200-290 degrees C, drying PC/ABS particles and then introducing into single screw extruder, drawing by tractor, traction winding speed is 10-120m/minute, cooling water temperature is 40-95 degrees C.

The compatibilizer includes blend of maleic anhydride grafted ABS and styrene, and cyclic anhydride grafted ABS. The impact modifier includes natural rubber, styrene, acrylonitrile-butadiene rubber, styrene-based thermoplastic elastomer, and thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer. The inorganic filler includes barium sulfate, glass microspheres, titanium dioxide, attapulgite, boron nitride, calcium carbonate, talc and clay. Preferred Conditions: The PC is bisphenol A polycarbonate with molecular weight of 20,000-40000g/mol and melt flow rate of 5-20g/minute. The ABS resin is acrylonitrile-butadiene-styrene terpolymer produced by polymerization using bulk method, emulsion method or suspension method. The plasticizer comprises odorless, environmentally friendly medium and high molecular weight polyester with molecular weight of 1500-5000 and viscosity of 1500-8000cps.

PN CN110127742-A
TI Preparation of nano calcium carbonate useful for            aqueous paint comprises e.g. preparing calcium            hydroxide slurry suspension, adding crystalline form            control agent, carrying out carbonization, and aging            nano-calcium carbonate slurry.
AU ZHONG T
   JIANG X
AE XUANCHENG XINWEIHUA CHEM TECH CO LTD (XUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019727616
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of nano calcium carbonate                comprises (i) preparing calcium hydroxide slurry                into a suspension having mass concentration of                90-120% and adding 0.1-3% crystalline form control                agent, carrying out carbonization by introducing a                gas containing carbon dioxide at 10-45 degrees C,                maintaining content of carbon dioxide gas between                20-35%, when the carbonization rate reaches to                20-55%, adding 0.01-1.5% dispersant, and                continuously carbonizing until pH is 6.5-7, (ii)                preparing surface treating agent into aqueous                solution having mass concentration of 5-15%, and                raising temperature to 60-90 degrees C to obtain                surface treating agent aqueous solution, (iii)                aging carbonized nano-calcium carbonate slurry in                an aging device, (iv) adding 2-6% surface treating                agent aqueous solution into aged nano-calcium                carbonate slurry, stirring uniformly, and coating                for 0.5-3.5 hours, and (v) filtering surface                treated nano-calcium carbonate slurry until solid                content is 20-30%.
   USE - The preparation of nano calcium carbonate is                useful for aqueous paint (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nano-calcium carbonate: has complete cube                shape, average grain diameter of 60-80 nm, specific                surface area of more than 26 m2/g, uniform particle                distribution, no agglomeration, hydrophilic and                lipophilic properties, good dispersibility, and                good binder property in aqueous paints; improves                rheology and opening effect of coatings; and                improves water resistance, scrub resistance and                reduces raw material cost.

The dispersing agent is                  silicate, alkali metal phosphate, polyacrylamide                  and/or polyethylene glycol fatty acid ester. The                  surface treating agent is at least two anionic                  surfactants.

PN US2019218687-A1; WO2019143851-A1; US10450677-B2
TI Manufacturing biocompatible capillary by dissolving polyethylene carbonate in chloroform, drawing fiber, depositing parylene by chemical vapor deposition on fiber, and heating to melt and partially decompose polyethylene carbonate.
AU CHOU T
   TAI Y
   PI Y
AE CALIFORNIA INST OF TECHNOLOGY (CALY-C)
   CALIFORNIA INST OF TECHNOLOGY (CALY-C)
   PI Y (PIYY-Individual)
GA 201962200L
AB    NOVELTY - A biocompatible capillary is manufactured by dissolving polyethylene carbonate (PEC) in chloroform to create liquid solution (102) with mass fraction of 12-25% PEC to chloroform; drawing PEC fiber (112) by contacting a point with liquid solution, pulling point away from solution, and allowing chloroform to evaporate to solidify drawn PEC fiber; depositing parylene by chemical vapor deposition on PEC fiber to coat PEC fiber; and heating coated PEC fiber to temperature between 180 degrees C and melting point of parylene to melt PEC and partially decompose PEC into gaseous carbon dioxide, such that gaseous carbon dioxide pushes molten PEC out of greater than or equal to 1 end of coated PEC fiber to leave a hollow parylene capillary.
   USE - Manufacture of biocompatible capillary.
   ADVANTAGE - The high-temperature self-expelling mechanism for molten PEC inside the capillaries greatly reduces the removal of the sacrificial fiber to tens of minutes. The introduction of a sacrificial polymer of PEC in a fiber drawing step leads to better results i.e., the length is extended from a millimeters in the prior art to tens of centimeters, an improvement in length of greater than or equal to 100 times.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of polyethylene carbonate (PEC) fiber with the seed.
   Liquid solution (102)
   Glass slide (104)
   Glass tube (106)
   Meniscus (108)
   PEC fiber (112)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: Parylene is selected from parylene N, parylene C, parylene D, parylene HT, parylene AF-4, and parylene F.

TI Manufacture of high-calorific synthetic natural gas involves producing synthesis gas comprising carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen, and carrying out methane synthesis using methane discharged from adiabatic reactor.
AU KIM J
   KOH D
   KIM J W
   KOH D J
   JUN-WOO K
   JUN K
   JIN J
   GAO D
AE RES INST IND SCI & TECHNOLOGY (POSC-C)
GA 201950384S
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of high-calorific synthetic natural gas involves reacting coal or biomass with oxygen and/or hydrogen to produce a synthesis gas comprising carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen, separating and supplying the synthesis gas into an isothermal reactor (200) and a adiabatic reactor (300), producing a hydrocarbon-compound-containing gas by Fischer-Tropsch reaction in the isothermal reactor, supplying methane to reactor (300) through 5C or more hydrocarbon compound discharged from the isothermal reactor to produce primary methane, supplying the methane discharged from reactor (300) to an adiabatic reactor (400) to synthesize secondary methane, supplying the gas discharged from reactor (400) to an adiabatic reactor (500), and supplying the gas discharged from reactor (500) and 1-4C hydrocarbon compound discharged from the isothermal reactor to an adiabatic reactor (600).
   USE - Manufacture of high-calorific synthetic natural gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively produces synthetic natural gas having a high caloric value of 10200 kcal/Nm3 ( plus minus 200 kcal/Nm3).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for synthetic natural gas manufacturing device, which has a separator (100) for separating synthesis gas, the isothermal reactor in which a portion of the synthesis gas is supplied by the separator to produce the hydrocarbon-compound-containing gas, and reactors (300)-(600).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the synthetic natural gas manufacturing device.
   Separator (100)
   Isothermal reactor (200)
   Adiabatic reactors (300,400,500,600)
   Condenser (700)
   Heat exchanger (1000)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The hydrocarbon-reforming catalyst comprises at least one chosen from nickel, rhodium, palladium and copper supported on at least one support chosen from alumina, cerium oxide and cerium zironate of formula: CeZrOx (I). The volume ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide in the synthesis gas is 2-3.5. The hydrocarbon compound synthesis catalyst further comprises at least one cocatalyst chosen from cobalt and manganese, and at least one cocatalyst chosen from ruthenium, iron, and molybdenum as a main catalyst. The alumina support comprises 5-20 wt.% cobalt, 1-6 wt.% manganese and 0.1-2 wt.% ruthenium. The methane synthesis catalyst is chosen from nickel, aluminum and zirconium, and further comprises a cocatalyst chosen from iron, copper, lanthanum and magnesium.x = not defined.N.B: x is not defined.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Manufacture of high-calorific synthetic natural gas further involves feeding 5C or more hydrocarbon compound discharged from the isothermal reactor to an endothermic furnace, and modifying the hydrocarbon compound discharged from the isothermal reactor at 400-800 degrees C in presence of a hydrocarbon-reforming catalyst. Preferred Conditions: The hydrocarbon compound is produced at a temperature of 240-400 degrees C in presence of a hydrocarbon compound synthesis catalyst. The methane is produced at a temperature of 280-720 degrees C in presence of a methane synthesis catalyst. Preferred Composition: The synthesis gas is supplied to the isothermal reactor in an amount of 10-70 vol.%.

A gap regulator                comprises sustained release agent and swelling                agent capable of releasing carbon dioxide in a                weight ratio of 1:0.5-0.8.
   USE - Used as recycled concrete.
   ADVANTAGE - The concrete: improves the workability of                concrete through fly ash and blast furnace slag;                reduces the voids generated after evaporation of                water; improves the internal structure of concrete;                reinforces the strength of concrete; and improves                the level of impermeability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the recycled concrete, comprising (i)                preparing recycled aggregate, crushing waste                concrete block, removing steel bar and sieving to                obtain recycled aggregate,(ii) pre-treating the                recycled aggregate, placing the recycled aggregate                in a closed container and carbonizing, controlling                the relative humidity of 50-75%, carbon dioxide                concentration of 65-85%, until the weight of the                recycled aggregate without carbonization is less                than or equal to 5%, and(iii) mixing the treated                recycled aggregate obtained in the step (ii) with                other components and stirring uniformly to                obtainrecycled concrete.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sustained release                  agent is aluminosilicate subjected to carbon                  dioxide immersion treatment. The swelling agent                  includes calcium oxide, calcium sulfoaluminate                  and magnesium oxide in a ratio of 1:1-3:1.5-3.                  The recycled concrete further comprises                  reinforcing fiber group. The reinforcing fiber                  group includes 5-15 pts. wt. polyparaphenylene                  terephthalamide fiber. The recycled aggregate has                  particle size of 5-40 mm. The recycled concrete                  further comprises air entraining agent including                  of 43-66 pts. wt. differential saponin. Preferred                  Method: In the step (i), taking recycled                  aggregate, sequentially subjecting to magnetic                  separation, air separating and washing and                  subjecting dust removal treatment to obtain                  qualified recycled aggregate and participate in                  subsequent processes. In the step (i), taking                  part is ground into a powder and uniformly                  spreading, and uniformly spraying phenolphthalein                  indicator. In the step (iii), maintaining the                  components in a positive pressure environment                  while stirring.

PN CN107973296-A
TI Preparing large pore size activated carbon comprises e.g. smashing the activated carbon raw material, performing pretreatment of activated carbon, adding surfactant, preparing pretreated activated carbon and activating.
AU LIN P
   LIN K
   HUANG Y
   XU Q
   LIN W
AE FUJIAN XINSEN CARBON IND CO LTD (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 201835153X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing large pore size activated carbon comprises e.g. (i) smashing the activated carbon raw material, immersing raw material in ethanol solution, and adding hydroxide into the ethanol solution as pore-enlarging agent, washing and drying, (ii) performing pretreatment of activated carbon, adding surfactant, stirring for 2-3 hours, (iii) heating under nitrogen protection for 2-4 hours, heating at 300-500 degrees to obtain pretreated activated carbon, introducing nitrogen into mixture of water vapor and carbon dioxide, and activating at 700-800 degrees for 1-3 hours to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing large pore size activated carbon.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing large pore size activated carbon comprises (i) smashing the activated carbon raw material, immersing raw material in ethanol solution, and adding hydroxide into the ethanol solution as pore-enlarging agent, performing ultrasonic vibration, washing with water and drying to obtain activated carbon, (ii) performing pretreatment of activated carbon, adding surfactant at 80-100 degrees C for 7-9 hours, stirring for 2-3 hours, (iii) heating under nitrogen protection for 2-4 hours, heating at 300-500 degrees to obtain pretreated activated carbon, introducing nitrogen into mixture of water vapor and carbon dioxide, and activating at 700-800 degrees for 1-3 hours to obtain final product. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for large-pore-size activated carbon obtained by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The weight ratio between pretreatment activated carbon and surfactant is 1:1-2. The molar concentration of surfactant is 10-15 mmol/l. The surfactant is cetylpyridinium chloride. The frequency of ultrasonic waves is 60-70 Hz and the oscillation time is 1-2 hours. The hydroxide is 1-2 mol/l sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide. The activated carbon raw material is wood activated carbon or coconut shell activated carbon.

AU FRANK M
   MARQUARDT D

   MEYER P
   BABKINA T
AE BOSCH GMBH ROBERT (BOSC-C)
GA 201826316Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves measuring infrared radiation from a solid angle range by an infrared detector array of an infrared measuring system (10). A two-dimensional temperature information is determined from measured infrared radiation. The distance information between measuring system and a scene is determined. The temperature information is corrected by measurement error. The distance information is determined as two-dimensional distance information in solid angle range. The temperature information is corrected by distance-dependent measurement error.
   USE - Method for non-contact determination of thermal image of scene through infrared measuring system (claimed) e.g. hand-held thermal imaging camera used for safety inspection of electrical circuit, for troubleshooting in machine processes or for identification of insufficient heat insulation in context of thermal and/or cold insulation.
   ADVANTAGE - The two-dimensional temperature information of scene is corrected accurately, since two-dimensional temperature information is corrected by distance-dependent measurement error. The determination of ambient temperature and/or ambient air humidity and/or carbon dioxide concentration of the surrounding air are realized in simple manner. The determination cost of ambient temperature and/or ambient air humidity and/or carbon dioxide concentration of the surrounding air is reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an infrared measuring system.

PN CN107805626-A
TI Recovering human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells, by collecting human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells in logarithmic growth phase, thawing culture solution, transferring cells to resuscitation solution, and incubating cells.
AU DENG Y
   WANG X
   ZHU C
AE WUHAN BEIDU BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 201823087S
AB    NOVELTY - Method for recovering human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells involves collecting human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells in logarithmic growth phase, storing culture solution in refrigerator, thawing the solution, preparing resuscitation solution by mixing logarithmic growth phase human umbilical mesenchymal stem cell culture medium with fresh mesenchymal stem cell culture medium, incubating the cells in water bath, transferring cells to culture bottle containing resuscitation solution, incubating cells in a carbon dioxide incubator, and adding fresh culture medium.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform serum-free cryopreservation of the human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells, and increases cell survival rate to 90% or more without damage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for recovering human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells involves collecting human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells in logarithmic growth phase, storing culture solution at -80 degrees C in refrigerator, thawing the solution at 4 degrees C before 2 days of recovery, preparing resuscitation solution by mixing logarithmic growth phase human umbilical mesenchymal stem cell culture medium with fresh mesenchymal stem cell culture medium, incubating the cells in 37-40 degrees C water bath in a freezing tube, shaking the tube during thawing process, transferring cells to culture bottle containing resuscitation solution, incubating cells at 37 degrees C in a carbon dioxide incubator, removing the culture medium after 24 hours and adding fresh culture medium.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The resuscitation solution is prepared by mixing logarithmic growth phase human umbilical mesenchymal stem cell culture medium with fresh mesenchymal stem cell culture medium in a ratio of 1:1-1:2. Preferred Component: The volume of resuscitation solution is at least 2 times greater than volume of culture bottle.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PN KR1825152-B1
TI Cement mortar composition useful for repairing concrete structures, comprises functional binder, fine aggregate, performance modifier and water.
AU KANG D K
   JUNG H H
AE KANG D K (KANG-Individual)
GA 201813191C
AB    NOVELTY - Cement mortar composition comprises 3-45 wt.% functional binder, 5-80 wt.% fine aggregate, 0.1-30 wt.% performance modifier and 0.1-30 wt.% water. The performance modifier comprises 25-98 wt.% vinyl acetate-vinyl chloride copolymer, 1-30 wt.% dimethylaminoethyl acrylate, 0.1-25 wt.% 2-ethylhexyl acrylate, 0.1-25 wt.% methyl methacrylate-acrylonitrile-butadiene-styrene copolymer, 0.01-25 wt.% ethylene-methyl acrylate copolymer, 0.01-10 wt.% hydroxybutyl polyester and 0.01-10 wt.% polyethylene glycol ester-polyethyleneimine copolymer.
   USE - The composition is useful for repairing concrete structures (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition prevents flexural strength, tensile strength and plastic cracking of structures, and improves adhesion, surface hardening, durability, improve freeze-thaw resistance and weather resistance of concrete structures, and prevents penetration of harmful substances including water, chlorine ions and carbon dioxide to concrete structures.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for repairing concrete structures, comprising (a) removing and cleaning the deteriorated portion, runtane or impurities of the concrete structure, (b) applying penetration type surface strengthening agent to improve the adhesion, surface hardening and durability of the concrete structures and the cement mortar composition on the cleaned site, (c) placing the cement mortar composition on the top of the penetration type surface strengthening agent, (d) curing the surface of the concrete structure, and (e) applying surface protective agent to prevent penetration of harmful substances such as water, chlorine ions, and carbon dioxide and improve freeze-thaw resistance and weather resistance after curing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The functional binder comprises 5-50 wt.% crude steel Portland cement, 5-40 wt.% alumina cement, 0.1-30 wt.% fine powder of blast furnace slag, 0.01-15 wt.% zinc oxide, 0.01-15 wt.% aluminum nitride, 0.01-10 wt.% bentonite, 0.01-10 wt.% zirconium, 0.01-10 wt.% chromium chloride, 0.01-10 wt.% retardant and 0.01-10 wt.% defoaming agent. The functional binder further comprises 0.01-10 wt.% high-performance water reducing agent to ensure reduction in water-cement ratio and workability, 0.01-10 wt.% inorganic pigment to express color and 0.001-10 wt.% hydrophilic fibers to prevent flexural strength, tensile strength and plastic cracking. The fine aggregate comprises 30-99 wt.% silica, 0.1-40 wt.% sericite, 0.1-35 wt.% phyllite and 0.1-35 wt.% vermiculite.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The hydrophilic fiber is produced by mixing nylon fiber, polypropylene fiber, polyester fiber and polyvinyl alcoholfiber.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AU ZHENG Q

   HUANG X

   USE - Clove essential oil used as bactericidal and/or bacterial resistant medicine, food, health products or cosmetics for inhibiting or killing drug-resistant Staphylococcus, Escherichia coli, Enterococcus faecium, Klebsiella, Pseudomonas, Acinetobacter baumannii, drug bowel ball bacteria and type-B hemolytic Streptococcus (all claimed).

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The preparation of clove essential oil comprises crushing cloves, adding 10 times distilled water, heating material liquid by water vapor distillation for 1 hour, extracting volatile oil, distilling for more than 3 hours, cooling volatile oil and aromatic water, settling overnight, collecting volatile oil, adding anhydrous sodium sulfate, and dehydrating.
   ACTIVITY - Antibacterial.

AU FUKAYA K
   PUTRO W S
   CHOI J C
   TAKEUCHI K

   MATSUMOTO S
   HAMURA S
AE NAT INST ADVANCED IND SCI & TECHNOLOGY (NIIT-C)
   TOSOH CORP (TOYJ-C)
GA 2021E3062T
AB    NOVELTY - Production of organic carbonate involves reacting alkoxysilanes with carbon dioxide.
   USE - Production of organic carbonate used as raw material for manufacturing polycarbonate or polyurethane, and for electrolyte of lithium ion battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces organic carbonate using carbon dioxide as a raw material and without producing water as by-product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal catalyst comprises one or more metal elements chosen from group 4 metal elements and group 14 metal elements.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves reacting alkoxysilanes with carbon dioxide at a temperature of 120-200 degrees C in presence of at least one metal catalyst in a reactor, in which the initial pressure of carbon dioxide is 0.1-10 MPa at 25 degrees C. Preferred Components: The metal catalyst is a metal alkoxide. The metal alkoxide is at least one chosen from zirconium alkoxide, titanium alkoxide and tin alkoxide. The alkoxysilane is of formula: (R2)4-aSi(OR1)a (I) or (II).R1,R2 = optionally substituted hydrocarbyl, in which two R1 are combined to form a ring;a = 1-4;R3 = optionally substituted hydrocarbyl, in which carbon is replaced with silicon atom;b = 1-3;m = 2-20;andor R1+R2 = ring.
   EXAMPLE - 3.3 g of 15.8 mmol tetraethoxysilane and 0.3 g tetraethoxyzirconium were added in an autoclave. The mixture was heated, and reacted for 24 hours. The reaction product mixture was cooled, and analyzed to obtain diethyl carbonate with a yield of 51%.

GA 2022D5056E
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to equipment for the                arrangement of an oil field. A method for                utilization of associated petroleum gases is                proposed, in which the first and second transported                process modules are connected to the process line                and the associated petroleum gases are burned in                the electric generator set of the first module with                an enriched or stoichiometric mixture. Next,                oxygen-free flue gases are fed to the condensing                heat exchanger of the second process module to cool                the gas mixture to a temperature below the dew                point and separate water in the form of a liquid                phase. Then the resulting chemically neutral                mixture, consisting mainly of carbon dioxide and                nitrogen, is sent to the compressor station of the                second process module and then the said chemically                neutral mixture is pumped into the oil                reservoir.
   USE - Oil industry.
   ADVANTAGE - Improving the environmental situation by                eliminating flaring and creating a unified                transportable module for processing and disposal of                flue gases, as well as improving the safety and                prevention of acidification of petroleum products                in the reservoir by pumping an oxygen-free gas                mixture into the well.

PN CN114314739-A
TI Activating periodate to remove organic pollutants with glow discharge plasma involves adding a certain amount of phosphate and periodate to the organic waste water, and stirring to make a solution, placed in a glow discharge plasma generator, and applied a certain direct current voltage.
AU ZHANG C
   YANG H
AE UNIV LIAONING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (ULST-C)
GA 202256245H
AB    NOVELTY - Activating periodate to remove organic pollutants with glow discharge plasma involves adding a certain amount of phosphate and periodate to the organic waste water, and stirring to make a solution. The solution is placed in a glow discharge plasma generator, and applied a certain direct current (DC) voltage and current to induce glow discharge in the solution to form plasma. The high-energy active substances generated in the plasma area enter the solution to activate the periodate and generated strong oxidants so that the organic pollutants in the wastewater are oxidized and removed. The content of organic pollutants in the organic wastewater is reduced to meet the treatment requirements.
   USE - Method for activating periodate to remove organic pollutants with glow discharge plasma
   ADVANTAGE - The organic matter in the system can be degraded into carbon dioxide, water and simple organic matter, no transition metal ions such as iron and cobalt are introduced, and the removal rate of organic matter in waste water can reach 100%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The high-energy active material is high-energy anions, high-energy cations or high-energy electrons. The organic pollutants include halogenated organic pollutants such as 3-chlorophenol and tetrabromobisphenol (A). The periodate is sodium periodate or potassium periodate. The phosphate is a glow discharge electrolyte, which is composed of phosphoric acid, sodium dihydrogen phosphate and sodium phosphate in a certain proportion.

PN CN113941358-A
TI Preparing catalytic material useful for degrading formaldehyde, comprises e.g. adding mesoporous molecular sieve into manganese nitrate solution, stirring, mixing uniformly, adding pH regulator, adding tungsten hexachloride solution, stirring, reacting, allowing to stand, collecting, and calcining.
AU BAI Y
   SHI Y
   LI Y
   ZHANG C
   ZHANG H
   YU X
   DU J
   LI J
   WAN X
   YIN H
   BAI P
   ZHU S
   ZHANG M
   WANG J
AE HENAN ACAD SCI (HENA-Non-standard)
   HENAN BUILDING MATERIAL RES & DESIGN CO (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 202219286N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing catalytic material comprises (1) adding the mesoporous molecular sieve into the manganese nitrate solution, stirring and mixing uniformly to obtain mixed solution, adding pH regulator into the mixed solution, adjusting the pH of the mixed solution to alkaline, adding tungsten hexachloride solution into the mixed solution, stirring, reacting, allowing to stand, reacting, and collecting the precipitate, and (2) washing and drying the precipitate, and calcining at 400- 500 for 3-6 hours to obtain catalytic material.
   USE - The catalytic material is useful in degradation of polluted gas i.e., degradation of formaldehyde at room temperature (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces degradation temperature of formaldehyde, has high degradation efficiency, and solves problem that the reaction conditions of manganese oxides alone to degrade formaldehyde are harsh, and problems of formaldehyde degradation can only be realized at higher temperature. The manganese oxide has synergistic effect with tungsten to catalyze the degradation of formaldehyde to generate carbon dioxide and water at room temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for catalytic material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (1), the molar ratio of manganese nitrate to tungsten hexachloride in the mixed solution is 1: (0.05-0.4). In the step (1), adjusting the pH of the mixed solution to 9-10.5 using pH regulator, where the pH regulator is aqueous ammonia. The tungsten hexachloride solution is prepared by dissolving tungsten hexachloride in ethanol. In the step (1), the time for allowing to stand is 8-16 hours. In the step (2), the temperature for the drying treatment is 70-90degrees Celsius. The preparation method of the mesoporous molecular sieve MCM-41 is as follows: (a) subjecting the bentonite to calcination and activation treatment, adding sodium hydroxide into the calcined and activated bentonite, grinding and mixing, and calcining at 450-550degrees Celsius to obtain molten material, cooling the melt to room temperature, adding water, stirring and mixing uniformly, centrifuging, and collecting the supernatant, (b) dropping the supernatant into the aqueous solution of cetyltrimethylammonium bromide, where the molar ratio of silicon dioxide and hexadecyl trimethyl ammonium bromide in the system 1:0.2, adjusting the pH of the system to 9.5-10.5, performing aging treatment for 1-3 hours, transferring the system to the reaction kettle to carry out hydrothermal reaction at 105-120degrees Celsius, where the reaction time is 20-28 hours, reacting, filtering, collecting the filter residue, washing the filter residue to neutrality, drying and grinding to obtain original mesoporous molecular sieve powder, and (c) calcining the mesoporous molecular sieve raw powder at 150-250degrees Celsius for 1-4 hours, and calcining at 500-650degrees Celsius for 6-10 hours to obtain mesoporous molecular sieve MCM-41. In the step (a), the calcination and activation treatment temperature is 600-700degrees Celsius, the time is 3-6 hours, the mass ratio of bentonite to sodium hydroxide is: 1:1.2, and the calcining time is 1-3 hours.Preferred Components: The mesoporous molecular sieve is mesoporous molecular sieve MCM-41.

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The fine aggregate is                  machine-made sand having a grain diameter of                  800-1000 mesh and an apparent density of                  1800-2000 kg/cm3. AU LU J
   HE C
   ZHANG B
AE UNIV CHINA GEOSCIENCES BEIJING (UYGJ-C)
GA 2022054342
AB    NOVELTY - Continuously removing hexavalent chromium from groundwater by microorganisms using synthetic gas comprises mixing synthesis gas and hexavalent chromium polluted underground water to obtain hexavalent chromium polluted water containing synthesis gas, where the synthesis gas comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide and introducing the hexavalent chromium-contaminated water body containing synthesis gas into a reactor inoculated with microorganisms to carry out the hexavalent chromium reduction reaction, where during the hexavalent chromium reduction reaction the microorganism reduces the hexavalent chromium to trivalent chromium using the synthesis gas as an electron donor.
   USE - The device is useful for removing hexavalent chromium in groundwater (claimed).

The reactor is filled with a mixture of soil sediment and quartz sand and the microorganisms are indigenous microorganisms in the soil sediment. The ratio of soil sediment and quartz sand is 1:10. The initial concentration of hexavalent chromium in the hexavalent chromium polluted groundwater is 0.25 mM.Preferred Method: The method comprises filling the synthesis gas in an airbag into the hexavalent chromium polluted underground water in a aeration bottle through an air pump to prepare hexavalent chromium polluted water containing synthesis gas, introducing the hexavalent chromium polluted water body containing the synthesis gas utilizing a peristaltic pump into the reactor to carry out the hexavalent chromium reduction reaction, where during the reduction reaction of hexavalent chromium, the microorganism uses the synthesis gas as an electron donor to reduce the hexavalent chromium to trivalent chromium and the hexavalent chromium-contaminated water body that has undergone the hexavalent chromium reduction reaction flows into a water collecting bottle.

PT P
PN WO2021230811-A1
TI Metal organic framework composition having university of Texas at San Antonio-16 structure useful for capturing carbon monoxide, comprises e.g. chromium, manganese and iron as first metal, and cadmium, manganese, and zinc as second metal.
AU ZHAO D
   PEH S B
   KARMAKAR A
AE UNIV SINGAPORE NAT (UNUS-C)
GA 2021D0685Y
AB    NOVELTY - Metal organic framework (MOF) composition having university of Texas at San Antonio-16 (UTSA-16) structure, comprises 0-80 mol.% first metal comprising chromium, manganese, iron, nickel, copper, and cobalt, and 20-100 mol.% second metal consisting of cadmium, manganese, and zinc.
   USE - The metal organic framework composition is useful for capturing carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The MOFs can be conveniently prepared and formed under milder conditions (e.g. lower temperature or pressure), uses a reflux synthetic process as compared to a solvothermal process to reduce equipment cost, safety compliance cost and energy cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) forming (M1) metal organic framework, comprising aging a mixture comprising from a first metal precursor, a second metal precursor, a base, citric acid, a first solvent and a second solvent for a specific period of time at a temperature of 15-200 degrees C; and
   (2) capturing (M2) carbon monoxide comprising a step of exposing a material containing a MOF to an environment containing carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The second metal is zinc. The second metal is present at a concentration of 25-100 mol.%, preferably 50-75 mol.% of the total metal present in the MOF. The first metal is iron and cobalt, preferably cobalt. The first metal is present at a concentration of 25-50 mol.% of the total metal present in the MOF. The second metal preferentially occupies a tetrahedral metal site within the MOF. The majority of the first metal, when present, occupies an octahedral metal site within the MOF. The MOF has: saturated carbon dioxide uptake of up to 5 mmol/g, preferably 3.39-3.5 mmol/g; and a breakthrough carbon dioxide working capacity of up to 2.2 mmol/g, preferably 1.65-1.7 mmol/g. The first metal, when present, and second metal preferentially occupy an octahedral metal site and a tetrahedral metal site within the MOF, respectively. The first and second metal precursors are a metal salt, where the metal is in a cationic form, which is balanced by at least one counterions including of halide (e.g. chloride), nitrate, sulfate, hydroxide, oxide, acetate anion, and/or its hydrates. The second metal precursor of method (M1), is zinc acetate or its hydrate (e.g. zinc acetate dihydrate), and/or the metal in the second metal precursor is present in an amount of from 25-100 mol.%, preferably 50-75 mol.% of the total metal present in the mixture. The first metal precursor of method (M1) is iron(II) acetate, cobalt(II) acetate or its hydrates (e.g. cobalt(II) acetate tetrahydrate), and the metal in the first metal precursor is present in an amount of from 25-50 mol.%, preferably 25-50 mol.% of the total metal present in the mixture. Preferred Method: In the method (M1), the temperature is 20-150 degrees C, preferably 60-80 degrees C; and the temperature used results in at least one solvent being refluxed. The method (M1) is performed under ambient atmospheric conditions (e.g. atmospheric pressure).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first solvent is water and the second solvent is an alkyl alcohol (e.g. alkyl alcohol is methanol, propanol, particularly ethanol). The alkali is a metal hydroxide (e.g. the base is potassium hydroxide).
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of mixed-metal composition that do not support the claims.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN113416572-A
TI Separating stable light hydrocarbon involves casting self-made film in porous stainless steel pipe, preparing film supporting body, installing film supporting body in shell, preparing single-pipe type film separator, and conveying refinery gas to gas tank.
AU DENG L
   WANG K
AE NINGXIA SHENRUI IND & TRADE CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
GA 2021B21620
AB    NOVELTY - Separating energy-saving and environment-friendly stable light hydrocarbon comprises casting self-made film in porous stainless steel pipe, preparing film supporting body, installing film supporting body in the shell, and then preparing single-pipe type film separator. The refinery gas is conveyed to the gas tank, and then compressed to 1.8-2.0 MPa by the compressor. The liquid separating tank is entered after being condensed by the condenser. The non-condensable gas is discharged, where the non-condensable gas is passed through light hydrocarbon coarse filter and light hydrocarbon fine filter to remove the liquid drop and dust to obtain the purified gas. The purified gas is added into the membrane separator to obtain carbon dioxide and nitrogen, and then compressed and condensed liquid to obtain the light hydrocarbon.
   USE - Method for separating light hydrocarbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is energy-saving and environment-friendly. The product has good separating performance, and high concentration of recycled light hydrocarbon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The self-made membrane is prepared by crushing halloysite with a steam kinetic energy mill and immersing in deionized water 20 times the mass of halloysite for 48-72 hours to obtain a slurry. The slurry is filtered with a 300-mesh sieve to obtain the filtrate, where the filtrate is placed at 50 degrees C, dried in a blast drying oven, and then ground with an agate mortar to obtain halloysite nanotubes. The halloysite nanotubes are added in absolute ethanol in a flask under stirring to obtain a mixed solution, and then added dodecyltrimethoxysilane in a paraffin bath at 120 degrees C under stirring for 20 hours to obtain the halloysite nanotube turbid solution. The obtained turbid solution is dried for 12-15 hours at 90 degrees C, taken out and ground into powder with an agate mortar, and passed through a 300-mesh sieve to obtain modified halloysite nanotubes. The titanium carbide, titanium hydride and aluminum powder are added into the ball milling tank at a mass ratio of 2:1:1, and milled for 3.5 hours to obtain titanium aluminum carbide powder. The titanium aluminum carbide powder and titanium aluminum carbide powder are etched with hydrofluoric acid in an oil bath at 60 degrees C for 72 hours, naturally cooled for 12-20 hours to prepare etching solution, poured out the unreacted hydrofluoric acid in the upper layer of the etching solution, and washed the lower turbid liquid with distilled water for 10-12 minutes to obtain the lower layer sediment of the supernatant liquid, which is passed through a 325-mesh sieve to obtain titanium carbide powder. Polyether copolyamide is added to anhydrous ethanol with 7 times the mass of polyether copolyamide and water with 3 times the mass of polyether copolyimide under stirring, condensing and refluxing for 2 hours at 80 degrees C to obtain a mixed liquid. The titanium carbide powder is mixed with modified halloysite nanotubes and polyether copolyamide under dispersing for 10-12 minutes, and then stirred at to prepare a casting film.

TI Gasification of biomass used as carbon neutral fuel, involves heating biomass in inert atmosphere which does not contain oxygen, obtaining half carbonized biomass, impregnating aqueous solution of catalyst component, and contacting with gasifying agent.
AU OTSUKA H
   TANAKA T
   KUROMOTO M
   YANO T
   MATSUNAGA K
   KANAZAWA K
AE OSAKA GAS CO LTD (OSAG-C)
GA 2021A4019T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves heating biomass at 300 degree Celsius or more and 350 degree Celsius or less in inert atmosphere which does not contain oxygen to obtain half-carbonized biomass. The semi-carbonized biomass is impregnated with an aqueous solution of a catalyst component containing potassium to obtain the semi-carbonized biomass carrying the catalyst component. The semi-carbonized biomass carrying the catalyst component is contacted with a gasifying agent containing steam under a temperature condition of 600 degree Celsius to 800 degree Celsius. The product gas obtained by the gasification process comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and methane. The potassium of 1.5 %-10% of mass ratio is supported with respect to the carbonization biomass. The biomass used for the semi-carbonization step has a moisture content of 20% or less by an industrial analytical method. The biomass subjected to the semi-carbonizing step has a fixed carbon of 10% to 30%.
   USE - Gasification method of biomass.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient and economical gasification of biomass at low temperature, and suppresses generation of tar.

TI Preparing biomass-based battery negative electrode material involves cleaning, drying and crushing biomass into powder, and then performing acid treatment and heating carbonization treatment.
AU SUN S
AE SUN S (SUNS-Individual)
GA 2021A22170
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing biomass-based battery negative electrode material involves cleaning, drying and crushing biomass into powder of 50-80 meshes, and then performing acid treatment and heating carbonization treatment on the powder to obtain a porous carbonized product. Manganese acetate is added into a urea aqueous solution under stirring, added a porous carbonized product for carrying out ultrasonic oscillation for 4-6 hours to obtain a precursor. The precursor, nano silicon powder, nano titanium dioxide and 1-aminopropyl-3-methylimidazolium bis (trifluoromethanesulfonyl) imide salt are added into 8-10 mol/L sodium hydroxide solution under stirring for carrying out heat treatment to obtain a surface-modified porous carbonized product. The surface-modified porous carbonized product is mixed with lithium hydride in a carbon dioxide atmosphere, and then ball-milled and heated to obtain the battery cathode material.
   USE - Method for preparing biomass-based battery negative electrode material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the biomass-based battery negative electrode material in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with good rate performance and high cycle stability, and good application prospect.

PT P
PN CN112962151-A
TI Preparing degradable silk involves drying degradable silk main material by drier, mixing dried degradable silk main material with auxiliary agent, and mixing formulation.
AU YIN F
   WANG H
   LI F
AE GUANGDONG YONGRUI IND CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202168684Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing degradable silk involves drying the degradable silk main material by drier at 60-120 degrees C, mixing the dried degradable silk main material with auxiliary agent, mixing formulation is 70-99.5 wt.% degradable silk main material, 0.5-30 wt.% auxiliary agent, placing the mixed material into an extruder to extrude. The extruder temperature is 120-250 degrees C. The extruded molten state resin is cooled and molded and stretched. The stretching ratio is 1-8 times. The product after stretching by shaping, shaping temperature is 50-250 degrees C. The shaped product is recycled to obtain the degradable silk product. The degradable silk main material is polylactic acid, polybutylene succinate, terephthalic acid acid/butanedioic acid ester, polysuccinic acid-terephthalic acid polycaprolactone, polyglycolide, poly-3-hydroxybutyric acid, poly-3-hydroxybutyric acid-3-hydroxyvalerate, carbon dioxide and propylene oxide copolymer, poly-hydroxyl fatty acid ester.
   USE - Method for preparing degradable silk.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to obtain a degradable silk with excellent elasticity and strength, and can be compared with the traditional plastic brush silk in the aspects of temperature resistance, elasticity and strength. The method uses relatively simple processing technique.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The auxiliary agent is one or more of color master batch, lubricant, nucleating agent, chain extender, anti-hydrolytic agent, compatilizer. The auxiliary agent comprises antistatic agent, graphene, spare carbon, antibacterial agent, bamboo powder, tea polyphenol, negative ion powder, wood powder, starch, coffee grounds, bagasse, coconut, jute, paperpulp, wheat straw. Preferred Compositions: The degradable silk main material comprises 5-30 wt.% polylactic acid, 35-89.5 wt.% polybutylene succinate, 5-30 wt.% terephthalic acid acid/butylene adipate. Preferred Conditions: The stretching is performed under the condition of heating the hot-air box and stretching under the condition of heating the hot water tank, comprises a primary stretching, secondary stretching and multiple stretching. The mode of ripple processing is making the degradable silk product pass through the middle of the two rotating gears. The product is processed into degradable silk with longitudinal ripple or surface pattern through the pressure of the gear. The shape of gear is regular ripple or irregular ripple shape. The gear is heated or not heated in the process of ripple processing. The surface is processed by laser, carving on the surface of the degradable silk is regular or irregular pattern. The section of the degradable silk product is processed into round, square, rhombus, cross, I-shaped, 8-shaped, plum blossom, single hole type, one of porous type. Preferred Process: The method for preparing degradable silk further involves grinding tip processing, obtaining the degradable grinding tip end of the end fiber or smooth. The end portion grinding tip processing includes preparing the degradable silk PE film and CPP film, packing material in polyfluortetraethylene film after packing, placing into the corrosion liquid with mass fraction of 5-80 wt.% for corrosion treatment. The corrosion liquid is prepared by food-grade sodium hydroxide and water according to mass percentage of 5-80 wt.%. The corrosion liquid temperature is 40-120 degrees C, processing time is 3-1440 minutes. The corrosion reaction is finished, then clean dried, and packaged to obtain the degradable grinding tip silk product.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A32 (Polymer fabrication - such as moulding, extrusion, forming, laminating, spinning.); F01 (Threads and fibres - natural or artificial; spinning - including the production of mineral and carbon fibres (D01).); ; D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).)

AU JIN Y
AE XIAMEN GUANGBOSHI OPTRONICS CO LTD (XIAM-Non-standard)
GA 202167201R
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a low residual protective film, comprising a protective film, the protective film comprises a first protective layer, a second protective layer and a third protective layer, the first protective layer is located on the outer surface of the second protective layer, the second protective layer is located on the outer surface of the third protective layer. PT P
PN CN112831470-A
TI In vitro culture of gamma-delta cells, by            obtaining peripheral blood mononuclear cells, adjusting            cell density, adding e.g. interleukin-2, seeding cells,            culturing, randomly collecting cells, checking and            harvesting mature cells.
AU ZHANG Z
   MA Y
   ZHENG K
   CHEN D
   CHEN T
AE GUANGDONG LONGSEE MEDICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
   FOSHAN LANGXIN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 202160720F
AB    NOVELTY - In vitro culture method of gamma - delta T                cells, involves obtaining peripheral blood                mononuclear cells, adjusting the cell density using                RPMI 1640 medium containing 7-10% fetal bovine                serum, adding zoledronic acid and interleukin-2 to                make the final concentration of zoledronic acid                2-200 mu mol/ml, where the concentration of                peptin-2 is 2-200 mu g/ml, seeding the cells in a                cell culture plate and placing in carbon dioxide                incubator for culture, replacing fresh complete                medium containing the final concentration of 1-50                mu g/ml interleukin-2 to expand on the 3rd, 5th,                7th, 9th and 11th day, culturing the cells,                randomly collecting cells on the 10th day and                checking the cell purity by flow cytometry, and                harvesting mature gamma - delta T cells on the 13th                day.
   USE - The in vitro method is useful for culturing                gamma - delta T cells (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts density gradient                centrifugation to obtain peripheral blood                mononuclear cells, which is simple and easy to                implement, and provides high amplification                factor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves adding                  peripheral blood in a sterile centrifuge tube,                  adding RPMI 1640 medium according to the ratio of                  1:1 to dilute, mixing, obtaining blood mixture,                  adding 15 ml sterile centrifuge tube and adding                  3-5 ml FICOLL separation solution, slowly adding                  8-10 ml blood mixture to 15 ml sterile centrifuge                  tube to distribute on the upper layer of FICOLL                  separation solution, centrifuging with                  centrifugal force, dividing liquid in the sterile                  centrifuge tube into 4 layers, from bottom to                  top, the red blood cell layer, the separation                  liquid layer, the mononuclear cell layer, and the                  plasma layer, aspirating mononuclear cell layer                  liquid, transferring to a new sterile centrifuge                  tube, adding RPMI 1640 medium according to the                  ratio of 1:5, discarding the supernatant after                  centrifugation, adding red blood cell lysate to                  the cell pellet, resuspending the cells and                  mixing, lysing at room temperature, adding RPMI                  1640 medium according to the ratio of 1:5,                  discarding the supernatant by centrifugation,                  adding 7-10% fetal bovine serum (FBS)-containing                  RPMI 1640 medium to the cell pellet, and                  resuspending the cells to obtain peripheral blood                  mononuclear cells. The centrifugal conditions are                  centrifugal force of 550-650 g, time of 20-30                  minutes, acceleration of 6-8 g/minute and                  acceleration of 2-4 g/minutes. The centrifugal                  conditions are 1500-1700 rpm, time of 5-8                  minutes, acceleration of 5-8 g/minute and                  acceleration of 5-8 g/minute. The volume of the                  red blood cell lysis solution is 5-8 ml and the                  lysis time is 5-8 minutes. The cell density is                  3-4x 106 cells/ml. The cell culture plate is                  24-well cell culture plate, and the volume of the                  medium is 1 ml. The culture conditions of the                  carbon dioxide incubator are 5% carbon dioxide                  and 36-38 degrees C. The method involves gently                  pipetting the cells on the 3rd day, aspirating                  the suspension and transferring to 50 ml sterile                  centrifuge tube, discarding the supernatant after                  centrifugation, adding fresh complete medium                  containing interleukin-2, pipetting to mix,                  preparing culture medium to 24 ml, culturing in                  24-well cell culture plate at a volume of 1 ml                  per well, pipetting the cells on the 5th day,                  aspirating the suspension and transferring to 50                  ml sterile centrifuge tube, discarding the                  supernatant after centrifugation, adding fresh                  complete medium containing interleukin-2,                  pipetting to mix, preparing culture medium to 24                  ml, culturing in a 24-well cell culture plate at                  a volume of 1 ml, pipetting the cells on the 7th                  day, aspirating the suspension and transferring                  to 50 ml sterile centrifuge tube, discarding the                  supernatant after centrifugation, adding fresh                  complete medium containing interleukin-2,                  pipetting to mix, preparing culture medium to 24                  ml, continuously culturing in 24-well cell                  culture plate at a volume of 1 ml per well,                  pipetting the cells on the 9th day, aspirating                  the suspension and transferring to 50 ml sterile                  centrifuge tube, discarding the supernatant after                  centrifugation, adding fresh complete medium                  containing interleukin-2, pipetting to mix, and                  preparing culture medium to 24 ml, culturing in a                  24-well cell culture plate at a volume of 1 ml                  per well, pipetting the cells on the 11th day,                  aspirating the suspension and transferring to 50                  ml sterile centrifuge tube, discarding the                  supernatant after centrifugation, adding fresh                  complete medium containing interleukin-2,                  pipetting to mix, and preparing culture medium to                  24 ml, and culturing in a 24-well cell culture                  plate at a volume of 1 ml per well. The complete                  medium comprises RPMI 1640 medium supplemented                  with 7-10% fetal bovine serum and 1% antibiotic                  solution. The antibiotic solution comprises 10000                  U/ml penicillin (base), 10000 mu g/ml                  streptomycin (base) and 25 mu g/ml amphotericin                  B.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PN CN112812398-A
TI Foam material based on renewable resources,            comprises bio-based resin and fossil energy-based            plastic resin through foaming agent blending,            cross-linking and foaming.
AU YANG C
   WEI Q
AE GUANGDE XIANGYUAN NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOG (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202158647G
AB    NOVELTY - Foam material based on renewable resources,                comprises bio-based resin and fossil energy-based                plastic resin through foaming agent blending,                cross-linking and foaming. The fossil energy-based                plastic resin is polyethylene, polypropylene,                thermoplastic elastomer and/or ethylene-vinyl                acetate copolymer. In the bio-based resin, the                resin content is greater than or equal to 30% foam                material and the content of biomass-derived carbon                in the bio-based resin is 60-100% of the total                organic carbon. In the foam material, the content                of biomass-derived carbon is 30-100% of the total                organic carbon.
   USE - Used as foam material based on renewable                resources.
   ADVANTAGE - The material is environmentally friendly,                reduces the emission of greenhouse gas carbon                dioxide, relieves the global warming environment                problem, and has wide prospect in medical                sanitation, human body protection, package buffer,                automobile interior trim and electronic sealing                field.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                manufacturing foam material based on renewable                resources, comprising blending, forming,                cross-linking, and foaming a bio-based resin and a                fossil energy-based plastic resin, and the                cross-linking method is electron irradiation                cross-linking.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The resin content in                  the bio-based resin is greater than or equal to                  70% foam material, and the biomass-derived carbon                  content in the bio-based resin is 70-100% of the                  total organic carbon. In the foam material, the                  content of biomass-derived carbon is 60-100% of                  the total organic carbon. The bio-based resin is                  low-density polyethylene, low-density linear                  polyethylene, high-density polyethylene,                  elastomer polyolefin elastomers, ethylene-vinyl                  acetate copolymer, and/or modified thermoplastic                  starch. The melt index of the bio-based resin is                  0.2-10 g/10 minute, and the density is 0.89-0.96                  g/cm3. The foaming agent is AC foaming agent. The                  foam material further comprises modified starch                  and catalyst. Preferred Method: The blending                  method is banburying granulation, and the molding                  method is extrusion molding or compression                  molding. The foaming method is free foaming or                  other methods.

TI Method for estimating tidal volume of patient during non-invasive ventilation by ventilation system, involves estimating respiratory flow of patient based on machine flow and leak flow and determining tidal volume of patient.

   MISSERI G
   GIARRATANO A
   ACCURSO G
AE UNIV PALERMO (UYPA-Non-standard)
GA 202151133Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves measuring a flow Qmach of gas supplied by the ventilator (10). A flow Qieak of gas is estimated through the intentional leak (40) as a function of gas pressure (Pp) within the helmet (20). A respiratory flow (JP) of the patient is estimated based on machine flow and leak flow. A tidal volume (Vr) of the patient is determined by mathematical integration of the respiratory flow over time.
   USE - Method for estimating tidal volume of patient during non-invasive ventilation by ventilation system which is used for generating continuous positive pressure.
   ADVANTAGE - The helmet which provides a base formed by an inflatable cushion which ensures the anchoring of the helmet to the patients neck. The intentional leak is in fact positioned on the outlet port and conventionally consisting of a device configured to facilitate the discharge to the environment of the carbon dioxide exhaled by the patient and avoiding the rebreathing phenomenon.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the mechanical ventilation system to measure the tidal volume.

   Flow sensor (11)
   Pressure sensor (12)

   Intentional leak (40)

PT P
PN CN112707395-A; CN112707395-B
TI Electrolyzing cyanide and recovering graphite            under acidic conditions involves crushing and grinding            aluminum electrolysis waste cathode to obtain spare            powder, preparing acid solution and spare powder and            placing prepared acid solution.
AU YUAN J
   ZHENG X
   MA A
   LI S
AE UNIV LIUPANSHUI NORMAL (UNLP-C)
GA 202151539J
AB    NOVELTY - Electrolyzing cyanide and recovering graphite                under acidic conditions involves crushing and                grinding the aluminum electrolysis waste cathode to                obtain a spare powder with a particle size of 0.15                millimeter, preparing acid solution and spare                powder according to the ratio of liquid-solid ratio                of 1, placing the prepared acid solution in the                electrolytic cell at the temperature of 60 degrees                C, energizing same time, passing oxygen to the                cathode of the electrolytic cell, adding the                prepared spare powder, reacting for a certain time                and filtering to obtain graphite powder and                filtrate. The carbon dioxide is passed into the                filtrate obtained to produce precipitation, which                is separated by filtration to obtain electrolyte                and tail liquid, and the tail liquid is                returned.
   USE - Method for electrolyzing cyanide and                recovering graphite under acidic conditions.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not generate secondary                pollution, is suitable for processing aluminum                electrolytic waste cathode with low cyanide content                and oxidizes and removes the cyanide in the                solution by the hydrogen peroxide generated by the                cathode of the electrolytic cell.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acid solution is                  selected from hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid,                  nitric acid, and hydrogen ion, where the acid                  solution concentration is 1-5 mole/L, and the                  liquid-solid ratio is 5-10:1. The graphite is                  selected as the anode and cathode materials of                  the electrolytic cell. Preferred Conditions: The                  applied voltage is 0.7-0.8V. The method involves                  introducing 0.01 L oxygen to the cathode of the                  electrolytic cell according to the proportion of                  0.01 L oxygen per 10g spare powder material,                  entering oxygen and using interval ventilation to                  pass in oxygen. The duration of a single                  ventilation is 1-5 minutes and the interval of                  ventilation is 1-10 minutes. The external power                  source is energized and ventilated at the same                  time to start and stop. The acid leaching                  temperature of the electrolytic cell is 20-60                  degrees C, and the acid leaching time is 30-180                  minutes. The carbon dioxide ventilation rate is                  controlled to 100-1000 ml/minutes per 10g spare                  powder material, and the ventilation is                  controlled time is 5-30 minutes. The method                  involves preparing spare powder and acid solution                  according to the liquid-solid ratio of 7-8:1. The                  electrolytic cell is kept warm at 38-42 degrees C                  in a water bath, controlled the applied voltage                  of the electrolytic cell to 0.7 V, added the                  spare powder material, continue to pass oxygen                  into the cathode of the electrolytic cell for at                  least 3 minutes, and turned off the power supply                  and the vent valve at an interval of 5-10                  minutes, started the power supply and feed in                  oxygen simultaneously, and repeated until the                  reaction is complete, filtered after the reaction                  to obtain graphite powder and filtrate. The                  carbon dioxide gas is introduced into the                  filtrate, the electrolyte powder is obtained by                  filtration, and the tail liquid is reused. The                  obtained graphite powder has a purity of 93-95%,                  the cyanide concentration in the filtrate                  obtained is 5 mg/L, and the decomposition rate of                  cyanide and cyanide ions in the carbon powder                  reaches 85%.

AB    NOVELTY - Inducing human mesenchymal stem cell to                differentiate into cartilage in vitro comprises (1)                preparing cell culture medium with serum substitute                and serum-free medium in a certain volume ratio,                after completing preparation storing at 2-8 degrees                C, (2) preparing compound cartilage induction                differentiation medium, after completing                preparation storing at 2-8 degrees C, (3)                re-suspending mesenchymal stem cells with cell                culture medium, adjusting density of mesenchymal                stem cells, and using 96-well plates to inoculate                mesenchymal stem cells, (4) after completing                inoculation, placing in a carbon dioxide incubator                for 2-3 hours, and adding 0.1-0.2 ml cell culture                medium, and (5) allowing to stand for 22-26 hours,                replacing medium with a chondrogenic                differentiation medium to performing chondrogenic                differentiation.

The inoculating density of the                  96-hole plate for inoculating the mesenchymal                  stem cell is 1x 105 to 2x 105 /10 mu l/well. The                  temperature of the carbon dioxide culture box is                  36-40 degrees C. The carbon dioxide concentration                  of the carbon dioxide culture box is 3-7%. The                  time for inducing and differentiating the                  cartilage is 20-25 days.
