PN CN112680031-A
TI Preparation of dust-free high-quality sea shell chalk from discarded shells involves washing discarded shells, bleaching with hydrogen peroxide, crushing, calcining, mixing with water-soluble binder, adding kaolin and drying.
AU CHEN H
   LI D
   XU B
   LI Q
   JIN X
   HUANG Z
   ZHANG T
   ZHANG Z
   ZHAN J
   HUANG L
   ZHANG M
   YE Z
AE UNIV LINGNAN NORMAL (ULNN-C)
GA 202145458W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of dust-free high-quality sea shell chalk from discarded shells involves (1) washing the discarded shells with water, bleaching with hydrogen peroxide, and drying at 100 degrees C, (2) crushing the dried shells into 200-300 mesh with a mechanical stirring pulverizer, calcining at 900-950 degrees C for 130-150 minutes, obtaining calcium oxide powder, (3) injecting carbon dioxide gas, heating, obtaining calcium carbonate powder with higher purity, (4) heating glycerol and water at a ratio of 1:60 to prepare a milky white solution, obtaining water-soluble binder, (5) mixing the calcium carbonate powder obtained in step (3) with the water-soluble binder in step (4) in a ratio of 4:1, adding kaolin, demolding and drying.
   USE - Preparation of dust-free high-quality sea shell chalk.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses porous structure of shell powder mixed with polyvinyl alcohol, which can reduce the dust generated by the chalk and reduce cause of serious damage to the skin and respiratory tract when teachers and students use chalk. The produces chalk is non-toxic and environmentally-friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: In the step (1), the discarded shells are washed with clean water, bleached with hydrogen peroxide and dried at 100 degrees C. In step (2), the shells dried in the step (1) are crushed into 200-300 mesh, preferably 250 mesh with a mechanical stirring pulverizer, and calcined at 900-950 degrees C, preferably 950 degrees C for 130-150 minutes, preferably 135 minutes to obtain calcium oxide powder. In step (3), the feeding carbon dioxide at a rate of 8 mL/hour, and calcination is performed at 950 degrees C. The heating standard in step (4) is when the milky white solution is boiled, then the heating process is stopped immediately. Preferred Composition: The mixing ratio of the calcium carbonate powder in step (3) and the water-soluble binder in the step (4) is 4:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In step (4), the ratio of glycerol to water in the water-soluble binder is 1:60.

AU MA B
AE LIANYUNGANG TEACHERS COLLEGE (LIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202140544H
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing nitrogen-doped carbonized bacterial cellulose supported palladium catalyst involves (i) soaking bacterial cellulose in a 3.2-5.2 wt.% diethylene triaminopentaacetic acid pentasodium salt solution, soaking sodium salt-impregnated bacterial cellulose in a 0.2-0.5 mg/l palladium nitrate solution, and freeze-drying to obtain nitrogen-doped carbonized bacterial cellulose-supported palladium catalyst precursor, and (ii) placing the nitrogen-doped carbonized bacterial cellulose supported palladium catalyst precursor in a tubular muffle furnace, performing carbonization treatment under protection of carbon dioxide atmosphere at 800 degrees C, and cooling to obtain the product with three-dimensional network structure.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nitrogen-doped carbonized bacterial cellulose supported palladium catalyst for catalytic reduction of hexavalent chromium pollutants in water (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has uniformly distributed palladium nano-particles and catalytic reduction rate of chromium(VI) up to 99.9%, and is stable in structure and suitable for needs of industrial mass production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a nitrogen-doped carbonized bacterial cellulose supported palladium catalyst prepared the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In step (i), the mass-to-volume ratio of bacterial cellulose and pentasodium diethylenetriaminopentaacetic acid solution is (0.05-0.1):(100-200) (g:ml), and the volume ratio of pentasodium diethylenetriaminopentaacetic acid solution and palladium nitrate solution is 1:2-2:1. The nitrogen content in the catalyst is 2.3-4.1 wt.%. The content of palladium is 0.9-1.9 wt.%. Preferred Conditions: In step (i), the soaking is carried out at 0-40 degrees C for 24-48 hours. In step (ii), the heating rate of carbonization is 1-4 degrees C/minute. In step (ii), the carbonization time is 1-3 hours. The palladium nanoparticles are supported on the surface and three-dimensional grid of nitrogen-doped carbonized bacterial cellulose, where the specific surface area is 413-560 m2/g, and the particle size of the palladium nanoparticles is 8-22 nm.

The air content analyzer includes a carbon dioxide detector and oxygen detector. The air content analyzer is installed in layers in various places in the tea garden through a support, and the installation height of main portion is consistent with the height of the tea tree.
   USE - Ecological tea garden cultivation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The air is prevented from affecting the growth of tea trees, thus effectively improving the quality of tea trees and the survival rate of tea trees.

PN CN112266005-A
TI Preparing nano calcium carbonate comprises stirring the calcium hydroxide powder and ethyl cellulose powder uniformly to obtain a mixed powder, adding absolute ethanol and removing the non-woven fabric and filter the layered liquid.
AU WU Y
   LI T
AE WU Y (WUYY-Individual)
GA 202112402X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano calcium carbonate comprises (1) stirring the calcium hydroxide powder and ethyl cellulose powder uniformly to obtain a mixed powder, adding absolute ethanol and stirring uniformly to obtain a viscous slurry, spinning at high temperature to form monofilament fibers, and weaving into a non-woven fabric, (2) stacking the non-woven fabric on the hollow plate in the middle section of the reactor, and soaking the non-woven fabric in the layering liquid, slowly introducing carbon dioxide gas until the stratified liquid no longer precipitates, removing the non-woven fabric and filter the layered liquid to obtain calcium carbonate precipitation, and (3) adding the calcium carbonate precipitate to absolute ethanol for ultrasonic dispersion for 20-40 minutes, spraying and settlingto obtain nanometer calcium carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nano calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes the process of dissolving and transforming calcium hydroxide to obtain nano calcium carbonate particles with uniform distribution, good dispersibility and no agglomeration.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The calcium hydroxide powder is a micron-sized powder, and the mass ratio of the calcium hydroxide to the ethyl cellulose is 2-7:6. The amount of ethanol added is 150-200% of the mass of ethyl cellulose, and the stirring speed for uniform stirring is 1000-2000 revolutions/minute. In the step (2), the feeding rate of carbon dioxide is 5-10 ml/minute, and the carbon dioxide is fed in from distilled water. In the step (3), the amount of the absolute ethanol added is 30-50% of the mass of the calcium carbonate precipitation, the ultrasonic frequency of the ultrasonic dispersion is 50-90 kHz, and the temperature is 10-20 degrees C. The spray area of the spray sedimentation is 10-40 cm2, and the spray volume is 2-4 ml/minute. Preferred Method: In the step (1), the high-temperature spinning temperature is 80-100 degrees C, the spinning voltage is 15 KV, the injection speed of the viscous slurry is 1.1-1.5 ml/hours, and the monofilament fibers are nano-scale monofilaments. The innocuous cloth adopts 3-10 strands of fiber monofilaments as woven monofilaments, and uses them as warp and weft at the same time. In the step (2), the stratification liquid is a stratification liquid of water and dichloroethane, and the non-woven fabric is always above the dichloroethane liquid level. The mid-section hollow board adopts an up-and-down adjustable hollow board, and the non-woven fabric is gradually soaked into distilled water from bottom to top by adjusting the hollow board up and down.

PN CN112263487-A
TI Preparing biomass polymer microsphere useful for sunscreen cosmetic, comprises e.g. mixing biomass polymer with sodium silicate, and then grinding, mixing micron-sized biomass polymer, adding water, quenching, washing and drying.
AU ZENG J
   CHEN Q
   BAI T
   SI W
AE CHENGDU NEW KELI CHEM ENG SCI & TECHNIC (XKLI-C)
GA 202112260N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing biomass polymer microsphere comprises (i) first mixing the biomass polymer with sodium silicate, and then grinding to obtain the micron-sized biomass polymer, (ii) first mixing the micron-sized biomass polymer obtained in the step (i) with nano-rutile titanium dioxide and nano-zinc oxide, adding water, and then grinding in a sand mill, then passing in enough carbon dioxide to continue grinding, and finally centrifuging to obtain biomass polymer loaded with nano rutile titanium dioxide and nano zinc oxide, and(iii) loading the nano rutile titanium dioxide obtained in the step (ii), spray drying the biomass polymer of nano-zinc oxide, quenching in water, washing and drying to obtain biomass polymer microsphere.
   USE - The microsphere is useful for sunscreen cosmetic (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The microsphere: does not only firmly load the physical sunscreen; has high sphericity, which is convenient for smearing and has good sunscreen effect; has simple preparation method; and is degradable without residue; and opens way for the development of biological environmental protection cosmetic additives.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the biomass polymer is polylactic acid, polybutylene succinate, polycaprolactone, and carbon dioxide polyester with a coarse particle diameter of 1-3 mm. In the step (ii), the raw material includes 100 pts. wt. micron grade biomass polymer, 5-10 pts. wt. nano rutile titanium dioxide, 3-5 pts. wt. nano zinc oxide and 150-200 pts. wt. water. Preferred Method: In the step (i), carrying out grinding using special airflow vortex mill for grinding heat-sensitive materials, and the main engine speed of the airflow vortex mill is 800-1000 revolutions/minute and classification speed is 1000-1200 revolutions/minute. The particle size of the micron-sized biomass polymer is 10-15 mu m. In the step (ii), the grinding speed is 800-1000 revolutions/minutes, the grinding time is 15-30 minutes, and the continuous grinding time is 30-60 minutes. In the step (iii), the pressure of the spray drying is 3-5 MPa, and the temperature is 50-70 degrees C. The temperature of the quenched water is 30-40 degrees C.

PN CN112143111-A
TI Processing of dental medical plastic product            involves melting mixture of polypropylene and            styrene-acrylonitrile, adding plasticizer, stabilizer            and adhesive, adding ground e.g. flame retardant and            injection molding mixture.
AU JIANG R
AE SIXIAN DONGFANG PLASTIC CO LTD (SIXI-Non-standard)
GA 2021031345
AB    NOVELTY - A dental medical plastic product comprises                50-70 wt.% polypropylene, 10-20 wt.%                styrene-acrylonitrile, 2-5 wt.% calcium carbonate,                1-3 wt.% sterilizing agent, 1-1.5 wt.% flame                retardant, 8-15 wt.% plasticizer, 5-10 wt.%                adhesive, 5-8 wt.% biodegradable agent, 2-5 wt.%                stabilizer and 1-5 wt.% antioxidant. The processing                of the product involves mixing polypropylene and                styrene-acrylonitrile, quickly heating to 90-95                degrees C, maintaining the temperature, stirring at                uniform speed for 5-10 minutes, preparing a mixed                solution, adding plasticizer, stabilizer and                adhesive, maintaining the temperature, continuously                stirring for 15-20 minutes, preparing a mixed                solution, grinding the remaining raw materials                separately, adding the ground powder to the mixed                solution, maintaining the temperature at 90-95                degrees C, continuously stirring for 15-25 minutes,                injection molding after heating to molten state,                and demolding after cooling.

PN CN111943220-A
TI MCM-41/SGU-29 composite molecular sieve material used as adsorbent for adsorbing carbon dioxide comprises MCM-41-coated SGU-29, where MCM-41 is mesoporous structure, and SGU-29 is microporous structure.
AU FANG Y
   CHEN Y
   XU Y
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 2020B7455Y
AB    NOVELTY - MCM-41/SGU-29 composite molecular sieve material comprises MCM-41-coated SGU-29, where the MCM-41 is a mesoporous structure, and the SGU-29 is a microporous structure.
   USE - The MCM-41/SGU-29 composite molecular sieve material is used as adsorbent for adsorbing CO2 (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Compared with the pure microporous zeolite molecular sieve SGU-29 and pure mesoporous molecular sieve MCM-41, the MCM-41/SGU-29 composite molecular sieve material has greatly improved CO2 adsorption ability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the MCM-41/SGU-29 composite molecular sieve material comprising adding SGU-29 into aqueous solution of hexadecyltrimethylammonium bromide, and stirring to obtain suspension I; adjusting the pH of the sodium silicate water solution, and stirring to obtain solution II; dropping the suspension I into the solution II, and stirring to obtain mixture III; transferring the mixture III into a crystallization kettle, and crystallizing to obtain mixture IV; and filtering the mixture IV, washing, drying, and roasting to remove the template agent and obtain MCM-41/SGU-29 composite molecular sieve material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Material: The composite molecular sieve material has specific surface area of 621.15-721.17 m2/g, micropore volume of 0.08-0.12 cm3/g, pore volume of 0.50-0.56 cm3/g, aperture of 2.75-3.52 nm, and carbon dioxide (CO2) adsorption capacity of 1.26-1.68 mmol/g.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the first step, the concentration of aqueous solution of hexadecyltrimethylammonium bromide is 0.106 g/ml, the mass ratio of SGU-29 to hexadecyltrimethylammonium bromide is 0.141-0.283:1, and the stirring is at room temperature for 24 hours. In the second step, the concentration of sodium silicate nonahydrate aqueous solution is 0.0979 g/ml, the pH is adjusted by sulfuric acid water solution, the concentration of the sulfuric acid water solution is 6 M, and the stirring is at room temperature for 10 minutes. In the third step, the volume ratio of suspension I and solution II is 0.4:1, and the stirring is at room temperature for 2 hours. In the fourth step, the crystallization is at 120 degrees C for 72 hours. In the fifth step, the roasting is at 540 degrees C for 6 hours.

The solvent replacement proceeds for 4-6 days. The solvent of the mixed solution gelled in step (c) is solvent-substituted with deionized water, but deionized water is replaced at intervals of 22-26 hours. The mixed solutio is dried at 0.05-0.2 millibar. Preferred Components: The solvent is formamide. The formula of the nickel compound is nickel(II) nitrate hexahydrate (Ni(NO3)2.6H2O), the formula of the cobalt compound is cobalt(II) nitrate hexahydrate (Co(NO3)2.6H2O), and the formula of the molybdenum compound is ammonium tetrathiomolybdate ((NH4)2MoS4).

TI Polymer used in exhaust treatment or gas storage            apparatus, comprises aryl diamines, monoaldehyde, and            heteroaromatic rings in polymerized form, and is            crosslinked and porous in bulk form.
AU AL H O C S
   ABDELNABY M M
AE UNIV DHAHRAN KING FAHD PETROLEUM & MINER (UYKF-C)
GA 202071190C
AB    NOVELTY - Polymer comprises 10-20 mol.% one or more                5-10C aryl diamines, 50-65 mol.% monoaldehyde, and                20-35 mol.% one or more 5-membered heteroaromatic                rings in polymerized form, where mol.% is relative                to the total moles of the 5-10C aryl diamine                monomer units, the monoaldehyde monomer units and                the 5-membered heteroaromatic ring monomer units,                and the polymer is crosslinked and is porous in                bulk form.
   USE - The polymer is used in an exhaust treatment or                gas storage apparatus (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The polymer selectively captures carbon                dioxide gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for separating the first gas from                the gas mixture, involving contacting a gas mixture                comprising a first gas and a second gas, with the                polymer; and
   (2) a method for obtaining an                amine-functionalized crosslinked porous polymer,                involving (a) adding hydrochloric acid to 5-10C                aryl diamine and monoaldehyde in a solvent to form                a first reaction mixture, (b) combining the                5-membered heteroaromatic ring with the first                reaction mixture to form a second reaction mixture,                and (c) heating the second reaction mixture at                70-120 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The heteroaromatic                  ring comprises a pyrrole group (preferred), an                  imidazole group, a pyrazole group, a thiazole                  group, an oxazole group, a furan group, and/or a                  thiophene group. The heteroaromatic ring                  comprises an unsubstituted pyrrole group. The                  diamine is chosen from 1,4-diaminobenzene                  (preferred), 1,3-diaminobenzene,                  2,6-diaminopyridine, 2,5-diaminopyridine,                  2,3-diaminopyridine, 2,4-diaminopyrimidine,                  1,4-diaminonaphthalene, 1,5-diaminonaphthalene,                  1,8-diaminonaphthalene, 2,3-diaminonaphthalene,                  and/or 1,2-diaminonaphthalene. The diamine is                  1,4-diaminobenzene having no further substituent.                  The monoaldehyde is chosen from formaldehyde                  (preferred), acetaldehyde, propionaldehyde,                  butyraldehyde, and/or isobutanal. The diamine,                  the monoaldehyde, and the heteroaromatic ring                  monomer units make up 100 mol.% of the polymer.                  The solvent comprises N,N-dimethylformamide.                  Preferred Product: The polymer comprises                  12.5-17.5 mol.% diamine, 55-60 mol.%                  monoaldehyde, and 25-32.5 mol.% heteroaromatic                  ring. The polymer is an amine-functionalized                  crosslinked porous polymer formed by acid                  catalyzed-condensation of 1,4-benzenediamine,                  paraformaldehyde, and pyrrole in a molar ratio of                  1:(2-8):(1-4). The crosslinked porous polymer                  comprises at least 10 mol.% 1,4-benzenediamine,                  at least 50 mol.% formaldehyde, and at least 25                  mol.% pyrrole. Preferred Properties: The polymer                  meets at least one of a porosity of 250-350 m2/g,                  a carbon dioxide uptake capacity of 25-45 cm3/g                  at 0 degrees C and 1 bar, a carbon dioxide uptake                  capacity of 17.5-30 cm3/g at 25 degrees C and 1                  bar, a coverage-dependent enthalpy of adsorption                  (Qst) of 25-45 kJ/mol, a carbon dioxide uptake                  versus nitrogen with a selectivity of 200-300 at                  273 K, a carbon dioxide uptake versus nitrogen                  with a selectivity of 125-165 at 298 K, a dynamic                  carbon dioxide uptake capacity of 5-12 cm3/g                  under dry conditions, a dynamic carbon dioxide                  uptake capacity of 10-20 cm3/g in at least 90%                  relative humidity, and an absorption diminution                  of not greater than 15% after greater than or                  equal to 45 cycles.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

Preferred Properties: The pore size of the covalent organic framework material is 0.5-3.5 nm. Preferred Composition: The molar amount of the acetic acid catalyst is 0.2-40 times the molar amount of 1,3,6,8-tetra-(p-aminophenyl)-pyrene. TI Low-thermally conductive composite foam material for industrial, construction, aerospace and thermal insulation components, consists of resin-based foam matrix and aerogel in preset mass ratio.
AU DONG H

   LV D
   CAO E

   ZHANG D
AE GONGYI FANRUIYIHUI COMPOSITE MATERIALS (GONG-Non-standard)
GA 202061148U
AB    NOVELTY - A low-thermally conductive composite foam material consists of a resin-based foam matrix and aerogel in mass ratio of 1:(0.1-15).
   USE - Low-thermally conductive composite foam material for industrial, construction, aerospace and thermal insulation components.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite foam material has desired mechanical strength, toughness, thermal insulation property and sound insulation property, and excellent mechanical properties, and can be produced by simple method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of low-thermally conductive composite foam material, which involves uniformly mixing a resin, hollow microbeads, thermally expandable microspheres, curing agent, accelerator, coupling agent, toughening agent and flame retardant in mass ratio of 100:(15-60):(1-5):(2-10):(1-3):(1-2):(3-5):(5-25), pouring the slurry into a mold, curing to produce a resin-based foam matrix, mixing a silicon source, ethanol, water, and catalyst in molar ratio of 1:(4-20):(2-10):(0.002-0.02) to produce a silica sol, impregnating the sol with resin-based foam matrix, gelling, aging at 20-60 degrees C for 6-36 hours, and drying using supercritical carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aerogel is silica aerogel. The hollow beads are hollow glass beads, aluminum silicate hollow beads, borate hollow beads, glass hollow beads, alumina hollow beads, silica hollow beads, ceramic hollow beads, zirconia hollow beads, fly ash beads, carbon hollow beads, phenolic hollow beads or polystyrene hollow beads. The catalyst is an acid catalyst or a basic catalyst. The catalyst is aqueous ammonia, ammonium fluoride or sodium hydroxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The curing agent is dicyandiamide and its derivatives, succinic hydrazide, adipic acid dihydrazide, isophthalic acid dihydrazide or p-hydroxybenzoic acid hydrazide. The accelerator is UR300 (RTM: urea based accelerator), UR500 (RTM: urea based accelerator), 2,4,6-tris(dimethylaminomethyl)phenol, pyridine, liquid imidazole, 2-methyl-4-hexyl imidazole, acetylacetone metal salt, benzoyl peroxide, benzyl dimethylamine, triethylamine or polyetheramine. The coupling agent is KH550 (RTM: 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane) or KH560 (RTM: glycidoxypropyltrimethoxysilane). The flame retardant is HY-50, AMP-30, 9,10-dihydro-9-oxa-10-phosphaphenanthrene 10-oxide, ammonium polyphosphate, isopropylated triphenyl phosphate, decabromodiphenyl ether, decabromodiphenyl ethane, aluminum hydroxide, and/or hydroxide. The foaming agent is a hydrocarbon such as isobutane or isopentane. The silicon source is ethyl orthosilicate, methyl orthosilicate, butyl orthosilicate and/or isopropyl orthosilicate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The resin is bisphenol A epoxy resin, bisphenol F epoxy resin, novolac epoxy resin or modified epoxy resin. The outer shell of the thermally expandable microspheres is a thermoplastic material, and the interior of the thermally expandable microspheres is filled with a foaming agent. The toughening agent is polysulfide rubber, liquid nitrile rubber, liquid acrylate rubber, liquid polybutadiene rubber, ethylene-vinyl acetate, styrene-butadiene-styrene, polyether, polysulfone or low molecular polyamide. The thermoplastic material is a dichloroethylene copolymer, an acrylonitrile copolymer or an acrylic copolymer. Preferred Properties: The particle diameter of the hollow microspheres is 50-125 mu m. The particle diameter of the thermally expandable microspheres is 10-50 mu m.

TI Carbonatable clinker material used in carbonatable binder and concrete, comprises amorphous phase and oxide composition comprising calcium oxide, magnesium oxide, sodium oxide, potassium oxide and silicon dioxide, and total soluble alkali.
AU PISCH A
   FEREY F
AE HOLCIM TECHNOLOGY LTD (HOLF-C)
   HOLCIM TECHNOLOGY LTD (HOLF-C)
   HOLCIM TECHNOLOGY LTD (HOLF-C)
GA 2020561386
AB    NOVELTY - Carbonatable clinker material comprises at least 60 wt.%, preferably 100 wt.% amorphous phase, and oxide composition comprising calcium oxide, magnesium oxide, sodium oxide, potassium oxide and silicon dioxide, and 0.01-0.06 wt.% total soluble alkali, where the molar ratio of calcium oxide, magnesium oxide, sodium oxide and potassium oxide to silicon dioxide is 0.7-1.8, preferably 0.9-1.6.
   USE - The clinker material is useful in a carbonatable binder and a concrete, mortar (all claimed) or stucco in building materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces energy consumption, reduces carbon dioxide emissions, decreases operating cost of cement plants using melting kilns, and prevents leaching from binder and/or concretes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the clinker material, involving (i) heating a raw mix at a temperature sufficient for the raw mix to be completely in a molten state for a time period making it possible to achieve the complete melting of the raw composition, and (ii) cooling down the molten mixture obtained at end of step (i).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The clinker material is in the form of a powder having a Blaine surface of 3000-7000 cm2/g. Preferred Method: The temperature in step (i) is equal or superior at 1250 degrees C and strictly inferior at 1600 degrees C, preferably 1350-1450 degrees C. The step (i) is carried out in a melting furnace, preferably a reverberatory furnace. The step (i) is carried out in a rotary kiln. The cooling down in step (ii) is carried out by suddenly lowering the temperature of the molten mixture obtained at end of step (ii) at a temperature below the crystallization temperature. The method further involves grinding the mixture obtained at the end of step (ii) until obtaining a powder having a Blaine specific surface area of 3000-7000 cm2/g, preferably 4000-6000 cm2/g. Preferred Components: The raw mix comprises raw materials chosen from limestone, marl, sand, dolomite, clays, olivine, monticellite, wollastonite, rankinite, forsterite, serpentine, talc, feldspar, anorthite, anorthosite, albite, microcline, sanidine, pyroxene, plagioclase, fluorspar, recycled concrete fines, asbestos fibers, cement clinker, blast furnace or steel slag.

PN CN111320528-A; CN211921378-U
TI Preparation of ethanol from exhaust gas generated            from steel plant involves processing e.g. converter gas            by e.g. purification, desulfurization and            decarbonization, to separate carbon monoxide, and            reacting resultant and hydrogen.
AU WANG Y
   DUAN W
   CHEN Y
   ZHANG E
   JIA Y
   LI Q
AE BEIJING PETROCHEMICAL ENG CO LTD (SNPC-C)
GA 202060677N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of ethanol from exhaust gas                generated from steel plant involves subjecting                converter gas and/or blast furnace gas to rough                purification, pressurization, deoxygenation,                desulfurization, decarbonization, and gas                separation to separate purified carbon monoxide,                subjecting coke oven gas to crude purification and                pressurization to extract hydrogen by pressure                swing adsorption, partially converting generated                methane-rich gas into hydrogen and mixing partial                fuel gas with the converter gas and/or blast                furnace gas, and reacting carbon monoxide and                hydrogen to synthesize ethanol.
   USE - Preparation of ethanol from exhaust gas                generated from steel plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical preparation of                ethanol by efficiently recycling exhaust gas                generated from steel plant, with reduced energy                consumption and suppressed carbon dioxide                emission.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system                for the preparation of ethanol from exhaust gas                generated from steel plant, which comprises a                converter gas and/or blast furnace gas purification                carbon monoxide device, coke oven gas hydrogen                extraction device, methane-rich gas conversion                hydrogen production device, ethanol synthesis                device, and fuel gas blending device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The fuel gas blending                  device comprises a fuel gas filter, a buffer                  tank, a mixer, a burner, a pressure reducer, a                  booster pressure device, and an automatic control                  device.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The processing of carbon                  monoxide and hydrogen to synthesize ethanol is                  preferably carried out by performing dimethyl                  etherification of methanol, carbonylating                  resultant dimethyl ether with purified carbon                  monoxide, obtaining methyl acetate, performing                  hydrogenation reaction of methyl acetate with                  hydrogen to produce ethanol and methanol as                  by-product, separating ethanol from resultant                  mixture, and recycling methanol to dimethyl                  etherification. The processing of methane-rich                  gas to hydrogen involves purifying methane-rich                  gas, performing water vapor conversion,                  performing carbon monoxide shift, and extracting                  hydrogen by pressure swing adsorption. The                  purification of methane-rich gas is performed by                  desulfurization, deoxygenation, deep                  purification, dechlorination, metal carbonyl                  removal, and fine desulfurization. The separation                  of carbon monoxide from converter gas and/or                  blast furnace gas preferably involves performing                  coarse decontamination by removal of dust and                  impurities, pressurizing using centrifugal screw                  or reciprocating compressor, desulfurizing by wet                  desulfurization or dry desulfurization,                  decarburizing by amine washing or pressure swing                  adsorption to remove carbon dioxide, performing                  gas separation, purification of carbon monoxide                  by cryogenic technology, pressure swing                  adsorption, or membrane separation, and obtaining                  purified product containing 85 mol% or more                  carbon monoxide, less than 0.1 ppm, less than 0.1                  ppm iron carbonyl, less than 0.01 ppm chloride,                  and less than 10 ppm water.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN KR2020059330-A; KR2150081-B1
TI Zeolite-based selective catalytic reduction catalyst comprises useful for reducing nitrogen oxides using carbon monoxide in exhaust gas as reducing agent, prepared by impregnating iridium in zeolite ion-exchanged with cation.
AU LEE J H
   HEO I J
   PARK M B
AE KOREA RES INST CHEM TECHNOLOGY (KRIC-C)
GA 202049609R
AB    NOVELTY - Zeolite-based selective catalytic reduction catalyst, prepared by impregnating iridium in zeolite ion-exchanged with cation of hydrogen, alkali metals or alkaline earth metals, and then calcining under wet air, is claimed. The zeolite is beta -zeolite, mordenite, mobil-type five type zeolite, ferrierite or chabazite type zeolite.
   USE - The zeolite-based selective catalytic reduction catalyst is useful for reducing nitrogen oxides using carbon monoxide in exhaust gas as reducing agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst is simple and economical to prepare.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) manufacturing zeolite-based selective catalytic reduction catalyst, comprising (i) preparing a zeolite, (ii) ion-exchanging the zeolite with cation including hydrogen, alkali metal or alkaline earth metal, (iii) supporting iridium on the ion-exchanged zeolite, and (iv) calcining the iridium-supported zeolite under wet air; and
   (2) method for treating exhaust gas using selective catalytic reduction catalyst, comprising (a) adsorbing nitrogen oxides in the exhaust gas using the catalyst, and (b) reacting nitrogen oxide with carbon monoxide and reducing agent to reduce nitrogen, carbon dioxide and water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of silicon/aluminum in the zeolite is 1-100. The catalyst comprises 0.1-10 wt.% iridium. The catalyst has nitrogen oxide reduction rate of greater than or equal to 50% at greater than or equal to 250 degrees C. Preferred Method: The catalyst is calcined at 300-800 degrees C for 1-10 hours. In the step (a), adsorbing nitrogen oxides in the exhaust gas at 500 degrees C. In the step (b), reduction reaction is carried out at 100-600 degrees C.
   EXAMPLE - Iridium(iii) chloride hydrate sample was dissolved in water, 5 g H- beta zeolite (silicon/aluminum of 14) was supported on 4 wt.% iridium incipient wetness impregnation method, calcined at 500 degrees C for 4 hours and dried under wet air to obtain 4 wt.% iridium/H- beta zeolite catalyst.

PT P
PN CN111154715-A
TI Separating and culturing cardiomyocytes, involves cutting heart tissue, adding type I collagenase, shaking, resuspending cell pellet with trypsin, adding digestion solution, adding culture medium, resuspending cardiomyocytes and culturing.
AU XU S
   ZHANG Y
   LU Y
AE GUANGDONG BIOCELL BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202044211P
AB    NOVELTY - Method for separating and culturing cardiomyocytes, involves (a) using heart tissue, washing away the residual blood with phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) liquid, cutting the heart tissue into 0.1-1 mm3 tissue blocks with ophthalmic scissors, adding 0.5-5 ml type I collagenase and shaking to digest at 36-37.5 degrees C for 3 hours, adding 2.5-50 ml PBS to dilute and stop, centrifuging at 1200 rpm for 5 minutes and discarding the supernatant to obtain a cell pellet, (b) resuspending the cell pellet with 0.5-5 ml trypsin, pipetting with an elbow pipette for 10-300 times, adding digestion solution to the turbidity, adding culture medium to stop the trypsin digestion, centrifuging at 1200 rpm for 5 minutes and discarding the supernatant to obtain primary cardiomyocytes, and (c) resuspending the primary cardiomyocytes with the culture solution, inoculating the culture solution at 0.1-10x 105 cells/cm2 and culturing in a 37 degrees C 5% carbon dioxide incubator.
   USE - The method is useful for separating and culturing cardiomyocytes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The PBS liquid comprises 1-100x 105 UI penicillin and 1-100x 105 UI streptomycin. The type I collagenase is formulated with Dulbecco's Modified Eagle's medium. The pancreatin is prepared from PBS. The culture solution comprises 10% fetal bovine serum and 90% Dulbecco's Modified Eagle's medium. Preferred Composition: The concentration of the type I collagenase is 200-800 U/ml. The concentration of the pancreatin is 0.1-0.35%. Preferred Method: The step (c) involves culturing for 2-5 days.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

TI Recycling glycidyl methacrylate distillation residue comprises e.g. determining hydroxy value of glycidyl methacrylate distillation residue, adding polyol to adjust total hydroxy value, adding phthalic anhydride and maleic anhydride, mixing.
AU LI Y
   CHENG Z
   SUN L
   SHUAI W
   YAO H
AE ANHUI XINYUAN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202041389J
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling glycidyl methacrylate distillation residue comprises (i) determining the hydroxy value of glycidyl methacrylate distillation residue, adding polyol to distillation residue to adjust the total hydroxy value, then adding phthalic anhydride and maleic anhydride and mixing, where the molar ratio between phthalic anhydride, maleic anhydride and total hydroxy group is 1:1:4.2-4.4, (ii) heating the above mixed liquid at 180-230 degrees C for conducting polycondensation reaction and monitoring the change of acid value, if acid value is less than 40 mgKOH/g , then the reaction is considered complete to obtain unsaturated polyester, and (iii) adding diluted styrene into unsaturated polyester to obtain unsaturated polyester resin, the molar weight ratio between styrene and maleic anhydride is 1.5-3:1.
   USE - The method is useful for recycling glycidyl methacrylate distillation residue.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: recycles and reuses glycidyl methacrylate rectification residual liquid; reduces the production cost of glycidyl methacrylate; and solves the problem that the existing rectification residual liquid processing method wastes resources, and large amount of carbon dioxide produced by incineration will cause damage to the environment.

AB    NOVELTY - Solid catalyst comprises 1-40 wt.% group VIII                metal nanoparticles, 60-98 wt.% adsorbent and 1-20                wt.% carbon-free grinding aid. The content of the                carbon-free grinding aid is preferably 5-20                wt.%.

   contacting treated solid with hydrogen to                obtain reduced solid; and
   co-grinding reduced solid with carbon-free                grinding aid to obtain solid catalyst.

TI Preparation of supported catalyst used for producing lower olefins involves roasting molecular sieve carrier, immersing in active metal salt solution, calcining, adding ammonium chloride solution, evaporating, roasting, and granulating.

   ZHANG S
   XUE L

AE SHAANXI COAL & CHEM IND TECHNOLOGY RES (SHCO-C)
GA 202039246Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of supported catalyst comprises roasting molecular sieve carrier at 400-700 degrees C for 3-8 hours, where molecular sieve carrier is SAPO-34 and SSZ-13; dissolving salt compound of active metal component in water to prepare first solution, where active metal component is element from groups IA, IIIA, IB, IIB, IVB, VIB, VIIB, and/or VIIIB; immersing molecular sieve carrier in first solution, stirring at room temperature to 90 degrees C for 2-24 hours, heating to evaporate water, and calcining to obtain catalyst precursor; and adding catalyst precursor to ammonium chloride solution, stirring at 20-90 degrees C for 2-18 hours, evaporating to dryness, roasting, and granulating.
   USE - The method is used for preparing supported catalyst for producing lower olefins (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively improves conversion rate of carbon dioxide while maintaining a high olefin selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for production of lower olefins comprising catalytic hydrogenating carbon monoxide with supported catalyst at 0.5-6 MPa, 1000-30000/hour, and 300-600 degrees C.

The active metal component is sodium, potassium, rubidium, cesium, aluminum, gallium, indium, copper zinc, zirconium, chromium, manganese, iron, copper, and/or nickel at mass fraction of 10-25 wt.%. The roasting temperature is 400-700 degrees C and roasting time is 2-8 hours.

The                suspension is transferred to ultrasonic shaker for                1 hours to make the filler uniformly and dispersed                in the polymer solution. PN FR3084742-A1; FR3084742-B1
TI Method for detecting anomalies in time series of samples of physical measurements e.g. to monitor moisture levels in soil, involves combining probabilistic process with active learning process to classify candidates.
AU HUNG L K
   PAOLO P
AE GREENCITYZEN SAS (GREE-Non-standard)
   GREENCITYZEN SAS (GREE-Non-standard)
GA 202011101A
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves defining time windows corresponding to time intervals. Anomalies or breakpoint candidates are searched without discriminating the anomalies. Score metric is calculated for candidate, which allows to assess isolated character. Probabilistic process is combined with active learning process to classify candidates whether the candidate belongings to isolated anomaly or collective anomaly or breaking point. Value of the candidate sample is compared with sum of the absolute median deviation. The metric as magnetude score is calculated as ratio between number of reciprocal neighbors of candidate.
   USE - Method for detecting anomalies in a time series of samples of physical measurements originating from sensors and separated by time intervals to monitor moisture levels in soil to trigger irrigation actions in an agricultural field and monitor carbon dioxide concentrations in buildings to trigger ventilation actions.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables minimizing measurement anomalies and achieving simple detection of anomaly in an effective manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view illustrating a method for detecting anomalies in a time series of samples of physical measurements. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'

MC S02-K02; S02-K07

PN KR2020008465-A; KR2124599-B1
TI New azo-based disperse dye compounds useful for supercritical fluid dyeing polyester fibers, synthetic fibers and polyester blend fibers.
AU SHIN S R
   JUN K
   SHIN J
   AN K L
AE KOREA RES INST CHEM TECHNOLOGY (KRIC-C)
GA 202009695G
AB    NOVELTY - Azo-based disperse dye compounds (I), are new.
   USE - (I) are useful for supercritical fluid dyeing (claimed) polyester fibers, synthetic fibers and polyester blend fibers.
   ADVANTAGE - (I) have excellent light fastness, wash fastness, physical properties and solubility characteristics for supercritical carbon dioxide, does not generate waste water due no water can be used and require drying process, can be recovered and reused in a dried state, and are environmentally friendly and economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Azo-based disperse dye compounds of formula (I), are new.
   A1 = substituted cyclic moieties of formulae (IIa) or (IIb);
   R1 and R2, R3 and R4 = H, amide, nitro, acetyl, 1-20C alkyl, 1-20C alkoxy, halo, cyano or SO2F;
   R5 and R6 = H, OH, acetamide, 1-20C alkyl, halo(1-20C)alkyl, (6-20C)aryl(1-20C) alkyl, hydroxy(1-20C)alkyl, (1-20C)alkoxycarbonyl(1-20C) alkyl or 1-20C alkoxycarbonyloxy; and
   R7 and R8 = hydrogen atom, hydroxy, acetamide, (1-20C)alkoxycarbonyl(1-20C)alkyl or 1-20C alkoxycarbonyloxy.
   Provided that: R8 is H or acetamide, when R2 is SO2F; and R4 is 1-20C alkyl, when R8 is OH or 1-20C alkoxycarbonyloxy. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises dispersing amine derivative of formula (A1-NH2) (IV) in solution containing water, acetic acid or a mixed solution of acetic acid and propionic acid, stirring, and diazotizing by adding hydrochloric acid, sodium nitrite or nitrosulfuric acid, (b) dissolving aryl derivative of formula (V) in alcohol or acetone to obtain coupler solution, and (c) mixing and stirring the diazotized solution obtain in the step (a) and the coupler solution obtain in the step (b).
   Preferred Definitions:
   R5 and R6 = e.g. -CH2-COOCH2-CH3, -CH2-CH3 and -CH2-CH2-OH;
   R8 = OH or -CH2-CH3; and
   R7 = -OH, -O-COOCH2-CH3 or -NHCOCH3.
   SPECIFIC COMPOUNDS - 36 Compounds (I) are disclosed e.g. N-(3-hydroxy-4-(2-(4-nitrophenyl)-diazenyl)phenyl)- N-(2-ethoxy-2-oxoethyl)-glycine ethyl ester of formula (Ia), (E)-diethyl 2,2'-(3-hydroxy-4-(p-tolyldiazenyl)phenylazanediyl)diacetate, (E)-diethyl 2,2'-(4-((4-acetylphenyl)diazenyl)-3-hydroxyphenylazanediyl)diacetate, (E)-diethyl 2,2'-(4-((4-carbamoylphenyl)diazenyl)-3-hydroxyphenylazanediyl)diacetate and (E)-diethyl 2,2'-(4-((2-chloro-4-nitrophenyl)diazenyl)-3-hydroxyphenylazanediyl)diacetate.
   EXAMPLE - 0.69 g 4-Nitro-5-aniline was dispersed in 10 ml acetic acid and stirred at room temperature for 30 minutes, 1.5 ml concentrated hydrochloric acid was slowly added and the reaction was cooled to 0-5 degrees C, 1.8 ml 3N sodium nitrite was slowly added for 20 minutes, reacted at 0-5 degrees C for 1 hour, 0.05 g sulfamic acid was added to remove excess nitrous acid and complete the diazotization reaction. 1.4 g N-(2-Ethoxy-2-oxoethyl)-N-(3-hydroxyphenyl)-glycine ethyl ester was completely dissolved in 5 ml methanol, the temperature was maintained at 0 degrees C, the diazotized solution was slowly added for 10 minutes, and stirred at 0-5 degrees C for 1.5 hours. 1.5 g Sodium acetate was added to the reaction solution, stirred at room temperature for 30 minutes, the reaction solution was added into 50 ml distilled water, stirred for 1 hour, the solid product obtained was filtered and washed with distilled water and methanol, and dried to obtain N-(3-hydroxy-4-(2-(4-nitrophenyl)-diazenyl)phenyl)- N-(2-ethoxy-2-oxoethyl)-glycine ethyl ester.

PT P
PN CN110726696-A
TI Method for imaging flowing gas, involves determining refractive index gradient of to-be-measured flowing gas to generate imaging of flowing gas according to ratio of emergent light intensity.
AU HU W
   ZHANG B
   WANG Y
   GU D
   SHEN C
AE AVIC SHENYANG ENGINE DESIGN INST (CHAV-C)
GA 202010489F
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves generating two parallel planar light beams. Two parallel planar light beams are converged to form an effective measurement area, where the effective measurement area is divided into multiple sub-areas, where the effective measurement area is perpendicular to to-be-measured flowing gas with carbon dioxide. Incident light intensity and emergent light intensity of the parallel planar light beams are obtained passing through the sub-area. Refractive index gradient of the to-be-measured flowing gas is determined to generate imaging of the flowing gas according to ratio of the incident light intensity and the emergent light intensity.
   USE - Method for imaging flowing gas.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a system for imaging flowing gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating a method for imaging flowing gas. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'

TI Damping composite material comprises thermoplastic elastomer, low density polyethylene, additives, oleic acid amide and polyethylene wax.
AU FANG J
   HE B
   DUAN Q
   HUANG L
   LIU Z
AE ANHUI ZHICHENG ELECTROMECHANICAL PARTS C (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2019A6116X
AB    NOVELTY - Damping composite material comprises 180-200 parts mass thermoplastic elastomer, 50-80 parts mass low density polyethylene, 80-150 parts mass additives, 60-90 parts mass oleic acid amide and 30-50 parts mass polyethylene wax.
   USE - As a damping composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material has excellent elasticity, shock absorption property, sound damping property, elasticity at low temperatures, compatibility, processing performance, aging resistance and reliability, overcomes shortcomings in bitumen-based damping and stiffening at low temperatures, does not produce toxic and harmful substances during its preparation process, adopts recyclable materials, has no pollution, and is environmentally-friendly and energy saving.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a thermal composite process for the damping composite material involves (a) weighing the above-mentioned raw materials, (b) adding the raw materials to a high speed mixer for heating and mixing, (c) adding the uniformly mixed mixture to a twin-screw extruder, melt-blending and extruding, placing the extruded material in an autoclave, introducing supercritical carbon dioxide to obtain a thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer composite material with micro-pores, and (d) after extruding the mixture, forming a sheet on the calender and determining the specific specifications of the sheet according to the actual use requirements.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The additives comprise a flame retardant, calcium carbonate, an antioxidant and an antistatic agent in a ratio of 3:2:1:1. The thermoplastic elastomer comprises a thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer or a thermoplastic polyolefin elastomer and/or styrene-ethylene-butene-styrene block copolymer (SEBS). Preferred Method: The step (c) involves mixing the thermoplastic polyolefin elastomer and styrene-ethylene-butene-styrene block copolymer by a process of extrusion lamination and coextrusion. The process of extrusion lamination and coextrusion involves adding the thermoplastic polyolefin elastomer and styrene-ethylene-butene-styrene block copolymer separately to 2 twin-screw extruders for melt-lamination and extrusion, combining the 2-layer melt of co-extrusion die formed, cutting the 2-layer melt, combining into a layered superposition unit, and cooling to obtain a layered composite material in which thermoplastic polyolefin elastomer and styrene-ethylene-butene-styrene block copolymer are alternately arranged. The step (d) involves extruding at a speed of 300 rpm and a temperature of the screw from the feed port to the head of 50 degrees C, 150 degrees C, 160 degrees C, 165 degrees C and 170 degrees C, respectively.

TI Improving corrosion resistance and abrasion resistance of brake disk involves cleaning, pre-oxidation, nitrocarburizing and subjecting to oxidation and immersing brake disk in cleaning liquid energized for certain time period.
AU ZHENG Z
   GU X
   XIAO W
   ZHAO Y
   JIA Z
   JING Z
AE TONGFU HEAT TREATMENT KUNSHAN CO LTD (TONG-Non-standard)
GA 201995531X
AB    NOVELTY - Improving corrosion resistance and abrasion resistance of a brake disk involves cleaning, pre-oxidation, nitrocarburizing and subjecting to oxidation. The brake disk is immersed in a cleaning liquid energized for a certain period of time, and then drying the brake disk after cleaning. The brake disk processed is placed immediately into the first furnace body and heating it for a certain period of time to perform pre-oxidation treatment on the brake disc. The brake disk processed is immediately placed in a second furnace body and heated for a certain period of time, and certain proportion of ammonia, carbon dioxide and nitrogen is continuously passed into the second furnace body, and heated for 360-380 minutes to perform nitrocarburizing treatment on the brake disc. The brake disk processed is immediately placed in third furnace body and heated for a certain time, and certain amount of water vapor is passed into the third furnace body to perform the brake disk Oxidation treatment.
   USE - Method for improving corrosion resistance and abrasion resistance of brake disc.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The temperature of the cleaning liquid is 40-60 degrees C. The concentration of the medium cleaning agent is 8-10 wt.%, current density in the cleaning solution is 144 A/m2 to 180 A / m2, and the brake disk is washed in the cleaning solution environment for 2-3 seconds, after drying the surface of the brake disk at a temperature of 220-280 degrees C. The temperature of the first furnace body is 410-450 degrees C, and the heating id carried out for 50-60 minutes. The temperature of the second furnace body is 550-600 degrees C for 360-380 minutes. The ratio of ammonia, carbon dioxide and nitrogen is 60%: 5%: 35%. The temperature of the third furnace body is 480-500 degrees C, and heating is carried out for 60-100 minutes. The temperature of the water vapor is 135-145 degrees C, and water vapor is used 2-2.2 times the volume of the third furnace body per hour. Preferred Composition: The solution of the cleaning liquid is deionized water, and solvent in the cleaning liquid includes 3.0-4.0g/L surfactant, 0.3-0.5 g/L builder and 1.2-1.6 g/L antifoaming agent.

TI Desiliconization and chromium retention of silicon and ferrochrome comprises taking pig iron, heating, sealing the ends of the glass tube, filling bottom with high-purity argon or nitrogen and measuring the content of silicon and vanadium.
AU LOU T
   WANG M
   LI C
   WANG J
   SUI Z
AE UNIV NORTHEASTERN (UYDB-C)
GA 201977095D
AB    NOVELTY - Desiliconization and chromium retention of silicon and ferrochrome comprises taking a certain amount of pig iron, placing in a graphite crucible, heating in an NH-GPL type high frequency induction furnace, placing the crucible in a glass tube, sealing the ends of the glass tube with a rubber plug, filling the bottom with high-purity argon or nitrogen, raising the temperature of the furnace and maintaining for 10 minutes, melting the molten iron into molten iron, opening the upper rubber plug, passing carbon dioxide gas directly into the bottom of the molten iron in the crucible through a fine corundum tube, maintaining a certain flow rate, taking the sample with another corundum tube after 10-40 minutes, cooling rapidly in the air and measuring the content of silicon, chromium and vanadium by plasma emission spectroscopy, where the furnace cover is quartz glass tube.
   USE - The method is useful for desiliconization and chromium retention of silicon and ferrochrome.
   ADVANTAGE - The method do not affect other elements in the molten iron and increases the content of other elements.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The furnace temperature is raised to 1350-1550 degrees C. The carbon dioxide gas flow rate is 3 l/minute.

TI Peony peptide used for providing anti-fatigue effect, comprises amino acids comprising lysine, phenylalanine and/or methionine.
AU XIE R
   WANG S
   ZHAO X
AE SHIKUNTANG GUANGDONG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (SHIK-Non-standard)
GA 201972724J
AB    NOVELTY - Peony peptide comprises 11-16 amino acids comprising lysine, phenylalanine and/or methionine.
   USE - The peony peptide is useful for providing anti-fatigue effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the peony peptide comprising (i) extracting peony protein from peony seed meal, (ii) extracting the peony protein obtained in the step (i) using supercritical carbon dioxide, (iii) adding the peony protein obtained in the step (ii) to distilled water in a mass ratio of 1:(20-25) to dissolve the peony protein, adjusting the temperature at 37-40 degrees C, and adjusting the pH value of the solution to be 8-10, (iv) adding 2-3 wt.% protease carrying out enzymolysis treatment for 30-50 minutes, (v) heating the solution obtained in the step (iv) to 100 degrees C, keeping for 10-15 minutes, and recovering at 50-55 degrees C, (vi) adding an 6-8% enzyme to the solution obtained in the step (v), carrying out enzymolysis for 2-2.5 hours, and rapidly boiling, (vii) carrying out centrifugal separation on the solution, and freeze-drying the supernatant, and (viii) dissolving the protein peptide obtained by freeze-drying by adding cyclodextrin again and spray drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The protease in step (iv) comprises ficin and subtilisin in a ratio of 1:(2-2.5), preferably 1:2. The addition amount of the protease in the step (iv) is 2.5 wt.%. The addition amount of the protease in step (vi) is 7 wt.%. Preferred Components: The protease in step (vi) is bromelain, chymotrypsin and endopeptidase-lys-2 in a ratio of (1-3):(2-5):(3-7), preferably 2:5:7. Preferred Conditions: The pH of the solution in step (iii) is adjusted to be 10. The enzymatic hydrolysis time in step (iv) is 40 minutes. The enzymatic hydrolysis time in step (vi) is 2.2 hours.
   ACTIVITY - CNS-Gen.; Muscular-Gen.

PN CN109928364-A; CN109928364-B
TI Preparation of deuterium by removing impurities of deuterium-containing heavy water penetrant membrane, pressurizing, heating, introducing hot water steam and high-purity methane into gas-phase catalytic reactor, purifying, and detecting.
AU CHEN Y
   CHEN L
   ZHAO Y
AE CHEN Y (CHEN-Individual)
   CHEN L (CHEN-Individual)
   ZHAO Y (ZHAO-Individual)
GA 201959066V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of deuterium comprises removing organic and inorganic impurities of deuterium-containing heavy water penetrant membrane, detecting impurity content if less than 10-6 g/L, pressurizing, heating, generating 403.15 K superheated steam at 1.5 atm, introducing hot water steam and high-purity methane into gas-phase catalytic reactor filled with nickel and cobalt catalyst, reacting at 1145-1155 K at 1.8-2.2 atm for 30-40 minutes, reducing pressure to 1.2 atm and temperature to 375.15 K, introducing into purification tower filled with soda lime, removing carbon dioxide and water vapor, purifying by mixed gas of hydrogen and helium, compressing, introducing into mixed liquid absorption fractionating tower filled with nickel, iron and manganese alloy, keeping at 21.5 K, collecting, and detecting isotope by spectrum detector if gas component is 98-99.99% hydrogen and liquid component is 84-88% deuterium.
   USE - The method is for preparing deuterium.
   ADVANTAGE - The method greatly reduces energy consumption and produces high-purity deuterium.

PN RD662108-A
TI Method for preventing contamination of immersion hood in lithographic immersion apparatus, involves turning off liquid and gas flow into or out of hood except for single gas flow out of outlet opening, and cleaning underlying surface.
AU 
AE ANONYMOUS (ANON-C)
GA 201950995A
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves turning off liquid and gas flow e.g. carbon dioxide gas flow, into or out of an immersion hood except for single gas flow out of an outlet opening in the immersion hood. An underlying surface e.g. wafer, sensor and encoder block, is cleaned by purging using the gas flow. The immersion hood is scanned over a chuck underneath the immersion hood such that contamination present on the underlying surface is blown away. Mopping movement is made by the immersion hood such that an area in front of the sensor and an area behind the sensor are cleaned.
   USE - Method for preventing contamination e.g. small fibers, of an immersion hood in a lithographic immersion apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preventing contamination of the immersion hood leading to less defectivity issues on customer product, leading to higher yield, and less machine downtime as servicing leading to higher availability.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a structure for preventing contamination of an immersion hood in a lithographic immersion apparatus.

PN CN109675634-A
TI Preparing heterogeneous Schiff base catalyst comprises e.g. adding diethylenetriamine and aldehyde into three-necked flask containing ethanol, stirring, dissolving Schiff base ligand in tetrahydrofuran, heating and drying.
AU JIA Q
   CHEN S
   SHAN S
   ZHAO W
   SU H
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 2019407872
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing heterogeneous Schiff base catalyst comprises (i) adding diethylenetriamine and aldehyde sequentially into three-necked flask containing ethanol, mixing, placing flask in oil bath, stirring reaction mixture at 70-80 degrees C for 16-24 hours, cooling at room temperature, evaporating solvent under reduced pressure to obtain concentrated liquid product, and drying to obtain Schiff base ligand, (ii) dissolving Schiff base ligand in tetrahydrofuran, heating at 35-45 degrees C, adding metal compound under nitrogen atmosphere, stirring for 16-24 hours, heating at room temperature, stirring under oxygen atmosphere for 2-4 hours, suction filtering, and drying solid product to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing heterogeneous Schiff base catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has simple operation and high selectivity and conversion rate of epoxide. The method is suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of heterogeneous Schiff base catalyst for synthesizing cyclic carbonate comprising adding epoxide, heterogeneous Schiff base catalyst, cocatalyst and dichloromethane into autoclave, and reacting at 60-80 degrees C for 2-8 hours in the presence of carbon dioxide to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aldehyde is salicylaldehyde, 3,5-di-tert-butyl salicylaldehyde, 3-tert-butyl-5-methoxy salicylaldehyde, 3,5-dichlorosalicylaldehyde and/or 3-chloromethyl-5-nitrosalicylaldehyde. The molar ratio between diethylenetriamine and aldehyde is 2:1-4:1. The metal compound is transition metal compound including chromium(II) chloride, manganese(II) acetate, cobalt(II) acetate tetrahydrate, copper(II) chloride or zinc chloride. The molar ratio between metal compound and Schiff base ligand is 1:1-3:1. The epoxide is propylene oxide or cyclohexene oxide. The cocatalyst is bis(triphenylphosphoranylidene)ammonium chloride or 4-dimethylaminopyridine. The molar ratio between epoxide, heterogeneous Schiff base catalyst, cocatalyst and dichloromethane is 1500:50:0.8-1:0.5-1.

AE SICHUAN AEROSPACE SYSTEM ENG INST (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2019248277
AB    NOVELTY - Vacuum insulating plate comprises composite core material formed by a composite aerogel sol on an ultrafine glass fiber substrate and the core material covered by an outer layer aluminum foil composite.
   USE - Used as vacuum insulating plate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method evolves carbon monoxide gas by 0.054 mu g/g and removes total organic matter by 1.372 mu g/g.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the vacuum insulating plate, comprising cutting and forming ultrafine glass fiber into a sol tank, uniformly injecting the aerogel into the sol tank, after finishing the sol gel composite, adding 15% hydrophobization agent trimethylchlorosilane into gel-sol tank for hydrophobization treatment, subjecting the hydrophobized aerogel to solvent replacement using 85% tetraethyl orthosilicate, removing the moisture in the aerogel by supercritical drying using carbon dioxide in a container, subjecting the dried fiber-reinforced aerogel material to secondary cutting, sewing core material by hot glue using aluminum foil composite material, leaving an air hole, vacuumizing and sealing.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the vacuum insulating plate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The volume ratio of ultrafine glass fiber and aerogel composite sol is 0.45:1. The ultrafine glass fiber is base material and aerogel carrier, the substrate is BGZ-type superfine glass fiber, fiber monofilament diameter is 2-3 mu m and the heat conductivity at room temperature is 0.032 W/(mK). The aerogel is silicon dioxide aerogel and the silicon dioxide is three-dimensional nano-solid structure with a density of 0.12 g/cm3 and the thermal conductivity is less than 0.005 W/(mK). The aluminum foil composite is used for vacuum wrapping a shaped core material of the order of 1235 aluminum alloy and having a thickness of 7 microns. Preferred Method: The supercritical drying is carried out at 38 degrees C using carbon dioxide with a pressure of 9.5 MPa for 4 hours.

PT P
PN CN109320419-A
TI Synthesizing a dialkyl carbonate of a biomass-based furfural comprises e.g. purifying biomass-based furfural by acid hydrolysis and dehydration cyclization using biomass-based agricultural and forestry waste as a starting material.
AU LI X
   WANG Z
   LI Q
   LIN G
AE QINGDAO INST BIOENERGY & BIOPROCESS TECH (CAQD-C)
GA 201917282W
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing a dialkyl carbonate of a biomass-based furfural comprises (i) purifying the biomass-based furfural by acid hydrolysis and dehydration cyclization using biomass-based agricultural and forestry waste as a starting material, (ii) subjecting the biomass-based furfural to an oxidation reaction under the action of hydrogen peroxide and catalyst to synthesize maleic anhydride, (iii) subjecting maleic anhydride to epoxidation under the action of catalyst and hydrogen peroxide to synthesize epoxy succinic acid, (iv) epoxy succinic acid and carbon dioxide ring addition reaction to obtain cyclic carbonate, and (v) subjecting the cyclic carbonate to exchange reaction with monohydric alcohol or polyhydric alcohol to obtain dialkyl carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing a dialkyl carbonate of a biomass-based furfural.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses the cheap agricultural and forestry waste as raw material, produces high added value tartaric acid, saves production cost, reduces environmental pollution, provides new way for industrialization by producing dialkyl carbonate, tartaric acid and agriculture and forestry waste, and has significant economic and social benefits.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the acid used for the acid hydrolysis is sulfuric acid, and the hydrolysis conditions is as follows: the reaction temperature is 110-130 degrees C, the holding time is 2-3 hours , the mass fraction of the sulfuric acid is 3-5%, and the liquid-solid ratio (V/W) of dilute sulfuric acid and agricultural and forestry waste is 8-15:1. In the step (i), the catalyst used in the dehydration cyclization reaction is sulfuric acid, the reaction temperature is 180-210 degrees C, the holding time is 5-6 hours, and the mass fraction of the sulfuric acid is 20-30%. In the step (ii), the maleic anhydride synthesis reaction is carried out under the action of a heteropolyacid molybdenum tungsten phosphorus/silicon dioxide (MoWP/SiO2) catalyst, and the amount ratio of the substances of phosphorus, molybdenum and tungsten in the catalyst is 1:0.8-0.9:0.03-0.04, and the heteropoly acid is loaded in a mass fraction of 40-50% of silicon dioxide. In the step (ii), the maleic anhydride synthesis reaction condition is as follows: the mass ratio of the catalyst to furfural is 0.09-0.1, the ratio of the amount of the solvent dichloroethane to the furfural substance is 2.5-3.5, the ratio of the amount of the oxidizing agent hydrogen peroxide to the amount of furfural substance is 2-3, and the reaction temperature is 50-70 degrees C. In the step (iii), the reaction of synthesizing the epoxy succinic acid is carried out under the action of a tungsten/Mobil Composition of Matter No. 41 (W/MCM-41) catalyst, and the loading of tungsten in the catalyst is 20-50%. In the step (iii), the reaction temperature of the epoxy succinic acid is 55-75 degrees C, the reaction time is 1.5-3 hours, where the mass ratio of 30% hydrogen peroxide to maleic anhydride is 1:1.3-1.8, and the concentration of the catalyst is 4-6% of the mass of maleic anhydride. In the step (iv), the reaction of synthesizing the cyclic carbonate is carried out under the action of a magnesium oxide, zinc oxide and cerium oxide compound oxide catalyst, where the concentration of cerium oxide is 0.1-1% of the mass of the compound oxide catalyst, and the molar ratio of magnesium oxide to zinc oxide is 1-3:1. In the step (iv), the reaction temperature of the cyclic carbonate is 90-160 degrees C, the reaction time is 1-2 hours, the molar ratio of epoxy succinic acid to carbon dioxide is 1:5-20, and the concentration of the catalyst is 1-4% of the mass of the epoxy succinic acid. In the step (v), the transesterification reaction is carried out under the action of a magnesium oxide, zinc oxide and cerium oxide compound oxide catalyst, where the concentration of the cerium oxide is 0.1-1% of the mass of compound oxide catalyst, and the molar ratio of magnesium oxide to zinc oxide is 1-3:1. In the step (iv), the molar ratio of dimethyl carbonate to monohydric or polyhydric alcohol in the transesterification reaction is 1:1-10, the weight of the catalyst is 0.1-3 wt.% based on the total weight of the dimethyl carbonate and monohydric or polyhydric alcohol, the reaction temperature is 120-180 degrees C, the reaction pressure is 1-4 MPa, and the reaction time is 90-120 minutes. Preferred Components: The monohydric or polyhydric alcohol is 2-8C linear alcohols, 3-8C branched alcohols and/or 3-8C cyclic alcohols, and alcohols bearing furan ring.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.)
MC E10-A11B1; E11-A01; E11-A02; E11-E03; E11-W; N01-B; N03-A02A; N03-C02; N03-D02; N03-F01; N04-C; N06-F; N07-C03; N07-F07; N07-F08

PN CN109293900-A; CN109293900-B
TI Preparation of microporous polymer useful for            storage and transportation of gases e.g. hydrogen,            involves dissolving monomer in anhydrous organic            solvent, adding alcohol and reacting, adding            concentrated hydrochloric acid and drying.
AU QIAO S
   ZHANG B
   LI Z
   JIN W
   LI Q
   ZHAO J
   XU Y
AE UNIV HEBEI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYKJ-C)
GA 201914678Q
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of microporous polymer                involves dissolving monomer in anhydrous organic                solvent (a1), adding mixed solution of anhydrous                ferric chloride and anhydrous organic solvent (a2),                stirring at room temperature for 20-30 hours,                adding alcohol (b1), continuously stirring at room                temperature, reacting for 0.8-1.5 hours and then                filtering, adding alcohol (b2) and then washing,                adding concentrated hydrochloric acid, stirring at                800-1200 rpm for 1.6-2.5 hours, washing using                water, adding alcohol (b3), extracting using                alcohol (b4) and organic solvent for 20-30 hours                and drying. The monomer, anhydrous ferric chloride,                anhydrous organic solvent (a1), anhydrous organic                solvent (a2) and alcohol (b1) are used in a ratio                of 0.25-0.35 kg:30-50 l:0.7-0.85 kg:30-50 l:80-120                l.
   USE - The method is useful for preparation of                microporous polymer, which is useful for storage                and transportation of gases such as hydrogen,                carbon dioxide and methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of microporous                polymer with high specific surface area and                excellent adsorption performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The anhydrous organic                  solvent (a1) and anhydrous organic solvent (a2)                  are chloroform, carbon tetrachloride,                  tetrahydrofuran, acetone or dichloromethane. The                  alcohol (b1), alcohol (b2), alcohol (b3) and                  alcohol (b4) are methanol, ethanol or                  isopropanol.

   BEIJING DEEZOO INTELLIGENT TECHNOLOGY CO (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2019090383
AB    NOVELTY - A modified graphene oxide-doped supercapacitor preparing method involves taking 60-80 pts. wt. water, 80-120 pts. wt. n-hexane, 15-20 pts. wt. nanometer graphene oxide and 4-8 pts. wt. polyallylamine, stirring and mixing uniformly and continuously passing a carbon dioxide during stirring to obtain a self-emulsion. 200-300 pts. wt. self-emulsion, 1-3 pts. wt. cerium salt and 10-20 pts. wt. manganese salt are taken, stirred and mixed, added with precipitant to adjust the pH to 8.5-9.0, after continuing to stir the reaction and allowed to stand to obtain aging material. The aging material is transferred to the reaction vessel, introduced with an inert gas continuously at 55-60 degrees C for 30-40 minutes, cooled, discharged, stand stratified, and an oil phase is collected. The oil phase is separated by centrifugation, and the lower layer precipitate is collected, washed and dried to obtain the finished product.
   USE - Method for preparing modified graphene oxide doped supercapacitor.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing modified graphene oxide-doped supercapacitor with excellent electrochemical performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cerium salt is cerium nitrate, cerium chloride and cerium sulfate. Preferred Composition: The precipitant is an ammonia water having a mass fraction of 8-10% or a urea solution having a mass fraction of 8-10%.

PN CN109046435-A
TI Preparation of nitrogen defect-modified inverse            opal structure carbon nitride for e.g. photocatalytic            degradation of antibiotics, involves mixing            dicyandiamide, silicon dioxide pellets and ammonium            chloride, calcining, etching and drying.
AU LIU Y
   LEI J
   TIAN Y
   ZHANG J
   WANG L
   ZHOU L
   CHEN B
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 201901085W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of nitrogen defect-modified                inverse opal structure carbon nitride, involves                mixing dicyandiamide, silicon dioxide pellets and                ammonium chloride, grinding, mixing, calcining at                520-550 degrees C in argon gas flow protection,                etching, washing and drying. The silicon dioxide                pellets are prepared by adding tetraethyl silicate                to ethanol, stirring to obtain solution (A), adding                water and ammonia separately to ethanol to form                solution (B), quickly adding solution (A) to                solution (B), stirring in an oil bath, washing the                sample by centrifugation, drying, dispersing the                silicon ball in water and evaporating to dryness at                a certain temperature.
   USE - Preparation of nitrogen defect-modified                inverse opal structure carbon nitride for                photocatalytic degradation of antibiotics,                photocatalytic hydrogen production, reduction of                carbon dioxide and production of hydrogen                peroxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method introduces nitrogen defects and                improves activity while ensuring the structure of                the inverse opal and excellent photoelectric                properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for nitrogen                defect-modified inverse opal structure carbon                nitride, which is provided with carbon-to-nitrogen                ratio of 0.74.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The amount of silica                  pellet is 1 g. The amount of ammonium chloride is                  0.6 g. Preferred Properties: The silica pellets                  have a diameter of 200 nm. Preferred Process: The                  silica pellets are prepared by adding 8 mL                  tetraethyl silicate to 92 mL ethanol and stirring                  evenly to obtain solution (A), then separately                  adding 29.4 mL water and 14 mL aqueous ammonia to                  56.6 mL ethanol to form solution (B), quickly                  adding solution (A) to solution (B), stirring in                  an oil bath at 25 degrees C for 24 hours, washing                  the sample by centrifugation, drying, dispersing                  5 %mass silicon pellets in water, placing in                  electric blower box at 110 degrees C and                  evaporating to dryness. The calcining process is                  carried out by heating in a tubular atmosphere                  furnace from room temperature to 520 degrees C at                  heating rate of 25 degrees C/minute, warming for                  2 hours, then increasing temperature to 550                  degrees C at a heating rate of 4 degrees C/minute                  for 2 hours. The whole process of calcination is                  carried out in the protection of argon gas at                  flow rate of 0.2 l/minute. After the calcined                  product is ground, the method involves etching                  with 4 mol/L ammonium bifluoride solution for 48                  hours and washing etched product several times.                  The water washing is centrifugal washing at 12500                  rpm for 15 minutes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The amount of                  dicyandiamide is 0.6 g.
