TI Manufacture of hydrogen used for manufacturing fuel, involves decomposing hydrocarbon into hydrogen and carbon by contacting with catalyst which is material containing metal chosen from e.g. rhodium and nickel supported by carrier.
AU ABE T
   SUZUKI M
   LU B W
AE UNIV TOYAMA (UTOY-C)
GA 2018950615
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of hydrogen involves decomposing hydrocarbon into hydrogen and carbon by contacting the hydrocarbon with a catalyst (23) at 200-250 degrees C. The catalyst is a material containing a metal supported by a carrier, or the metal. The metal is chosen from ruthenium, nickel, platinum, palladium, rhodium, and iridium.
   USE - Manufacture of hydrogen used for manufacturing fuel (claimed) for domestic fuel cell and fuel cell for electric vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of hydrogen with suppressed carbon dioxide emission using catalyst having high activity at low temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) catalyst; and
   (2) manufacture of fuel, which involves contacting a natural gas or a liquefied petroleum gas with the catalyst at 200-250 degrees C, decomposing a portion of natural gas or liquefied petroleum gas into hydrogen and carbon, and obtaining fuel in which hydrogen and natural gas or liquefied petroleum gas are mixed.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the cross-sectional view of fixed bed flow-type reactor.
   Silica wool (22)
   Catalyst (23)
   Heater (24)
   Water bubbler (25)
   Container (26)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The particle size of the metal is 8 nm or less.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Manufacture of hydrogen further involves supplying steam to the catalyst, adhering steam to the surface of the metal or the carrier, decomposing hydrocarbon into hydrogen and carbon dioxide by contacting the hydrocarbon with steam at 250-350 degrees C or contacting the hydrocarbon and the steam with the catalyst at 350-500 degrees C, sputter-stirring the carrier in a vacuum container, when the internal cross-sectional shape rotates or moves the vacuum container having a circular shape or a polygon by setting a rotating shaft as the vertical direction with respect to the cross-section, and carrying out dispersion of the material containing the metal or the metal on the surface of the carrier. Manufacture of fuel further involves recovering carbon, after decomposing into hydrogen and carbon, and adjusting the hydrogen ratio in the fuel to 0-100% by adjusting the temperature.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The carrier contains microparticles chosen from carbon microparticles, metal microparticles, nitride microparticles, polymer particulate, silylated microparticles, and oxide microparticles.

TI Method for making tobacco extract in e.g.            cigarettes, involves exposing extraction solvent            containing tobacco components to conditions in vessel            at which extraction solvent is subcritical, and flowing            entrapment solvent through vessel.
AU SINTYUREVA M
   DIGARD H
AE BRITISH AMERICAN TOBACCO INVESTMENTS LTD (BRTA-C)
   BRITISH AMERICAN TOBACCO PLC (BRTA-C)
   BRITISH AMERICAN TOBACCO INVESTMENTS LTD (BRTA-C)

   NICOVENTURES TRADING LTD (NIVE-C)
   NICOVENTURES TRADING LTD (NIVE-C)
GA 201891350B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves contacting tobacco with an                extraction solvent such that tobacco components are                extracted from the tobacco into the solvent, where                the extraction solvent comprises supercritical                fluid comprising carbon dioxide and subcritical.                Residual tobacco solids are separated from the                supercritical extraction solvent containing the                tobacco components. The extraction solvent                containing the tobacco components is exposed to                conditions in a vessel at which the extraction                solvent is subcritical for releasing the tobacco                components from the extraction solvent, where the                tobacco components include nicotine and tobacco                aromas and flavors. Entrapment solvent is flowed                through the vessel, where the entrapment solvent                dissolves the tobacco components and comprises an                aerosol generating agent.
   USE - Method for making a tobacco extract in a                smoking article (all claimed) e.g. cigarettes and                cigars. Uses include but are not limited to                licorice, hydrangea, chamomile, fenugreek, clove,                menthol, aniseed, cinnamon, herb and                wintergreen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficiently and effectively                separating the tobacco components and extraction                solvent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                cartridge for use in a smoking article.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - The entrapment solvent comprises glycerol                  and/or propylene glycol.

TI Producing sodium bicarbonate comprises e.g.            providing solid-liquid mixture containing crude alkali            as reaction raw material, and decomposing reaction raw            material to obtain decomposition solution containing            sodium carbonate.
AU KONG D
   TANG Y
   SONG G
   LEI L
   GONG Y
   LIU C
AE BEIJING EDGEIN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
   SICHUAN GOLDEN ELEPHANT CHEM IND GROUP C (CNCC-C)
GA 2018872654
AB    NOVELTY - Producing sodium bicarbonate comprises (a)                providing a solid-liquid mixture containing a crude                alkali as a reaction raw material, and decomposing                the reaction raw material to obtain decomposition                solution containing sodium carbonate and                decomposition gas containing carbon dioxide, and                (b) purifying decomposed gas to obtain carbonized                gas, introducing carbonized gas in decomposing                liquid, carrying out a carbonation crystallization                reaction, and carrying out solid-liquid separation                to obtain sodium bicarbonate and the recovered                mother liquid.
   USE - The method is useful for producing sodium                bicarbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is environmentally friendly, and                economical; and reduces energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                sodium bicarbonate production device comprising                decomposition tower connected in series, reaction                crystallizer and a separation device, and a                purification treatment device. The inlet of the                purification treatment device is connected to a gas                outlet of the decomposition tower. The outlet of                the purification treatment device is connected to                the gas inlet of the reaction crystallizer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows flow diagram of the                producing sodium bicarbonate (Drawing includes                non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amount of carbon                  dioxide in carbonized gas is greater than or                  equal to 90%. Preferred Method: The decomposition                  temperature of the reaction raw material is                  60-150 degrees C. In the process, subjecting                  decomposition gas for purification treatment,                  condensing at 10-70 degrees C and washing using                  soft water or refined brine. In the process,                  subjecting decomposing gas for heat transfers                  reaction with reaction raw material to exchange                  heat, maintaining flow rate of the decomposition                  gas at 5-50 m/second, and flow rate of the                  reaction raw material at 1-10 m/second. In the                  process, providing gas containing carbon dioxide                  as supplementary gas, mixing with decomposition                  gas, subjecting for purification treatment to                  obtain carbonized gas or supplying gas containing                  carbon dioxide as a supplementary gas, purifying,                  introducing into the decomposition liquid for                  carbonation reaction, where gas is a calciner gas                  produced during the alkali production process. In                  the process, recycling portion of the recovered                  solution to step (a) as a mother liquor to                  dissolve the crude heavy base for reuse and                  discharging another portion.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The purification                  treatment device and the reaction crystallizer                  are connected by a gas pressure device. The                  purification treatment device comprises a                  condenser and a scrubber which are connected. The                  gas outlet of the decomposition tower is                  connected to an inlet of the condenser. The                  outlet of the scrubber is coupled to an inlet of                  the gas boosting device, and an outlet of the gas                  boosting device is coupled to a gas inlet of the                  reactive crystallizer. The heat exchanger is                  disposed between the decomposition tower and                  condenser. The gas outlet of the decomposition                  column is connected to a gas inlet of the heat                  exchanger. The gas outlet of the heat exchanger                  is connected to an inlet of the condenser. The                  liquid inlet of the heat exchanger is connected                  to a mixer. The liquid outlet of the heat                  exchanger is connected to the liquid inlet of the                  decomposition column. The reaction crystallizer                  is a forced circulation crystallizer comprising                  an inner circulation crystallizer and an outer                  circulation crystallizer, preferably an OSL                  crystallizer. The separation device comprises a                  centrifuge and a dryer, the inlet of the                  centrifuge is connected to the liquid outlet of                  the reaction crystallizer. The liquid outlet of                  the centrifuge is connected to a mixer.

PN CN108654403-A
TI Method for preparing carbon dioxide gas separation membrane, involves mixing methanol solution and hexafluorobisphenol A, placing in ice bath and stirring, followed by filtering separation membrane liquid and drying.
AU HU L
   LIN M
   ZHU C
AE FOSHAN LINGXIE ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 2018847756
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide gas separation membrane preparing method involves mixing methanol solution and hexafluorobisphenol A, placing in an ice bath, stirring to obtain pre-reaction solution. The pre-reaction solution is mixed with N,N-dimethylthiocarbonyl chloride, stirred and filtered to obtain filter residue. The filter residue is washed to obtain a self-made diamine monomer, followed by pulverizing soy protein isolate, egg white and squid shall, kneading, mixing with water and protease, putting into an enzymatic hydrolysis tank, performing enzymatically dissolving, heating, stirring and separating the filtrate, mixing the pulverized zeolite with the filtrate, and charging it into a fermenter for fermenting and filtering to obtain the fermentation filter residue, mixing the fermentation filter residue with the polymer solution and ultrasonically oscillating to obtain a separation membrane liquid, filtering the separation membrane liquid and drying to obtain the finished product.
   USE - Method for preparing carbon dioxide gas separation membrane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing carbon dioxide gas separation membrane with increased permeation rate of carbon dioxide and the separation coefficient of the gas pair, enhanced solubility of carbon dioxide, excellent permeability and selective separation, and broad application prospects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon dioxide gas separation membrane preparing method involves mixing 10% methanol solution and hexafluorobisphenol A into a three-necked flask equipped with a stirrer, and placing the three-necked flask in an ice bath at 0 degrees C, stirring for 30-40 minutes to obtain pre-reaction solution. The pre-reaction solution is mixed with N,N-dimethylthiocarbonyl chloride, stirred in ice bath at 0 degrees C for 4-5 hours and filtered to obtain filter residue. The filter residue is washed with 50% methanol solution at 4 degrees C for 3-5 times to obtain a self-made diamine monomer, followed by weighing soy protein isolate, egg white and squid shall, placing in a tissue pulverizer, and pulverizing for 30-40 minutes to obtain a pulverized material, kneading the pulverized material into a dough and repeatedly beating for 200-250 times to obtain a pulverized product, mixing the pulverized product with water and protease, putting it into an enzymatic hydrolysis tank, performing enzymatically dissolving, heating the enzymatic hydrolyzed product to 80-90 degrees C, stirring for 20-30 minutes, and separating the filtrate by filtration, weighing the zeolite into a pulverizer and pulverizing for 1-2 hours to obtain a pulverized zeolite, mixing the pulverized zeolite with the filtrate, and charging it into a fermenter, first placing the fermenter at 25-30 degrees C under the condition of 3-5 days, allowing the re-sealed tank mouth to stand for fermentation at 35-45 degrees C for 7-10 days, and filtering to obtain the fermentation filter residue, mixing diamine monomer with 1,4-bis(3,4-dicarboxyphenylthio)phthalic anhydride and m-cresol, charging the mixture into a three-necked flask, heating the mixture under a flowing nitrogen atmosphere until the temperature is raised to 50-60 degrees C, stirring until the reactants are transparent, and heating to 150-180 degrees C, stirring for 12-16 hours to obtain a polymer solution, mixing the fermentation filter residue with the polymer solution, placing it in an ultrasonic oscillator and ultrasonically oscillating to obtain a separation membrane liquid, filtering the separation membrane liquid with a nylon mesh, pouring it on a horizontal glass plate, and drying using an oven at 55-60 degrees C for 24-30 hours, raising the temperature to 105-110 degrees C, and continuing drying for 24-30 hours to obtain the finished product.

TI Method for preparing trichloroisocyanuric acid            involves mixing pretreated carbide slag and leaching            agent solution, mixing cyanuric acid and obtained            calcium carbonate, introducing chlorine gas to generate            trichloroisocyanuric acid, calcium chloride and carbon            dioxide, and centrifuging.

   LIU B
   PENG Y
   ZHANG H
   HAN Q
AE DEZHOU SHIHUA CHEM CO LTD (CNCC-C)
GA 202277278L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing trichloroisocyanuric acid                using calcium carbide slag, involves mixing                pretreated carbide slag and leaching agent                solution, filtering the reaction solution,                introducing carbon dioxide to obtain calcium                carbonate precipitate and recycling leaching agent                solution, washing calcium carbonate and drying to                obtain calcium carbonate product, mixing cyanuric                acid, calcium carbonate and water, introducing                chlorine gas into the mixed solution to react to                generate trichloroisocyanuric acid, calcium                chloride and carbon dioxide, centrifuging, washing                and drying to obtain trichloroisocyanuric acid,                mixing centrifugal mother liquor and product                washing liquid, collecting the mother liquor,                adding lime slurry to the mother liquor, filtering                the mixture, and calcining concentrated solution to                obtain anhydrous calcium chloride and introducing                generated carbon dioxide into the mixed solution of                cyanuric acid and calcium carbonate to absorb                chlorine gas.
   USE - Method for preparing trichloroisocyanuric acid                using calcium carbide slag
   ADVANTAGE - The process is simple, and                environmentally-friendly recycling of calcium                carbide slag produced in chlor-alkali enterprises                for the preparation of trichloroisocyanuric acid.                The chlorine gas by-product of the chlor-alkali                enterprise is used as raw material to realize                on-site consumption of chlorine gas, and the                wastewater and waste gas generated in the                production process are treated to realize clean                production of trichloroisocyanuric acid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing trichloroisocyanuric acid                using calcium carbide slag, involves mixing                pretreated carbide slag and leaching agent solution                to dissolve and react, collecting the reaction                solution, filtering the reaction solution,                introducing carbon dioxide to obtain calcium                carbonate precipitate and recycling the leaching                agent solution, washing calcium carbonate and                drying to obtain calcium carbonate product, mixing                cyanuric acid, calcium carbonate and water,                introducing chlorine gas into the mixed solution to                react to generate trichloroisocyanuric acid,                calcium chloride and carbon dioxide, centrifuging                the reaction product, washing and drying to obtain                trichloroisocyanuric acid, mixing centrifugal                mother liquor and product washing liquid, adjusting                pH value to 1-2 with hydrochloric acid, reacting to                generate chlorine gas and cyanuric acid, collecting                the mother liquor, reusing chlorine gas and                cyanuric acid generated by the reaction                respectively, adding lime slurry to the mother                liquor until the pH value of the mixture reaches                6-7, filtering the mixture under pressure,                evaporating and concentrating the filtrate until                the concentration of calcium chloride is 30-35                wt.%, and calcining the concentrated solution to                obtain anhydrous calcium chloride, introducing                generated carbon dioxide into the mixed solution of                cyanuric acid and calcium carbonate to absorb                chlorine gas and using calcium carbonate for                preparation.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a system for preparing                trichloroisocyanuric acid with calcium carbide                slag, which includes a pretreatment device, a                leaching device, a carbonization device, a                filtration device, a first washing/drying system, a                raw material preparation device, a chlorination                reaction device, a centrifugal separation device, a                second washing/drying system, a static mixer, a                mother liquor treatment device, a neutralization                reactor, a filter press device, an evaporative                concentration device, and a calcination device,                where the pretreatment device, leaching device,                carbonization device, filtering device, first                washing/drying system, raw material preparation                device, chlorination reaction device, centrifugal                separation device and the second washing/drying                system are connected in sequence, the centrifugal                separation device and second washing/drying system                are connected with a static mixer, the static                mixer, mother liquor treatment device,                neutralization reactor, filter press device,                evaporation concentration device and calcination                device are connected in sequence, and the mother                liquor treatment device is connected with the raw                material preparation device and the chlorination                reaction device; and
   (2) an environmentally-friendly recycling                system of waste residue, which includes the system,                where the waste residue is carbide slag

PT P
PN WO2022058188-A1
TI Production of glyoxal by converting input stream comprising ethylene glycol and oxygen in presence of heterogeneous catalyst containing copper or silver to output stream containing glyoxal, water, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide and cooling output stream to obtain aqueous solution of glyoxal.
AU ZAKZESKI J J
   SIEMER M
   WALSDORFF C
   LUENSE J
   MOHL K
   HORN C
   STEFAN R
   HAMMEN O
   KARPOV A
AE BASF SE (BADI-C)
GA 202242935P
AB    NOVELTY - A process for continuous production of glyoxal by oxidation of ethylene glycol in gas phase with oxygen, comprises (a) feeding an input stream (I) comprising ethylene glycol and oxygen to a reactor (A),(b) converting the input stream (I) in reactor (A) at temperature of 180-700 degrees C and pressure of 0.05-1 MPa abs in the presence of a heterogeneous catalyst containing at least one of copper and silver to output stream (II) containing glyoxal, water, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, (c) cooling the output stream (II) in cooling zone (B) to obtain an aqueous solution of glyoxal (III) and a glyoxal depleted, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide containing gas stream (IV), and (d) oxidizing carbon monoxide in the glyoxal depleted gas stream (IV) with oxygen in reactor (C) in the presence of heterogeneous oxidation catalyst to carbon dioxide, and obtaining a carbon monoxide depleted gas stream (V).
   USE - The process is used for continuous production of glyoxal, which is used as synthesis building block in production of higher refined chemicals or as reducing agent. The glyoxal is uses as crosslinker of functionalized polymers (such as cellulose, polyacrylamide, polyvinyl alcohol or keratin) and as a synthesis building block for production of heterocycles.
   ADVANTAGE - The process enables continuous production of glyoxal in easy manner, and enables the use of common ethylene glycol oxidation catalysts and in which glyoxal can be obtained as an aqueous solution in high yield and high purity. The process enables a high selectivity in the conversion of ethylene glycol to glyoxal in order to better utilize the raw material and to reduce unwanted by-products. The process is safe in performance, and constant in high glyoxal quality and functions stably over long operating times and enables easy retrofitting of existing glyoxal production plants. The process does not require the addition of external inert diluent gases since the recycle gas functions as diluent and significantly reduces the amount of off gas to be disposed, and increases the efficiency of the whole process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A process for continuous production of glyoxal by oxidation of ethylene glycol in gas phase with oxygen, comprises (a) feeding an input stream (I) comprising 1-10 vol.% ethylene glycol and 1-10 vol.% oxygen based on the input stream (I) to a reactor (A), (b) converting the input stream (I) in reactor (A) at temperature of 180-700 degrees C and pressure of 0.05-1 MPa abs in the presence of a heterogeneous catalyst containing at least one of copper and silver to output stream (II) containing glyoxal, water, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, (c) cooling the output stream (II) in cooling zone (B) to obtain an aqueous solution of glyoxal (III) and a glyoxal depleted, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide containing gas stream (IV), (d) oxidizing carbon monoxide in the glyoxal depleted gas stream (IV) with oxygen in reactor (C) in the presence of heterogeneous oxidation catalyst to carbon dioxide, and obtaining a carbon monoxide depleted gas stream (V), (e) recycling the carbon monoxide depleted gas stream (V) as part of the input stream (I) to reactor (A),and (f) adding ethylene glycol (VI) and oxygen (VII) as further part of the input stream (I), where the input stream (I) comprises 10-3000 vol. ppm carbon monoxide based on the input stream (I).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a simplified block diagram of process for continuous production of glyoxal.
   Reactor (A, C)
   Cooling zone (B)
   Input stream (I)
   Output stream (II)
   Aqueous glyoxal solution (III)
   Glyoxal depleted gas stream (IV)
   Carbon monoxide depleted gas stream (V)
   Ethylene glycol (VI)
   Oxygen (VII)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The heterogeneous catalyst used in reactor (A) in step (b) contains copper. The heterogeneous oxidation catalyst used in reactor (C) in step (d) is a supported catalyst. The heterogeneous oxidation catalyst has a monolithic shape. The heterogeneous oxidation catalyst used in reactor (C) in step (d) contains at least one of platinum, palladium, ruthenium and gold. Preferred Properties: The monolithic heterogeneous oxidation catalyst has a cell density of 4-250 cells per cm2. The monolithic heterogeneous oxidation catalyst contains platinum in an amount of 0.035-7 g/dm3. Preferred Composition: The reactor (A) is operated isothermally and the silver/copper mass ratio of the total amount of the catalyst filling in reactor (A) is 0-0.25.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The input stream (I) comprises 10-2000 vol. ppm carbon monoxide based on the input stream (I). Preferred Process: The oxidation of carbon monoxide in step (d) is effected adiabatically. The pressure drop in reactor (C) in step (d) is less than 20 kPa. The process involves between steps (c) and (d), adding oxygen to the gas stream (IV). The gas stream (IV) is heated with the reaction heat gained in step (b). The heat transfer is performed by a heat exchanger (D) which is connected with streams (II) and (IV). A part of gas stream (IV) is injected to the output stream (II).
   EXAMPLE - The example section provides details of general preparation of glyoxal that do not support the claims.

TI Processing rapid propagation of banana seedlings involves using sugar-free culture device including a carbon dioxide cylinder, a sugar-free culture box, an electric air pump, and a vent tube, and inoculating sterilized banana explants.
AU LUO Y
   HUANG S
   LIU S
   WU B
   ZOU J
   CAO J
   LIU W
   QIN P
AE GUANGZHOU AGRIC SCI RES INST (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202154786S
AB    NOVELTY - Processing rapid propagation of banana seedlings involves using sugar-free culture device including a carbon dioxide cylinder, a sugar-free culture box, an electric air pump, and a vent tube, inoculating sterilized banana explants into an induction medium including Murashige and Skoog (MS) medium, 5 mg/L 6-benzylaminopurine (6-BA), 5mg/L inositol, 30 g/L sucrose and 6 g/L carrageenan, where temperature of incubation room is 27/|1 degrees C, daily light is 10 hours, light intensity is 500-1000 lx for the first 7 days, light intensity is 1000-15 00 lx from eighth day, and air humidity is 60-70%, inoculating the induced adventitious buds into a proliferation medium in a cluster of 2-3 plants, then placing the proliferation medium in a sugar-free culture device for culture at 27/|1 degrees C, where light starts on the second day after inoculation.
   USE - Method for processing rapid propagation of banana seedlings.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures that pollution rate is greatly reduced, operation is simple, production efficiency is high, transplanting seedling process is simplified, and transplanting survival rate is high.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing rapid propagation of banana seedlings involves using sugar-free culture device including a carbon dioxide cylinder, a sugar-free culture box, an electric air pump, and a vent tube, inoculating sterilized banana explants into an induction medium including Murashige and Skoog (MS) medium, 5 mg/L 6-benzylaminopurine (6-BA), 5mg/L inositol, 30 g/L sucrose and 6 g/L carrageenan, where temperature of incubation room is 27/|1 degrees C, daily light is 10 hours, light intensity is 500-1000 lx for the first 7 days, light intensity is 1000-15 00 lx from eighth day, and air humidity is 60-70%, inoculating the induced adventitious buds into a proliferation medium in a cluster of 2-3 plants, then placing the proliferation medium in a sugar-free culture device for culture at 27/|1 degrees C, where light starts on the second day after inoculation, and the light is 12 hours per day, light intensity is 1500-2000 lx, and carbon dioxide concentration is 800 ppm, and time of introducing carbon dioxide is synchronized with the light, unfolding the green leaves, cutting the tissue culture seedlings with a height of 3-4 cm into individual plants, connecting them to the rooting medium, and placing them in a sugar-free culture device for culture, and connecting other seedlings to the proliferation medium to continue the proliferation culture, where temperature of incubation room is 27/|1 degrees C, and the light will be started on the second day after inoculation, daily light is 12 hours, light intensity is 3000-3500 lx, carbon dioxide concentration is 1200 ppm, and time of carbon dioxide introduction is synchronized with the light, and transplanting the tissue-cultured seedlings after rooting into a black seedling bag with a diameter of 90 mm. The transplanting substrate contains peat soil and vermiculite in 3:1 ratio. The components of proliferation medium includes MS, 3mg/L 6-BA, 0.1 mg/L naphthaleneacetic acid, 6g /L carrageenan and 5g/L sucrose. The ratio of rooting medium river sand, vermiculite and coconut bran is 2:3:1, and the nutrient solution formula is 1/2 MS and 0.1 mg/L naphthaleneacetic acid.

PN EP3628390-A1
TI Biogas treatment assembly for extracting flow of biomethane, has heating unit which increases temperature of water conveyed into column to temperature value so as to reduce solubility of carbon dioxide and promote generation of gas phase.
AU ANTONINI M
   SOLARO S
   SALDIVIA A
   FARINA C
   MARCHISIO L
   IELPO G
AE HYSYTECH SRL (HYSY-Non-standard)
GA 2020247175
AB    NOVELTY - The assembly (1) has an absorption column (4) to which biogas is conveyed at the inlet together with a flow of water in which portion of the carbon dioxide contained in biogas is dissolved. The absorption column comprises a first outlet (4b) for exiting the treated biogas depleted of carbon dioxide. A stripping column (5) separates the carbon dioxide dissolved in water inside absorption column. The stripping column comprises a first outlet for exiting a gas phase rich in carbon dioxide. A heating unit increases the temperature of water conveyed into stripping column to a temperature value which is higher than that of the water inside absorption column so as to reduce the solubility of the carbon dioxide and promote the generation of gas phase. A second treatment unit (20) purifies the treated biogas exiting from absorption column of first unit (10) and second unit comprises separation membrane (6A).
   USE - Biogas treatment assembly used for extracting flow of biomethane and flow of OFFGAS.
   ADVANTAGE - The treatment assembly can obtain flow of biomethane and flow of OFFGAS with a high concentration of carbon dioxide, by relatively simple and uncomplicated processes. The treatment assembly can work on relatively small volumes of biogas with low construction and plant costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The absorption column is connected to stripping column by first hydraulic line (102) to transfer water from absorption column to stripping column. The first unit comprises pressure reducing unit (70) arranged along first hydraulic line to reduce the pressure of the water exiting from absorption column to an operating value provided for stripping column. The absorption column is connected to stripping column by second hydraulic line (103) to transfer the water exiting from the stripping column into the absorption column. The first unit comprises cooling unit for cooling the water running along second hydraulic line before entering into absorption column. An exchanger-recovery unit (62) transfers portion of the thermal energy from the water exiting from stripping column to the water exiting from absorption column. The heating unit comprise an exchanger-reboiler (63) to increase the temperature of water used in stripping column by heat exchange with hot water circulating in a hydraulic line (105) outside second column. The water for the recovery of thermal waste of a co-generation system or the cooling water of a compressor or the cooling water of an endothermic engine circulates in external hydraulic line. The first unit comprises a branch line (104) of the water collected inside stripping column. The exchanger-reboiler is operatively arranged along the branch line to heat the water circulating in branch line to the detriment of the temperature of the water circulating in external hydraulic line. A discharge line (108) collects the gas phase which is generated in stripping column. The discharge line develops from stripping column. The first unit comprises an exchanger-condenser (64) installed along the discharge line and is configured to cool gas phase exiting from stripping column. The second treatment unit comprises a first membrane of several membranes to treat the biogas exiting from absorption column and a second membrane to treat the gases separated through first membrane. The second unit comprises a compressor to increase the pressure of the gases addressed to second membrane.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of biogas treatment assembly.
   Biogas treatment assembly (1)
   Absorption column (4)
   Outlet (4b)
   Stripping column (5)
   Separation membrane (6A)
   Treatment unit (10,20)
   Exchanger-recovery unit (62)
   Exchanger-reboiler (63)
   Exchanger-condenser (64)
   Pressure reducing unit (70)
   Hydraulic line (102,103,105)
   Branch line (104)
   Discharge line (108)

PN US2018000104-A1; WO2018009486-A1; US10285413-B2
TI Preparing lactose-free milk, comprises e.g. pasteurizing skim milk, pre-cooling skim milk, pre-filtering skim milk with filter, cooling pre-filtered, pasteurized skim milk, and separating solid and liquid phases of pasteurized skim milk.
AU CLARK G H
   CLARK M A
AE CLARK G H (CLAR-Individual)
   CLARK M A (CLAR-Individual)
   CLARK G H (CLAR-Individual)
   CLARK M A (CLAR-Individual)
GA 201801014B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing (M1) lactose-free milk, comprises e.g.: providing skim; pasteurizing skim milk; pre-cooling skim milk at 5-9 degrees C; pre-filtering skim milk with a filter having pore sizes of 30-40 mu to remove any suspended solids to form pre-filtered, pasteurized skim milk; cooling pre-filtered, pasteurized skim milk to 4 degrees C; and separating solid and liquid phases of pre-filtered, pasteurized skim milk with ultra-centrifugation to produce solid phase comprising casein micelles, fat globules and globular proteins and a liquid phase comprising water, dissolved sugars, minerals and lipoprotein particles.
   USE - The methods are useful for preparing lactose-free milk, and preparing phosphorus-reduced skim milk (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides either low calorie, lactose-free skim milk, or skim milk with low phosphorus content.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing (M1) lactose-free milk, comprises: providing skim; pasteurizing the skim milk; pre-cooling the skim milk at 5-9 degrees C; pre-filtering the skim milk with a filter having pore sizes of 30-40 mu to remove any suspended solids to form pre-filtered, pasteurized skim milk; cooling the pre-filtered, pasteurized skim milk to 4 degrees C; separating solid and liquid phases of the pre-filtered, pasteurized skim milk with ultra-centrifugation to produce a solid phase comprising casein micelles, fat globules and globular proteins and a liquid phase comprising water, dissolved sugars, minerals and lipoprotein particles; collecting and storing the solid phase in a stainless-steel tank; pumping the liquid phase into a feed section of an ultra-filtration unit; filtering the liquid phase by passing it through a filter secured in the ultra-filtration unit, where the filter has many pores having pore sizes of 0.5-40 mu , where a portion of lactose and a portion of minerals present in the liquid phase are filtered out to form a filtered liquid phase; cooling the filtered liquid phase to 5-7 degrees C; bubbling carbon dioxide into the filtered liquid phase to reduce the pH of 4.5-5 to cause any lactose present to form into lactose crystals and to form a carbon dioxide infused filtered liquid phase; returning the carbon dioxide infused filtered liquid phase to the pre-filter; passing the carbon dioxide infused filtered liquid phase through the pre-filter to remove any lactose crystals present to form a lactose-free, carbon dioxide infused filtered liquid phase; and recombining the solid phase with the lactose-free, carbon dioxide infused filtered liquid phase in the stainless-steel tank to form lactose-free skim milk. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing (M2) phosphorus-reduced skim milk, comprising providing skim milk, heating or cooling the skim milk at 65 degrees F, bubbling carbon dioxide gas into the skim milk until a pH of 4.5-4.6 is reached, coagulating a casein component in the skim milk to form coagulated skim milk, filtering the coagulated skim milk by passing it through a filter having pores, where each pore has a size of 40 mu to separate the coagulated casein as retentate and liquid serum as permeate, retaining the coagulated casein as coagulated retentate, adding calcium chloride to the permeate, adjusting the pH of the permeate to 6.5-7 with a base so that sodium hydroxide to form a white crystalline precipitate of calcium phosphate in the permeate, filtering the permeate in ultra-filtration unit to separate the calcium phosphate from the permeate to form a phosphorus-reduced serum, and mixing the coagulated retentate with the phosphorus-reduced serum to form phosphorus-reduced skim milk.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The method (M1) further comprises: providing raw milk comprising skim milk and cream, separating the skim milk and the cream by lowering the temperature of the raw milk at 2-7 degrees C, and removing the cream; pooling the cream in a jacketed stainless-steel tank, and chilling the separated cream at 7 degrees C; storing the pasteurized skim milk at 4 degrees C before the pre-cooling step; agitating the pre-filtered, pasteurized skim milk while cooling it down before the ultra-centrifugation step; agitating the solid phase when collecting and storing the solid phase in a jacketed stainless-steel tank to maintain solids in suspension; passing the filtered liquid phase into a concentrate section of the ultra-filtration unit to remove and direct concentrates to a path selected from the group consisting of a downstream path for further processing, a drain path for elimination and/or a recycling path; permitting the carbon dioxide infused liquid phase to stand for 6-10 hours before recycling the carbon dioxide infused liquid phase through the pre-filter; and spray drying the lactose-free skim milk to product lactose-free skim milk powder. The method (M2) further comprises: providing raw milk comprising skim milk and cream, separating the skim milk and the cream by lowering the temperature of the raw milk at 2-7 degrees C, and removing the cream; and adding calcium to the phosphorus-reduced skim milk in the form of calcium gluconate or calcium citrate, where the calcium is added to achieve a final calcium concentration of 4.8-5.3 g/gallon of skim milk. In the method (M2), the calcium chloride is added in a concentration of 3-4 moles of calcium for every 2 moles of phosphorus in the skim milk.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Reforming hydrocarbon example methane, involves            reacting hydrocarbon with water in presence of catalyst            to form hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide,            cooling obtained outlet stream, and separating into            liquid phase and vapor phase.
AU JAMAL A
   HARALE A
   YOUNES M
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
GA 2021B50489
AB    NOVELTY - Reforming hydrocarbon involves reacting                hydrocarbon with water in presence of a catalyst to                form hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide                within a reactor, selectively passing hydrogen                through a hydrogen separation membrane (104)                positioned within the reactor to a permeate side                (102a) of the reactor while preventing water and                carbon-containing compounds from flowing from a                retentate side (102b) of the reactor to the                permeate side of the reactor through the separation                membrane, flowing an outlet stream from the                retentate side of the reactor to a side (A) of a                heat exchanger (106), cooling the outlet stream in                the side (A) of the heat exchanger to form a cooled                stream (107), and separating the cooled stream into                a liquid phase (109) and a vapor phase (115) by a                separator (108). The liquid phase comprises a                portion of unreacted water from the reactor.
   USE - Reforming hydrocarbon e.g. methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical, and uses hydrogen                separation membrane which selectively allows                hydrogen to pass through, while preventing water                and carbon-containing compounds from passing                through the membrane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Reforming hydrocarbon involves reacting                hydrocarbon with water in presence of a catalyst to                form hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide                within a reactor, selectively passing hydrogen                through a hydrogen separation membrane (104)                positioned within the reactor to a permeate side                (102a) of the reactor while preventing water and                carbon-containing compounds from flowing from a                retentate side (102b) of the reactor to the                permeate side of the reactor through the separation                membrane, flowing an outlet stream from the                retentate side of the reactor to a side (A) of a                heat exchanger (106), cooling the outlet stream in                the side (A) of the heat exchanger to form a cooled                stream (107), and separating the cooled stream into                a liquid phase (109) and a vapor phase (115) by a                separator (108). The liquid phase comprises a                portion of unreacted water from the reactor. The                vapor phase comprises a portion of hydrogen, carbon                monoxide and carbon dioxide formed within the                reactor, and a portion of unreacted hydrocarbon                from the reactor. The liquid phase is flowed to a                side (B) of the heat exchanger from the separator,                and heated to form steam (111). The vapor phase is                cooled to form condensed water (121) and an offgas                stream (117). The offgas stream (117) is cooled to                form condensed carbon dioxide (119) and an offgas                stream (B). The formed steam and the offgas stream                (B) is recycled to the reactor.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                system (100a) for reforming hydrocarbon.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram                of the system.
   System (100a)
   Permeate side (102a)
   Retentate side (102b)
   Hydrogen separation membrane (104)
   Heat exchanger (106)
   Cooled stream (107)
   Separator (108)
   Liquid phase (109)
   Steam (111)
   Vapor phase (115)
   Offgas stream (117)
   Condensed carbon dioxide (119)
   Condensed water (121)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The condensed carbon                  dioxide is purified to form a carbon dioxide                  product stream.

AU CUI J
   YANG Z
   CHEN W
AE UNIV SHANGQIU NORMAL (USQN-C)
   HENAN JINDOUZI VEGETABLE FOODS CO LTD (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 2021620182
AB    NOVELTY - Cultivating pollution-free bean sprouts                involves washing raw materials with water at room                temperature to remove dead beans and impurities in                the raw materials, using 0.01-0.05 g/mL natural                plant fungicides to soak and sterilize the raw                materials, sterilizing for 2-12 hours at 15-25                degrees C, putting the sterilized raw materials                into mobile cultivation vehicle in the cultivation                room, and preparing for cultivation, setting the                water temperature to 20-25 degrees C, controlling                the temperature of cultivation room to 20-29                degrees C, adjusting the humidity of cultivation                room, controlling the content of oxygen, carbon                dioxide, and ethylene in the air. During the                incubation period from day 1 to day 2, humidity of                the air in incubation room is 80-85%, the volume of                oxygen in air is 19-21%, and volume of carbon                dioxide is 0.5-2.0%, and content of ethylene is                0-30 PPM.
   USE - Method for preparing precise control ethylene                mixed gas breeding non-polluted bean sprout                product.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is mature. The breeding system is                stable in working condition, and it is suitable for                large-scale production. It can cultivate good                taste, good appearance, stable quality and high                quality green non-polluted bean sprout                product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cultivating pollution-free bean sprouts                involves washing raw materials with water at room                temperature to remove dead beans and impurities in                the raw materials, using 0.01-0.05 g/mL natural                plant fungicides to soak and sterilize the raw                materials, sterilizing for 2-12 hours at 15-25                degrees C, putting the sterilized raw materials                into mobile cultivation vehicle in the cultivation                room, and preparing for cultivation, setting the                water temperature to 20-25 degrees C, controlling                the temperature of cultivation room to 20-29                degrees C, adjusting the humidity of cultivation                room, controlling the content of oxygen, carbon                dioxide, and ethylene in the air. During the                incubation period from day 1 to day 2, humidity of                the air in incubation room is 80-85%, the volume of                oxygen in air is 19-21%, and volume of carbon                dioxide is 0.5-2.0%, and content of ethylene is                0-30 PPM. In the incubation period of 3rd to 4th                day, the humidity of air in the incubation room is                90-95%, the volume of oxygen in the air is 17-18%,                and the volume of carbon dioxide is 2.0-5.0%, and                ethylene content is 30-50 PPM. During the                incubation period from 5-8 days, the humidity of                air in the incubation room in incubation room is                95-100%, volume of oxygen in the air is 15-16%,                volume of carbon dioxide is 5.0-8.0%, and ethylene                content is 50-100 PPM. After 5-8 days of                incubation, the product is shaped, washed, and the                incubation is over.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: Preparing precise                  control ethylene mixed gas breeding non-polluted                  bean sprout product involves using the raw                  material as soybean or mung bean. The natural                  plant fungicide is one or a combination of two or                  more of the ethanol extract of neem, tea                  polyphenols, propolis, and sesame flower extract.                  Preferred Processes: The preparation method of                  neem Seed ethanol extract or sesame flower                  extract involves using alcohol extraction method,                  weighing dry neem Seed coarse powder or dry                  sesame pollen raw materials, and adding 5-10                  times amount of the raw materials with 95%                  ethanol with a volume concentration, reflux                  extraction at 70-80 degrees C for 2-3 times,                  1-1.5 hours each time, filtering, combining the                  filtrate, recovering ethanol under reduced                  pressure, and concentrating into a solution with                  a mass concentration of 15-20% at 60 degrees                  C.

TI Reducing increase in temperature on surface of earth and increase in content of carbon dioxide in atmosphere due to combustion of fossil fuels and non-fossil, comprises making pure oxygen or make hydrogen peroxide and reducing.
AU BOUKARI M
AE PRODOSE (PROD-Non-standard)
   BOUKARI M (BOUK-Individual)

GA 202057074E
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing (P1) the increase in temperature on the surface of the earth and the increase in the content of carbon dioxide in the atmosphere due to the combustion of fossil fuels and non-fossil, where the process comprises reducing the increase in the temperature of the globe and the increase in the content of carbon dioxide in the atmosphere, which reductions in the temperature of the terrestrial globe and the carbon dioxide content is achieved by reducing the drop in the oxygen content in the atmosphere, which reduction in the drop in oxygen content, comprises making pure oxygen or make hydrogen peroxide, and use for the combustion of fossil and non-fossil fuels the oxygen or hydrogen peroxide to reduce the consumption of oxygen contained in the air during the combustion operations.
   USE - The process is useful for reducing the increase in temperature on the surface of the earth and the increase in the content of carbon dioxide in the atmosphere due to the combustion of fossil fuels and non-fossil.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) reducing (P2) the increase in temperature on the surface of the terrestrial globe due to the release of hot gases into the terrestrial atmosphere, comprising cooling the combustion gases to temperatures so that the wavelength of the infrared thermal radiation emitted at the cooling temperature does not correspond either to the wavelength of absorption of water vapor or to the absorption wavelength of carbon dioxide to prevent them from heating atmospheric air;
   (2) device for implementing the process (P2), comprising cooling the hot combustion gases by means of at least one condensing heat exchanger of the type hot gases and air with the hot gas circuit and the air circuit separated and operating against the current or of hot gas and liquid type with the hot gas circuit and the liquid circuit separated and operating against the current or of hot gas-liquid type and air whose hot gas, liquid and air circuits are separated from each other with the air circuit and the liquid circuit operating on the one hand in co-current between them and on the other hand against -current with the hot gas circuit;
   (3) vehicle for implementing the process (P1) or (P2) and equipped with the device, comprising a fossil fuel and air containing the oxygen manufactured for the combustion of a fossil fuel, or a non-fossil fuel and pure oxygen for combustion of non-fossil fuel; and
   (4) maintenance station of the vehicle, comprising many tanks associated with pipes capable of simultaneously connecting to the vehicle, with a tank of fossil fuel or not to supply the vehicle tank, and a tank for emptying the gas storage tank fitted to the vehicle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The pure oxygen produced is diffused into the atmosphere (A) at the place of its production and is recovered at another place for the purpose of combustion of the oxygen with fossil and non-fossil fuels by extraction in the air. The process (P1) comprises: using oxygen or peroxide produced as a complement or as a supplement and as a partial replacement or as a complement and total replacement of the oxidant for the combustion of fossil and non-fossil fuels; manufacturing pure oxygen or hydrogen peroxide before its use in combustion so that any consumption of oxygen is compensated beforehand by the manufacture of oxygen or hydrogen peroxide; injecting the oxygen produced in a combustion chamber so as to maintain an oxygen level in the combustion gases leaving the combustion chamber equal to that of the oxygen contained in the surrounding atmospheric air in which the combustion device is located or operates; maintaining an oxygen level in the combustion gases at the outlet of the combustion equal to 21%; and maintaining an oxygen level in the combustion gases leaving the combustion chamber of 4-21%, preferably 2-21% or 15-21% or 17-21%. The oxygen extraction is carried out by cryogenic cooling of atmospheric air (A) or by filtration of atmospheric air (A) on supports or materials in zeolite. The pure oxygen is subjected, before combustion, to one of operations having humidification with water and mixing with dry water vapor. The hydrogen peroxide is subjected, before combustion, to one of operations having dissolution, dissolved and mixed with water vapor. The pure oxygen or hydrogen peroxide are produced from electric energy from sustainable means of production, green energies such as solar panels, wind turbines, the sun, hydraulic dams. The pure oxygen is produced from a culture of phytoplankton. The oxygen is produced from hydrogen peroxide. The pure oxygen is produced by catalytic decomposition of hydrogen peroxide to produce oxygen and water, where the water is separable from the oxygen before introduction of oxygen into a combustion chamber. The combustion gases are cooled so that the temperature of the cooled gases is below the dew point temperature of the hot gases so as to condense and eliminate the water vapor contained in these hot gases and their latent heat to prevent them from heating the atmospheric air. The part of the combustion gases cooled and freed of steam is recycled and then mixed with pure oxygen with a view to the introduction of this mixture into the combustion chamber of fossil and non-fossil fuels. The combustion gases cooled and freed from water vapor are filtered through filters containing chemical substances comprising e.g. lithium superoxide, sodium perhydroxide, sodium hydroxide, hydrogen peroxide, potassium peroxide, lithium hydroxide and potassium hydroxide, then part of the cooled and filtered gases is recycled, then mixed with pure oxygen with a view to introducing this mixture into the combustion chamber of fossil and non-fossil fuels. The part of the cooled combustion gases and freed of water vapor is not recycled and is then filtered through filters containing chemical substances comprising e.g. lithium superoxide, sodium perhydroxide, sodium hydroxide, hydrogen peroxide, potassium peroxide, lithium hydroxide and potassium hydroxide before being released to the atmosphere. The combustion gases cooled and freed of water vapor is recycled and then mixed with pure oxygen with a view to introducing this mixture into the combustion chamber of fossil and non-fossil fuels and with a view to maintaining an oxygen level in the combustion gases at the outlet of the combustion of 4-21%, and preferably 2-21%, and preferably 15-21%, and preferably 17-21%. The process (P2) includes: recovering the heat and water vapor contained in the combustion fumes of fossil and non-fossil fuels from rolling vehicles and/or flying vehicles and/or maritime vehicles and store this heat on these vehicles, which recovery of the latent and/or sensitive type of the combustion gases is done by cooling the combustion gases below their dew point, which cooling the combustion gases is done by heat exchange of combustion fumes with an on-board coolant stored on these vehicles through one or more hot combustion smoke/liquid heat exchangers, which heat exchanger is condensing; store in a storage container on the vehicle the coolant reheated after heat exchange with the combustion fumes and after condensation of the water contained in these fumes; and recover the hot coolant stored on the vehicle for the purpose of using the coolant itself or for the purpose of extracting the heat contained in the hot coolant. The heat recovery from the hot coolant stored on the vehicle is effected by heat exchange between this hot coolant and a gas or cold liquid through a heat exchanger located or not inside the hot liquid storage container, which heat exchange takes place by circulating the cold gas or the cold liquid whose temperature is lower than the temperature of the hot coolant and which circulation gas or cold liquid causes the hot coolant to cool. The demineralized water can be injected into the combustion fumes before the entry of these combustion fumes into the condensing heat exchanger. The water resulting from the condensation of the vapor is recovered then treated in order to neutralize its acidity. The pure oxygen can be produced from hypogen peroxide. The circuit for recovering heat and water vapor from combustion fumes comprises two circuits of a primary circuit operating in closed circuit and containing a first coolant which recovers the heat from combustion fumes through a condensing heat exchanger, and a second open or semi-open or closed circuit containing a second coolant which recovers the heat contained in the first coolant contained in the primary circuit through another heat exchanger. The first coolant and the second coolant are identical. The process (P2) applies to land vehicles (cars, trucks, trains), boats, flying vehicles, thermal power plants, nuclear power plants or any installation which rejects heat and/or steam. The cooled gases can be stored in situ in a storage tank. The heat stored in the hot coolant can be recovered once the land, air or sea vehicle has arrived at its parking or stopping point. The process (P2) comprises: (1) reducing the reduction in the rate of oxygen in the atmosphere by production and/or use, for the combustion of fossil fuels and not fossils of pure oxygen produced from renewable energies such as wind turbines, solar, solar panels and hydroelectricity; (2) recovering the heat and water vapor contained in the combustion fumes of fossil fuels and not fossil and store this heat, which recovery of the latent and/or sensitive type of the combustion gases is done by cooling the combustion gases below their dew point, which cooling the combustion gases is by fusion hot melt chemical substances of an organic nature or of a mineral nature or of an organic and mineral nature contained in one or more hermetic, leak tight and isothermal containers e and the melting point of which is located below the dew point of the combustion gases, which fusion takes place by heat exchange between the hot gases from the combustion of fossil and non-fossil fuels and the hot-melt chemicals through a heat exchanger mounted inside the container containing the hot melt chemicals; (3) and recovering the heat stored in the melted hot melt chemicals by heat exchange between these melted chemicals and a cold gas or liquid through a heat exchanger located inside the container for storing molten and non-molten chemicals, which heat exchange takes place by recovering the latent heat of fusion of the hot-melt chemicals by circulating the cold gas or the cold liquid whose temperature is below the melting temperature of the chemicals inside the heat exchanger e t which circulation of gas or cold liquid causes recrystallization or solidification of hot melt chemicals. The step (3) of the process takes place after step (2) and is not carried out simultaneously. The process (P2) comprises steps (2) and (3) only. The part of the cooled combustion gases is recycled and then mixed with pure oxygen with a view to the introduction of this mixture into the combustion chamber of fossil fuels and not fossils. The water resulting from the condensation of steam is recovered and then treated in order to neutralize its acidity. The process (P2) can be applied to land vehicles (cars, trucks, trains), boats, flying vehicles, thermal power plants, nuclear power plants or any facility that rejects heat and/or steam. The cooled gases are stored in situ in a storage tank. The heat stored in the hot melt chemicals is recovered once the land vehicle, or air or sea vehicle has arrived at its parking point or stopping point. The substances making it possible to avoid the super cooling of the hot-melt chemicals are intimately mixed with these hot-melt substances. The substances for avoiding the supercooling of hot melt chemicals are not hot melt. The hot-melt chemicals have a melting temperature of 20-55 degrees C or 55-70 degrees C or 70-85 degrees C or 85-100 degrees C. The hot-melt chemicals have a latent heat of fusion of 1-10 (preferably 10-50, 50-100, 100-150, 150-200, 200-250 or 250-300) Kwh/m3. Preferred Components: The coolant is water. The vehicle includes an on-board device for producing demineralized water from raw water which is itself loaded on the vehicle. The coolant contains chemicals to prevent freezing of the liquid when it is cold. The liquid to be heated is water. The gas to be heated is air. The storage container is an airtight, sealed and isothermal container is a rotary heat accumulator. The storage container is hermetic, sealed and isothermal container is a static heat accumulator. The container additionally contains chemical substances which are thermofusible with other substances which make it possible to avoid the overcooling of the chemical substances. The hermetic container is sealed and isothermal and is a rotary heat accumulator. The hermetic container is sealed and isothermal and is a static heat accumulator. The vehicle comprises a heat exchanger hot gas-air or hot gas-liquid or hot gases-liquid-air with condensation provided a condensate collector to collect condensate regardless of the inclination of the vehicle uphill or downhill. The opposite the direction of advance of the vehicle, the inlet of the hot gases on the volume of the exchanger is located downstream and the outlet of the cooled gases on the volume of the exchanger is located upstream. The vehicle provided with a tank making it possible to store the gases of combustion cooled and freed of water vapor and as they are produced and as the vehicle circulates. The tank for storing cooled gases composed mainly of carbon dioxide is provided with at least one inlet for the entry of gas cooled, which inlets are equipped with non-return valves and an outlet provided with an opening or closing means for withdrawing the stored gas which opening or closing means is manual, pneumatic or electric. The storage tank for cooled gases mainly composed of carbon dioxide contains one or more chemical reagents in solid and/or liquid form of dissolution and or chemical neutralization of the dioxide of carbon. The hot gas-air or hot gas-liquid or hot gas-liquid-air condensing heat exchanger is installed under the vehicle in a plane parallel to the floor of the vehicle. The condensing heat exchanger hot gas-air or hot gas-liquid or hot gas-liquid-air is installed under the vehicle in a plane parallel to the vehicle floor and is provided with cooling fins, the plane of which is parallel to the direction of movement of the vehicle. The exchanger comprises gas inlet and outlet pipes and is equipped with at least one partition separating the exchanger from pipes. The station further comprises a tank of manufactured oxygen or of hydrogen peroxide produced to supply the tank of manufactured oxygen or of hydrogen peroxide of the vehicle.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN111116298-A
TI Preparing olefins from synthesis gas by separating comprises separating product gas stream in a carbon dioxide stream and decarbonized stream, and separating decarburization stream and methane stream at top of tower.
AU HU S
   YANG W
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC SHANGHAI RES INST PETROCHEMICAL (SNPC-C)
GA 202041402P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing olefins from synthesis gas by separating comprises (i) separating the product gas stream in a carbon dioxide stream and a decarbonized stream containing mainly carbon dioxide components through the decarburization zone, (ii) separating the decarburization stream into the C2 and below component streams mainly containing carbon monoxide, hydrogen, ethylene and methane, (iii) separating C2 and below component streams in a circulating stream mainly containing carbon monoxide and hydrogen and a crude methane stream through the dehydrogenation zone, and (iv) sending the crude methane stream into the lower part of the ethylene recovery tower, and the upper part into the absorbent stream to contact in the tower, and separating the methane stream at the top of the tower to obtain the tower kettle rich absorbent stream rich in ethylene and sending the rich absorbent stream and the C2 and above component streams into the product separation zone as feed.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing olefins from synthesis gas by separating.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has advantages of high equipment efficiency, high product recovery rate, high product purity and low energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing olefins from synthesis gas by separating comprises (i) separating the product gas stream into a carbon dioxide stream and a decarbonized stream containing mainly carbon dioxide components through the decarburization zone, (ii) separating the decarburization stream into the C2 and below component streams mainly containing carbon monoxide, hydrogen, ethylene and methane, and the C2 and above component streams comprises carbon monoxide, hydrogen, ethylene and methane, (iii) separating C2 and below component streams in a circulating stream mainly containing carbon monoxide and hydrogen and a crude methane stream through the dehydrogenation zone, and (iv) sending the crude methane stream into the lower part of the ethylene recovery tower, and the upper part into the absorbent stream to contact in the tower, and separating the methane stream at the top of the tower to obtain the tower kettle rich absorbent stream rich in ethylene and sending the rich absorbent stream and the C2 and above component streams into the product separation zone as feed. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for separation device for preparing olefins from synthesis gas, comprising (a) decarbonization zone configured to receive product gas stream, emit carbon dioxide stream and decarbonize stream, (b) coarse separation tower configured to receive the decarbonization stream, discharge C2 and below component streams at the top of the tower, and discharge C2 and above component streams at the tower, (c) dehydrogenation zone configured to receive the C2 and below component streams, and discharge the recycle stream and crude methane stream, and (d) ethylene recovery tower configured to receive the crude methane stream in the lower part, the absorbent stream in the upper part, the methane stream in the top of the tower, and the rich absorbent stream in the tower.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the product gas stream comes at least partly from the synthesis gas production olefin reactor, or at least part of at least part of the recycle stream obtained is returned to the synthesis gas production olefin reactor. The separation of olefin products and C4 and above component streams in the product separation zone. The absorbent stream and gasoline product stream separated from the C4 and above component streams in the absorbent tower. The absorbent stream is sent to the upper part of the ethylene recovery tower. The separation of carbon dioxide in the decarburization zone is by absorption analysis. The generation of bicarbonate ions is in the decarburization zone. At least one of pressure swing adsorption separation method, temperature swing adsorption separation method and membrane separation method is used in the dehydrogenation zone to separate carbon monoxide or/and hydrogen gas. The temperature of the decarburized stream is greater than or equal to minus 15 degrees C, preferably greater than or equal to minus 17 degrees C. The decarburization stream pressure is more than 2.4 MPaG, preferably more than 2.5 MPaG.

PT P
PN WO2019158516-A1; CN111727170-A; EP3752460-A1; US2021047741-A1
TI Use of catalyst material containing iron single            atom on nitrogen doped carbon matrix prepared from a            iron(II)-doped zinc-zeolitic imidazole framework for            electroreduction of carbon dioxide to carbon            monoxide.
AU HU X
   GU J
AE GAZNAT SA (GAZN-Non-standard)
   GAZNAT SA (GAZN-Non-standard)
   GAZNAT SA (GAZN-Non-standard)
GA 2019724846
AB    NOVELTY - Use of a catalyst material containing iron                single atom on nitrogen doped carbon matrix                prepared from a iron(II)-doped zinc-zeolitic                imidazole framework (ZIF) for the electroreduction                of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide, is                claimed.
   USE - The catalyst material containing iron single                atom on nitrogen doped carbon matrix is useful in                electroreduction system for electrochemical                reduction of carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst material: exhibit a high                catalytic activity allowing increasing carbon                dioxide electroreduction rates, while maintaining a                high selectivity to carbon monoxide formation;                exhibit a production cost that allows a large scale                use; is stable over time of use, thus limiting the                amounts of catalytic material to use per reduction                process and avoiding the need of costly catalyst                regeneration, which would reduce the overall                production costs of the carbon dioxide                electroreduction products; is made of abundant                metals that allows a large scale use; provides a                cost-effective method for preparing an efficient                catalyst material in aqueous electrolyte; and is                selective and cost effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing (M1) the catalyst material                comprising (a) providing a iron-doped zinc-ZIF in                dried form under inert atmosphere, (b) subjecting                the iron(II)-doped zinc-ZIF to a pyrolysis under                inert atmosphere during 2-24 hours, preferably 3                hours and (c) collecting the obtained pyrolysis                product as a catalyst material;
   (2) preparing (M2) a iron(II)-doped zinc-ZIF                material comprising (i) providing an imidazole or                imidazole derivative precursor, (ii) dissolving the                imidazole or imidazole derivative precursor in                degassed polar solvent, in a reductive atmosphere                to prevent the oxidation of ferrous cations to                obtain a solution A, (iii) dissolving an iron (II)                precursor and a zinc salt in degassed polar solvent                to obtain a solution B, (iv) adding solution B into                solution A, (v) collecting and washing the                precipitate, (vi) leaving the washed precipitate                drying at room temperature to obtain a                iron(II)-doped zinc-ZIF in the form of a dry                powder, where steps (ii)-(vi) are conducted under                stirring in an inert atmosphere;
   (3) a catalyst material containing iron single                atom on nitrogen doped carbon matrix obtainable                from the method;
   (4) an electroreduction system for the                electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide                comprising the catalyst material prepared from a                iron(II)-doped zinc-ZIF;
   (5) a kit (K1) for preparing the catalyst                material comprising at least one container                comprising a iron(II)-doped zinc-ZIF in dry form                and under inert atmosphere; and
   (6) a kit (K2) for preparing a iron(II)-doped                zinc-ZIF, comprising in separate compartments or                containers (a) an imidazole or imidazole derivate                precursor, (b) an iron(II) precursor including                ferrous chloride or iron(II) acetate, (c) a zinc                salt including zinc nitrate or zinc chloride, and                (d) optionally a reductive agent to prevent                oxidation of the ferrous ions including an iodide                derivative such as potassium, sodium or ammonium                iodide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The pyrolysis under step                  (b) is carried out at a temperature of 800-950                  degrees C, where the pyrolysis under step (b) is                  carried out through the ramping of temperature at                  a rate of 3-10 degrees C/minute. The solution B                  is added into solution A dropwise under stirring                  for 1 hour under step (iv). The reacting mixture                  from step (iv) is kept under stirring under inert                  atmosphere for one day after the completion of                  the addition of solution B. The imidazole                  precursor is 2-methyl imidazole, where the                  iron(II) precursor is ferric chloride. The inert                  atmosphere is provided by a nitrogen flow. The                  precipitate is collected under step (v) by                  ambient-pressure filtration or centrifugation                  under inert atmosphere. The provided                  iron(II)-doped zinc-ZIF is obtained from the                  process. The catalyst material is obtained from                  the pyrolysis of a iron(II)doped-zinc-ZIF-8.                  Preferred Components: The catalyst material is a                  material. The iron(II)-doped zinc-ZIF is has a                  crystal structure of zinc- ZIF-8, where some                  zinc(II) ions are substituted by iron(II) ions.                  The iron(II)-doped zinc-ZIF has a crystal                  structure of zinc-ZIF-8, where some zinc(II) ions                  are substituted by iron(II) ions.
   EXAMPLE - Iron(II) doped zinc imidazolate precursor was                immersed in hexane during the transfer from glove                box to the tube furnace to prevent possible                oxidation caused by exposing to air. The pyrolysis                was then carried out in the tube furnace with                nitrogen for 3 hours to give the catalytic                material.

TI Asymmetric membrane used as e.g. gas permeation            membrane, comprises crosslinked polyimide resin            obtained by crosslinking polyimide resin comprising            tetracarboxylic dianhydride-derived unit and            diamine-derived unit, with crosslinking agent.
