GA 201966289J
AB    NOVELTY - An asymmetric membrane comprises a crosslinked                polyimide resin obtained by crosslinking a                polyimide resin with a crosslinking agent. The                polyimide resin comprises a structural unit (A)                derived from tetracarboxylic dianhydride and a                structural unit (B) derived from diamine. The unit                (A) includes a structural unit (A-1) derived from                dispiro(4-oxatricyclo(5.2.1.02,6)decane-8,1'-cyclopentane-3',8''-(4)oxatricyclo(5.2.1.02,6)decane)-2',3,3'',5,5''-pentone                (I). The structural unit (B) includes 0.1-50 mol%                structural unit (B-1) derived from a diamine                compound (II). The crosslinking agent is a compound                containing two or more functional groups which bind                to a carboxy group.
   USE - Asymmetric membrane in the form of hollow                fiber and film is used as gas permeation membrane                (all claimed) used in oxygen enrichment of air,                nitrogen enrichment, recovery or purification of                helium from spent helium mixed with welding gas,                concentration of helium in natural gas, recovery of                hydrogen from ammonia purge gas, recovery of                hydrogen from purge gas of hydrodesulfurization                plant, separation or recovery of carbon dioxide gas                from hydrocarbon during recovery of crude oil,                separation or removal of carbon dioxide from                natural gas, and separation or recovery of methane                from biogas generated by anaerobic microbial                decomposition. Can also be used for desalination of                water by reverse osmosis, deep desulfurization of                gasoline and diesel fuel, ethanol/water separation,                pervaporation dehydration of aqueous/organic                mixture, separation of olefin/ paraffin, iso/normal                paraffin, carbon dioxide/methane, carbon                dioxide/nitrogen, hydrogen/methane,                oxygen/nitrogen, hydrogen sulfide/methane, and                other light gas mixtures, catalytic reaction and                fuel cell application.
   ADVANTAGE - The asymmetric membrane has excellent                workability, carbon dioxide permeability, high                carbon dioxide selectivity recovery rate and long                product life. The polyimide resin has excellent                solvent solubility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An asymmetric membrane comprises a crosslinked                polyimide resin obtained by crosslinking a                polyimide resin with a crosslinking agent. The                polyimide resin comprises a structural unit (A)                derived from tetracarboxylic dianhydride and a                structural unit (B) derived from diamine. The unit                (A) includes a structural unit (A-1) derived from                dispiro(4-oxatricyclo(5.2.1.02,6)decane-8,1'-cyclopentane-3',8''-(4)oxatricyclo(5.2.1.02,6)decane)-2',3,3'',5,5''-pentone                of formula (I). The structural unit (B) includes                0.1-50 mol% structural unit (B-1) derived from a                diamine compound of formula (II). The crosslinking                agent is a compound containing two or more                functional groups which bind to a carboxy                group.
   m1+m2+m3+m4 = 1 or more;
   m3,m4 = 0-5;
   Q1,Q2 = groups containing aryl, aliphatic                hydrocarbyl and/or alicyclic hydrocarbyl;
   X = single bond, optionally substituted                alkylene, carbonyl, ether or group of formula (1)                or (2);
   p = 0-2;and
   m1,m2 = 0-4.
   When p is 0, m1 is 1-4.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compound: The compound (II) is                  preferably of formula (IV). The crosslinking                  agent is a compound having 2 or more oxazolyl                  groups in a molecule, or a compound having 2 or                  more hydroxyl groups in a molecule.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The unit (B) includes                  10-99.9 mol% unit (B-2) chosen from unit (B-2-1)                  derived from a                  4-(9-(4-aminophenyl)-9H-fluoren-9-yl)aniline                  compound of formula (V), unit (B-2-2) derived                  from                  1-(4-aminophenyl)-1,3,3-trimethyl-2,3-dihydro-1H-inden-5-ylamine                  of formula (VI) and unit (B-2-3) derived from                  2-(2-amino-5-(4-amino-5-(1,1,1,3,3,3-hexafluoro-2-hydroxypropan-2-yl)-2-methylphenyl)-4-methylphenyl)-1,1,1,3,3,3-hexafluoropropan-2-ol                  of formula (VII).R = H, F or methyl.

PN CN107899545-A
TI Spherical core-shell nano composite-amine nano-structured carbon dioxide adsorbent.
AU YUE H
   DING M
   TANG S
   LIU C
   LIANG B
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 201830167E
AB    NOVELTY - Spherical core-shell nano composite (CSNS)-amine nano-structured carbon dioxide adsorbent (I), is claimed. The organic amines are grafted onto the surface of the spherical structure of the copper silicate nano needle array coated with an outer surface of silica nano-colloidal sphere as template.
   USE - Used as spherical core-shell nano composite -amine nano-structured carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent: has excellent thermal stability, lower adsorption and desorption temperatures, suction and desorption cycle stability, and adsorption capacity; and captures carbon dioxide more.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Spherical core-shell nano composite (CSNS)-amine nano-structured carbon dioxide adsorbent is of formula xAmine/silica-ycopper(II) metasilicate (I), is claimed. The organic amines are grafted onto the surface of the spherical structure of the copper silicate nano needle array coated with an outer surface of silica nano-colloidal sphere as template.
   x = organic amine; and
   y = thermal history of copper silicate nano spheres.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the spherical core-shell nano composite-amine nano-structured carbon dioxide adsorbent, comprising (i) taking 5 ml ethyl orthosilicate, dissolving in a mixture of 20 ml deionized water and 260 ml anhydrous ethanol, adding 14 ml ammonia into the mixture and mixing uniformly, mechanically stirring and reacting at room temperature for 3 hours, centrifuging the milky liquid at 7000 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 2 minutes, washing the resulting white gum, and washing with ethanol and drying at 60 degrees C, (ii) taking 0.52 g colloidal ball A, and ultrasonically treating in a 160 ml deionized water, dissolving 2.8 mmol copper(II) nitrate trihydrate and 24 ml ammonium hydroxide solution, dissolving in a 240 ml deionized water to obtain blue copper ammonia solution, ultrasonically dispersing the colloidal sphere A emulsion and the cuprammonium solution, rapidly pouring into a mechanically stirred round bottom flask, stirring for 10-20 minutes, transferring to high pressure hydrothermal reactor, reacting at 140 degrees C for 1.5 hours, 3 hours and 10 hours, and centrifuging to obtain blue product, baking baked blue product in a muffle furnace at 450 degrees C for 4 hours, controlling heating at rate 1-2 degrees C/minutes, and naming according to the different heat and humid time CSNS1.5, CSNS3, and CSNS10, (iii) adding 0.3 g y core-shell nano composite material into a 20 ml anhydrous toluene, placing in the three-necked flask containing the mixture into a oil bath and fixing, connecting the reflux condenser to the flask, placing the magnetic stirrer rotor into a reactor, flushing the flask with nitrogen for ventilating the air, uniform stirring and heating at 95 degrees C, stabilizing at 95 degrees C, taking 3 ml 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane (APTES), N-( beta -aminoethyl)- gamma -aminopropyltrimethoxysilane (AEAPS), adding diethylenetriamine trimethoxysilane into a three sets of flasks, respectively, maintaining stirring at constant speed, allowing the above mentioned reaction proceeding at 95 degrees C for 10 hours, filtering the synthesized material and washing for three times with 50 ml anhydrous ethanol, drying in a vacuum oven at 70 degrees C for 12 hours to obtain adsorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The formation of copper(II) metasilicate-silica core-shell composite material and copper(II) metasilicate nano hollow spheres. The type of organic amine and copper silicate nano-sphere shape are modulated to obtain different adsorbent materials. The size of the coated silica colloidal sphere regulated by changing the amount of silicon source, and the copper silicate nanospheres can be regulated by changing the hydrothermal conditions. The content of grafted organic amine N can be regulated by changing the type of amine.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN217276600-U; CN115014599-A
TI Echo wall mold type micro-bubble probe resonator            for use in pressure sensing system, has micro-nano            optical fiber that is coupled on deformation wall area            of echo wall mould type micro bubble cavity.
AU WANG Y
   LIAO C
   ZHANG Q
   LIU B
   LIU S
AE UNIV SHENZHEN (UYSZ-C)
GA 2022B1351P
AB    (CN217276600-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a echo wall mould                type micro-bubble probe resonator, comprising a                micro-nano optical fibre and a echo wall mould type                micro-bubble cavity, the echo wall mould type                micro-bubble cavity comprises a deformation wall                area and a stress wall area, the deformation wall                area is stressed and deformable, surrounding on the                equatorial plane circumference of the echo wall                mould type micro-bubble cavity; the stress wall                area is provided with a probe structure, the axial                direction of the probe structure is vertical to the                equatorial plane of the echo wall mould type                micro-bubble cavity; the micro-nano optical fibre                coupling on the deformation wall area of the echo                wall mould type micro-bubble cavity. The echo wall                mould type micro-bubble probe resonator can realize                point contact with the target to be measured, so as                to improve the precision of the single-point                pressure measurement for the target to be measured.                The utility model further claims a pressure sensing                system, comprising the echo wall mould type                micro-bubble probe resonator.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                pressure sensing system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of an echo                wall mode micro-bubble probe resonator.
   2Quartz capillary pipe
   21Capillary cone part
   22Quartz capillary pipe
   23Echo wall mode micro-bubble cavity
   24Contact probe
EA    NOVELTY - The method involves adjusting a light spot                position of a carbon dioxide laser. The light spot                of the carbon dioxide light is fixed on a                predetermined point of a thin pipe structure. The                predetermined points are fixed on a side of an echo                wall mold type micro-bubble cavity. Gas in a quartz                capillary tube is released. The carbon dioxide                light is re-turned on. A preset point of the thin                tube structure is heated and softened. A waveguide                is coupled with equatorial plane circumference of                an echo wall mode microbubble chamber.
   USE - Method for preparing an echo wall mode                micro-bubble probe resonator in a pressure sensing                system (all claimed) by using a carbon dioxide                laser used in micro-system, material detection, and                scientific research.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables realizing pressure and                displacement generated by pressing of the contact                probe and single point pressure measurement, and                defining action direction of pressure as axial                direction of a contact probe so as to avoid                different of equatorial plane radius variation                caused by different stress direction and stress                position. The method allows a cavity wall of the                echo wall mould type micro-bubble cavity to be                provided with a special sensitive area and a                non-sensitive area, so that the sensitive area is                stressed and deformable.

PT P
PN EP3730457-A1
TI Producing ammonia involves introducing feed comprises carbon containing material selected from natural gas, methane, propane, butane, and liquid petroleum gas to synthesis gas generation section such as steam reforming.
AU WARD A M
   STEVENSON S
   OPRINS A J M
   ZHAO Z
   ABBOTT T
   LAWSON K F
   SCHROER J W
   HUCKMAN M E
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 2020A6170R
AB    NOVELTY - Producing ammonia involves introducing feed comprises carbon containing material selected from natural gas, methane, propane, butane, liquid petroleum gas, naphtha, coal, petroleum coke, or combinations to synthesis gas generation sectionsuch as steam reforming or partial oxidation to produce synthesis gas product comprises hydrogen andcarbon monoxide. The energy required for synthesis gas generation section is supplied by heat input Q1. The reformer product is cooled to produce cooled synthesis gas by effecting heat removal Q2. The cooled synthesis gas is shifted to produce shifted synthesis gas product. The shifted synthesis gas is cooled by effecting heat removal Q3 to produce cooled, shifted synthesis gas. The cooled, shifted synthesis gas is purified by removing carbon dioxide from the cooled, shifted synthesis gas, heating by heat input Q4 to produce heated carbon-dioxide reduced gas. The heated carbon-dioxide reduced gas is methanated to produce methanator product.
   USE - Method for producing ammonia.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce ammonia that increases overall energy efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing ammonia involves introducing feed comprises carbon containing material selected from natural gas, methane, propane, butane, liquid petroleum gas, naphtha, coal, petroleum coke, or combinations to synthesis gas generation sectionsuch as steam reforming or partial oxidation to produce synthesis gas product comprises hydrogen andcarbon monoxide. The energy required for synthesis gas generation section is supplied by heat input Q1. The reformer product is cooled to produce cooled synthesis gas by effecting heat removal Q2. The cooled synthesis gas is shifted to produce shifted synthesis gas product. The shifted synthesis gas is cooled by effecting heat removal Q3 to produce cooled, shifted synthesis gas. The cooled, shifted synthesis gas is purified by removing carbon dioxide from the cooled, shifted synthesis gas, heating by heat input Q4 to produce heated carbon-dioxide reduced gas. The heated carbon-dioxide reduced gas is methanated to produce methanator product. The water is cooled and condensed from methanator product by heat removal Q5 to provide purified gas. The purified gas is compressed. The compressed, purified gas is heated by heat input Q6 to provide heated gas. The ammonia synthesis feed is provided and comprises heated gas. The ammonia synthesis feed comprises hydrogen and nitrogen. The nitrogen is present in the synthesis gas or subsequently added. The product is produced and comprises ammonia from the ammonia synthesis feed. The product is cooled and comprises ammonia by heat removal Q7 to remove ammonia from the product comprises ammonia. The recycle gas stream is provided and comprises nitrogen and hydrogen. The gas stream is compressed and comprises nitrogen and hydrogen via recycle compressor and purged via purge gas system. The cooling is effected by a heat removal Q8. The net energy needed for mechanical work is provided by non-carbon based energy source, renewable energy source, and/or electricity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The electricity is produced from renewable energy source. The renewable energy source comprises wind, solar, geothermal, hydroelectric,nuclear, tide, wave, or combination. The energy required for the synthesis gas generation section is supplied without combusting fuel or carbon-based fuel. Preferred Process: The method involves producing non-carbon based energy source via intermittent energy source (IES), maintaining the temperature of one or more reactors in the synthesis gas generation section without combusting fuel or carbon-based fuel when IES is available, and maintaining the temperature of one or more reactors in the synthesis gas generation section via stored supply of energy from the IES or by combusting fuel or carbon-based fuel when IES is not available. The method involves utilizing thermoelectric device to convert some of the heat removedat Q2, Q3, Q5, Q7, and/or Q8 to electricity, utilizing electric heating to control temperature profile of any or all of number of reactors of the synthesis gas generation section, shifting reactors utilized for shifting, methanationreactors utilized for the methanating, and ammonia synthesis reactors utilized for producing the product comprises ammonia from ammonia synthesis feed. The one of number of reactors of the synthesis gas generation section is operated to approximate isothermal operation.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN111393422-A
TI Producing puerarin from Pueraria lobata comprises e.g. rinsing kudzu root with clean water, adding Pueraria lobata slices into vacuum packaging bag, soaking performing sublimation dehydration, drying filtering and concentrating.
AU CHEN B
   LIU Q
   MA D
   ZHOU J
AE UNIV SICHUAN SCI & ENGINEERING (USLG-C)
GA 202066705E
AB    NOVELTY - Producing puerarin from Pueraria lobata comprises e.g. (i) rinsing the kudzu root with clean water, slicing to obtain kudzu root slices, adding Pueraria lobata slices into vacuum packaging bag, adding disinfectant cleaning agent into the vacuum packaging bag, soaking at normal pressure, performing vacuum package in vacuum packaging bag, sealing the pressurized cavity and pressurizing, (ii) freezing the Pueraria lobata slices, performing sublimation dehydration and drying to obtain freeze-dried kudzu root slices and pulverizing, (iii) adding the irradiated Pueraria lobata powder into the mixed solution of ethanol and water, adding the first impurity remover and stirring, filtering, and concentrating the filtrate, (iv) adding the filtrate into supercritical carbon dioxide reactor, adding second impurity remover to inject carbon dioxide into the supercritical carbon dioxide reactor, stirring, relieving, filtering, and concentrating.
   USE - The method is useful for producing puerarin from Pueraria lobata.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has high product purity; and is simple and easy to implement and is suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing puerarin from Pueraria lobata comprises (i) rinsing the kudzu root with clean water, slicing to obtain kudzu root slices, adding Pueraria lobata slices into vacuum packaging bag, adding disinfectant cleaning agent into the vacuum packaging bag, soaking at normal pressure for 30-45 minutes, performing vacuum package in vacuum packaging bag, controlling the vacuum degree at 0.1 MPa, adding the vacuum packaging bag into high static pressure processing equipment, sealing the pressurized cavity and pressurizing for high static pressure treatment, (ii) freezing the Pueraria lobata slices treated with high static pressure at -30 to -15 degrees C, freezing for 10-15 hours, adding frozen Pueraria lobata slices into a vacuum pressure of 0.02-0.05 MPa, performing sublimation dehydration and drying in a vacuum container at a temperature of -60 to -40 degrees C for 24-36 hours to obtain freeze-dried kudzu root slices, adding the freeze-dried kudzu root slices into a pulverizer and pulverizing to obtain kudzu root powder, adding the kudzu root powder into a sterile polyethylene packaging bag, sealing and encapsulating and irradiating the packaged kudzu root powder, (iii) adding the irradiated Pueraria lobata powder into the mixed solution of ethanol and water, adding the first impurity remover and stirring under reflux for 45-90 minutes, filtering, and concentrating the filtrate under reduced pressure to a solvent content of 45-50%, (iv) adding the filtrate into the supercritical carbon dioxide reactor, adding the second impurity remover to inject carbon dioxide into the supercritical carbon dioxide reactor, stirring at 35-45 degrees C under a pressure of 12-18 MPa for 60-90 minutes, relieving the pressure, filtering, and concentrating the filtrate under reduced pressure until the solvent is completely recovered and vacuum drying 40 degrees C to constant weight to obtain puerarin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the disinfectant cleaning agent comprises 15-25 pts. wt. alkyl glycosides, 3-5 pts. wt. sodium succinate, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. sodium chloride, 1.5-2 pts. wt. tea polyphenols, 1-3 pts. wt. sodium citrate, 1-3 pts. wt. sorbitol, 1-2 pts. wt. sodium carbonate, 0.5-0.8 pts. wt. soybean lecithin and 100-120 pts. wt. water. The Pueraria lobata tablets in the disinfectant cleaning agent comprises: 15-20 ml sterilizing cleaning agent to every 10 g Pueraria lobata tablets. In step (iv), the second impurity removing agent includes attapulgite, diatomite, and kaolin; and In step (iii), the mass ratio between second impurity removing agent of the filtrate is 1:0.1-0.2. Preferred Method: The parameters of the high static pressure treatment are: increased pressure of 400-500 MPa at a pressure increase rate of 3-5 MPa/second and maintaining pressure treatment at room temperature for 10-20 minutes. The step (ii) comprises adding freeze-dried kudzu root slices into pulverizer to initially crush to mesh number of 100, passing the preliminarily crushed kudzu root powder through a 100-mesh sunscreen to remove large particles of kudzu root powder, adding the filtered large-particle kudzu root powder into the pulverizer and repeatedly pulverizing to mesh number of 100, mixing to a mesh number of 100 kudzu root powder obtained twice, adding into the superfine powder grinder, crushing to a mesh number of 1200, maintaining the temperature during the crushing process of the ultrafine powder pulverizer not greater than 35 degrees C and the relative humidity of not greater than 10%. In step (ii), the irradiation dose rate of the irradiation treatment is 5-10 Gy/minute, the total radiation dose is 5-15 KGy; and the irradiation device used in the irradiation treatment is 60 Co radiation source. In step (iii), the volume ratio between ethanol and water is 5-7:3; the mass ratio between kudzu root powder and the mixed liquid is 1:40-80; the mass ratio between kudzu root powder and the first impurity removing agent is 1:0.1-0.3; the heating and reflux stirring temperature is 80-90 degrees C, and the stirring speed is 150-300 revolutions/minute. The first impurity removing agent is prepared by: adding isoprene glycol and tetraisopropyl titanate into the catechin solution, heating and stirring for 0.5-2 hours, naturally cooling the resulting mixture to room temperature and freeze-drying to obtain solid obtained and making into fine powder by ultrafine pulverizer to obtain final product, where the mass ratio between catechin, isoprene glycol, and tetraisopropyl titanate is 10:3-5:0.5-1.5; and the mass fraction of the catechin solution is 5-10 wt.%. The second impurity remover is attapulgite calcined at high temperature, and preparation process comprises adding attapulgite into a high-temperature calcination furnace for high-temperature calcination, and controlling the calcination temperature at 400-550 degrees C for 2-3 hours.
DC B02 (Fused ring heterocyclics.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PT P
PN CN109264713-A; CN109264713-B
TI Preparing biomass tar based high specific surface area porous carbon as carbon dioxide solid adsorbent for carbon dioxide physical adsorption comprises e.g. placing egg shell in muffle furnace, grinding to obtain porous calcium oxide.
AU YUAN H
   LI D
   CHEN J
AE GUANGZHOU ENERGY INST CONVERSION CHINESE (CAEC-C)
GA 2019114022
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing biomass tar based high specific surface area porous carbon, comprises e.g. placing egg shell in the temperature range of 900-1000 degrees C of muffle furnace for 60 minutes, then grinding into powder to obtain the porous calcium oxide, stirring the biomass tar, and porous calcium oxide for 8 hours, transferring to a quartz boat, placing into 80-85 degrees C oven and drying for 10-12 hours, adding potassium hydroxide in water, placing into a tubular furnace, activating for 1-2 hours under the inert atmosphere, slowly cooling to the room temperature, then taking out, adding hydrochloric acid in de-ionized water, heating, stirring for 4 hours, washing porous carbon material with de-ionized water until the pH is stabilized at 7, placing in the freeze drying box, drying at constant temperature for 36 hours, grinding into powder, obtaining porous carbon material obtained as carbon dioxide solid adsorbent.
   USE - The porous carbon as carbon dioxide solid adsorbent is useful for carbon dioxide physical adsorption (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The porous carbon has high specific surface area, high pore volume, high micropore proportion, as solid adsorbent has high physical absorption, and stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing biomass tar based high specific surface area porous carbon, using biomass tar as carbon source, porous calcium oxide formed by calcination of egg shells as a template, and potassium hydroxide as activator comprises (i) placing egg shell in the temperature range of 900-1000 degrees C of muffle furnace for 60 minutes, then grinding into powder to obtain the porous calcium oxide, (ii) stirring the biomass tar, and porous calcium oxide for 8 hours, transferring to a quartz boat, placing into 80-85 degrees C oven and drying for 10-12 hours, adding potassium hydroxide in water according to mass ratio of 1:1-3:1-4, rotating at a speed of 600-700 revolutions/minute, placing into a tubular furnace, activating for 1-2 hours at 800-850 degrees C under the inert atmosphere, slowly cooling to the room temperature, then taking out, adding hydrochloric acid in de-ionized water according to the volume ratio of 1:3-37, heating to 100-105 degrees C, stirring for 4 hours, washing porous carbon material with de-ionized water until the pH is stabilized at 7, placing in the freeze drying box, drying at constant temperature for 36 hours, grinding into powder, obtaining porous carbon material obtained as carbon dioxide solid adsorbent, where the obtained porous carbon material has a specific surface area of 1684-2424 m2/g, micropore specific surface area is 474-1162 m2/g, pore volume is 1.21-1.86 cm3/g, pore volume is 0.215-0.513 cm3/g, and the average aperture is 2.27-3.40 nm.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC J01-E02B; J01-E03C
IP B01J-020/20; B01J-020/28; B01J-020/30; C01B-032/33; C01B-032/348

PT P
PN US2018036670-A1; WO2018026516-A1; US10350537-B2
TI Producing hydrogen, nitrogen, and carbon dioxide            involves passing exhaust stream into carbon dioxide            swing adsorption reactor, passing resulting stream into            nitrogen swing adsorption reactor, purging, and            recovering hydrogen stream.
AU SUNDARAM N
   THOMANN H
AE EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
GA 2018136157
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrogen (H2), nitrogen (N2), and carbon                dioxide (CO2) from exhaust stream are produced by                passing exhaust stream containing 40-80 vol.% N2,                5-30 vol.% CO2, and 1-20 vol.% H2 into CO2 swing                adsorption reactor; recovering N2 and H2 stream                from forward end of reactor; reducing pressure in                reactor; purging reactor with steam purge; passing                N2 and H2 stream into N2 swing adsorption reactor                to adsorb N2 on N2 adsorbent material; recovering                H2 stream; reducing pressure in reactor; and                purging reactor with a portion of H2 stream to                generate N2 recovery stream.
   USE - Production of hydrogen (H2), nitrogen (N2),                and carbon dioxide (CO2) from exhaust stream.
   ADVANTAGE - A portion of the exhaust gas can be                effectively separated to generate a high purity                carbon dioxide stream, while reducing and/or                minimizing the energy required for the separation,                and without needing to reduce the temperature of                the flue gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Hydrogen (H2), nitrogen (N2), and carbon                dioxide (CO2) from exhaust stream are produced by                passing exhaust stream containing 40-80 vol.% N2,                5-30 vol.% CO2, and 1-20 vol.% H2 into CO2 swing                adsorption reactor comprising CO2 adsorbent                material selective for CO2 over N2 and H2, where                exhaust stream has pressure of 10-30 bara;                adsorbing CO2 on CO2 adsorbent material; recovering                N2 and H2 stream from forward end of CO2 swing                adsorption reactor, where recovered N2 and H2                stream has pressure that differs from pressure of                exhaust stream by less than or equal to 0.5 bara;                reducing pressure in CO2 swing adsorption reactor                to pressure of 1-4 bara by outputting                blow-downstream from greater than or equal to 1 end                of CO2 swing adsorption reactor; purging CO2 swing                adsorption reactor with steam purge at a pressure                of 1-4 bara to generate CO2 recovery stream, where                CO2 recovery stream comprises greater than or equal                to 90% of the CO2 in the exhaust stream, where                steam purge contains less than or equal to 1 moles                water per mole of CO2 in the reactor exhaust                stream; passing N2 and H2 stream into N2 swing                adsorption reactor comprising N2 adsorbent material                selective for N2 over CO2 and H2, adsorbing N2 on                N2 adsorbent material; recovering H2 stream having                a purity of greater than or equal to 95% from                forward end of N2 swing adsorption reactor, where                recovered H2 stream has a pressure that differs                from pressure of N2 and H2 stream by less than or                equal to 0.5 bara; reducing pressure in N2 swing                adsorption reactor to pressure of 1-4 bara by                outputting blow-downstream from greater than or                equal to 1 end of N2 swing adsorption reactor; and                purging N2 swing adsorption reactor with a portion                of H2 stream at a pressure of 1-4 bara to generate                N2 recovery stream which comprises 90% of the N2                present in the exhaust stream.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of an                alternative configuration for a pressure swing                adsorption process for producing nitrogen and                hydrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: Exhaust stream is                  passed to CO2 swing adsorption reactor before N2                  swing adsorption reactor. Exhaust stream is                  passed to N2 swing adsorption reactor before CO2                  swing adsorption reactor. The method further                  includes repressurizing CO2 swing adsorption                  reactor to pressure of greater than or equal to                  10 bara with the exhaust stream. Steam purge is                  fed to 2nd CO2 swing adsorption reactor. H2 purge                  is fed to 2nd N2 swing adsorption reactor.                  Preferred Compositions: Exhaust stream comprises                  power plant flue stream and/or refinery flue                  stream. Exhaust stream is generated by combustion                  of fuel in reducing atmosphere. Steam purge                  contains less than or equal to 0.8 moles water                  per mole of CO2 in the exhaust stream. CO2                  adsorbent material contains alkali metal                  carbonate and oxide of alkaline earth metal or                  transition metal. N2 adsorbent material is a                  zeolite comprising (i) CTS-1 zeolite, (ii) ETS-4                  zeolite, (iii) ITQ-55, (iv) 4A zeolite, or (v) 3A                  zeolite. Preferred Conditions: Exhaust gas has                  exhaust temperature of 400-500 degrees C. CO2                  swing adsorption reactor and N2 swing adsorption                  reactor, respectively, have CO2 and N2 adsorption                  temperature that differs from exhaust temperature                  by 20 degrees C. Preferred Device: CO2 swing                  adsorption reactor and N2 swing adsorption                  reactor are arranged concentrically within a                  single vessel.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); Q51 (Internal combustion engines, reciprocating engines, rotary engines (F01, F02B,D,F,G,M,N,P))

PN WO2021097425-A1; US2021146299-A1; US11446605-B2
TI Method for producing water and dry-clean air,            involves recovering removed water and treating portion            of recovered water for use as drinking water and using            or selling remainder of recovered water for            non-drinking application.
AU BESARATI S M
   GIARDELLO M A
   GROSS W
   PEDRETTI A
AE CARBON CAPTURE (CARB-Non-standard)
   CARBON CAPTURE (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 202155722J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves removing water from                ambient air to produce a dry air stream. CO2 is                removed from the dry air stream to produce                dry-clean air. The removed water is recovered. A                portion of the recovered water is treated for use                as drinking water. The remainder of the recovered                water is used and sold for non-drinking                applications. A portion of the recovered water is                electrolyzed to produce hydrogen and oxygen. A                portion of the hydrogen produced to chemically                reduce a portion of the recovered CO2 to produce                methanol is used. An electrolysis unit is capable                of converting a portion of the recovered water into                hydrogen and oxygen. A catalytic reaction unit is                capable of converting recovered CO2 and hydrogen                into methanol.
   USE - Method for producing water and dry-clean                air.
   ADVANTAGE - The relative amounts of the drinking and                non-drinking water are balanced that provides                optimal profitability while maintaining overall                carbon negativity. The parallel units are generally                operated in sequence for continuous direct air                capture (DAC) operation since the sorbents used in                the water and carbon dioxide removal units need to                be frequently regenerated to release the captured                water and carbon dioxide. The electric vehicle and                biofuel truck reduces the carbon footprint of                transportation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for producing carbon-advantaged drinking water and                dry-clean air.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of                different potential sources of revenue for the                direct air capture plant.
   Condensation unit (13)
   Storage tank (15)
   Catalytic reaction (300)
   Electrolysis unit (301)
   Water treatment unit (302)

TI Preparing dicyclopentadiene dicarboxylate useful as polymer cross-linking agent to prepare dicyclopentadiene dicarboxylic acid and metallocene composite derivatives, comprises e.g. in-situ reacting and carboxylating alkali metallocene and carbon dioxide, and dimerizing cyclopentadiene carboxylate.
AU LI H
   LIU M
   LI X
   MA D
   CHEN X
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
   ZHENGZHOU AOXIANG MEDICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (ZHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202214931E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing dicyclopentadiene dicarboxylate comprises using single-pot method to complete the four-step reaction, i.e. thermal cracking of dicyclopentadiene produces cyclopentadiene, performing in-situ reaction of cyclopentadiene and alkali metal to generate alkali metal (alkali metal salt of cyclopentadiene), carboxylating alkali metallocene and carbon dioxide to generate cyclopentadiene carboxylate, and dimerizing cyclopentadiene carboxylate to obtain dicyclopentadiene dicarboxylate, specifically comprises (1) adding dicyclopentadiene, inert solvent and alkali metal to reactor equipped, (2) heating up and stirring reaction until no bubbles are generated to obtain cyclopentadiene alkali metal salt/inert solvent suspended in slurry, (3) cooling down, and feeding carbon dioxide into reactor while stirring, and (4) stopping reaction, separating solid matter and liquid matter, collecting liquid matter for recycling, washing solid matter with low-boiling solvent and drying.
   USE - The dicyclopentadiene dicarboxylate is useful as polymer cross-linking agent to prepare dicyclopentadiene dicarboxylic acid and metallocene composite derivatives (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high yield and excellent purity of the target product, simple process, environmental protection, simple technological process, few side reactions, high raw material utilization rate and low solvent loss.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing dicyclopentadiene dicarboxylate comprises using single-pot method to complete the four-step reaction, i.e. thermal cracking of dicyclopentadiene produces cyclopentadiene, performing in-situ reaction of cyclopentadiene and alkali metal to generate alkali metal (alkali metal salt of cyclopentadiene), carboxylating alkali metallocene and carbon dioxide to generate cyclopentadiene carboxylate, and at same time, dimerizing cyclopentadiene carboxylate to obtain dicyclopentadiene dicarboxylate, specifically comprises (1) adding dicyclopentadiene, inert solvent and alkali metal to reactor equipped with reflux condenser in inert atmosphere, (2) heating up and stirring reaction until no bubbles are generated to obtain cyclopentadiene alkali metal salt/inert solvent suspended in slurry, (3) cooling down, and feeding carbon dioxide into reactor while stirring or adding dry ice to carry out carboxylation reaction, and (4) stopping reaction after air pressure in reaction kettle is stable, separating solid matter and liquid matter in obtained reaction product, collecting liquid matter for recycling, washing solid matter with low-boiling solvent and drying to obtain powder of dicyclopentadiene dicarboxylate.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for dicyclopentadiene dicarboxylate prepared by the preparation method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the composition is 100 pts. wt. dicyclopentadiene, 20-300 pts. wt. inert solvent and 90-100 pts. wt. alkali metal. The dicyclopentadiene comprises unsubstituted dicyclopentadiene (DCPD) and dialkyl substituted cyclopentadiene (DRCPD). The dialkyl substituted cyclopentadienes comprises bismethylcyclopentadiene (DMCPD) and diethylcyclopentadiene (DECPD). The inert solvent is high-boiling saturated hydrocarbon solvent, comprising decalin, kerosene and/or diesel. The alkali metal comprises metallic lithium, metallic sodium or metallic potassium. The dicyclopentadiene dicarboxylate lithium salt, dicyclopentadiene dicarboxylate sodium salt or dicyclopentadiene dicarboxylate potassium salt are obtained. In the step (3), the amount of carbon dioxide ventilation or the amount of dry ice added is controlled so that the pressure in the kettle does not exceed the gauge pressure of 0.3 atmospheres. In the step (4), the low-boiling point solvent for washing the solid matter is petroleum ether, alkane with carbon number of 5-7, and low-boiling point ester solvent.Preferred Method: In the step (2), the reaction temperature is 115-200degrees Celsius. In the step (3), the carboxylation reaction temperature is 30-70degrees Celsius. The reaction kettle is maintained at constant temperature and stirred for at least 1 hour and no pressure change occurs after the carbon dioxide or dry ice feeding is stopped, and the carboxylation is deemed to be completed.

TI Co-ground active having product used as e.g. veterinary product, has carrier material being surface-reacted calcium carbonate, in which the carbonate is product of natural ground calcium carbonate with carbon dioxide and hydronium ion donor.
AU HILTY-VANCURA F M
   DE MIGUEL L
AE OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
GA 2021F08331
AB    NOVELTY - Co-ground active(s) comprising product comprises a carrier material being surface-reacted calcium carbonate, where the surface-reacted calcium carbonate is a reaction product of natural ground calcium carbonate with carbon dioxide and at least one hydronium ion donors, the carbon dioxide is formed in-situ by the hydronium ion donors treatment, and at least one at least partially X-ray amorphous active(s) comprising pharmaceutical active(s) and/or its inactive precursor, nutraceutical active(s) and/or its inactive precursor, veterinary active(s) and/or its inactive precursor and/or agricultural active(s) and/or its inactive precursor, where the active(s) is/are rendered at least partially X-ray amorphous during co-grinding.
   USE - The product is useful as pharmaceutical, nutraceutical, veterinary or agricultural product in the form of a liquid dosage form e.g. an emulsion, dispersion, creme, solution, spray or inhalation spray, or solid dosage form e.g. a powder, tablet, mini-tablet, sachet, granule, capsule, suppositories or film (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product: utilizes the surface-reacted calcium carbonate as carrier material for reducing the X-ray crystallinity of solid active(s); and is free of materials differing from surface-reacted calcium carbonate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing the co-ground active(s) comprising product comprising (a) providing a surface-reacted calcium carbonate, (b) providing the solid active, (c) co-grinding the surface-reacted calcium carbonate of step (a) and the solid active(s) of step (b) e.g. to obtain the co-ground active(s) comprising product, where the co-grinding in step (c) is carried out in the absence of solvent(s); and
   (2) use of a surface-reacted calcium carbonate as a carrier material for reducing the X-ray crystallinity of solid active(s) comprising pharmaceutical active(s) and/or its inactive precursor, veterinary active(s) and/or its inactive precursor, nutraceutical active(s) and/or its inactive precursor and/or agricultural active(s) and/or its inactive precursor, where the surface-reacted calcium carbonate is a reaction product of natural ground calcium carbonate with carbon dioxide and at least one hydronium ion donors, where the carbon dioxide is formed in-situ by hydronium ion donors treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The at least partially X-ray amorphous active(s) has/have a crystallinity of less than 50 wt.%, preferably less than 20 wt.%. The natural ground calcium carbonate comprises calcium carbonate containing minerals comprising marble, chalk and/or limestone. The surface-reacted calcium carbonate is a reaction product of natural ground calcium carbonate with carbon dioxide and phosphoric acid, where the carbon dioxide is formed in-situ by the phosphoric acid treatment. The at least partially X-ray amorphous active(s) has/have a melting point of at least 30 degrees C, preferably 35-400 degrees C. The co-ground active(s) product comprises 1-45 wt.%, preferably 3-30 wt.% the at least partially X-ray amorphous active(s). The carrier material of the co-ground active(s) comprising product is free of materials differing from surface-reacted calcium carbonate. The co-ground active(s) comprises product is prepared according to the method. Preferred Method: The surface-reacted calcium carbonate provided in step (a) (a1) has a Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) specific surface area of 1-200 m2/g, preferably 40-110 m2/g, measured using nitrogen and the BET method according to ISO 9277:2010 and/or (b1) comprises particles having a volume median grain diameter d50(vol) of 0.5-50 mu m, preferably 1-10 mu m as measured by laser diffraction and/or (c1) has an intra-particle intruded specific pore volume of 0.15-1.6 cm3/g, preferably 0.3-1.5 cm3/g, preferably 0.3-1.35 cm3/g, calculated from a mercury intrusion porosimetry measurement. The co-grinding in step (c1) is carried out in a mill, preferably a ball mill, e.g. a planetary ball mill, roller mill, table mill, sand mill, ring roller mill, rod mill, vibrating mill, centrifugal impact mill, vertical bead mill and attrition mill. The volume median grain diameter d50 of the surface-reacted calcium carbonate provided in step (a1) is reduced in co-grinding step (c) by more than 20%. The co-grinding in step (c) is carried out at an energy input of at least 100 kJ/kg, preferably 200-1300 kJ/kg.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of comparative chemical entities that do not support the claims.

PN WO2021225643-A1; US2021340015-A1
TI Converting feed gas comprising carbon dioxide and hydrogen to a product gas comprising carbon monoxide and water by heating feed gas in a preheater outside the main reactor vessel and sending heated feed gas to a main reactor vessel.
AU SCHUETZLE R
   SCHUETZLE D
   WRIGHT H
   HANBURY O
   CALDWELL M
   RODRIGUEZ R
AE INFINIUM TECHNOLOGY LLC (INFI-Non-standard)
   INFINIUM TECHNOLOGY LLC (INFI-Non-standard)
GA 2021C58899
AB    NOVELTY - A feed gas comprising carbon dioxide and hydrogen is converted to a product gas comprising carbon monoxide and water by (a) heating the feed gas to an inlet temperature of greater than or equal to 1400 degrees F in a preheater (4) outside the main reactor vessel to produce a heated feed gas; (b) making the preheater uses electricity to generate heat and produce the heated feed gas; and (c) sending heated feed gas to a main reactor vessel; where the main reactor vessel is an adiabatic or nearly adiabatic vessel where heat loss is minimized, main reactor vessel contains catalyst that converts the heated feed gas to the product gas. The product gas leaves the main reactor at an exit temperature where the exit temperature is lower than the inlet temperature.
   USE - Conversion of feed gas comprising carbon dioxide and hydrogen to a product gas comprising carbon monoxide and water. Can be used for producing fuels e.g., diesel fuel, jet fuel, gasoline, and kerosene.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst exhibits high activity at low metal concentrations. The initial temperature rise is done without the use of direct combustion of carbon containing gas to provide the heat.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for conversion of feed gas comprising carbon dioxide and hydrogen to a product gas comprising carbon monoxide and water which involves heating feed gas to an inlet temperature of greater than or equal to 1500 degrees F in a preheater outside the main reactor vessel to produce a heated feed gas, using preheater that uses electricity to generate heat and produce the heated feed gas, and sending heated feed gas to a main reactor vessel; where the main reactor vessel is maintained at or near isothermal conditions by addition of heat produced by electricity to the vessel; main reactor vessel contains catalyst that converts the heated feed gas to the product gas; and the product gas leaves the main reactor at an exit temperature where the exit temperature is 5 degrees C of the inlet temperature.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the reverse water gas shift reactor.
   Hydrogen gas stream (1)
   Carbon dioxide stream (2)
   Stream (3)
   Preheater (4)
   Heated feed gas stream (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Parameters: The exit temperature is greater than or equal to 50 degrees F lower than the inlet temperature. The catalyst has high thermal stability of less than or equal to 1100 degrees C, that does not form carbon. The preheater uses less than or equal to 0.5 MWh, preferably less than or equal to 0.4 MWh electricity per Metric Ton carbon dioxide (CO2) in the feed gas. Preferred Components: The feed gas also comprises methane. Preferred Process: The product gas is heated in a second preheater to be a second preheated gas and the second preheated gas is fed to a second reactor vessel. The product gas is cooled and compressed and then is heated in a second preheater to be a second preheated gas and second preheated gas is fed to the main reactor vessel. The CO2 conversion from the heated gas to the product gas is greater than or equal to 60 mol.%. The CO2 conversion from the heated feed gas to the product gas is greater than or equal to 70 mol.%.

TI Separation of two gaseous streams involves            purifying and sending of gaseous stream containing            carbon monoxide, hydrogen and acid gas in acid gas            removal absorber, and enriching at least portion of            stream in hydrogen from separation unit.
AU HERNANDEZ A
   GUAN W
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   HERNANDEZ A (HERN-Individual)
   GUAN W (GUAN-Individual)
   GEORGES CLAUDE METHODS STUDY & DEV (GEOR-Non-standard)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 2020C0217V
AB    NOVELTY - Separation of two gaseous streams involves (i)                purifying a gaseous stream (A) containing at least                carbon monoxide, hydrogen and at least one acid gas                in a acid gas removal absorber (a1) to remove at                least one acid gas, purifying in a temperature                swing adsorption unit (T1) to remove at least                carbon dioxide and then separating at a cryogenic                temperature in a separation unit (CB) to produce a                stream of fluid enriched in carbon monoxide and a                stream of fluid enriched in hydrogen, (ii) sending                gaseous stream (B) containing at least carbon                monoxide, hydrogen and at least one acid gas to a                shift reaction unit and converting carbon monoxide                and water vapor in the stream (B) to carbon dioxide                and hydrogen.
   USE - Separation of two gaseous streams.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical separation of                two gaseous streams using adsorbents which removes                impurities that would freeze under cryogenic                temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separation of two gaseous streams involves (i)                purifying a gaseous stream (A) containing at least                carbon monoxide, hydrogen and at least one acid gas                in a acid gas removal absorber (a1) to remove at                least one acid gas, purifying in a temperature                swing adsorption unit (T1) to remove at least                carbon dioxide and then separating at a cryogenic                temperature in a separation unit (CB) to produce a                stream of fluid enriched in carbon monoxide and a                stream of fluid enriched in hydrogen, (ii) sending                gaseous stream (B) containing at least carbon                monoxide, hydrogen and at least one acid gas to a                shift reaction unit and converting carbon monoxide                and water vapor in the stream (B) to carbon dioxide                and hydrogen, purifying the shifted stream (s2) in                a acid gas removal absorber (a2) to remove carbon                dioxide and sending the purified stream as a feed                stream to a pressure swing adsorption unit (PSA) to                produce a hydrogen enriched stream (21) richer in                hydrogen that the feed stream, and (iii) enriching                at least portion of the stream in hydrogen from the                separation unit (CB) and sending as a feed stream                to the pressure swing adsorption unit (PSA) to                produce the hydrogen-enriched stream (21). The                stream comprises at least one acid gas, carbon                monoxide and hydrogen. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for an apparatus.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gaseous streams                  (A) and (B) contains at least one of carbon                  dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, nitrogen, methane, and                  carbonyl sulfide. The method is performed by                  using gaseous mixture containing at least carbon                  monoxide, hydrogen and at least one acid                  gas.

PT P
PN CN111890761-A; CN111890761-B
TI Carbon dioxide-propylene oxide copolymer-based            paper-plastic composite film contains polypropylene            carbonate (PPC) composite film layer compounded on            paper layer, where film layer has inner PPC base film            layer and outer PPC base film layer.
AU GAO F
   DONG Y
   ZHAO X
   ZHOU Q
   WANG X
AE CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
GA 2020B2818M
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide-propylene oxide copolymer-based                paper-plastic composite film comprises a paper                layer and a polypropylene carbonate (PPC) composite                film layer compounded on the surface of the paper                layer, where the PPC composite film layer includes                an inner PPC base film layer and an outer PPC base                film layer. The inner PPC base film layer is made                of raw materials including 15-25 pts. wt. PPC,                75-85 pts. wt. poly(butylene                adipate-co-terephthalate) (PBAT) and 0.2-0.5 pt.                wt. chain extender. The outer PPC base film layer                is made of raw materials including 98-99.9 pts. wt.                PPC, and 0.1-2 pts. wt. slip agent.
   USE - As carbon dioxide-propylene oxide                copolymer-based paper-plastic composite film.
   ADVANTAGE - The components of the inner layer raw                materials are matched, so that it can be directly                laminated with paper under appropriate hot pressing                temperature and pressure, without adhesives, and                obtain high bonding force. The combination of the                components of the outer layer raw materials makes                the outer layer difficult to bond with the iron,                and realizes the PPC composite film and paper                directly through the hot pressing roller equipment                hot pressing compound. The outer layer and the                inner layer have high matching of raw materials,                which improves the processability and improves the                mechanical properties of the composite film.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing a carbon dioxide-propylene oxide                copolymer-based paper-plastic composite film,                involving (a) melting and blowing the inner PPC                base film modified material and the outer PPC base                film modified material through a double-layer film                blowing device to obtain a PPC composite film,                where the inner PPC base film modified material is                prepared by after mixing PPC, PBAT and a chain                extender, and performing melt granulation to obtain                the inner PPC base film modified material, and the                outer PPC base film modified material is prepared                by mixing PPC and a slip agent, and performing melt                granulation to obtain the outer PPC base film                modified material, and (b) hot-pressing and                compounding the paper and the PPC composite film by                a hot press roller, and then flattening by a cold                press roller to obtain a carbon dioxide-propylene                oxide copolymer-based paper-plastic composite                film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The slip agent                  includes one or more of erucamide, oleamide, zinc                  stearate, calcium stearate and stearic                  acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The inner PPC base                  film layer is prepared from raw materials                  including 20 pts. wt. PPC, 80 pts. wt. PBAT and                  0.2-0.5 pt. wt. chain extender. In step (a), the                  mass ratio of the inner PPC base film modified                  material and the outer PPC base film modified                  material is 1:1. Preferred Components: The chain                  extender is one or more chosen from methyl                  acrylate-glycidyl methacrylate copolymer,                  styrene-methyl acrylate-glycidyl methacrylate                  copolymer, glycidyl methacrylate homopolymer and                  ethylene-methyl acrylate-glycidyl methacrylate                  copolymer. Preferred Conditions: In step (a), the                  temperature of the granulation is 140-185 degrees                  C, and the temperature of the blown film is                  160-175 degrees C. In step (b), the temperature                  of the hot pressing roller is 120-160 degrees C,                  the pressure of 0.1-0.3 MPa, and the hot pressing                  time is 1-5 seconds. In step (b), the temperature                  of the cold pressing roll is 20-30 degrees C, and                  the pressure is 0.08-0.12 MPa. Preferred                  Properties: The number average molecular weight                  of the PPC is 80-150 kg/mol, the molecular weight                  distribution of 1.8-3, and the catalyst residue                  of 0-50 ppm. The number average molecular weight                  of the PBAT is 40-80 kg/mol, and the molecular                  weight distribution of 1.9-2.5. In step (a), the                  thickness of the PPC composite film is 10-100 mu                  m.
DC A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); F09 (Paper-making production of cellulose, chemical treatment of wood - including chipboard and fibre-board (D21).); P73 (Layered products (B32).)

PT P
PN CN111606400-A
TI Pre-treatment of desulfurized wastewater involves primarily treating desulfurized wastewater using calcium carbide slag, performing air-flotation treatment to obtain divalent-calcium-free wastewater, secondarily treating, and precipitating.
AU HUANG Z
   ZHANG F
   ZHANG J
   LIANG W
   LI F
   LIU M
AE BEIJING METALLURGICAL EQUIP RES & DESIGN (CMEG-C)
GA 202090132H
AB    NOVELTY - Pre-treatment of desulfurized wastewater involves performing primary treatment of desulfurized wastewater in a reactor (R1), adding calcium carbide slag to reactor (R1) for reaction by reacting magnesium sulfate and calcium hydroxide to obtain magnesium hydroxide precipitate and calcium sulfate, precipitating primarily-treated wastewater in a sedimentation tank, performing flotation treatment in an air-flotation chamber, passing a thermal power plant flue gas or high-concentration carbon dioxide into the chamber to obtain divalent-calcium-free wastewater, performing secondary treatment of divalent-calcium-free wastewater in a reactor (R2), adding sodium carbonate to reactor (R2), precipitating secondarily-treated wastewater in a sedimentation tank, and discharging water which meets the standard. Air-flotation reaction is performed by carrying out reversible chemical reaction by reacting aqueous divalent calcium and divalent carbonate to obtain solid calcium carbonate.
   USE - Pre-treatment of desulfurized wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method economically pre-treats desulfurized wastewater using the calcium carbide slag comprising calcium hydroxide instead of lime, which increases adsorption property of the slag and improves sedimentation performance of magnesium hydroxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Pre-treatment of desulfurized wastewater involves performing primary treatment of desulfurized wastewater in a reactor (R1), adding calcium carbide slag to reactor (R1) for reaction by reacting magnesium sulfate and calcium hydroxide to obtain magnesium hydroxide precipitate and calcium sulfate, or reacting magnesium dichloride and calcium hydroxide to obtain magnesium hydroxide precipitate and calcium dichloride, precipitating primarily-treated wastewater in a sedimentation tank, performing flotation treatment in an air-flotation chamber, passing a thermal power plant flue gas or high-concentration carbon dioxide into the chamber to obtain divalent-calcium-free wastewater, performing secondary treatment of divalent-calcium-free wastewater in a reactor (R2), adding sodium carbonate to reactor (R2), precipitating secondarily-treated wastewater in a sedimentation tank, and discharging water which meets the standard. Air-flotation reaction is performed by carrying out reversible chemical reaction by converting aqueous calcium hydroxide to aqueous divalent calcium and 2 molar equivalent of hydroxide, or converting gaseous carbon dioxide to aqueous carbon dioxide, or reacting aqueous carbon dioxide and hydroxide to obtain aqueous bicarbonate, or reacting aqueous bicarbonate and hydroxide to obtain aqueous divalent carbonate and water, or reacting aqueous divalent calcium and divalent carbonate to obtain solid calcium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Pre-treatment of desulfurized wastewater further involves returning a portion of a sludge generated in the primary sedimentation tank to reactor (R1), and controlling the sludge reflux ratio to 1:10-20. Preferred Conditions: The calcium carbide slag and desulfurized wastewater is reacted at 15-40 degrees C for 10-30 minutes while mechanically stirring. Reaction of desulfurized wastewater and flue gas of thermal power plant or high-concentration carbon dioxide in the air-flotation chamber is performed at less than 30 degrees C for 15-30 minutes with a gas-solid ratio of 0.02-0.08. Preferred Composition: The calcium carbide slag is used in an amount of 8-12 g/L. Reactor (R1) is lined with an anticorrosion material.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

PT P
PN CN111218697-A; CN111218697-B
TI Lead electrode with an octahedral crystal face in            electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, comprises            a surface covered with a layer of octahedral lead            particles, and prepared by pre-treating lead substrate,            polishing lead substrate.
AU YU W
   WEN L
   SONG S
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 202051434L
AB    NOVELTY - Lead electrode with an octahedral crystal face                comrpsies a surface covered with a layer of                octahedral lead particles.
   USE - Lead electrode with an octahedral crystal face                used in electrocatalytic reduction of carbon                dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The lead electrode with an octahedral crystal                face has low edge/coordination sites of the                multiple, and unique nanometre particle                structure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for preparing a Pb electrode                having an octahedral crystal plane, which involves                pre-treating lead substrate, polishing lead                substrate to obtain p-lead electrode, preparing                lead electrode with octahedral crystal plane,                pouring the mixed aqueous solution of nitirc acid                solution and sodium fluoride solution into the                reaction vessel, where the reaction vessel is a                three-electrode system with working electrode slot,                counter electrode slot and reference electrode                slot, placed p-lead electrode obtained into the                working electrode tank of the reaction vessel,                placing platinum electrode into the counter                electrode slot of the reaction vessel. The                reference electrode is placed in the reference                electrode tank of the reactor; connect the p-Pb                electrode, platinum electrode and reference                electrode to the electrochemical workstation,                placing reaction vessel into a constant temperature                environment of 20-35 degrees C, setting the                temperature to 25 degrees C, opening the                electrochemical workstation and adjusting to cyclic                voltammetry, taking out the p-lead electrode after                cyclic voltammetry and cleaned to obtain a Pb                electrode with an octahedral crystal plane;                and
   (2) a method for using a Pb electrode with an                octahedral crystal plane, which involves using                electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide in the                electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide                reaction includes H type Reaction cell, circulating                water bath and electrochemical workstation, H-type                reaction cell is equipped with anode cell, cathode                cell and communication pipeline, two ends of the                connecting pipe are respectively connected to the                anode pool and the cathode pool, a cation exchange                membrane is provided in the middle of the                communication pipe, cation exchange membrane                separates the anode cell and the cathode cell,                anode liquid and anode are set in the anode cell,                catholyte, reference electrode and cathode are                provided in the cathode cell, a sampling port is                provided on the top of the cathode cell, cathode                uses the aforementioned Pb electrode with                octahedral crystal plane, starting the                electrochemical workstation to perform                electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The octahedral lead                  particles on the surface of the lead electrode                  having an octahedral crystal surface are passed                  through a nitri solution containing sodium                  fluoride in-situ generation of cyclic                  voltammetry. The cleaning and pre-treating the                  lead substrate is carried out by flattening the                  cut lead sheet as lead base, ultrasound with                  acetone for 3-20 minutes, rinsing with deionized                  water, subjecting to ultrasonicated with                  anhydrous ethanol for 3-20 minutes, rinsing with                  deionized water, and then blow drying with                  high-purity nitrogen to obtain pre-treated lead                  matrix. The grinding and polishing the lead                  substrate is carried out by pretreating lead                  obtained substrate is wet with deionized water,                  used sandpaper to polish the lead substrate and                  rinse with deionized water, rinsed with distilled                  water for 2-5 times, and blow dried with                  high-purity nitrogen to obtain p-Pb electrode,                  where the mesh number of the sandpaper is 2000                  mesh. The molar concentration of the nitirc acid                  solution is 0.1 mole L-1. The molar concentration                  is 0.01 mole L-1. The liquid levels of the three                  electrode tanks in the reaction vessel are flush.                  The experimental parameters corresponding to                  cyclic voltammetry are as follows: the initial                  potential is -2.0V. The high potential is 2.0V,                  the low potential is -2.0V, the termination                  potential is -2.0V, the data point collection                  interval is 0.001s, the cycle number is 20 weeks,                  and the scan rate is 0.1V S-1. The anode uses a                  platinum electrode. The reference electrode uses                  calomel electrode. The anolyte is an aqueous                  solution of sodium hydroxide. The catholyte is an                  aqueous solution of potassium bicarbonate through                  carbond dioxide to saturation. The anode, cathode                  and saturated reference electrode are all                  connected to the electrochemical workstation. The                  distance between anode and cathode is 7cm, and                  the constant voltage into anode and cathode is                  -1.9-1.3V. The electrolyte in the anode pool is                  flush with the electrolyte level in the cathode                  chamber, the electrolyte in the cathode pool is a                  potassium hydrogen carbonate solution through                  carbondioxide to saturation. The electrolyte in                  the anode cell is a sodium hydroxide                  solution.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); M11 (Electroplating; electrolytic treatment of or with metals - including electro-deposition of metals, electro-plating apparatus, electro-forming, electro-erosion, spark erosion, anodising (electrophoretically) coating metals and electrolytic cleaning and polishing (C25).)

PT P
PN CN110122084-A
TI Propagating Morinda officinalis tissue culture seedlings by inducing differentiation culture, transplanting shoot buds to strong seedling medium, inserting into matrix, spraying carbendazim or chlorothalonil and transplanting to matrix.
AU YE Z
   DING L
   LIU K
   LI S
   CHEN Z
   ZHENG Y
AE UNIV NINGDE NORMAL (UNDN-C)
GA 2019728851
AB    NOVELTY - Propagating Morinda officinalis tissue culture seedlings involves (a) inducing differentiation culture and transplanting shoot buds to strong seedling medium comprising Murashige and Skoog (MS) and naphthaleneacetic acid (NAA) to obtain strong seedlings, (b) cutting strong seedlings into sections with 1-3 stems, 2-6 leaves and stem length of 1-3 cm and soaking in clean water, (c) adding loose and breathable matrix to seedling tray and adding nutrient solution without organic ingredients and sugar, (d) inserting the cuttings into matrix of seedling tray to depth of 1-2 cm, spraying carbendazim or chlorothalonil and placing the seedling tray in a closed small environment or incubator equipped with a light, humidity generator, carbon dioxide cylinder and controller, (e) extending illumination time and introducing carbon dioxide synchronized with illumination time, and (f) developing root system, and directly transplanting to matrix.
   USE - The method is useful for propagating M.officinalis tissue culture seedlings.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves survival rate by 95%, consumes less time and is rapid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for propagating Morinda officinalis tissue culture seedlings involves (a) using existing plant tissue culture technique, inducing differentiation culture and transplanting shoot buds to strong seedling medium comprising Murashige and Skoog (MS) and 0.2-1 mg/l naphthaleneacetic acid (NAA) to obtain strong seedlings, (b) cutting strong seedlings into sections with 1-3 stems, 2-6 leaves and stem length of 1-3 cm and soaking in clean water, (c) adding loose and breathable matrix to seedling tray and adding nutrient solution without organic ingredients and sugar, (d) inserting the cuttings into matrix of seedling tray to depth of 1-2 cm, spraying carbendazim or chlorothalonil and placing the seedling tray in a closed small environment or incubator equipped with a light, humidity generator, carbon dioxide cylinder and controller, (e) maintaining humidity to above 85%, culture temperature to 26 plus minus 2 degrees C and carbon dioxide per day to 800-1200 ppm, extending illumination time, and introducing carbon dioxide synchronized with illumination time, and (f) after 30-60 days of rooting and strong seedling cultivation, when 5-10 cm new buds and 4-8 leaves are grown and plant height reaches 6-15 cm, developing root system, and directly transplanting to matrix.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The nutrient solution comprises 0.5-1 MS and 0-0.5 mg/l NAA. The illumination is performed at 2000-5000 Lx for 16-20 hours per day.
DC C03 (Other organic compounds, inorganic compounds and multicomponent mixtures. Polymers and proteins.); C02 (Heterocyclic.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)

PN CN112221479-A
TI Active regeneration treatment of desulfurization wastewater defluorination adsorbent by injecting water into reaction device for defluorination adsorbent, injecting calcium chloride solution, soaking defluorination adsorbent and drying.
AU YANG C
   GUO J
   TAO Z
   YANG Y
   SUN Y
AE HEBEI JIYAN ENERGY SCI & TECHNOLOGY RES (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 202109190T
AB    NOVELTY - Active regeneration treatment method of desulfurization wastewater defluorination adsorbent involves (a) injecting the deionized water into reaction device for defluorination adsorbent, soaking the adsorbent, placing the ultrasonic generator close to the outer wall of the adsorbent device, cleaning the adsorbent twice, (b) connecting carbon dioxide gas to the air inlet at the bottom of the adsorption device, and opening the exhaust valve on the top of the adsorption device to ventilate slowly, contacting the carbon dioxide gas fully and reacting with the defluorination adsorbent, (c) injecting 5-6% calcium chloride solution into the adsorption device, soaking the defluorination adsorbent, and rinsing and replacing the reaction device with deionized water to rinse the calcium chloride solution, and (d) laying the adsorbent treated in step (c) flat on a large area and drying, and placing the dried adsorbent in a large high-temperature heater for calcination for 1 hour.
   USE - As active regeneration treatment method of desulfurization wastewater defluorination adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes the recycling of the defluorination adsorbent through the active regeneration treatment of the defluorination adsorbent, greatly reduces the cost of desulfurization wastewater defluorination, increases the cycle rate of desulfurization wastewater and improves the quality of gypsum, reduces wastewater reduction and even reduces the treatment cost of zero discharge of wastewater in the whole plant, has excellent social benefits, and is environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Active regeneration treatment method of desulfurization wastewater defluorination adsorbent involves (a) injecting the deionized water into reaction device for defluorination adsorbent, soaking the adsorbent, placing the ultrasonic generator close to the outer wall of the adsorbent device, cleaning the adsorbent twice, and draining the cleaning waste liquid in the device, (b) connecting carbon dioxide gas to the air inlet at the bottom of the adsorption device, and opening the exhaust valve on the top of the adsorption device to ventilate slowly, contacting the carbon dioxide gas fully and reacting with the defluorination adsorbent, stopping the carbon dioxide gas source, (c) injecting 5-6% calcium chloride solution into the adsorption device, soaking the defluorination adsorbent, and rinsing and replacing the reaction device with deionized water to rinse the calcium chloride solution, and (d) laying the adsorbent treated in step (c) flat on a large area and drying for 6-8 hours, and placing the dried adsorbent in a large high-temperature heater for calcination for 1 hour.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Conditions: The soaking time in the step (c) is 2 hour. In the step (d), the flattened thickness of the adsorbent is 0.5 cm, the drying temperature is 65 degrees C, and the calcination temperature is controlled at 500 degrees C. In the step (a), the level of deionized water is 10 cm above the adsorbent layer. The ultrasonic generator in the step (a) adopts two 25 cm x 40 cm ultrasonic generators, where the two cleaning frequencies of the ultrasonic generator are respectively 30-50 Hz, and the two cleaning times are both 30 minutes. In the step (b), the working pressure of carbon dioxide is 0.05-0.08 MPa, and the flow rate of carbon dioxide is 5-10 l/minutes.

TI Converting carbon monoxide used for synthesizing coke furnace gas, involves passing raw material gas containing carbon monoxide using reactor, mixing purified feed gas with water vapor, generating carbon dioxide and hydrogen, and separating.
AU WANG J
   CAI J
   ZHANG J
