   ZHU Y
   HUANG X
   XU B
   WU L
AE SINOPEC NANJING CHEM RES INST CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 2021031613
AB    NOVELTY - Method for converting carbon monoxide, involves (a) sequentially passing raw material gas containing carbon monoxide using desulfurization and dechlorination reactor, cooling and separating gas-liquid by water cooling system to obtain purified feed gas with sulfide concentration of less than 0.1 ppm and chloride concentration of less than 0.05 ppm, (b) passing deionized water pump to vaporizer for vaporization, and generating steam after vaporization, (c) uniformly mixing purified feed gas with water vapor, (d) passing obtained mixed gas to transformation reactor from upper end of transformation reactor, passing mixed gas of feed gas and water vapor in conversion reactor using bed layer containing transformation catalyst, and generating carbon dioxide and hydrogen for generating carbon dioxide and hydrogen, and (e) after performing conversion reaction, discharging gas from lower end of transformation reactor, cooling the gas by water-cooling, and performing gas-liquid separation.
   USE - The method is used for synthesizing gas, coke furnace gas, blast furnace gas, self-compounding synthesis gas and laboratory gasification product gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method removes the reaction heat, exhibits excellent protection effect, widens the use condition of catalyst, and ensures more than 90% conversion rate of carbon monoxide and 1-30% carbon monoxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for converting carbon monoxide, involves (a) sequentially passing raw material gas containing carbon monoxide using desulfurization and dechlorination reactor, cooling and separating gas-liquid by water cooling system to obtain purified feed gas with sulfide concentration of less than 0.1 ppm and chloride concentration of less than 0.05 ppm, (b) passing deionized water pump to vaporizer for vaporization, and generating steam after vaporization, (c) uniformly mixing purified feed gas with water vapor, (d) passing obtained mixed gas mixed to transformation reactor from upper end of transformation reactor, passing mixed gas of feed gas and water vapor in conversion reactor using bed layer containing transformation catalyst, and generating carbon dioxide and hydrogen under the reaction pressure of 2.5-3 MPa for generating carbon dioxide and hydrogen, and (e) after performing conversion reaction, discharging gas from lower end of transformation reactor, cooling the gas by water-cooling, and sequentially performing first-stage gas-liquid separation and the second-stage gas-liquid separation to obtain product gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In step (a), the pressure in desulfurization and dechlorination reactor is controlled under 2.5-3 MPa, and controlling the temperature at 200-250 degrees C. In step (a), the crude desulfurization catalyst (A) and fine desulfurization catalyst (B) are respectively installed inside desulfurization reactor, and the volume ratio of catalyst loading is (1.5-2):1. The step (b) involves pressurizing deionized water and pumping to vaporizer at 250-300 degrees C using water pump. In step (b), the internal pipelines of vaporizer are distributed in spiral shape. The step (b) involves passing deionized water from lower end of vaporizer and discharging from upper end. The step (c) involves uniformly mixing purified raw material gas and water vapor in holding furnace at 180-250 degrees C. Preferred Device: In step (d), the shift reactor is isothermal shift reactor, which is equipped with fixed bed tubular reactor, the reactor has ring structure, the sleeve is in middle, and passing cold air and exits from lower end of sleeve for removing heat generated by reaction. In step (d), the inner wall of shift reactor is equipped with annular electric heating jacket. In step (d), the shift reactor is filled with self-developed medium temperature copper-based carbon monoxide shift catalyst.

PN CN111925826-A
TI Wet pyrolysis and gasification treatment of kitchen waste, by pre-treating waste to decompose organic matter into carbon monoxide, hydrogen and carbon dioxide, react hydrogen and carbon monoxide to form methane, and make gasification water.
AU WANG X
   CHEN J
   GU M
   JIAO X
AE UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
GA 2020B55073
AB    NOVELTY - Method for wet pyrolysis and gasification treatment of kitchen waste, involves (a) pre-treating the kitchen waste and placing in a high temperature and high pressure closed container for wet pyrolysis and gasification reaction, placing the kitchen waste under high temperature and high pressure conditions, so that the organic matter in the kitchen waste is decomposed into carbon monoxide, hydrogen and carbon dioxide, and the hydrogen and carbon monoxide chemically react to form methane, and (b) making the water in the kitchen waste to form gasification water, by adjusting the density of gasification water in the closed container, controlling the component ratio of the mixed gas composed of carbon monoxide, hydrogen, carbon dioxide and gasification water in the closed container, and controlling the proportion of methane in the closed container. The high temperature and high pressure conditions refer to the control temperature at 300-500 degrees C and the control pressure at 10-22 MPa.
   USE - Method for wet pyrolysis and gasification treatment of kitchen waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The method directly subjects the deoiled kitchen waste to wet pyrolysis and gasification reaction, rapidly and efficiently performs harmless and reduction treatments on the kitchen waste, and recovers waste oil, methane and organic fertilizer. The method avoids the spread of harmful bacteria, reduces environmental pollution, and effectively recycles useful substances in kitchen waste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for wet pyrolysis and gasification treatment of kitchen waste, involves (a) pre-treating the kitchen waste and placing in a high temperature and high pressure closed container for wet pyrolysis and gasification reaction, placing the kitchen waste under high temperature and high pressure conditions, so that the organic matter in the kitchen waste is decomposed into carbon monoxide, hydrogen and carbon dioxide, and the hydrogen and carbon monoxide chemically react to form methane, and (b) making the water in the kitchen waste to form gasification water, by adjusting the density of gasification water in the closed container, controlling the component ratio of the mixed gas composed of carbon monoxide, hydrogen, carbon dioxide and gasification water in the closed container, and controlling the proportion of methane in the closed container. The high temperature and high pressure conditions refer to the control temperature at 300-500 degrees C and the control pressure at 10-22 MPa. The duration of the wet pyrolysis and gasification reaction is 30-240 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The method includes (a1) deoiling by using physical separation, chemical separation, biochemical separation or/and physical and chemical separation methods to dissolve the oil in the kitchen waste, and performing oil-water separation, simultaneously, collecting waste grease, (b1) mechanically crushing the kitchen waste into a slurry of less than 2 mm, (c1) wet pyrolysis reaction by homogenizing the pretreated food waste and placing in closed container, heating after sealing, controlling the temperature at 300-500 degrees C, and controlling the pressure at 10-22 MPa so that the high temperature and high pressure is formed in the closed container, placing the kitchen waste in a high temperature and high pressure closed container for wet pyrolysis and gasification reaction, placing the kitchen waste under high temperature and high pressure, decomposing the organic matter in the kitchen waste into carbon monoxide, hydrogen and carbon dioxide, so that the water in the kitchen waste further forms gasification water, and the duration is 30-240 minutes, by adjusting the density of gasification water in the closed container, controlling the component ratio of the mixed gas composed of carbon monoxide, hydrogen and carbon dioxide and gasification water in the closed container, and controlling the proportion of methane in the closed container, placing the kitchen waste in a high temperature and high pressure closed container for wet pyrolysis and gasification reaction that includes adding a catalyst to increase the reaction speed, where the catalyst is one or more of ruthenium/carbon, platinum/carbon, and lead/carbon, and the closed container is a high-pressure reaction kettle (autoclave), the adjusting the density of the gasification water in the closed container including adjusting the density of the gasification water in the closed container by controlling the temperature and pressure in the closed container, and controlling the density of gasification water at 0.05-0.06 g/cm3, and controlling the proportion of methane in the closed container including controlling the volume percentage of methane gas in the gaseous environment in the closed container to 14.98-56.32%, (d1) collecting gas by cooling down after reacting, and collecting methane gas in the closed container, and (e1) opening the closed container, collecting the residue liquid after the wet pyrolysis and gasification reaction, filtering and drying to obtain organic fertilizer. The pretreatment of the kitchen waste includes deoiling the kitchen waste, and mechanically crushing the deoiled food waste into a slurry of less than 2 mm. The deoiling treatment includes using physical separation, chemical separation, biochemical separation or/and physical and chemical separation methods to dissolve the oil in the food waste, and performing oil-water separation. The kitchen waste is pretreated and placed in a high temperature and high pressure closed container for wet pyrolysis and gasification reaction, including adding a catalyst to increase the reaction speed, where the catalyst is one or more of ruthenium/carbon, platinum/carbon, and lead/carbon, preferably lead/carbon. The adjusting the density of the gasification water in the closed container includes adjusting the density of the gasification water in the closed container by controlling the temperature and pressure in the closed container, and controlling the density of the gasification water at 0.05-0.06 g/cm3. The controlling the proportion of methane in the closed container includes controlling the volume percentage of methane gas in the gaseous environment in the closed container to 14.98-56.32%. Preferred Conditions: The closed container is a high pressure reaction kettle. The catalyst in step (c1) is lead/carbon.

TI Preparing Porcine circovirus type II-porcine seneca valley virus double inactivated vaccine antigen comprises e.g. culturing BHK-21 cells to monolayer, then adding minimum essential medium nutrient solution, diluting and inoculating.
AU CUI L
   JIANG X
   XU L
   CHEN L
   BAO S
AE FUZHOU DABEINONG BIOTECH CO LTD (FUZH-Non-standard)
   JOFUNHWA BIOTECHNOLOGY FUZHOU CO LTD (JOFU-Non-standard)
   JOFUNHWA BIOTECHNOLOGY NANJING CO LTD (JOFU-Non-standard)
   BEIJING KEMUFENG BIOLOGICAL PHARM CO LTD (DBNO-C)
   BEIJING BIOMEDICAL TECHNOLOGY CENT BIOTE (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202004602D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing Porcine circovirus type II-porcine seneca valley virus double inactivated vaccine antigen comprises e.g. (i) culturing BHK-21 cells to monolayer, digesting with trypsin according to conventional digestion method, then adding minimum essential medium (MEM) nutrient solution includes 8-8.5 wt.% fetal calf serum, stopping digesting, cell counting, diluting to a BHK-21 cell suspension with density of 2x 104 cells/ml, (ii) inoculating Porcine circovirus type II into the above BHK-21 cell suspension, where the seeding volume is 5-5.5 wt.% of the cell suspension volume, incubating in a carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C for 48 hours, then changing the cell maintenance solution and inoculating porcine sonica valley virus, where the seeding amount is 0.1-0.12 wt.% of the cell maintenance solution, and then culturing in a 37 degrees C carbon dioxide incubator, and(iii) inoculating BHK-21 cells with two virus antigens, culturing and harvesting.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing Porcine circovirus type II-porcine seneca valley virus double inactivated vaccine antigen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: increases production efficiency; saves production costs; and improves product quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing Porcine circovirus type II-porcine seneca valley virus double inactivated vaccine antigen comprises (i) culturing BHK-21 cells to monolayer, digesting with trypsin according to conventional digestion method, then adding minimum essential medium (MEM) nutrient solution includes 8-8.5 wt.% fetal calf serum, stopping digesting, cell counting, diluting to a BHK-21 cell suspension with density of 2x 104 cells/ml, (ii) inoculating Porcine circovirus type II into the above BHK-21 cell suspension, where the seeding volume is 5-5.5 wt.% of the cell suspension volume, incubating in a carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C for 48 hours, then changing the cell maintenance solution and inoculating porcine sonica valley virus, where the seeding amount is 0.1-0.12 wt.% of the cell maintenance solution, and then culturing in a 37 degrees C carbon dioxide incubator, and(iii) inoculating BHK-21 cells with two virus antigens, culturing in a carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C for 22-26 hours, and harvesting the cells according to the cytopathic condition, repeatedly thawing the virus solution to obtain Porcine circovirus type II-porcine seneca valley virus double inactivated vaccine antigen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the concentration of the trypsin is 0.23-0.27 wt.%. In the step (ii), the porcine circovirus type II is DBN-SX07 strain with titer of 107.5TCID 50/ml. In the step (ii), the porcine seneca valley virus is SD02 strain with a titer of 109.4TCID 50/ml. In the step (ii), the cell maintenance solution is MEM nutrient solution supplemented with 1 wt.% fetal bovine serum. Preferred Method: In the step (iii), when the cytopathy reaches 80%, and then harvesting the cells. In the step (iv), storing the obtained porcine circovirus type II-porcine seneca valley virus dual inactivated vaccine antigen in an environment below -40 degrees C.

TI Preserving fresh water and vegetables with rapid            moisture structure comprises e.g. using low-cost            non-polar gas argon, helium, nitrogen or carbon            dioxide, peeling raw materials of fruits and vegetables            and inhibiting microbial action.
AU CHEN X
   CUI M
   ZHAO J
   XIE F
AE UNIV NORTHEAST AGRIC (UNEA-C)
GA 2019179211
AB    NOVELTY - Preserving fresh water and vegetables with                rapid moisture structure comprises e.g. (i) using                low-cost non-polar gas argon, helium, nitrogen or                carbon dioxide at mixed ratio; (ii) peeling raw                materials of fruits and vegetables and dividing                into two kinds of non-polar gas argon, helium,                nitrogen or carbon dioxide, packaging in                polyethylene /polyethylene terephthalate                /polyethylene composite film packaging bag and                sealing; (ii) placing a non-polar mixed gas and a                sealed bag containing fresh-cut fruits and                vegetables in an ultra-high pressure container,                pressurizing and controlling pressure increase rate                at 50 MPa/s and pressure relief speed to 100 MPa/s;                and (iii) structuring rapidly water in the surface                and tissues of fresh-cut fruits and vegetables                based on maintaining the original nutritional value                and sensory quality of fruits and vegetables,                reducing activity of water molecules, inhibiting                enzymatic reaction and microbial action and                extending shelf life.
   USE - The method is useful for preserving fresh                water and vegetables with rapid moisture                structure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preserving fresh water and vegetables with                rapid moisture structure comprises (i) using                low-cost non-polar gas argon, helium, nitrogen or                carbon dioxide at mixed ratio; (ii) peeling raw                materials of fruits and vegetables and dividing                into two kinds of non-polar gas argon, helium,                nitrogen or carbon dioxide at ratio of 1:1,                controlling pressure density of 98-110x 102g/cm3 ,                packaging in polyethylene /polyethylene                terephthalate /polyethylene composite film                packaging bag with a thickness of 72 plus minus 2                mu m and sealing package; (ii) placing a non-polar                mixed gas and a sealed bag containing fresh-cut                fruits and vegetables in an ultra-high pressure                container at 10-15 degrees C, pressurizing at                300-400 MPa for 20-30 minutes and controlling                pressure increase rate at 50 MPa/s and pressure                relief speed to 100 MPa/s, where treating does not                include the time of boosting and pressure relief;                and (iii) structuring rapidly water in the surface                and tissues of fresh-cut fruits and vegetables                based on maintaining the original nutritional value                and sensory quality of fruits and vegetables,                reducing activity of water molecules, inhibiting                enzymatic reaction and microbial action and                extending shelf life of fresh-cut fruits and                vegetables to 10-15 days.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The raw materials of                  the fruits and vegetables are fresh cut fruits                  and tubers. Preferred Method: The method                  comprises treating fresh-cut fruits and                  vegetables by ultra-high pressure treatment to                  increase 30-50% in cell liquid viscosity of fresh                  cuts, and reducing activities of peroxidase and                  polyphenol oxidase to 5-10% respectively and                  forming 10-15% of micropores containing non-polar                  gases in fruit and vegetable tissues.

PN WO2018158389-A1; KR2019118648-A; CN110603280-A; EP3589679-A1; US2020062899-A1; JP2020509128-W
TI Preparing polycarbonate ether polyol used to prepare polyurethane or other higher polymer, comprises reacting carbon dioxide and epoxide in presence of double metal cyanide catalyst, metal-ligand catalyst, and starter compound.
AU KEMBER M
   ANDERSON C
AE ECONIC TECHNOLOGIES LTD (ECON-Non-standard)
   ECONIC TECHNOLOGIES (ECON-Non-standard)
   ECONIC TECHNOLOGIES LTD (ECON-Non-standard)
   ECONIC TECHNOLOGIES LTD (ECON-Non-standard)
GA 201870669Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a polycarbonate ether polyol, comprises reacting carbon dioxide and an epoxide in the presence of a double metal cyanide (DMC) catalyst, metal-ligand catalyst (I), and a starter compound having hydrocarbyl derivatives (II).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a polycarbonate ether polyol, which is useful for preparing polyurethane or other higher polymer (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing a polycarbonate ether polyol, comprises reacting carbon dioxide and an epoxide in the presence of a double metal cyanide (DMC) catalyst, metal-ligand catalyst of formula (I), and a starter compound having hydrocarbyl derivatives (Z1(R1z)a) (II).
   M1 = metal cation of formula (M1-(L1)v2) (III);
   cyclic ring = multidentate ligand (e.g. it may be either (i) a tetradentate ligand, or (ii) two bidentate ligands);
   (E1)m = at least one activating group attached to the ligand;
   E1 = activating functional group;
   m = 1-4;
   L1 = coordinating ligand, e.g. neutral ligand or an anionic ligand that is capable of ring-opening an epoxide;
   v = 0-4;
   v2 = valency of M1, or is so that (I) has an overall neutral charge;
   Z1 = optionally substituted alkylene, alkenylene, alkynylene, heteroalkylene, heteroalkenylene, heteroalkynylene, cycloalkylene, cycloalkenylene, heterocycloalkylene, heterocycloalkenylene, arylene, heteroarylene, or alkylarylene, heteroalkylarylene, heteroalkylheteroarylene and or alkylheteroarylene;
   a = at least 2; and
   R1z = -OH and/or -C(O)OH (both preferred), -NHR1a, -SH, PR1a(O)(OH)2, -P(O)(OR1a)(OH) or -PR1a(O)OH, preferably -OH, -C(O)OH.
   Provided that where if v2 is 0 or if v2 is a positive integer and L1 is a neutral ligand which is not capable of ring opening an epoxide, then (i) v is 1-4, or (ii) the step of reacting the carbon dioxide with the epoxide is additionally carried out in the presence of a co-catalyst. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a polymerisation system for the co-polymerisation of carbon dioxide and the epoxide, comprising (I), the DMC catalyst, and the starter compound;
   (2) polycarbonate ether polyol prepared by the method, where the polydispersity index is of 1 to less than 2, preferably 1 to less than 1.3;
   (3) polycarbonate ether polyol (VI); and
   (4) polyurethane or other higher polymer prepared by a reaction of a polyol with a composition comprising a di- or polyisocyanate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: (I) is metal-ligand catalyst of formulae e.g. (Ia)-(Id).X1 = anion, preferably e.g. F, Br, I, Cl, BF4, OAc, O2COCF3 and NO3; andL1 = e.g. OAc, OC(O)CF3, lactate, 3-hydroxypropanoate, NO3, ethyl , CH3, OSO(CH3)2) and OCH3.The starter compound is diols e.g. 1,2-ethanediol, 1-2-propanediol, 1,3-propanediol, 1,2-butanediol, 1-3-butanediol, 1,4-butanediol, 1,5-pentanediol, 1,6-hexanediol, 1,8-octanediol, 1,10-decanediol, 1,4-cyclohexanediol, 1,2-diphenol, 1,3-diphenol, 1,4-diphenol, neopentyl glycol, catechol, cyclohexenediol, 1,4-cyclohexanedimethanol, dipropylene glycol, diethylene glycol, tripropylene glycol, triethylene glycol, tetraethylene glycol, polypropylene glycols and polyethylene glycols, triols e.g. glycerol, benzenetriol, 1,2,4-butanetriol, 1,2,6-hexanetriol, tris(methanol)propane, tris(methanol)ethane, tris(methanol)nitropropane, trimethylol propane, polypropylene oxide triols and polyester triols, tetraols e.g. calix(4)arene, 2,2- bis(methanol)-1,3-propanediol, erythritol, pentaerythritol or polyalkylene glycols, polyols e.g. sorbitol or polyalkylene glycols, diacids e.g. oxalic acid, malonic acid, succinic acid, glutaric acid, adipic acid, pimelic acid, suberic acid, azelaic acid, sebacic acid, undecanedioic acid, dodecanedioic acid or other compounds having mixed functional groups including lactic acid, glycolic acid, 3-hydroxypropanoic acid, 4-hydroxybutanoic acid, 5-hydroxypentanoic acid. The DMC catalyst comprises at least two metal centers and cyanide ligands. The DMC catalyst additionally comprises at least one complexing agent, water, a metal salt and/or an acid, optionally in non-stoichiometric amount. Preferred Method: The reaction is carried out at a pressure of 1-20 (preferably 1-15) bar carbon dioxide, at a temperature of 5-200 degrees C, preferably 40-80 degrees C. The DMC catalyst is prepared by treating a solution of a metal salt with a solution of a metal cyanide salt in the presence of at least one complexing agent, water, and/or an acid, preferably the metal salt is of the formula M2(X2)p (IV),M2 = Zn(II), Ru(II), Ru(III), Fe(II), Ni(II), Mn(II), Co(II), Sn(II), Pb(II), Fe(III), Mo(IV), Mo(VI), Al(III), V(V), V(VI), Sr(II), W(IV), W(VI), Cu(II) or Cr(III);X2 = anion including halide, oxide, OH, sulfate, carbonate, CN, oxalate, thiocyanate, isocyanate, isothiocyanate, carboxylate or nitrate; andp = at least 1, and the charge on the anion multiplied by p satisfies the valency of M2.The metal cyanide salt is of formula (Y1)qM3(CN)b(A1)c.M3 = Fe(II), Fe(III), Co(II), Co(III), Cr(II), Cr(III), Mn(II), Mn(III), Ir(III), Ni(II), Rh(III), Ru(II), V(IV) or V(V);Y1 = proton or alkali metal ion or an alkaline earth metal ion (e.g. K);A1 = anion including halide, oxide, OH, sulfate, CN, oxalate, thiocyanate, isocyanate, isothiocyanate, carboxylate or nitrate;q, b = at least 1; andc = 0 or at least 1, where the sum of the charges on the anions Y1, CN and A1 multiplied by q, b and c (e.g. Y x q + CN x b + A1 x c) satisfies the valency of M3.The complexing agent is a (poly)ether, a polyether carbonate, a polycarbonate, a poly(tetramethylene ether diol), a ketone, an ester, an amide, an alcohol and/or a urea, preferably propylene glycol, polypropylene glycol, (m)ethoxy ethylene glycol, dimethoxyethane, tert-butyl alcohol, ethylene glycol monomethyl ether, diglyme, triglyme, methanol, ethanol, isopropyl alcohol, n-butyl alcohol, isobutyl alcohol and sec-butyl alcohol, 3-buten-1-ol, 2-methyl-3-buten-2-ol, 2-methyl-3-butyn-2-of formula and/or 3-methyl-1-pentyn-3-ol, and the acid, if present, has the formula (HrX3) (V).X3 = anion including halide, sulfate, phosphate, borate, chlorate, carbonate, CN, oxalate, thiocyanate, isocyanate, isothiocyanate, carboxylate or nitrate; andr = integer corresponding to the charge on the counter ion X3.(I) is used in combination with a co-catalyst including tetraalkyl ammonium salts (e.g. a tetrabutyl ammonium salt), tetraalkyl phosphonium salts (e.g. a tetrabutyl phosphonium salt), bis(triarylphosphine)iminium salts (e.g. a bis(triphenylphosphine)iminium salt), or a nitrogen containing nucleophile (e.g. methylimidazole (e.g. N-methyl imidazole), dimethylaminopyridine (e.g. 4-methylaminopyridine), 1,5,7-triazabicyclo(4.4.0)dec-5-ene, 7-methyl-1,5,7-triazabicyclo(4.4.0)dec-5-ene or 1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene). The polycarbonate ether polyol has a molecular weight of 500-6000 Da, preferably 700-5000 Da or 500-3000 Da.
   Preferred Definitions:
   M1 = Mg, Ca, Zn, Ti, Cr, Mn, V, Fe, Co, Mo, W, Ru, Al or Ni;
   R1z = -OH;
   a = 2-6; and
   v, m = 2.
   EXAMPLE - 1,6-Hexanediol (0.30g) and 0.005 g DM0 catalyst 1 (comprised of zinc complex i.e. Zn3(Co(CN)6)2 h.ZnCl2 0.5H2O2((CH3)3COH) were dried prior to copolymerization in the reactor at 100 degrees C for 0.5 hours under vacuum. The autoclave was then allowed to cool to ambient temperature before charging with 0.072 g solution of 1-(acetyloxy)-4,23-dimethyl-6,21-bis(4-methyl-1,7-bis(tributylazaniumyl)heptan-4-yl)-2,25-dioxa-10- lambda (5),17- lambda (5)-diaza-1-cobaltahexacyclo(15.8.0.0(1,10).0(3,8).0(11,16).0(19,24))pentacosa-3,5,7,9,17,19(24),20,22-octaene-10,17-bis(ylium) tetrakis(nitric acid) in 15 ml propylene oxide. The autoclave was then pressurized with 2 bar carbon dioxide and allowed to heat to 70 degrees C. Upon stabilization of the autoclave at the desired temperature, the autoclave was pressurized to 20 bar carbon dioxide pressure stirred at 800 rpm for 16 hours. The reaction was terminated by cooling the reactor to 5 degrees C and vented slowly to give 97% polycarbonate ether polyol.
DC A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A17 (Polymers of unsubstituted aliphatic monoolefins; including polyethylene.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.)
MC A02-A00A; A05-E06A; A05-G02; A05-G03; A05-H01A; A10-D03; A10-D05; A10-E01

PN CN114910476-A
TI Device for simulating ultra-critical carbon            dioxide storage in layered salt rocks under dynamic            differential pressure, has liquid injection pump whose            outlet is connected with lower cavity of pressure            difference simulation tank, and transfusion pipe is            provided with pressure regulating valve.
AU TANG L
   CHEN S
   LIU W
   CHEN J
   JIANG D
   YANG F
   FAN J
AE UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
GA 2022A91218
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a pressure difference                simulation tank provided with a tank body and a                tank cover. A middle part of the tank body is fixed                with an annular baffle plate. The tank cover is                provided with an air inlet joint and an exhaust                joint. An inner cavity of the pressure difference                simulation tank is divided into an upper cavity and                a lower cavity. An inlet of a water pump is                connected with a first brine container. An outlet                of the water pump is connected with an upper cavity                of the pressure difference simulation tank. An                outlet of a liquid injection pump is connected with                a lower cavity of the pressure difference                simulation tank. A transfusion pipe is provided                with a pressure regulating valve and a hydraulic                meter.
   USE - Device for simulating ultra-critical carbon                dioxide storage in layered salt rocks under dynamic                differential pressure.
   ADVANTAGE - The device can simulate that the layered rock                salt test piece is soaked in brine, one side of the                layered rock salt test piece is subjected to brine                and supercritical carbon dioxide pressure, another                side of the layered rock salt test piece is                subjected to formation pressure, and the layered                rock salt test piece is in complex environment                state under the dynamic fluctuation pressure                difference condition so as to closely grasp change                rule of micro-structure characteristics of the                layered salt rock.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of a device                for simulating ultra-critical carbon dioxide                storage in layered salt rocks under dynamic                differential pressure in partial sectional.

PN GB2602318-A; WO2022136831-A1
TI System for producing liquid methane from process fluid created from biowaste material, has cooling unit that is arranged to cool second stage to lower temperature than first stage so as to create temperature gradient through separator.
AU MILTON G
   COLES R
   ATKINS A
AE RICARDO UK LTD (RCRD-C)
GA 202285001X
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) comprises an inlet (180) that receives a process fluid including carbon dioxide and methane, both in a gaseous phase. A separator (113) includes a cooling unit for cooling the process fluid to a temperature in which carbon dioxide in the process fluid freezes to a solid phase and in which methane in the process fluid condenses to a liquid phase. The separator includes a first stage (190) for receiving the process fluid via the inlet, and a second stage (195) downstream of the first stage for collecting condensed methane in a liquid phase. The first stage has a collector for collecting frozen carbon dioxide in a solid phase. The cooling unit is arranged to cool the second stage to a lower temperature than the first stage so as to create a temperature gradient through the separator.
   USE - System for producing liquid methane from process fluid created from biowaste material.
   ADVANTAGE - The system advantageously removes the carbon dioxide and/or other impurities and converts the biogas into liquid methane, which can be cheaply and conveniently stored and transported. The second stage is configured to provide an enlarged internal area to increase the efficacy of cooling by the cooling unit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for: (1) a method for producing liquid methane from a process fluid; (2) a computer readable non-transitory storage medium that comprises a program for a computer configured to cause a processor to perform a method for producing liquid methane from a process fluid.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a system for producing liquid methane from biowaste material.
   System for producing liquid methane (100)
   Separator (113)
   Inlet (180)
   First stage (190)
   Second stage (195)

   LIU Y
   XU J
   WU J
AE UNIV BENGBU (UBBU-C)
   ANHUI DENGYUN GLASS TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202275390J
AB    NOVELTY - Method for treating inner surface of warm                glass container, involves uniformly spraying water                (S101) on the inner surface of a warm glass                container, wiping off the water on the inner                surface of the warm glass container except in the                crack, uniformly spraying powdered sodium silicate                (S102) on the inner surface of the warm glass                container, rotating the warm glass container in a                horizontal position, continuously introducing                carbon dioxide gas (S103) into the warm glass                container, blowing out sodium silicate on the inner                surface of the warm glass container except inside                the crack through carbon dioxide gas, aging (S104),                heating by gradient heating method, frosting (S105)                the surface of the warm glass container, and                coating the surface of the warm glass                container.

   ADVANTAGE - The method changes the traditional crack                processing mode, especially aiming at the                processing effect of glass surface more crack and                large crack size, specifically, surface to clean to                the temperature glass container, and the sodium                silicate is prohibited in the crack. The method                does not use high corrosive acid, in the way of                filling the crack, it can realize the effect of                eliminating the crack practical and                environment-friendly.

PN US2022138649-A1
TI Method for verifying implementation and            maintenance of regenerative management practices in            agricultural parcels, involves indicating that            compliance with regenerative practices plan is            verified.
AU KOSMALA M C
   COFFIN M A
   BRAU E
   BHAMBHANI P C
   ASHTEKAR J M
AE CIBO TECHNOLOGIES INC (CIBO-Non-standard)
GA 202259935P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining a regenerative                carbon footprint value for a parcel with a                difference of a regenerative carbon footprint and a                baseline carbon footprint. The carbon credits that                are valued according to the regenerative practices                plan are approved and published. The remotely                sensed images against corresponding crop curves are                processed and evaluated to determine                compliance/noncompliance indicators that correspond                each key date corresponding to implementation and                maintenance of each of the one or more regenerative                management practices. The compliance/noncompliance                indicators are stored within a parcel database. The                compliance is determined with the regenerative                practices plan at a certification date. The                components of the baseline and regenerative average                yearly greenhouse gas emissions comprise average                carbon dioxide flux from the soil, nitrous oxide                flux from the soil, carbon dioxide from tractor                fuel use, and carbon dioxide from production of                nitrogen fertilizer.
   USE - Method for carbon footprint determination,                monitoring, and verification for agricultural                parcels based on implementation of regenerative                management practices such as crop rotation, cover                cropping, low-till/low-toweling, and low nitrogen                content fertilization.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables providing a superior                technique for employing a combination of public                data, commercial data, field trial data, and crop                simulation data to determine, monitor, and verify                the potential and effectiveness of regenerative                management practices and to generate agro-economic                metrics and objective valuations for a vast number                of agricultural parcels.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a computer-readable storage medium storing                program instructions; and
   (2) a system for verifying implementation and                maintenance of regenerative management practices in                agricultural parcels with a carbon dioxide                equivalent (CO2E) sequestration server.

PT P
PN CN114225853-A
TI Preparation of composite nano-aerogel material for capturing carbon dioxide, involves dissolving silicon source in organic solvent, dripping catalyst solution and mixing with directing agent solution, adding active doping component to obtained transparent porous sol, and drying and calcining.
AU ZHANG Z
   QIU J
AE LAFARRI SHENZHEN LLC (LAFA-Non-standard)
GA 202249073E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of composite nano-aerogel material involves dissolving silicon source in an organic solvent and stirring to obtain silicon source solution, adding catalyst and composite structure directing agent into water respectively to prepare the catalyst solution and directing agent solution, dripping the catalyst solution to the silicon source solution for heating and stirring to obtain a uniform low-density transparent sol, adding the directing agent solution and performing secondary heating and stirring to obtain a uniform transparent porous sol, adding active doping component solution to the transparent porous sol, heating and stirring, and adjusting pH value of obtained mixed system during stirring process to obtain a composite porous wet gel, and subjecting the composite porous wet gel to supercritical drying and graded calcination.
   USE - Preparation of composite nano-aerogel material used for capturing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method fully combines variety of active group doping modification and porous structure control methods, and can significantly improve the physical and chemical adsorption and catalytic performance of the obtained nanoporous aerogel material. The composite nano-aerogel material has adsorption capacity of carbon dioxide of more than 4.5-7.95 mmol/g, and extremely high carbon dioxide capture efficiency. The preparation method is simple, easy to operate, and has excellent repeatability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the composite nano-aerogel material, which includes 5-20 nm nano-silica and active doping components, with a porous structure and specific surface area of more than 950 m2/g, where the active doping component is doped between silica particles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further involves introducing dispersant. Preferred Components: The composite structure directing agent is two or more of citric acid, lauryl ammonium, sodium lauryl sulfonate, polyvinyl alcohol, polyethylene glycol, polyacrylic acid, and ammonium citrate. The catalyst is hydrochloric acid, nitric acid, oxalic acid, acetic acid or aqueous ammonia. The dispersant is polyvinylpyrrolidone, polyacrylamide, ethanolamine, mercaptan or sodium citrate. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the active doping component to the dispersant is (1-10):1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The silicon source is a silane or a silicon salt precursor which can be hydrolyzed and condensed to form silicon dioxide. The active doping component is one or more of alkali metal and/or non-metal components which do not chemically react with silica sol. The active doping component is one or more of iron salts, magnesium salts, barium salts, zinc salts, strontium salts, aluminum salts, titanium salts, nickel salts, copper salts and at least one of high-melting point group component. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the silicon source to the active doping component is (1-5):(0.01-0.5). The mass ratio of silicon source, catalyst and composite structure directing agent is (1-5):(0.0004-0.5):(0.1-1). The concentration of the silicon source solution is 30-70 wt.%. The concentrations of the catalyst solution and the directing agent solution are 0.1-10 wt.% and 0.5-10 wt.%, respectively.

PT P
PN WO2022055125-A1; KR2022033812-A; KR2409244-B1
TI Purification and recirculation system for improving methane recovery efficiency in biogas, has gas generator for generating electric power using by-product gas, where by-product gas generated in separation membrane process unit is recycled or supplied to gas generator to produce power.
AU CHANG W S
   LIM J W
   PARK S Y
   OH J S
   KIM K M
   LEE J J
   NAMGUNG H G
   HA S Y
   LEE C S
   HAN S H
   YIM J H
   KIM S J
   JANG W S
   LIM J
   JUN L
   NAMGUNG H
   HASUNGYONG
   LEE C
   LIM J H
AE KOREA DISTRICT HEATING CORP (KODI-Non-standard)
   AIRRANE CO LTD (AIRR-Non-standard)
GA 202241444R
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a compressor (100) for compressing the incoming biogas. A separation membrane processing unit (200) is provided for separating compressed biogas into biomethane, concentrated carbon dioxide and by-product gas. A gas generator (300) is provided for generating electric power using the by-product gas. The by-product gas generated in the separation membrane process unit is recycled or supplied to the gas generator to produce power. The separation membrane process unit uses a multi-stage separation membrane process. The separation membrane of the separation membrane process unit has a carbon dioxide permeability of 50GPU or more. The separation membrane of the separation membrane process unit has a selectivity of carbon dioxide/methane of 10 or more.
   USE - Purification and recirculation system for improving methane recovery efficiency in biogas used as fuel for power generation, boilers, factories and automobiles, or city gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The power generation process is combined with separation membrane process that significantly reduces methane recovery loss rate by supplying recycle gas to a generator from a separation membrane device to produce electricity. The methane recovery loss rate is greatly reduced to 1% or less by supplying the recycle gas to the generator by the biogas separation membrane device and separating and discharging carbon dioxide, thus contributing to the high quality of biogas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a purification and recirculation method for improving methane recovery efficiency in biogas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the separation membrane processing unit. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Compressor (100)
   Separation membrane processing unit (200)

PN DE102020113447-B3; WO2021233498-A1
TI Carbon dioxide or ambient air direct collector device has first and second adsorption-desorption area whose adsorber volumes arranged temporarily one above other for desorption along original air flow, with time delay.
AU LEIB F
   SCHARRER B
AE DACMA GMBH (DACM-Non-standard)
GA 2021C2251A
AB    NOVELTY - The device (1) has a first adsorption-desorption area (131) and a second adsorption-desorption area (132) whose displaceable adsorber volumes (161-163,164-166) are arranged on opposite sides of a common volume partition (14). The volume partition is displaceable in its lateral position for dividing the volume between the first and the second adsorption-desorption area. A thermal desorption unit (17) of the adsorbent is provided. A vacuum suction collection arrangement (181,182) is provided for carbon dioxide. The respective adsorber volumes (151,152,153,161,162,163) in the first and in the second adsorption-desorption area temporarily one above the other for desorption along the original air flow can be arranged, with a time delay either in the first or second adsorption-desorption area.
   USE - Carbon dioxide or ambient air direct collector device.
   ADVANTAGE - The pressure losses that occur when air or, in general, a fluid flows through the adsorber within the device is reduced to minimum. The pressure loss resulting from the flow through the adsorber volume can be reduced by dividing it into several sections through which the flow passes one after the other, compared to a simple bed with the same adsorbent mass. The packing density can be increased for the desorption process, which significantly increases the energy efficiency for the desorption process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the carbon dioxide or ambient air direct collector device.
   Device (1)
   Common volume partition (14)
   Thermal desorption unit (17)
   First adsorption-desorption area (131)
   Second adsorption-desorption area (132)
   Adsorber volume (151,152,153,161,162,163)
   Adsorber volumes (161-163,164-166)
   Vacuum suction collection arrangement (181,182)

AU KIM Y W
   SUEN K
   SAH I
   KIM M H
AE KOREA ATOMIC ENERGY RES INST (KAER-C)
GA 202150496Y
AB    NOVELTY - Graphite dust removal system comprises a primary circulation unit circulation pump (100) for supplying coolant to a high temperature gas furnace (200) and supplying steam generated in the high temperature gas furnace to a heat exchanger by opening and closing an isolation valve of the primary circulation unit. The oxygen supplied to the heat exchanger by opening the isolation valve of the oxygen supply unit (500) generates carbon dioxide by redox reaction with the graphite dust deposited in the heat exchanger flow path and dust removal unit collects and removes the generated carbon dioxide by opening an isolation valve of the carbon dioxide collecting unit (600). A secondary circulation part circulation pump (800) is used for supplying heated gas to the heat exchanger to accelerate the oxidation reaction of graphite deposited in the heat exchanger.
   USE - The system is useful for removing the graphite dust.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can prevent or improve the flow path of a printed board type intermediate heat exchanger or a tube type steam generator used in a high temperature gas furnace and an ultra high temperature gas furnace system to prevent deterioration of the performance of the heat exchanger.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for graphite dust removal method.

   USE - Used as polystyrene/thermoplastic polyurethane supercritical foam composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite material: optimizes the content of each raw material, which fully exerts their respective advantages, and complements and promotes each other; has improved the quality, high tensile strength and excellent mechanical properties, low compression permanent deformation rate, excellent resilience performance, high elasticity, high cell density, small cell size, uniform cells, excellent foaming effect, and has both super lightness.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the composite material comprising (i) mixing polystyrene, thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer, polysiloxane-polyurethane block copolymer, carbon fiber, dispersant and oxidized polyethylene wax, adding into a internal mixer and internal mixing at 120-130 degrees C for 15-30 minutes, adding crosslinking agent and nucleating agent, mixing at 130-135 degrees C until the components are mixed evenly to prepare mixed compound, (ii) pressing the intimate mixed compound into a plate by using a tableting machine and cutting it, (iv) placing the obtain supercritical foamed plate into an oven, preheating at 130-140 degrees C, placing the preheated foamed mother board into a high-temperature mold to pass carbon dioxide gas, and quickly releasing the carbon dioxide gas in the mold when the carbon dioxide gas reaches saturation in the sheet to rapidly foam the sheet to obtain final product, where the temperature of the high-temperature mold is 135-145 degrees C, and the pressure of the carbon dioxide gas in the mold is 14-17 MPa.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The composite material comprises 65 pts. wt. polystyrene, 23 pts. wt. thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer, 15 pts. wt. polysiloxane-polyurethane block copolymer, 5.5 pts. wt. carbon fiber, 5 pts. wt. oxidized polyethylene wax, 0.45 pts. wt. nucleating agent, 1.6 pts. wt. crosslinking agent and 3 pts. wt. dispersant. Preferred Components: The nucleating agent is mixture of nano calcium carbonate, calcium phosphate and zinc bismethacrylate in a mass ratio of 10:3.5-4.5:1.5-2.2. The crosslinking agent is zinc peroxide. The dispersant is random polystyrene maleic anhydride. The composite material further comprises 2-3 pts. wt. light stabilizer, and 2-4 pts. wt. antioxidant. The light stabilizer is benzotriazole and/or benzophenone. The antioxidant is an antioxidant 1010 (pentaerythritol tetrakis(3-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propionate).

PN EP3622966-A1
TI Particulate pharmaceutical or nutraceutical            excipient useful for preparing pharmaceutical or            nutraceutical composition, comprises microcrystalline            cellulose, and surface-reacted calcium carbonate.
AU DE MIGUEL L
   LANDER S
AE OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
GA 202021898E
AB    NOVELTY - Particulate pharmaceutical or nutraceutical                excipient comprises (a) microcrystalline cellulose,                and (b) surface-reacted calcium carbonate, where                the surface-reacted calcium carbonate is a reaction                product of natural ground calcium carbonate or                precipitated calcium carbonate with carbon dioxide                and one or more hydronium ion donors, where the                carbon dioxide is formed in situ by the hydronium                ion donors treatment and/or is supplied from an                external source, where the weight ratio of the                microcrystalline cellulose to the surface-reacted                calcium carbonate is 99.9:0.1-50:50.
   USE - The excipient is useful for preparing                pharmaceutical or nutraceutical composition                (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a process (P1) for preparing particulate                pharmaceutical or nutraceutical excipient involves                (a) mixing microcrystalline cellulose and                surface-reacted calcium carbonate, where the                surface-reacted calcium carbonate is a reaction                product of natural ground calcium carbonate or                precipitated calcium carbonate with carbon dioxide                and one or more hydronium ion donors, where the                carbon dioxide is formed in situ by the hydronium                ion donors treatment and/or is supplied from an                external source, in a weight ratio of the                microcrystalline cellulose to the surface-reacted                calcium carbonate from 99.9:0.1-50:50, and (b)                co-processing the mixture obtained in step (a),                thus obtaining the particulate pharmaceutical or                nutraceutical excipient;
   (2) a pharmaceutical or nutraceutical                composition comprising the particulate                pharmaceutical or nutraceutical excipient and                optionally at least one active ingredient chosen                from active ingredients, inactive pharmaceutical or                nutraceutical precursors, biologically active                ingredients, inactive biological precursors and                their mixtures; and
   (3) a process (P2) for preparing                pharmaceutical or nutraceutical composition                involves (i) providing a particulate pharmaceutical                or nutraceutical excipient, and (ii) subjecting the                particulate pharmaceutical or nutraceutical                excipient to dry granulation, wet granulation, melt                granulation or direct compression, preferably                direct compression.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The surface-reacted                  calcium carbonate has (i) a volume median                  particle size d50 of 0.5-50 mm, preferably 1.5-15                  mm, and/or (ii) a Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET)                  specific surface area of 5-200 m2/g, preferably                  40-100 m2/g, measured using nitrogen and the BET                  method according to ISO 9277:2010, and/or (iii)                  an intra-particle intruded specific pore volume                  of 0.1-2.3 cm3/g, preferably 0.6-1.6 cm3/g,                  calculated from mercury porosimetry measurement.                  The (i) natural ground calcium carbonate is                  chosen from marble, chalk, limestone, and their                  mixtures, or (ii) precipitated calcium carbonate                  is chosen from precipitated calcium carbonates                  having an aragonite, vaterite or calcite crystal                  form, and their mixtures. The hydronium ion donor                  is chosen from hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid,                  sulfurous acid, phosphoric acid, citric acid,                  oxalic acid, an acidic salt, acetic acid, formic                  acid, and their mixtures, preferably at least one                  hydronium ion donor is chosen from hydrochloric                  acid, sulfuric acid, sulfurous acid, phosphoric                  acid, oxalic acid, dihydrogen phosphate ion,                  being at least partially neutralised by a cation                  chosen from lithium ion, sodium ion and/or                  potassium ion, hydrogen phosphate, being at least                  partially neutralised by a cation chosen from                  lithium ion, sodium ion, potassium ion, magnesium                  ion and/or calcium ion, and their mixtures, more                  preferably at least one hydronium ion donor is                  chosen from hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid,                  sulfurous acid, phosphoric acid, oxalic acid, or                  their mixtures, and most preferably at least one                  hydronium ion donor is phosphoric acid. The                  microcrystalline cellulose has a (i) a loose bulk                  density of 0.20-0.52 g/ml, preferably 0.26-0.36                  g/ml, and/or (ii) a weight median particle size                  d50 of 10-1000 mm, preferably 20-200 mm. The                  weight ratio of the microcrystalline cellulose to                  the surface-reacted calcium carbonate is                  99.9:0.1-75:25, preferably 98:2-90:10. The                  excipient comprises co-processed microcrystalline                  cellulose and surface-reacted calcium carbonate                  in that the surface-reacted calcium carbonate is                  in intimate association with the microcrystalline                  cellulose. The excipient has a loose bulk density                  of 0.25-0.90 g/ml, preferably 0.25-0.65 g/ml.                  Preferred Process: The co-processing step (b) is                  performed by dry- or wet-processing, preferably                  high-shear mixing, spray drying, milling or their                  mixtures. The mixing step (a) is performed in an                  aqueous medium such as to form an aqueous slurry                  comprising the microcrystalline cellulose and the                  surface-reacted calcium carbonate. The process                  further involves (c) contacting the particulate                  pharmaceutical or nutraceutical excipient before                  step (b) or the composition obtained in step (b)                  with at least one active ingredient, preferably                  at least one active ingredient is chosen from                  active ingredients, inactive pharmaceutical or                  nutraceutical precursors, biologically active                  ingredients, inactive biological precursors and                  their mixtures, and/or at least one adjuvant                  chosen from natural or synthetic scenting agents,                  natural or synthetic flavoring agents, natural or                  synthetic coloring agents, natural or synthetic                  sweeteners, lubricants, disintegrants, glidants,                  and their mixtures. Preferred Composition: The                  composition further comprises at least one                  adjuvant chosen from natural or synthetic                  scenting agents, natural or synthetic flavoring                  agents, natural or synthetic coloring agents,                  natural or synthetic sweeteners, lubricants,                  disintegrants, glidants, and their                  mixtures.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN WO2019194676-A1; NL2020731-B1
TI Method for temporary storage of energy in pressure vessel of energy buffer system, involves discharging liquid from pressure vessel, for de-charging energy buffer and producing electrical energy with liquid.
AU BOONTJES L
   BRINKMAN M L
AE ITREC BV (ITRE-C)
GA 201984926P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves pumping a liquid into a pressure vessel (122), such that decreasing the volume of a carbon dioxide and charging an energy buffer. The liquid is discharged from the pressure vessel, for de-charging the energy buffer and producing electrical energy with the liquid. The carbon dioxide is temporarily stored in a condensation state, throughout the cycle. The pressure vessel is operated as the energy buffer that has a de-charged condition where the total amount of potential energy stored in the energy buffer is low. A charged condition where the total amount of potential energy stored in the energy buffer is high.
   USE - Method for temporary storage of energy in pressure vessel of energy buffer system, and in wind turbine assembly of wind farm used for generating electricity and buffering energy (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The liquid is pumped into the pressure vessel to charge the energy buffer, and generating electrical energy when de-charging the energy buffer by discharging the liquid from the pressure vessel through a liquid driven generator, that is much more efficient than pumping air into a pressure vessel and recovering energy by driving a generator with compressed air. The liquid is sprayed into the pressure vessel is that the liquid sprayed into the pressure vessel absorbs some of the heat generated when reducing the volume of the carbon dioxide, and the spraying results in a more efficient heat absorption. The fluid barrier prevents the mixing or dissolution of the carbon dioxide in the liquid, thus ensuring efficient charging and de-charging of the energy buffer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a wind turbine assembly.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a detailed outline of the system.
   Fluid barrier (15)
   Electrical generator (18)
   Pressure vessel (122)
   Fluid line (141)
   Lower end cap (1222)

   HAO Y
   GUO X
   LIU M
   LIU Y
   HUANG Z
   GAO P
   WANG C
   WANG Z
   LIU R
AE CHINA NAT OFFSHORE OIL CORP (CNOO-C)
   CNOOC RES INST CO LTD (CNOO-C)

PN CN109136973-A; CN109136973-B
TI Preparing non-precious metal doped molybdenum carbide hydrogen evolution electrode comprises dissolving ammonium molybdate and non-precious metal salt in water, adding benzylamine, and stirring, adjusting the pH of system and stirring.
AU SONG T
   XIE J
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 201905441B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing non-precious metal doped molybdenum carbide hydrogen evolution electrode, comprises (i) dissolving ammonium molybdate and a non-precious metal salt in water, adding benzylamine, and stirring for 2-4 hours, (ii) adjusting the pH of the system obtained in the step (i) to 4-6 with hydrochloric acid, stirring at 40-80 degrees C for 3-6 hours, filtering, and washing and drying the obtained precipitate, (iii) calcining the sample obtained by drying step (ii) under an inert gas atmosphere, washing and drying to obtain a non-precious metal doped molybdenum carbide catalyst, and (iv) formulating the non-noble metal doped molybdenum carbide catalyst obtained in the step (iii) into a catalyst solution, and immersing the pretreated electrode in the catalyst solution, stirring for 6-10 hours, taking out, and drying.
   USE - The electrode is useful for reducing carbon dioxide to produce acetic acid in a microbial electrosynthesis system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: avoides shortcomings of slow biofilm electron transfer rate, and improves the acetic acid production efficiency of biodiesel reduction carbon dioxide. purpose.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) non-precious metal-doped molybdenum carbide hydrogen evolution electrode prepared by above mentioned method; and
   (2) Use of non-precious metal-doped molybdenum carbide hydrogen evolution electrode, comprising in the microbial synthesis system, carbon felt, carbon cloth or carbon paper as anode and as cathode in the non noble metal doped molybdenum carbide hydrogen evolution electrode proton exchange membrane synthesis system separating the microorganism into the cathode chamber and the anode chamber, the cathode chamber inoculating autotrophic microorganism, applied potential of - 0.8V to -1.2V vs silver/silver chloride, and introducing carbon dioxide gas electric catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to produce acetic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The non-noble metal salt in the step (i) is a mixture of an iron salt and a cobalt salt, where the molar ratio of the iron ion to the cobalt ion is 1-5:1. The molar ratio of the ammonium molybdate and the non-noble metal ion in the non-noble metal salt in the step (1) is 5-10:1, and the ratio of the ammonium molybdate, water and benzylamine is 1 - 5 mol: 250 mL: 10mL. The calcination in the step (iii) is carried out at 600-900 degrees C for 6-10 hours . The solvent used in the catalyst solution in the step (iv) is obtained by mixing a 1-5 wt.% aqueous solution of a perfluorosulfonic acid type polymer and an aqueous solution of ethanol having a volume concentration of 70-95%, and the volume ratio of the two is 0.1-5: 10. The electrode in the step (iv) is carbon felt, carbon cloth or carbon paper. The loading amount of the non-noble metal doped molybdenum carbide catalyst in the step (iv) is from 1-30 mg cm2 with respect to the surface area of the electrode.

PT P
PN CN108554936-A
TI Semiconductor 8-inch quartz wafer film production E-MAX process regeneration method, involves removing contaminants of quartz parts, sublimating dry ice into carbon dioxide, and cleaning quartz parts with de-ionized water and lens paper.
AU FAN Y
AE SUZHOU PEIKAI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 201877006C
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves clamping quartz parts of E-MAX process of to-be-generated semiconductor wafer film production by a rotating clamping device. Liquid carbon dioxide is passing in a storage bottle through an atomizing nozzle into a mixing chamber. Liquid nitrogen in a liquid nitrogen tank is sprayed into the mixing chamber through a liquid nitrogen nozzle. Contaminants on a surface of the quartz parts are removed by liquid nitrogen and ice mixture spray. The liquid nitrogen is volatilized in ambient environment. Dry ice is sublimated into carbon dioxide. The quartz parts are cleaned with de-ionized water and a lens paper, where the liquid nitrogen nozzle is a sapphire nozzle.
   USE - Semiconductor 8-inch quartz wafer film production E-MAX process regeneration method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables providing simple wafer film production E-MAX process regeneration process, volatilizing the liquid nitrogen into nitrogen in surrounding environment when the wafer film production E-MAX process regeneration process is completed, sublimating the dry ice into the carbon dioxide in a non-toxic environment-friendly and pollution-free manner, generating low temperature impact on the surface of the quartz parts by the liquid nitrogen and dry ice mixture to decrease surface temperature of the quartz parts in a rapid manner, generating large stress in an inner part of the quartz parts and an impact surface, so that a pollutant on the surface of the quartz parts is stripped in an easy manner, avoiding damage on the surface of the quartz parts by using ice particles with low hardness generating weak impact force, providing better wafer film production E-MAX process regeneration effect is better and low production cost.
DC P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).); U11 (Semiconductor Materials and Processes)

PN CN107855134-A; CN107855134-B
TI Asymmetric imine-pyridin-cobalt metal catalyst for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, is represented by asymmetric hexacoordinate octahedral model structure, where catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide is formic acid.
AU LIU F
   KANG P
AE TECH INST PHYSICS & CHEM CHINESE ACAD (CAPC-C)
   UNIV CHINESE ACAD SCI (UCAS-C)
GA 2018264790
AB    NOVELTY - Asymmetric imine-pyridin-cobalt metal catalyst is represented by compound (I) which has asymmetric hexacoordinate octahedral model structure.
   USE - Asymmetric imine-pyridin-cobalt metal catalyst for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claim).
   ADVANTAGE - The asymmetric imine-pyridin-cobalt metal catalyst used in catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide which is formic acid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Asymmetric imine-pyridin-cobalt metal catalyst is represented by compound of formula (I) which has asymmetric hexacoordinate octahedral model structure.
   R1 and R2=alkyl, trimethyl silicon group, amido-cyanogen, imine, alkoxy, benzyl or halogen substituent;
   halogen substituent=florine, chlorine, bromine or iodine;
   R3=hydrogen atom, alkyl group, alkoxy , phenyl, benzyl, amino, pyridyl, oxazolyl or biotin;
   R4 and R5=hydrogen atom, alkyl group, alkoxy group, amino group, nitrile group, aryl group or biotin; and
   L1 and L2=halogen atom, acetonitrile, carboxylate groups, methanol molecules, water molecules, or tetrahydrofuran group.
   .. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing asymmetric imine-pyridin-cobalt metal catalyst, which involves:
   (A) dissolving first reactant in tetrahydrofuran to obtain second mixed solution;
   (B) dissolving alkyl magnesium bromide in diethyl ether to obtain third mixed solution;
   (C) pouring third mixed solution and mixed solution, mixing and reacting to obtain fourth mixed solution;
   (D) saturating ammonium containing solution and fourth mixed solution for quenching reaction to obtain first reactant which is represented by formula (II);
   (E) adding sixth reagent into alcohol to obtain mixed solution;
   (F) adding sixth mixed solution and acid to obtain mixed seventh liquid;
   (G) mixing solution of aniline compound and reacting to obtain reactant; and
   (H) adding tetrahydrofuran, obtained reagent and cobalt salt, mixing and reacting to obtain asymmetric imine-pyridin-cobalt metal catalyst.
   ..
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The alkyl bromide is methyl magnesium bromide, ethyl magnesium bromide, butyl magnesium bromide, propyl magnesium bromide. The saturated ammonium-containing solution is preferably 0.01-1 mole per liter in saturated ammonium chloride aqueous solution and saturated ammonium bromide aqueous solution. The concentration of second mixed solution third mixed solution is 1-2 mole per liter. The volume ratio of second mixed solution to third mixed solution is 1-10:1. The mixed solution C is added drop-wise into second mixed solution, stirred and reacted to obtain fourth mixed liquid, where dropping temperature is 0-10 degrees C, stirring reaction temperature is 15-20 degrees C and time is 2-4 hours, preferably 3-4 hours. The saturated ammonium containing solution and fourth mixed solution of mixed mode is that saturated ammonium solution into fourth mixed solution till reaction liquid surface not smoked and solution become orange, where dropping temperature is in ice-bath condition 0-10 degrees C. The preparation of mixed liquid and aniline compound involves adding aniline compound in mixed solution for reaction, where mole ratio of reactant and aniline compound in mixed solution is 0.8-1:1, heating refluxing and stirring reaction condition for 22-24 hours, reflowing at 90-110 degrees C in way of mixing mixed solution and acid to mixed solution dripping acid. The volume ratio of mixed solution and acid is 1:0,001- 0.01 in mixed liquid G and mixing aniline compound, added aniline compound in mixed solution for reaction. The mole ratio of reactant and aniline compound in mixed solution G is 0.8-1:1. The reaction condition is heating reflux stirring for 22-24 hours, and reflux temperature is 90-110 degrees C. The mole ratio of tetrahydrofuran, reactant and cobalt salt is 1-1 and time for stirring reaction 2-4 hours. Preferred Component: The alcohol is methanol, ethanol, propanol, butanol or glycol. The acid is formic acid, acetic acid, propionic acid, butyric acid or oxalic acid. The aniline compound is 2, 6-diisopropyl aniline, aniline, 2, 6-dinitroaniline or 2 6-dibromo aniline. The concentration of mixed solution is 0.01-0.2 mole per liter. The volume ratio of mixed solution and dripping acid is 1:0,001-0.01.

TI Desulfurizing flue gas carbon hydrogen fuel involves performing photo-catalysis reduction of carbon dioxide in flue gas by using electrostatic precipitator and oxygen-enriched burning power plant flue gas condensation.
AU ZHAO Y

   ZHENG C
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)

AB    NOVELTY - Desulfurizing flue gas carbon hydrogen fuel involves performing photo-catalysis reduction of carbon dioxide in flue gas by using electrostatic precipitator (2) and oxygen-enriched burning power plant flue gas condensation (1) and carbon dioxide compressing unit and simultaneously removing oxidizing sulfur dioxide in flue gas.
   USE - Method for desulfurizing flue gas carbon hydrogen fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to desulfurizing flue gas carbon hydrogen fuel is cost effective, uses solar energy to realizes oxygen enriched combustion flue gas conversion and desulfurization of smoke, and has small energy consumption. The oxygen enriched combustion technology has important significance for popularization and application.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for photocatalytic unit which comprises reaction unit and separation unit, where reaction unit comprises upper end opening of reaction container whose inner bottom is set with stirring unit. The reaction container is provided with mixed solution which comprises bicarbonate solution which is suspended in bicarbonate solution of photo catalyst. The reaction container (51) is respectively provided with flue gas inlet, gas outlet, liquid outlet and liquid inlet, where top end of reacting container is installed with quartz glass cover plate to ensured illumination required for photo-catalysis reaction into reaction container and separation unit is centrifugal extraction unit, and separation unit (4) is connected with liquid outlet of reaction vessel for obtaining carbon dioxide for formation of photo catalytic reduction. The separating unit is connected with liquid inlet of reaction vessel for separating mixed liquid of liquid hydrocarbon product is then transported back to reaction vessel.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of flue gas carbon hydrogen fuel.
   Oxygen-enriched burning power plant flue gas condensation (1)
   Electrostatic precipitator (2)
   Flue gas desufurization (3)
   Separation unit (4)
   Reaction container (51)

PN IN201611014709-A
TI Preparing oral care formulation having e.g. antibacterial property, involves extracting clove oil by supercritical fluid carbon dioxide extraction process, mixing extracted clove oil with water and ultrasonically processing mixture.
AU KAMRA P
   KAMRA V
AE KAMRA P (KAMR-Individual)
   KAMRA V (KAMR-Individual)
GA 201811791E
AB    NOVELTY - Process of preparing an oral care formulation comprising 1-1.5% clove oil in water, involves (a) extracting clove oil by supercritical fluid (SCF) carbon dioxide extraction process, (b) mixing the SCF-carbon dioxide extracted clove oil with deionized water, and (c) ultrasonically processing the mixture using high intensity sonication energy (HISE) device to obtain the oral care formulation, where the sonication is performed at a frequency of 20 kHz, temperature of 28-32 degrees C and amplitude of 100% for 15 minutes.
   USE - The process is useful for preparing an oral care formulation, which provides anti-plaque, antimicrobial and pain relieving properties and antibacterial activity against Streptococcus pyogenes and Staphylococcus aureus (all claimed). The formulation is useful for enhancing human immunity, preventing and treating cancer, tooth decay, periodontitis, tooth ache, sore gums, mouth ulcer and corrosion produced by food residues on teeth, and for cleaning oral cavity anytime.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is simple, prevents pollution of machinery, requires minimum energy and labor and avoids the addition of any external emulsifying agents or chemical stabilizers, hence is cost-effective and suitable for large scale production of the mouth Wash, and offers better yield as compared to the conventional techniques. The formulation is free of alcohol, has increased stability and shelf-life, ensures strong permeability, rapid and effective action for a long duration with very less side-effect, is more durable, retains the bioactive compounds with natural fragrance and aroma, and is highly concentrated enriched with all the natural properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an oral care formulation, which comprises 1-1.5% SCF-carbon dioxide extracted clove oil in water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Process: The clove oil extracted by SCF-carbon dioxide method is rich in eugenol and eugenol acetate. The extracted clove oil and water are combined to form a homogeneous solution using HISE device.
   ACTIVITY - Antibacterial; Analgesic; Immunostimulant; Cytostatic; Antiinflammatory; Antiulcer. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - The formulation in the form of mouth wash or mouth spray is administered by oral route (all claimed). No dosage details given.

PN CN115029159-A
TI Continuous operation non-tar low nitrogen biomass            gas production device, has feeding device connected to            feeding port of gasification furnace, where hot gas is            conveyed to combustion device through gas conveying            device from gas outlet.
AU CHEN J
AE FOSHAN YICHEN HONGYAN ENERGY TECHNOLOGY (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 2022B7694J
AB    NOVELTY - Continuous operation non-tar low nitrogen                biomass gas production device comprises an                oxidation reaction layer located at the position of                the lowest layer of the gasification furnace                cavity. The oxidation reactor layer is an input                gasification agent from the bottom, and the biomass                raw material in the oxidation reactor is oxidized                with the oxidant to produce carbon dioxide, carbon                monoxide and/or carbon mononucleic acid. The                reduction reaction layer is used for exhausting                oxygen through the oxidation reaction of the                oxidation response layer. The biomass raw materials                are not completely burned to generate carbon                dioxide and releases heat. The carbon dioxide                generated in the oxidation reaction layer rises and                rises to the reduced reaction layer, and reduces                the carbon dioxide produced in the reduction                reactor layer in the reduced reactor layer.
   USE - Continuous operation non-tar low nitrogen                biomass gas production device for producing biomass                energy and geothermal energy, and actively                developing tidal energy resources along sea                tide.
   ADVANTAGE - The device has high stability, high                efficiency, high automation degree, simple                operation, fast ignition speed, ignition for many                minutes to enter the normal gas state, the whole                combustion process adopts negative oxygen                combustion, and gas conveying is stable. It has                strong mixing effect for the large-power combustion                engine gas. The combustion is more complete, the                tail gas oxygen content is very low, energy-saving                and environment-friendly. The explosion gas will be                discharged from the explosion door, avoiding the                occurrence of safety accident.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                continuous operation tar-free low-nitrogen biomass                gas production method.
