PN CN113943947-A
TI Composite thin-film electrode for electrochemical            reduction of carbon dioxide to formic acid, comprises            lead sheet as substrate which is attached to            halogenated nicotinic acid-lead organic framework film            layer formed by coating nicotinic acid-lead organic            framework slurry on substrate and calcining.
AU SONG S
   HE Z
   WEN L
   DONG S
   WANG D
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 202218873C
AB    NOVELTY - A composite thin-film electrode comprises a                lead sheet as a substrate. The substrate is                attached with a halogenated nicotinic acid-lead                organic framework film layer. The halogenated                nicotinic acid-lead organic framework film is                formed by coating halogenated nicotinic acid-lead                organic framework slurry on a substrate and                calcining.
   USE - Composite thin-film electrode for                electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide to                formic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite thin-film electrode has stable                performance. The decomposition and reduction                phenomenon of the halogenated nicotinic acid-lead                organic framework film layer does not occur in the                working range of 0.19-0.98 V. During the                electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide, the                divalent lead in the organic frame film of lead                halonicotinate and the zerovalent lead on the lead                sheet forms a redox pair. The organic frame film                layer of the lead halogenated nicotinate is kept                stable during the reduction process, the lead sheet                is not reduced to zero-valent lead and has superior                stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                preparation method for electrochemically reducing                carbon dioxide composite thin-film electrode, which                involves (1) mixing lead precursor, metal organic                framework ligand and alkaline solution, adding pure                acetonitrile, and hydrothermally reacting to obtain                lead metal organic framework slurry, where the                metal organic framework ligand is 5-chloronicotinic                acid, 2-chloronicotinic acid, 4-chloronicotinic                acid or 5,6-dichloronicotinic acid, (2) coating the                lead metal organic frame slurry on a lead sheet,                and calcining to obtain a composite thin film                electrode with lead metal organic framework film                attached to a lead sheet.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The halogenated                  nicotinic acid is 5-chloronicotinic acid,                  2-chloronicotinic acid, 4-chloronicotinic acid or                  5,6-dichloronicotinic acid. Preferred Process:                  The composite thin-film electrode is used as a                  cathode in the three-electrode system to perform                  electrochemical reduction reaction of carbon                  dioxide to formic acid. Preferred Composition:                  The volume ratio of acetonitrile to alkaline                  solution is 80:1-100:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The reference                  electrode in the three-electrode system is                  silver/silver chloride electrode, and the counter                  electrode is platinum wire.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The electrolyte in                  the anode pool is 0.1-2 M sodium hydroxide                  aqueous solution. The electrolyte in the cathode                  pool is 0.1-2 M potassium bicarbonate aqueous                  solution saturated with carbon dioxide. In the                  mixed solution of lead precursor, metal organic                  framework ligand and alkaline solution, the lead                  precursor is a divalent soluble lead salt, and                  the concentration of divalent lead is 1.33 mol/L.                  The substance concentration of the metal organic                  framework ligand is 1-1.5 times that of divalent                  lead. When the lead metal organic framework                  slurry is coated on the lead sheet, the amount of                  lead metal organic framework slurry coated on the                  surface of each 1 cm2 of lead sheet is 0.001-0.1                  g. Preferred Component: The alkaline solution is                  aqueous ammonia solution, potassium hydroxide                  solution or sodium hydroxide solution.

TI Coal stratum composite phase carbon dioxide seepage characteristic testing device, has displacement sensor for monitoring axial deformation of rock core test piece, and confining pressure loading pump for monitoring radial deformation amount.
AU JIANG Y
   HE W
   YAO H
   LIANG W
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 2021E1173H
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a phase change monitoring device mainly provided with an internal pressure interface (30) and a temperature sensor on a cavity wall of a test piece. A fluid outlet (22) of a fixed plug is connected with an outlet pressure sensor, a back pressure valve, a gas dryer (25), a mass flow meter (26) and a gas collecting bottle (29). A deformation measuring and data collecting device is provided with a displacement sensor and a confining pressure loading pump and a computer. The displacement sensor monitors axial deformation of a rock core test piece and a liquid for monitoring radial deformation amount. The computer collects experiment data.
   USE - Coal stratum composite phase carbon dioxide seepage characteristic testing device for use in a coal-bed methane storage country. Can also be used for a waste oil and gas field, salty water layer, deep part is not coal bed, and a coal bed methane reservoir.
   ADVANTAGE - The device solves the problem of linear average value of density, viscosity and so on in the past permeability calculation process. It can solve the problem that the mixed fluid concentration and relative seepage rule change process in the process of increasing production of coal-bed gas by gas injection displacement.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a coal stratum composite phase carbon dioxide seepage characteristic testing method.

TI Energy-saving device useful for strengthening            low-temperature methanol washing absorption in coal            chemical industry, has carbon dioxide gas-liquid            separating tank whose gas outlet is connected with gas            inlet of hydrogen sulfide gas-liquid separating            tank.
AU YANG S
   LI D
   QIAN Y
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 2021D71351
AB    NOVELTY - The device has an absorption tower divided                into upper tower and lower tower, providing upper                tower of the absorption tower with bottom liquid,                gas inlet, poor methanol inlet, and top synthesis                gas outlet. Liquid inlets of a Carbon Dioxide (CO2)                gas-liquid separating tank are connected in turn. A                third pipeline is connected with a pressurizing                pump. A gas outlet of the carbon dioxide gas-liquid                separating tank is connected with a gas inlet of a                hydrogen sulfide gas-liquid separating tank. The                gas outlet of Hydrogen Sulfide (H2S) gas-liquid                separating tank is connected a compressor and a                cooler. The outlet of cooler is connected with a                rough synthesis gas inlet at bottom of lower tower                of absorption tower. The gas outlet at top of lower                tower of absorption tower is connected with gas                inlet of liquid-gas jet pump.
   USE - The device is useful for strengthening                low-temperature methanol washing absorption in coal                chemical industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: realizes the enhanced carbon                dioxide absorption process of low-temperature                methanol washing; reduces the amount of methanol                and the gas flow rate at the top of the gas-liquid                separation tank in the process of low-temperature                methanol washing absorption; reduces the heat load                of the reboiler during the methanol thermal                regeneration process; reduces the amount of steam;                and reduces the circulation of refrigerant and                methanol in the process to achieve the purpose of                energy saving and consumption reduction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an                energy-saving technique for strengthening                low-temperature methanol washing absorption.

PT P
PN WO2021219529-A1; TW202200207-A; AU2021263871-A1
TI Preparing granules used in nutraceutical product,            by providing aqueous suspension comprising            surface-reacted calcium carbonate, homogenizing aqueous            suspension comprising surface-reacted calcium            carbonate, and removing liquid from aqueous suspension            by spray drying.
AU DE MIGUEL L
   MAURER M
   CREMASCHI A
   KARL F
   SESMA PARDO I
   MOHLER G
   CAMARGO J
   SHARMA L
   HOEPFL W
   SESMA P I
   HOPFL W
AE OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
   OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
GA 2021C22707
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing granules comprising                surface-reacted calcium carbonate, involves (a)                providing aqueous suspension comprising                surface-reacted calcium carbonate, where the                surface-reacted calcium carbonate comprises                reaction product of natural ground or precipitated                calcium carbonate with carbon dioxide and acids,                and the carbon dioxide is formed in situ by acid                treatment and/or supplying from external source,                (b) homogenizing aqueous suspension comprising                surface-reacted calcium carbonate, and (c) removing                obtained liquid from aqueous suspension comprising                surface-reacted calcium carbonate by spray drying                for obtaining granules comprising surface-reacted                calcium carbonate.
   USE - The method is used for preparing granules used                in nutraceutical product, agricultural product,                veterinary product, cosmetic product, such as dry                cosmetic and/or dry skin care composition, such as                shaving powder, body powder, baby powder, foot                powder and deodorant powder, home product, food                product, packaging product and personal care                product, preferably oral care composition, such as                toothpaste, toothgel, toothpowder, cement, mouth                strip or buccal adhesive patch, (chewable) tooth                tablet, chewable pastille or chewable gum,                preferably toothpaste, toothpowder or chewable                tooth tablet, or as excipient in pharmaceutical                product, such as analgesics/anesthetics, antiseptic                ingredients, throat soothing agents (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces free-flowing granules with                a high bulk density, flow properties and                compactability and high mechanical stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the                granules comprising surface-reacted calcium                carbonate, where the surface-reacted calcium                carbonate comprises reaction product of natural                ground or precipitated calcium carbonate with                carbon dioxide and acids, the carbon dioxide is                formed in situ by acid treatment and/or supplying                from external source, the granules have bulk                density of 0.25-0.70 g/ml, preferably 0.35-0.60                g/ml.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves adding                  at least one disintegrant before, during and/or                  after step (b) in concentration of 0.3-10 wt.%,                  preferably 1-5 wt.% based on total dry weight of                  surface- reacted calcium carbonate. Preferred                  Components: The natural ground calcium carbonate                  comprises calcium carbonate comprising minerals                  comprising marble, chalk and/or limestone, and                  the precipitated calcium carbonate comprises                  precipitated calcium carbonates having amorphous,                  aragonitic, vateritic and/or calcitic                  mineralogical crystal forms. Preferred                  Properties: The surface-reacted calcium carbonate                  in aqueous suspension of step (a) has volume                  median grain diameter d50 of 0.5-50 mu m,                  preferably 1-10 mu m measured by using laser                  diffraction, and/or Brunauer-Emmett-Teller                  specific surface area of 1-200 m2/g, preferably                  40-70 m2/g measured using nitrogen and                  Brunauer-Emmett-Teller method according to ISO                  9277:2010. The aqueous suspension of step (a) has                  solid content of 1-40 wt.% preferably 7-26 wt.%                  based on total weight of aqueous suspension. The                  homogenizing process in step (b) is carried out                  for 1-5 times, preferably 1-3 times. The                  homogenizing in step (b) is carried out by                  milling. The homogenizing process in step (b) is                  carried out at pressure ranging of 50-900 bar,                  preferably 200-650 bar, and/or initial                  temperature of 5-95 degrees C, preferably 15-60                  degrees C. The spray drying process in step (c)                  is carried out at pressure of 0.1-300 bar,                  preferably 7-25 bar, and/or temperature measured                  as inlet temperature of 150-950 degrees C,                  preferably 180-550 degrees C. The granules have                  volume particle size d90 of 50-500 pm, preferably                  70-350 pm as measured dry at 0.1 bar dispersion                  pressure by laser diffraction, volume median                  particle size d50 of 5-300 pm, preferably 12-175                  pm as measured dry at 0.1 bar dispersion pressure                  by laser diffraction, and volume particle size                  d10 of 1-100 mu m, preferably 1-80 mu m as                  measured dry at 0.1 bar dispersion pressure by                  laser diffraction, and/or spherical shape. The                  granules have volume median grain diameter d50 of                  0.5-50 mu m, preferably 1-10 mu m, measured using                  laser diffraction, and/or intra-particle intruded                  specific pore volume of 0.15-1.60 cm3/g,                  preferably 0.30-1.35 cm3/g calculated from                  mercury intrusion porosimetry measurement.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The disintegrant                  comprises sodium croscarmellose, starch, modified                  cellulose gums, modified starch, insoluble                  cross-linked polyvinylpyrrolidone, starch                  glycolate, such as sodium starch glycolate, micro                  crystalline cellulose, pregelatinized starch,                  sodium carboxymethyl starch, low-substituted                  hydroxypropyl cellulose, homopolymers of                  N-vinyl-2-pyrrolidone, alkyl-, hydroxyalkyl-,                  carboxyalkyl-cellulose esters, alginic acid,                  microcrystalline cellulose and its polymorphic                  forms, ion exchange resins, gums, chitin,                  chitosan, clays, gellan gum, crosslinked                  polacrilin copolymers, agar, gelatine, dextrins,                  acrylic acid polymers, carboxymethylcellulose                  sodium/calcium, hydroxypropyl methylcellulose                  phthalate, shellac and/or effervescent mixtures                  such as bicarbonates in combination with acids,                  such as citric acid and/or tartaric acid.
   EXAMPLE - The example section describes the particle                size distribution, specific surface area, bulk                density, Brookfield viscosity, granule stability                and granule particle size distribution of the                granules that does not support the claims.

Polymeric adhesives are also classified in Section A (C09H, J).); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).); B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); B06 (Inorganics - including fluorides for toothpastes etc.); C07 (Apparatus, formulation, general. including veterinary syringes, general formulations where the active compound is not central to the invention (e.g. wettable powders) and analysis.); C03 (Other organic compounds, inorganic compounds and multicomponent mixtures. PT P
PN US2021298198-A1
TI System for cooling processors within data center in building or enclosed space using carbon dioxide comprises e.g. cooling device located in proximity to processors in which working fluid is expanded for cooling air and for discharging cooled air around processors.
AU SCAPINI P
   BOSTICK G L
AE NOOTER ERIKSEN INC (NOOT-Non-standard)
GA 2021B0129U
AB    NOVELTY - System (100) comprises: a compressor (130) for compressing a carbon dioxide working fluid; an air cooled heat exchanger (140) downstream from the compressor located in an ambient atmosphere out-of-doors for cooling the working fluid, and an expansion device downstream provided from the exchanger; a cooling device adapted to be located in proximity to processors in which working fluid is expanded for cooling air and for discharging cooled air around the processors for cooling processors; and a return line returns working fluid from the cooling device to the compressor, where the cooling system is configured so that the carbon dioxide is maintained in a supercritical state throughout an entirety of a cooling cycle.
   USE - The system is useful for cooling many processors within a data center in a building or enclosed space using carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - THe sytem: removes the need for water loops, make up water, and evaporative towers often associated with high heat removal systems; provides reduced capital, operational, and maintenance costs; eliminates a heat exchanger and a pump of the typical cooling system, while improving performance and functionality; and improves performance because removal of heat exchangers that improves the overall thermal cycle efficiency by reducing pressure losses within the flow loop.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for cooling many processors within a data center in a building or enclosed space.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a simplified schematic view of the cooling system for the data center.
   System (100)
   Adjacent rows (110)
   Compressor (130)
   Air cooled heat exchanger (140)
   Expansion nozzle (150)

AB    NOVELTY - The plant lamp has a lamp frame (1) which includes several lamp structure components (11) side by side. A frame component (12) that connects the left and right ends of each lamp structure component. A gas pipe component (2) is installed on the right side of each lamp structure component. The lamp structure assembly includes a lamp structure (111) provided with a cavity, a printed circuit board (PCB) board (112) with an LED lamp installed on the bottom surface of the lamp structure, and a power cord connected to the PCB board with the LED lamp. Several exhaust holes are provided on both sides of the lamp structure. The air pipe assembly includes a screw cap fixed on the top surface of each lamp structure and a T-shaped air pipe joint. The bottom end holes of the T-shaped air pipe joint are installed on the corresponding nut portions, and the top end holes are connected by air pipes.
   USE - Plant lamp used in plant lighting system (claimed) for discharging carbon dioxide.

PN CN111550862-A; CN212930212-U; WO2021227538-A1
TI Constant temperature water supply system using carbon dioxide heat pump, has heat exchange tube whose outlet is provided with return water temperature sensor provided to detect water temperature at outlet of heat exchange tube.
AU TANG X
   XIONG D
   PAN H
   YOU J
   ZHOU D
   KANG Q
   SONG X
AE JIANGSU SUJING PURIFICATION GROUP CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
   SUZHOU SUJING AIMFAR AIR CONDITIONING CO (SUZH-Non-standard)
   JIANGSU SUJING GROUP CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020818399
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a primary circuit that includes a vertical return water tank. The bottom of the return water tank is provided with a return water outlet (41) communicating with a heat pump water heater. Multiple backwater inlets are connected to the heat exchanger are sequentially arranged on the side of the backwater tank in a vertical direction. A backwater valve is provided on each backwater inlet. The return water tank is provided with multiple return water tank temperature sensors that detect the water temperature in the return water tank corresponding to the height of each of the return water inlets. The outlet of a first heat exchange tube (21) is provided with a return water temperature sensor that detects the water temperature at the outlet of the first heat exchange tube.
   USE - Constant temperature water supply system using carbon dioxide heat pump.
   ADVANTAGE - Since the system detects the water temperature and multiple return water tank temperature sensors through the return water temperature sensor, even if the temperature of the return water flowing out of the first heat exchange tube fluctuates, the return water can be input into the return water tank with a temperature close to that of the return water tank. The water always maintains a stable layered state, avoiding the fluctuation of water temperature in the water flow sent from the return water tank to the heat pump water heater, thus realizes constant temperature water supply.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the constant temperature water supply system using carbon dioxide heat pump.
   Carbon dioxide heat pump water heater (1)
   Heat exchanger (2)
   First heat exchange tube (21)
   Second heat exchange tube (22)
   Return water outlet (41)

TI Desorption device for flue gas carbon dioxide capture system, has steam condensate heat recoverer that is set with steam condensate produced by stripping reboiler to exchange heat with heat-exchanged hot rich liquid staged flow.
AU WANG T
   DONG W
   FANG M
   YI N
   WANG Q
   LUO Z
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 202049838K
AB    NOVELTY - The desorption device has a variable diameter desorption tower (1001) whose upper portion of the desorption section is fed with hot rich liquid main flow (2). The hot rich liquid graded flow (3) is flowed through a steam condensate heat recoverer (1004) and is sent to the flash section. The heat-exchanged hot rich liquid staged flow (4) is desorbed by the variable-diameter desorption tower and the circulating and regenerated hot lean liquid is generated. The regenerated hot lean liquid (6) is sent to a stripping reboiler (1002) and is heated by steam (9) to generate secondary steam (7) and desorb partion of carbon dioxide to produce waste lean liquid (8), which is sent to the variable diameter desorption tower respectively. The steam condensate heat recoverer is set with the steam condensate (10) produced by the stripping reboiler to exchange heat with the heat-exchanged hot rich liquid staged flow.
   USE - Desorption device for flue gas carbon dioxide capture system.
   ADVANTAGE - The desorption device is provided to strengthen the gas-liquid heat transfer effect to improve the heat exchange effect of the stripping reboiler, and reduce the consumption of carbon capture steam.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the desorption device for flue gas carbon dioxide capture system.

   Secondary steam (7)
   Waste lean liquid (8)
   Steam (9)

   Steam condensate heat recoverer (1004)

PN WO2018237336-A1; US2020114333-A1
TI Porous ceramic structure used for separation of            gas e.g. carbon dioxide from flue gas of fossil fuel            burning power plant, comprises ceramic material having            open cell structure with pores, and aqueous sorbent            solution in pores.
AU CAMPBELL P
   BAKER S
   HERNANDEZ M
   KNIPE J M
   STOLAROFF J K
   HERNANDEZ M C
AE LAWRENCE LIVERMORE NAT SECURITY LLC (USGO-C)
   LAWRENCE LIVERMORE NAT SECURITY LLC (USGO-C)
GA 201900088U
AB    NOVELTY - A porous ceramic structure (202) comprises a                ceramic material having an open cell structure with                pores (206) connected through the ceramic material                from one side of the ceramic material to an                opposite side of the ceramic material, and an                aqueous sorbent solution (204) in the pores of the                ceramic material, in which a portion of the aqueous                sorbent solution is retained in the pores by                capillary action.
   USE - Porous ceramic structure is used for                separation of gas (claimed) e.g. carbon dioxide                from flue gas of fossil fuel burning power                plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The porous ceramic structure has controlled                porosity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) ink for three-dimensional printing of                ceramic material, which comprises 50 wt.% or more                metal oxide nanoparticles and a polymer                resin;
   (2) formation of porous ceramic material,                which involves preparing ink, forming component                using the ink by additive manufacturing process                with the ink, curing, heating for removing the                polymer and for forming porous ceramic material                from the metal oxide nanoparticles, and infilling                the porous ceramic material with aqueous sorbent                solution; and
   (3) separation of gases with a system of                porous ceramic material and an aqueous sorbent,                which involves infilling porous ceramic material in                a sorbent solution, placing the infilled porous                ceramic material in a container having known                pressure of gas, measuring absorbance of the gas in                the porous ceramic material, and heating the porous                ceramic material with carbon dioxide absorbed in                the sorbent for releasing carbon dioxide from the                sorbent and regenerating the system of porous                ceramic material and the sorbent.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                porous ceramic structure.
   Porous ceramic structure (202)
   Aqueous sorbent solution (204)
   Pores (206)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Composition: The ceramic material                  comprises yttrium(III) oxide-doped zirconium                  dioxide. The ceramic material is in the form of a                  crushed ceramic structure having crushed                  component having average diameter of less than                  400 microns. The ceramic material is nanoporous                  having nanostructural support for the aqueous                  sorbent solution. The component is free standing                  porous structure, and has average diameter of                  more than 1 cm. The porous ceramic material has                  porous ceramic discs and shaped structures having                  a uniform shape, which are packed in an                  absorbance bed. Preferred Process: The additive                  manufacturing process is direct ink writing, in                  which the ink is extruded through a nozzle, or                  projection micro-stereolithography. The                  absorbance of the gas in the infilled porous                  ceramic material is measured in terms of a                  pressure change of the gas in the container. The                  separation method involves heating the infilled                  porous ceramic material for desorption of                  absorbed gas, in which the sorbent solution is                  recycled for absorption of gas. Preferred                  Properties: The average diameter of the pores is                  50-500 nm, preferably 50-200 nm. The density of                  the ceramic material is 20-50% of density of a                  solid nonporous ceramic form having the same                  composition as the ceramic material. The features                  of the component have an average diameter of                  diameter of the nozzle. The features of the                  component have an average diameter of 10 mu m or                  more.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - IMAGING AND COMMUNICATION - Preferred Composition: The ink contains a                  crosslinking agent. The ink contains 50-80 wt.%,                  preferably 70 wt.% metal oxide                  nanoparticles.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The aqueous sorbent                  solution is an ionic solution. The aqueous                  sorbent solution is sodium carbonate solution                  with concentration of 20 wt.% at room                  temperature. The metal oxide nanoparticles                  comprise yttrium(III) oxide-doped zirconium                  dioxide. The absorbed gas is carbon dioxide.                  Preferred Property: The metal oxide nanoparticles                  have average diameter of 20-600 nm.

TI Apparatus for producing methanol from organic material, has chemical reaction arrangement which reacts methane gas with water vapor and carbon dioxide in stoichiometric condition between methane steams reforming and methane dry reforming.

   AVOCET IP LTD (AVOC-Non-standard)
   AVOCET IP LTD (AVOC-Non-standard)

GA 201842847U
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (10) has anaerobic digestion arrangement (20) for receiving the organic material and for anaerobically-digesting the organic material in oxygen-depleted conditions to generate an anaerobic digestion gas (AD gas) comprising methane, and carbon dioxide. Pressure swing absorption (PSA) arrangement is provided for the removal of excess carbon dioxide. A chemical reaction arrangement (30) is provided for reacting the methane gas with water vapor and carbon dioxide in a stoichiometric condition between methane steams reforming and methane dry reforming to generate a synthesis gas, and converting the synthesis gas to methanol. A recovery arrangement is provided for recovering unreacted methane and feeding the recovered unreacted methane into the exit stream from the anaerobic digestion arrangement. An arrangement is provided for feeding hydrogen into the exit stream from the anaerobic digestion arrangement.
   USE - Apparatus for producing methanol from organic material such as agricultural organic waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The stoichiometric condition allows for highly efficient production of methanol, based on biogas supplied from an anaerobic digester supplied for organic material such as organic agricultural waste. The potential yield of methanol is maximized by preventing any stoichiometric imbalance during steam reforming. The number of bi-products formed during production of methanol despite operating the chemical reaction arrangement at low temperature is reduced significantly and using low cost and/or less active catalysts.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for using apparatus for producing methanol from organic material.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the structure of apparatus for producing methanol from organic material.
   Apparatus for producing methanol from organic material (10)
   Anaerobic digestion arrangement (20)
   Chemical reaction arrangement (30)
   Biogas feed-pipe arrangement (40)
   Control arrangement (50)

PT P
PN CN107973274-A
TI Preparing nano spherical ammonium perchlorate using supercritical gas antisolvent method comprises e.g. setting the temperature of the autoclave water bath, obtaining ammonium perchlorate acetone solution and ultrasonically oscillating.
AU XUAN J
AE XUAN J (XUAN-Individual)
GA 201835154K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano spherical ammonium perchlorate using supercritical gas antisolvent method comprises (i) setting the temperature of the autoclave water bath and keeping for 10-15 minutes, (ii) obtaining ammonium perchlorate acetone solution, ultrasonically oscillating and placing the solution in an autoclave, (iii) introducing the supercritical carbon dioxide gas as antisolvent into the autoclave, (iv) precipitating the ammonium perchlorate and (v) collecting the ammonium perchlorate particles trapped and precipitated.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nano spherical ammonium perchlorate using supercritical gas antisolvent method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides stable ammonium perchlorate and is simple.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing nano spherical ammonium perchlorate using supercritical gas antisolvent method comprises (i) setting the temperature of the autoclave water bath, and keeping for 10-15 minutes after the temperature is stabilized at a set value of 40-43 degrees C, (ii) dissolving the ammonium perchlorate in acetone solvent to obtain 1-1.3 g/100ml ammonium perchlorate acetone solution, ultrasonically oscillating for 10-12 minutes in a constant temperature water bath sonicator and placing the solution in an autoclave, (iii) introducing the supercritical carbon dioxide gas as antisolvent into the autoclave at an inlet rate of 33-35 kg/hour when the temperature of the ammonium perchlorate acetone solution was the same as the autoclave temperature, (iv) releasing the pressure to normal when the pressure in the autoclave reaches to 9-11 MPa, removing the acetone solvent and carbon dioxide antisolvent by gasification under reduced pressure and precipitating the ammonium perchlorate from the acetone solvent in microcrystalline form and (v) purging the autoclave with dry carbon dioxide gas and collecting the ammonium perchlorate particles trapped and precipitated by the sintered metal filter in the autoclave to obtain the spherical ammonium perchlorate fine particles having a particle size of 30-40 nm

AE YANG J (YANG-Individual)
GA 201825227M
AB    NOVELTY - Methanol washed oxygen generator comprises electrolytic cell including cathode (2) and anode (3) that are connected with an electrolytic power (4) supply. The inside of the electrolytic cell is a closed space. The electrolyte (5) in the electrolytic cell (1) is a molten metal oxide, and cathode and the anode are graphite electrodes. The electrolytic reaction in the electrolytic cell produces a mixed gas of oxygen and carbon dioxide and is collected in an electrolytic cell. A methanol scrubber (7) is connected with the electrolytic cell.
   USE - Methanol washed oxygen generator.
   ADVANTAGE - The methanol washed oxygen generator has simple structure and reasonable design, enables to obtain oxygen with high purity, and utilizes resources fully.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Methanol washed oxygen generator comprises electrolytic cell including cathode (2) and anode (3) that are connected with an electrolytic power (4) supply. The inside of the electrolytic cell is a closed space. The electrolyte (5) in the electrolytic cell (1) is a molten metal oxide, and cathode and the anode are graphite electrodes. The electrolytic reaction in the electrolytic cell produces a mixed gas of oxygen and carbon dioxide and is collected in an electrolytic cell. A methanol scrubber (7) is connected with the electrolytic cell, and the methanol scrubber contains a low temperature methanol of -5 degrees C to -20 degrees C. The mixed gas of oxygen and carbon dioxide in the electrolytic cell enters the methanol scrubber. The carbon dioxide is dissolved in the low-temperature methanol, and oxygen is discharged from the discharge pipe (8) connected to the methanol scrubber.

GA 201807524T
AB    NOVELTY - The sampling apparatus has a collar (130A, 130B) that is connectable to housing and is positioned in a connected position or a disconnected position. An external wall of collar prevents removal of an inserted cannula from a slot when collar is in connected position. The cannula is insertable and/or removable from slot when collar is in disconnected position. A sampled breath is carried to an end tidal carbon dioxide monitor device (140) after passing through the housing. A detection element is sensitive to carbon dioxide.
   USE - Sampling apparatus for sampling exhaled breath from a patient who has a nasal cannula (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The tubular channel permits the flow of exhaled breath to go from the patient to an end tidal carbon dioxide (ETCO2) monitoring device, where the carbon dioxide in the breath exhaled by the patient is monitored for the safety of the patient. The indicator of the detection apparatus can help prevent accidental reuse, which could cause infections due to cross contamination. The detection apparatus can also prevent unintentional and unnecessary waste, which occurs due to operators unsure whether the apparatus is previously used, in which case, the apparatus is generally thrown away due to uncertainty. The apparatus can eliminate the situation where sampling line is taped to the facial skin of patient. The abutting end minimizes the chance for accidental extraction of the sampling tube attachment from the mask or port by restricting movement through abutting surfaces.

   RICHARTS F
AE RICHARTS F (RICH-Individual)
   SCHLITT R (SCHL-Individual)
GA 2022B58434
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves cooling the exhaust gas                from the furnace (1) in a cooling section that                includes a recuperator (6) for preheating the                supplied gas mixture of carbon dioxide and oxygen.                The waste heat boiler (2) is made to extract usable                heat and cooling stage with cooling of the exhaust                gas well below the water vapor dew point of the                water vapor included in the exhaust gas and from                which the largely water vapor-free exhaust gas from                the furnace is recirculated with an increased                temperature in the recuperator and with the added                oxygen required for combustion is supplied.
   USE - Method for obtaining energy and raw materials                from biomass.
   ADVANTAGE - The high-quality biogenic carbon dioxide is                produced efficiently. By using a recuperator to                preheat the recirculated gas, the efficiency of the                combustion is improved and an efficient generation                of electricity is realized.

   2Waste heat boiler

   4Water vapor

PT P
PN KR2022069491-A; KR2430993-B1
TI Metal coating catalyst useful for biogas            conversion and syngas production in electric field,            comprises nickel-alumina composite catalyst coated on            metal foam.
AU WOOK K
   YANG J
   PARK J C
   OH K
   LEEHAKKEUN
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 202274009M
AB    NOVELTY - Metal coating catalyst comprises a metal foam                and a nickel-alumina composite catalyst coated on                the metal foam.
   USE - The metal coating catalyst useful for biogas                conversion and syngas production (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The metal coating catalyst: is economical; has                mass transfer properties, reduced catalyst usage;                and can convert carbon dioxide without carbon                dioxide separation process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) producing the metal coating catalyst                comprising preparing nickel salt-alumina hybrid                powder supported on a porous alumina support using                melt impregnation method, preparing highly                supported nickel-alumina catalyst powder by                performing hydrogen heat treatment on the nickel                salt-alumina hybrid powder, preparing a catalyst                suspension using the catalyst powder, coating the                catalyst suspension on the metal foam and                calcining; and
   (2) producing syngas, comprising preparing the                metal coating catalyst, preparing the catalyst in a                reactor, connecting a power source to impart an                electric field to the catalyst, and supplying                methane, carbon dioxide and water vapor into the                reactor to perform steam-added biogas conversion                reaction.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The schematic representation of flowchart                shows method for manufacturing the metal coating                catalyst (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nickel-alumina                  composite catalyst is a catalyst in which a                  nickel-containing compound is melt-impregnated                  into the pores of a gamma -phase porous alumina                  support prepared as a suspension and coated on                  the metal foam. The metal foam includes nickel,                  aluminum, iron, stainless steel, copper,                  nickel-chromium alloy, nickel-iron alloy,                  copper-nickel alloy, nickel-chromium-iron alloy,                  nickel-chromium-aluminum alloy and/or                  iron-chromium-aluminum alloy. The highly                  supported nickel-alumina catalyst powder includes                  25-35 wt.% nickel supported on alumina support.                  The particle size of nickel in highly supported                  nickel-alumina catalyst powder is 2.5-7.5 nm. The                  catalyst suspension includes catalyst powder,                  organic dispersing agent, inorganic dispersing                  agent and/or ethanol. Preferred Method: The                  syngas production method comprises connecting the                  power source to impart electric field to the                  catalyst at 150-250V. The biogas conversion                  reaction is carried out at 500-900 degrees C with                  a reaction pressure of 1-10 bar and the molar                  ratio of water vapor/methane is 0.2-1.
   EXAMPLE - 10 g gamma -alumina and 21.23 g nickel nitrate                hexahydrate were ground in a mortar. The mixture                was added into a polypropylene bottle and aged for                24 hours at 60 degrees C. The catalyst was                heat-treated by rotating at a rate of 11 rotations                per minute at 400 degrees C for 2 hours, then                calcined for 4 hours to obtain 30 wt.% catalyst                supported on alumina.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC E31-A01; J04-E04; J04-E11; N01-C03; N02-A01; N02-C01; N02-D01; N06-C01; N06-E01; N06-F; N07-J
IP B01J-019/08; B01J-021/04; B01J-023/755; B01J-035/04; B01J-037/00; B01J-037/02; B01J-037/08; C01B-003/34

TI Molten halide electrolyte useful for preparing carbon product or carbon monoxide, comprises molten mixture of auxiliary agent and lithium halide, where auxiliary agent comprises at least one metal phosphate, metal borate, metal silicate and metal vanadate.
AU DU K
   HU L
   DENG B
   WANG D
AE UNIV WUHAN (UYWU-C)
GA 202251226T
AB    NOVELTY - Molten halide electrolyte comprises at least one molten mixture of auxiliary agent and lithium halide, where the auxiliary agent is at least one of metal phosphate, metal borate, metal silicate and metal vanadate.
   USE - The molten halide electrolyte is used as a trapping agent and molten electrolyte of carbon dioxide, and selectively preparing carbon product or carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple technique, carbon dioxide capturing efficiency and high conversion efficiency, is suitable for atmospheres containing different concentrations of carbon dioxide, has low electrolysis energy consumption, and controls distribution ratio of carbon products and carbon monoxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing carbon product or carbon monoxide using molten halide electrolyte, using anode composed of at least one tin dioxide ceramic electrode, graphite electrode, platinized titanium noble metal coating electrode and metal-based oxide coating electrode of nickel, iron, copper, chromium, aluminum and alloys, and cathode composed of at least one graphite electrodes and electrodes of nickel, iron, copper, chromium, molybdenum, titanium, aluminum and the alloys.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molten mixture for constituting the molten halide electrolyte further comprises alkali metal or alkaline earth metal halide. The alkali metal or alkaline earth metal halide is at least one of sodium fluoride, potassium fluoride, calcium fluoride, barium fluoride, magnesium fluoride, sodium chloride, potassium chloride, calcium chloride, barium chloride and magnesium dichloride. The lithium halide is lithium fluoride and/or lithium chloride.

PN US2022054980-A1; EP3960280-A1; CN114087848-A; IN202114037323-A
TI Method for separating carbon monoxide isotope            compound, involves maintaining evaporation of liquid            present in rectification column by heating liquid by            heating unit provided in each rectification            section.
AU ALEKSEEV A
AE LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
   LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
   LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
GA 202227753P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves operating a rectification                column system comprising a set of rectification                sections arranged adjacent to one another in a                chain-like manner. Evaporation of liquid present in                the column is maintained by heating the liquid by                heating units provided in each rectification                section. The heating of one of the set of sections                is provided by a heating unit comprising a heat                pump cycle, where the heating at the other section                is provided using an electrical heater. The                liquefied working fluid of the pump cycle is                supplied to a condenser using a pump e.g. gas                pump.
   USE - Method for separating carbon dioxide isotope                compound from natural carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of effectively                separating the carbon monoxide isotope compound                from the natural carbon dioxide with high energy                efficiency and reduced energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for apparatus                for separating at least one CO isotope                compound.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic illustration                of the apparatus for separation of carbon monoxide                isotope compound.
   Seperation apparatus (100)
   Line (102)
   Rectification column system (110)
   Rectification section (112,114)
   Condenser throttle (122)
   Liquid nitrogen source (124)

TI Preparing phenylene main chain containing siloxane            polymer, comprises performing hydrosilation reaction of            phenylsilane monomer, organosilicon monomer and carbon            dioxide in presence of Lewis acid catalyst including            e.g. boron trichloride.
AU LIU X
   CUI D
AE CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
GA 2021A4474D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing phenylene main chain containing                siloxane polymer, comprises subjecting phenylsilane                monomer (I) and carbon dioxide as co-monomer to                hydrosilation reaction in the presence of a Lewis                acid catalyst to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing phenylene                main chain containing siloxane polymer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has mild reaction conditions and                easy adjustment of product structure; has no need                to separate by-product operations, and ensures                silylidyne bond end capping at both ends for easy                chain extension.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing phenylene main chain containing                siloxane polymer, comprises subjecting phenylsilane                monomer of formula (I) and carbon dioxide as                co-monomer to hydrosilation reaction in the                presence of a Lewis acid catalyst to obtain final                product.
   R1 = H, -CH=CH2 or (CH2) n1CH3;
   n1, n2 = 0-7;
   R2 = 6-30C aryl group;
   M = silicon, 1-10C alkyl groups or 6-20C aryl                groups; and
   R3, R5 = -CH3, -CH=CH2, -OCH(CH3)2, -(CH2)                n2CH3 or -(CH2)2(CF2)n2CF3.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                phenylene main chain containing siloxane polymer of                formula (III).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The hydrosilylation                  reaction is performed at 20-60 degrees C for                  20-60 hours. The hydrosilation reaction further                  includes copolymerization of organosilicon                  monomer of formula (R4-O-M(R5R3)-O-R4) (II). The                  preparation of phenylene main chain containing                  siloxane polymer comprises (a) reacting (I) with                  (II) in the presence of a Lewis acid catalyst to                  obtain pre-polymer, and (b) reacting the                  prepolymer with carbon dioxide as co-monomer to                  obtain final product.R4 = H, CH3, C2H5 or CH(CH3) 2.Preferred Components: The Lewis acid                  catalyst is boron trichloride, boron trifluoride,                  aluminum chloride, gallium trichloride, zinc                  chloride, tris(pentafluorophenyl)borane or                  bis(pentafluorophenyl)zinc. The molar ratio of                  (I) to Lewis acid catalyst is 100-10000:1. In                  step a), the molar feed ratio of (I) to (II) is                  more than 1; and the reaction is carried out                  under the protection of inert gas at 20-30                  degrees C for 1-10 hours.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R1 = methyl;
   R2 = phenyl;
   R3, R5 = -CH3, -CH=CH2, -(CH2)2CF3; and
   R4 = -CH3 or -C2H5.

PN CN112939809-A
TI Preparation of diethyl-1-cyano-3-methylbutylmalonate, involves reacting isovaleraldehyde and diethyl malonate in solvent to form 1,3-diethyl 2-(3-methylbutylidene)malonate, and reacting with sodium cyanide under action of carbon dioxide.
AU GAO D
   LI A
   SUN L
   ZHONG Z
   WU Z
   ZHANG J
AE JIANGSU PHARMSYN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202170774P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of diethyl-1-cyano-3-methylbutylmalonate (I) involves (1) reacting isovaleraldehyde (II) and diethyl malonate (III) in solvent to obtain 1,3-diethyl-2-(3-methylbutylidene)malonate (IV), and (2) adding compound (IV), sodium cyanide, water and solvent to closed kettle, reacting under action of carbon dioxide to obtain crude product, adding hexane, filtering to remove solid and recovering the solvent from organic layer.
   USE - Preparation of diethyl-1-cyano-3-methylbutylmalonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method is carried out using inexpensive raw material to produce product with high purity, high yield, and low generation of three wastes compared with existing method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of diethyl-1-cyano-3-methylbutylmalonate of formula (I) involves (1) reacting isovaleraldehyde of formula (II) and diethyl malonate of formula (III) in solvent to obtain 1,3-diethyl-2-(3-methylbutylidene)malonate of formula (IV), and (2) adding compound (IV), sodium cyanide, water and solvent to closed kettle, reacting under action of carbon dioxide to obtain crude product, adding hexane, filtering to remove solid and recovering the solvent from organic layer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The preparation involves adding compound (II), compound (III), dipropylamine and acetic acid as catalyst and solvent to reaction kettle, refluxing in oil bath, azeotropically separating water until the raw material is consumed and concentration of compound (III) to total amount of compound (II), compound (III) and compound (IV) is less than or equal to 4%, lowering temperature to 20-40 degrees C, washing with water and recovering the solvent in organic layer under reduced pressure to obtain compound (IV), adding compound (IV), water, solvent and sodium cyanide to closed kettle, raising the temperature to 30-150 degrees C, preferably 40-60 degrees C, introducing carbon dioxide, controlling the pressure in the kettle to be 0.1-10 atm, preferably 1-5 atm, and reacting until the concentration of compound (IV) to total amount of compound (IV) and compound (I) is less than 3%. Preferred Component: The solvent used in step (1) is one of cyclohexane, methylcyclohexane, hexane, and heptane. The solvent used in step (2) is one of dimethylformamide, ethanol, isopropanol, and dimethyl sulfoxide.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.)

PN CN112892522-A; CN112892522-B
TI Porous microspheres used for photocatalysis for            photocatalytic carbon dioxide conversion, comprise            tungsten oxide crystalline particles aggregate.
AU HU Y
   JIANG H
   LI C
   LI Y
   LEI J
   WAN X
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 202166867G
AB    NOVELTY - Porous microspheres comprise tungsten(VI)                oxide crystalline particles aggregate having                diameter of 10-30 nm, where the diameter of the                porous microspheres is 0.5-3 mu m, and the porous                microspheres comprise a hole having a pore diameter                of 0-50 nm.
   USE - The porous microspheres used for                photocatalysis for photocatalytic carbon dioxide                conversion (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The porous microspheres have a large specific                surface area, large light response range, high                visible light absorption rate, and high methane                selectivity and carbon dioxide conversion                rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for preparing porous microspheres                involving (a) spraying pyrolyzing slurry (A) to                obtain precursor, where the slurry (A) comprises                tungsten source, template material and solvent, the                precursor comprises tungsten(VI) oxide and template                material pyrolysate, in the precursor, the mass                ratio of the tungsten(VI) oxide to the pyrolyzed                product of the template material is (2-10):1, and                atomizing the spray pyrolysis to obtain the slurry                (A) to form an aerosol and pyrolyzing the aerosol                and (b) etching the template material pyrolysate in                the precursor; and
   (2) a Z-type photocatalyst comprising                photooxidation catalyst and photoreduction                catalyst, where the photooxidation catalyst is the                porous microspheres.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The pore size of                  porous microspheres is 10-40 nm, the pore volume                  of the porous microspheres is 0.2-0.5 cm3/g,                  preferably 0.2-0.3 cm3/g, the porous microspheres                  are tungsten (VI) oxide crystalline particle                  aggregate and the specific surface area of the                  porous microspheres is 20-200 M2G-1. In step (i),                  the mass ratio of tungsten (VI) oxide in the                  precursor and the pyrolyzed product of the                  template material is (2-4):1, the template                  material comprises polymethylmethacrylate,                  polystyrene or silicon dioxide nanoparticles,                  when the template material is silicon dioxide                  nanoparticles, the pyrolysis product of the                  template material is silicon dioxide, and the                  precursor comprises tungsten (VI) oxide and                  silicon dioxide. In step (i), the concentration                  of the tungsten source in the slurry (A) is                  0.01-1 mol/l, preferably 0.1 mol/l. In step (i),                  the size of the silicon dioxide nanoparticles is                  15-130 nm, preferably 108 nm. In step (ii), the                  concentration of the hydrofluoric acid aqueous                  solution is 1-10%, preferably 5%. In step (ii),                  the concentration of the precursor in the                  hydrofluoric acid aqueous solution is 5-50 g/l,                  preferably 15 g/l. The mass of iron                  phthalocyanines is 0.1-2 %mass, preferably                  0.6-0.8%, when the photoreduction catalyst is                  iron phthalocyanines based on the Z-type                  photocatalyst. Preferred Conditions: In step                  (ii), when the template material pyrolyzed                  product is silicon dioxide, the etching is                  performed with a hydrofluoric acid aqueous                  solution 0.5-12 hours, preferably 3-6 hours. In                  step (i), the frequency of the atomizing device                  is 1.7-2.4 MHz. In step (i), the carrier gas flow                  rate of the atomizing device is 9-10 l/minutes.                  In step (i), the temperature of the pyrolysis is                  600-1000 degrees C, preferably 600 degrees C.                  Preferred Device: In step (i), the atomizing                  device comprises ultrasonic sprayer having three                  vibrators. In step (i), the carrier gas of the                  atomizing device is air. In step (i), the                  pyrolysis equipment comprises tube furnace                  comprising quartz tube reactor. Preferred                  Property: The diameter of the quartz tube reactor                  is 900 mm, the length is 1400 mm, and the                  diameter of the constant temperature zone is 900                  mm. Preferred Method: In step (i), the spray                  pyrolyzing process further involves performing                  collection operation by collecting the powdered                  product on the filter paper through the airflow                  under the action of a vacuum pump. In step (ii),                  the step of separating and drying involves                  etching. In step (ii), the separation is                  performed by a centrifuge. The Z-type                  photocatalyst is prepared by mixing the                  dispersion of the porous microspheres with a                  concentration of 1-8 g/l and the iron                  phthalocyanines solution with a concentration of                  0.04-1 g/l, when the photoreduction catalyst is                  iron phthalocyanines, stirring, and drying, where                  the concentration of the dispersion of the porous                  microspheres is 6 g/l, and the concentration of                  the iron phthalocyanines solution is 0.048 g/l.                  Preferred Components: In step (i), the tungsten                  source comprises tungstate, metatungstate,                  paratungstate, ammonium metatungstate, ammonium                  paratungstate and/or ammonium tungstate. The                  dispersion liquid of the porous microspheres and                  solvent of the iron phthalocyanines solution                  comprises water and/or ethanol, where the drying                  equipment is an oven, the stirring temperature is                  50-80 degrees C, the drying temperature is 60                  degrees C and the stirring speed is 200-500 rpm,                  preferably 300 rpm, and the drying is overnight                  drying. The photoreduction catalyst comprises                  MPc, rare earth peptide cyanine complex and                  dicyanocyanamide, preferably iron                  phthalocyanines.M = iron, copper, cobalt, aluminum, nickel,                  calcium, sodium, magnesium and/or zinc.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the                  solvent comprises water and/or alcohol solvent.                  Preferred Method: In step (i), the preparation                  method of the slurry (A) involves mixing the                  aqueous solution of the tungsten source and the                  aqueous dispersion of the template                  material.

GA 202154027P
AB    NOVELTY - Flow electrolysis method of stably realizing high-efficiency electro-catalysis carbon dioxide reduction under large current condition comprises dissolving copper salt and iron salt in solvent, adding surfactant, stirring and mixing for 10-120 minutes to obtain reaction liquid, introducing argon gas or nitrogen into reaction liquid, dissolving sodium borohydride in solvent to obtain sodium borohydride solution, dropping sodium borohydride solution into reaction liquid, reacting to obtain primary product solution, separating primary product solution, washing with absolute ethyl alcohol or n-hexane, drying to obtain primary product, dispersing copper iron catalyst in solvent to obtain catalyst ink, spraying catalyst ink on gas diffusion layer by gas brush method, drying to obtain gas diffusion electrode, dispersing copper iron catalyst in solvent to obtain catalyst ink, spraying catalyst ink on gas diffusion layer by gas brush method and drying to obtain gas diffusion electrode.
   USE - The method is useful for flow electrolysis method of stably realizing high-efficiency electro-catalysis carbon dioxide reduction under large current condition.

PN AU2021100359-A4
TI Remediation of heavy metal-contaminated soil involves e.g. crushing heavy metal-contaminated soil, and separately introducing reversible surfactant-containing carbonate solution, chelating agent-containing solution and carbon dioxide.
AU LI Y
   LIU H
   TIAN S
   LIU X
   ZHAO Q
   NING P
   HUANG J
   HU X
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 2021420168
AB    NOVELTY - Remediation of heavy metal-contaminated soil involves (1) crushing the heavy metal-contaminated soil until a particle size is 50 mm or less, (2) adding a reversible surfactant into water and introducing carbon dioxide to obtain a reversible surfactant-containing carbonate solution, (3) separately introducing the reversible surfactant-containing carbonate solution obtained in step (2), a chelating agent-containing solution and carbon dioxide into a porous medium to obtain reversible surfactant foam, (4) blowing the reversible surfactant foam obtained in step (3) into the heavy metal-contaminated soil crushed in step (1) at a uniform speed to obtain pollution-free soil and heavy metal-containing foam, and (5) introducing air or an inert gas into the heavy metal-containing foam obtained in step (4), and standing, where the resulting lower solid is the reversible surfactant.
   USE - Remediation of heavy metal-contaminated soil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple remediation of heavy metal-contaminated soil having excellent surfactant recovery efficiency and recyclability, reduced cost, effectively separating the surfactant from the heavy metals, and promotes the application of remediation of contaminated soil with foam.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The porous medium in step (3) has a porosity of 3% or more and a particle size of 0.05-50 mm. The flow speed of the reversible surfactant foam in step (4) is 0.02-100 mL/minute. Preferred Composition: The flow speed ratio of reversible surfactant-containing carbonate solution in step (3), carbon dioxide, and the chelating agent-containing solution is 1:(0.02-0.5):(0.05-0.8).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reversible surfactant in step (2) is one or more of long-chain alkyl compound containing amidinate, guanyl and imidazoline functional groups. Preferred Composition: The reversible surfactant-containing carbonate solution in step (2) comprises 0.1-2 %mass reversible surfactant. The chelating agent-containing solution in step (3) comprises 1-5 %mass chelating agent containing one or more of ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid, diethylenetriaminepentaacetic acid, ethylenebistetraacetic acid, rhamnose, saponin, oxalic acid, citric acid, and salicylic acid.
DC J02 (Mixing and including dispersing (B01F).); P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).)
MC J02-A03C; P43-J
IP B09C-001/08; B01F-017/00; B09C-001/00

   YIN Y
AE SINOPEC NANJING CHEM RES INST CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 2021516489
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises active component of copper, nickel, platinum, palladium, ruthenium, gold, silver, or rhodium, where the promoter component is alkali metals, alkaline earth metals or rare earth element oxides, and the carrier component is silicon dioxide, aluminum oxide, zirconium dioxide, activated carbon, or molecular sieve.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for oxidation of cyclohexane by carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can simultaneously solve the problem of the utilization of cyclohexane by-product of the cyclohexanone production by the cyclohexene hydration method and the problem of carbon dioxide emission reduction; and has good application prospects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of the catalyst comprising (a) processing the carrier under water vapor conditions, immersing the carrier with the soluble salt solution of the co-catalyst component to obtain the precursor 1, (b) heat-treating impregnated precursor 1 in air, and then processing under the reducing atmosphere to obtain precursor 2, and (c) impregnating the precursor 2 with a soluble salt solution of the active component of the catalyst, and heat-treating impregnated precursor 2 in nitrogen and then treated in a reducing atmosphere to obtain the catalyst
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkali metals are sodium oxide and potassium oxide. The alkaline earth metals are magnesium oxide, calcium oxide, strontium oxide and barium oxide. The rare earth element oxides are lanthanum oxide and cerium oxide. The temperature is 80-100 degrees C, and the treatment time is 4-10 hours. In steps (a) and (c), the impregnation is excessive impregnation. The impregnation temperature is 25-90 degrees C, and the impregnation time is 2-10 hours. In step (b), the air treatment temperature is 200-600 degrees C, and the treatment time is 4-10 hours. In steps (b) and (c), the reducing atmosphere is a hydrogen-nitrogen mixed gas with a hydrogen volume concentration of 5-30%. The treatment temperature is 250-550 degrees C, and the treatment time is 2-10 hours. Preferred Components: The catalyst is shaped into a spherical shape of phi 3-5 mm. The molar ratio of carbon dioxide/cyclohexane in the raw material is 0.1-3.5. The hourly space velocity of cyclohexane liquid is 0.5-10/hour. The reaction temperature is 250-650 degrees C. The pressure is between 1-10 MPa. The product also includes benzene or cyclohexene, and methane, methanol, formic acid, or carbon monoxide.

PN CN112276111-A
TI High-layer thick low-temperature sintering method for selective laser sintering involves laying metal powder on piston of working cylinder, laying polymer powder on metal powder, and e.g. using carbon dioxide laser and for sintering.
AU WEN J
   LUO Q
   LI Z
   TANG J
AE HUNAN FARSOON HIGH-TECH CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 202113192Q
AB    NOVELTY - High-layer thick low-temperature sintering method for selective laser sintering involves (1) laying metal powder with a thickness of 0.01-0.05 mm on the piston of the working cylinder, laying polymer powder with a layer thickness of 0.3-2.0 mm on the metal powder, preheating the above two layers of powder to 10-150 degrees C lower than the melting point of the polymer powder, (2) using carbon dioxide laser to sinter the above two layers of powder, melting the polymer powder, using fiber laser for sintering, (3) repeating steps (1) and (2) until the workpiece is sintered.
   USE - High-layer thick low-temperature sintering method for selective laser sintering.
   ADVANTAGE - The method greatly accelerates the sintering speed and forming efficiency of the selective laser sintering process. The prepared workpiece has excellent performance, surface resistivity, and can be used as excellent antistatic material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for high-layer thick sintering equipment for selective laser sintering, which comprises carbon dioxide laser, fiber laser, metal powder supply system and polymer powder supply system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The metal powder is chosen from one or more of iron powder, copper powder, nickel powder, aluminum powder, cobalt powder, titanium powder and silver powder. Preferred Property: The average particle size of the metal powder is 1-50 mu m.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The polymer powder is chosen from one or more of polyamide powder, polyethylene powder, polyurethane powder, polypropylene powder, polystyrene powder, polybutylene terephthalate powder, polyphenylene sulfide powder and polyether ether ketone powder. Preferred Property: The average particle size of the polymer powder is 40-80 mu m.

   DING Z
   ZHAO M
   LIU R
   ZHAO T
   XU F
AE SHANDONG IRON & STEEL GROUP RIZHAO CO (JGJT-C)
GA 2020C2065L
AB    NOVELTY - Removing impurity in Ruhrstahl-Heraeus single-link molten steel under vacuum comprises (i) controlling the vacuum degree of the vacuum system at 6-8 KPa in the initial stage of the Ruhrstahl-Heraeus vacuumizing, (ii) weighing 50-80 kg dry high-calcium limestone to a vacuum hopper from the storage hopper, (iii) adding the high-calcium limestone in the weighed vacuum hopper to the vacuum groove through the alloy chute, (iv) observing the concentration of carbon dioxide in the waste gas analyzer to reach the level before the first batch of high calcium limestone, and (v) repeating the above steps for 3-4 times.
   USE - The method is useful for removing impurity of RH single-link molten steel under vacuum.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses simple raw material, has low cost, uses high calcium limestone in the high temperature molten steel fast decomposition, generates a large amount of carbon dioxide in the overflow process, promotes micro impurity collision growth, and absorbs the impurity on the surface of the bubble in the process of bubble overflowing.

PN CN111905817-A
TI Preparing high-efficiency photocatalytic material            porous coordination network-222-zinc comprises e.g.            weighing zinc chloride powder, adding into mixed            solution, transferring mixed solution into hydrothermal            reactor and cooling.
AU JIN J
   GONG W
   CHEN X
   ZHU F
AE UNIV HUIZHOU (UHZO-C)
GA 2020B39669
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-efficiency photocatalytic                material porous coordination network-222-zinc                comprises (i) weighing certain amount of zinc                chloride powder and adding into mixed solution,                ultrasonically dissolving at 40-60 degrees C for                5-15 minutes, then adding certain amount of pure                phase PCN-222 crystals, and stirring at room                temperature for 30-60 minutes to obtain a mixed                solution, (ii) transferring mixed solution into                hydrothermal reactor for reaction, and reacting at                140-160 degrees C for 10-14 hours, (iii) acidifying                the mixed liquid using acidic compounds in the                hydrothermal reactor to remove metal ions other                than zinc(II) ions, and (iv) cooling acidified                mixed solution to room temperature, centrifuging,                washing and drying the mixed solution at room                temperature to obtain PCN-222-Zn target                product.
   USE - The photocatalytic material is useful for                reducing carbon dioxide to formic acid and in                photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction reaction                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalytic product can reduce carbon                dioxide to formic acid, has selectivity of formic                acid of up to 98%, and has remarkable catalytic                effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the mixed                  solution includes dimethylformamide and                  2,6-lutidine, and volume ratio between                  2,6-lutidine and dimethylformamide is                  0.8-1.2:4.5-5.5. In step (i), molar concentration                  of the zinc chloride powder in the mixed solution                  is 0.007-0.020 mmol/ml; mass concentration of the                  pure phase PCN-222 crystal in the mixed solution                  is 3.6-4.8 mg/ml. In step (iv), the volume ratio                  between dimethylformamide and the hydrochloride                  in the mixed liquid is 15-25:2-4. The acidic                  compound includes hydrochloride. Preferred                  Method: The method further comprises a step of                  preparing pure phase PCN-222 crystals. During                  preparation of pure phase PCN-222 crystals, mass                  ratio between zirconium(IV) chloride powder and                  porphyrin complexing agent H2TCPP is 6-8:9-11;                  mass concentration of the porphyrin complexing                  agent H2TCPP in dimethylformamide is 0.5-1.5                  mg/ml; and volume ratio between dimethylformamide                  and formic acid is 3-4:0.5-1.5. The preparation                  step of pure phase PCN-222 crystal includes (i)                  weighing certain amount of zirconium(IV) chloride                  powder into dimethylformamide solution, and                  dissolving it ultrasonically at 40-60 degrees C                  for 20-40 minutes to obtain mixed solution, (ii)                  weighing porphyrin complexing agent H2TCPP and                  formic acid regulator in turn, adding into the                  mixed solution of step (i), ultrasonically                  dissolving at 40-60 degrees C for 20-40 minutes,                  then transferring to a hydrothermal reactor,                  reacting at 110-130 degrees C for 14-18 hours,                  cooling to room temperature, and centrifuging to                  collect the product, and (iii) washing and drying                  the product collected by centrifugation in step                  (ii) to obtain pure phase PCN-222 crystal.

The polyfunctional (meth) acrylate compound (A) comprises a (meth)acrylate compound containing a urethane bond. Preferred Property: The organic solvent has an solubility parameter (SP) value of 7.5-11 (cal/cm3)(1/2).
