PN CN111569930-A
TI Preparing zinc monoatomic catalyst by mixing fruit            biomass and water, carrying out hydrothermal reaction,            soaking hydrogel in nitrogen-containing liquid biomass,            adding powder to zinc chloride solution, annealing,            washing, and grinding.
AU ZHOU M
   WANG N
AE UNIV NORTHEAST NORMAL (UYNO-C)
GA 2020848803
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing zinc monoatomic catalyst,                involves (a) mixing fruit biomass and water,                carrying out hydrothermal reaction, washing the                product, water-bathing, and freeze-drying to obtain                a biomass-derived hydrogel, and (b) soaking the                hydrogel in nitrogen-containing liquid biomass,                drying, grinding to obtain a powder (A), adding the                powder (A) to zinc chloride solution, stirring,                drying the mixture, stirring, annealing in a                nitrogen atmosphere, cooling the product to room                temperature, treating the product in an acid                solution, washing with water to neutral, drying and                grinding to obtain a powder (B) to obtain the                catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing zinc                monoatomic catalyst used for preparing carbon                dioxide reduction reaction catalyst and/or oxygen                reduction reaction catalyst, reducing carbon                dioxide and/or oxygen and carbon dioxide reduction                reaction and/or oxygen reduction reaction (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared catalyst has excellent                electrocatalytic activity and high carbon monoxide                faradaic efficiency under relatively low                overpotential with high half-wave potential and                high starting potential.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The hydrothermal reaction                  is carried out in a stainless steel autoclave at                  a temperature of 180 plus minus 5 degrees C for                  10-12 hours. The step (a) involves washing the                  product with a mixture of water and ethanol in a                  volume ratio of 1:1, water-bathing using a water                  bath at 60 plus minus 5 degrees C for 45-50                  hours, and freeze-drying at -44 degrees C for                  24-48 hours. The step (b) involves soaking the                  hydrogel in nitrogen-containing liquid biomass                  for 0.5-1 hour and drying at 60 degrees C for 6-8                  hours. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of                  the powder (A) to zinc chloride is 1:(15-20). The                  mixture is dried at 100 degrees C for 30-35                  hours, annealed at 900 plus minus 10 degrees C                  for 2 hours, treated with acid solution for 4-5                  hours, and dried at 60 degrees C for 10-12 hours.                  Preferred Components: The acid solution is a                  hydrochloric acid solution with a concentration                  of 2 M. Preferred Conditions: In step (b), during                  the entire heating and cooling process, the rate                  is 5 degrees C/minute.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The fruit biomass                  comprises apple, pear or watermelon, and is                  massive. The water is distilled water. The                  nitrogen-containing liquid biomass comprises egg                  white, cow milk or soy milk. Preferred                  Composition: The mass ratio of the fruit biomass                  to water is 1:(1.5-2.5).

AU YUE X
   HAN D
   YAO T
   LI J
   ZHAO J
   YUE Y

   CAS LIQUID SUNSHINE SUZHOU HYDROGEN TECH (CASL-Non-standard)
GA 2020200988
AB    NOVELTY - A methanol steam reforming and hydrogen separation integrated device comprises reaction chamber arranged outside a heating chamber (3) which provides reaction temperature. The reaction chamber a comprises air inlet and air outlet and is filled with catalyst filler. Methanol water vapor is introduced into the reaction chamber from the air inlet for catalytic reaction to generate a mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide. A hydrogen absorption pipe (1) is inserted into the catalyst filler of the reaction chamber and separates generated mixed gas of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide. Pure hydrogen is collected from the output of the hydrogen absorption pipe and residual carbon dioxide mixed gas is output from the outlet.
   USE - Methanol steam reforming and hydrogen separation integrated device.
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces volume of the entire hydrogen production and separates pure hydrogen which can be used as fuel.

Preferred Method: In step (i) the process of                  preparing steam comprises (a) taking 7-9 pts. wt.                  southernwood, 6-7 pts. wt. rose, 10-12 pts. wt.                  small Chrysanthemum morifolium, 0.5-0.6 pts. wt.                  N-methyl pyrrolidone, 0.2-0.4 pts. wt.                  4-methylmorpholine N-oxide and 300-380 pts. wt.                  anhydrous ethanol, (b) adding the southernwood,                  rose and small Chrysanthemum morifoliuminto an                  enzyme solution for soaking, maintaining the                  temperature of the enzyme solution at                  38-42&#176; C for 30-40 minutes, filtering                  out the southernwood, rose and small                  Chrysanthemum morifolium, where the enzyme                  solution comprises 1-2 pts. wt. pectinase,                  0.7-0.9 pts. wt. cellulose, 0.5-0.7 pts. wt.                  hemicellulase and 60-70 pts. wt. pure water and                  (c) heating the filtered southernwood, rose and                  small Chrysanthemum morifolium, N-methyl                  pyrrolidone, 4-methylmorpholine N-oxide and                  anhydrous ethanol, allowing it to boil using                  medium heat and observing for the generation of                  steam. In step (iii) the supercritical carbon                  dioxide treatment is carried out at                  120-124&#176; C under a working pressure of                  20-24 MPa. The microwave treatment in step (iv)                  is carried out under a pressure of 500-600                  W.

The hardening of the sand-mixing core is hardened by CO2 blowing, and the ambient temperature is maintained at 25-30 degrees C during the blowing process, the humidity is preferably less than or equal to 40% RH. The hardening process is blowing CO2 for 15-30 seconds, the flow rate is greater than or equal to 5 L / minute. The flow rate is particularly preferably 5-15 L/minute. The sample prepared by the material has high tensile strength, the instantaneous strength is greater than or equal to 0.15 Mega pascals (MPa), the tensile strength of the sample is greater than or equal to 1.2 MPa for 24 hours, and the residual strength at high temperature is less than or equal to 0.05 MPa. The water glass is ordinary water glass or modified water glass.

AU VAN ECK P A C
AE VAN ECK BEHEER BV P (VECK-Non-standard)
GA 201900033V
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing liquid medium, which includes carbon dioxide, nitrogen, and/or mixture. The liquid medium is converted to gaseous medium by evaporating the liquid medium. The gaseous medium is heated above 0 degree Celsius. Fire is extinguished with the heated gaseous medium. PT P
PN CN108658072-A
TI Preparation of flax waste activated carbon for adsorbing e.g. heavy metal, involves placing flax material in furnace, purging with nitrogen, heating, cooling, inletting nitrogen, heating, stopping nitrogen, and introducing carbon dioxide.
AU INVENTOR U
AE QIDONG XIANGRUI CONSTR CO LTD (QIDO-Non-standard)
GA 2018836524
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of flax waste activated carbon involves placing 20-30 pts. wt. flax material in a tubular furnace, purging the tubular furnace with nitrogen at a flow rate of 85-95 mL/minute, increasing the temperature of the furnace at a rate of 8 degrees C/minute to 470-490 degrees C for 70-80 minutes, and cooling to room temperature to obtain carbonized material, placing the carbonized material in the tubular furnace, inletting nitrogen to the tubular furnace, increasing the temperature to 810-830 degrees C, stopping the flow of nitrogen, introducing carbon dioxide at a rate of 180-190 mL/minute, reacting for 45-55 minutes, stopping the flow of carbon dioxide, introducing nitrogen, naturally cooling to room temperature, washing with deionized water, and drying at 50-60 degrees C.
   USE - Preparation method of flax waste activated carbon used for adsorbing heavy metal and organic substances.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of flax waste activated carbon with excellent adsorbing ability of heavy metal and organic substances, by simple and easy process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The flow rate of the nitrogen is 90 mL/minute. The temperature of the furnace is increased at a rate of 8 degrees C/minute to 480 degrees C for 75 minutes. The temperature is increased to 820 degrees C after inletting nitrogen to the tubular furnace. The carbon dioxide is introduced at a rate of 185 mL/minute and the reaction time is 50 minutes. The drying process is performed at 55 degrees C.

PN CN107858752-A
TI Crystalline silicon etching solution comprises sodium hydroxide, isopropanol, anhydrous ethanol, liquid carbon dioxide, sodium hypochlorite, alkali catalyst, nonionic surfactant and deionized water.
AU LIN B
   XIE Y
   TANG L
   CHENG Y
   GUAN Z
AE TONGWEI SOLAR ENERGY ANHUI CO LTD (TWCO-C)
GA 201826396K
AB    NOVELTY - A crystalline silicon etching solution comprises 30-50 %mass sodium hydroxide, 0.2-0.5 %mass isopropanol, 0.4-0.8 %mass anhydrous ethanol, 5-10 %mass liquid carbon dioxide, 5-10 %mass sodium hypochlorite, 2-5 %mass alkali catalyst, 3-8 %mass nonionic surfactant and balance amount of deionized water.
   USE - Crystalline silicon etching solution.
   ADVANTAGE - The crystalline silicon etching solution can be prepared with reduced amount of organic solvent including isopropanol and absolute ethanol, and can greatly reduce the operating environment pollution and wastewater treatment cost caused by solvent volatilization, and reduce chemical oxygen demand.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the crystalline silicon etching solution, which involves: (1) adding deionized water to a reaction kettle, then dissolving sodium hydroxide in deionized water, and sequentially adding isopropanol and absolute ethanol; (2) pouring the mixed solution obtained in step (1) into a storage tank, and simultaneously adding the liquid carbon dioxide and the alkali catalyst; and (3) pressurizing and filtering the storage tank in step (2), then adding sodium hypochlorite and nonionic surfactant to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nonionic surfactant is composed of sodium citrate, fructose and lignosulfonate in a mass ratio of 1:1:3.

PN CN115057474-A
TI Preparing ammonium metavanadate process medium            circulation by using calcium vanadate, involves mixing            calcium vanadate and ammonium acetate solution,            carrying out transfer reaction to obtain reaction            liquid, continuously introducing carbon dioxide            leaching carbonization reaction.
AU LV Y
   LIU B
   WANG S
   DU H
AE INST PROCESS ENG CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAGC-C)
GA 2022C20318
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ammonium metavanadate process medium                circulation by using calcium vanadate, involves                mixing calcium vanadate and ammonium acetate                solution, under the heating condition, carrying out                transfer reaction to obtain reaction liquid,                continuously introducing carbon dioxide leaching                carbonization reaction, solid-liquid separation to                obtain calcium carbonate and liquid phase product,                and cooling and crystallizing the liquid phase                product obtained, obtaining ammonium metavanadate                crystal and crystal mother liquor, crystallization                mother solution obtained can be reused in the                solution reaction.
   USE - Method for preparing ammonium metavanadate                process medium circulation by using calcium                vanadate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient preparation of                ammonium metavanadate process medium with high                conversion rate, high purity and high yield, the                ammonium salt medium can be recycled, is clean and                environment-friendly, and has no waste water to                generate, controls the size of the gas, enhances                the solubility of carbon dioxide in the reaction                system, and improves the binding rate of calcium                ion and carbonate ion, is carried out at normal                pressure and low temperature, is easy for                industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The calcium vanadate                  comprises calcium vanadate. The solid-to-liquid                  ratio of the calcium vanadate to the ammonium                  acetate solution is 1:(5-20). The concentration                  of ammonium (NH4+) in the ammonium acetate                  solution is 0.1-15 mol/L, preferably 2-10 mol/L,                  preferably the ammonium acetate solution                  (CH3COO-)concentration is 0.1-15 mol/L, the                  concentration of vanadate ion (VO3-) in the                  ammonium acetate solution is 0 -0.2mol/L, the                  ammonium acetate solution in NH4+/CH3COO-mol                  ratio is 0.5-1. The flow rate of the carbon                  dioxide is 0.2-1 L/min. The end point temperature                  of the cooling crystallization is                  10-40&#8451;. The crystallization mother                  liquor contains NH4+, CH3COO-, and VO3-.Preferred Method: The method for preparing                  ammonium metavanadate process medium circulation                  by using calcium vanadate involves mixing calcium                  vanadate and ammonium acetate solution with                  solid-to-liquid ratio of 1:(5-20) at normal                  pressure at 60-100&#8451; for transferring                  and reacting for 10-120min to obtain the reaction                  liquid then at normal pressure, continuously                  introducing carbon dioxide with flow rate of                  0.2-1 L/min for 15-90 min at 60-100&#8451;,                  separating solid and liquid to obtain calcium                  carbonate and liquid phase product, the                  concentration of NH4+in the ammonium acetate                  solution is 0.1-15 mol/L, the concentration of                  CH3COO-is 0.1-15 mol/L, the concentration of VO3-                  is 0-0.2mol/L, the mol ratio of NH4+/CH3COO-in                  the solution is 0.5-1; cooling and crystallizing                  the liquid phase product obtained at                  10-40&#8451;, obtaining ammonium metavanadate                  crystal and crystal mother liquid containing                  NH4+, CH3COO-and VO3-, the crystallization mother                  liquid can be reused in the solution                  reaction.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The alkali metal or alkaline earth metal carbonate is lithium carbonate, sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, calcium carbonate, barium carbonate or/and magnesium carbonate. The alkali metal or alkaline earth metal hydroxide salt is a mixture of lithium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, calcium hydroxide, barium hydroxide or/and magnesium hydroxide. The additive is the mixture of sodium borate and sodium metaborate, the mixture of sodium tetraborate and sodium borate, the mixture of sodium tetraborate and sodium metaborate, the mixture of sodium vanadium oxide and sodium orthovanadate, the mixture of sodium metasilicate and sodium orthosilicate, or the mixture of trisodium phosphate and sodium triphosphate.

AE SINOPEC BEIJING RES INST CHEM IND (SNPC-C)

GA 202202880X
AB    NOVELTY - Supported catalyst comprises carrier and active component supported on carrier. The carrier is halloysite. The active component includes alkali metal element, manganese element and tungsten element.
   USE - Supported catalyst used for producing carbon dioxide and hydrocarbon from methane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The supported catalyst is prepared by simple preparation process and cost-effective, and has high reaction conversion rate and selectivity when used in methane oxidative coupling reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1)a method for preparing supported catalyst, which involves: (a)loading precursor of the active component on the carrier;(b)adding orderly loaded material;(c)drying;(d)calcining to obtain the supported catalyst, where, the carrier is halloysite, and the active component includes alkali metal element, manganese element and tungsten element; and
   (2)a method for producing carbon dioxide and hydrocarbon from methane, which involves: (a)contacting methane with the supported catalyst in the presence of oxygen and under the condition of an oxidative coupling reaction of methane;(b)preparing supported catalyst;(c)contacting methane with the obtained supported catalyst in the presence of oxygen and under the conditions of an oxidative coupling reaction of methane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkali metal element includes sodium element and potassium element. The supported catalyst further includes doping element supported on the carrier. The doping element is selected from lithium, lanthanum, cesium, cerium, yttrium, barium, titanium, ruthenium, rhodium, nickel, strontium, silver and platinum. Preferred Compositions: The supported catalyst comprises 0.05-15 pts. wt. alkali metal element, preferably 1-13 pts. wt. alkali metal element, 0.5-30 pts. wt. manganese, preferably, 1-26 pts. wt. manganese, 0.5-55 pts. wt. tungsten element, preferably, 4.5-51 pts. wt. tungsten element, 0.01-5 pts. wt. doping element. Preferred Conditions: The halloysite is a hollow nanotube structure, where the halloysite hollow nanotube has an inner diameter of 10-20 nanometer, an outer diameter of 40-70 nanometer, and a length of 200-1000 nanometer. The doping element exists in an oxidized state. The preparation of supported catalyst involves loading precursor of the active component on the carrier by dipping at temperature is 20-40degrees Celsius and the time is 1-5 hours, adding orderly loaded material, drying at temperature of 80-180degrees Celsius and a time of 10-24 hours, heating the dried mass to the calcination temperature at a rate of 1-10degrees Celsius/minutes, calcining at temperature of 500-950degrees Celsius, and the time is 2-10 hours, and loading a doping element on the carrier to obtain the supported catalyst. The molar ratio of the consumption of methane and oxygen is 2-10:1, preferably, 3-8:1. The contact temperature is 550-850degrees Celsius. The space velocity of methane is 5,000-100,000 mL/(g.hours).

PN CN113548700-A
TI Preparing iron-nickel-nitrogen-carbon nanomaterial used for reducing greenhouse gas carbon dioxide, involves adding potassium ferrate, nickel nitrate, urea and sucrose into deionized water, stirring strongly, and ball milling.
AU JIANG K
   LIU N
   WANG H
   CHEN C
   LIU H
   MA Z
   HAN X
AE UNIV HENAN NORMAL (UYNR-C)
GA 2021C4173C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing iron-nickel-nitrogen-carbon nanomaterial involves adding potassium ferrate, nickel nitrate, urea and sucrose into deionized water at a mass ratio of 1:1.5:3:10-20, stirring strongly for 10 hours, ball milling into powder sample after vacuum drying, placing the obtained powder sample in a porcelain boat, using argon as a protective gas in the tube furnace, carbonizing at 700-1200degrees Celsius for 2 hours, cooling the tube furnace to room temperature naturally to obtain a black powdery sample, washing with hydrochloric acid 3 times, washing with deionized water 3 times, freezing, and drying to obtain the target product iron-nickel-nitrogen-carbon nanomaterial.
   USE - Method for preparing iron-nickel-nitrogen-carbon nanomaterial used for reducing greenhouse gas carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, cost-effective, and the requirement for preparation equipment are not high. The prepared iron-nickel-nitrogen-carbon nanomaterial has large specific surface area, stable structure, high catalytic activity, large operating potential window, and the method is conducive to -scale production, and used in reducing the greenhouse gas carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of potassium ferrate, nickel nitrate, urea and sucrose is 1:1.5:3:15. The carbonization temperature is 950degrees Celsius.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of hollow nano-bowl-shaped structure comprises plating surface of highly ordered polystyrene ball array prepared by self-assembly method with metal particles by magnetron sputtering; subjecting to ion source etching; performing second magnetron sputtering on the sample and growing a metal film on the surface of the sample; pasting and fixing the sample completed by sputtering on the surface of the tape to obtain a film; soaking the film with a solvent; and third magnetron sputtering and growing metal particles on the surface of the film, where metal particles include gold particles or copper particles.
   USE - The method is used for preparing hollow nano-bowl-shaped structure for nanomaterial in catalysis of carbon dioxide (claimed).

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The soaking solvent is tetrahydrofuran.

TI Process for production of sponge iron, involves            providing released hydrogen as component portion of            reducing gas to direct reduction shaft, and reducing            iron ore in direct reduction shaft using reducing gas            to produce sponge iron.
AU GROENKVIST S
   ANDERSSON J
   GRONKVIST S
AE HYBRIT DEV AB (HYBR-Non-standard)
GA 2021A74267
AB    NOVELTY - The process involves producing electrolytic                hydrogen and oxygen by electrolysis of water. A                methanol is produced by reacting the electrolytic                hydrogen with carbon dioxide, and the methanol is                stored (203). The methanol is reformed using water                and/or oxygen to provide carbon dioxide and                released hydrogen. The released hydrogen is                provided as a component portion of a reducing gas                to a direct reduction shaft (211). The iron ore                (207) in the direct reduction shaft is reduced                using the reducing gas to produce a sponge iron                (209).
   USE - Process for production of sponge iron for                producing steel used as engineering and                construction material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce sponge iron that                is widely applicable, does not require the use of                fossil fuels, may be performed with relatively low                carbon dioxide emissions, and is relatively cost                and energy efficient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                system for the production of sponge iron.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic diagram of                the system for the production of sponge                iron.
   Methanol store (203)
   Iron ore (207)
   Sponge iron (209)
   Direct reduction shaft (211)
   Electrolysis unit (219)

PT P
PN CN113278004-A; CN113278004-B
TI Preparation of cyclic carbonate involves using carbon dioxide, olefin and aldehyde as raw materials, performing condensation reaction in presence of functionalized composite nano catalyst in reactor or fluidized bed.
AU MU X
   ZHANG X
   WANG W
   YIN B
   SHI J
   LIU T
AE YUANCHUANG HEXIN BEIJING NEW MATERIAL (YUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021995355
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of cyclic carbonate (I) involves using carbon dioxide, olefins (A) and aldehyde (B) as raw materials, reacting in presence of a functionalized composite nano catalyst in a reactor or fluidized bed, and performing condensation reaction at temperature of 50-250 degrees C and pressure of 1-15 MPa for 1-48 hours.
   USE - Preparation of cyclic carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses inexpensive and readily available raw materials, uses non-corrosive catalysts, and has simple process operation without other reaction steps, high efficiency and simple operation, and is economic, environmentally-friendly and suitable for large-scale industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of cyclic carbonate of formula (I) involves using carbon dioxide, olefins of formula (A) and aldehyde of formula (B) as raw materials, reacting in presence of a functionalized composite nano catalyst in a reactor or fluidized bed, and performing condensation reaction at temperature of 50-250 degrees C and pressure of 1-15 MPa for 1-48 hours.
   R1-R3 = H, 1-8C alkyl, 6-18C aryl or 5-18C heteroaryl containing 1-3 heteroatoms e.g. N, O, or S.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carrier in the functionalized composite nano catalyst is chosen from hydrogen molecular sieve, silicon dioxide-aluminum oxide, silicon dioxide-titanium dioxide, silicon dioxide-zirconium dioxide, silicon dioxide-tungsten trioxide, silicon dioxide-molybdenum trioxide, silicon dioxide-stannic oxide, titanium dioxide-zirconium dioxide, titanium dioxide-zirconium dioxide, titanium dioxide-aluminum oxide, H-ZSM-5 and SBA-15. The active component in functionalized composite nano catalyst is the elementary substance, oxide or complex of one or more of zinc, nickel, aluminum, palladium, rhodium, ruthenium, tin, copper, cobalt, manganese and iron. The carrier in the functionalized composite nano catalyst is preferably chosen from titanium dioxide-zirconium dioxide, H-ZSM-5 and SBA-15. The active component is preferably oxide or complex of one or more of nickel, cobalt, copper and iron. Oxide or complex. The complex of the active component is one or more of porphyrin, bis-butanedione oxime, diacetylacetone and Schiff base and the complex formed with the active component metal, preferably Salen-metal complex. Preferred Composition: The active component is added in an amount of 0.5-20 wt.%, preferably 3-10 wt.% with respect to the weight of catalyst composition.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The compound (B) is added in an amount of 20-100 %mass. The molar ratio of the compound (A) to the compound (B) is 1:1-10:1, preferably 5:1-7:1. The molar ratio of carbon dioxide to compound (A) is 3:1-1000:1. The amount of the catalyst is 0.5-20% based on the weight of the fatty aldehyde. Based on the weight of the compound (B), the amount of tetrabutylammonium bromide is 1-20%. The amount of toluene is 0.1-50 times the weight of the compound (B). Preferred Condition: The reaction is performed at 50-180 degrees C, preferably 70-130 degrees C, and pressure of 2-15 MPa for 5-18 hours. The preparation of the cyclic carbonate can be carried out in the presence of water, and the content of water in the reaction system is 0-65% of the total mass of the reaction system. Preferred Process: The functionalized composite nano catalyst is prepared by (1) pre-treating carrier, (2) configuring the metal salt or complex of the active component into a precursor solution, loading the precursor of the metal salt or complex of the active component on the carrier by methods such as amino grafting, sol-gel or impregnation loading-ultrasonic dispersion, (3) calcining the carrier loaded with the precursor of the metal salt or complex of the active component obtained in step (2) in an inert atmosphere at 100-300 degrees C. The metal salt as the active component is preferably a halide, preferably chloride salt. Preferred Components: The compound (B) is preferably chosen from formaldehyde, acetaldehyde, propionaldehyde, isopropanal, n-butyraldehyde, isobutyraldehyde, tert-butyraldehyde, and these aldehyde dimers, trimers and oligomers with a degree of polymerization of 3-10, preferably chosen from formaldehyde, acetaldehyde, trioxane, paraformaldehyde and formaldehyde aqueous solutions, acetaldehyde aqueous solutions, trioxane aqueous solutions, trioxane dioxane solutions, and trioxane tetrahydrofuran. The compound (A) is preferably one or more of double bond structures, preferably ethylene, n-propylene, isopropylene, n-butene, isobutylene, n-pentene, isopentene, n-hexene and isohexene, more preferably n-propylene, iso-propylene, n-butene and isobutylene, or butadiene, pentadiene, hexadiene, octadiene and heptadiene. A reaction additive is also added to the preparation method of the cyclic carbonate. The reaction additive is chosen from tetrabutylammonium bromide, tetrabutylammonium chloride and toluene. The Schiff base of the complex precursor of the active component is a Salen-metal complex, where the Salen ligand is chosen from condensation of salicylaldehyde, tert-butyl salicylaldehyde and ethylenediamine, o-phenylenediamine, cyclohexanediamine and amines with ethylenediamine substructures with Schiff base of nitrogen and oxygen structure.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R 1-R3 = H, methyl, ethyl, n-propyl, isopropyl, n-butyl, isobutyl, tert-butyl, phenyl, methylphenyl, ethyl phenyl, n-propyl phenyl, isopropyl phenyl, n-butyl phenyl, isobutyl phenyl, tert-butyl phenyl, naphthyl, anthryl, phenanthryl, or 5-18C heteroaryl containing 1-3 heteroatoms e.g. N, O or S.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC E06-H; E07-A04; E11-A01; E11-F02B; E11-F03; E11-W; J04-E04; N01-C02; N02-B01; N02-C01; N02-D01; N02-E01; N02-E02; N02-F03; N03-E; N03-F01; N03-G03; N05-B; N06-C09; N06-E01; N06-F; N07-D02B; N07-D03; N07-D04; N07-F07B; N07-K01

PN CN112723997-A
TI Extracting and purifying bakuchiol from fructus psoraleae comprises drying and crushing fructus psoraleae, extracting by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction technology, dissolving in solvent, and purifying crude extract of bakuchiol.
AU FU S
   ZHANG L
   XU M
   CAO H
AE CHENGDU AKTIN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2021472831
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting and purifying bakuchiol from fructus psoraleae comprises (i) drying and crushing fructus psoraleae to 40-100 mesh, adding into an extraction kettle, and extracting by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction technology to obtain an oily liquid containing bakuchiol, where optionally, the supercritical carbon dioxide further comprises ethyl acetate as cosolvent; (ii) dissolving the oily liquid obtained with 6-12 times the volume of a low-polarity organic solvent, filtering out insolubles, vacuum extracting and concentrating the filtrate to obtain a crude bakuchiol extract; (iii) purifying the crude extract of bakuchiol by column chromatography, eluting with a mixed solvent, collecting the eluate containing bakuchiol, and vacuum extracting the eluate to recover the solvent to obtain oily bakuchiol; and (iv) optionally, decolorizing the obtained oily bakuchiol to obtain a bakuchiol product.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting and purifying bakuchiol from fructus psoraleae.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is efficient and environment-friendly, has high sample yield and high purity, is simple, safe, good in controllability, economical, and short in production cycle, and is suitable for large-scale industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the low-polarity organic solvent used for dissolution is petroleum ether, n-hexane, and/or cyclohexane, preferably petroleum ether, and the amount of petroleum ether is 8-10 times the volume of the oily liquid. In the step (iii), the filler in the chromatography column is silica gel or alumina, preferably silica gel, and the mixed solvent used for elution is a mixed solvent of solvent A and solvent B, where the solvent A is petroleum ether, n-hexane or cyclohexane, and the solvent B is ethyl acetate or acetone, preferably mixed solvent is composed of petroleum ether and ethyl acetate in volume ratio of 30:1-5:1. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the supercritical extraction condition comprises: drying and crushing fructus psoraleae to 60-80 mesh, extraction at 20-60 degrees C,preferably 45-55 degrees C and 10-40 MPa, preferably 20-30 MPa with extraction solvent flow rate of 20-60 l/hour, preferably 35-45 l/hour for 0.5-3 hours, preferably 1-2 hours where the extraction solvent is supercritical carbon dioxide, the temperature of analyzing kettle A is 20-80 degrees C, preferably 40-60 degrees C and the pressure of analyzing kettle A is 5-15 MPa, preferably 8-12 MPa, the temperature of analyzing kettle B is 15-50 degrees C, preferably 20-40 degrees C, and the pressure of analyzing kettle B is 2-10 MPa, preferably 4-6 MPa, and the weight ratio of carbon dioxide and ethyl acetate is 10-30:1, preferably 15-25:1. In the step (iv), the activated carbon is used for decolorization.

PN CN112183665-A
TI Convolutional neural network based carbon dioxide welding molten pool image identification method, involves collecting molten pool image, and outputting identification result of molten pool image when precision requirement is satisfied.
AU SUN Y
   YANG C
   HE J
   LI W
AE UNIV GUANGXI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYGN-C)
GA 202107360U
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves collecting a molten pool image through an infrared image sensor. The molten pool image is divided according to a threshold value. Contrast and brightness of the molten pool image are adjusted after segmentation processing. A data set is constructed. The data set is marked and divided as a training set and a test set. The training set is trained by using a convolutional neural network. A parameter of the convolutional neural network is obtained. An identification result of the molten pool image is output when precision requirement is satisfied. A rectangular pulse waveform with same current change frequency is obtained.

   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple operation, mild reaction conditions, high yield, low impurity content, easy purification, and reduced production cost; and is suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing pimavanserin intermediate of formula (I) comprises reacting 4-isobutoxy-benzylamine of formula (II) with halogenated compound of formula (R-X) (III) and carbon dioxide in organic solvent in the presence of a base to obtain final product.
   R = 1-4C alkyl, benzyl or substituted benzyl, preferably methyl, ethyl or propyl; and
   X = Cl, Br or I.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing pimavanserin, comprising (i) preparing (I), and (ii) reacting (4-fluoro-benzyl)-(1-methyl-piperidine-14-yl)-amine (IV) with (I) in organic solvent in the presence of a base to form final product; and
   (2) preparing pimavanserin hemi-tartrate, comprising salting pimavanserin with tartaric acid to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio between (III) and (II) is 1-3:1, the base is organic base or inorganic carbonate, the organic base is triethylamine, N,N-diisopropylethylamine, pyridine, 4-dimethylaminopyridine and/or 1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene, the inorganic carbonate base is lithium carbonate, sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate and/or cesium carbonate, the organic solvent is N,N-dimethylformamide, acetonitrile, tetrahydrofuran and/or dioxane. The molar ratio between base and (II) is 1-10:1, the temperature of the reaction is 0-50 degrees C, and the reaction time is 1-10 hours. The molar ratio between base and (II) is 2-3:1, the reaction temperature is 15-30 degrees C, and the reaction time is 1-5 hours. In step (ii), the molar ratio between (IV) and (I) is 1-3:1, the base is sodium methoxide, sodium ethoxide, sodium hydrogen, sodium amide, lithium diisopropylamide, butyl lithium, t-butyl lithium and/or potassium t-butoxide, the molar ratio between base and (IV) is 0.2-1:1, the organic solvent is toluene, tetrahydrofuran, 2-methyltetrahydrofuran, diethyl ether and/or methyl tert-butyl ether, the reaction temperature is 80-110 degrees C, and the reaction time is 4-10 hours. The molar ratio between (IV) and (I) is 1-1.5:1, the molar ratio between base and (IV) is 0.2-0.6:1, and in step (ii), the reaction temperature is 95-105 degrees C, and the reaction time is 6-8 hours. The molar ratio between pimavanserin and tartaric acid is 1-3:1, the temperature of the salt formation is 0-100 degrees C, the time of the salt formation is 2-10 hours, and the salt-forming solvents are ethanol, isopropanol, isopropyl acetate, tetrahydrofuran, acetone and/or methyl ethyl ketone. In step (ii), the molar ratio between compound and tartaric acid is 1.95-2.05:1, the temperature of the salt formation is 60-80 degrees C, the time of the salt formation is 4-6 hours, and the salt-forming solvent is ethanol and/or isopropanol.

PT P
PN CN110810803-A
TI Extracting plant salt from Salicornia europaea by grinding Salicornia europaea and distilled water, separating supernatant, using supercritical fluid carbon dioxide synergistic entrainer, decolorizing liquid phase and drying liquid phase.
AU WANG S
   LI B
   CAO L
   MAO Q
   YUAN Y
AE SHANDONG SUNWAY SALINE-ALKALI SOIL TECHN (SUAW-C)
GA 202018312W
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting plant salt from Salicornia europaea, involves (a) washing the upper part of the fresh stems and leaves of S.europaea, adding distilled water in a mass ratio of 1:(3-5), grinding, separating the filtrate using a centrifuge and separating the supernatant, (b) using a supercritical fluid carbon dioxide synergistic entrainer to extract the supernatant in an extraction kettle at 25-50 degrees C at a pressure of 20-40 MPa to obtain an extract solution, (c) passing the extract solution flows out of the extraction kettle through a pressure reducing valve, discharging carbon dioxide as a gas phase, decolorizing the remaining liquid phase and removing the entrainer by vacuum drying to obtain a final liquid phase, and (d) drying the final liquid phase at low temperature and vacuum to obtain a solid plant salt product.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting plant salt from S.europaea, where the plant salt is useful in health food, cosmetics, lotions and living environment supplies applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method extracts plant salts, amino acids, flavones and useful substances in S.europaea.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The entrainer is ethanol and benzenesulfonic acid. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of ethanol to the supernatant is (1-1.5):10. The mass ratio of benzenesulfonic acid to the supernatant is (0.3-0.6):10. Preferred Method: The supercritical fluid carbon dioxide flow rate is 10-15 l/minute. In step (c), the temperature of removing ethanol by vacuum drying is 40-50 degrees C, the degree of vacuum is 70-80 KPa, the temperature of benzenesulfonic acid is 75-85 degrees C, and the degree of vacuum is 70-80 KPa. The step (d) involves drying the final liquid phase in a low-temperature vacuum drying at -35 degrees C to -50 degrees C at a vacuum degree of 12-45 MPa. The step (c) involves using a decoloring agent comprising activated carbon. The centrifuge in step (a) is carried out at a speed of 10000-20000 rpm.

PN CN110394159-A
TI Preparing ion exchange ZIF-8 adsorbent useful for carbon dioxide adsorption, comprises dissolving zinc nitrate hexahydrate, 2-methylimidazole and polyvinylpyrrolidone in methanol, mixing, adding lithium nitrate, centrifuging and drying.
AU SONG F
   CAO Y
   LIU Y
   YANG B
   YAN J
   XU Q
AE YANCHENG INST TECHNOLOGY (YANH-C)
GA 2019927650
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ion exchange ZIF-8 adsorbent comprises (i) dissolving zinc nitrate hexahydrate, 2-methylimidazole and polyvinylpyrrolidone in methanol in a molar ratio of 1:5:2-3, uniformly mixing to obtain a precursor solution of ZIF-8; (ii) adding lithium nitrate to the precursor solution of ZIF-8, where the molar ratio of lithium and zinc is 1-10:1 and stirring uniformly to obtain precursor solution of ion-exchanged ZIF-8; (iii) transferring precursor solution of ion exchanged ZIF-8 to a Teflon (RTM: Polytetrafluoroethylene)-lined stainless steel reaction vessel, placing reaction kettle in a homogeneous reactor and reacting at 120-150 degrees C for 48-72 hours, cooling the product, centrifuging the precipitate, washing the precipitate with methanol, and drying to obtain a white solid precipitated ZIF-8 adsorbent; and (iv) vacuum drying the obtained adsorbent.
   USE - The adsorbent is useful for carbon dioxide adsorption (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the specific surface area, surface ion dispersibility and surface basic sites of the ion exchange adsorbent, and improves the adsorption amount and selectivity of ZIF-8 for carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of zinc nitrate hexahydrate, 2-methylimidazole to polyvinylpyrrolidone is 1:5:2. Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the reaction kettle is placed in a homogeneous reactor and reacted at a speed of 60 revolutions/minute and a temperature of 140 degrees C for 72 hours and the drying is performed for 12 hours at 110-120 degrees C. In the step (iv), the white solid precipitated ZIF-8 adsorbent is vacuum dried at 160 degrees C for 12 hours and the drying oven is evacuated every 2 hours.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - COMPUTING AND CONTROL - Preferred Components: The purification device includes separation membranes for separating carbon dioxide from the biogas. The control device changes the number of separation membranes to which the biogas is supplied by controlling the opening and closing unit based on the Wobbe index. The purification apparatus includes a pressure adjustment unit that adjusts the pressure of the biogas supplied to separation membranes, control device controls the biogas by controlling the pressure adjustment unit based on the Wobbe index, and flow rate adjusting unit that adjusts the flow rate of the biogas supplied to separation membranes. When the control device controls the purification condition, the methane concentration of purified gas becomes constant. When Wobbe index is reduced, then pressure of the biogas increases and flow rate of the biogas reduces.

PN CN110052281-A; CN110052281-B
TI Oxygen vacancy-enriched nitrogen-doped tin oxide for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to formic acid, is prepared by calcining dicyandiamide, grinding graphitic-carbonitride nanosheet, mixing with stannous chloride, calcining.
AU GONG J
   HU C
   ZHANG L
   LI L
   ZHAO Z
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
   UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 201967020U
AB    NOVELTY - An oxygen vacancy-enriched nitrogen-doped tin oxide is prepared by (1) calcining the dicyandiamide at a temperature of 500-550 degrees C for 4-5 hours and naturally cooling to room temperature to obtain a block carbonitride, (2) grinding carbonitride to a powder form, calcining the carbonitride powder at a temperature of 450-500 degrees C for 2-3 hours and naturally cooling to room temperature to obtain graphitic-carbonitride nanosheet, (3) grinding graphitic-carbonitride nanosheet, mixing with stannous chloride dihydrate in a mass ratio of 1:1, calcining at a temperature of 500-600 degrees C for 3-5 hours and naturally cooling to room temperature, (4) collecting and grinding.
   USE - Oxygen vacancy-enriched nitrogen-doped tin oxide for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to formic acid (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) method for preparing oxygen vacancy-enriched nitrogen-doped tin oxide; and
   (2) application of oxygen vacancy-enriched nitrogen-doped tin oxide for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to formic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The step (1) comprises calcining at temperature of 550 degrees C for 4 hours. The step (2) comprises calcining at temperature of 500 degrees C for 2 hours. Preferred Process: The application of oxygen vacancy-enriched nitrogen-doped tin oxide involves dripping oxygen vacancy-enriched nitrogen-doped tin oxide in glassy carbon electrode used as working electrode, saturated calomel electrode as reference electrode and platinum as counter electrode, assembling into electrochemical cell and energizing for electrocatalytic reaction.

PT P
PN CN109503785-A; CN109503785-B
TI Preparation of alkali-resistant boron-modified phenolic resin, involves stirring phenol and formaldehyde, heating, adjusting pH, heat-preserving, condensing with cold water, adding borax, introducing carbon dioxide, and dehydrating.
AU MENG F
   LIU Q
   ZHOU D
   WANG L
   LI S
AE SHANGHAI INST TECHNOLOGY (SHGH-C)
GA 2019286499
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of alkali-resistant boron-modified phenolic resin, involves adding 0.1 mol phenol and 0.16 mol formaldehyde to a three-necked flask equipped with an electric stirrer, a reflux condenser, and a thermometer, uniformly stirring, heating in a water bath to 85-95 degrees C, adding aqueous ammonia to adjust the pH of the system to 7.5-8.0, heat-preserving at 90-100 degrees C for 1.5-2.5 hours, condensing with cold water to 45-55 degrees C, adding 0.75 g borax, simultaneously introducing carbon dioxide for 6-9 minutes at 50 ml/minute, and dehydrating under reduced pressure.
   USE - Preparation of alkali-resistant boron-modified phenolic resin (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses carbon dioxide, which reduces the alkalinity of basic phenolic resin system, making the resin weakly acidic from alkaline. Borax is converted to boric acid and further reacts with the resin to significantly reduce the phenolic hydroxyl group and methylol group content in the phenolic resin.

TI Coal-based direct reduction method using auxiliary reduction, involves increasing carbon monoxide concentration in coal-based shaft furnace to accelerate diffusion of carbon monoxide into iron oxide pellets.
AU FAN X
AE WISDRI ENG & RES INC LTD (CMEG-C)
GA 2019253606
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves placing iron oxide pellets and the coal-based raw materials into a coal-based shaft furnace reduction chamber (1). The reduction chamber with a combustion chamber (2) is heated to promote a reduction reaction of the charge in the reduction chamber. The auxiliary gas (5) is introduced into the reduction chamber. The auxiliary gas is a gas containing carbon dioxide and having carbon monoxide to carbon dioxide concentration ratio greater than 1. Carbon dioxide is reacted with carbong in the charge to produce carbon monoxide. The carbon monoxide concentration is increased in the coal-based shaft furnace to accelerate the diffusion of carbon monoxide into the iron oxide pellets to improve the reduction conditions.
   USE - Coal-based direct reduction method using auxiliary reduction.
   ADVANTAGE - The diffusion of carbon monoxide into the iron oxide pellets is accelerated. The reduction time is shortened. The production efficiency is improved.

TI Manufacturing method of artificial lung, involves            making either one of outer surface and inner surface            into contact with colloidal solution and circulating            carbon dioxide gas to other surface side.
AU ANZAI T
   KIDO T
AE TERUMO CORP (TERU-C)
   TERUMO CORP (TERU-C)
   TERUMO CORP (TERU-C)
GA 201874067K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves making either one of an                outer surface (3aB) and the inner surface (3cB)                into contact with a colloidal solution containing                an anti-thrombotic high molecular compound (18) and                circulating a carbon dioxide gas to the other                surface side. The 5000 ml-or-less 50 mL or more of                carbon dioxide gas is circulated with respect to                the colloidal solution of 1g. The colloidal                solution contains 0.01 mass % or more of                anti-thrombotic high molecular compounds. A                structural unit is derived from the alkoxyalkyl                (meth)acrylate.
   USE - Manufacturing method of artificial lung.
   ADVANTAGE - The coating amount of the anti-thrombotic                polymeric material with respect to a hollow fiber                membrane can be made to increase so as to easing                the burden on a patient and improve anti-thrombotic                property. The high producibility can be achieved                while suppressing and preventing the gelatinization                in a polymerization process effectively.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                artificial lung.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of the                hollow fiber membrane.
   Hollow fiber membrane (3)
   Outer surface (3aB)
   Inner surface layer (3c)
   Inner surface (3cB)
   Anti-thrombotic high molecular compound                (18)

PT P
PN CN108285799-A; CN108285799-B
TI Utilizing lignite resource comprises ion-exchanging lignite with calcium ion-rich mine wastewater, pyrolyzing, co-gasification activating, flue gas desulfurizing, performing chemical chain combustion, and recycling.
AU LIAO J
   HAN Y
   FENG G
   CHANG L
   BAO W
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 201857027R
AB    NOVELTY - Utilizing lignite resource comprises ion-exchanging lignite with calcium ion-rich mine wastewater to obtain lignite loaded with calcium ions, the lignite loaded with calcium ions is used as a raw material, subjecting to medium temperature pyrolysis to obtain pyrolysis gas, coal tar and pyrolysis semi-coke, carrying out co-gasification activation of the pyrolysis semi-coke by using water vapor and carbon dioxide to obtain post-gasification semi-coke of calcium-loaded gas, and at the same time, by-product gasification gas, flue gas desulfurizing by calcified gas-loaded semi-coke, and removing sulfur dioxide in flue gas to obtain vulcanized semi-coke, performing chemical chain combustion by vulcanized semi-coke as raw material to obtain heat in the semi-coke and producing high-purity carbon dioxide and ash, and returning a portion of the resulting high purity carbon dioxide to the co-gasification activation step for recycle as a gasification agent.
   USE - The method is useful for utilizing lignite resource.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes high-efficiency grading lignite, reduces carbon emission and realizes calcium ions in mine wastewater.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lignite is lignite pulverized coal having a particle size of less than 0.3 mm. The calcium ion-rich mine wastewater in calcium ion is 5-25%. The mixed atmosphere is water vapor and carbon dioxide, and the volume percentage of water vapor is 20-80%. Preferred Method: The lignite and the calcium ion-rich mine wastewater are mixed at a mass ratio of 1:2-10, and ion exchange is performed at room temperature for 24-36 hours. The lignite loaded with calcium ions is first subjected to hot pressing drying at 150-220 degrees C and 8-12 MPa, and carry out medium temperature pyrolysis. The medium temperature pyrolysis temperature is 650-800 degrees C, and the pyrolysis time is 30-60 minutes. The intermediate temperature pyrolysis process is carried out in a fixed bed reactor. The co-gasification activation is carried out in a fluidized bed, the gasification temperature is 800-1000 degrees C, and the gasification time is 15-60 minutes. The flue gas desulfurization process is carried out by countercurrent moving bed in a continuous desulfurization manner, the vulcanization temperature is 600-900 degrees C, the vulcanization time is 10-48 hours, and the desulfurizer after vulcanization is discharged at the bottom of the moving bed.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)
MC H04-A01; H04-F02A; H09-C
IP C10B-053/04; C10G-001/00; C10J-003/60

PN CN108023086-A
TI Preparation of iron-based polyanion-high porosity biomass carbon composite involves dissolving preparation material, oxidant and reducing agent in water, adding high-porosity biomass carbon, drying and calcining obtained precursor.
AU DEND C
   ZHANG G
   ZHANG J
AE UNIV HARBIN NORMAL (UYBI-C)
GA 201838413P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of iron-based polyanion-high porosity biomass carbon composite involves surface-cleaning biomass, acid-treating, activating, drying at 60-80 degrees C, activating, placing the activated product in vacuum closed container, filling the container with carbon dioxide gas, vacuumizing, refilling the carbon dioxide gas, repeating the process thrice, storing in carbon dioxide atmosphere for 24 hours, obtaining treated material, carbonizing treated material under protection of inert gas, obtaining high-porosity biomass carbon, dissolving weighed preparation material, oxidant and reducing agent in water, adding high-porosity biomass carbon, evaporating-drying at 80-100 degrees C, obtaining precursor powder, placing obtained powder in high-temperature furnace, and calcining at 600-800 degrees C for 3-5 minutes. The molar ratio of iron element with respect to oxidant is 1:0.5-2. The molar ratio of oxidizing agent with respect to reducing agent is 1:1.
   USE - Preparation of iron-based polyanion-high porosity biomass carbon composite having multidimensional hierarchical structure.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently and rapidly provides iron-based polyanion-high porosity biomass carbon composite without affecting the environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Composition: Biomass is kelp, cotton, rice shell or banana peel.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composite iron-based material is lithium iron phosphate, iron phosphate, sodium-iron-phosphate (Na3.12Fe2.44(P2O7)2) or lithium-iron-phosphate (Li2-xFeP2O7). The oxidant is nitric acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The reducing agent is tartaric acid or citric acid.

AB    NOVELTY - A dosage form comprises (a) at least one                functionalized calcium carbonate-comprising                material (FCC), which is a reaction product of                natural ground calcium carbonate or precipitated                calcium carbonate with carbon dioxide and hydronium                ion donor(s), where carbon dioxide is formed in                situ by the hydronium ion donor treatment and/or is                supplied from an external source, and (b) at least                one hot melt extruded polymer resin, where the FCC                material is dispersed in the polymer resin and the                weight ratio of FCC material to the polymer resin                is 95:5-5:95.
   USE - The dosage form is useful in a pharmaceutical,                nutraceutical, cosmetic, home and personal care                product (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The dosage form has excellent stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for producing the dosage form, which involves (a)                providing at least one FCC, (b) providing at least                one polymer resin, (c) mixing the FCC material and                the polymer resin, (d) hot melt extruding the                mixture, and (e) milling or pelletizing the hot                melt extruded product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Form: The dosage form further                  comprises at least one active agent and/or                  inactive agent, where the active agent and/or                  inactive agent are chosen from pharmaceutical                  active agents, active or inactive prodrugs,                  nutraceuticals, food additives, cosmetic                  additives and their mixtures. The dosage form                  further comprises at least one excipient chosen                  from disintegrant chosen from modified cellulose                  gums, insoluble crosslinked                  polyvinylpyrrolidones, starch glycolates,                  microcrystalline cellulose, pregelatinized                  starch, sodium carboxymethyl starch,                  low-substituted hydroxypropyl cellulose,                  homopolymers of N-vinyl-2-pyrrolidone, alkyl,                  hydroxyalkyl, carboxyalkyl-cellulose esters,                  alginates, microcrystalline cellulose and its                  polymorphic forms, ion exchange resins, gums,                  chitin, chitosan, clays, gellan gum, cross-linked                  polacrilin copolymers, agar, gelatine, dextrines,                  acrylic acid polymers, carboxymethylcellulose                  sodium/calcium, hydroxypropyl methylcellulose                  phthalate, shellac or their mixtures, lubricants,                  inner-phase lubricants, outer-phase lubricants,                  impact modifiers, plasticizers, waxes,                  stabilizers, pigments, coloring agents, scenting                  agents, taste masking agents, flavoring agents,                  sweeteners, mouth-feel improvers, binders,                  diluents, film forming agents, adhesives,                  buffers, adsorbents, odor-masking agents and                  their mixtures. The dosage form is in the form of                  powder, tablets (multi-layered tablets, tablets                  of altered geometric configuration or                  matrix-tablets, or mini-tablets), pellets,                  capsules, granules and/or a tab let-in-cup.                  Preferred Components: The natural ground calcium                  carbonate is chosen from calcium carbonate                  containing minerals chosen from marble, chalk,                  dolomite, limestone and their mixtures. The                  precipitated calcium carbonate is chosen from                  precipitated calcium carbonates having                  aragonitic, vateritic or calcitic mineralogical                  crystal forms and their mixtures. Preferred                  Property: The FCC material has a                  Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) specific surface                  area of 20-450 m2/g, preferably 50-140 m2/g                  measured using the nitrogen and BET method                  according to ISO 9277. The FCC material comprises                  particles having a volume median grain diameter                  d50 (vol) of 1-50 mu m, preferably 4-12 mu m,                  and/or has an intra-particle intruded specific                  pore volume of 0.15-1.35 cm3/g, preferably                  0.40-1.25 cm3/g, calculated from a mercury                  intrusion porosimetry measurement. Preferred                  Method: The active agent and/or inactive agent                  is/are (a) loaded onto or mixed with the FCC                  material, (b) dispersed in the hot melt extruded                  polymer resin comprising the FCC material, (c) in                  form of a core, preferably a compacted tablet,                  which is at least partially covered by at least                  one hot melt extruded polymer resin comprising                  the FCC-comprising material, (d) in form of a                  layer which at least partially covers a core,                  preferably a compacted tablet, made from the hot                  melt extruded polymer resin comprising the FCC                  material, or (e) in form of a layered structure                  of at least 2 layers, where at least one layer is                  made from the hot melt extruded polymer resin                  comprising the FCC material. The method further                  involves (i) providing at least one active agent                  and/or inactive agent, or (ii) providing at least                  one excipient, where the active agent and/or                  inactive agent of step (i) and/or (ii) is/are                  loaded onto or mixed with the FCC material before                  mixing, and/or mixed with the FCC material and                  the polymer resin before hot melt extruding,                  and/or coated in form of one or more layers onto                  the dosage form. The method involves compacting                  the dosage form, where the active agent, inactive                  agent, and/or the excipient is/are mixed and                  compacted to form a core tablet. The method                  further involves partially covering the formed                  core tablet with the obtained dosage form and                  compacting the obtained product, where the active                  agent and/or inactive agent and/or excipient are                  (a) loaded onto the FCC material before mixing,                  and/or (b) mixed with the FCC material and the                  polymer resin before hot melt extruding.

PN CN112341501-A
TI New tetramethylphenanthroline-ligand-containing tetranuclear nickel-based complex used as photocatalyst for reducing carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide.
AU XU Q
   LU X
AE UNIV YUNNAN (UNYN-C)
GA 202117492M
AB    NOVELTY - A tetramethylphenanthroline-ligand-containing tetranuclear nickel-based complex (I) is new.
   USE - New tetramethylphenanthroline-ligand-containing tetranuclear nickel-based complex is used as photocatalyst for reducing carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The tetramethylphenanthroline-ligand-containing tetranuclear nickel-based complex has excellent catalytic effect and is obtained economically.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A tetramethylphenanthroline-ligand-containing tetranuclear nickel-based complex of formula (I) is new. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for synthesis of complex (I), which involves synthesizing 3,4,7,8-tetramethyl-1,10-phenanthroline and nickel chloride hexahydrate under solvothermal condition to obtain blue-green block crystal.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: Complex (I) has a solid crystal morphology with a monoclinic crystal system and a space group of P21/c, and unit cell parameters a of 17.9539(11) Angstrom , b of 20.8015(14) Angstrom , c of 9.2667(7) Angstrom , alpha and gamma of 90 degrees , and beta of 91.246(6) degrees , and unit cell volume of 3460.0(4) Angstrom 3. Preferred Process: Synthesis of complex (I) preferably involves dissolving 3,4,7,8-tetramethyl-1,10-phenanthroline and nickel chloride hexahydrate in a molar ratio of 1:1 in an acetonitrile solvent to obtain a transparent blue-green mixed liquid, sealing the mixed solution, subjecting to solvothermal reaction at 120-140 for 4-8 days, cooling to room temperature at a rate of 5 degrees C/hour to obtain a blue-green block crystal, and washing the crystal with ether to obtain a single crystal sample of complex (I).

PN CN110725299-A
TI Reinforcing calcareous sand soil and desert sand for microbe mineralized carbonate gelled material, involves mixing calcium nitrate, solid carbon bacteria, foam carbon dioxide, and calcareous sand soil and/or desert sand and solidifying.
AU WANG J
   YU X
   FU H
   WANG Z
   WANG P
   YUAN G
   NI J
   GAO Z
   YE Q
   YANG K
   JIN J
   XU D
   XIA Z
   LV Y
   GUAN Z
   JIANG Z
   CHEN Z
AE UNIV WENZHOU (UNWZ-C)
GA 2020105214
AB    NOVELTY - Reinforcing the calcareous sand soil and desert sand, involves mixing slaked lime or calcium nitrate with mass ratio 1, solid carbon bacteria with mass ratio 2, foam carbon dioxide with mass ratio 1, and calcareous sand soil and/or desert sand with mass ratio 10 to form biological carbonate cement. Solidification is performed after finishing reaction.
   USE - Method for reinforcing the calcareous sand soil and desert sand for microbe mineralized carbonate gelled material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The mass of slaked lime is 1. The mass of Streptomyces flavus is 2. The mass of foamy carbon dioxide is 1 and the mass of calcareous sand is 10. The mixture is reacted to form bio-carbonate cement. After the reaction is finished, it solidifies. Calcium nitrate mass ratio is 1, Streptomyces flavus mass ratio is 2, foam carbon dioxide mass ratio is 1 and they are made into a mixed solution and sprayed on desert sand to react on the surface of the desert sand to form biological carbonate cement, which solidify after the reaction. Preferred Process: The method for reinforcing the calcareous sand soil and desert sand further involves configuring the drill pipe and a spiral stirring rod, a spiral stirring rod with the mixed liquid conveying passage. The spiral stirring rod lower portion side has a liquid discharging hole, upper end of the spiral stirring rod connected with the pumping mechanism. The pipeline is connected with the mixing liquid transporting channel conduction of pumping mechanism, when operating, firstly the drill pipe drill into the calcareous sand soil by pile, and then stirring the bolt rod is mounted on the pile machine. The pumping mechanism control pile machine and the spiral stirring rod from the drill pipe is spiral stirred, gradually enters the pipe bottom, the spiral stirring rod gradually enters the drill pipe in the drill pipe input and the mixed solution and standing for more than 24, then upward lifting the drill pipe. The calcareous sand land on the cycle to operate, land in the calcareous sand of sand soil calcareous sandy soil consolidation, and the calcareous sand soil reinforcement.

AU GUO L
   DOU R
   WU Y
   WANG Y
   GONG Z
   WU X
AE UNIV WUHAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UWSC-C)
GA 2019632222
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of cyclic carbonate using lignin as catalyst involves subjecting carbon dioxide and epoxide to cyclo addition reaction under action of main catalyst and co-catalyst to obtain cyclic carbonic acid ester.
   USE - Preparation of cyclic carbonate using lignin as catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The cyclic carbonate has low pollution, high activity, high selectivity and mild conditions. Less amount of co-catalyst is used, separation of the product and recycling of the catalyst are easy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The co-catalyst is one of potassium iodide, potassium chloride, potassium bromide, tetrapropylammonium iodide, and tetrabutylammonium bromide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The preparation preferably involves adding main catalyst and co-catalyst to the epoxide, heating under a carbon dioxide atmosphere, cooling to room temperature, separating the main catalyst and the co-catalyst by filtration. Preferred Condition: The reaction temperature of the cyclo addition reaction is 40-150 degrees C, and the cyclo addition reaction time is 1-14 hours. The pressure for maintaining carbon dioxide in the cyclo addition reaction is 0.1-2 MPa. Preferred Component: The main catalyst is lignin. The lignin is of formula (I). The epoxide is of formula (II).Ring S = syringyl-based lignin monomer;Ring G = guaiacyl-based lignin monomer;Ring H = p-hydroxyphenyl-type lignin monomer;andR = -CH3, -CH2Cl, -(CH2)3CH3 or phenyl.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The lignin is one of basic lignin, enzymatic lignin and sodium lignosulfonate. Rings S, G and H are three monomers in lignin. The basic lignin and the enzymatic lignin comprise three monomers of S, G and H. The sodium lignosulfonate comprises sulfonate group in addition to the three monomers S, G, and H. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of the reactive hydroxyl group to the epoxide in the lignin is 1:50-500. The co-catalyst and epoxide molar ratio is 1:50-500.

AU FU G
   ZHANG Y
   ZHONG G
   LIU C
AE ZHEJIANG LANTIAN ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTIO (SNCM-C)
   SINOCHEM LANTIAN CO LTD (SNCM-C)
GA 201853947G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a polar monomer-modified polyvinyl fluoride copolymer in a continuous process comprises carrying out the supercritical carbon dioxide under the action of the free radical initiator, adding the vinyl monomer and polar monomer into the reaction kettle, reacting vinyl monomer and polar monomer copolymerization to obtain polyvinyl fluoride copolymer modified by polar monomer.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a polar monomer-modified polyvinyl fluoride copolymer in a continuous process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves melting process and film adhesive performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The polar monomer is unsaturated olefin and/or unsaturated fatty acid or ester. The unsaturated olefin is CnHxCly. The unsaturated fatty acid or ester is CnHxCly0z. The n is independently integer of 1-15, x, y, and z are independently integer is 0-2n, x + y=2n, x + y + z=2n. The polar monomer is ethylene, propylene, isobutylene, vinyl chloride, vinyl butyrate, vinyl acetate, vinyl butyrate, vinyl methacrylate, crotonic acid and glycidyl methacrylate. The mass ratio of vinyl monomer and polar monomer is 60/40-99.999/0.001; preferably 40-500:1. The free radical initiator is azo compound and/or a perfluorinated peroxide compound. The dosage of the free radical initiator is a fluorinated vinyl monomer is 0.1-3 wt.%. The azo compound is 2,2-azobisisobutyronitrile, 2,2-azobis-2,4,4-trimethylvaleronitrile, azobis dimethyl butyrate and azobiscyclohexylcarbonitrile. The perfluoroperoxidic compound is perfluoroperoxydicarbonate, perfluoroperoxydi(2-n-propoxy)propionyl, perfluoroacyl peroxide and perfluoroxanthene acyl peroxides. The polymerization temperature is 40-120 degrees C preferably 70-100 degrees C. The polymerization pressure is 8-50MPa preferably 15-30MPa.

PN CN112915114-A
TI Preparation of ginseng extract by heating ginseng slice involves high temperature saturated vapor, soaking in ethanol solvent, ultrasonically extracting and using carbon dioxide supercritical extraction technology to extract.
AU NIU Z
   CHEN G
AE GUANGDONG NIUDAFUJIA BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202170675V
AB    NOVELTY - A ginseng extract is prepared by making ginseng into ginseng slice, heating by high temperature saturated vapor, soaking ginseng slice in ethanol solvent, performing ultrasonic extraction to obtain original extract and using carbon dioxide supercritical extraction technology to extract original extract.
   USE - The method is used for preparing ginseng extract.
   ADVANTAGE - The method keeps biological activity of ginseng, and greatly reduces extraction time and influence of environment change to extract purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a manufacturing device of ginseng extract.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves making ginseng into ginseng slice, heating by high temperature saturated vapor at 20 degrees C for 8-15 minutes, soaking ginseng slice in 95% ethanol solvent, performing ultrasonic extraction for 20-30 minutes to obtain original extract and using carbon dioxide supercritical extraction technology to extract original extract. Carbon dioxide supercritical extraction involves introducing carbon dioxide, adjusting environment air pressure to 35 Pa, adjusting environment temperature to 50 degrees C, adding entrainer, extracting original extract for 30-40 minutes, cooling and drying.

AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 2018A36713

   USE - The adsorbent is useful in methane/carbon dioxide separation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent has excellent adsorption cycle stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing adsorbent, comprising (i) mixing resorcinol, formaldehyde and polyamine, and then polymerizing to obtain emulsion, (ii) curing the emulsion, and drying to obtain solid, and (iii) carbonizing the solid to obtain adsorbent.

Preferred Method: In step (ii), the curing temperature is 90-150 degrees C, and the curing time is 2-12 hours; the drying temperature is 40-80 degrees C (preferably 50-70 degrees C). In step (iii), the carbonization temperature is 600-800 degrees C, and time is 100-600 minutes.

PN EP3388524-A1; EP3388524-B1
TI Producing D-or L-2-hydroxy-4-methylmercaptobutanoic acid involves reacting a mixture comprising 3-(methylthio)-propanal (methional), carbon dioxide, a decarboxylase, its corresponding cofactor, an alcohol dehydrogenase and NADH or NADPH.
AU SKERRA A
   MARTIN J
   JAKOB H
   FISCHER D
AE EVONIK DEGUSSA GMBH (EVON-C)
   EVONIK OPERATIONS GMBH (EVON-C)
GA 2018802901
AB    NOVELTY - Producing D-or L-2-hydroxy-4-methylmercaptobutanoic acid (MHA) involves reacting a mixture comprising 3-(methylthio)-propanal (methional), carbon dioxide, a decarboxylase (EC 4.1.1), its corresponding cofactor, an alcohol dehydrogenase (EC 1.1.1) and NADH or NADPH to form D-or L-2-hydroxy-4-methylmercaptobutanoic acid (MHA) or its salt.
   USE - The method is used for producing D-MHA or L-MHA.
   ADVANTAGE - The MHA production method is a hydrogen cyanide-free process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the method above, the cofactor comprises thiamine pyrophosphate. The decarboxylase is selected from pyruvate decarboxylase Pdc1, which originates from Saccharomyces cerevisiae, phenylpyruvate decarboxylase Aro10, which originates from S. cerevisiae, and branched chain decarboxylase KdcA, which originates from Lactococcus lactis, as well as mutants and variants of these decarboxylase having decarboxylase activity. The method is used for producing D-MHA, where the alcohol dehydrogenase is a D-hydroxyisocaproate dehydrogenase, where the D-hydroxyisocaproate dehydrogenase is D-HicDH from Lactobacillus casei or any of its mutants and variants having alcohol dehydrogenase activity. The method is used or producing L-MHA, where the alcohol dehydrogenase is a L-hydroxyisocaproate dehydrogenase, where the L-hydroxyisocaproate dehydrogenase is L-HicDH from Lactobacillus confusus or any of its mutants and variants having alcohol dehydrogenase activity. The carbon dioxide is applied to the mixture at a pressure of 10-7400 kPa. The mixture further comprises formic acid or its salt and a formate dehydrogenase (EC 1.17.1.9), where the formate dehydrogenase is selected from formate dehydrogenase from Pseudomonas sp. and formate dehydrogenase from Candida sp. as well as mutants and variants of these formate dehydrogenases having formate dehydrogenase activity.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.)

TI Preparation of cyclic carbonate involves reacting carbon dioxide and epoxide with conjugated manganese porphyrin microcellular polymeric material and tetra-n-butylammonium bromide.
AU ZHANG X

AE HENAN INST ENG (HEAN-C)

AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of cyclic carbonate comprises using carbon dioxide and epoxide as raw materials, taking conjugated manganese porphyrin microcellular polymeric material as a heterogeneous catalyst and tetra-n-butylammonium bromide as cocatalyst, and reacting for 12-48 hours at normal temperature and normal pressure without solvent to obtain the cyclic carbonate.

N.B: n is not defined. Preferred Component: The epoxy compound is propylene oxide, epoxy butane, epoxy chloropropane, epoxy bromopropane, styrene oxide, phenyl glycidyl ether or cyclohexene oxide. The active component of the multiphase catalyst dosage is 0.2-0.5 mol% of the amount of the epoxy compound. The dosage of the auxiliary catalyst is 3.6-7.2 mol% of the epoxy compound.

AU JURECKOVA Z

GA 201818904R
AB    NOVELTY - Combination drink based on wine and fruit juice, comprises: 80 vol.% of mix of water and grape wine; 12-15 vol.% of sweeteners; 5-8 vol.% of fruit components; and 0.5 g/l of carbon dioxide.
   USE - The combination drink is useful as refreshing drink.
   ADVANTAGE - The combination drink: has low alcohol content based on wine and fruit juice, which is improved with other ingredients; has flavor of the wine and the qualities of the fruit juice; and is good quality alternative to wine and other alcoholic beverages.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The fruit part is 100% fruit juice or fruit juice concentrate. The fruit juice is Aronia or blackberry juice. The combination drink comprises: 50 vol.% of water, 30 vol.% of white wine of grape, 15 vol.% of rectified concentrated must, 5 vol.% of 100% Aronia juice, and 0.5 g/l of carbon dioxide; 70 vol.% of water, 10 vol.% of red wine of grape, 12 vol.% of fructose, 8 vol.% of 100% juice of blackberry, and 0.5 g/l of carbon dioxide; 60 vol.% of water, 20 vol.% of rose wine grape, 15 vol.% of sucrose, 5 vol.% of 100% juice delinquency, and 0.5 g/l of carbon dioxide; or 60 vol.% of water, 20 vol.% of white grape wine, 15 vol.% of glucose, 5 vol.% of concentrate buckthorn juice, and 0.5 g/l of carbon dioxide.
   EXAMPLE - Typical combination drink comprised of: 50 vol.% water; 30 vol.% white wine of grape; 15 vol.% rectified concentrated must; 5 vol.% juice Aronia (100%); 0.5 g carbon dioxide; and ascorbic acid.
