PT P
PN WO2021009781-A1; CN114340405-A; EP3998873-A1; US2022364015-A1
TI Discontinuous extracting nutraceutical components            comprising carotenoids and omega-3 from microalgae            biomass, and their separation by using carbon dioxide            in supercritical conditions both pure and in mixture            with solvent.
AU CICCI A
   IAQUANIELLO G
   MAZZELLI A
AE BIO P SRL (BIOP-Non-standard)
   NEXTCHEM SPA (NEXT-Non-standard)
   NEXTCHEM SPA (NEXT-Non-standard)
   NEXTCHEM SPA (NEXT-Non-standard)
GA 202108506V
AB    NOVELTY - Discontinuous extracting nutraceutical                components comprising carotenoids and omega -3 from                microalgae biomass, and their separation by using                carbon dioxide in supercritical conditions both                pure and in mixture with a solvent.
   USE - The method is useful for discontinuous                extracting nutraceutical components comprising                carotenoids and omega -3 from microalgae                biomass.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes carbon dioxide which is                not toxic, nonflammable, odorless, inert and easily                separable; has competitive cost when compared to                common organic solvents and is recognized generally                recognized as safe; is used for development of                innovative process for extraction of components                with high added value including carotenoids and                omega -3 from microalgae; removes unwanted                component (tripalmitin) from the lipid extract;                utilizes environmentally friendly and generally                recognized as safe recognized solvents for                nutraceutical purposes; and uses supercritical                carbon monoxide in all the extraction steps which                ensures easy implementation of process and reduces                cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an                apparatus comprising (a) a first extractor in which                the triglycerides of eicosapentaenoic acid and                docosahexaenoic acid (DHA) are separated from the                biomass formed by microalgae by extraction with                pure supercritical carbon dioxide, (b) a second                extractor in which the tripalmitin is separated                from the residue of the previous extractor by                extraction with pure supercritical carbon dioxide,                (c) a third extractor in which the carotenoids are                separated from the residue of the previous                extractor, by extraction with supercritical carbon                dioxide mixed with solvent consisting of 5 wt.%                ethanol, (d) a first phase separator in which is                routed the extract outcoming from the first                extractor, consisting of carbon dioxide and                triglycerides, and which allows the recovery of                carbon dioxide by desorption, and its recirculation                at the beginning of the triglyceride extraction                process, (e) a second phase separator in which is                routed the extract outcoming from the second                extractor, consisting of carbon dioxide and                tripalmitin, and which allows recovery of carbon                dioxide by desorption, and its recirculation at the                beginning of the triglyceride extraction process,                (f) a third phase separator in which is routed the                extract outcoming from the third extractor,                consisting of the mixture of carbon dioxide/ethanol                and carotenoids, and which allows the separation of                carbon dioxide from the carotenoids and ethanol by                desorption, and its consequent recirculation at the                beginning of the carotenoid extraction process, (g)                an evaporation operating at lower pressure or under                vacuum condition, in which is routed the residue of                the previous third separator, consisting of                carotenoids and ethanol, and which allows the                separation, by evaporation of ethanol from                carotenoids, and thus the recirculation of ethanol                at the beginning of the carotenoid extraction                process, and (h) a storage tanks in which pressure                and temperature are controlled, for storage of the                extracted product, where all the auxiliary                equipment (pumps, compressors, heat exchangers and                valves), are able to reach the process conditions                for the optimal performance of the process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The separation takes place                  selectively with respect to the components. The                  separation of nutraceutical component by many                  subsequent extractions, where the conditions are:                  (a) a first extraction in which the triglycerides                  of EPA and DHA are separated from the biomass                  formed by microalgae by extraction with pure                  supercritical carbon dioxide, (b) second                  extraction in which the tripalmitin is separated                  from the residue of the previous extractor by                  extraction with pure supercritical carbon                  dioxide, (c) a third extraction in which the                  carotenoids are separated from the residue of the                  previous extractor, by extraction with                  supercritical carbon dioxide mixed with solvent                  consisting of 5 wt.% ethanol, (d) a first phase                  separation in which is routed the extract                  outcoming from the first extractor, consisting of                  carbon dioxide and triglycerides, and which                  allows the recovery of carbon dioxide by                  desorption, and its recirculation at the                  beginning of the triglyceride extraction process,                  (e) second phase separation in which is routed                  the extract outcoming from the second extractor,                  consisting of carbon dioxide and tripalmitin, and                  which allows recovery of carbon dioxide by                  desorption, and its recirculation at the                  beginning of the triglyceride extraction process,                  (f) third phase separation in which is routed the                  extract outcoming from the third extractor,                  consisting of the mixture of carbon                  dioxide/ethanol and carotenoids, and which allows                  the separation of carbon dioxide from the                  carotenoids and ethanol by desorption, and its                  consequent recirculation at the beginning of the                  carotenoid extraction process, (g) an evaporation                  operating at lower pressure or under vacuum                  condition, in which is routed the residue of the                  previous third separator, consisting of                  carotenoids and ethanol, and which allows the                  separation, by evaporation of ethanol from                  carotenoids, and thus the recirculation of                  ethanol at the beginning of the carotenoid                  extraction process, and (h) the storage of the                  extracted product in storage tanks in which                  pressure and temperature are controlled, where                  all the equipment (pumps, compressors, heat                  exchangers and valves) are able to reach the                  process conditions for the optimal performance of                  the process. In first, second and third                  extraction: pressure is 130-600 bar; and                  temperature is 30-100 degrees C. In first, second                  and third extraction solvent to solid ratios                  (SSR) are 2-20/hour. In first extraction:                  pressure is 200-300 bar, preferably 200 bars;                  temperature is 50-60 degrees C, preferably 55                  degrees C; and SSR is 5-20/hour, preferably                  5/hour. In second extraction: pressure is 200-300                  bar, preferably 250 bar; temperature is 50-65                  degrees C, preferably 55 degrees C; and SSR is                  5-20/hour, preferably 5/hour. In third                  extraction: pressure is 200-500 bar, preferably                  500 bar; temperature is 40-60 degrees C,                  preferably 60 degrees C; and SSR is 2-10/hour,                  preferably 5/hour. In stored product: pressure is                  1.02-2.5 bar; and temperature is -10 degrees C to                  10 degrees C. The method further comprises                  collecting extracted products in storage tanks                  under pressure of 1.5 bar at 4 degrees C. The                  method further comprises: collecting product from                  the first extractor, then extracting (according                  to step (a)) consisting of triglycerides and less                  than 0.0018% harmful components (tripalmitin);                  collecting product from the second extractor,                  extracting (according to step (b)) mainly                  consisting of tripalmitin extracted and removed                  from the microalgae biomass; and collecting                  product from the evaporator, and separating                  (according to step (g)) consisting of carotenoids                  not contaminated with tripalmitin without further                  purifying.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

TI Supercritical carbon dioxide in crude oil emulsion            diffusion coefficient system, has temperature control            device surrounded with outside of visual microscopic            model, and pressure control device fixed with visual            microscopic model.
AU LIU J
   PAN Z
   MENG X
   WANG Q
   LI K
   SUN T
   LI Z
   TANG S
   ZHANG H
   ZHONG C
   YAO M
   WU H
   MENG F
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 2019436964
AB    (CN208818610-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a system for                measuring supercritical carbon dioxide emulsion in                the diffusion coefficient of crude oil, comprising                a visualization model, emulsion injection device,                oil injection device, a temperature control device,                pressure control device, wherein: visual                microscopic model of diffusion space and fluid                channel; two ends of the fluid channel are                respectively connected with a first liquid inlet                tube and a first liquid discharging pipe of                emulsion injection device and oil injection device                are respectively used for the supercritical carbon                dioxide and crude oil emulsion injection fluid                channel; a temperature control device for adjusting                the experiment temperature, pressure control for                adjusting test confining pressure; first liquid                discharging pipe is provided with a pressure                regulating valve for adjusting the test back                pressure, first liquid inlet pipe is provided with                a pressure gauge, the pressure for measuring                supercritical carbon dioxide emulsion diffusion                into the crude oil. using the system, can simulate                the supercritical carbon dioxide in the crude oil                emulsion diffusion process so as to determine the                diffusion coefficient and provides technical                support for researching the carbon dioxide                displacing oil principle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for determining the diffusion coefficient of                supercritical carbon dioxide emulsion in crude                oil.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                system for determining a diffusion coefficient of a                supercritical carbon dioxide emulsion in crude                oil.
   5Confining pressure control device
   14Groove
   17Pressure regulating valve
   18Pressure gauge
   42Temperature control device
EA    NOVELTY - The system has a visualized microscopic model                that is provided with an upper slide and a lower                slide that are set up and down. A blind hole and a                groove communicate with the blind hole. The two                blind holes collide to form a diffusion space for                the supercritical carbon dioxide emulsion to                diffuse into the crude oil. A first liquid inlet                pipe and a first liquid discharge pipe are                respectively connected to two ends of the fluid                channel. A pressure regulating valve (17) is                provided on the first liquid discharge pipe to                adjust the back pressure of the visual microscopic                model to an experimental back pressure. A pressure                gauge (18) is arranged on the first liquid inlet                pipe. The emulsion injection device and the crude                oil injection device are both in communication with                the first liquid inlet pipe. A temperature control                device (42) is arranged outside the visualized                microscopic model. A confining pressure control                device (5) is surrounded outside the visualized                microscopic model.
   USE - System for determining diffusion coefficient                of supercritical carbon dioxide emulsion in crude                oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can simulate the diffusion process                of supercritical carbon dioxide emulsion in crude                oil, and then determine the diffusion                coefficient.

TI Preparing environmentally friendly graphene            anti-corrosive paint comprises e.g. swelling aqueous            carrier resin into supercritical carbon dioxide resin            solution, agglomerating graphene, atomizing, subjecting            to gasification and dispersing.
AU CHEN Q
   ZAN H
AE CHENGDU NEW KELI CHEM SCI CO LTD (XKLI-C)
   HEBEI CHENYANG IND & TRADE GROUP CO LTD (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 201815430L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing environmentally friendly graphene                anti-corrosive paint comprises (i) swelling a                aqueous carrier resin into the solution in a                supercritical carbon dioxide resin swelling system,                and taking the aqueous carrier resin (ii)                agglomerating graphene, exfoliating graphene                powder, spraying supercritical carbon dioxide resin                solution, atomizing, covering the graphene surface,                subjecting to rapid gasification, coating remaining                resin on the surface of the graphene, recycling the                carbon dioxide gas, and (iii) dispersing e.g water,                graphite composite particle and dispersant.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                environmentally friendly graphene anti-corrosive                paint.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, and environmentally                friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing environmentally friendly graphene                anti-corrosive paint comprises (i) preparing a                two-stage compressor and a cooling system for                carbon dioxide with a resin solution, swelling a                aqueous carrier resin into the solution in a                supercritical carbon dioxide resin swelling system                according to the designed proportion, and taking                the aqueous carrier resin from at least one of                polyvinyl alcohol, water-soluble epoxy resin,                aqueous polyurethane, and aqueous acrylic resin                according to the designed proportion, where the                mass ratio of supercritical carbon dioxide to resin                is 1:0.05-0.2, (ii) opening a inlet port of the                carbon dioxide heater and the exhaust gas hole of                the fluidized bed host, loading into graphene,                driving graphene under the airflow, agglomerating                graphene, incompletely exfoliating the graphene                powder under the impact of airflow, completely                dispersing in the air flow in the fluidized bed,                turning on a spraying device in the fluidized bed,                spraying the supercritical carbon dioxide resin                solution into the graphene fluid through a high                pressure nozzle, electrostatically atomizing,                quickly dispersing the resin solution into fine                droplets under the strong impact force and the                electrostatic interaction, quickly covering the                graphene surface, where the carbon dioxide in the                droplets changes suddenly due to the pressure,                subjecting to rapid gasification, uniformly coating                the remaining resin on the surface of the graphene                to obtain graphene composite particles, connecting                the inlet and exhaust holes of the fluidized bed                device with the carbon dioxide device, completely                recycling the carbon dioxide gas, where the inlet                pressure is 0.5-3 MPa, and the exhaust pressure is                0.2-2 MPa, and (iii) uniformly dispersing 22-28                pts. wt. water, 4-10 pts. wt. graphite composite                particle, 0.2-0.5 pts. wt. dispersant, 1-4 pts. wt.                antifoaming agent, 1.5-2.5 pts. wt. propanediol,                30-40 pts. wt. inorganic filler, 19-25 pts. wt.                resin and 1-3 pts. wt. flatting agent into a                viscolizer, where the dispersant is tributyl                phosphate, primary amine, secondary amine or                tertiary amine, the antifoaming agent is styrenic                block copolymer, the inorganic filler is talc                powder, mica powder, light calcium carbonate,                calcined kaolin, titanium oxide, zinc oxide or                white carbon, the resin is polyvinyl alcohol,                water-soluble epoxy resin, aqueous polyurethane or                aqueous acrylic resin, and the flatting agent is                acrylic acid flatting agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  pressure of the supercritical carbon dioxide is                  7.3-10 MPa, and the temperature is 32-60 degrees                  C. The viscosity of the solution is 20-200 cp. In                  the step (ii), the graphene of is a multi-layered                  or single-layered graphene material with less                  than ten layers produced by a general process.                  The function of air heater comprises maintaining                  the temperature of the fluidized bed at 20-60                  degrees C, avoiding rapid gasification of                  supercritical carbon dioxide, reducing the                  temperature in the fluidized bed, and affecting                  the gasification rate, which affects the coating                  quality. The pressure of high pressure nozzle is                  7-10 MPa; the electrostatic device voltage in                  electrostatic atomization is 2000-6000 V. In the                  preparation of graphene composite particles                  comprises electrostatically charging the                  discharged solution, spraying, thinning, and                  uniformly coating the solution on the surface of                  the graphene.

   ZHANG Z
   WU J
AE ZHEJIANG SHUMA TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202276468L
AB    NOVELTY - Porous liquid wax has micropore. The porosity                of micropore is 80%.
   USE - Porous of liquid wax used for preparing porous                liquid wax used for applying liquid wax on yarn                surface to make it adhere and dip into the inside                of the yarn, to reach the purpose of soft yarn,                increasing the yarn and antistatic property and                attaching the hairiness.
   ADVANTAGE - The porous of liquid wax ensures that the                porosity of the generated porous liquid wax reaches                80%, and has a simple structure, and ensured the                full reaction of the liquid wax and carbon dioxide                gas through the arrangement of the multistage                liquid wax reaction tanks, so that the porosity of                the generated porous liquid wax is ensured to reach                80%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   1. a device for preparing porous liquid wax,                which comprises: a carbon dioxide gas tank (1),                where the carbon dioxide gas tank is communicated                with the gas preheater (2) through a pipeline, the                gas preheater is communicated with the gas booster                pump (3) through a pipeline, the gas booster pump                is communicated with the first liquid wax reaction                tank (5) and the second liquid wax reaction tank                (6) through the gas heater (4), respectively, the                first liquid wax reaction tank is communicated with                the second liquid wax reaction tank through a                pipeline, the second liquid wax reaction tank is                communicated with the sizing device (7) through a                pipeline; and
   2. a method for preparing porous liquid wax                using device, which involves: (a) putting the solid                wax into the first liquid wax reaction tank,                heating to melt, keeping the temperature at                70-80degrees Celsius;
   (b) preheating, pressurizing the carbon                dioxide gas flowing out in the carbon dioxide gas                tank;(c) pressurizing the preheated carbon dioxide                gas through the gas booster pump;
   (d) heating the pressurized thermal carbon                oxide gas to 70-80degrees Celsius; and
   (e) passing the heated high-pressure carbon                dioxide gas into the first liquid wax reaction tank                (5) and the second liquid wax reaction tank (6)                respectively.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                device for preparing porous of liquid wax.

   Second liquid wax reaction tank (6)
   Sizing device (7)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The carbon dioxide gas                  flowing out of the carbon dioxide gas tank (1) is                  preheated to 40-50degrees Celsius. The gas                  booster pump (3) boosts the preheated carbon                  dioxide gas to 40 megapascal. The flow rate of                  carbon dioxide is 40 g/minutes. The high-pressure                  carbon dioxide gas is first introduced into the                  first liquid wax reaction tank (5) for a reaction                  time of 60 minutes, and the reaction time for                  introduction into the second liquid wax reaction                  tank (6) is 30 minutes.

argon module, intended for storage and supply of argon gas, containing an automatic ramp with argon cylinders; control unit as part of the oxygen flow regulator, argon flow regulator, discharge electromagnetic valve, pressure sensor and gas mixer; gas control unit with an oxygen gas analyzer operating in a medium with an increased argon content of up to 50% by volume, with a gas analyzer for carbon dioxide operating in a medium with an increased argon content of up to 50% by volume, with a gas analyzer for argon with a control range of 0-50% by volume; automatic control unit; the connection of the oxygen module, the argon module, the carbon dioxide module with the control unit, the carbon dioxide module with the sealed object, and the control unit with the sealed object is carried out with the help of gas lines; while the gas control unit, the control unit, the oxygen module control panel, and the control panel of the carbon dioxide module are electrically connected to the information management control unit.

TI Catalytic production of methanol and/or its            derivatives dimethyl ether, ethene or propene from gas            mixture comprising at least hydrogen and carbon            monoxide and/or carbon dioxide in at least one reactor,            by bringing gas mixture, separating reaction            products.
AU SCHILDHAUER T J
   MOIOLI E
AE SCHERRER INST PAUL (SERR-C)
   SCHERRER INST PAUL (SERR-C)
GA 2022C4036G
AB    NOVELTY - Process for catalytic production of methanol                and/or its derivatives dimethyl ether, ethene or                propene from a gas mixture comprising at least                hydrogen and carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide                in at least one reactor, involves (i) bringing the                gas mixture at a reaction temperature of                180-360&#176; C and a pressure smaller than 50                bar at gas flow conditions within the at least one                reactor into contact with a catalyst and/or a solid                sorbent particles, such as molecular sieves like                zeolite, alumina, silica, silicate, and/or carbon                material for the generation of at least water and                methanol and absorbing water and/or methanol and/or                derivative molecules on solid sorbent particles                removal of water and/or methanol and/or derivative                molecules from the flow of the gas mixture, (ii)                separating reaction products in the reactor close                to the reaction temperature by extracting the                particles of the solid sorbent from the gas flow,                and (iii) operating at least one of the reactors as                fluidized bed reactor.
   USE - The process is used for catalytic production                of methanol and/or its derivatives dimethyl ether,                ethene or propene from gas mixture.
   ADVANTAGE - None given.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Process for catalytic production of methanol                and/or its derivatives dimethyl ether, ethene or                propene from a gas mixture comprising at least                hydrogen and carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide                in at least one reactor, involves (i) bringing the                gas mixture at a reaction temperature of                180-360&#176; C and a pressure smaller than 50                bar at gas flow conditions within the at least one                reactor into contact with a catalyst and/or a solid                sorbent particles, such as molecular sieves like                zeolite, alumina, silica, silicate, and/or carbon                material for the generation of at least water and                methanol and absorbing water and/or methanol and/or                derivative molecules on the solid sorbent particles                in order to achieve or intermediate partial removal                of water and/or methanol and/or derivative                molecules from the flow of the gas mixture, (ii)                separating at least partially at least one of the                reaction products in the reactor or between a                number of subsequent reactors at a temperature                close to the reaction temperature by extracting the                particles of the solid sorbent from the gas flow,                and (iii) operating at least one of the reactors as                fluidized bed reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves                  providing at least one further line for the flow                  of the gas mixture to allow switchable operation                  for un interrupted generation of methanol and/or                  its derivatives dimethyl ether, ethene or propene                  and an alternating regeneration of the solid                  sorbent particles. The method involves                  introducing a second catalyst or catalytic                  functionality to the reactor to enable follow-up                  reactions of methanol and other reaction                  products, converting the remaining reactants such                  as carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and/or                  hydrogen after the methanol production to methane                  and/or , and converting other hydrocarbons in an                  appropriate reactor, using a catalyst containing                  nickel, ruthenium, iron or cobalt. The another                  gas stream is mixed with the reactants carbon                  monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen remaining                  after methanol production before conversion to                  electricity and heat in the suitable device.                  Preferred Conditions: The regeneration of the                  solid sorbent particles is conducted within at                  least one of the reactor(s) by a temperature                  increase and/or an addition of dry gases. The                  regeneration of the solid sorbent particles is                  conducted in the upper part of the reactor(s) by                  a temperature increase and/or an addition of dry                  gases. The regenerated solid sorbent particles                  are fed back from the upper part of the reactor                  to the reactive bed in the lower part by means of                  a cyclone. The regeneration of the solid sorbent                  particles is conducted outside the reactor(s) by                  a temperature increase and/or a pressure decrease                  and/or addition of dry gases. The heat of the                  methanol reaction in one production line is at                  least partially used for the regeneration of the                  solid sorbent in the other line(s). The                  combination of separation methods, such as the                  size and specific weight of solid sorbents                  particles, fluids with low vapor pressure, with                  different properties is applied to at least                  partly enrich the materials used in this                  separation method during ad-/absorption with the                  reaction products. The water and/or methanol or                  other compounds are at least partially removed                  from the fluidized bed reactor by combined use of                  larger and/or more dense solid sorbent particles                  which are separated by gravity from the reactor                  and fine and/or less dense solid sorbent                  particles, which are entrained from the reactor                  or floating on the bed of the reactor. The water                  and/or methanol or other compounand mixing the                  another gas stream with the reactants such as                  remaining after the methanol production before                  the conversion to methane and/or other                  hydrocarbons in an appropriate reactor and are at                  least partially removed from the fluidized bed                  reactor by soaking structures containing or                  consisting of solid sorbent particles. The mixing                  the another gas stream with the reactants such as                  remaining after the methanol production before                  the conversion to methane and/or other                  hydrocarbons in an appropriate reactor. The                  method is used for methanol production before                  conversion to electricity and heat in the                  suitable device. Preferred Components: The                  reactor is operated with catalysts based on                  metals such as copper, indium, iridium, silver,                  palladium, platinum, zinc, gallium, or their                  oxides supported on aluminum oxide, silicon                  dioxide, titanium oxide, cerium oxide, zirconium                  oxide, carbides, nitrides, phosphides or a                  mixture thereof or a perovskite type material                  containing one of the mentioned elements. The                  feed stream comprises carbon dioxide, biogas, gas                  from fermentations or sewage sludge digestion or                  producer gas from gasification or pyrolysis. The                  feed stream comprises carbon dioxide, biogas, gas                  from fermentations or sewage sludge digestion or                  producer gas from gasification or pyrolysis. The                  feed stream comprises carbon dioxide, biogas, gas                  from fermentations or sewage sludge digestion or                  producer gas from gasification or pyrolysis. The                  method is used for methanol production before                  conversion to electricity and heat in the                  suitable device. Preferred Composition: The                  carbon dioxide and hydrogen ratio or carbon                  monoxide and hydrogen ratio in the feed stream is                  different from the stoichiometric ratio of the                  methanol synthesis reaction wherein an excess of                  reactant, not converted in the reactor(s), is at                  least partly recycled and mixed with the feed                  stream. The process according to claim 15 wherein                  hydrogen is fed in stoichiometric excess.

PT P
PN DE102020129303-A1; WO2022096592-A1
TI Production of hydrocarbons e.g. ethylene, involves feeding carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide with hydrogen in process gas stream, partially converting to oxygenates, converting oxygenates to hydrocarbons, and passing over into gas stream.
AU MEISWINKEL A
   ZANDER H
AE LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
GA 202265146R
AB    NOVELTY - Production (100) of one or more hydrocarbons involves feeding carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide with hydrogen in a process gas stream (1) to a reactor arrangement, at least partially converting in a reaction step (S1) to one or more oxygenates, passing into the process gas stream, converting the one or more oxygenates in the process gas stream at least in portion in a reaction step (S2) to one or more hydrocarbons, and passing over into the process gas stream. The process gas stream is guided through the reactor arrangement in one direction of flow. The reactor arrangement comprises one or more reactors (10) which comprise a reaction zone (11) and a reaction zone (12). The reaction zone (12) is arranged in the flow direction after the reaction zone (11), and the reaction zone (11) and the reaction zone (12) are equipped with catalysts in such a way that the reaction step (S1) and (S2) are catalyzed in the reaction zone (11).
   USE - Production of hydrocarbons e.g. ethylene and propylene (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - None given.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production (100) of one or more hydrocarbons involves feeding carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide with hydrogen in a process gas stream (1) to a reactor arrangement, at least partially converting in a reaction step (S1) to one or more oxygenates, passing into the process gas stream, converting the one or more oxygenates in the process gas stream at least in portion in a reaction step (S2) to one or more hydrocarbons, and passing over into the process gas stream. The process gas stream is guided through the reactor arrangement in one direction of flow. The reactor arrangement comprises one or more reactors (10) which comprise a reaction zone (11) and a reaction zone (12). The reaction zone (12) is arranged in the flow direction after the reaction zone (11), and the reaction zone (11) and the reaction zone (12) are equipped with catalysts in such a way that the reaction step (S1) and (S2) are catalyzed in the reaction zone (11). The reaction step (S2) is catalyzed in the reaction zone (12), and that in the reaction zone (12) the reaction step (S1) is not catalyzed or to a lesser extent than in the reaction zone (11).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view illustrating production of hydrocarbons.
   Gas stream (1)
   Reactors (10)
   Fixed catalyst beds (11a, 11b, 11c, 12a)
   Reaction zones (11,12)
   Production of hydrocarbons (100)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reaction zone (11) is provided with one or more catalysts (C1) which catalyzes or catalyzes the reaction step (S1) and one or more catalysts (C2) which catalyses or catalyzes the reaction step (S2). The reaction zone (11) with one or more bifunctional catalyst catalyzes the reaction step (S1) and (S2). The fixed catalyst beds (11a, 11b, 11c, 12a) are used in the reaction zone (11) and in the reaction zone (12). The reactor or reactors is or are designed as a tube bundle reactor, which is or are cooled using a cooling medium that is carried in co-current or counter-current with the process gas stream. The reaction zone (11) comprises catalyst beds (11a, 11b, 11c) which are arranged one behind the other in the direction of flow and different catalysts or one catalyst with different activity. Catalyst-free inert zones is or are formed in the reactor and one or more further reaction zones is or are arranged upstream of the reaction zone (11), which contains or contain one or more catalysts, which or which at least one further reaction, in particular a water gas shift reaction and/or the formation of methanol and/or dimethyl ether as interludes diet, catalyzed or catalyze. The oxygenates comprise methanol and/or dimethyl ether and hydrocarbons comprise ethylene and/or propylene. The process gas stream further comprises methane and/or higher hydrocarbons. Preferred Conditions: The method is carried out at a pressure level of 10-100 bar and temperature level of 150-580 degrees C. Preferred Property: The process gas stream is fed to the reactor arrangement with a stoichiometric modulus of 1.5-10.

PT P
PN CN114088873-A; CN216525633-U
TI Device for testing dynamic fireproof performance of tire of automobile, has high temperature electronic scale that is fixed at bottom part of box housing and connected with computer through data line, and K-type thermocouple that is provided with power supply line and electrode.
AU CHONG Q
   SONG P
   CHENG N
   LIU X
   YIN N
   SONG H
   SUN C
   WANG J
   LV B
   ZHAO X
AE QINGDAO DOUBLESTAR TIRE IND CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 202236238H
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a box body (1) whose side wall is provided with an air flow cover. A fireproof door (2) is fixed on a side of the box body. An upper part of the box body is provided with a door handle. A carbon dioxide storage tank (13) is arranged at an outer side of the box body. A pipe piece is provided with a pressure gauge (12) and a flow switch (11). A ventilation window (4) is arranged on a side wall of the box body (1) close to a bottom part. A smoke sensor is provided on an inclined upper unit of an air flow bonnet. A K-type thermocouple is connected with a plug that comprises a power supply line and a second electrode. The second electrode comprises a convex hemispherical electrode and an annular electrode. A filter screen is arranged on an inner side wall of a bottom side of a filter screen and the ventilation window.
   USE - Device for testing dynamic fireproof performance of a tire of an automobile.
   ADVANTAGE - The test device of tire fireproof performance is provided with a high video camera and a K-type thermocouple to simulate the heating condition of each part of the tire operation, so that test data is accurate. The gas in the tire is quickly discharged, when the pressure is greater than the set pressure, and the electromagnetic switch controls to open the annular sealing port, so that the explosion can be prevented, and the safety test can be ensured. The tire static fire-proof performance test is the fire resistance test of the rubber material. The device can open the carbon dioxide storage tank after detecting, for extinguishing the internal flame, thus ensuring the safety.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for testing dynamic fireproof performance of a tire of an automobile.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a device for testing dynamic fireproof performance of a tire of an automobile.
   Box body (1)
   Fireproof door (2)
   High temperature electronic scale (3)
   Ventilation window (4)
   Visible window (5)
   Measuring window (6)
   High definition video camera (7)
   Infrared temperature measuring instrument (8)
   Telescopic electric heating ring device (9)

PN CN113694929-A
TI Supported single-atom copper-based metal oxide catalyst used for low-temperature production of methanol by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, comprises copper, zirconium and zinc, copper presents a cationic state in the catalyst and maintains a monodisperse state.
AU ZHAO H
   TAN L
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 2021E2177V
AB    NOVELTY - Supported single-atom copper-based metal oxide catalyst comprises 0.5-21 mass% copper, 70-95 mass% zirconium and 0-21 mass%zinc, copper presents a cationic state in the catalyst and maintains a monodisperse state
   USE - Supported single-atom copper-based metal oxide catalyst used for low-temperature production of methanol by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide (Claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method of supported single-atom copper-based metal oxide catalyst is simple and easy to implement, safe and high in repeatability, active metal copper species in the obtained catalyst exist in a monodispersed state, strong interaction exists between the active metal copper species and a carrier, and the loading amount is synthesized to be up to 15 wt.% by changing the proportion of metal input% of monatomic copper catalyst. The monatomic catalyst contains unique Copper1Oxygen3 active site reduces carbon dioxide into methanol at low temperature with high selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for method for preparing a supported monoatomic copper-based metal oxide catalyst involves weighing the corresponding amount of soluble nitrate and dissolving it in deionized water, according to the conditions that the molar ratio of copper/zirconium (Cu/Zr) is 0.01-0.4, the molar ratio of zinc/zirconium (Zn/Zr) is 0-0.6, and the total concentration of metal ions is 0.2-0.4 M, clear and transparent solution, record as solution A, weighing anhydrous sodium carbonate in deionized water, stirring until a clear solution is formed to prepare a 0.3-0.7 M solution, record as solution B, weighing 80-150 ml deionized water, stirring and heating to 50-90 degrees Celsius, record as solution C. Under the condition that the precipitation temperature is 50-90 degrees Celsius, adding solutions A and B to solution C simultaneously at a rate of 0.1-0.9 milliliter/minute through a peristaltic pump, and continue stirring at the precipitation temperature after the addition is complete 0.5-1 hours, then turn off the stirring and age for 1-5 hours at the precipitation temperature. Washing the precipitate to neutrality by in-situ slow washing method, filtering by suction, then drying at the precipitation temperature for 20-40 hours, calcining at 300-500 degrees Celsius for 3-7 hours, and cooling to room temperature, granulation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Specific operation of washing to neutrality by in-situ slow washing method involves adding the precipitate to containing 1.5 litre of deionized water at 80 degreesC, stirring quickly for 0.5 hours, then let it stand and discarding the supernatant, repeating the above steps until the pH is adjusted to be neutral.

PN CN113651804-A
TI Preparing cynaropicrin involves crushing artichoke leaves, extracting by carbon dioxide supercritical extraction, adding protective agent to extract residue, adding organic solvent, extracting, concentrating, adding petroleum ether, filtering, crystallizing, centrifuging, rinsing and drying.
AU WEI B
   FAN Y
   DU M
   WEI Y
   JI Y
   ZHANG Y
AE SHAANXI JIAHE BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 2021D9327C
AB    NOVELTY - Industrialized method for preparing cynaropicrin involves picking the fresh leaves of artichoke, drying, crushing, and extracting by carbon dioxide supercritical extraction method to obtain extraction residue, (b) (b1) adding a protective agent to the extraction residue to protect the cynaropicrin from being oxidized, (b2) adding an organic solvent to the solution for multiple extractions at room temperature, and collecting the extracts after extractions, and concentrating into an extract, (c) (c1) adding petroleum ether or n-hexane to the concentrated extract, stirring, filtering and collecting the filter residue, (c2) adding 30-50 %mass methanol, ethanol or isopropanol to the filter residue, stirring, filtering, and collecting the filter residue, and (d) adding ethanol to the filter residue collected, heating to dissolve, filtering, concentrating, placing for crystallization, centrifuging, rinsing and drying to obtain the finished product.
   USE - The industrialized method is used for preparing cynaropicrin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids the use of resin column separation, involves simple purification and refining process, is easy to operate, reduces production cycle, ensures high recovery rate and strong operability, and is suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Industrialized method for preparing cynaropicrin involves picking the fresh leaves of artichoke, drying, crushing and extracting by carbon dioxide supercritical extraction method to obtain extraction residue, (b) (b1) adding a protective agent to the extraction residue to protect the cynaropicrin from being oxidized, (b2) adding an organic solvent to the solution in step (b1) for multiple extractions at room temperature, and collecting the extracts after multiple extractions, and concentrating into an extract, where the organic solvent comprises pure ethyl acetate, a mixed solvent of ethyl acetate and petroleum ether, or a mixed solvent of ethyl acetate and n-hexane, and the amount of the organic solvent is 5-8 times the mass of the extraction residue obtained in step (a), (c) (c1) adding petroleum ether or n-hexane to the concentrated extract in step (b2), stirring, filtering, and collecting the filter residue, (c2) adding 30-50 %mass methanol, ethanol or isopropanol to the filter residue collected in step (c1), stirring, filtering, and collecting the filter residue, and (d) adding ethanol to the filter residue collected in step (c2), heating to dissolve, filtering, concentrating the filtrate to a minimum amount, placing for crystallization, centrifuging, and adding a small amount of ethanol to the solid, rinsing and drying to obtain the finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In step (a), the extraction pressure of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method is 30-45 MPa, the extraction temperature is 30-50degrees Celsius, and the flow rate of carbon dioxide is 30-50 l/hour, and the entrainer is petroleum ether or n-hexane, and the mass is 1-2 times the mass of the raw material.Preferred Components: The protective agent in step (b1) is citric acid or volatile compound, and the addition amount is 0.1-0.3 %mass of the extract residue obtained in step (a). In step (b2), the volume ratio of ethyl acetate to petroleum ether in the mixed solvent of ethyl acetate and petroleum ether is 5-10:1. The volume ratio of ethyl acetate to n-hexane in the mixed solvent of ethyl acetate and n-hexane is 5-10:1. The ratio of the volume of petroleum ether or n-hexane in step (c1) to the mass of the concentrated extract in step (b2) is 1-1.5:1. The volume of methanol, ethanol or isopropanol in step (c2) is 20-30 %mass of the concentrated extract. The concentration of ethanol in step (d) is 60-80 %mass, preferably 80 %mass, and the amount is 8-12 times, preferably 8 times the mass of the filter residue collected in step (c2), and the dissolution temperature is 60degrees Celsius.

PN CN113651762-A
TI Preparing methyl 1-methyl-1H-1,2,4-triazole-3-carboxylate comprises e.g. reacting 1,2,4 triazole and methyl chloride, protecting at 5-position by lithium reagent, reacting with carbon dioxide at 3-position, then with thionyl chloride and methanol, removing 5-position protection.
AU ZENG S
   GAO F
AE SHANGHAI JINLU PHARM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021D6279D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing methyl 1-methyl-1H-1,2,4-triazole-3-carboxylate (I) comprises (i) mixing 1,2,4-triazole (II), potassium hydroxide and ethanol, slowly adding methyl chloride, heating and refluxing to obtain 1-methyl-1,2,4-triazole (III), (ii) dissolving (III) in tetrahydrofuran and tetramethylethylenediamine (TMEDA), adding n-butyl lithium after cooling down, then adding dibromomethane to react to obtain 1-methyl-1H-(1, 2,4)triazole derivative (IV) or dissolving (III) in tetrahydrofuran, adding lithium diisopropylamide (LDA) after cooling down, and then adding trimethylchlorosilane to react to obtain (IV), (iii) mixing (IV) and tetrahydrofuran, adding LDA after cooling down, and add carbon dioxide to react to obtain 1-methyl-1H-(1,2,4)triazole-3-carboxylic acid derivative (V), (iv) mixing (V) with methanol, adding thionyl chloride drop wise and reacting.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing methyl 1-methyl-1H-1,2,4-triazole-3-carboxylate (I).
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes raw materials which are readily available, and the problems of N methyl isomerization and excessive quaternization reactions are avoided from the source.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing methyl 1-methyl-1H-1,2,4-triazole-3-carboxylate of formula (I) comprises (i) mixing 1,2,4-triazole of formula (II), potassium hydroxide and ethanol, slowly adding methyl chloride, heating and refluxing to obtain 1-methyl-1,2,4-triazole of formula (III), (ii) dissolving (III) in tetrahydrofuran and tetramethylethylenediamine (TMEDA), adding n-butyllithium after cooling down, then adding dibromomethane to react to obtain 1-methyl-1H-(1, 2,4)triazole derivative of formula (IV) or dissolving (III) in tetrahydrofuran, adding lithium diisopropylamide (LDA) after cooling down, and then adding trimethylchlorosilane to react to obtain (IV), (iii) mixing (IV) and tetrahydrofuran, adding LDA after cooling down, and add carbon dioxide to react to obtain 1-methyl-1H-(1,2,4)triazole-3-carboxylic acid derivative of formula (V), (iv) mixing (V) with methanol, adding thionyl chloride dropwise to react to obtain methyl 1-methyl-1H-(1,2,4)triazole-3-carboxylate derivative of formula (VI), (v) mixing (VI), 5% palladium on carbon, equivalent 1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene (DBU) and methanol, introducing in hydrogen to remove bromine under pressure to obtain final product; or mixing (VI) with acetic acid, adding zinc powder in batches to react to obtain final product; or reacting (VI) and tetrabutylammonium fluoride (TBAF) with 2-methyltetrahydrofuran to obtain final product.
   R = Br or trimethylchlorosilane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the molar ratio of (II), potassium hydroxide and methyl chloride is 1:1.4-1.5:1-1.5. In step (ii): the molar ratio of (III), TMEDA, n-butyllithium and dibromomethane is 1:0.20-0.25:1.15-1.20:1.35-2.35; the molar ratio of (III), LDA and trimethylchlorosilane is 1:1.10-1.20:1.20-1.30. In step (iii), the molar ratio of (IV), LDA and carbon dioxide is 1:1.10-1.20:2-7. In step (iv), the molar ratio of (V) to thionyl chloride is 1:1.20-1.30. In step (v), the molar ratio of (VI) to DBU is 1:1-1.05, and the hydrogenation pressure is 0.1-0.3 Mpa, and the temperature is 25-35degrees Celsius; the molar ratio of (VI) to zinc powder is 1:1.50-1.65, and the temperature is 15-35degrees Celsius; the molar ratio of (VI) to TBAF is 1:1.10-1.15, and the temperature is 0-25degrees Celsius.

TI Synthesizing 2,4 dichloro acetophenone comprises e.g. placing m-dichlorobenzene, anhydrous aluminum hydroxide and toluene into a four-necked flask, stirring mixture, pouring excess carbon dioxide into material, stirring and reacting.
AU TIAN Y
   SHI X
   MA Y
   SHEN J
   MENG X
   HU K
   SONG J
   TENG Y
   LIU L
AE JIANGSU RUIHENG NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (JIAN-Non-standard)
   JIANGSU YANGNONG CHEM GROUP CO LTD (SNCM-C)
GA 2021A75870
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing 2,4 dichloro acetophenone comprises (i) placing m-dichlorobenzene, anhydrous aluminum hydroxide and toluene into a four-necked flask, stirring the mixture, adding concentrated hydrochloric acid dropwise to the mixture, separating acidic water from the water separator, carrying water, and remove toluene under negative pressure, (ii) cooling the material to the reaction temperature, slowly adding acetyl chloride dropwise to the material through a constant pressure dropping funnel, (iii) cooling to 70-80 degrees C, and slowly dropwise adding water to the material with negative pressure, insulating and reacting for 1 hour, (iv) separated oil layer, and rectifying, and(v) slowly adjusting separated water layer to neutral with sodium hydroxide solution, pouring excess carbon dioxide into the material, vigorously stirring for reacting, filtering, and washing filter cake with water to neutrality and drying and applying to the next batch of reactions.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing 2,4 dichloro acetophenone.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: recycles catalyst components; does not produce aluminum-containing wastewater; saves costs; improves competitiveness of the product 2,4 dichloroacetophenone; and reduces emissions of three wastes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesizing 2,4 dichloro acetophenone comprises (i) placing m-dichlorobenzene, anhydrous aluminum hydroxide and toluene into a four-necked flask, stirring the mixture, adding concentrated hydrochloric acid dropwise to the mixture, and then bubbling in excess hydrogen chloride under the bubbling state of hydrogen chloride, heating the reactant, and refluxing the toluene with water using a water trap, separating acidic water from the water separator, carrying water, and remove toluene under negative pressure, (ii) cooling the material to the reaction temperature, slowly adding acetyl chloride dropwise to the material through a constant pressure dropping funnel, adding the acetyl chloride, insulating for 0.5 hour, slowly heating up the material to 105 degrees C, insulating for 3 hours, (iii) cooling to 70-80 degrees C, and slowly dropwise adding water to the material with negative pressure, insulating and reacting for 1 hour, then cooling, transferring the materials to the separating funnel and allowing to stand for layering, (iv) separated oil layer, and rectifying to obtain 2,4 dichloro acetophenone finished product, and(v) slowly adjusting separated water layer to neutral with sodium hydroxide solution, pouring excess carbon dioxide into the material, vigorously stirring for reacting, filtering, and washing filter cake with water to neutrality and drying to obtain anhydrous aluminum hydroxide and applying to the next batch of reactions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the molar ratio of dichlorobenzene and anhydrous aluminum hydroxide is 1:1.05-1.15. In the step (ii), the molar ratio of intermediate dichlorobenzene and acetyl chloride is 1:1.02-1.05. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the reaction temperature is 40-60 degrees C. In the step (i), the amount of concentrated hydrochloric acid added dropwise is 1-10 wt.% of the amount of anhydrous aluminum hydroxide.

PN WO2020260170-A1; GB2585014-A; CA3144288-A1; EP3986116-A1; CN114364252-A; US2022264809-A1
TI Greenhouse has climate control chamber located            relative to crop space so that air inlet opening and            air outlet opening are distributed across crop space            such that chamber both receives and delivers air from            and to crop space, respectively.
AU OLAFSSON H P
   TIELEMAN A N
AE GENTLE GREEN BV (GENT-Non-standard)
   GENTLE GREEN BV (GENT-Non-standard)
   GENTLE GREEN BV (GENT-Non-standard)
GA 202101289J
AB    NOVELTY - The greenhouse (1) has an airtight enclosure                comprising a crop space (12) and climate control                chambers (13) to control a climate in the crop                space. The climate control chamber has an air inlet                opening, an air outlet opening through which an                interior of the chamber is in fluid communication                with the crop space, an air displacement unit to                create an air flow from the crop space to the                interior of the chamber through the air inlet                opening, and from the interior of the chamber to                the crop space through the air outlet opening, and                an air conditioning unit to condition air in the                interior of the chamber such that a conditioned                climate is created in the interior of the chamber.                The climate control chamber is located relative to                the crop space such that the air inlet opening and                air outlet opening are distributed across the crop                space such that the chamber both receives and                delivers air from and to the crop space,                respectively, locally at locations distributed                across the crop space.
   USE - Greenhouse such as building structure for                covered growth of all kinds of plants, such as                vegetables, fruits, potatoes, pot plants, bedding                plants, cut flowers, ornamentals, herbs, medicinal                Cannabis, flowers and other types of organisms like                algae and mushrooms.
   ADVANTAGE - Since the enclosure is airtight, heat, cold                and water from the crop space are kept as much as                possible inside the greenhouse, which allows for                optimizing the climate in the crop space while                using a minimum amount of energy. Since the                enclosure is airtight, the ingress of contaminants                such as particulate matter, insects, bacteria,                viruses and other microbes, such as molds, fungi,                viroids may be prevented, or at least reduced,                which presents a significant step forward in fully                sustainable crop protection and in crop and human                health through protecting the crop and humans from                insects, bacteria and/or viruses and other microbes                in the interior space of the greenhouse. Carbon                dioxide is used much more efficiently as compared                to fully vented greenhouses, specifically, only                carbon dioxide used by the plants plus the very                limited volume of carbon dioxide that is lost due                to limited air refreshment have to be added to the                interior of the greenhouse.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side view of a vertical                cross-section of the greenhouse.
   Greenhouse (1)
   Crop space (12)
   Climate control chambers (13)
   Inflow fan coil (18)
   Outflow fan coil (19)

PN CN112126797-A
TI Preparation of high-purity aluminum by crushing bauxite ore, mixing with sodium hydroxide solution, filtering solid-liquid mixture, removing filter residue, introducing carbon dioxide, hydrolyzing, drying, calcining to obtain aluminum oxide.
AU WEI J
   ZHANG X
AE HONHUA TECHNOLOGY INC (HONH-Non-standard)
GA 2021026573
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of high-purity aluminum involves taking 2 mole/L sodium hydroxide solution and bauxite ore, crushing bauxite ore to 100 meshes particles, mixing with 2 mol/L sodium hydroxide solution to prepare sodium aluminate Na(Al(OH)4) (tetrahydroxy) sodium aluminate, filtering solid-liquid mixture, removing filter residue, introducing carbon dioxide to filtrate to make sodium aluminate (Na(Al(OH)4)) (tetrahydroxy) sodium aluminate hydrolysis to produce aluminum carbonate precipitate, filtering and separating aluminum carbonate precipitate, drying and calcining aluminum carbonate precipitate to decompose to obtain aluminum oxide, co-placing molten cryolite sodium hexafluoroaluminate and aluminum carbonate in helium gas protection environment, heating to 620 degrees C and electrolyzing to obtain refined aluminum.
   USE - Method for preparation of high-purity aluminum.
   ADVANTAGE - Compared with the traditional cooling from the periphery to the center, the segregation method of impurity to the center is separated out, the waste of the aluminum material is less; and the flat is paved at the bottom of the vacuum smelting furnace for condensing, the processing efficiency is greatly improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of high-purity aluminum involves taking 2 mole/L sodium hydroxide solution and bauxite ore, crushing bauxite ore to 100 meshes particles, mixing with 2 mol/L sodium hydroxide solution to prepare sodium aluminate Na(Al(OH)4) (tetrahydroxy) sodium aluminate, filtering solid-liquid mixture, removing filter residue, introducing carbon dioxide to filtrate to make sodium aluminate (Na(Al(OH)4)) (tetrahydroxy) sodium aluminate hydrolysis to produce aluminum carbonate precipitate, filtering and separating aluminum carbonate precipitate, drying and calcining aluminum carbonate precipitate to decompose to obtain aluminum oxide, co-placing molten cryolite sodium hexafluoroaluminate and aluminum carbonate in helium gas protection environment, heating to 620 degrees C and electrolyzing to obtain refined aluminum, putting refined aluminum into quartz boat and putting into vacuum smelting furnace and heating to 680 degrees C, pouring molten fine aluminum in quartz boat on top end of tower plate and slowly flowing downwards along tower plate for 10 minutes, continuously cooling molten aluminum at 0.9 degrees C/minute when flowing on tower plate, cooling middle area of bottom of vacuum smelting furnace to 659 degrees C, keeping surrounding edge area at 662 degrees C, cooling molten refined aluminum by tower plate and falling to middle area of bottom of vacuum smelting furnace to be solidified, and taking out aluminum block solidified in middle area and putting into quartz boat after cold rolling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The un-solidified residual liquid is discharged by the discharging valve opened on the edge of the vacuum smelting furnace, and argon gas is continuously introduced to protect the solidified aluminum in the vacuum smelting furnace and avoids the oxidation.
DC M28 (Electrolytic and electrothermic production and refining of non-ferrous metals - excluding heat treatment (C25).); M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)

PN CN111286220-A
TI Producing soft carbon black involves using exhaust gas from which the soft carbon black is separated, which comprises carbon monoxide, hydrogen, carbon dioxide, hydrogen Sulfide, nitrogen gas and water and pressurizing by the exhaust gas.
AU CHEN B
   YELKEN A
AE XINJIANG JUNTHIN CHEM CO LTD (XINJ-Non-standard)
GA 202056478T
AB    NOVELTY - Producing soft carbon black involves using exhaust gas from which the soft carbon black is separated, where soft carbon black comprises 8-9 wt.% carbon monoxide, 10-12 wt.% hydrogen, 2.2 wt.% carbon dioxide, 0.03-0.05 wt.% hydrogen sulfide, and 0.2-0.8 wt.% HmCn, 36-39 wt.% nitrogen content and 38-40 wt.% water and pressurizing by the exhaust gas pressurized fan, where the pressure reaches 0.7-1.8 kilopascal. The pressurized exhaust gas is sent to the exhaust gas combustion furnace through the transmission line and mixed with the air supplied by the air blower in the ratio of 0.44-0.65:1 and burned. The combustion brings the temperature of the exhaust gas furnace to 900-1200 degrees C, and the resulting hot gas flow enters the tube heat exchanger again. The high-temperature hot air is flowed through the tube side, and the process air is moved through the shell side through heat exchange.
   USE - Method for producing soft carbon black.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce soft carbon black that solves the problem of traditional soft carbon black production cannot increase the air preheat temperature due to easy wall hanging and clogging, and reduces the production cost of soft carbon black by 3-5%, and reduces the environmental pressure of direct exhaust emission.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing soft carbon black involves using exhaust gas from which the soft carbon black is separated, where soft carbon black comprises 8-9 wt.% carbon monoxide, 10-12 wt.% hydrogen, 2.2 wt.% carbon dioxide, 0.03-0.05 wt.% hydrogen Sulfide, and 0.2-0.8 wt.% HmCn, 36-39 wt.% nitrogen N2 content and 38-40 wt.% water and pressurizing by the exhaust gas pressurized fan, where the pressure reaches 0.7-1.8 kilopascal. The pressurized exhaust gas is sent to the exhaust gas combustion furnace through the transmission line and mixed with the air supplied by the air blower in the ratio of 0.44-0.65:1 and burned. The combustion brings the temperature of the exhaust gas furnace to 900-1200 degrees C, and the resulting hot gas flow enters the tube heat exchanger again. The high-temperature hot air is flowed through the tube side, and the process air is moved through the shell side through heat exchange. The air temperature is raised to 300-550 degrees C, re-entered into the carbon black reaction furnace, and the residual fuel gas after heat exchange is emptied after treatment.

PN CN111227215-A
TI Preparing residue-free (or less residue) brine material by extracting spice oleoresin (or essential oil), compounding on basis of quantitative analysis, and combining modern flavoring technology of edible essence and spice.
AU HUANG Q
AE GUANGZHOU CHILLIHOT FOOD INGREDIENTS CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202052691P
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing residue-free (or less residue) brine material involves extracting the spice oleoresin (or essential oil) by using the extraction technologies of a traditional solvent method, a supercritical carbon dioxide method, and a subcritical method, compounding according to a certain proportion on the basis of quantitative analysis, and then blending the flavor by combining the modern flavoring technology of edible essence and spice. The method involves (a) performing quantitative analysis on effective characteristic components of various spices, (b) pulverizing various natural edible spices, and extracting aromatic oleoresin or essential oil from the spices, (c) compounding the product A into compound spice oleoresin (or essential oil) according to a certain proportion on the basis of quantitative analysis according to different requirements to obtain a product B, and (d) blending flavor.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing residue-free (or less residue) brine material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has stable and controllable brine effect and simple process method, and is easy for mass industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing residue-free (or less residue) brine material involves extracting the spice oleoresin (or essential oil) by using the extraction technologies of a traditional solvent method, a supercritical carbon dioxide method, and a subcritical method, and compounding according to a certain proportion on the basis of quantitative analysis, and then blending the flavor by combining the modern flavoring technology of edible essence and spice. The method involves (a) performing quantitative analysis on effective characteristic components of various spices, (b) pulverizing various natural edible spices, and extracting aromatic oleoresin or essential oil from the spices by conventional solvent method, supercritical carbon dioxide method, and subcritical method to obtain product A, (c) compounding the product A into compound spice oleoresin (or essential oil) according to a certain proportion on the basis of quantitative analysis according to different requirements to obtain a product B, and (d) compounding product B, sugar, thermally-reacted pork extract, thermally-reacted cow paste, thermally-reacted chicken extract, yeast extract, salt, flavoring agent, and edible essence at a certain ratio to obtain product C (i.e. brine material). The sugar comprises monosaccharides, disaccharides, and polysaccharides. The flavor enhancer comprises monosodium glutamate, disodium inosinate and disodium guanylate, and polypeptide. The edible essence and spice (which refers to various food essences and spices allowed to be used in GB2760 national food safety standard food additive use standard) mainly comprises various meat essences and sauce-stewed essences.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The seasoning comprises 90-110 pts. wt. salad oil, 20-25 pts. wt. water, 10-12 pts. wt. liquor, 10-12 pts. wt. garlic, 8-10 pts. wt. ginger, 18-25 pts. wt. salt, 5-8 pts. wt. white granulated sugar, 15-20 pts. wt. monosodium glutamate, 4-5 pts. wt. chicken flavoring agent, 0.8-1 pts. wt. In the step (i), the garlic is temporarily stored in vegetable oil after being chopped. In the step (v), the filling method is vacuum filling. In the step (i), the storage is carried out in a light-shielded environment at 0-4 degrees C. In the step (ii), the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is accompanied by steam distillation extraction and mixing with thirteen essential oil extracted by supercritical carbon dioxide.

PN CN110317769-A; CN110317769-B
TI Cultivating selenium-enriched microalgae comprises e.g. preparing culture solution, adjusting pH of culture solution, pressurizing by air and maintaining the compression, accepting microalgae strains and culture and adding sodium selenite.
AU ZHANG X
AE ENSHI XISHENG PLANT TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ENSH-Non-standard)
   ENSHI XISHENG PLANT TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ENSH-Non-standard)
GA 2019873220
AB    NOVELTY - Cultivating selenium-enriched microalgae comprises (i) preparing the culture solution using sodium hydrogencarbonate, disodium hydrogen phosphate, ferric ammonium citrate, calcium chloride, magnesium sulfate, boric acid, manganese chloride, zinc sulfate and copper sulfate, adjusting pH of culture solution to 5.5-5.9, pressurizing by air and maintaining the compression; (iii) accepting microalgae strains and culture; (iv) adding sodium selenite, culturing by air pressure and daytime pressure control is 4.6-5.2 Mpa, culturing under natural light conditions during day, the proportion of pressurized air at night is oxygen: carbon dioxide: sulfur dioxide: nitrogen is 3.0:6.3:0.3:0.4 and the dark time is 12-14 hours/day and controlling the night pressure at 3-3.4 Mpa; (v) washing and filtering after removing the microalgae.
   USE - The method is useful for cultivating selenium-enriched microalgae.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is easy to operate, rich in nutrition, shortens the operation, rapidly grows microalgae, has strong selenium-enriching ability, selenium content is 1200-2400ppm, the protein content is over 46% and the nutritional value is rich.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cultivating selenium-enriched microalgae comprises (i) preparing the culture solution using 21-34 mg/l sodium hydrogencarbonate, 67-74 mg/l disodium hydrogen phosphate, 3-7 mg/l ferric ammonium citrate, 180-240 mg/l calcium chloride, 60-90 mg/l magnesium sulfate, 1.5-2 mg/l boric acid, 1.3-3 mg/l manganese chloride, 0.2-0.5 mg/l zinc sulfate and 0.05-0.1 mg/l copper sulfate; (ii) adjusting the pH of the culture solution prepared in step (i) to 5.5-5.9, pressurizing to 9.5-10.5 MPa by air and maintaining the compression for 20-40 minutes; (iii) accepting microalgae strains and culture; (iv) adding sodium selenite, culturing by air pressure, and the air pressure is specifically includes the proportion of pressurized air during the day is oxygen: carbon dioxide: sulfur dioxide: nitrogen is 4.0:5.4:0.4:0.2, and daytime pressure control is 4.6-5.2 Mpa, culturing under natural light conditions during day, the proportion of pressurized air at night is oxygen: carbon dioxide: sulfur dioxide: nitrogen is 3.0:6.3:0.3:0.4 and the dark time is 12-14 hours/day and controlling the night pressure at 3-3.4 Mpa; (v) washing and filtering after removing the microalgae.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: In step (ii) the hydrochloric acid is added to adjust the pH. In step (iii) the microalgal strain is Chlorella and transplanted at a mass ratio of 1:1000-1:800. In step (iv) the addition ratio of sodium selenite is 60-80 mmol/l. Preferred Method: The step (iii) is cultured at 20-25 degrees C for 7-15 days under natural light conditions. In step (iv) sodium selenite is added after the microalgae strain is cultured for 7-15 days, the culture time by air compression is 15-18 days and the light intensity under natural light conditions during the day is 20-80 W. In step (v) the culture solution is filtered to remove the microalgae and the ultrapure water is ultrasonically washed for 3-5 times for 6-9 minutes each time and then filtered to obtain selenium-enriched microalgae.

PN KR1975872-B1
TI Buoy comprises plasma reaction unit comprising inlet for introducing water molecules, and plasma generator, portion coated with fluorescent material which is poly(N-phenylmaleimide), and coating composition containing polyurea coating agent.
AU YEAL K S
AE URIM ECHO LTD (URIM-Non-standard)
GA 201980816A
AB    NOVELTY - A buoy comprises a plasma reaction unit comprising an inlet for introducing water molecules, a flow path, an injection unit for converting water molecules flowing in direct communication with the flow path into water molecules and fine particles, a plasma generator, and a carbon dioxide storage unit, a portion coated with a fluorescent material that changes the fluorescent color according to the pH, at predetermined intervals on an inner wall of the plasma reaction unit, a viewing window, a heater unit, and a nozzle unit. The fluorescent material is poly(N-phenylmaleimide) synthesized using N-phenylmaleimide as a monomer. The buoy is coated with a buoy coating composition comprising lead, a polyurea coating agent, an ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer, an ultraviolet absorber, and the fluorescent material. The polyurea coating agent comprises a prepolymer, a curing agent, an antifoaming agent and an organosilicon compound.
   USE - Buoy.
   ADVANTAGE - The buoy has desired fluorescence expression, ultraviolet protection function, alkali resistance, and long-term durability, and can be manufactured by economical method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A buoy comprises a plasma reaction unit comprising an inlet for introducing water molecules, a flow path for introducing and injecting water molecules from the inlet, an injection unit for converting the water molecules flowing in direct communication with the flow path into water molecules and fine particles, provided inside the plasma reaction unit, a plasma generator for discharging water molecules and fine particles in the plasma reaction unit to generate oxygen and hydroxide plasmas, and a carbon dioxide storage unit for storing and discharging carbon dioxide for reacting with the hydroxide plasma, provided on one side of the buoy, a fluorescent material coating portion coated with a fluorescent material that changes the fluorescent color according to the pH, at predetermined intervals on an inner wall of the plasma reaction unit, a viewing window provided transparently to enable identification of the fluorescent material inside the plasma reaction unit, a heater unit for heating water molecules introduced from the flow path, and a nozzle unit for discharging the water molecules heated by the heater unit into the plasma reaction unit as fine particles. The fluorescent material is poly(N-phenylmaleimide) synthesized using N-phenylmaleimide as a monomer. The buoy is coated with a buoy coating composition comprising lead, a polyurea coating agent, an ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer, an ultraviolet absorber, and the fluorescent material. The polyurea coating agent comprises a prepolymer, a curing agent, an antifoaming agent and an organosilicon compound.
