PN CN114875083-A
TI Promoting co-generation of oil and astaxanthin by            filamentous microalgae comprises e.g. obtaining algae            seeds, culturing, expanding seed, introducing            compressed air containing carbon dioxide, washing algal            seeds with fresh mBBM liquid culture medium, and            collecting algal cells by centrifugation.
AU ZHANG C
   ZHANG H
   GAO B
AE UNIV JINAN (UNJN-C)
GA 2022A9775F
AB    NOVELTY - Promoting co-generation of oil and astaxanthin                by filamentous microalgae comprises (i) obtaining                algae seeds from algae seed chamber, culturing for                4-5 days, transferring algal seed to 1000 ml                culture flask containing mBBM liquid medium, and                expanding seed, (ii) inoculating algae seed into                columnar glass optical bioreactor, introducing                compressed air containing 1% carbon dioxide,                blasting and culturing, (iii) washing algal seeds                with fresh mBBM liquid culture medium with 4                different initial nitrogen concentrations (0, 3, 9,                18 mM nitrogen) for 2 times respectively, and                transferring to culture medium containing the above                four different initial nitrogen concentrations                according to the same initial seeding density of                0.4 g/l, and (iv) periodically measuring the                biomass concentration every day during the                culturing process, reserving samples, collecting                the algal cells by centrifugation (3500 g, 5                minutes), and freeze-drying in vacuum to obtain                freeze-dried algal powder.
   USE - The method is useful for promoting                co-generation of oil and astaxanthin by filamentous                microalgae.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the yield of oil and                astaxanthin; is simple and convenient; and provides                important technical support for efficient                co-production of oil and astaxanthin using                filamentous microalgae.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Promoting co-generation of oil and astaxanthin                by filamentous microalgae comprises (i) obtaining                algae seeds from the algae seed chamber,                transferring to a culture bottle (500 ml)                containing 250 ml of mBBM liquid culture medium,                and culturing for 4-5 days, transferring algal seed                to 1000 ml culture flask containing mBBM liquid                medium for 5-7 days, and expanding the seed, (ii)                inoculating the algae seed obtained in the step (i)                into columnar glass optical bioreactor with                diameter of 6 cm (length = 60 cm), where the                culture medium is mBBM liquid culture medium,                introducing compressed air containing 1% carbon                dioxide, blasting and culturing for 5-7 days, (iii)                washing the algal seeds obtained in the step (ii)                with fresh mBBM liquid culture medium with 4                different initial nitrogen concentrations (0, 3, 9,                18 mM nitrogen) for 2 times respectively, and                transferring to culture medium containing the above                four different initial nitrogen concentrations                according to the same initial seeding density of                0.4 g/l, and (iv) periodically measuring the                biomass concentration every day during the                culturing process, reserving samples for every 3                days, collecting the algal cells by centrifugation                (3500 g, 5 minutes), and freeze-drying in vacuum to                obtain freeze-dried algal powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  mBBM liquid culture medium comprises sodium                  nitrate, magnesium sulfate heptahydrate, sodium                  chloride, dipotassium phosphate, monopotassium                  phosphate, calcium chloride dihydrate, boric                  acid, ethylenediaminetetracetic acid disodium,                  potassium hydroxide, ferrous sulfate                  heptahydrate, cobaltous nitrate hexahydrate, zinc                  sulfate heptahydrate, manganese(II) chloride                  tetrahydrate, sodium molybdate dihydrate, and                  copper sulfate pentahydrate. The light intensity                  is 30-40 &#956;mol photons m-2s-1. In the                  step (ii), the light intensity is 70-100                  &#956;mol photons m-2s-1.Preferred Method: The preparation process of                  mBBM liquid culture medium comprises adding                  sodium nitrate, magnesium sulfate heptahydrate,                  sodium chloride, dipotassium phosphate,                  monopotassium phosphate, calcium chloride                  dihydrate, boric acid, ethylenediaminetetracetic                  acid disodium, potassium hydroxide, ferrous                  sulfate heptahydrate, cobaltous nitrate                  hexahydrate, zinc sulfate heptahydrate,                  manganese(II) chloride tetrahydrate, sodium                  molybdate dihydrate, and copper sulfate                  pentahydrate with 1 liter deionized water, and                  sterilizing by high temperature at 121&#8451;                  for 20 minutes. In the step (iii), culturing in                  800 ml columnar photobioreactor, setting the                  light quality to full-spectrum white light,                  monochromatic red light, monochromatic blue                  light, where the light intensity is 300                  &#177; 25 &#956;mol photons m-2s-1, and                  passing compressed air containing 1% carbon                  dioxide through at 0.2 vvm for aeration and                  mixing during the culture.

PT P
PN CN114075580-A
TI Improving erythritol product concentration and production conversion rate by inoculating yeast in middle and late stage of logarithmic growth in fermentation medium, performing fermentation culture to produce erythritol and feeding feed medium in accordance with carbon dioxide evolution rate.
AU ZHANG L
   SHI S
   CAO D
   LIU M
   ZHAO W
AE SHANDONG FUYANG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202235317S
AB    NOVELTY - Method for improving erythritol product concentration and production conversion rate involves inoculating yeast in middle and late stage of logarithmic growth in fermentation medium, performing fermentation culture to enter the stage of fermentation and conversion to produce erythritol, continuing performing fermentation culture and feeding feed medium in accordance with the change of carbon dioxide evolution rate (CER) data during the fermentation process.
   USE - The method is useful for improving erythritol product concentration and production conversion rate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for improving erythritol product concentration and production conversion rate involves (a) inoculating 8-10% yeast in middle and late stage of logarithmic growth in fermentation medium and performing fermentation culture at tank pressure of 0.02-0.08 MPa and aeration ratio of 0.5-1.5 vessel volumes per minute (vvm) under initial pH of 5.5-6 while stirring at 200-600 revolutions per minute (rpm) and controlling the temperature of the fermentation tank at 30plus minus0.5degrees Celsius using circulating water and dissolved oxygen content at 15-30% by adjusting rotational speed and ventilation ratio, (b) continuing fermenting until optical density at 600 nm reaches 18-30 and the pH is 3-4 to enter the stage of fermentation and conversion to produce erythritol and performing fermentation culture at tank pressure of 0.02-0.05 MPa and ventilation ratio of 0.5-1 vvm under pH of 3-3.5 while stirring at 200-500 rpm and controlling the temperature of the fermentation tank at 32plus minus0.5degrees Celsius using circulating water and dissolved oxygen content at 20-30% by adjusting rotational speed and ventilation ratio and (c) when the glucose content in the fermentation broth is less than 150 g/l, feeding feed medium at a feeding rate of 0.1-1.2 l/hour which is regulated in accordance with the change of carbon dioxide evolution rate (CER) data during the fermentation process, when the CER is gradually increased from 5 to 10, continuously accelerating sugar supplementation rate from 0.1 l/hour to 1 l/hour, when the CER is higher than 10, adopting a low feeding rate of 0.1 l/hour and when the CER was lower than 5, adopting a high feeding rate of 1.2 l/hour.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In step (a), the yeast includes Candida lipolytica, Saccharomyces cerevisiae or Torulopsis glabrata. In step (a), the yeast of middle and late stages of logarithmic growth refers to the yeast having OD 600nm value of 1.2-2. In step (a), the fermentation medium comprises 250-300 g/l glucose, 5 g/l potassium dihydrogen phosphate, 15 g/l yeast powder, 10 g/l corn steep liquor, 0.25 g/l magnesium sulfate, and 8 g/l citric acid. In step (c), the feed medium comprises 500 g/l glucose, 10 g/l magnesium sulfate, 5 g/l phytic acid and 5 ml/l trace element solution, where the trace element solution comprises 2.5 g/l cobalt chloride, 15 g/l manganese chloride, 1.5 g/l copper chloride, 8 g/l zinc chloride and 14.5 g/l vitamin B. In step (c), the CER is the carbon dioxide release rate, which is obtained by using mass spectrometer to collect the intake and exhaust gases in the fermentation process in real time and performing online calculation through star software.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC D05-H09; D05-B04
IP C12N-001/16; C12P-007/18; C12R-001/645; C12R-001/73

PN CN113813963-A
TI Anti-carbon deposition type nickel-based catalyst useful for catalyzing methane carbon dioxide dry reforming reaction comprises active components, auxiliary agents, and metal oxide components, where active components and additives loaded on metal oxide and active component comprises metal nickel.
AU BAI Y
   LIU Z
   PENG Z
   GAO J
AE UNIV NORTHWESTERN POLYTECHNICAL (UNWP-C)
GA 202206688G
AB    NOVELTY - Anti-carbon deposition type nickel-based catalyst comprises active components, auxiliary agents, and metal oxide components. The active components and additives are loaded on metal oxide. The active component comprises metal nickel. The auxiliary agent comprises gallium or boron. The metal oxide comprises ceric oxide.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for catalyzing the methane carbon dioxide dry reforming reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: realizes the dispersion and anchoring of nickel by metal oxide ceric oxide; inhibits the sintering of nickel particles and improves the catalytic stability of the catalyst by doping of gallium or boron; supports nickel and gallium or boron on ceric oxide by the co-impregnation method; exhibits excellent catalytic stability, anti-sintering and anti-carbon deposition properties; and maintains the conversion rate of carbon dioxide above 92% at 800degreesC for 45 hours, where the product hydrogen/carbon monoxide is 0.95, which is close to 1. The method: is simple, and widely used in nickel-based catalysts supported by various metal oxide supports and easy to industrialize production; and has specific universality
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing anti-carbon deposition type nickel-based catalyst, comprising (i) dividing preparation method of metal oxide ceric oxide into two types comprises (a) preparing metal oxide ceric oxide by water bath method comprising respectively adding the precursor salt of cerium (iii)ion, alkaline substance to de-ionized water to dissolve, uniformly mixing and stirring, allowing to stand to obtain flocculent precipitate solution, carrying out hydrothermal reaction, washing the reaction product, drying, calcining to obtain the metal oxide ceric oxide, and marking as ceric oxide-HT, and (b) preparing metal oxide ceric oxide by sol-gel method comprising adding the precursor salt of cerium (iii)ion, and citric acid into the de-ionized water to dissolve, uniformly mixing and stirring, allowing to stand to obtain clear solution, adjusting the pH value of the solution to 8, carrying out the reaction on adjusted solution in a water bath to obtain a viscous colloid, drying, grinding, calcining to obtain metal oxide ceric oxide, marking as ceric oxide-SG, and (ii) mixing and dissolving the metal nickel and the precursor of the auxiliary agent gallium or boron, ultrasonically processing, slowly dropping to the ceric oxide obtained in the step (i), drying, and calcining to obtain the carbon-resistant nickel-based catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of the active component to the auxiliary agent is 1:0.2-0.6. The mass ratio of the metal oxide to the active component is 1:0.02-0.1. In the step (i), the precursor salts of cerium (iii)ion and alkaline substances in the hydrothermal preparation process respectively comprises cerium nitrate hexahydrate, and sodium hydroxide. In the step (ii), the precursors of metal nickel, auxiliary agent gallium and auxiliary agent boron respectively comprises nickel nitrate hexahydrate, gallium chloride and boric acid. In the preparation process of the sol-gel method the precursor salt of cerium (iii)ion comprises cerium nitrate hexahydrate. The mass ratio of the precursor salt of cerium (iii)ion to citric acid is 1:3. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the hydrothermal reaction is carried out at 80-180degreesC for 10-48 hours. The water bath reaction in the sol-gel method is carried out at 50-120degrees Celsius for 3-24 hours. In the step (i), and (ii), the calcination is carried out at 400-700degreesC for 2-6 hours.

TI Producing high purity S-allyl-L-cysteine of garlic used in e.g. health functional food, comprises e.g. removing garlic peel, adding washed garlic into beaker, adding hypochlorous acid to sterile water, and sterilizing with ultrasonic waves.
AU HYUN S
AE HYUN S (HYUN-Individual)
GA 2020887750
AB    NOVELTY - Producing high purity SAC (S-allyl-L-cysteine) of garlic, comprises e.g. (a) removing garlic peel, putting washed garlic into a beaker, adding 2% hypochlorous acid to sterile water, and sterilizing with ultrasonic waves for 10 minutes, (b) sectioning sterilized garlic side in a clean bench after washing three or more times with sterile water, (c) inducing callus for 12 hours at a temperature of 22 plus minus 2 degrees C, (d) transplanting callus on a sterilized MS (Murashige and Skoog) agar medium to which growth hormone composition is added, and culturing for 4 weeks to yield a jelly-like callus, (e) subculturing calculated callus 7-8 times or more at intervals of 4 weeks on agar medium to grow pale yellow callus in large quantities, (f) separating only pure callus from callus, and putting it into a supercritical device chamber, and (g) injecting carbon dioxide into supercritical device chamber, and fixing supercritical device chamber door.
   USE - The high purity SAC of garlic is useful in health functional food (claimed) and medicine for treating cancer and Alzheimer's.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing high purity SAC (S-allyl-L-cysteine) of garlic, comprises (a) removing garlic peel, putting washed garlic into a beaker, adding 2% hypochlorous acid to sterile water, and sterilizing with ultrasonic waves for 10 minutes, (b) sectioning sterilized garlic side in a clean bench after washing three or more times with sterile water, (c) inducing callus for 12 hours at a temperature of 22 plus minus 2 degrees C, (d) transplanting callus on a sterilized MS (Murashige and Skoog) agar medium to which growth hormone composition is added, and culturing for 4 weeks to yield a jelly-like callus, (e) subculturing calculated callus 7-8 times or more at intervals of 4 weeks on agar medium to grow pale yellow callus in large quantities, (f) separating only pure callus from callus, and putting it into a supercritical device chamber, (g) injecting carbon dioxide into supercritical device chamber, and fixing supercritical device chamber door, (h) fixing temperature at 45 degrees C, and treating air pressure with supercritical carbon dioxide (SC-CO2) pressurized to 250 bar with a high-pressure compressor, and (i) freeze-drying at a shelf heating temperature of 30-50 degrees C and vacuum degree of 15-35 torr.An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for high purity SAC composition prepared by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The plant growth hormone contains 2 mg/l 2.4-D (2-4-dichloropienoxy acetic acid) naphthalene acetic acid, and kenetin. The agar medium is LS (Linsmaier and Skoog) agar medium to which 0.223mg/L 2.4-D and 0.213mg/L kinetin are added. Preferred Method: The purified air is fixed at a temperature of 0.8-0.7 vvm, 22-24 degrees C and subcultured 7-8 times every 4 weeks. The supercritical carbon dioxide treatment is carried out for 3 hours.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic; Neuroprotective; Nootropic. Test details are described but no results given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

GA 202035894Q
AB    NOVELTY - Frosted glass laser cutting method, comprises e.g. (i) first placing frosted glass on a mobile platform that can move along the X and Y axes, and placing the frosted glass under the cutting head, (ii) focusing the beam passing through the cutting head on the bottom surface of the frosted glass by adjusting the position of the cutting head, (iii) converting the laser light emitted by the pico-second laser into a Bessel beam to cut the position to be cut of the lower layer of the frosted glass using the cutting head, (iv) focusing the beam passing through the cutting head on the upper surface of the frosted glass by adjusting the position of the cutting head, (v) converting the laser light emitted by the pico-second laser into a Bessel beam using the cutting head, (vi) placing the frosted glass cut in step (iv) on a fixed platform, (vii) scanning the position using carbon dioxide laser, thus the position to be cut is completely cracked.

   ADVANTAGE - The method: has excellent cutting quality and high cutting efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Frosted glass laser cutting method, comprises (i) first placing frosted glass on a mobile platform that can move along the X and Y axes, and placing the frosted glass under the cutting head, (ii) focusing the beam passing through the cutting head on the bottom surface of the frosted glass by adjusting the position of the cutting head, (iii) converting the laser light emitted by the pico-second laser into a Bessel beam to cut the position to be cut of the lower layer of the frosted glass using the cutting head, (iv) focusing the beam passing through the cutting head on the upper surface of the frosted glass by adjusting the position of the cutting head, (v) converting the laser light emitted by the pico-second laser into a Bessel beam to cut the position of the upper layer of the frosted glass to be cut using the cutting head, (vi) placing the frosted glass cut in step (iv) on a fixed platform, (vii) scanning the position to be cut on the frosted glass using carbon dioxide laser, thus the position to be cut is completely cracked. PN CN110862841-A
TI Preparation of natural gas using water-coal-slurry            by preparing low-rank coal into water coal slurry by            crushing and grinding, heating under anaerobic or            microaerobic condition, and removing sulfur through            pre-desulfurization process.
AU PAN J
   CHEN F
   HE W
   SHU W
   YANG J
   LV B
AE ZHEJIANG TIANLU ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020209080
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of natural gas using                water-coal-slurry includes preparing low-rank coal                into water coal slurry by crushing and grinding,                heating at 800-1300 degrees C under anaerobic or                microaerobic condition, obtaining synthesis gas                comprising carbon monoxide (CO) and hydrogen (H2),                removing sulfur through pre-desulfurization process                to obtain pre-desulfurized synthesis gas,                converting all unsaturated hydrocarbons of the                pre-desulfurized synthesis gas to the corresponding                saturated hydrocarbons by hydrogenation process,                while converting organic sulfur to hydrogen sulfide                (H2S), obtaining hydrogenated synthesis gas,                removing sulfur in the hydrogenated synthesis gas                through a desulfurization process to obtain                desulfurized synthesis gas, subjecting the                desulfurized synthesis gas to a shift conversion                process to obtain shift conversion gas, and                removing carbon dioxide in the converted gas                through a decarburization process to obtain natural                gas synthesis gas.
   USE - The method is for preparation of natural gas                using water-coal-slurry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides natural gas with low                impurity and high quality, fully and effectively                utilizes low-rank coal and meets with the national                coal comprehensive utilization direction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of natural gas using                water-coal-slurry comprises preparing low-rank coal                into water coal slurry by crushing and grinding,                heating at 800-1300 degrees C under anaerobic or                microaerobic condition, obtaining synthesis gas                comprising carbon monoxide (CO) and hydrogen (H2),                removing sulfur through pre-desulfurization process                to obtain pre-desulfurized synthesis gas,                converting all unsaturated hydrocarbons of the                pre-desulfurized synthesis gas to the corresponding                saturated hydrocarbons by hydrogenation process,                while converting organic sulfur to hydrogen sulfide                (H2S), obtaining hydrogenated synthesis gas,                removing sulfur in the hydrogenated synthesis gas                through a desulfurization process to obtain                desulfurized synthesis gas, subjecting the                desulfurized synthesis gas to a shift conversion                process to obtain shift conversion gas, where the                molar ratio of hydrogen to CO in the shift                conversion gas is 3-10:1, removing carbon dioxide                in the converted gas through a decarburization                process to obtain natural gas synthesis gas, so                that the hydrogen-carbon ratio in the natural gas                synthesis gas is 2.95-3.05:1, subjecting the                natural gas synthesis gas to methane synthesis                process in which greater than or equal to 4                methanation reactors are connected in series, so                that carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen                in the desulfurized synthesis gas react in the                presence of a methanation catalyst to synthesize                methane, obtaining methane product stream,                introducing into a liquefaction process, and                producing not less than 90 vol.% methane by using a                cryogenic liquefaction process to obtain liquefied                natural gas (LNG) product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The pre-desulfurization                  comprises using pre-desulfurization liquid, where                  the synthesis gas enters from the lower portion                  of first desulfurizing device and                  countercurrently contacted with the                  pre-desulfurization liquid sprayed from an upper                  portion of the first desulfurization device,                  removing hydrogen sulfide from the synthesis gas,                  and the desulfurization liquid comprises PDS                  catalyst. It comprises using a second                  desulfurization device with resistance wire                  and/or pre-desulfurizing catalyst, where the                  resistance wire comprises nickel and chromium,                  the pre-desulfurizing catalyst comprises basic                  copper carbonate, copper oxide, copper hydroxide,                  basic zinc carbonate, zinc oxide, and/or zinc                  hydroxide, the synthesis gas is passed into the                  second desulfurization device, the resistance                  wire heats the synthesis gas at 200-500 degrees                  C, so that H2S in the synthesis gas is decomposed                  into elemental sulfur, and filtered to remove the                  elemental sulfur and obtain pre-desulfurized                  synthesis gas. The pre-desulfurization process                  comprises using a filtration device loaded with a                  composition comprising an adsorbent material and                  pre-desulfurization catalyst.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The water-coal-slurry                  further comprises upgraded coal, which is low                  rank coal prepared by gasification reduction. In                  the water-coal-slurry, the mass ratio of upgraded                  coal and low-rank coal is 1-10:1. The synthesis                  gas is prepared by heating water-coal-slurry at                  800-1300 degrees C with resistance wire                  containing nickel and chromium.

PN US2019358579-A1; GB2574289-A; JP2019202298-A; CN110523216-A; AU2019201807-A1; AU2019201807-B2; US11090603-B2; GB2574289-B
TI Acidic gas absorbent used for removing acidic gas for recovering carbon dioxide comprises amine compound and cyclic amino compound.
AU MURAI S
   KATO Y
   SUZUKI A
   UDATSU M
   UTATSU M
AE TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS CORP (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS CORP (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS KK (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS CORP (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS CORP (TOKE-C)
GA 201999439B
AB    NOVELTY - Acidic gas absorbent comprises an amine compound (I) and a cyclic amino compound (II) or (III).
   USE - The acidic gas absorbent is useful for removing an acidic gas (claimed) for recovering carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The acidic gas absorbent: is excellent in acidic gas absorption and desorption performance, and thus makes it possible to absorb and remove acidic gases efficiently; reduces energy necessary for desorbing the acidic gas (for regeneration of the absorbent), and thus the carbon dioxide absorption and desorption steps can be carried out efficiently and economically; utilizes the amine compound which has high anticorrosive properties for metallic materials e.g. carbon steel, as compared to conventional alkanol amines e.g. 2-aminoethanol; saves energy consumption used for pressure reduction to enhance the desorption performance; and has large absorbing capacity and provides small diffusion of amine at the same time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Acidic gas absorbent comprises an amine compound of formula (R2-N(R1)2) (I) and a cyclic amino compound of formula (II) or (III).
   R1 = straight-chain hydroxyalkyl group of 2-6C having a hydroxy group at the terminal;
   R2 = branched-chain secondary alkyl group of 3-6C;
   R3 = H, OH, or hydroxyalkyl or aminoalkyl group of 1-8C;
   R4 = H, OH, or hydroxyalkyl or aminoalkyl group of 1-8C;
   p = 2-4;
   R5 = H, OH, or hydroxyalkyl or aminoalkyl group of 1-8C; and
   q = 3-8.
   Provided that: at least one of R3 and R4s is hydroxyalkyl or aminoalkyl group of 1-8C; the cyclic skeleton in (II) may contain oxygen atom as constituting member; at least one of R5s is hydroxyalkyl or aminoalkyl group of 1-8C; and the cyclic skeleton in (III) may contain oxygen as a constituting member. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) an acidic gas removal method comprising contacting a gas containing an acidic gas with the acidic gas absorbent so as to remove the acidic gas from the acidic gas-containing gas; and
   (2) an acidic gas removal apparatus (1) comprising an absorption unit (2) for contacting the gas containing the acidic gas with the acidic gas absorbent, so that the absorbent absorbs the acidic gas to remove it from the acidic gas-containing gas, and a regeneration unit (3) in which the absorbent holding the acidic gas is made to desorb the acidic gas so as to be regenerated, so that the absorbent regenerated in the regeneration unit is reused in the absorption unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of the acidic gas removal apparatus.
   Acidic gas removal apparatus (1)
   Absorption unit (2)
   Regeneration unit (3)
   Heat exchanger (7)
   Heater (8)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amine compound (1) is N-isopropyldiethanolamine, N-isopropyldipropanolamine, 3-((2-hydroxyethyl)(propan-2-yl)amino)propan-1-ol, N-sec-butyldiethanolamine, N-sec-butyldipropanolamine, N-sec-butyldibutanolamine, 3-((2-hydroxyethyl)(butan-2-yl)amino)propan-1-ol, N-cyclopentyldiethanolamine, N-cyclopentyldipropanolamine or 3-((2-hydroxyethyl)(cyclopentyl)amino)propan-1-ol. The cyclic amino compound (II) or (III) is 2-azetidinemethanol, 2-(2-aminoethyl)azetidine, 2-pyrrolidinemethanol, 2-(2-aminoethyl)pyrrolidine, 4-hydroxypiperidine, 2-piperidinemethanol, 3-piperidineethanol, 2-(2-aminoethyl)pyrrolidine, 1-(2-hydroxyethyl)piperazine or 2-(hydroxymethyl)piperazine. The acidic gas absorbent comprises 10-60 wt.% the amine compound (I) and 1-50 wt.% the cyclic amino compound (II) or (III).
   EXAMPLE - In water, 20 wt.% N-isopropyldiethanolamine and 20 wt.% 1-piperazineethanol were dissolved to prepare 50 ml of an aqueous solution as acidic gas absorbent.

PT P
PN CN110237728-A; CN110237728-B
TI Preparation of mixed matrix membrane compounded with metal organic framework for separating carbon dioxide/methane gas, involves dissolving polymer of intrinsic microporosity-1 in organic solvent and applying to substrate or mold.
AU HOU R
   SU P
   LI Y
   XU Z
   ZHANG G
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 201981889H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of mixed matrix membrane compounded with metal organic framework involves dissolving polymer of intrinsic microporosity-1 in organic solvent (A) comprising chloroform, dichloromethane or tetrahydrofuran to obtain solution (B), dissolving ZIF-8 in organic solvent (C), uniformly stirring to obtain solution (D) comprising chloroform, dichloromethane, tetrahydrofuran or N,N-dimethylformamide, dissolving UIO-66-azanide in organic solvent (E), stirring to obtain solution (F), mixing solution (D) and solution (B) to form homogeneously mixed solution (G), where amount of solution (D) added is based on mass of ZIF-8 and mass ratio of ZIF-8 to polymer of intrinsic microporosity-1 is 1:(5-10), adding solution (F) to solution (G), stirring uniformly to obtain homogeneously mixed solution (H), where solution (G) is added in amount of ZIF-8 and mass ratio of ZIF-8 to UIO-66-azanide is 1:(1-1.4), applying solution (H) to substrate or mold, leaving still at room temperature for 48 hours.
   USE - Preparation of mixed matrix membrane compounded with metal organic framework used for separating carbon dioxide/methane gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The mixed matrix membrane has large selectivity and permeability for carbon dioxide/methane gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of mixed matrix membrane compounded with metal organic framework involves dissolving polymer of intrinsic microporosity-1 in organic solvent (A) comprising chloroform, dichloromethane or tetrahydrofuran, uniformly stirring to obtain solution (B), dissolving ZIF-8 in organic solvent (C), uniformly stirring to obtain solution (D) comprising chloroform, dichloromethane, tetrahydrofuran or N,N-dimethylformamide, dissolving UIO-66-azanide in organic solvent (E) comprising chloroform, dichloromethane, tetrahydrofuran or N,N-dimethylformamide, stirring to obtain solution (F), mixing solution (D) and solution (B) to form homogeneously mixed solution (G), where amount of solution (D) added is based on mass of ZIF-8, solution B is added in amount of mass of polymer of intrinsic microporosity-1 and mass ratio of ZIF-8 to polymer of intrinsic microporosity-1 is 1:(5-10), adding solution (F) to solution (G), stirring uniformly to obtain homogeneously mixed solution (H), where amount of solution (F) added is based on mass of UIO-66-azanide, solution (G) is added in amount of ZIF-8 and mass ratio of ZIF-8 to UIO-66-azanide is 1:(1-1.4), applying solution (H) uniformly to substrate or mold, leaving still at room temperature for 48 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The organic solvent (A) is added in an amount of 16-20 mL/g based on the mass of the polymer of intrinsic microporosity-1. The organic solvent (C) is added in an amount of 20-50 mL/g based on the mass of the ZIF-8. The organic solvent (E) is added in an amount of 20-50 mL/g based on the mass of the UIO-66-azanide. The mass ratio of 5,5',6,6'-tetrahydroxy-3,3,3',3'-tetramethyl-1,1'-helix double indane to tetrafluoroterephthalonitrile is 3:(1-2). The mass ratio of the 5,5',6,6'-tetrahydroxy-3,3,3',3'-tetramethyl-1,1'-helix double indane to potassium carbonate is 1:(1-2). The amount of dimethylacetamide added is 4-6 mL/g based on the mass of the 5,5',6,6'-tetrahydroxy-3,3,3',3'-tetramethyl-1,1'-helix double indane. The volume ratio of dimethylacetamide to toluene is (1-2):1. Preferred Process: The preparation of UIO-66-azanide involves adding zirconium chloride and 1,4-phthalic acid to an organic solvent N, N-dimethylformamide to obtain a homogeneous mixture (A), where the mass ratio of the zirconium chloride to the 2-aminoterephthalic acid is (1-2):1, placing mixture (A) in a reaction vessel placed in an oven at 120 degrees C for 2 days, cooling to room temperature, washing with an organic solvent of N,N-dimethylformamide at room temperature, and drying at room temperature.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The preparation of polymer of intrinsic microporosity-1 involves mixing 5,5',6,6'-tetrahydroxy-3,3,3',3'-tetramethyl-1,1'-helix double indane, tetrafluoroterephthalonitrile, potassium carbonate in dimethylacetamide and toluene solution, refluxing at 160 degrees C for 40 minutes, pouring reaction product into methanol, stirring to obtain noodle-like solid, washing with acetone and drying in oven at 100-130 degrees C. Preferred Component: The substrate is a glassware or a Teflon (RTM: polytetrafluoroethylene) dish.

PN CN109852637-A; CN109852637-B
TI Improving transfection efficiency of an adeno-associated virus, comprises passing the cells into T75 bottles, culturing with nutrient solution containing bovine serum and cell attachment factor, and harvesting 293T suspension cells.
AU LI H
   LI Z
   DING Z
   ZHANG R
   WANG T
AE GUANGZHOU PACKGENE BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019533106
AB    NOVELTY - Improving transfection efficiency of an adeno-associated virus, comprises passing the cells into T75 bottles, culturing for 3 generations with nutrient solution containing 8-10% bovine serum and cell attachment factor, and harvesting 293T suspension cells with good cell status and inoculating into 10-20 cm plate culture, after the specified cell culture parameters are set, adding into the incubator, setting the culture parameters of the carbon dioxide incubator, culturing for 20-28 hours, when the cell confluence rate reaches 80-90%, the mixture containing the adherent cells is obtained; and removing the supernatant from the mixed cell containing the adherent cells, slowly adding 15-25 ml of DMEM medium containing bovine serum, and then mixing, adding the transfection mixture containing the adeno-associated virus dropwise, and gently mixing by shaking to obtain a transfection suspension.
   USE - The method is useful for improving transfection efficiency of an adeno-associated virus.
   ADVANTAGE - The transfection efficiency is 80-90%, the success rate of virus transfection is improved, and the method is simple in operation and greatly saves the experiment cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Improving transfection efficiency of an adeno-associated virus, comprises (i) passing the cells into T75 bottles, culturing for 3 generations with nutrient solution containing 8-10% bovine serum and cell attachment factor, and harvesting 293T suspension cells with good cell status and inoculating into 10-20 cm plate culture, after the specified cell culture parameters are set, adding into the incubator, setting the culture parameters of the carbon dioxide incubator, culturing for 20-28 hours, when the cell confluence rate reaches 80-90%, the mixture containing the adherent cells is obtained; (ii) removing the supernatant from the mixed cell containing the adherent cells, slowly adding 15-25 ml of DMEM medium containing bovine serum, and then mixing, adding the transfection mixture containing the adeno-associated virus dropwise, and gently mixing by shaking to obtain a transfection suspension; (iii) incubating the transfection suspension in a nutrient solution containing bovine serum for 20-28 hours, taking out in a carbon dioxide incubator, setting the culture parameters of the incubation, and culturing for 20-28 hours, discarding the supernatant, adding serum-free DMEM culture solution, setting the culture parameters after incubation, and incubating for 40-55 hours to obtain a mixture containing adeno-associated virus; and (iv) centrifuging the mixture containing the adeno-associated virus at 1000-1500 revolutions per minute for 1-10 minutes, collecting the cells, observing under a fluorescence microscope, and transforming the cells by flow cytometry, and measuring the dyeing rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The cell attachment factor is composed of E-cadherin, type III collagen and polylysine in a mass ratio of 3:7:4. The serum type in the adeno-associated virus-containing transfection mixture in the step (ii) is 1-9. The mass concentration of bovine serum in the DMEM medium containing bovine serum in step (ii) is 1-50%. The transfection mixture containing the adeno-associated virus in the step (ii) comprises three plasmids for packaging adeno-associated virus, three kinds of plasmids were pDeltaF6, pRep2Cap2 and rAAV2EGFP, respectively, at 10-30 mu g, and the mass ratio of the three was 2:2:1. The calcium chloride solution is a calcium chloride aqueous solution, and the concentration is 2 mol/l, and the addition amount is 10-100 mu l. The pH of the 2xHBS salt solution is 6-7.5. Preferred Method: The cell culture parameter is a seeding density of 0.8x 106 to 1.2x 106 cells/ml; the culture parameters of the incubator are 35-38 degrees C and the carbon dioxide concentration is 3-7%. The preparation method of the transfection mixture containing the adeno-associated virus in the step (ii) is mixing the plasmid, sterile water and calcium chloride solution to a volume of 624 mu l, adding an equal volume of 2x HBS salt solution dropwise, and gently bouncing the tube wall so that each drop is mixed in time and placed at room temperature for 1-10 minutes. The incubation parameter set in the incubation in the step (iii) is a temperature of 30-37 degrees C and a carbon dioxide concentration of 3-7%. The culture parameter after the incubation in the step (iii) is a temperature of 36-38 degrees C and a carbon dioxide concentration of 5%.

PN CN109850923-A
TI Extracting sylvite from potassium salt crystal area of potassium-rich brine, comprises preparing refined potassium hydrogen carbonate and filtrate C, and food grade potassium hydrogen carbonate, recovering and acidifying.
AU MIAO S
   LI Z
   XU S
   XUE Y
   WANG J
   WANG Z
AE UNIV JILIN (UYJI-C)
GA 201953354R
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting sylvite from potassium salt crystal area of potassium-rich brine, comprises (i) taking carbonate saline brine in salt field evaporating and condensing obtained potassium-rich brine as the raw material in the tank, evaporating, concentrating, and controlling to obtain solution A; (ii) preparing potassium hydrogen carbonate and filtrate B; (iii) evaporating and crystallizing the filtrate B at normal temperature, filtering to separate, and washing to obtain refined potassium hydrogen carbonate and filtrate C; (iv) combining the refined potassium hydrogen carbonate and drying at 40 degrees C for 6 hours to obtain food grade potassium hydrogen carbonate; and (v) recovering the filtrate C and placing into the next round of potassium-rich brine for reuse until sodium ion salt formed after acidification by adding carbon-dioxide, where this system is no longer used for extracting food grade potassium hydrogen carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting sylvite from potassium salt crystal area of potassium-rich brine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical; easy to control technique condition, easy amplification reaction dosage, industrialization, GB 25589-2010 food grade standard.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting sylvite from potassium salt crystal area of potassium-rich brine, comprises (i) taking carbonate saline brine in salt field evaporating and condensing the obtained potassium-rich brine as the raw material in the tank, evaporating the potassium-rich brine in a biochemical culture box and concentrating, controlling the evaporation at 25 degrees C until the solution reaches saturation to obtain a solution A; (ii) placing the solution A into high pressure kettle, introducing high voltage into the kettle carbon-dioxide, controlling system temperature at 25 degrees C under the pressure of 5.80-7.05 MPa, crystallizing for 30-60 minutes, where the solution A into sodium ion + potassium + chloride ion + bicarbonate system, crystal precipitation in the solution, filtering to separate, and washing to obtain potassium hydrogen carbonate and filtrate B; (iii) evaporating and crystallizing the filtrate B at normal temperature, filtering to separate, washing to obtain refined potassium hydrogen carbonate and filtrate C; (iv) combining the refined potassium hydrogen carbonate and drying at 40 degrees C for 6 hours to obtain food grade potassium hydrogen carbonate; and (v) recovering the filtrate C and placing into the next round of potassium-rich brine for reuse until sodium ion salt formed after acidification by adding carbon-dioxide, where this system is no longer used for extracting food grade potassium hydrogen carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the potassium-rich brine, the concentration ratio of potassium ions to sodium ions higher than 1.20, and the ratio of carbonate ions to chlorine ions higher than 8.24.

PN CN108608097-A; CN108608097-B
TI Welding double-sided forming of thick-walled vertical welded joint involves using method of inert gas tungsten arc welding for carbon dioxide flux-cored wire, arising arc at upper edge of thick-wall vertical weld of concentrated down pipe.
AU CHENG P
   CHEN C
   LI Z
AE CHINA HUAYE GROUP CO LTD (CMEG-C)
GA 2018804628
AB    NOVELTY - Welding double-sided forming of thick-walled vertical welded joint involves using a method of inert gas tungsten arc welding for a carbon dioxide flux-cored wire, arising the arc at the upper edge of thick-wall vertical weld of the concentrated down pipe, keeping the welding torch and carbon dioxide flux-cored wire pulled down synchronously and moving to the lower groove for bottom welding, and then the arc, welding the underfill layer to the bottom layer and the lower slope joint after the bottom layer is welded, and welding to the initial welding position after welding for one week along the vertical joint, and melting the initial weld beyond the preset length, welding the filling layer at the fusion of the bottom layer and the upslope, and welding the vertical welding joint to return to the initial welding position, arranging the upper filling layer and the lower filling layer, and forming a slight concave between them.
   USE - Method for welding double-sided forming of thick-walled vertical welded joint.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to weld double-sided forming of thick-walled vertical welded joint, which improves the welding efficiency and welding quality of the concentrated down pipe, avoids waste of production equipment, resources, and man-hours caused by a large number of repairs after welding, and shortens the construction period while reducing construction costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The welding wire is extended to the lower filling layer and the groove fusion portion. The arc is opened at a predetermined interval after the lower filling layer and the downcomer pipe wall joint are moved toward the joint opening direction. The underfill layer is melted and soldering it along the vertical weld to a position of the initial weld. The initial weld seam is melted beyond a predetermined length to form an arc of the first thickening layer, and then at the joint of the first thickening layer and the lower filling layer, the angle of the welding torch is adjusted along the vertical welding joint to form a second thickening layer according to a preset spacing. The angle of the welding gun according to the preset spacing is adjusted to form a third thickening layer at the second thickening layer and the upper filling layer joint along the vertical welding joint. The second thickening layer, and third thickening layer performing cover welding according to a preset interval to form a cover layer on the first thickening layer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The carbon wire flux-cored wire is extended to the back surface of the base material at the edge of the upper edge of the vertical weld by rotating the wire and rotating toward the inner side of the palm. The arc is triggered by high-frequency arc striking method, and then the pivot is slid at the edge of the outer edge of the vertical weld under the vertical weld. The welding torch and the carbon dioxide flux-cored wire are pulled down to the lower slope, and staying at the lower slope at a preset time to heat, and at the lower arc position, the interest arc is attenuated at the position of the lower slope. Preferred Conditions: The predetermined length is 8-12 mm. The preset interval is 80-110 mm. The preset spacing is 25-35mm. The angle between the welding gun and the vertical direction is 100 degrees , when the first thickening layer is welded. The angle of the welding torch is adjusted to 90 degrees , when welding the second thickening layer. The angle of the welding torch is adjusted to 80 degrees when welding the third thickening layer.

PN CN108410804-A; CN108410804-B
TI Promoting proliferation of adipose stem cells, by cutting and digesting subcutaneous adipose tissue, adding complete medium, centrifuging, culturing cell pellets, digesting cells, and adding incomplete medium for culturing process.
AU TIAN H
   HAN Z
AE NANJING QIANNIANJIAN STEM CELLS GENETIC (NANJ-Non-standard)
   JIANGXI HEALTH & BIOTECH STEM CELL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018665419
AB    NOVELTY - Method of promoting proliferation of adipose stem cells, involves (a) taking subcutaneous adipose tissue from patients or healthy volunteers, rinsing in phosphate-buffered saline (PBS), cutting into pieces, digesting using collagenase type I and trypsin, adding complete medium, centrifuging, discarding supernatant to remove suspended fat cells and lipid droplets, resuspending cell pellet in complete medium, inoculating in culture flask, culturing, after cell fusion, digesting cells using pancreatin and EDTA at 37 degrees C for 3-5 minutes, where the complete medium is used for digestion in a ratio of 1:2 and (b) seeding the passage cells at a density of 1.5x 104/m2 in a culture flask containing complete medium, incubating 37 degrees C and carbon dioxide concentration of 5% for 48 hours, changing the medium to incomplete medium, and at the same time, continuing the addition of pseudoditerpenoids for 24-72 hours.
   USE - The method is useful for promoting proliferation of adipose stem cells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method of promoting proliferation of adipose stem cells, involves (a) taking subcutaneous adipose tissue from patients or healthy volunteers, rinsing 2-3 times in phosphate-buffered saline (PBS), removing adipose tissue membrane and blood vessels using ophthalmic scissors and ophthalmic forceps, cutting into pieces of 1 mm3 size, digesting using 0.1% collagenase type I and 0.25% trypsin at 37 degrees C for 40 minutes, stirring for 4-5 times during digestion, adding complete medium, centrifuging at 1500 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 10 minutes, discarding supernatant to remove suspended fat cells and lipid droplets, resuspending cell pellet in complete medium, inoculating in culture flask, culturing in 5% carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C, changing culture medium to remove unadhered cells after 24 hours, then changing medium for every 3 days, after cell fusion, digesting cells using 0.25% pancreatin and 1 mmol/l EDTA at 37 degrees C for 3-5 minutes, where the complete medium is used for digestion in a ratio of 1:2 and (b) seeding the passage cells at a density of 1.5x 104/m2 in a culture flask containing complete medium, incubating 37 degrees C and carbon dioxide concentration of 5% for 48 hours, changing the medium to incomplete medium, and at the same time, continuing the addition of pseudoditerpenoids for 24-72 hours. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for Dulbecco's modified eagle's medium (DMEM) culture medium for promoting fat stem cell proliferation, prepared by adding pseudoditerpenoid chosen from Euphorbia helioscopia terpene A or B or F to medium.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Medium: The complete medium is DMEM containing 10% fetal bovine serum. The incomplete medium is DMEM without fetal bovine serum. Preferred Method: In step (b), passage cells are subcultured for 10-15 generations by inducing proliferation.

TI Enriching and purifying saw palmetto extract involves crushing palm fruit, adding to carbon dioxide extractor, taking ethanol as entrainer, extracting and processing saw palmetto extract using hydrochloric acid to adjust pH value.
AU YAO D
   CHEN Y
   LI Y
   WAN L
   WANG L
AE DAXINGANLING LINGONBERRY FRIGID ZONE BIO (DAXI-Non-standard)
GA 201833431U
AB    NOVELTY - Enriching and purifying saw palmetto extract involves crushing palm fruit, adding to carbon dioxide extractor, taking ethanol as entrainer, extracting at pressure of 20-40 megaPascals at 50-60 degrees C for 80-120 minutes to obtain extract, processing saw palmetto extract using hydrochloric acid to adjust pH value to 4.0-6.0, allowing to stand, filtering, decompressing, concentrating to obtain saw palmetto extract, absorbing resulting saw palmetto extract by macroporous absorption resin, where flow speed is 3-5 BV/hour and absorbing temperature is 40-45 degrees C.
   USE - Method for enriching and purifying saw palmetto extract.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to shorten extracting time, reduce dissolution of impurity and improve yield in fast manner and purity of product is high, yield is high and there is no chemical residue.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Enriching and purifying saw palmetto extract involves crushing palm fruit, adding to carbon dioxide extractor, taking ethanol as entrainer, extracting at pressure of 20-40 megaPascals at 50-60 degrees C for 80-120 minutes to obtain extract, processing saw palmetto extract using hydrochloric acid to adjust pH value to 4.0-6.0, allowing to stand, filtering, decompressing, concentrating to obtain saw palmetto extract, absorbing resulting saw palmetto extract by macroporous absorption resin, where flow speed is 3-5 BV/hour and absorbing temperature is 40-45 degrees C. Enriching and purifying saw palmetto extract further involves eluting macro-porous adsorption resin using 40%, 50%, 60%, 70%, 80% or 90% concentrations of ethanol, using thin layer chromatography to track, collecting eluent saw palmetto at each stage, concentrating, recycling ethanol to obtain saw palmetto semi-finished product with content of more than 50%, performing silica gel column chromatography by dissolving obtained powder in lye, adding proper silica gel, stirring, drying, sieving with 60-80-mesh sieve, drying sample, performing industrial scale column silica gel column chromatography by using acetone and methanol in volume ratio of 1-7:1 to elute, performing thin layer chromatography to track, collecting saw palmetto at each stage of eluent, reducing pressure, condensing, recycling solvent, freezing for 24 hours, putting in vacuum drying equipment, pumping vacuum, heating at 45 degrees C, drying and taking out to obtain high purity saw palmetto extract, where its content is more than 90%, concentration of ethanol solution is 75%, macroporous resin is AB-8, LX-100, D101 or D201, freezing and drying vacuum pressure is 10-12 Pascals and time is 3-4 hours.

PN CN113354201-A
TI Treating garlic processing wastewater with algae            by flocculating garlic processing wastewater,            recovering allicin by carbon dioxide supercritical            extraction, performing photocatalytic Fenton oxidation            reaction, precipitating, treating and degrading organic            matter using ozone and red mud ceramsite.
AU WANG W
   LIANG Q
   WANG Y
   ZHAO L
AE RIZHAO POLYTECHNIC (RIZH-Non-standard)
GA 2021A7561C
AB    NOVELTY - Method for treating garlic processing                wastewater with algae involves (a) performing algae                treatment of garlic, garlic fermentation,                flocculation precipitation, allicin recovery,                photocatalytic Fenton oxidation, anaerobic                treatment and ozone oxidation, adjusting the pH                value of garlic processing wastewater with strong                antioxidant capacity and then feeding into a                flocculation sedimentation tank for flocculation,                (b) recovering allicin by carbon dioxide                supercritical extraction in the wastewater, (c)                adding the wastewater to acidic substance and                feeding the wastewater into a photocatalytic Fenton                reactor to perform the photocatalytic Fenton                oxidation reaction, (d) adding the wastewater to                garlic treated with algae and feeding into                sedimentation tank for precipitation, (e) feeding                the wastewater into anaerobic baffle reactor (ABR)                for treatment, (f) feeding the wastewater into                ozone reactor and degrading the organic matter                under the action of ozone and red mud                ceramsite.
   USE - The method is useful for treating garlic                processing wastewater with algae.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, cost-effective and                suitable for sewage treatment with larger water                volume and has very good effects on garlic                wastewater treatment, and high operating                efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for treating garlic processing                wastewater with algae involves (a) performing algae                treatment of garlic, garlic fermentation,                flocculation precipitation, allicin recovery,                photocatalytic Fenton oxidation, anaerobic                treatment and ozone oxidation, adjusting the pH                value of garlic processing wastewater with strong                antioxidant capacity to 8 and then feeding into a                flocculation sedimentation tank for flocculation                treatment, (b) recovering allicin by carbon dioxide                supercritical extraction in the wastewater obtained                in step (a), (c) adding the wastewater obtained in                the step (b) to non-polluting acidic substance to                adjust the pH to 3-4, and feeding the wastewater                into a photocatalytic Fenton reactor to perform the                photocatalytic Fenton oxidation reaction, (d)                adding the wastewater obtained in step (c) to                garlic treated with algae to adjust the pH to 8 and                then feeding into sedimentation tank for                precipitation for 1-2 days, (e) feeding the                wastewater obtained in step (d) into anaerobic                baffle reactor (ABR) for treatment, where the                reaction chamber of the ABR is filled with complex                filler, (f) feeding the wastewater obtained in step                (e) into ozone reactor, and degrading the organic                matter in the wastewater under the action of ozone                and red mud ceramsite. In steps (a)-(f), the                equipment used is the photocatalytic Fenton                reactor, ABR, and ozone reactor connected to an                external control module, where the control module                performs data fusion processing, the control module                is connected to the cloud server, a high-voltage                electrode fixing insulating plate is arranged                inside the ozone reactor, a high-voltage electrode                is arranged on the high-voltage electrode fixing                insulating plate, a certain gap is present between                the high-voltage electrodes, an oxygen                concentration acquisition module, an air humidity                acquisition module, and a current and voltage                acquisition module are arranged inside the ozone                reactor, the ozone reactor is connected to the                oxygen input pump through a pipeline, the                high-voltage electrode performs high-voltage                discharge, the oxygen input pump fills the ozone                reactor with oxygen and the oxygen is ionized out                of ozone. In the process of producing ozone from                oxygen, the oxygen concentration collection module                collects the concentration of oxygen, the air                humidity collection module collects the humidity of                the air, and the current and voltage collection                module collects the current and voltage of the                high-voltage electrode. The air humidity                acquisition module is equipped with an electric                signal generation module, an electric signal                denoising filter module, an analog-to-digital                conversion module, and a signal extraction and                analysis module. The specific process for the                signal denoising filtering module to process the                electrical signal is as follows: making a priori                prediction using specific equation:                X(k+1|k)=AX(k|k)+BU(k)+w(k) (I), calculating the                prior variance using specific equation:                P(k+1|k)=AP(k|k)A'+Q(k) (II), determining the gain                matrix using specific equation:                Kg(k+1)=P(k+1|k)H'/(HP(k+1|k)H'+R(k+1)) (III),                performing posterior estimatation using specific                equation: X(k+1|k+1)=X(k+1|k)+Kg(k+1)                (Z(k+1)-HX(k+1|k)) (IV), predicting Variance of                posterior using specific equation:                P(k+1|k+1)=(1-Kg(k+1)H)P(k+1|k) (V), where Q(k)                system process excitation noise covariance and R(k)                is the measurement noise covariance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (b) involves                  performing carbon dioxide supercritical                  extraction using 50 ml/l complex extratant, the                  complex extratant is prepared by mixing tributyl                  phosphate to trioctyl decyl tertiary amine in a                  weight ratio of 1:1-5, and diluting the mixture                  of tributyl phosphate and trioctyl decyl tertiary                  amine with diluent in a weight ratio of 1:5-10,                  where the diluent is n-octanol, isooctyl alcohol,                  chloroform or carbon tetrachloride. The step (b)                  involves adding the composite extractant into the                  inlet of the wastewater pump, feeding into the                  centrifugal extractor along with the garlic                  wastewater, reacting under the action of                  centrifugal force, so that the dense liquid phase                  moves away from the center of the drum and leans                  toward the drum during the upward flow, where the                  low-density liquid phase is far away from the                  drum wall and leaning toward the center, feeding                  the clarified two-phase liquid into collection                  chamber through their respective weir plates and                  leading out of the machine by the guide pipes to                  complete the two-phase separation process and                  transferring allicin from the wastewater phase to                  the organic phase. The step (b) involves                  repeatedly adding 2-4 %mass sulfuric acid or 2-4                  %mass hydrochloric acid into the centrifugal                  extractor for complex extractant and performing                  regeneration for 0.5-1.5 hours at 20-30 degrees                  C, where the complex extractant and 2-4 %mass                  sulfuric acid or 2-4 %mass hydrochloric acid are                  in a weight ratio of 1:1-10. In step (e), the                  complex is prepared by uniformly mixing red mud,                  fly ash, activated carbon and cerium oxide in a                  weight ratio of 1:1.5-2:0.5-1.2:0.2 and calcining                  at 1100 degrees C to produce microspheres having                  a particle size of 0.6-0.8 cm. The method                  involves subjecting the wastewater in the                  photocatalytic Fenton reactor to photocatalytic                  degradation for 30 minutes under UV irradiation                  using 15 ml/l 30% hydrogen peroxide and 1.2 g/l                  ferric ion. The red mud ceramsite is prepared by                  uniformly mixing red mud, sawdust, manganese                  nitrate and cobalt nitrate in a weight ratio of                  1:0.1-0.2:0.1-0.3:0.2-0.3 and calcining at 1100                  degrees C to produce microspheres with a particle                  size of 0.3-0.5 cm. The method involves adding                  0.05-30 mg flocculant per 1 l wastewater during                  the flocculation process, where the flocculant                  comprises polyaluminum chloride and                  polyacrylamide in a ratio of 1:2.5-10. The                  specific process of the control module for data                  fusion processing involves establishing N                  different types of data sets in accordance with                  the operating data of the photocatalytic Fenton                  reactor, ABR anaerobic baffle reactor, and ozone                  reactor, performing feature extraction                  transformation on N different types of data sets,                  extracting feature vectors representing                  observation data, using the clustering algorithm                  to recognize the feature vector pattern,                  completing the description of various data sets                  on the target, and establishing the corresponding                  correlation.

TI Thermally insulated glass plate having roller-supported insulating cavity formed by sealing glass sheets with two sealants comprises a glass plate and adhesive sealant, the glass plate is rolled out by a pressing roller on the glass plate.
AU XU B
AE ZIBO ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION PUBLICITY (ZIBO-Non-standard)
   XU B (XUBB-Individual)
   ZIBO HUANNENG HAICHEN ENVIRONMENTAL PROT (ZIBO-Non-standard)
   XU B (XUBB-Individual)
GA 202054004B
AB    NOVELTY - Thermally insulated glass plate having roller-supported insulating cavity formed by sealing glass sheets with two sealants comprises a glass plate and adhesive sealant. The glass plate is rolled out by a pressing roller on the glass plate. The sizes correspond to each other, the edges of the glass plates that make up the interlayer cavity, after applying airtight sealant on the supporting frame tape, or applying transparent glue on the supporting frame, after the airtight sealant is coated on the support frame tape, the two glass plates are fastened and closed by bonding. A piece of flat glass and a concave glass with a pressing roller rolling support frame pass the airtight sealant coated on the adhesive sealing surface, filling with low thermal conductivity gas including argon gas, carbon dioxide, etc. to fill the glass hollow interlayer with low thermal conductivity gas, after which, the pieces are bonded.
   USE - Used as thermally insulated glass plate having roller-supported insulating cavity formed by sealing glass sheets with two sealants.
   ADVANTAGE - The glass plate: improves quality of welding of glass and metal; has tempering maintenance, high strength, low production costs, excellent air tightness and thermal insulation properties, low energy consumption, and high transparency; achieves protective effects for the insulated glass. The stainless frame prevents damages caused by the corners thereof being struck during transport.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Thermally insulated glass plate having roller-supported insulating cavity formed by sealing glass sheets with two sealants comprises a glass plate and adhesive sealant. The glass plate is rolled out by a pressing roller on the glass plate. The sizes correspond to each other, the edges of the glass plates that make up the interlayer cavity, after applying airtight sealant on the supporting frame tape, or applying transparent glue on the supporting frame, after the airtight sealant is coated on the support frame tape, the two glass plates are fastened and closed by bonding. A piece of flat glass and a concave glass with a pressing roller rolling support frame pass the airtight sealant coated on the adhesive sealing surface, filling with low thermal conductivity gas including argon gas, carbon dioxide, etc. to fill the glass hollow interlayer with low thermal conductivity gas, after which, the pieces are bonded; or one piece of sunken glass with a pressing roller rolling support frame, and another piece of sunken glass with a pressing roller rolling support frame through the airtight sealant coated on the adhesive sealing surface, filling with low thermal conductivity gas including argon gas, carbon dioxide, etc. to fill the glass hollow interlayer with low thermal conductivity gas, after which, the pieces are bonded; or on the periphery of the double-layer glass plate sealed by two glass plates covered and sealed with airtight bonding, a layer of metal tape with good plasticity is bonded through an airtight sealant to act as an airtight sealing ring for the isolation sealing layer. The outer circumference of the hollow laminated glass plate body is wrapped with a closed-loop corrugated stainless steel frame with an inverted U-section. The groove of corrugated stainless steel frame is filled with structural sealant that does not produce chemical reaction with airtight sealant, using the self-elasticity of the inverted U-shaped closed-loop corrugated stainless steel frame, it stretches with the outer periphery of the hollow laminated glass plate body and using the self-rebound of the closed-loop corrugated stainless steel frame, the cross-section is an inverted U-shaped closed-loop corrugated stainless steel frame, closely adhere to the outer periphery of the hollow laminated glass plate; or the outer periphery of the hollow laminated glass plate body, wrapped with a closed-loop stainless steel frame with a cross section of L and an anti-L shaped, a hollow laminated glass plate structure protection frame formed by a snap fit set; there are two kinds of ring-shaped closed adhesive sealing tapes on the periphery of the insulated glass plate, and stainless steel closed-loop protection frame, glass hollow interlayer insulation and lighting glass plate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the thermally insulated glass plate having roller-supported insulating cavity formed by sealing glass sheets with two sealants.
   U-shaped stainless steel corrugated protective frame (1)
   Structural sealant (2)
   Lower tempered flat glass (3)
   Hollow interlayer (4)
   Upper tempered flat glass (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The ring-shaped closed glass supporting frame is bonded by the first airtight sealant, the first airtight sealant includes butyl type sealant, e.g. polyisobutylene glue and hot melt butyl glue. The second sealant is a weather-resistant structural sealant, including elastic sealants for insulating glass e.g. polysulfides, silicones, and polyurethanes, and hot-melt structural sealants include hot-melt polyisobutylene glue and hot-melt butyl glue. A thin metal strip with excellent plasticity that extends the bonding thickness of the airtight sealant is an aluminum or stainless steel strip. The outer periphery of the hollow laminated glass plate body is wrapped with a closed-loop corrugated stainless steel frame with an inverted U-section, the U-shaped corrugated stainless steel groove profile is made of stainless steel slats by stamping and drawing, or U-shaped corrugated stainless steel groove profiles are stainless steel strips, which are rolled and formed by a rolling mill, the closed-loop corrugated stainless steel frame is a U-shaped corrugated stainless steel groove profile, which is made by bending welding or cutting and welding. The outer periphery of the hollow laminated glass plate body is covered with a hollow laminated glass plate structure protection frame formed by a closed-loop stainless steel frame with a cross section of L-and a reverse L-shaped snap-fitting set, the L-shaped stainless steel profile is stainless steel lath, which is formed by stamping and drawing through the die; or L-shaped stainless steel profiles are stainless steel strips, which are rolled and formed by a rolling mill, the closed-loop L-shaped stainless steel frame is an L-shaped stainless steel profile, which is made by bending welding or cutting welding.

PN CN111185224-A; CN111185224-B
TI Preparing carbon dixoide aromatization catalyst comprises e. g. adding molecular sieve ZSM-5 into deionized aqueous solution, stirring, cooling to room temperature, filtering and washing to neutrality.
AU XIAO G
   WU Y
   ZHANG Z
   YUAN H
   CHEN Y
   WEI R
   GAO L
   ZHANG J
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 202046603W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dixoide aromatization catalyst comprises e. g. (i) adding molecular sieve ZSM-5 with solid-liquid ratio of 0.01-20 g/l into a deionized aqueous solution containing 0.01-2 mol/l acid or alkali, stirring at 20-120 degrees C for 1-48 hours, cooling to room temperature, filtering and washing to neutrality, ion-exchanging the obtained ZSM-5 with 0.5-2.5 mol/l ammonium chloride solution at 20-120 degrees C for 1-5 times each time for 1-24 hours, drying at 40-120 degrees C for 6-48 hours, roasting at 400-800 degrees C for 3-7 hours, and obtaining modified ZSM-5 with rich hydroxy groups on the surface; and (ii) introducing active metal by atomic layer deposition method and placing the modified ZSM-5 of step (i) in the reaction chamber of the atomic layer deposition equipment, heating to make the temperature of the reaction chamber reach 50-350 degrees C, evacuating to make the air pressure in the reaction chamber 0.01-10 torr, introducing high-purity argon gas with flow rate of 1-100 sccm as carrier gas.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon dioxide aromatization catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high adsorption performance of carbon dioxide, and promotes the aromatization reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing carbon dixoide aromatization catalyst comprises (i) adding molecular sieve ZSM-5 with solid-liquid ratio of 0.01-20 g/l into a deionized aqueous solution containing 0.01-2 mol/l acid or alkali, stirring at 20-120 degrees C for 1-48 hours, cooling to room temperature, filtering and washing to neutrality, ion-exchanging the obtained ZSM-5 with 0.5-2.5 mol/l ammonium chloride solution at 20-120 degrees C for 1-5 times each time for 1-24 hours, drying at 40-120 degrees C for 6-48 hours, roasting at 400-800 degrees C for 3-7 hours, and obtaining modified ZSM-5 with rich hydroxy groups on the surface; (ii) introducing active metal by atomic layer deposition method and placing the modified ZSM-5 of step (i) in the reaction chamber of the atomic layer deposition equipment, heating to make the temperature of the reaction chamber reach 50-350 degrees C, evacuating to make the air pressure in the reaction chamber 0.01-10 torr, introducing high-purity argon gas with flow rate of 1-100 sccm as carrier gas, passing the diethyl zinc gaseous metal source into the reaction chamber, reacting gaseous diethyl zinc with hydroxy group on the surface of modified ZSM-5 to form methyl structure, purging with high purity argon, helium or nitrogen, introducing gaseous oxygen source into the reaction chamber, making the methyl structure react with the gaseous oxygen source chemically, and generating zinc oxide on the surface of modified ZSM-5; (iii) introducing in-situ growth method to layered metal organic structure, adding the ZSM-5 with gold zinc oxide on the surface prepared in step (ii) into the hydrothermal reactor, adding organic solvent with a solid-liquid ratio of 0.01-20 g/l into the hydrothermal reactor, and stirring at 20-80 degrees C for 1-12 hours, adding 1-50 mmol of organic ligand to the hydrothermal reactor, stirring at 20-80 degrees C for 1-12 hours, performing subsequent solvent thermal reaction at 120-150 degrees C for 12-48 hours, cooling to room temperature, filtering and washing the precipitate in the hydrothermal kettle 3 times, transferring to hydrothermal reactor and adding organic solvent with solid-liquid ratio of 0.01-20 g/l, forming a layer of organic ligand on the surface of ZSM-5, adding an equimolar amount of metal salt into the organic ligand to the hydrothermal reactor, stirring for 1-6 hours at 20-80 degrees C, performing subsequent solvent thermal reaction at 120-150 degrees C for 12-48 hours, cooling to room temperature, filtering and washing the precipitate in the hydrothermal kettle 3 times, forming a layer of metal organic structure on the surface of ZSM-5, adjusting the repetition times, synthesizing ZSM-5 as core, and obtaining layered structure carbon dioxide aromatization catalyst with alternating organic ligand and metal ion layers.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the deionized aqueous solution of alkali is ammonia, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, calcium hydroxide, sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, potassium carbonate and/or potassium bicarbonate aqueous solution. The deionized aqueous solution of the acid is sulfuric acid, hydrochloric acid, nitric acid, phosphoric acid, acetic acid, oxalic acid, acetic acid and/or formic acid solution. In the step (iii), the washing solution for washing the organic ligand is methanol, ethanol, nitrogen dimethylformamide, nitrogen diethyl formamide, methylene chloride, chloroform, tetrachloromethane and/or tetrahydrofuran, the washing solution for washing metal salts is deionized water, methanol, ethanol and/or nitroxyl formamide. In the step (iii), the organic ligand is 2-methyl imidazole, benzimidazole or triethylene diamine. The metal salt includes zinc chloride, cobalt chloride, zinc bromide, cobalt bromide, zinc perchlorate, zinc nitrate, zinc acetate, zinc sulfate, cobalt dinitrate, cobalt acetate and/or cobalt sulfate. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the purging time is greater than 60 seconds. The gaseous oxygen source introduced into the reaction chamber is water, oxygen, ozone and/or hydrogen peroxide water, the initial temperature range of the diethyl zinc gaseous metal source is 150-250 degrees C, and the initial temperature range of the gaseous oxygen source is 10-80 degrees C. In the step (iii), forming a layer of metal organic structure on the surface of ZSM-5, the layer thickness is equal to the distance between two metal ions and different nitrogen atoms in the same organic ligand to form stable structure.
