PN CN109592639-A
TI Separating and purifying carbon monoxide and            hydrogen from tail gas of low-hydrogen, low-sulfur,            high-carbon monoxide ore furnace by pretreating raw            material gas, purifying, deoxidizing, hydrogenating,            desulfurizing and decarbonizing.
AU WANG X
   HE X
   XU K
   SHAN X
   ZHOU J
   WU Y
   YU T
   ZHANG L
   HUI Y
AE HUALU ENG & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CNCE-C)
GA 201935213C
AB    NOVELTY - Separating and purifying carbon monoxide and                hydrogen from a tail gas of a low-hydrogen,                low-sulfur, high-carbon monoxide ore furnace                involves: (1) pretreating raw material gas by                compressing the tail gas of low-hydrogen,                low-sulfur, high-carbon monoxide ore furnace to a                certain pressure by a raw material gas compressor                (A), and then removing impurities such as a small                amount of tar, naphthalene and benzene contained in                the tail gas of the ore furnace; (2) purifying the                raw material gas by again compressing the                pretreated ore tail gas to a certain pressure by a                raw material gas compressor (B), and then removing                chlorine, fluorine and other trace impurities in                the tail gas of the ore furnace through a                purification tower; (3) carrying out deoxidation on                the raw material gas; (4) carrying out raw material                gas conversion and hydrogenation; (5) carrying out                desulfurization and decarbonization; and (6)                carrying out carbon monoxide/hydrogen separation                and purification.
   USE - The method is useful for separating and                purifying carbon monoxide and hydrogen from a tail                gas of a low-hydrogen, low-sulfur, high-carbon                monoxide ore furnace.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the series of problems such                as poor degassing effect of the tail gas of the                low-hydrogen, low-sulfur, high-carbon monoxide,                high temperature change, easy conversion of the                catalyst, and high energy consumption of                desulfurization and decarbonization of the tail gas                of the ore furnace.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separating and purifying carbon monoxide and                hydrogen from a tail gas of a low-hydrogen,                low-sulfur, high-carbon monoxide ore furnace                involves: (1) pretreating raw material gas by                compressing the tail gas of low-hydrogen,                low-sulfur, high-carbon monoxide ore furnace to a                certain pressure by a raw material gas compressor                (A), and then removing impurities such as a small                amount of tar, naphthalene and benzene contained in                the tail gas of the ore furnace through a                pretreatment unit; (2) purifying the raw material                gas by again compressing the pretreated ore tail                gas to a certain pressure by a raw material gas                compressor (B), and then removing chlorine,                fluorine and other trace impurities in the tail gas                of the ore furnace through a purification tower;                (3) carrying out deoxidation on the raw material                gas by introducing the purified ore tail gas into a                deoxidation furnace to remove the oxygen in the                tail gas of the ore furnace to ensure the                deoxidation effect, and setting hydrogen                replenishment measure, where the hydrogen source is                any one or two of hydrogen product gas and                replacement tail gas in the step (6); (4) carrying                out raw material gas conversion and hydrogenation                by introducing the deoxidized ore furnace tail gas                into a temperature control converter, a                hydrogenation reactor and a shift gas cooling unit,                which are sequentially connected, converting a                portion of carbon monoxide with water vapor to                produce hydrogen and carbon dioxide, filling high                pressure cobalt-molybdenum wide temperature                sulfur-tolerant shift catalyst in the temperature                control converter, filling a hydrogenation catalyst                in the hydrogenation reactor to avoid the reverse                temperature of the wide temperature sulfur-tolerant                shift catalyst, and setting the sulfur                supplementation measure needs, where the                sulfur-supplement gas source is rich in hydrogen                sulfide acid gas in the step (5) to avoid high                carbon monoxide conversion over temperature, and                using a temperature control conversion furnace for                conversion; (5) carrying out desulfurization and                decarbonization by introducing the converted tail                gas of the furnace into a desulfurization and                decarbonization system, removing carbon dioxide,                hydrogen sulfide and carbonyl sulfide from the tail                gas of the converter, discharging the carbon                dioxide tail gas after removal to the atmosphere,                introducing a portion of the rich hydrogen sulfide                acid gas into a sulfur recovery, and returning a                portion to the first step to supplement the sulfur                in the step (4); and (6) carrying out carbon                monoxide/hydrogen separation and purification by                introducing the desulfurization and decarburization                tail gas into a carbon monoxide/hydrogen separation                and purification unit to realize the separation of                carbon monoxide, hydrogen and other components,                introducing a portion of the hydrogen product gas                into an outside as the hydrogen product gas,                introducing a portion of the hydrogen product gas                to the step (2) to supplement the hydrogen in the                step (3), and introducing the replacement tail gas                into the step (1) to supplement the hydrogen in the                step (3).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: In the step (4), the                  temperature control converter comprises a heat                  transfer section and a heat insulation section.                  The heat transfer section is provided with a heat                  transfer tube and an external steam drum. The                  steam drum has a steam specification of 1-5 MPa                  saturated steam. The water gas molar ratio in the                  tail gas of the furnace of the temperature                  control furnace is 0.2-1. The gas temperature in                  the outlet gas composition of the temperature                  control converter is 0.5-60 mol%. In the step                  (6), the carbon monoxide/hydrogen separation and                  purification unit comprises a methanol                  temperature change adsorption tower, a hydrogen                  pressure swing adsorption tower, a carbon                  monoxide-rich compressor, a carbon monoxide                  pressure swing adsorption tower, and a carbon                  monoxide compressor, which are connected in                  sequence. The tail gas of the ore furnace from                  the desulfurization and decarbonization is                  subjected to the methanol temperature change                  adsorption tower to remove trace methanol                  contained in the gas and then is subjected to the                  hydrogen pressure swing adsorption tower.                  Hydrogen is a non-adsorbed phase, the other gas                  is an adsorbed phase, and a portion of the                  unadsorbed hydrogen product gas is introduced                  into the outside as the hydrogen product gas. The                  carbon monoxide-rich compressor has an outlet                  pressure of 0.1-1.5 MPaG. The outlet pressure of                  the carbon monoxide compressor is 0.1-6                  MPaG.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The raw material gas                  compressor (A) has an outlet pressure of 0.1-2                  MPa. In the step (2), the outlet pressure of the                  raw material gas compressor (B) is 1-5 MPa. In                  the step (2), the temperature of the purification                  tower is 30-300 degrees C. In the step (3), the                  temperature of the deoxidizing furnace is 200-350                  degrees C. The temperature of the tail gas                  entering the hydrogenation reactor is 300-400                  degrees C. The sulfur content in the tail gas of                  the furnace is required to be greater than or                  equal to 30 mg/Nm3. Preferred Components: In the                  step (3), the hydrogen/oxygen molar ratio of the                  tail gas into the deoxidation furnace is greater                  than 2. The concentration of the hydrogen sulfide                  rich acid gas is 2-23 mol%. The total sulfur in                  the tail gas of the purification furnace is less                  than 0.1 ppm, and the carbon dioxide                  concentration is less than 20 ppm. The hydrogen                  purity in the hydrogen product gas is 95-99.99%.                  The carbon monoxide purity in the carbon monoxide                  product gas is 95-99.9%.

TI Processing corrosion-resistant mechanical transmission arm comprises e. g. taking raw materials, smelting, mixing sulfur hexafluoride and carbon dioxide, melting the chemical element, stirring and pouring molten metal into metal mold.
AU ZHOU H
   LU C
AE SUZHOU WEISIDAO INTELLIGENT TECHNOLOGY (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 201839834N
AB    NOVELTY - Processing corrosion-resistant mechanical transmission arm comprises e. g. taking all the raw materials, adding into smelting furnace, smelting, mixing sulfur hexafluoride and carbon dioxide to protect the gas, melting the chemical element, stirring for 10-15 minutes, allowing to stand for 20-30 minutes, pouring the molten metal into metal mold, preheating to 200-300 degrees C, obtaining the sheet blank, where the temperature of the billet in the preheating section of the furnace is controlled below 500 degrees C, heating, rolling, hot rolling, cooling to 730-740 degrees C and cooling to room temperature.
   USE - The method is useful for processing corrosion-resistant mechanical transmission arm.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing corrosion-resistant mechanical transmission arm comprises (i) taking 0.02-0.03 wt.% carbon, 0.36-0.53 wt.% aluminum, 0.73-0.84 wt.% zinc, 0.21- 0.28 wt.% silicon, 0.57-0.68 wt.% manganese, less than or equal to 0.030 wt.% sulfur, less than or equal to 0.030 wt.% phosphorus, 0.02-0.04 wt.% chromium, 0.64-0.79 wt.% nickel, 0.18-0.19 wt.% copper, 0.06- 0.12 wt.% molybdenum, 0.67-0.79 wt.% titanium, 0.02-0.04 wt.% palladium, 0.16- 0.29 wt.% platinum, 0.33-0.46 wt.% tungsten, 0.03-0.07 wt.% neodymium, 0.01- 0.02 wt.% cerium , 0.11-0.14 wt.% europium , 0.04-0.09 wt.% lutetium, 0.15- 0.21 wt.% gold, 0.42-0.54 wt.% silver, 0.01-0.02 wt.% gallium, 0.12- 0.17 wt.% yttrium, 0.54-0.67 wt.% stannum, 0.06-0.11 wt.% zirconium, 0.02-0.05 wt.% rhenium, 0.11-0.14 wt.% bismuth, 0.23-0.25 wt.% magnesium oxide, 0.01-0.08 wt.% copper oxide, 0.05-0.11 wt.% iron oxide, 0.05- 0.09 wt.% iron hydroxide and balance of iron, adding into smelting furnace, smelting, mixing sulfur hexafluoride and carbon dioxide to protect the gas, melting the chemical element, stirring for 10-15 minutes, allowing to stand for 20-30 minutes, pouring the molten metal into metal mold, preheating to 200-300 degrees C, obtaining the sheet blank, where the temperature of the billet in the preheating section of the furnace is controlled below 500 degrees C, heating to 600-800 degrees C for 1-3 hours, rolling at 700-750 degrees C, rolling finally at 800-850 degrees C, hot rolling, cooling to 730-740 degrees C and cooling to room temperature; (ii) adding the rolled billet into heating furnace, heating to 740-820 degrees C, cooling to room temperature by water cooling and air cooling, water-cooling to 390-420 degrees C with cooling rate of 3.8-4.3 degrees C/second, air cooling to 200-310 degrees C, cooling with water, cooling to 175-190 degrees C at 2.7-3.5 degrees C/seconds and air cooling to room temperature; (iii) heating the cooled blank to 550-610 degrees C with tempering induction heater for 6-9 seconds, incubating and cooling to room temperature; (iv) heating the cooled billets to 390-420 degrees C for 50 minutes for grain stabilization; (v) loading the stabilized billet and cooling to room temperature to obtain transmission arm; (vi) coating the surface of the transmission arm with metal wear resistant coating, where the metal wear resistant coating comprises 0.22-0.25 wt.% carbon, 0.23-0.26 wt.% boron, 2.2-2.4 wt.% chromium, 3.1-3.6 wt.% titanium, 0.3- 0.8 wt.% vanadium, 0.13-0.25 wt.% cobalt, 0.15-0.23 wt.% niobium, 0.12-0.17 wt.% calcium, 0.21-0.27 wt.% barium, 0.64-0.77 wt.% tungsten, 3.3-3.6 wt.% zinc, 1.5-1.8 wt.% samarium, 1.2-1.7 wt.% neodymium, 0.22- 0.35 wt.% promethium, 0.49-0.54 wt.% europium, 0.2-0.5 wt.% gadolinium, 4.21 - 5.28 wt.% aluminum, 3.1-3.4 wt.% auxiliary agent and balance of iron and the auxiliary agent includes 6-10 pts. wt. zircon and 12-15 pts. wt. kaolinite.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the raw material comprises 0.02 wt.% carbon, 0.36 wt.% aluminum, 0.73 wt.% zinc, 0.21 wt.% silicon, 0.57 wt.% manganese, less than or equal to 0.030 wt.% sulfur, less than or equal to 0.030 wt.% phosphorus, 0.02 wt.% chromium, 0.64 wt.% nickel, 0.18 wt.% copper, 0.06 wt.% molybdenum, 0.67 wt.% titanium, 0.02 wt.% palladium, 0.16 wt.% platinum, 0.33 wt.% tungsten, 0.03 wt.% neodymium, 0.01 wt.% cerium, 0.11 wt.% europium , 0.04 wt.% lutetium, 0.15 wt.% gold, 0.42 wt.% silver, 0.01 wt.% gallium, 0.12 wt.% yttrium, 0.54 wt.% stannum, 0.06 wt.% zirconium, 0.02 wt.% rhenium, 0.11 wt.% bismuth, 0.23 wt.% magnesium oxide, 0.01 wt.% copper oxide, 0.05 wt.% iron oxide, 0.05 wt.% iron hydroxide and balance of iron.

PN CN114304251-A
TI Controlling fading of freeze-dried fruits and vegetables stored at room temperature comprises e.g. (i) selecting fresh fruits and vegetables, cleaning the surface stains using water, cutting the trimmed fruits and vegetables into blocks, placing into high pressure carbon dioxide device and cooling.
AU HUANG S
   ZHAO K
   CHEN J
   SHEN J
   ZHANG M
AE UNIV JIANGNAN (UYJN-C)
   NINGBO HAITONG FOOD TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (EGIN-C)
GA 202255934S
AB    NOVELTY - Controlling fading of freeze-dried fruits and vegetables stored at room temperature comprises e.g. (i) selecting fresh fruits and vegetables with no surface spots and uniform color as raw materials and avoiding the adverse effect of the color of the raw materials on the color of the finally freeze-dried fruits and vegetables, (ii) cleaning the surface stains of the selected fruit and vegetable raw materials with clear water and removing unnecessary parts of the fruit and vegetable, (iii) cutting the trimmed fruits and vegetables into fruit and vegetable blocks with specific specifications by slicer according to the shape and texture, (iv) placing the fruit and vegetable blocks after cutting into a glass plate, placing in the high pressure carbon dioxide device, reaction kettle reaching the set pressure, processing for a period of time, after releasing pressure, cooling the processed fruit and vegetable blocks in ice bath for cooling.
   USE - The method is useful for controlling fading of freeze-dried fruits and vegetables stored at room temperature.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can protect the freeze-dried fruit and vegetable product; reduce the pigment substance loss degree; ensure the final water content of the product of less than 5% and prolong the fading time under the normal temperature storage condition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Controlling fading of freeze-dried fruits and vegetables stored at room temperature comprises (i) selecting fresh fruits and vegetables with no surface spots and uniform color as raw materials and avoiding the adverse effect of the color of the raw materials on the color of the finally freeze-dried fruits and vegetables, (ii) cleaning the surface stains of the selected fruit and vegetable raw materials with clear water and removing unnecessary parts of the fruit and vegetable, (iii) cutting the trimmed fruits and vegetables into fruit and vegetable blocks with specific specifications by slicer according to the shape and texture, (iv) placing the fruit and vegetable blocks after cutting into a glass plate, placing in the high pressure carbon dioxide device, reaction kettle reaching the set pressure, processing for a period of time, after releasing pressure, cooling the processed fruit and vegetable blocks in ice bath, (v) placing the fruit and vegetable blocks pretreated in the resultant step in a glass container, stacking the fruit and vegetable blocks on a dielectric resistance block for a specific thickness, performing radio frequency treatment and then placing the fruit and vegetable blocks in an ice bath for cooling, (vi) preparing microcapsule emulsion by taking gelatin as a wall material and grapefruit peel extract as a core material, performing microencapsulation in a freeze drying mode and dissolving microcapsule powder in distilled water to form a solution, (vii) mixing fruit and vegetable blocks according to a material-liquid ratio of 1: 6, immersing the microcapsule solution obtained in the resultant step for a period of time, taking out and placing on a sieve plate for draining, (viii) placing the fruit and vegetable blocks subjected to the dipping and film coating treatment in the resultant step at room temperature for airing, then spreading the fruit and vegetable blocks in a plastic culture dish and pre-freezing the fruit and vegetable blocks in a refrigerator at the temperature of -80degrees Celsius overnight for about 10 hours, (ix) freeze-drying the fruit and vegetable blocks treated in the resultant step for 10 hours until the final water content is lower than 5%, where the freeze-drying conditions are as follows, the temperature of the heating plate is 50degrees Celsius, the temperature of the cold trap is -40degrees Celsius and the vacuum condition is kept at 80 microPa, (x) sub packaging the freeze-dried fruit and vegetable blocks obtained in the resultant step into light-proof aluminum foil bags and filling nitrogen to extrude air components in the packaging bags for 10 s, (xi) sealing the aluminum foil bag by adopting a heat sealing machine immediately after the aluminum foil bag is filled with nitrogen, where the sealing condition is as follows 3 s at 180degrees Celsius, and (xii) sealing the freeze-dried fruit and vegetable in aluminum foil bags filled with nitrogen atmosphere and then placing in a normal-temperature environment to be protected from light.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In the step (iv) the high pressure carbon dioxide pre-treatment process is carried out by placing the fruit and vegetable blocks at 40degrees Celsius, processing for 30 minutes at 30 microPa, releasing pressure and taking out and cooling for 3 minutes in the ice bath. In the step (v) the radio frequency pretreatment is carried out at a frequency of 27.12 MHz and a power of 1600W, the stacking height of the fruit and vegetable blocks placed in a glass container reaches to 6-7cm, the fruit and vegetable blocks are treated for 10-15 minutes, the central temperature reaches about 60-70degrees Celsius, and the temperature is reduced by ice bath after the treatment. In the step (vi) the wall material of the microcapsule solution is prepared by dissolving 3 wt.% gelatin as wall material in the distilled water, magnetically stirring for 2 hours at the temperature of 40degrees Celsius under the condition of 600 rotation/minute (rpm). The microcapsule core material is prepared by using grapefruit peel as raw material, placing the grapefruit peel in a plastic tray, completely freezing at-80degrees Celsius, freeze-drying and grinding to obtain powder, mixing 1.5 g freeze-dried powder with 25 ml 70% ethanol solution for preparation, continuously stirring for 15 minutes and centrifuging to obtain supernatant, evaporating ethanol under vacuum conditions and flushing with nitrogen to remove residual solvent. The microcapsule solution was prepared by mixing grapefruit peel extract and gelatin solution in a mass ratio of 1:6 using a magnetic stirrer at 40degrees Celsius for 30 minutes at 200 rotation /minute (rpm) and the mixture was freeze-dried into a solid powder and rehydrated to obtain a microcapsule solution.

TI Copolyester, co-polyesterpolycarbonate or            co-polyesterpolyether copolymer useful for obtaining            foamable composition, foam or foamed product, comprises            either differing polyester units, and polycarbonate,            and coupling agents.
AU PORTER M E
   BARGER M A
   HE Y
   COSTEUX S
   BILLOVITS G F
   HUANG W
   NICOLI E
   KOSTER S
AE DDP SPECIALTY ELECTRONIC MATERIALS US (DUPO-C)
   DDP SPECIALTY ELECTRONIC MATERIALS US (DUPO-C)
   DDP SPECIALTY ELECTRONIC MATERIALS US (DUPO-C)
GA 202097063X
AB    NOVELTY - Copolyester, co-polyesterpolycarbonate or                co-polyesterpolyether copolymer comprises either                differing polyester units, or polyester units and                polycarbonate or polyether units or both, and one                or more coupling agents, and being characterized by                having polymerized units of one or more aromatic                diacid monomer, 10-40 mol.% sum of polymerized                units of one or more aliphatic diols, where the                mol.% is the sum of the moles of polymerized units                of the one or more aliphatic diols in the copolymer                expressed as a percentage of the total moles of                polymerized units that make up the copolymer, a                primary Tg of 85-125 degrees C as measured from the                inflection point on second heat up of the                differential scanning calorimetry (DSC) curve using                a heating/cooling rate of 10 degrees C/minute, and                a heat of melting Delta Hm peak not greater than 10                J/g after exposure to hydrostatic pressure of 1000                psi carbon dioxide at 135 degrees C for 4                hours.
   USE - The copolyester, co-polyesterpolycarbonate or                co-polyesterpolyether copolymer is useful for                obtaining foamable composition or foam or foamed                product (all claimed). The product is used in                insulation and/or other building and industrial                applications.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Copolyester, co-polyesterpolycarbonate or                co-polyesterpolyether copolymer comprises (1)                either differing polyester units, or polyester                units and polycarbonate or polyether units or both,                and one or more coupling agents, and being                characterized by having polymerized units of one or                more aromatic diacid monomer, 10-40 mol.% sum of                polymerized units of one or more aliphatic diols,                where the mol.% is the sum of the moles of                polymerized units of the one or more aliphatic                diols in the copolymer expressed as a percentage of                the total moles of polymerized units that make up                the copolymer, a primary Tg of 85-125 degrees C as                measured from the inflection point on second heat                up of the differential scanning calorimetry (DSC)                curve using a heating/cooling rate of 10 degrees                C/minute, and a heat of melting Delta Hm peak not                greater than 10 J/g after exposure to hydrostatic                pressure of 1000 psi carbon dioxide at 135 degrees                C for 4 hours, or (2) polymerized structural units                chosen from (a) one or more aliphatic diol, (b) one                or more aromatic diacid, (c) one or more aromatic                diol, (d) one or more organic carbonate, where the                copolyester copolymer comprises polymerized                structural units a+b and the                copolyester-polycarbonate copolymer comprises                polymerized structural units a+b+c+d, and the                copolyester-polyether copolymer comprises                polymerized structural units a+b+c optionally d,                and ether functionality in the polymer backbone,                where the copolymer being characterized by (i) the                polymerized structural units of one or more                aliphatic diols (a) are present in an amount                totaling of 15-40 mol.%, where the mol.% is the sum                of the moles of polymerized structural units of the                one or more aliphatic diols in the copolymer                expressed as a percentage of the total moles of                polymerized structural units, a+b+c+d, that make up                the copolymer, (ii) a primary Tg of 85-125 degrees                C as measured from the inflection point on second                heat up of the DSC curve using a heating/cooling                rate of 10 degrees C/minute, and (iii) a heat of                melting Delta Hm peak not greater than 10 J/g after                exposure to hydrostatic pressure of 1000 psi carbon                dioxide at 135 degrees C for 4 hours. INDEPENDENT                CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a foamable composition comprising the                copolyester, co-polyesterpolycarbonate or                co-polyesterpolyether copolymer and one or more                blowing agents, and an immiscible polyolefin, a                colorant, a filler, a flame retardant agent, an                infra-red attenuator, a nucleating agent, a                lubricant, an antistatic agent, or an antioxidant,                or their combinations;
   (2) a foam or foamed product obtained from the                composition; and
   (3) a method of forming the copolyester,                co-polyesterpolycarbonate or co-polyesterpolyether                copolymer involves (i) melting a blend of at least                two polymers chosen from a first polyester polymer                and one or more other polymer chosen from one or                more polycarbonate polymer and one or more other                polyester polymer, or their combinations, and in                the presence of a transesterification catalyst, and                optionally, a chain coupling agent, (ii)                maintaining a temperature greater than 200-330                degrees C for at least 3 minutes and not greater                than 180 minutes, optionally with mixing, and (iii)                cooling to produce a solid copolymer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Component: The copolyester,                  co-polyesterpolycarbonate or                  co-polyesterpolyether copolymer has a B(X) or                  B(X+X') value of 0.20 or greater, where B is the                  Koenig B value for copolymer randomness, (X) is                  the molar fraction of comonomer polyester                  structural units or comonomer polycarbonate                  and/or polyether structural units, and (X+X') is                  the molar fraction of comonomer polyester                  structural units or comonomer polycarbonate                  and/or polyether structural units, including any                  units comprising residue fragments in the                  copolymer. The copolyester,                  co-polyesterpolycarbonate or                  co-polyesterpolyether copolymer has a Koenig B                  value of greater than 0.90 for copolymers having                  a ratio of polyester:polycarbonate structural                  units in the copolymer, or                  polyester:(polycarbonate+polyether) structural                  units in the copolymer in a mole ratio of from                  65:35-85:15. The aromatic diacid monomers are                  chosen from phthalic acid, terephthalic acid,                  isophthalic acid, or 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid.                  The portion of the polyester units in the                  copolymer are derived from recycled polyethylene                  terephthalate. The diols are chosen from ethylene                  glycol, 1,2-propylene glycol, 1,3-propylene                  glycol, butylene glycol, cyclohexane dimethanol,                  isosorbide, and spiroglycol. The copolyester,                  co-polyesterpolycarbonate or                  co-polyesterpolyether comprises at least 7 mol.%                  polymerized units of bisphenol A. The mole ratio                  of polyester polymer structural units to the                  total of polycarbonate polymer structural units                  and polyether polymer structural units is from                  40:60-85:15. The blowing agent is chosen from one                  or more pentane hydrocarbons, one or more                  hydrofluoroolefin, carbon dioxide, nitrogen,                  oxygen, water, alcohols, ketones, ethers,                  halogenated hydrocarbons, or olefins, or their                  combinations. The blowing agent is chosen from                  one or more chemical blowing agents. The foam or                  foamed product is obtained by (a) extrusion                  foaming of the foamable composition, or (b)                  expansion of a solid foamable bead made from the                  foamable composition. The foam has a density of                  0.01-0.1 g/cm3. The weight ratio of first                  polyester polymer to one or more other polymers                  is 40:60-85:15. The weight ratio of the total of                  all polyester polymers to polycarbonate polymers                  is 40:60-85:15. The polyester polymers are                  recycled polyethylene terephthalate. Preferred                  Method: The method further involves collecting at                  least a portion of any ethylene carbonate                  produced.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN110367103-A
TI Cultivating mustard leaves comprises e.g. taking seeds by using sieve, soaking in warm water, removing floating seeds and debris, removing detached seed shells, soaking in normal water, drying, sowing, adding to fresh water, and exposing.
AU LAI H
   LI J
   YAN W
AE FUJIAN SINO-SCI BIOLOGY CO LTD (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 201990398G
AB    NOVELTY - Cultivating mustard leaves comprises e.g. (i) taking seeds by using a sieve with a pore size of 1-2 mm, soaking in warm water at 55 degrees C for 5-10 minutes with constant stirring to remove floating seeds and debris, taking the seeds which will sink to the bottom, spreading in the light for half an hour, removing the detached seed shell, soaking mature and intact seeds in normal temperature water for 2-6 hours, drying, sowing in an autoclaved moist medium, adding to fresh water at 20-25 degrees C and a humidity of 60-100%, germinating in a germination box for 24-36 hours, and exposing the roots after whitening, and (ii) cultivating seedlings by using circulating nutrient solution, where the seedling stage comprises root growth period and strong seedling stage, comprising root growth period: when the seed is germinated, cracking the seed coat, moving the roots are moved to the seedling bed for root growth at a density of 1000-1500 plants/m2, and adding to a hole tray of the seeding medium.
   USE - The method is useful for cultivating mustard leaves.
   ADVANTAGE - The mustard is crispy, delicious, tender, slag-free, sweet and delicious; and hasa increased yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cultivating mustard leaves comprises (i) taking seeds by using a sieve with a pore size of 1-2 mm, soaking in warm water at 55 degrees C for 5-10 minutes with constant stirring to remove floating seeds and debris, taking the seeds which will sink to the bottom, spreading in the light for half an hour, removing the detached seed shell, soaking mature and intact seeds in normal temperature water for 2-6 hours, drying, sowing in an autoclaved moist medium, adding to fresh water at 20-25 degrees C and a humidity of 60-100%, germinating in a germination box for 24-36 hours, and exposing the roots after whitening, (ii) cultivating seedlings by using circulating nutrient solution, where the seedling stage comprises root growth period and strong seedling stage, comprising root growth period: when the seed is germinated, cracking the seed coat, moving the roots are moved to the seedling bed for root growth at a density of 1000-1500 plants/m2, adding to a hole tray at the bottom of the seeding medium to provide nutrients and nutrient solution with low concentration nutrient solution, beginning the liquid level to contact the bottom of the medium, when the root system is longer than 30 mm, the liquid level of the nutrient solution is lowered to contact the bottom of the perforated tray, strong seedling stage: after the seedling grows out of the cotyledon, it enters the strong seedling stage, where the environmental conditions during the nursery period are: the temperature of day and night is maintained at 20-25 degrees C/15-20 degrees C, humidity is 50-80%, the temperature of the nutrient solution is maintained at 18-22 degrees C, and the concentration of carbon dioxide is 400-800 parts per million (ppm), (iii) cultivating seedlings by using a circulating nutrient solution, using shortened stem growth phase and a leaf growth phase, short stem length: when the seedling grows to 2-4 true leaves, planting, where the planting density is 30-100 plants/m2, the nutrient level is lowered to 1/3 of the root system, and the nutrient solution concentration is increased, the environmental conditions are: the temperature of day and night is maintained at 20-25 degrees C/15-20 degrees C, humidity is 50-80%, the temperature of the nutrient solution is maintained at 18-22 degrees C, the concentration of carbon dioxide is 300-500 ppm, leaf growth period: after one week of planting, entering the leaf growth period, at this time increase the content of nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium, boron and calcium in the nutrient solution, and the environmental conditions are: the temperature of day and night is maintained at 20-25 degrees C/15-20 degrees C, humidity is 50-80%, the temperature of the nutrient solution is maintained at 18-22 degrees C, and the concentration of carbon dioxide is 800-1200 ppm, and (iv) harvesting the plants within three days after planting for 35 days, and harvesting the whole plant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: The root growth period comprises: the light intensity of 150-250 mu mol.m-2.s-1, the illumination duration of 12-14 hours/day, the spectral composition is: the photon number of the wavelength at 400-499 nm is 21-25%, the ratio of the photon number of the wavelength of 500-599 nm is 14-15%, the proportion of the photon number of the wavelength of 600-699 nm is 46-50%, and the proportion of the photon number of other wavelengths is 12-17%. The root growth period nutrient solution conditions comprise: nutrient solution EC of 0.6-1 mS/cm, and pH value is controlled at 5.5-7. The light source conditions in the strong seedling stage are: the light intensity of 200-300 mu mol.m-2.s-1, and the illumination duration is 12-18 hours/day. The spectral composition is: the ratio of the photon number of the wavelength of 400-499 nm is 21-25%, the proportion of the photon number of the wavelength of 500-599 nm is 14-15%, and the proportion of the photon of the wavelength of 600-699 nm is 46-50%, and the proportion of other wavelengths of light quantum is 12-17%. The nutrient solution EC in the strong seedling stage is 0.8-1.5 mS/cm, and the pH value is 5.5-7. The light source condition during planting has a light intensity of 200-400 mu mol.m-2.s-1, and the illumination duration is 12-14 hours/day, where the spectral composition comprises the ratio of the photon number of the wavelength of 380-399 nm is less than or equal to 0.1%, the proportion of the photon of the wavelength of 400-499 nm is 13-15%, the ratio of the photon number of the wavelength of 500-599 nm is 17-19%, the ratio of the photon number of wavelength of 600-699 nm is 50-55%, and the proportion of the photon of the wavelength of 700-780 nm is 13-17%. The growth period EC of the shortening stem is 2-3.5 mS/cm, and the pH value is 5.5-7.5. The leaf growth period EC condition comprises is 2-3.5 mS/cm, and the pH is controlled is 5.5-7.5. Preferred Components: The nutrient solution comprises 780 mg/l potassium nitrate, 150 mg/l ammonium dihydrogen phosphate, 330 mg/l potassium sulfate, 30 mg/l ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid-sodium iron, 450 mg/l calcium nitrate tetrahydrate, 110 mg/l magnesium sulfate, 2 mg/l boric acid, 1.5 mg/l manganese sulfate monohydrate, 0.25 mg/l zinc sulfate heptahydrate, 0.13 mg/l anhydrous copper sulfate, 0.03 mg/l ammonium tetrahydrate molybdate, 0.1 ml/l sugar alcohol calcium, 0.6 ml/l sugar alcohol magnesium, and 0.3-0.8 ml/l pure potassium type liquid fertilizer. The nutrient solution at strong seedling stage comprises 780 mg/l potassium nitrate, 150 mg/l ammonium dihydrogen phosphate, 330 mg/l potassium sulfate, 30 mg/l ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid-sodium iron, 450 mg/l calcium nitrate tetrahydrate, 110 mg/l magnesium sulfate, 2 mg/l boric acid, 1.5 m/l manganese sulfate monohydrate, 0.25 mg/l zinc sulfate heptahydrate, 0.13 mg/l anhydrous copper sulfate, 0.03 mg/l ammonium tetrahydrate molybdate, 0.1 ml/l sugar alcohol calcium, 0.6 cm/l sugar alcohol magnesium, and 0.3-0.8 ml/l pure potassium type liquid fertilizer. The nutrient solution used during growth period EC of the shortening stem comprises: 780 mg/l potassium nitrate, 150 mg/l ammonium dihydrogen phosphate, 330 mg/l potassium sulfate, 30 mg/l ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid-sodium iron, 450 mg/l calcium nitrate tetrahydrate, 110 mg/l magnesium sulfate, 2 mg/l boric acid, 1.5 mg/l manganese sulfate monohydrate, 0.25 mg/l zinc sulfate heptahydrate, 0.13 mg/l anhydrous copper sulfate, 0.03 mg/l ammonium tetrahydrate molybdate, 0.1 ml/l sugar alcohol calcium, 0.6 cm/l sugar alcohol magnesium, and 0.3-0.8 ml/l pure potassium type clear liquid fertilizer. The nutrient solution used during leaf growth period comprises 790 mg/l potassium nitrate, 150 mg/l ammonium dihydrogen phosphate, 330 mg/l potassium sulfate, 30 mg/l ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid-sodium iron, 460 mg/l calcium nitrate tetrahydrate, 110 mg/l magnesium sulfate, 2.5 mg/l boric acid, 1.5 mg/l manganese sulfate monohydrate, 0.25 mg/l zinc sulfate heptahydrate, 0.13 mg/l anhydrous copper sulfate, 0.03 mg/l ammonium tetrahydrate molybdate, 0.2 ml/l calcium sugar alcohol, 0.6 ml/l magnesium alkoxide, and 0.4-1 ml/l pure potassium type clear liquid fertilizer.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.

PT P
PN WO2022137038-A1
TI Plant for producing decarbonized oxide or            hydroxide, consists of electric calciner adapted to            receive flow of carbonate at inlet, electric power and            to release at least one flow of carbonic gas and at            least one flow of oxide adapted to provide flow of            buffering substance.
AU CAPPELLO G
AE CAPPELLO G (CAPP-Individual)
GA 202285989M
AB    NOVELTY - A plant (100) consists of an electric calciner                (10), a contactor (20), a pH correction apparatus                (30) and a dosing device (40). The electric                calciner is adapted to receive a flow of carbonate                (110) at the inlet, electric power (120) and to                release at least one flow of carbonic gas (140) at                the outlet at high temperature and at least one                flow of oxide (130) adapted to provide a flow of                buffering substance. The contactor is adapted to                receive the flow of carbonic gas released by the                electric calciner at the inlet, a flow of carbonate                (220), and a flow of water (210) and is adapted to                make the carbonate react with water (210) and the                carbon dioxide present in the carbonic gas. The pH                correction apparatus (30) is adapted to receive at                the inlet at least one flow of buffering substance                (440) and the flow of ionic mixture (230) is                adapted to make the ionic mixture (230) react with                the buffering substance (440).
   USE - Plant used for producing decarbonized oxide or                hydroxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The plant can efficiently generated oxides by                calcination of carbonates with permanent storage of                carbon dioxides at lower cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A plant (100) consists of an electric calciner                (10), a contactor (20), a pH correction apparatus                (30) and a dosing device (40). The electric                calciner is adapted to receive a flow of carbonate                (110) at the inlet, electric power (120) and to                release at least one flow of carbonic gas at the                outlet at high temperature and at least one flow of                oxide (130) adapted to provide a flow of buffering                substance. The contactor is adapted to receive the                flow of carbonic gas released by the electric                calciner at the inlet, a flow of carbonate (220),                and a flow of water (210) and is adapted to make                the carbonate (220) react with water (210) and the                carbon dioxide present in the carbonic gas in which                calcium can be substituted with magnesium if                present in the carbonate and to release at the                outlet at least one flow of ionic mixture (230).                The contactor is provided with a unit for releasing                non-soluble gases (221). The pH correction                apparatus (30) is adapted to receive at the inlet                at least one flow of buffering substance (440) and                the flow of ionic mixture (230) is adapted to make                the ionic mixture (230) react with the buffering                substance (440) according to reactions of calcium                oxide, hydrogen and carbon dioxide to obtain                calcium bicarbonate, and reaction of calcium                hydroxide and carbon dioxide to obtain calcium                bicarbonate, and to release at the outlet a flow of                buffered ionic mixture (240). The dosing device is                adapted to receive at the inlet a flow of buffering                substance (130) deriving from the electric calciner                (10) and to release at the outlet a predetermined                quantity of buffering substance (440) to feed the                pH correction apparatus (30) and possibly a                quantity of buffering substance (450) available for                use. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                decarbonized calcination method, which involves                providing the electric calciner, feeding the                electric calciner with electric power and flow of                carbonate to obtain the calcination of the                carbonate, releasing the flow of carbonic gas at                high temperature and a flow of oxide (130), at the                outlet of the electric calciner, conveying the flow                of carbonic gas, conveying the flow of oxide,                obtaining the flow of buffering substance from the                flow of oxide, providing the contactor, feeding the                contactor with the flow of carbonic gas produced by                the electric calciner and a predetermined flow of                water in order to obtain flow of ionic mixture                through the reaction, releasing the flow of ionic                mixture at the outlet of the contactor, conveying                the ionic mixture, providing the dosing device,                feeding the flow of buffering substance to the                dosing device, releasing a predetermined flow of                buffering substance at the outlet of the dosing                device, conveying the flow of buffering substance,                providing pH correction apparatus, feeding the pH                correction apparatus with the flow of ionic mixture                (230) and with the predetermined flow of buffering                substance so that the reactions take place,                releasing the flow of buffered ionic mixture (240)                at the outlet of the pH correction apparatus and                placing the flow of buffered ionic mixture into the                sea.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                plant.
   Electric calciner (10)
   Contactor (20)
   Correction apparatus (30)
   Dosing device (40)
   Electric power (120)
   Buffering substances (130,440,450)
   Carbonic gas (140)
   Water (210)
   Carbonate (220)
   Ionic mixture (230)
   Buffered ionic mixture (240)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The plant comprises a                  hydroxide production unit (60) installed between                  the electric calciner (10) and the pH correction                  apparatus (30), to receive at the inlet at least                  one flow (130, 440) of oxide originated by the                  electric calciner, a predetermined flow of water                  (610), a hydroxide production unit adapted to                  make the oxide (130, 440) react with water (610).                  The buffering substance is hydroxide produced by                  the hydroxide production unit (60). The contactor                  (20) has a volume that allows a minimum contact                  time of the water with the carbonate and the                  carbonic gas (140) of 1000 seconds. The quantity                  of carbonate introduced into the contactor (20)                  is zero and the contactor (20) has a volume that                  allows a contact time of the water with the                  carbonic gas (140, 151) of more than 10 seconds,                  preferably 60-300 seconds. The plant further                  comprises a control unit and a meter of the                  chemical parameters of water (51), adapted to                  measure the pH and/or the alkalinity and/or the                  hardness of the ionic mixture (230) or of the                  buffered ionic mixture (240) and to provide the                  measurement to the control unit, adapted to                  control the dosing device so that it feeds the pH                  correction apparatus with the quantity of                  buffering substance that is suitable for                  obtaining a buffered ionic mixture (240) with a                  desired pH, a conduit adapted to feed the flow of                  vapor released by the hydroxide production unit                  (60) to the electric calciner and carbonate                  preheating unit adapted to receive at the inlet                  the flow of carbonic gas at high temperature                  released by the electric calciner, a flow of                  carbonate and to release at the outlet a flow of                  carbonic gas (151) and a preheated flow of                  carbonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The buffering                  substance is oxide (130, 440) originated by the                  electric calciner and the pH correction apparatus                  (30) is adapted to make the ionic mixture (230)                  react with the oxide (440) according to the                  reaction of calcium oxide, hydronium ion and                  calcium carbonate, in which calcium can be                  substituted with magnesium if present in the                  carbonate. Preferred Process: The method further                  involves feeding the contactor with a                  predetermined flow of carbonate so that the                  reaction can take place, conveying the flow of                  vapor, feeding the flow of vapor to the electric                  calciner, providing a carbonate preheating unit,                  feeding the carbonate preheating unit with the                  flow of carbonic gas at high temperature released                  by the electric calciner and flow of carbonate,                  releasing a flow of preheated carbonate from the                  carbonate preheating unit and feeding the                  electric calciner with the preheated flow of                  carbonate.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN114958517-A
TI Preparing low-degree fermentation type sparkling            rice wine used as a beverage for daily drinking of            farm, involves selecting full glutinous rice with good            quality from glutinous rice, sieving glutinous rice for            one to two times by using sieving machine to eliminate            secondary rice.
AU HUANG L
   QU L
AE SHANGHAI STRAY & STAY TRADING CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022B78117
AB    NOVELTY - Low-degree fermentation type sparkling rice                wine involves selecting full glutinous rice with                good quality from the glutinous rice, sieving the                glutinous rice for 1-2 times by using a sieving                machine to eliminate secondary rice. The glutinous                rice is 500-600 pts. wt. The distiller's yeast is                selected from 5-10 pts. wt., putting tap water into                a distillation machine, adding a potassium                permanganate solution during primary distillation,                removing organic matters and carbon dioxide through                the potassium permanganate solution, adding a                non-volatile acid during secondary distillation to                enable ammonia to become a non-volatile ammonium                salt, and hermetically filling for later use after                the distillation is finished, putting 600 portions                of 500-fold polished glutinous rice into a cleaning                machine, where the rotating speed of the cleaning                machine is 150-fold 200r/min, cleaning for 1-3min                for 2-3 times.
   USE - Method for preparing low-degree fermentation                type sparkling rice wine used as a beverage for                daily drinking of the farm.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare low-degree                fermentation type bubble rice wine, which has good                quality of sticky rice, and high yield and quality                of rice wine. The rice wine has good taste, and                permeates into faint scent of bamboo in steaming                way, making the subsequent brewing rice wine more                flavour.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Low-degree fermentation type sparkling rice                wine involves selecting full glutinous rice with                good quality from the glutinous rice, sieving the                glutinous rice for 1-2 times by using a sieving                machine to eliminate secondary rice. The glutinous                rice is 500-600 pts. wt. The distiller's yeast is                selected from 5-10 pts. wt., putting tap water into                a distillation machine, adding a potassium                permanganate solution during primary distillation,                removing organic matters and carbon dioxide through                the potassium permanganate solution, adding a                non-volatile acid during secondary distillation to                enable ammonia to become a non-volatile ammonium                salt, and hermetically filling for later use after                the distillation is finished, putting 600 portions                of 500-fold polished glutinous rice into a cleaning                machine, where the rotating speed of the cleaning                machine is 150-fold 200r/min, cleaning for 1-3min                for 2-3 times, cleaning the rice flour on the                surface of the polished glutinous rice, pouring the                cleaned glutinous rice into a filter screen, taking                the distilled water in the sealing tank, washing                the polished glutinous rice in the filter screen                for 1-2 times, washing for 1-2min each time,                washing away impurities attached to the surface of                the polished glutinous rice in the filter screen,                and filling a large barrel for standby after                washing, adding glutinous rice in the barrel into                3-5kg of distilled water in the sealed tank,                soaking for 3-5h, taking out after soaking, putting                into gauze, and draining water for later use,                putting the gauze into a steamer tray, uniformly                spreading the sticky rice in the gauze, putting the                steamer tray into a steamer, continuously steaming                for 25-40min after the water temperature in the                steamer is raised to 90-100&#8451;, and taking                out the steamed sticky rice for later use after                steaming, taking out the steamed sticky rice,                pouring the sticky rice into a sieve, washing and                cooling the sticky rice by using the distilled                water, scattering the sticky rice by wearing                gloves, wherein the temperature of the sticky rice                is 25-35&#8451; when the cooling is finished,                and putting the cooled sticky rice into an aseptic                tank for later use after the cooling is finished,                adding the glutinous rice in the aseptic tank into                a mixer, adding 3-6 pts. wt. of the sweet distiller                'S yeast, controlling the rotating speed of the                mixer at 75-150r/min, controlling the temperature                of the mixer at 30-35&#8451;, putting the mixer                into the aseptic tank again after uniformly                stirring, cleaning and sterilizing the mixer,                adding 150ml of distilled water in the sealed tank                adding 2-4 pts. wt. of the sweet distiller' S yeast                for mixing, controlling the rotating speed of the                mixer at 200-300r/min, controlling the temperature                of the mixer at 30-35&#8451;, and uniformly                adding the mixture into the aseptic tank after                uniformly mixing, transferring the sterile tank                into a fermentation chamber, pressing a groove on                the top of the glutinous rice by using a sterile                round bar, covering a sealing cover, controlling                the temperature of the fermentation chamber to be                35-45&#8451;, controlling the fermentation time                to be 2-3 days, and keeping the fermented glutinous                rice for later use, pouring the glutinous rice and                distiller' S yeast mixed solution fermented into a                centrifuge, controlling the rotation speed of the                centrifuge at 2500-3000r/min, transferring the rice                wine filled in the sterile box to a packaging                machine for packaging, and printing a label by                using a labeling machine for pasting.

PN CN114848759-A
TI Processing Yao bath liquid supercritical            extraction method useful for improving sub-health and            enhancing immunity, comprises e.g. taking hawthorn,            radix sophorae flavescentis, golden cypress, radix            zanthoxyli, and knotweed, stirring and frying in a            casserole, and then homogenizing by high pressure.
AU JIANG R
   CHEN J
   LI M
AE GUIZHOU SAATCHITAIHO PHARM TECHNOLOGY CO (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2022A4663A
AB    NOVELTY - Processing Yao bath liquid supercritical                extraction method comprises taking 1-4 pts. wt.                hawthorn, 5-9 pts. wt. radix sophorae flavescentis,                2-4 pts. wt. pseudo-ginseng, 2-9 pts. wt. golden                cypress, 10-14 pts. wt. radix zanthoxyli, 2-6 pts.                wt. Chinese lizardtail rhizome or herb, 1-5 pts.                wt. Polygonum multiflorum, 5-10 pts. wt. common                cnidium fruit, 10-13 pts. wt. light bamboo leaves,                mixing 2-6 pts. wt. Polygonatum glutinosa and 1-3                pts. wt. knotweed, stirring and frying in a                casserole at 80-90&#176;C for 20-30 minutes,                covering with plastic film and simmering at                50-60&#8451; for 20-50 minutes, mixing                above-mentioned extract with glycerin, water and                ethanol, and pectin, soaking soluble chitosan at                30-35&#8451; for 3-5 minutes and then                homogenizing by high pressure.
   USE - The liquid is useful for improving sub-health                and enhancing immunity (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The liquid: improves the health care effect;                produces product having good stability; and                utilizes active components are more                sufficient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing Yao bath liquid supercritical                extraction method comprises (i) taking 1-4 pts. wt.                hawthorn, 5-9 pts. wt. radix sophorae flavescentis,                2-4 pts. wt. pseudo-ginseng, 2-9 pts. wt. golden                cypress, 10-14 pts. wt. radix zanthoxyli, 2-6 pts.                wt. Chinese lizardtail rhizome or herb, 1-5 pts.                wt. Polygonum multiflorum, 5-10 pts. wt. common                cnidium fruit, 10-13 pts. wt. light bamboo leaves,                mixing 2-6 pts. wt. Polygonatum glutinosa and 1-3                pts. wt. knotweed, stirring and frying in a                casserole at 80-90&#176;C for 20-30 minutes,                covering with plastic film and simmering at                50-60&#8451; for 20-50 minutes, (ii) placing                the above medicinal materials in a pulverizer and                pulverizing for 20-30 minutes to obtain grits,                placing the coarse powder into an ultrafine                pulverizer and pulverizing for 1-3 hours to obtain                ultrafine powder, mixing the superfine powder with                rice vinegar and rice wine, adding water to adjust                the moisture content of the mixture to 88-90%,                placing the mixture in a clay pot, soaking the                outside in warm water at 50-55&#176;C,                insulating for 3-5 hours, and lowering the                temperature of the mixture to room temperature,                (iii) inoculating Lactobacillusinto the mixture,                fermenting at 25-28&#176;C for 15-18 hours,                stirring for 20-30 minutes, lowering the                temperature to 23-25&#176;C and continuously                fermenting for 5-10 hours, inoculating yeast into                the mixture, fermenting at 28-30&#176;C for                17-22 hours, lowering the temperature to                25-28&#176;C, and fermenting for 10-15 hours,                (iv) freezing the above fermentation into a                high-speed blender, stirring at speed of 500-700                revolutions/minute for 14-16 hours, then sealing                and placing in a refrigerator at minus                20-30&#8451; for 9-13 hours, adjusting the                temperature to minus 2-5&#176;C, continuously                freezing for 20-30 hours, placing the frozen                material in an ice crusher and crushing into ice                slag with a particle size of 1-3 mm, (v) where                spices includes 2-4 pts. wt. mint, 3-5 pts. wt.                fresh tea leaves, 10-15 pts. wt. honeysuckle, 8-13                pts. wt. dendrobium leaves, 10-15 pts. wt. Bletilla                striata leaves, 3-5 pts. wt. camphor leaves, mixing                5-10 pts. wt. rosemary, rinsing with water for                10-15 minutes, controlling the moisture, placing                all the materials into the beater for beating, and                using the rubber mill for 3-5 times to obtain                slurry, (vi) mixing the ice slag obtained in the                above step (iv) and the slurry obtained in the step                (v), and placing into the extraction kettle, then                filling the extraction kettle with liquid carbon                dioxide and adjusting the temperature for                increasing the pressure, controlling temperature in                the extraction kettle to be 20-30&#176;C under                pressure of 40-50 MPa, and extracting for 2-3                hours, and introducing the solution for dissolving                the extract into the separator, and separating to                obtain raw drug extract, microwaving residue after                extraction for 20-30 seconds, placing into the                extraction kettle again, filling with liquid carbon                dioxide and adjusted the temperature and pressure,                controlling the temperature in the extraction                kettle to 50-55 &#8451; under pressure of 60-70                MPa, extracting for 5-8 hours, introducing the                solution for dissolving the extract into the                separator, and separating to obtain raw drug                extract, combining two extracts, where the mass                ratio of the slurry and ice residue is 1:2-7, and                power of the microwave is 500-600 W, and (vii)                mixing above-mentioned extract with glycerin, water                and ethanol, and pectin, soaking soluble chitosan                at 30-35&#8451; for 3-5 minutes and then                homogenizing by high pressure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the                  consumption of the rice vinegar is 13-15 wt.% of                  the quality of the ultrafine powder. The content                  of acetic acid in the rice vinegar is 2-4 g/l.                  The consumption of the rice wine is 5-8 wt.% of                  the rice vinegar quality, and the alcohol content                  in the rice wine is 13-18 wt.%. The depth of the                  warm water exceeds the depth of the fermentation                  product in the pot. In the step (iii), the                  Lactobacillus is prepared by mixing Lactobacillus                  delbrueckii and Lactobacillus acidophilus in a                  mass ratio of 1:3-7. The dosage of the                  Lactobacillus is 4-6%. The yeast is Saccharomyces                  cerevisiae. The yeast dosage is 6-10 wt.% of the                  mass of the mixture. In the step (vii), the                  glycerin consumption is 1-3 wt.% of the extract                  quality. The consumption of the water is 10-15                  times the quality of the extract. The consumption                  of the ethanol is 2-3 times of glycerin quality.                  The dosage of the pectin is 5-8 wt.% of the                  water. The dosage of the soluble chitosan is 3-5                  wt.% of the water.
   ACTIVITY - Immunostimulant.

PN CN113229036-A
TI Cultivating Ganoderma lucidum, by sterilizing selenium-enriched medium, inoculating Ganoderma lucidum strain in medium, performing management of Ganoderma lucidum and harvesting Ganoderma lucidum.
AU XU M
   DENG Z
   WANG C
   GU Z
   BU H
   XIA L
   CHEN Z
   ZHENG W
   DU D
   LI J
   ZHOU M
   QIN J
AE CHANGDE VOCATIONAL TECH COLLEGE (CHAN-Non-standard)
   HUNAN CHUNLU AGRIC DEV CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 202194926E
AB    NOVELTY - Method for performing cultivation of selenium-enriched and high-quality Ganoderma lucidum involves (a) preparing culture medium (A)-(D), (b) making culture medium, (c) preparing selenium solution, (d) performing selenium supplementation in medium, (e) performing sterilization, (f) performing inoculation by placing the strain on the surface of the culture medium for inoculation, disinfecting the strain with alcohol before inoculation, sterilizing utensils to ensure the uniformity of the bacterial species distribution during the inoculation process, and bandaging bag opening in time after inoculation, (g) performing management of strain and selenium supplementation, (h) performing management of G.lucidum, and (i) harvesting G.lucidum.
   USE - The method is useful for cultivating G.lucidum.
   ADVANTAGE - The method cultivates selenium-enriched G.lucidum with high-quality, high yield, and improved nutritional value and medicinal value, in a simple and cost-effective manner, and is suitable for large-scale mass production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for performing cultivation of selenium-enriched and high-quality Ganoderma lucidum involves (a) preparing culture medium (A)-(D), (b) making culture medium by pulverizing raw materials of the culture medium, mixing them, adding water to mix well, adding glacial acetic acid solution when mixing the materials, piling up for 1-2 hours after mixing, turning the pile once, adding lime to adjust its pH, covering it, piling, fermenting for 3-5 days, and turning pile once a day to obtain culture medium, (c) preparing selenium solution by mixing selenium fertilizer with water to form 70-120 mg/kg selenium solution, where selenium fertilizer is a mixture of sodium selenite and nitrogen fertilizer, (d) performing selenium supplementation in medium by adding 6-10 kg selenium solution to every 50 kg medium, stirring well and mixing well to obtain selenium-enriched medium for cultivation, (e) performing sterilization by bagging selenium-enriched medium, and sterilizing by normal pressure sterilization or high pressure sterilization, (f) performing inoculation by placing the strain on the surface of the culture medium for inoculation, disinfecting the strain with alcohol before inoculation, sterilizing utensils to ensure the uniformity of the bacterial species distribution during the inoculation process, and bandaging bag opening in time after inoculation, (g) performing management of strain and selenium supplementation by putting inoculated bacteria bag into culture room, laying it horizontally on the growth rack with the bag opening facing outward, placing 10-15 bags in each row with a row spacing of 0.6 m, keeping indoor ventilation to avoid light when the room temperature exceeds 28 degrees C and the material temperature exceeds 30 degrees C, controlling indoor temperature at 25-28 degrees C, providing intermittent ventilation to control the carbon dioxide concentration, cultivating in dark light, applying selenium fertilizer solution 1-2 times when the mycelium germinates to bud, spraying 1-2 kg selenium fertilizer solution every 10 m2, paying attention to a small amount of multiple times when supplementing selenium, where fog point is small and the spraying is even, and trimming buds during the bud period, leaving only a robust bud, which takes 20-30 days from inoculation to growing buds, (h) performing management of G.lucidum by filing G.lucidum with bacteria, placing it in a row of 90-100 cm with a height of 6-7 storey, traveling north and south, punching the opening, where opening is the size of a dime, immediately sealing greenhouse, controlling temperature at 27-30 degrees C, increasing humidity, providing scattered light, controlling the relative humidity of the air at 85-90% after 2-3 days, gradually increasing ventilation, keeping temperature at 27-30 degrees C, where oxygen consumption is large when the G.lucidum is out, providing ventilation, opening the lid, ventilating G.lucidum for at least 5 hours a day, reducing ventilation when the primordium is formed and the long stalk period, and increasing carbon dioxide content, where water is always clear from the opening to the spore powder, and it takes 18-23 days from the formation of leaves to the spore powder, and (i) harvesting G.lucidum when cap of G.lucidum is fully unfolded, the light white or light yellow on the edge disappears, the cap begins to become leathery, G.lucidum appears brown and starts to eject spores, collecting spore powder after 7 days of bagging, harvesting G.lucidum after the spore powder harvesting, removing debris after harvesting, drying, and packing G.lucidum by natural drying or drying in a drying room. The culture medium (A) comprises 20-30 pts. wt. miscellaneous sawdust, 15-25 pts. wt. bran, 5-18 pts. wt. straw, 0.3-0.8 pt. wt. sugar and 0.1-0.5 pt. wt. gypsum. The culture medium (B) comprises 25-45 pts. wt. sawdust, 5-20 pts. wt. cotton seed husk, 10-15 pts. wt. decomposed peanut shells, 8-12 pts. wt. bran, 0.3-0.8 pt. wt. sugar and 0.1-0.5 pt. wt. gypsum. The culture medium (C) comprises 30-40 pts. wt. cottonseed husk, 10-20 pts. wt. decomposed bagasse, 8-16 pts. wt. rice bran, 3-7 pts. wt. soybean powder and 0.2-0.8 pt. wt. gypsum. The culture medium (D) comprises 25-35 pts. wt. corn flour, 15-20 pts. wt. cottonseed husk, 5-16 pts. wt. fermented bean hulls, 0.1-1 pt. wt. sugar and 0.3-0.5 pt. wt. gypsum.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In step (b), the water content of the culture medium is 62-67%, the addition ratio of the total mass of raw materials to the water in the culture medium is 1:1.2-1.5, and the pH value is 7.9-8.2. In the step (b), the stack is turned once when the medium is heated to 45-50 degrees C, and the fermentation temperature is controlled to 45-50 degrees C. The step (e) involves performing bagging by (i) using a low-pressure polyethylene bag with a width of 10-14 cm and a length of 25-30 cm, performing normal pressure sterilization, raising the temperature to 80 degrees C, keeping it for 15-18 hours, cooling it naturally, and inoculating it when the temperature is lowered to 35-32 degrees C, or (ii) bagging polypropylene bags manually or using machine, performing high-pressure sterilization, keeping it for 2.2-3 hours when the pressure rises to 0.15 MPa, and waiting for the pressure to drop naturally. In step (f), inoculation amount is 5-6% of the total weight of the dry culture medium. The inoculation in the step (f) adopts any one of liquid strain or solid strain. In the step (g), when the hyphae at both ends grow into the material to more than 6 cm, the tie rope is cut off, and the opening of the bag is opened to form a trumpet shape. In management of selenium supplementation, the relative humidity of the air during the growth stage of the mycelium is maintained at 70-75%, and the relative humidity of the air during the growth and development stage of the fruit body is maintained at 86-92%. In the step (h), in the management of the production of mushrooms, it is ensured that each grass curtain has scattered light, and sun shoots in with three points of sun and seven points of yin.
DC A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)
MC A12-W04B; P13-A01A; P13-A01B; P13-A01C; P13-B01; P13-E01; P13-E02; P13-E03
IP A01G-018/00; A01G-018/20
