PT P
PN CN110628478-A
TI Co-production of fuel oil and aromatic hydrocarbon involves pressurizing raw coal, gasifying coal gas using pure oxygen and pure oxygen steam, removing e.g. sulfur-containing acid gas, and performing catalytic synthesis of methane or fuel.
AU LI N
   LI K
AE CHENGDU JUSHI ENERGY SAVING TECHNOLOGY (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202004603L
AB    NOVELTY - Co-production of fuel oil and aromatic hydrocarbon involves pressurizing raw coal, gasifying using pure oxygen or pure oxygen steam into coal gas comprising carbon monoxide and hydrogen as main components, adjusting carbon monoxide conversion by adjusting ratio of carbon monoxide and hydrogen, removing carbon dioxide and sulfur-containing acid gas in the coal gas, performing catalytic synthesis of methane or fuel, and performing dehydration separation of methane or fuel and conserving energy of the reaction system using coil-fired boiler steam power generation and a process steam system. The moving bed used for processing raw coal, is a three-stage structure comprising a hydrogen gas section (A), an inter-section transition section (B), and a water gas section (C). The hydrogen gas section (A) comprises a hydrocarbon-rich hydrogen gas-drying layer, a methane-rich hydrogen gas hydrogenation distillation layer, a semi-coke hydrogenation layer, and a coke hydrogenation layer.
   USE - Co-production of fuel oil and aromatic hydrocarbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables environmentally-friendly and economical co-production of fuel oil and aromatic hydrocarbon with reduced energy and water consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Co-production of fuel oil and aromatic hydrocarbon involves pressurizing raw coal, gasifying using pure oxygen or pure oxygen steam into coal gas comprising carbon monoxide and hydrogen as main components, adjusting carbon monoxide conversion by adjusting ratio of carbon monoxide and hydrogen, removing carbon dioxide and sulfur-containing acid gas in the coal gas, performing catalytic synthesis of methane or fuel, and performing dehydration separation of methane or fuel and conserving energy of the reaction system using coil-fired boiler steam power generation and a process steam system. The moving bed used for processing raw coal, is a three-stage structure comprising a hydrogen gas section (A), an inter-section transition section (B), and a water gas section (C). The hydrogen gas section (A) comprises from top to bottom comprising a hydrocarbon-rich hydrogen gas-drying layer, a methane-rich hydrogen gas hydrogenation distillation layer, a methane-less hydrogen gas semi-coke hydrogenation layer, and a high-temperature coke hydrogenation layer, a stirrer is provided in the methane-rich hydrogen gas hydrotreating layer. The methane-less hydrogen gas semi-coke hydrogenation layer and high-temperature coke hydrogenation layer, comprises components for heat exchange or regulating the bed temperature provided for methane generation and increasing driving force of reaction, a transition section including residual coke after the coke hydrogenation reaction is moved from the high-temperature coke hydrogenation layer to the water bed section, a residual coke accumulation layer and the unloading structure preferably having a funnel-shaped unloading hopper, a discharge structure which carries the residual coke from the coking hydrogenation layer, a drain for draining the residual coke to the water and gas section. The outside of the discharge structure and the wall of the gasification furnace constitute a gas gathering area. At the same time, the residual coke accumulation layer and the discharging structure are used to block gas from each other between water gas and hydrogen. The center of the discharging structure is provided with an intermediate gas collecting box with a inverted v-shaped or n-shaped, that is, a lower air inlet, for collecting gas rising in the middle area of the water-coal reaction layer, an intermediate gas collecting box and the gas gathering area are connected by pipes so that the gas flows into the gas gathering area. The water gas section designed from top to bottom comprising a water gas reaction layer, an oxygen combustion layer, an ash and slag layer, and a mixed gasifier which uses the residual coke from the transition section as the raw material to produce hydrogen and carbon monoxide water gas, and provides hydrogen feed gas to the hydrogen gas section. The processing in sections (A) and (B) is carried out by adding the raw coal to the hydrogen gas section, pressurizing moving bed through the coal lock, and successively moves in the same pressurized gasification bed layer that moves downward continuously, preferably heating the hydrocarbon-rich hydrogen gas drying layer by the rising hydrocarbon-rich hydrogen gas, removing moisture in the dry raw coal, hydrolyzing the methane-rich hydrogen gas hydrogenation retort layer, pyrolyzing, and gasifying by the ascending methane-rich hydrogen gas to analyze coal tar and oxygen, nitrogen, and sulfur compounds, removing semi-coke by methane-depleted hydrogen in the semi-coke hydrogenation layer, vaporizing activated carbon element to obtain coke, gasifying coke into residual coke by hydrogen gas in the high-temperature coke hydrogenation layer, slowly transferring the residual coke to coke accumulation layer, discharging transition section structure which uses the resistance of the carbon layer to block the water gas and hydrogen produced by section (B), discharging residual coke from the discharging structure into a low-carbon residual coke in the water gas reaction layer, oxidizing low-carbon residual coke in the oxygen combustion layer and combusting by oxygen in the steam and oxygen gasifier to form a slag.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The hydrogen cycle machine transfers the hydrogen produced from the hydrocarbon-rich hydrogen gas to a hydrogen distributor through the hydrogen heater, the startup heating device and the hydrogen gasifier inlet (4). Down the heat exchange portion after being heated by the heat exchange portion, the hydrogen entering the high-temperature coke-hydrogenation layer from the outlet of the lower end of the heat exchange portion and then deflected upward. The descending high-temperature coke is further heated, and about 20% of the hydrogen is carried out with activated carbon elements in the coke. A portion of the heat emitted directly heats the high-temperature methane-depleted hydrogen gas and coke, and portion of the hydrogen in the heating part is heated by the heat exchange component. The methane hydrogen gas enters the semi-coke hydrogenation layer. The hydrogen and semi-coke contain hydrocarbon rich in active carbon elements. The content of methane in the medium increases greatly, to form methane-rich hydrogen gas, and the heat in the heat exchange portion is heated while the bed temperature is released. The methane-rich hydrogen gas ascends up into the methane-rich hydrogen gas hydrogenation retort layer, which provides heat for pyrolysis, carbonization and gasification of coal. The hydrogen in the gas phase penetrates into the interior of the coal particles through diffusion and infiltration. The combination of solid hydrocarbons in coal generates a large amount of high-quality 10-20C coal tar. After leaving the furnace, the hydrocarbon-rich hydrogen gas is removed by the tar dust separator, the temperature is reduced by the heat exchanger and cooled by a cooling oil-water separator to normal temperature and the oil and water are separated and removed to enter the acid gas. The gasifier comprises a mixture of oxygen and water vapor which enters the furnace from the furnace bottom inlet, passes through the grate, and passes through the ash and slag layer, absorbs heat, temperature is increased, and enters the oxygen gasification combustion layer at 600 degrees C. The oxygen in the gasification agent rapidly burns and gasifies the remaining carbon elements in the residual coke, generates carbon dioxide and emits a lot of heat, such that the water vapor in the gasification agent and the temperature of the bed reaches more than 1000 degrees C and below the ash melting point. The high-temperature water vapor containing carbon dioxide ascends up into the water gas reaction layer, and transfers heat to the hydrogenated residual coke from the hydrogen gas section, and the residual coke. Carbon element undergoes endothermic water gas reaction. The water and gas coming out of the furnace pass through the dust removal, a hydrogen heater, and a steam superheating device, and enters waste heat steam boiler to produce portion of the steam required for gasification, and enters the water gas washing device to remove dust and colloids. Preferred Conditions: The process pressure of the pressurized coal gasification bed is 1-3 MPa, 3-6 MPa, or 6-10 MPa. The process pressure of the hydrogenation of coal tar to fuel aromatic is 8-15 MPa.
DC H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)
MC H04-E08; H04-F02E; H06-B05; H06-P
IP C10G-001/00; C10J-003/16; C10J-003/66; C10J-003/72; C10J-003/84; C10L-003/08

AD CN110628478-A    CN10890177    20 Sep 2019

PN CN108936692-A
TI Preparing American ginseng peptide useful for improving immunity, comprises e.g. adding American ginseng, washing, drying into sheet, subjecting flaky ginseng into kneading machine for kneading process, rolling and kneading.
AU SHI F
AE SHI F (SHIF-Individual)
GA 2018A07878
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing American ginseng peptide comprises e.g. adding American ginseng, washing, drying into sheet, subjecting flaky ginseng into kneading machine for kneading process, rolling and kneading the each vacuum, where each time after pumping the vacuum kneading, then discharging vacuum, and circulating, performing super-fine crushing, sieving to obtain American ginseng powder into microwave processor, according to the American ginseng, adding distilled water, then filtering to obtain ginseng material residue and filter liquid, where the material residue of American ginseng by carbon dioxide supercritical extracting soluble components of residue, extracting ginseng material, removing the solvent into a container with a stirrer, adding the filtrate, adjusting pH of the solution, stirring at normal temperature to form the American ginseng extracting solution, adding pseudo-ginseng, Acanthopanax, fried malt, gordon euryale, seaweed, smoked plum, dandelion, mushroom, and honey.
   USE - The American ginseng peptide is useful for improving immunity (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The American ginseng peptide: contains the macromolecule of American ginseng, which is converted into an easily-absorbed active substance, namely small molecule polypeptide, a saponin or a polysaccharide, so that the nutrition is more easily absorbed; has high extraction efficiency of active substance; is reasonable in compatibility with traditional Chinese medicine extracts and each component is safe to eat and balanced in nutrients; can meets the variety of nutrients required by the human body and has good nutrition and health care functions, can meets the nutritional needs of the human body; and regulates the function of human body and has a unique effect on people who are prone to aging or accompanied by low predisposition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing American ginseng peptide comprises (i) adding 50 pts. wt. American ginseng, washing, drying into sheet with thickness of 5 cm, subjecting flaky ginseng into kneading machine for kneading process, rolling and kneading the each vacuum for once every 10 minutes, where each time after pumping the vacuum kneading for 15 minutes, then discharging vacuum, and circulating for 3 times; (ii) performing super-fine crushing, sieving with 400-mesh sieve to obtain the American ginseng powder into the microwave processor, according to the American ginseng by weight ratio of 3.2:1.0, adding distilled water, where the microwave frequency is 2500 MHz, power is 500 W at intervals, interval time of microwave processing is 8 minutes, where each microwave processing for 8 minutes, continuously for 3 times, then filtering to obtain the ginseng material residue and filter liquid, where the material residue of American ginseng by carbon dioxide supercritical extracting soluble components of the residue after extracting ginseng material after removing the solvent into a container with a stirrer, adding the filtrate, adjusting pH of the solution to 8, stirring for 1 hour at normal temperature to form the American ginseng extracting solution; (iii) adding 8 pts. wt. pseudo-ginseng, 11 pts. wt. Acanthopanax, 5 pts. wt. fried malt, 7 pts. wt. gordon euryale, 5 pts. wt. seaweed, 10 pts. wt. smoked plum, 4 pts. wt. dandelion, 9 pts. wt. mushroom, and 6 pts. wt. honey, cleaning and mixing uniformly, then placing in the extracting tank, adding water to extract twice, first time adding water of 8 times of the total weight of the composition by weight, the second time adding water of 6 times of the total weight of the composition the weight, and the extraction in each time is 2 hours to obtain the liquid medicine, filtering the liquid medicine, combining the medicine liquid, and decocting for two times, vacuum extracting and concentrating the filtrate to 60 degrees C, where the relative density is 1.2, vacuum extracting and concentrating to 60-70 degrees C at 0.05-0.07 MPa to obtain the traditional Chinese medicine extract; (iv) adding 8 pts. wt. Phoebe zhennan, adopting vapor distillation to extract, obtaining the Phoebe zhennan volatile oil extract crude product, extracting the Phoebe zhennan volatile oil extract product by organic solvent, where the organic phase is obtained by extracting through dehydrating, volatilizing the solvent to obtain the Phoebe zhennan volatile oil extract; (v) adding the obtained American ginseng extracting solution into the preparing tank, adding 5 times of pure water, adding protein hydrolase and digesting for 2 hours, then heating to 95 degrees C, inactivating enzyme for 30 minutes, cooling, adding prepared honey extract into the preparing tank, stirring to dissolve completely, heating to 95 degrees C for 10-15 minutes, then sterilizing, adding sodium bicarbonate to adjust pH to 6, adding mixture of alpha -amylase and complex protease for 2 hours, where the complex protease is Protamex (RTM: Protease), then heating to 95 degrees C, inactivating enzyme for 30 minutes, cooling, adding citric acid to adjust the pH to 4, adding the mixture of pectinase and endonuclease and digesting for 2 hours, then heating to 95 degrees C, inactivating enzyme for 30 minutes, cooling, adjusting to neutral to obtain enzymatic hydrolysate, where the mass ratio of proteolytic enzyme, alpha -amylase, complex protease, pectinase, endonuclease to American ginseng powder is 11:3:5:3:6:960; and (vi) filtering the enzymatic hydrolysate of step (v), when concentrated under reduced pressure to 60 degrees C, and the relative density is 1.2, and stirring with the volatile oil extract of Phoebe bournei.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (i) uniformly mixing 30 pts. wt. American ginseng peptide, 16 pts. wt. beta -cyclodextrin, 0.7 pts. wt. stevioside, 0.7 pts. wt. citric acid and 1.2 pts. wt. mannitol, then adding 20 pts. wt. purified water and mixing uniformly to obtain the mixed solution; (ii) placing the mixed solution obtained in the step (i) in the spray drier to spray, where the spray dryer working condition is: the air inlet temperature is 160 degrees C, the air outlet temperature is 80 degrees C, speed 200 ml/hour, and obtaining dry powder; and (iii) adding the mixed liquid of the dried powder obtained in step (ii) for granulating.
   ACTIVITY - Immunostimulant.

TI Preparing beverage comprises by using grape and tea comprises e.g. picking fresh grapes, crushing fresh grapes with juicer to obtain grape solid-liquid mixture, adding grape solid-liquid mixture into fermenter, and adding tea leaves.
AU TANG C
AE HUNAN GUDONG SPRING TEA CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 201873352N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing beverage comprises by using grape and tea comprises e.g. (1) picking fresh grapes; (2) crushing fresh grapes with juicer to obtain grape solid-liquid mixture; (3) adding grape solid-liquid mixture into the fermenter, adding tea leaves to fermenter; peeling pulp and core of the grape; soaking tea leaves in fruit juice, at the same time, adding small amount of milk white glue-type kombucha strain and water, carrying fermentation, where some of the sugar contained in the grapes is gradually converted into alcohol, carbon dioxide and kombucha, sugar is gradually reduced, and alcohol level gradually increased; (4) immediately adding sulfur dioxide sulfur dioxide to the fermenter after the end of the fermentation process, where the sulfur dioxide can prevent the oxidation caused by the oxygen in air, using filter for preliminary filtration to remove peel, core, fruit stem and tea leaves; (5) in the second week after end of fermentation, adding diatomite and filtering by the filter.
   USE - The method is useful for rich in tea fragrance.
   ADVANTAGE - The beverage: has health care effect; and is rich in tea fragrance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing beverage comprises by using grape and tea comprises (1) picking fresh grapes; (2) crushing fresh grapes with juicer to obtain grape solid-liquid mixture; (3) adding grape solid-liquid mixture into the fermenter, adding 0.5-1 wt.% tea leaves to fermenter; peeling pulp and core of the grape; soaking tea leaves in fruit juice, at the same time, adding small amount of milk white glue-type kombucha strain and water, carrying fermentation at 25-30 degrees C for 20-40 days, where some of the sugar contained in the grapes is gradually converted into alcohol, carbon dioxide and kombucha, sugar is gradually reduced, and alcohol level gradually increased; (4) immediately adding sulfur dioxide sulfur dioxide to the fermenter after the end of the fermentation process, where the sulfur dioxide can prevent the oxidation caused by the oxygen in air, using filter for preliminary filtration to remove peel, core, fruit stem and tea leaves; (5) in the second week after the end of fermentation, adding diatomite and filtering by the filter; (6) in the third week after end of fermentation, adding bentonite, stirring, pumping into depositing cylinder for precipitation, sucking clarification liquid in upper part of the depositing cylinder by siphoning to obtain the initial product; (7) stability testing with testing instrument: where stability testing including tartar stability, pigment stability, metal ion stability, and biostability; and (8) using the membrane sterilization filter to filter, directly filling filtered wine by filling machine.    (2) device for preparing beverage comprises by using grape and tea comprising juicer, fermenting tank, vibrating screen, filter, sedimentation cylinder, detecting instrument, membrane sterilization TI Method for enhancing power generation performance            of carbon dioxide mineralized battery, involves placing            cation exchange membrane in carbon dioxide mineralized            battery container and dividing container into anode and            cathode region.
AU XIE H
   WANG Y
   LIU T
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 2019171408
AB    NOVELTY - A power generation performance enhancing                method involves placing the cation exchange                membrane in a carbon dioxide mineralized battery                container, adding a Glauber's salt and conductive                solution containing calcium hydroxide as an anolyte                and sodium bicarbonate as a catholyte. The                catholyte is connected to the cathode and cathode                electrodes to form a current path. Ionizes hydrogen                ion and bicarbonate after passing carbon dioxide                into the cathode region of the battery and ionized                hydrogen ion is reduced to hydrogen at the cathode                and the remaining bicarbonate is transmitted from                the anode region. Sodium ion is combined to form                sodium bicarbonate and the cathode hydrogen is                recycled back to the anode and releases electrons                to hydrogen ion under the action of the anode                electrode. The hydrogen ion is neutralized the                hydroxide ionization of calcium hydroxide in the                anolyte and the calcium ion and sulfate is combined                in the mirabilite to produce a calcium sulfate                precipitate.
   USE - Method for enhancing power generation                performance of carbon dioxide mineralized battery                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables enhancing power generation                performance of carbon dioxide mineralized battery                with rich resource and enhanced electrogenesis                capacity, in simple manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A power generation performance enhancing                method involves placing the cation exchange                membrane in a carbon dioxide mineralized battery                container, dividing the container into an anode                region and cathode region and adding with Glauber's                salt and conductive solution containing calcium                hydroxide as an anolyte to the anode region and                sodium bicarbonate as a catholyte to the cathode                region. The catholyte is connected to the cathode                and cathode electrodes to form a current path.                Ionizes hydrogen ion and bicarbonate after passing                carbon dioxide into the cathode region of the                battery and ionized hydrogen ion is reduced to                hydrogen at the cathode and the remaining                bicarbonate is transmitted from the anode region.                Sodium ion is combined to form sodium bicarbonate                and the cathode hydrogen is recycled back to the                anode and releases electrons to hydrogen ion under                the action of the anode electrode. The hydrogen ion                is neutralized the hydroxide ionization of calcium                hydroxide in the anolyte and the calcium ion and                sulfate is combined in the mirabilite to produce a                calcium sulfate precipitate. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM                is also included for a carbon dioxide mineralized                battery, comprises casing container of a carbon                dioxide mineralized battery and a cation exchange                membrane is separated the vessel into an anode zone                and cathode zone, where the anode zone is added                with a Glauber's salt and a conductive solution                containing calcium hydroxide as an anolyte and the                cathode region is added with sodium bicarbonate to                form a catholyte, and the cathode is connected to                cathode electrodes to form a current path and                carbon dioxide is introduced into the cathode                region.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The calcium-based                  alkaline solid waste containing calcium hydroxide                  is selected from the group consisting of calcium                  carbide slag, cement dust and waste combustion                  waste. The cathode electrode of the carbon                  dioxide mineralized battery is a porous electrode                  made of metal platinum, metal palladium or                  metallic nickel and the anode electrode is a gas                  diffusion electrode.

PN US2021146300-A1
TI Integrated system for carbon capture and sequestration, has gas feed source containing reaction gas of carbon dioxide, reaction chamber having reactor column, gas distributor plate and electrical heating element, and control panel having power switch.
AU MOHAMED A O
   EL-GAMAL M M
   HAMEEDI S
AE UNIV ZAYED (UYZA-Non-standard)
GA 2021557414
AB    NOVELTY - The integrated system comprises a feed gas source (101) with an initial concentration of carbon dioxide (CO2) reaction gas controlled by at least one mass control valve (5), and a reaction chamber (102) comprising a reactor column (9) having a lid (14) over a top open end receiving a reaction solid and a bottom open end communicating with a gas entrance receiving the reaction gas. At least one gas distributor plate connects the open end to the gas entrance. Each distributor plate includes at least two disks having symmetrical holes to provide uniform distribution of the reaction gas through the reactor column and at least one mesh, having a mesh size smaller than a particle size of the reaction solid, in between the at least two disks. At least one controllable electrical heating element (10) provides an insulated heating source to the reactor column.
   USE - The integrated system is used for carbon capture and sequestration, and stabilization of alkaline solid wastes, and conducting a carbonation reaction of solid mineral waste particles automatically under determined reaction parameters (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system enhances the reaction process. The system fits the recent environmental applications, optimizes the required reaction time, and ensures a sustainable use of carbon dioxide during the application process. The system minimizes operational cost, and saves energy saving, and reduces both the capital and operating costs for mineral carbonation processes. The system provides inexpensive technology by where: no need for grinding the waste stream because it is already in its fine particle sizes which are convenient for the carbon dioxide sequestration; no thermal energy consumption through the application of direct gas-solid carbonation in this innovative system since reaction is taking place at room temperature; and no external mechanical pressure source is required since the fluidization using the pressurized carbon dioxide provides a well-mixed waste with maximum available exposed particles surface areas for reaction with carbon dioxide. The system has the ability to: highly control gas flow and distribution within the reactor due to optimal design of gas flow holes distribution geometry of the gas distributor unit; maximum interaction between gas and alkaline solid waste particles; control gas pressure inside the reactor while running experiments; and control the entering gas flow rate to the reactor with a wide range up to 50 l/minute with an accuracy of 0.1. The system has the ability to: control and apply uniformly distributed heat to the reactor rapidly by an electrical heating source in a double jacket around the full height of the reactor; automatically monitor temperature along the reactor from 3 different positions along the reactor height; and provide high level of gas purity before entering the reactor, (through 7 microm filter, and after leaving the reactor as there is direct interaction between gas and solid, through cyclone dust collector and storage container, and another 7 microm filter at the outlet stream). The system has ability to: identify the optimum reaction time, which is required for direct gas-solid interaction (carbonation), by monitoring the outlet gas stream (unreacted carbon dioxide) using the gas analyzer; and calculate carbonation efficiency using data from the identification of the optimum reaction time above and theoretical equation without the need to conduct thermo-mineralogical experiments such as thermogravimetric analysis (TGA). The system has ability to: identify the exact time to terminate the experiment based on information provided in the identification of the optimum reaction time above; eliminate the needs for additional thermo-mineralogical testing to quantify the maximum carbonation and carbonation efficiency; automatically monitor temperature, pressure, flow rate and gas (carbon dioxide) concentration by automatic data acquisition system and computer software; enhancing data analysis capabilities; and environmentally sustain the use of carbon dioxide gas stream through adsorption and by-pass units.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A computer control panel (103) has data acquisition and analysis software configured to automatically take the measurements of the outlet concentration of CO2 by the gas analyzer and analyze the measurements of the outlet concentration of CO2 over time. The computer further includes software for a supervisory control and data acquisition (SCADA) system configured to automatically control the power supply, the flowrate of reaction gas with the mass control valve, a pressure valve connecting the gas analyser with a cyclone separator, and the electrical heating element, and automatically takes and analyzes the measurement of the pressure gauge and the gas analyser.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for conducting a carbonation reaction of solid mineral waste particles automatically under determined reaction parameters.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a diagram of an integrated system for carbon capture and sequestration, and stabilization of alkaline metal wastes.
   Mass control valve (5)
   Gas entrance (7)
   Gas distributor plate (8)
   Reactor column (9)
   Electrical heating element (10)
   Lid (14)
   Power switch (22)
   Temperature controller (30)
   Integrated system (100)
   Gas feed source (101)

TI Preparing polycarbonate comprises mixing epoxy butane, double metal cyanide catalyst, co-catalyst and carbon dioxide, and reacting.
AU ZHENG Z
   WANG X
   HE P
   CAO Y
   LI H
   ZENG X
   LIU C
   WANG L
AE INST PROCESS ENG CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAGC-C)
GA 202203427W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polycarbonate comprises mixing epoxy butane, double metal cyanide catalyst, co-catalyst and carbon dioxide, and reacting.
   USE - The method is sueful for preparing polycarbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses improves the fixing amount of carbon dioxide, reduces the probability of side reaction, improves the conversion rate of the monomer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The epoxy butane comprises 1,2-epoxy butane and/or 2,3-epoxy butane, preferably 1,2-epoxy butane. The double metal cyanide catalyst includes at least two of zinc-cobalt double-metal cyanide catalyst, zinc-iron double-metal cyanide catalyst or iron-cobalt double-metal cyanide catalyst, preferably zinc-cobalt double-metal cyanide catalyst chemical catalyst. The cocatalyst includes quaternary ammonium salt and/or organic base, preferably quaternary ammonium salt. The quaternary ammonium salt includes bistriphenylphosphine ammonium chloride and/or tetrabutylammonium chloride. The organic base includes 4-dimethylaminopyridine and/or triethylamine. The mass ratio of the epoxy butane and double metal cyanide catalyst is (100-2000):1 and the mass ratio of the double metal cyanide catalyst and cocatalyst is 1:(1-2).Preferred Method: The preparation method comprises mixing epoxy butane, double metal cyanide catalyst, auxiliary catalyst and carbon dioxide, reacting at 80-140degrees Celsius under the pressure of 0.5-5 MPa, reacting for 6-24 hours, adding solvent, filtering, vacuum distilling, washing, and drying, where the solvent comprises at least two of methylene dichloride, trichloromethane or absolute ethyl alcohol.

PN CN115235134-A
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide circulating system            for fusion reactor, has carbon dioxide purifying device            whose output end is connected with pressure stabilizing            tank, and is used for removing gaseous impurities in            loop.
AU LIU S
   LI C
   LEI M
AE CHINESE ACAD SCI HEFEI INST PHYS SCI (CAWZ-C)
GA 2022D5470F
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a main loop device, a pressure                control device and a carbon dioxide purifying                device. A main loop device is provided with a main                loop and a circulating compressor. A circulating                compressor is fixed on the main loop, a pressure                stabilizing tank (2) and a heat exchange mechanism.                The circulating compressor, the pressure                stabilizing tank and the heat exchange mechanism                are sequentially connected. The pressure control                device is provided with a pressure sensor and is                set in the pressure stabilizing tank. A main loop                adjusting valve (10) is fixed between the main loop                and the pressure stabilizing tank. The pressure                control device is used for adjusting the air                pressure in the main loop. The input end of the                carbon dioxide purifying device is connected with                the main loop. The first output end of the carbon                dioxide purifying device is connected with the                pressure stabilizing tank. The carbon dioxide                purifying device is used for removing gaseous                impurities in the loop.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide circulating                system for fusion reactor such as Tokka nuclear                fusion device e.g. ITER, for studying fusion of                magnetic confinement nuclear fusion.
   ADVANTAGE - The system is capable of realizing the heat                generated in the fusion reactor out, at the same                time, suitable for the system with multiple                temperature range difference in fusion reactor is                large, and the cooling working substance type of                each different system. The main loop device can                realize the multi-working substance in a fusion                reactor, the heat energy generated by a multi-heat                source is guided out, the pressure control device                and the carbon dioxide purifying device are used                for assisting the main loop to safely and stably                operate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a structure schematic                diagram of a supercritical carbon dioxide                circulating system for fusion reactor. (Drawing                includes non-English language text)

PT P
PN CN113699304-A; CN113699304-B
TI Method of using carbon dioxide and nitrogen to            splash slag to protect furnace, involves fixing gun            position at position specified value higher than            blowing gun position, ending slag splashing operation,            pouring out dry slag in furnace and performing loading            operation of secondary furnace.
AU ZHU R
   FENG C
   HU S
   ZHU Q
   WEI G
AE GANSU JIU STEEL GROUP HONGXING IRON & (JGHX-C)
GA 2021E21597
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves shaking the furnace to the                normal smelting position after tapping and adding                0.8-2kg/t of slag splashing material containing                15-25% carbon and 14-24% magnesium according to the                tapping temperature. The splashing slag gas is                provided as all carbon dioxide and the constant gun                position is continued to blow carbon dioxide until                slag appears at the furnace mouth. The content                curves of the above two gases is provided to differ                by half a sine cycle. The gun position is changed                by 1.0-1.5m during the injection process until the                furnace mouth splashes out the dry slag. The gun                position is fixed at a position 200mm higher than                the blowing gun position. The slag splashing gas is                provided as all carbon dioxide and the slag                splashing is continued until no more dry slag is                thrown out from the furnace mouth. The slag                splashing operation is end. The dry slag in the                furnace is poured out and the loading operation of                the secondary furnace is performed.
   USE - Method of using carbon dioxide and nitrogen to                splash slag to protect furnace used in converter                smelting process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts the process of carbon                dioxide and nitrogen mixed slag splashing, thus                realizing fast thickening of the low-viscosity slag                in the prior period and through the principle of                carbon and carbon dioxide reaction heat absorption                in the slage splashing material, quickly reducing                the temperature of the slag. The middle stage uses                the continuous change of carbon-dioxide and                nitrogen, the kinetic energy of the gas changes                continuously, enriches the splash angle of slag                block, enlarges the splashing furnace slag of the                hearth area, reduces the single gas slag, later                using carbon-dioxide and carbon reaction                heat-absorption mechanism, realizes the fast                cooling solidification, and improves the                anti-erosion ability of a slag layer. The method is                more beneficial for maintenance of converter                furnace condition.
DC M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).)
MC M24-C07; M24-B02C
IP C21C-005/36; C21C-005/44

PT P
PN WO2019011796-A1; CA3065889-A1; IN202017001513-A; CN110869324-A; KR2020028998-A; EP3652117-A1; BR112019027413-A2; JP2020526390-W; US2021147266-A1; RU2769268-C2; RU2020106124-A; US11390542-B2; CN110869324-B
TI Increasing magnesium ion concentration of feed            water used in domestic installations, involves            providing inlet flow of feed water, increasing            concentration of carbon dioxide to obtain flow of            carbon dioxide containing feed water.
AU NELSON N C
   SCHMID M
   SCHMIDTMARIUS
   NELSON N CH
AE OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
   OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
   OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
GA 201907038G
AB    NOVELTY - Increasing the magnesium ion concentration of                feed water, involves providing an inlet flow of                feed water (QIN), increasing the concentration of                carbon dioxide in inlet flow (QIN) to obtain a flow                of carbon dioxide-containing feed water (Qco2), and                passing flow Qco2 through a solid bed to obtain an                outlet flow of treated water (QOUT), where the                solid bed comprises a magnesium ion source in the                form of solid particles having a solubility limit                in water of 10 g/L or less, measured at 20 degrees                C and 100 kilopascal.
   USE - Method for increasing magnesium ion                concentration of feed water used in domestic                installations.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to increase magnesium ion                concentration of feed water, which has a                concentration of dissolved magnesium ions of 10                mg/L or less, preferably 5 mg/L or less, and more                preferably 2 mg/L or less, and total alkalinity                (calcium carbonate) of from 5-200 mg/L, preferably                from 10 to 150 mg/L, and more preferably from                20-100 mg/L.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a water treatment system for increasing                the magnesium ion concentration of feed water,                which comprises a line configured to receive an                inlet flow of feed water, a pretreatment unit                connected to line, and is configured to increase                the concentration of carbon dioxide in inlet flow                of feed water to obtain a flow of carbon dioxide-                containing feed water, and a solid bed located                downstream from the pretreatment unit, where the                solid bed is configured to receive the flow of                carbon dioxide-containing feed water from                pretreatment device to obtain an outlet flow of                treated water; and
   (2) a flow reactor for use in a water                treatment system for increasing the magnesium ion                concentration of feed water, which comprises an                inlet configured to receive a flow of carbon                dioxide-containing feed water, a solid bed                configured to receive the flow of carbon                dioxide-containing feed water from inlet and to                obtain an outlet flow of treated water, and an                outlet being configured to release the outlet flow                of treated water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Conditions: The feed water has a                  concentration of dissolved magnesium ions of 10                  mg/L or less, preferably 5 mg/L or less, and more                  preferably 2 mg/L or less. The feed water has a                  total alkalinity (calcium carbonate) of from                  5-200 mg/L, preferably from 10 to 150 mg/L, and                  more preferably from 20-100 mg/L. The feed water                  has a Langelier Saturation Index (LSI) of                  (-)2.0-1.0, preferably (-)1.0-0.7, and more                  preferably from (-)0.5-0.5. The concentration of                  carbon dioxide is increased by injecting gaseous                  carbon dioxide into the inlet flow. The                  concentration of carbon dioxide in the carbon                  dioxide-containing feed water of flow is adjusted                  to a concentration of 5-500 mg/L, preferably                  10-200 mg/L, and more preferably from 20-100                  mg/l. The pH of the carbon dioxide-containing                  feed water of flow is adjusted to 5.0-8.5,                  preferably 5.5-8.0, and more preferably from                  6.0-7.5, where pH is preferably adjusted by                  injecting an appropriate amount of carbon dioxide                  into inlet flow. The temperature of the carbon                  dioxide-containing feed water of flow is adjusted                  to 5-35 degrees C, preferably 10-30 degrees C,                  and most preferably from 15-25 degrees C. The                  magnesium ion source is selected from natural                  magnesium- containing minerals, preferably                  brucite, calcareous dolomite, dolomite, dolomitic                  limestone, hydromagnesite or magnesite, partially                  or fully calcined natural magnesium-containing                  minerals, preferably partially or fully calcined                  brucite, calcareous dolomite, dolomite, dolomitic                  limestone, hydromagnesite or magnesite, more                  preferably half-burned dolomite, and synthetic                  magnesium-containing minerals, preferably                  synthetic magnesium carbonate, magnesium                  hydroxide or hydromagnesite, more preferably                  precipitated hydromagnesite. The particles have a                  weight median particle size of 0.05-20 mm,                  preferably from 0.1-15 mm, more preferably 0.5 to                  1.5 mm. The solubility limit in water is 5 g/L or                  less, preferably 2 g/L or less, more preferably                  0.2 g/L or less, each measured at 20 degrees C                  and 100 kilopascal. The contact time between flow                  (Qco2) and the solid bed is 0.1 minute,                  preferably 0.2 minute, more preferably 1 minute,                  and/or 30 minutes, preferably less than 20                  minutes, more preferably less than 5 minutes. The                  solid bed is provided by a cavity of a flow                  reactor, where flow reactor has an inlet being                  configured to receive the flow of carbon                  dioxide-containing feed water and an outlet being                  configured to release the outlet flow of treated                  water, preferably flow reactor is a flow                  cartridge. The concentration of carbon dioxide in                  the carbon dioxide-containing feed water of flow                  Qco2 is adjusted to a concentration of from                  20-100 mg/L, and the magnesium ion source is                  selected from one or more of brucite,                  hydromagnesite or partially calcined dolomite.                  The particles have a weight median particle size                  of 0.5-1.5 mm, and the contact time between flow                  and the solid bed is 0.1 minutes and less than 5                  minutes. The pretreatment device is an injection                  device being configured to inject gaseous carbon                  dioxide into inlet flow (QIN). The flow reactor                  is a flow cartridge.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)

TI Production of alkene e.g. ethylene, involves            contacting stream comprising alkane with oxygen in            presence of catalyst, removing unconverted oxygen,            carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide from obtained            stream, and recycling unconverted alkane.
AU MITKIDIS G

GA 201867634Q
AB    NOVELTY - Production of an alkene by alkyl oxidative                dehydrogenation involves subjecting a stream (s1)                comprising an alkane to oxidative dehydrogenation                which involves contacting the alkane with oxygen in                the presence of a catalyst comprising a mixed metal                oxide to obtain a stream (s2), removing water from                at least portion of the stream (s2) to obtain a                stream (s3), removing unconverted oxygen, carbon                monoxide and optionally alkyne from at least                portion of the stream (s3) and oxidizing carbon                monoxide and optionally alkyne into carbon dioxide                to obtain a stream (s4) comprising alkene,                unconverted alkane and carbon dioxide, and                optionally removing carbon dioxide from at least                portion of the stream (s4) to obtain a stream (s5)                comprising alkene and unconverted alkyl, and                optionally recycling unconverted alkane from at                least portion of the stream (s4) to oxidative                dehydrogenation.
   USE - Production of alkene e.g. ethylene and                propylene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical and efficient                production of alkene.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of an alkene by alkyl oxidative                dehydrogenation involves subjecting a stream (s1)                comprising an alkane to oxidative dehydrogenation                which involves contacting the alkane with oxygen in                the presence of a catalyst comprising a mixed metal                oxide to obtain a stream (s2) comprising alkene,                unconverted alkane, water, carbon dioxide,                unconverted oxygen, carbon monoxide and optionally                an alkyne, removing water from at least portion of                the stream (s2) to obtain a stream (s3) comprising                alkene, unconverted alkane, carbon dioxide,                unconverted oxygen, carbon monoxide and optionally                alkyne, removing unconverted oxygen, carbon                monoxide and optionally alkyne from at least                portion of the stream (s3) and oxidizing carbon                monoxide and optionally alkyne into carbon dioxide                to obtain a stream (s4) comprising alkene,                unconverted alkane and carbon dioxide, optionally                separating at least portion of the stream (s5) into                a stream (s6) comprising alkene and a stream (s7)                comprising unconverted alkyl, and optionally                removing carbon dioxide from at least portion of                the stream (s4) to obtain a stream (s5) comprising                alkene and unconverted alkyl, and optionally                recycling unconverted alkane from at least portion                of the stream (s4) to oxidative                dehydrogenation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The oxidation                  catalyst comprises one or more metals chosen from                  nickel, copper, zinc, palladium, silver,                  platinum, gold, iron, manganese, cerium, tin,                  ruthenium and chromium, preferably copper.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step of removing                  unconverted oxygen, carbon monoxide and                  optionally alkyne from stream (s3) is carried out                  at a temperature of 100-400 degrees C, preferably                  at most 250 degrees C in the presence of an                  oxidation catalyst, preferably an oxidation                  catalyst comprising a transition metal. The                  additional oxygen is fed during the step of                  removing unconverted oxygen, carbon monoxide and                  optionally alkyne from stream (s3).

PN CN114231328-A
TI Purifying biogas, involves introducing methane            containing hydrogen sulfide from lower portion of            desulfurization absorption tower, sending methane into            desulfurization tank for desulfurization, and then            detecting content of hydrogen sulfide.
AU CHEN C
   HOU W
   SHU J
   YANG J
AE JIANGSU HONGRUN BIOMASS ENERGY TECHNOLOG (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202248948V
AB    NOVELTY - Purifying biogas, involves introducing methane                containing hydrogen sulfide from the lower portion                of the desulfurization absorption tower, where                methane containing hydrogen sulfide is in                countercurrent contact with alkaline solution                filled from top to bottom in a packing area of the                desulfurization absorption tower in the                desulfurization absorption tower, and portion of                hydrogen sulfide in the methane is absorbed by the                alkaline solution. The liquid water is removed from                the methane after the hydrogen sulfide is removed                through a first gas-liquid separator. The methane                after primary desulfurization is sent into a                desulfurization tank, and secondary desulfurization                is realized through the absorption of a                desulfurizing agent in the desulfurization tank,                and further detected whether the content of                hydrogen sulfide in the biogas after two                desulfations is lower than a preset range, if yes,                entered the next, and if not, returned to previous                one.
   USE - Method for purifying biogas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to realize cold and heat                exchange in the decarburization process and the                recycling of the complex amine solution through                combined use of the regeneration tower and the                decarburization absorption tower, which further                realizes energy saving and emission                reduction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Purifying biogas, involves introducing methane                containing hydrogen sulfide from the lower portion                of the desulfurization absorption tower, where                methane containing hydrogen sulfide is in                countercurrent contact with alkaline solution                filled from top to bottom in a packing area of the                desulfurization absorption tower in the                desulfurization absorption tower, and portion of                hydrogen sulfide in the methane is absorbed by the                alkaline solution. The liquid water is removed from                the methane after the hydrogen sulfide is removed                through a first gas-liquid separator. The methane                after primary desulfurization is sent into a                desulfurization tank, and secondary desulfurization                is realized through the absorption of a                desulfurizing agent in the desulfurization tank,                and further detected whether the content of                hydrogen sulfide in the biogas after two                desulfations is lower than a preset range, if yes,                entered the next, and if not, returned to previous                one. The desulfurized biogas is placed into a                primary filter to remove large-particle impurities                in the biogas. The filtered biogas is cooled by a                heat exchanger, the generated condensed water is                discharged from the bottom of the heat exchanger,                and fine liquid drops are separated in a second                gas-liquid separator in a rotating manner along                with the gas flow. The dehydrated methane is                pressurized and output by a fan, and send into a                precision filter to remove small particle                impurities in the methane. The biogas is                respectively detected through a pressure sensor, a                temperature sensor, a methane measuring instrument,                an oxygen measuring instrument, a carbon dioxide                measuring instrument and a hydrogen sulfide                measuring instrument to determine whether the                pressure. The temperature and the corresponding gas                content required by the subsequent process are                reached, if yes, entered the next, and if not,                returned to previous one. The qualified biogas is                sent into a biogas compressor for secondary                pressurization. The pressurized methane is sent                into an amine decarburization system, liquid water                in the methane is removed after the methane passes                through a first gas-liquid separation process. The                methane entered the bottom of a decarburization                absorption tower and is in countercurrent contact                with a compound amine solution flowing downwards                from top to bottom in the decarburization                absorption tower from bottom to top, carbon dioxide                in the methane is absorbed in the heat transfer                process. The purified methane is discharged from                the top of the tower, the temperature is reduced                through a first cooler, and the methane is sent to                the next process after passing through a second                gas-liquid separation process and condensate                recovery. The composite amine solution absorbing                the carbon dioxide is discharged from the bottom of                the decarburization absorption tower, exchanges                heat with the composite amine solution not                absorbing the carbon dioxide, entered the top of                the regeneration tower to flash off most of the                carbon dioxide in the composite amine solution, and                then is in countercurrent contact with stripping                steam generated by a steam boiler at the bottom of                the regeneration tower from top to bottom to desorb                the residual carbon dioxide in the composite amine                solution. The composite amine solution without                carbon dioxide discharged from the bottom of the                regeneration tower exchanges heat with the                composite amine solution absorbing carbon dioxide,                and is pressurized and cooled by a booster pump and                a second cooler and then sent to the top of the                decarburization absorption tower for recycling. The                carbon dioxide discharged from the top of the                regeneration tower is cooled and pressurized by a                third cooler, and then is subjected to a third                gas-liquid separation process to realize the                storage of liquid carbon dioxide. When the                pressurized and purified marsh gas is contacted                with the adsorbent with low surface steam pressure,                the steam in the pressurized and purified marsh gas                is transferred to the surface of the adsorbent, and                the surface steam pressure of the adsorbent is                gradually increased until the pressure is balanced,                so that the pressurized marsh gas is adsorbed and                dried, and then pressurized the dried methane by                using a circulating compressor to promote the flow                of the methane. The odorizing agent is directly                injected into the biogas pipeline through the                odorizing equipment to odorize the dried biogas in                proportion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The alkaline solution                  absorbing the hydrogen sulfide flows into the                  liquid storage tank from the bottom of the                  desulfurization absorption tower, and is sent                  into the desulfurization absorption tower through                  the circulating pump to realize circulating                  absorption. The sodium carbonate and/or sodium                  hydroxide are supplemented into the alkaline                  solution according to a preset period. The                  desulfurizer filled in the desulfurization tank                  is an iron oxide desulfurizer. The preset range                  in the primary detection process is that the                  content of hydrogen sulfide in the biogas is                  1.0-1.2 parts per million. The biogas compressor                  pressurizes the biogas to more than 0.4 MPa. The                  adsorbent is heated at high temperature to                  realize desorption of water vapor from the                  adsorbent, and meanwhile, when the cyclic                  compressor pressurizes and flows the regenerated                  gas, the water vapor is condensed, cooled and                  liquefied, and after liquid water is subjected to                  gas-liquid separation, the gas is sent to the                  cyclic compressor again. The odorizing device                  adopted a diaphragm type plug metering pump,                  odorous agent is sucked in through an inlet                  one-way valve in the suction stroke, and odorous                  agent is discharged into a biogas pipeline                  through an outlet one-way valve in the discharge                  stroke.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. AU SERRAIOCCO L
   PORRO L G
AE YARA INT ASA (YARI-C)
   YARA INT ASA (YARI-C)
   YARA INT ASA (YARI-C)
GA 201997366S
AB    NOVELTY - Shell-and-tube stripper comprises: top end in                fluid connection with bottom end through many tubes                disposed within shell, where the top end comprises                inlet for urea/carbamate mixture and outlet for gas                mixture of stripping gas and stripped compound; the                bottom end comprising outlet for urea/carbamate                stream concentrated in urea and, where the stripper                further comprises first heating fluid inlet, second                heating fluid inlet, and heating fluid outlet is in                fluid connection with shell-side space disposed                between the tubes and the shell; the first heating                fluid inlet is adjacently disposed to the top end                of the stripper and the heating fluid outlet is                adjacently disposed to the rear end of the                stripper; or the first heating fluid inlet is                adjacently disposed to the bottom end of the                stripper and the heating fluid outlet is adjacently                disposed to the top end of the stripper and the                second heating fluid inlet is disposed between the                first heating fluid inlet and the heating fluid                outlet.
   USE - The stripper is useful: for stripping                urea/carbamate mixture; and in a system for                producing urea (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Shell-and-tube stripper for stripping a                urea/carbamate mixture comprises: a top end in                fluid connection with a bottom end through many                tubes disposed within a shell; the top end                comprises an inlet for a urea/carbamate mixture and                an outlet for a gas mixture comprising the                stripping gas and at least one stripped compound;                the bottom end comprises an outlet for a                urea/carbamate stream concentrated in urea and,                where the stripper further comprises a first                heating fluid inlet, a second heating fluid inlet,                and a heating fluid outlet is in fluid connection                with a shell-side space disposed between the tubes                and the shell; the first heating fluid inlet is                adjacently disposed to the top end of the stripper                and the heating fluid outlet is adjacently disposed                to the rear end of the stripper; or the first                heating fluid inlet is adjacently disposed to the                bottom end of the stripper and the heating fluid                outlet is adjacently disposed to the top end of the                stripper and the second heating fluid inlet is                disposed between the first heating fluid inlet and                the heating fluid outlet. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are                also included for:
   (1) a system for producing urea comprising a                carbamate condenser, a urea reactor and the                stripper, where the carbamate condenser is arranged                to partially and exothermically transform ammonia                and carbon dioxide into ammonium carbamate, and to                partially convert the thus forming ammonium                carbamate to urea, thus obtaining a condenser                effluent comprising ammonium carbamate, the system                is arranged to provide the condenser effluent to                the urea reactor, the urea reactor is arranged to                adiabatically convert at least a part of the                ammonium carbamate in the condenser effluent into                urea, thus obtaining a urea/carbamate mixture, the                system is arranged to provide the urea/carbamate                mixture to the stripper, the stripper is arranged                to convert the urea/carbamate mixture into a                urea/carbamate stream concentrated in urea and a                gaseous stream comprising carbon dioxide and                ammonia;
   (2) a system for the producing urea comprising                at least a combined reactor that serves both as                carbamate condenser and urea reactor, where the                system further comprises the stripper, the combined                reactor is arranged to partially and exothermally                transform ammonia and carbon dioxide to ammonium                carbamate, the combined reactor further is arranged                to partially convert the ammonium carbamate into                urea, thus obtaining a urea/carbamate mixture, the                system is arranged to provide the urea/carbamate                mixture to the stripper, the stripper is arranged                to convert the urea/carbamate mixture into a                urea/carbamate stream concentrated in urea and a                gaseous stream comprising carbon dioxide and                ammonia; and
   (3) stripping a urea/carbamate mixture                comprising providing the stripper, providing the                urea/carbamate mixture to the inlet for the                urea/carbamate mixture, providing a heating fluid                to the shell-side space by means of the first                heating fluid inlet, by means of the second heating                fluid inlet, and optionally by means of the further                heating fluid inlets, contacting the urea/carbamate                mixture and the stripping gas in a tube-side space                disposed within the tubes, and heating the                urea/carbamate mixture by means of the heating                fluid, thus obtaining a urea/carbamate stream                concentrated in urea, extracting the urea/carbamate                stream concentrated in urea at the outlet for the                urea/carbamate stream concentrated in urea,                extracting a gas mixture comprising at least one                stripped compound at the outlet for the gas                mixture, the stripped compounds comprising ammonia,                carbon dioxide and water, and extracting the                heating fluid at the heating fluid outlet.

PN KR2022120279-A
TI Preparing spherical amine-based carbon dioxide            adsorbent useful for adsorbing carbon dioxide,            comprises e.g. adding support material to solution with            amine compound dissolved in solvent, reacting, and            spray-drying solution.
AU KI C M
   SUNG C W
   PARK J
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 2022B2236U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing spherical amine-based carbon dioxide                adsorbent, comprises (a) adding a support material                to a solution in which an amine compound is                dissolved in a solvent, and reacting; and (b)                spray-drying the solution to obtain the product                from which the solvent is removed.
   USE - The adsorbent is useful for adsorbing carbon                dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and suitable for mass                production as the amine compound and the support                raw material are mixed in one-pot and manufactured                by spray drying only once.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solution further                  comprises an additive of sodium silicate, calcium                  silicate, calcium aluminate and/or bentonite.                  Preferred Method: The drying is spray-dried at                  333-573 K.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amine compound                  comprises a unit skeleton structure of formula                  (-[(CH2)x-NR]y) (I) and (-[(CH2)x-NH2]) (II). The                  amine compound cis ethylenediamine,                  triethylenetetramine, tetraethylenepentamine,                  pentaethylenehexamine, hexaethyleneheptamine,                  polyethyleneimine, polypropyleneimine, and an                  ethyleneimine amine compound containing a hydroxy                  group-containing carbon chain as specified. The                  support material is silica, alumina, activated                  carbon and/or zeolite. The solvent is water,                  methanol, ethanol, methyl chloride, carbon                  tetrachloride and/or tetrahydrofuran. The amount                  of amine compound is 5-75 wt.% based on the total                  mass of the adsorbent. The support material is in                  an amount of 25-95 wt.% based on the total mass                  of the adsorbent. The weight ratio of amine                  compound, support material and solvent is                  1:0.1-5:1-10. The adsorbent has a diameter of                  20-500 &#956; m.R=hydrogen or a straight or branched alkyl                  group having 1-18C;x=2-6; andy=1-100.
   EXAMPLE - One-pot preparation of an adsorbent using the                1,2-epoxybutane-modified polyethyleneimine as an                amine compound and silica as a support was                performed. The modified polyethyleneimine solution                was stirred until completely dissolved by                dissolving 30 kg of polyethyleneimine in 200 kg of                water under 298K. Then, 18 kg of 1,2-epoxybutane                was added to the stirred polyethyleneimine aqueous                solution so that the molar ratio of 1,2-epoxybutane                to the nitrogen atom (N) present in the                polyethyleneimine was 0.37, and further stirred for                12 hours to synthesize a modified                polyethyleneimine/water solution. Fumed silica and                sodium silicate as silica support raw materials                were added to the modified polyethyleneimine/water                solution, and the ratio of the amine compound to                the total adsorbent was adjusted be 50 wt.%, 47.5                wt.%, 45 wt.% and 40 wt.%. The amounts added (fumed                silica, sodium silicate) were 50 wt.%, 47.5 wt.%,                45 wt.%, 40 wt.%, respectively (41.5 kg, 4.1 kg),                (45.5 kg, 4.55 kg), (50.5 kg, 5.05 kg), (62 kg, 6.2                kg) were added and stirred until a uniform solution                was formed. Then, the solutions prepared in the                one-pot were sprayed in a spray drying device to                form fine droplets, and water was evaporated using                a hot drying gas (air) of 423 K to dry the amine                compound and the support. The samples prepared were                50 wt.% EB-PEI/silicon dioxide, 47.5 wt.%                EB-PEI/silicon dioxide, 45 wt.% EB-PEI/silicon                dioxide, and 40 wt.% EB-PEI/silicon dioxide,                respectively.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

The amount of solute and solvent is 80-99.9 wt.% of the decarburization solution. The conditions of decarburization solution is at pressure 0-1.2 MPa and temperature 10-140 degrees C.

   JIA D
   SU X
   ZHANG F
   CHEN Z
   GUO J
   WANG Z
AE UNIV XIAN SHIYOU (UXSY-C)
GA 202235074P
AB    NOVELTY - Magnetic nano modified carbon dioxide                vermiform foam fluid comprises 1-10 wt.%                viscoelastic surfactant, 0-4.24 wt.% anti-ion salt,                0.1-10 wt.% surface modified nano iron oxide                particles, 30-50 wt.% liquid carbon dioxide and                deionized water (remaining amount), where the                particle size of the surface-modified nano iron                oxide particles is 5-50 nm.
   USE - The foam fluid is useful as well fracturing                fluid, crack monitoring fluid and oil displacement                fluid (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The foam fluid combines the surface modified                nano iron oxide particles, VES worm-type micelle                liquid and carbon dioxide, and has good stability,                high temperature resistance, low VES concentration                and strong magnetic field and rheological                properties with controllable performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing vermiform foam fluid comprises (i) adding                viscoelastic surfactant into deionized water to                fully dissolve, adding anti-ion salt, and stirring                for 25-35 minutes to obtain VES worm-type micelle                liquid I; (ii) allowing VES worm-typemicelle liquid                I to stand without bubbles, gradually adding the                surface-modified nano iron oxide particles II, so                that the weight percent of the surface-modified                nanoscale iron oxide particles II in the target                solution is 0.1-10%, and adding into an ultrasonic                cleaner for ultrasonic dispersion for 25-35 minutes                to form a magnetic nano-modified worm-type micelle                solution; and (iii) using a foam generator to make                carbon dioxide foam 3 from liquid carbon dioxide                and mixing with the magnetic nano-modified                worm-type micelle solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The viscoelastic                  surfactant is sodium oleate and/or octadecyl                  trimethyl ammonium chloride. The anti-ion salt is                  sodium carbonate or sodium salicylate. The                  surface-modified nano- iron oxide particles are                  surface-modified nano- iron oxide particles,                  where the trade name is oleic acid-modified                  ferric oxide magnetic nanoparticles, and the                  product number/package is F196576-100 mg. The                  viscoelastic surfactant is sodium oleate and the                  weight percentage of the sodium oleate in the                  magnetic nano-modified carbon dioxide worm-type                  foam fluid is 1.83%, anti-ion salt is sodium                  carbonate, and the weight percentage of the                  sodium carbonate in the magnetic nano-modified                  carbon dioxide worm-type foam fluid is                  2.65-4.24%. The viscoelastic surfactant is                  octadecyl trimethyl ammonium chloride, the                  anti-ion salt is sodium salicylate, and the                  quality of the octadecyl trimethyl ammonium                  chloride and sodium salicylate is 5:1. The ratio                  of material of sodium oleate and octadecyl                  trimethyl ammonium chloride is 1: 6 and weight                  percentage of the counter ion salt in the                  magnetic nano-modified carbon dioxide worm-type                  foam fluid is 0%.

PN US2021062649-A1; WO2021041899-A1; US11047233-B2
TI Method for evaluating hydrocarbon-bearing            formation, involves identifying a hydrocarbon sweet            spot comprising the steps of: determining a carbon            dioxide content of a gas sample retrieved from the            hydrocarbon-bearing formation; and determining an            isotopic signature of carbon dioxide of the gas            sample.
AU LUO P
   LU P
   AROURI K
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
GA 2021246874
AB    NOVELTY - The method (100) involves identifying (114) a                hydrocarbon sweet spot. A carbon dioxide content of                a gas sample retrieved from the hydrocarbon-bearing                formation is determined. An isotopic signature of                carbon dioxide of the gas sample is determined. The                carbon dioxide content of the gas sample has a mole                percentage ranging from 9 to 20 percent. A cutoff                range of the carbon dioxide content corresponding                to the hydrocarbon sweet spot is determined (112).                The cutoff range has a mole percentage ranging from                9 to 20 percent. A gas flow rate of the                hydrocarbon-bearing formation is obtained. The gas                flow rate is greater than 10 million standard cubic                feet per day.
   USE - Method for evaluating a hydrocarbon-bearing                formation during reservoir evaluation, reservoir                simulation, field development and facility design,                used for example in the the petroleum                industry.
   ADVANTAGE - (1) The increased formation pressure improves                reservoir quality and enhances the productivity of                the hydrocarbon-bearing tight formation.
(2) The increased formation pressure prevents                compaction of the tight formation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating a                method for evaluating hydrocarbon-bearing                formation.
   Method for evaluating hydrocarbon-bearing                formation (100)
   Step for locating and evaluating a prospect                hydrocarbon-bearing formation (102)
   Step for retrieving a gas sample from the                pilot well (104)
   Step for determining a cutoff range of the                carbon dioxide content corresponding to the                hydrocarbon sweet spot (112)
   Step for identifying a hydrocarbon sweet spot                (114)
