PT P
PN CN111879900-A
TI Carbon source discharge metering device useful for tourism carbon collection has carbon dioxide detector mechanism, carbon dioxide detector connected to external power supply by lead, and air inlet end of vacuum pump connected with box body.
AU FAN H
AE HAINAN ECONOMY & TRADE VOCATIONAL TECH (HAIN-Non-standard)
GA 2020B2056G
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon source discharge metering device comprises a box body (1) and a carbon dioxide detector mechanism. The carbon dioxide detector mechanism includes a carbon dioxide detector and a lead (3). The carbon dioxide detector is connected to an external power supply through lead. The carbon dioxide detector is fixedly installed inside the box body. The lead passes through the box body. The penetration point between the lead and box body is sealed and arranged. A transparent glass observing window (4) is installed on one side of the box body. An air inlet pipe (5) is connected to the top center of the box body. The top of the air inlet pipe is provided with a sealing plug body (6). A transverse rod is fixedly installed on two sides of the box body. The sliding block is slidably arranged in the guide rail. A driving mechanism is provided outside the box body. A vacuum pump is fixedly installed on one side of the box body. The air inlet end of the vacuum pump is connected with the box body.
   USE - The device is useful for tourism carbon collection.
   ADVANTAGE - The device can realize the movement of the sealing plug body by cam mechanism.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the carbon source discharge metering device for useful for tourism carbon collection.
   Box body (1)
   Lead (3)
   Transparent glass observing window (4)
   Air inlet pipe (5)
   Sealing plug body (6)

PT P
PN US2020325427-A1; US11053467-B2
TI System used for accelerating aging of alcohol spirits, processing wood pieces to artificial aging, comprises machine frame, pressure vessel, carbon dioxide pressurization source, heating mechanism, processor, and unprocessed alcohol spirits.
AU MITCHELL J B
   GEIGER A R
   MITCHELL W H
AE PB FUNDING LLC (PBFU-Non-standard)
   PB FUNDING GROUP LLC (PBFU-Non-standard)
GA 2020A05432
AB    NOVELTY - System (200) comprises machine frame, pressure vessel (226), carbon dioxide pressurization source (228), heating mechanism (230), processor (208), where the method is performed by processor which involves detecting pressure vessel which is attached to machine frame; providing pressure vessel with unprocessed alcohol spirits; causing carbon dioxide pressurization source to pressurize vessel with carbon dioxide; causing heating mechanism to heat alcohol spirits in pressure vessel during pressurization; holding pressure vessel at pressure for first predefined time; and releasing carbon dioxide under pressure after first predetermined time.
   USE - System used for accelerating aging of alcohol spirits and processing wood pieces to artificial aging.
   ADVANTAGE - The system enables to enhances or minimizes aging, increases or decreases different flavors, mouthfeel, and other tasting characteristics, and increases processing times.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method which involves:
   (A) receiving unprocessed alcohol spirits in pressure vessel;
   (B) pressurizing alcohol spirits in pressure vessel with carbon dioxide to predefined pressure, which is being at least 30pound per square inch;
   (C) maintaining at least predefined pressure in pressure vessel for at least first predefined time, where first predefined time is being at least 5 minutes;
   (D) returning pressure vessel to atmospheric pressure; and
   (E) removing processed alcohol spirits from pressure vessel.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of system for accelerating aging of alcohol spirits.
   System (200)
   Processor (208)
   Pressure vessel (226)
   Carbon dioxide pressurization source (228)
   Heating mechanism (230)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The pressure of carbon dioxide in pressure vessel is 30pound per square inch (psi), 40 psi, 50 psi, 60 psi, 70 psi, 80 psi, 100 psi, 120 psi, 140 psi, and 160 psi. The alcohol spirit is heated at 80Fahrenheit, 90Fahrenheit, 100Fahrenheit, 110Fahrenheit, 120Fahrenheit, 130Fahrenheit, 140Fahrenheit and 150Fahrenheit. The first predetermined time is 30minutes, which is longer than second predetermined time. The second predetermined time is being at least 15minutes. The pressure vessel is heated at least 80Fahrenheit and maintaining at least predefined pressure.

PT P
PN CN111443380-A; CN211826544-U
TI Seismic exploration gas explosion type seismic source projectile comprises pressure vessel provided with carbon dioxide filling port, first exhaust port and branch pipe and branch pipe connected with pressure vessel.
AU JIANG Y
   ZHU Z
   LIANG S
   LIU Z
   JIA L
   LV M
   DUAN L
   HUANG J
AE ENERGY RES INST SHANDONG ACAD SCI (UYQQ-C)
GA 202071653T
AB    NOVELTY - Seismic exploration gas explosion type seismic source projectile comprises pressure vessel, carbon dioxide filling port, first exhaust port, branch pipe, liquid carbon dioxide, reaction vessel, quicklime, water injection port, second exhaust port and insulation layer. The pressure vessel is provided with a carbon dioxide filling port, a first exhaust port and a branch pipe. The branch pipe is connected with the pressure vessel, and the branch pipe is loaded with liquid carbon dioxide. The branch pipe is installed in the reaction vessel, and the reaction vessel is filled with quicklime, and the quicklime wraps the pipe. The water injection port and the second exhaust port are both arranged on the reaction vessel. The carbon dioxide filling port, the first exhaust port, the water injection port, the other end ports of the second exhaust ports all extend to the outside of the thermal insulation layer, and valves are provided on these ports.
   USE - Used as seismic exploration gas explosion type seismic source projectile.
   ADVANTAGE - The projectile is very suitable for field and underwater seismic exploration operations and uses carbon dioxide expansion as the blasting working fluid and uses water and quicklime hydration to generate heat, which is simpler than electric explosion and does not consume electric power.

PN WO2018052885-A1; CN109790018-A; EP3512803-A1; US2019233350-A1
TI Production of unsaturated hydrocarbon e.g. ethylene, involves oxidatively pyrolyzing hydrocarbon feedstock to form acetylene and carbon dioxide and syngas products, hydrogenating acetylene and converting product portion to syngas product.
AU SANKARANARAYANAN K
   GAUTAM P S
   MAMEDOV A K
   PANNALA S
   RAMACHANDRAN NAIR B
   LENGYEL I
   WEST D
   RAMACHANDRAN N B K
   RAMACHANDRAN N B
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SANKARANARAYANAN K (SANK-Individual)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 2018232050
AB    NOVELTY - Production of unsaturated 2C hydrocarbons involves subjecting a hydrocarbon feedstock (11) to an oxidative pyrolysis to form at least acetylene, a carbon dioxide product, and a syngas product (s1), hydrogenating the acetylene to form at least ethylene and a hydrogenation product, and converting at least a portion of the carbon dioxide product through an oxidative dry reforming process to form at least a syngas product (s2).
   USE - Production of unsaturated 2C hydrocarbon e.g. ethylene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently produces 2C unsaturated hydrocarbons with high yield, carbon dioxide and syngas products, utilizes carbon dioxide side products for catalytic hydrogenation, and improves carbon efficiency of the overall process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system, which comprises cracking reactor (200) to effect at least a cracking or combustion of the hydrocarbon feed stream to form the product mixture comprising acetylene, carbon dioxide product, and syngas product (s1), separator (210) configured downstream from the cracking reactor to effect a separation among the product mixture components, catalytic hydrogenation (230) reactor configured downstream from the separator to effect at least hydrogenation of acetylene and formation of the hydrogenation product, and dry reforming reactor (240) configured subsequent to the separator to effect at least conversion of carbon dioxide to the syngas product (s2).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a reactor block diagram for the integration of methane-to-acetylene conversion with acetylene hydrogenation and methane dry oxidative reforming reaction.
   Hydrocarbon feedstock (11)
   Cracking reactor (200)
   Separator (210)
   Catalytic hydrogenation reactor (230)
   Dry reforming reactor (240)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The catalyst comprises palladium-zinc aluminum oxide complex.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ratio of hydrogen gas to carbon monoxide in the syngas product (s1) is 2:1 and ratio of hydrogen gas to carbon monoxide in the syngas product (s2) is 1:5. Preferred Components: The hydrocarbon feedstock comprises saturated hydrocarbon, methane, heavy residue, natural case, ethane, propane, naphtha, and/or unsaturated gas. Preferred Process: The method further involves separating the carbon dioxide product from the acetylene and syngas product (S1), separating the acetylene from the syngas product (s1) and the carbon dioxide product, and/or separating the syngas from one or more carbon dioxide product and acetylene. The separation processes involve amine adsorption or pressure swing adsorption. The method further involves combining the syngas products (s1) and (s2) and directing the syngas products to the hydrocarbon feedstock for oxidative pyrolysis and to promote hydrogenation of acetylene. The oxidative pyrolysis involves combustion of methane in the presence of oxygen and a subsequent oxidation, to generate heat and conversion of separate methane feed to form acetylene using the generated heat. The hydrogenation involves reacting acetylene in a liquid phase in the presence of a catalyst by liquid phase selective hydrogenation. The method further involves directing the hydrogenation product to the hydrocarbon feedstock for oxidative pyrolysis. The oxidative dry reforming process involves combusting the carbon dioxide product and methane in the presence of a reforming catalyst that facilitates conversion of carbon dioxide product, which has a conversion of 95% in the presence of oxygen. The syngas products (s1) and (s2) are combined to form a feedstock of a syngas conversion process.

TI System used for packaging meat, consists of retail meat or poultry tray comprising tray with one or more oxygen scavenger sachets and absorbent pad, full poultry birds or bird pieces, sealed master bag, and carbon dioxide generator.
AU TEWARI G
AE TEWARI G (TEWA-Individual)
GA 202097883H
AB    NOVELTY - A system consists of retail meat or poultry tray comprising tray with one or more oxygen scavenger sachets, carbon dioxide generator and absorbent pad over-wrapped with gas permeable plastic-based film permitting atmosphere exchange between interior and exterior of the meat tray, full poultry birds or bird pieces with one or more oxygen scavengers sachets and carbon dioxide generator, full poultry birds or bird pieces with one or more oxygen scavengers sachets, and sealed master bag comprising at least one retail meat tray or full poultry birds or chicken pieces, and carbon dioxide generator formed by mixture of malic acid and sodium carbonate to generate appropriate amount of carbon dioxide of at least 100 mL per pound of meat or poultry. The oxygen scavenger sachet comprises an iron-based oxygen absorbing material self-activated in an atmosphere having a relative humidity of more than 70%. The carbon dioxide generator is activated by injecting small amount of water into the sachet.
   USE - System used for packaging meat and poultry for extending shelf life of meat and poultry (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A system consists of retail meat or poultry tray comprising tray with one or more oxygen scavenger sachets, carbon dioxide generator and an absorbent pad over-wrapped with a gas permeable plastic-based film permitting atmosphere exchange between interior and exterior of the meat tray, full poultry birds or bird pieces with one or more oxygen scavengers sachets and carbon dioxide generator, full poultry birds or bird pieces with one or more oxygen scavengers sachets, and sealed master bag comprising at least one retail meat tray or full poultry birds or chicken pieces, and carbon dioxide generator formed by mixture of malic acid and sodium carbonate to generate appropriate amount of carbon dioxide of at least 100 mL per pound of meat or poultry in period of 7 days of sealing master bag. The oxygen scavenger sachet comprises an iron-based oxygen absorbing material self-activated in an atmosphere having a relative humidity of more than 70%. The carbon dioxide generator is activated by injecting small amount of water into the sachet. The master bag is flushed before sealing to remove oxygenated air from the interior of the bag to obtain an initial atmosphere equal to or less than 5% oxygen and injected with an oxygen-free, carbon dioxide gas or 50% inert gas. The oxygen absorption capacity of the oxygen scavenger sachet is sufficient to achieve a half-life for 5% residual oxygen of equal to or less than 10.0 hours and reaches zero oxygen storage atmosphere within 96 hours of sealing master bag. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) packaging meat; and
   (2) poultry.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxygen scavenger sachet comprises a porous bag having an active surface area of 4-64 square inches and porosity of 20-120 gurly per second, chemical granules having a diameter of 0.001-1.5, and 1-300 g absorbing chemical. The system comprises carbon dioxide sachets comprising a porous bag having an active surface area of 4-64 square inches and porosity of 20-120 gurly per second, chemical granules having a diameter of 0.001-30 and 10-500 g carbon dioxide generating material, and injected gas comprising carbon dioxide or nitrogen. Preferred Composition: The chemical granules in oxygen scavenger sachet comprise less than 25% iron, less than 35% carbon, less than 20% vermiculite, less than 10% deionized water, less than 10% sodium chloride salt, and less than 10% zeolites. The chemical granules in carbon dioxide sachet comprise less than 80% sodium carbonate, and less than 30% malic acid. The injected gas comprises 100% carbon dioxide, or more than 50% inert gas and balance carbon dioxide, or more than 50% carbon dioxide and balance inert gas. The oxygen scavengers comprise 15-20% iron, 20-25% carbon, 10-15% vermiculite, 5-10% deionized water, 5-10% sodium chloride salt, and 0-10% zeolites. The carbon dioxide generator comprises 60-80% sodium carbonate, and 20-40% malic acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The poultry packed is treated with peracetic acid, and is air-chilled or water-chilled.

AU WANG J
   CHEN N
   PAN Z
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 202044146X
AB    NOVELTY - Device includes a confocal micro Raman spectrometer, a high-pressure ventilation pipeline, a circulating balance pipeline system and a hot and cold stage. The high-pressure ventilation pipeline is provided for receiving nitrogen steel cylinder (1), carbon dioxide steel cylinder (2), vacuum pump (3) and quantitative pump (4). The nitrogen steel cylinder and the carbon dioxide steel cylinder are connected to the quantitative pump by pipeline, where the vacuum pump is also connected to the quantitative pump by using pipeline. The circulation balance pipeline system is provided with a phase balance kettle (9), a quartz capillary (11) and a circulation pump (10). The phase balance kettle, a quartz capillary and a circulation pump are composed of pipelines connection to form loop.
   USE - Device for in-situ on-line determination of solubility of carbon dioxide in alkanes.
   ADVANTAGE - The device has characteristics of high data reliability without requiring sampling, safety, high efficiency, intuitiveness, strong operability and repeatable operation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Device includes a confocal micro Raman spectrometer, a high-pressure ventilation pipeline, a circulating balance pipeline system and a hot and cold stage. The high-pressure ventilation pipeline is provided for receiving nitrogen steel cylinder (1), carbon dioxide steel cylinder (2), vacuum pump (3) and quantitative pump (4). The nitrogen steel cylinder and the carbon dioxide steel cylinder are connected to the quantitative pump by pipeline, where the vacuum pump is also connected to the quantitative pump by using pipeline. The circulation balance pipeline system is provided with a phase balance kettle (9), a quartz capillary (11) and a circulation pump (10). The phase balance kettle, a quartz capillary and a circulation pump are composed of pipelines connection to form loop. The pipeline connected between the phase balance kettle, the quartz capillary and the circulation pump is wrapped with a thermal insulation layer to prevent the material system in the pipeline from being exposed to external temperature impact. The quartz capillary in the circulating balance pipeline system is placed on the hot and cold stage, where the quartz capillary is provided with a transparent visual window. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for in-situ on-line determination of solubility of carbon dioxide in alkanes, which involves:
   (A) injecting nitrogen into circulation balance pipeline system to check air tightness of the device;
   (B) using vacuum pump to evacuate high-pressure ventilation pipeline and circulation balance pipeline system;
   (C) injecting alkane into phase balance kettle;
   (D) adding carbon dioxide gas by the quantitative pump;
   (E) drawing the standard curve by using quantitative pump to add excess carbon dioxide gas to the phase equilibrium kettle;
   (F) circulating the carbon dioxide-alkane system in the phase balance kettle to the quartz capillary by using a circulation pump; and
   (G) using the Raman laser spectrometer to detect the quartz capillary Raman spectrum of saturated carbon dioxide-alkane system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the device for in-situ on-line determination of solubility of carbon dioxide in alkanes.
   Nitrogen steel cylinder (1)
   Carbon dioxide steel cylinder (2)
   Vacuum pump (3)
   Quantitative pump (4)
   Phase balance kettle (9)
   Circulation pump (10)
   Quartz capillary (11)

AU EREMIN V I
   EREMIN V
AE MOSHKOV V D (MOSH-Individual)
   MOSHKOV V (MOSH-Individual)
GA 2018847891
AB    NOVELTY - A nitrogen-free smelting of iron in blast furnace involves blowing cold oxygen and cold carbon monoxide in working blast furnace including air separation unit, head gas purification unit from carbon dioxide and dust, compressor equipment, carbon dioxide purification unit, and carbon dioxide liquefier, blowing hot air of 1200 degrees C and natural gas of 20-25 degrees C into blast furnace in which slow decrease in supply of hot air and natural gas begins at the start-up hour by supplying cold oxygen of 25 degrees C in 15 vol.% of total supply every 10 minutes, reduction in the supply of hot blast (atmospheric air) and natural gas and increase in the supply of oxygen obtained from the air separation unit is made within 1 hour. The blast furnace gas at outlet of blast furnace goes through purification into general collector of enterprise and blast furnace runs on one cold oxygen of 20-25 degrees C for 90 minutes in which all the nitrogen will leave the blast furnace.
   USE - A nitrogen-free smelting of iron in blast furnace.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A nitrogen-free smelting of iron in blast furnace involves blowing cold oxygen and cold carbon monoxide in working blast furnace including air separation unit, head gas purification unit from carbon dioxide and dust, compressor equipment, carbon dioxide purification unit, and carbon dioxide liquefier, blowing hot air of 1200 degrees C and natural gas of 20-25 degrees C into blast furnace in which slow decrease in supply of hot air and natural gas begins at the start-up hour by supplying cold oxygen of 25 degrees C in 15 vol.% of total supply every 10 minutes, reduction in the supply of hot blast (atmospheric air) and natural gas and increase in the supply of oxygen obtained from the air separation unit is made within 1 hour. The blast furnace gas at outlet of blast furnace goes through purification into general collector of enterprise; blast furnace runs on one cold oxygen of 20-25 degrees C for 90 minutes in which all the nitrogen will leave the blast furnace, after which the gate on gas duct of scheme opens and the gate on top gas feed to plant's common collector closes, the top gas at the blast furnace outlet goes through the fiberglass gas ducts to head gas cleaning unit carbon dioxide and dust, in which gas is purified from dust, water and carbon dioxide, carbon dioxide from the installation goes to after-treatment plant, liquefaction and storage, and from there to consumers, at the same time, after the top gas purification unit, pure carbon monoxide is supplied to compressor equipment capable of injecting carbon monoxide to 0.4 MPa, after compressing carbon monoxide is supplied through eight tuyeres to the blast furnace, through the other eight tuyeres the necessary amount of oxygen is fed into the blast furnace, hitting the furnace chamber, carbon monoxide is oxidized to carbon dioxide with heat, increasing the temperature inside the hearth to greater than or equal to 2100 degrees C, saving coke, after which carbon dioxide in the blast furnace falls into upper portion of hearth where there is no oxygen tvuet carbon coke with little loss of heat passing from carbon dioxide.

TI Extracting components from botanical material, involves passing subcritical carbon dioxide solvent through botanical material, collecting first component, passing supercritical carbon dioxide solvent and collecting second component.
AU BRAY S
   BRAY J A
AE AZOTH SOLUTIONS LLC (AZOT-Non-standard)
GA 2020A5803R
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting (M1) components from a botanical material, comprises passing subcritical carbon dioxide solvent through the botanical material to extract a first component from the botanical material, collecting the first component in a first collection chamber, passing supercritical carbon dioxide solvent through the botanical material to extract a second component from the botanical material and collecting the second component in a second collection chamber separate from the first collection chamber.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting components from a botanical material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) extracting (M2) components from a botanical material, comprising passing subcritical solvent through the botanical material to extract a first component from the botanical material and passing supercritical solvent through the botanical material to extract a second component from the botanical material; and
   (2) extracting (M3) components from a botanical material, comprising passing subcritical carbon dioxide solvent through the botanical material to extract a first component from the botanical material, collecting the first component in a first collection chamber, passing subcritical carbon dioxide solvent mixed with a second solvent through the botanical material to extract a second component from the botanical material and collecting the second component in a second collection chamber separate from the first collection chamber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step of passing subcritical carbon dioxide through the botanical material and passing supercritical carbon dioxide through the botanical material are carried out in the same extraction vessel. The plant material is repositioned at least once while passing subcritical carbon dioxide through the plant material and/or while passing supercritical carbon dioxide through the plant material. The method is carried out in the absence of alcohol. The modifier is added to the subcritical carbon dioxide and/or the supercritical carbon dioxide. The method (M1) further comprises: heating the first component in a separation chamber prior to collecting the first component in the first collection chamber, where the heating boils off carbon dioxide without boiling the first component to separate carbon dioxide from the first component and heating the second component in the separation chamber prior to collecting the second component in the second collection chamber, where the heating boils off carbon dioxide without boiling the second component to separate carbon dioxide from the second component; collecting the boiled carbon dioxide, condensing the boiled carbon dioxide to provide liquid carbon dioxide and using the condensed liquid carbon dioxide in subsequent passing steps; mixing the first component with the second component, after further processing the first component and the second component, to form a product; and heating the first component in a separation chamber prior to collecting the first component in the first collection chamber, where the heating boils off carbon dioxide without boiling the first component to thereby separate carbon dioxide from the first component; and heating the second component in the separation chamber prior to collecting the second component in the second collection chamber, where the heating boils off carbon dioxide without boiling the second component to thereby separate carbon dioxide from the second component. The temperature and pressure are maintained steady inside the extraction vessel and the separation chamber while passing subcritical carbon dioxide or supercritical carbon dioxide through the botanical material. The temperature and pressure while passing supercritical carbon dioxide through the botanical material is higher than the temperature and pressure while passing subcritical carbon dioxide through the botanical material. The method (M1) or (M2) operates thermodynamically and in the absence of a powered pump. The method (M3) further comprises collecting the boiled carbon dioxide, condensing the boiled carbon dioxide to provide liquid carbon dioxide and using the condensed liquid carbon dioxide in subsequent passing steps. Preferred Components: The modifier is an alcohol. The first component is a volatile component and the second component is a non-volatile component.

The carbon dioxide adsorbent is calcium oxide doped-carbon material, and the molar ratio of calcium oxide to carbon is (1-4):1.

AU XIA A
   WANG Y
   JIANG X
   SHEN J
   HUANG X
   DENG Y
   HU J
   YANG M
   ZHAI K
   DENG S
AE UNIV NANJING NORMAL (UNNO-C)
GA 201942284Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing rod-shaped carbon dioxide cation doped sodium yttrium fluoride: ytterbium(III), erbium (III) (NaYF4:Yb3+, Er3+) luminescent nanomaterial comprises (i) adding precursor containing different ratios of lanthanide rare earth ions lanthanide(3+) and carbon dioxide cation into container containing oleic acid and octadecene, stirring and heating until all the solid in the flask dissolved to clear, and cooling to room temperature, where lanthanide(3+) is yttrium(3+), ytterbium(3+) and erbium(3+), (ii) adding methanol solution containing sodium hydroxide and ammonium fluoride into solution obtained in step (i), reacting at 110-120 degrees C, then removing methanol, reacting at 280-300 degrees C for 1-2 hours, and cooling to room temperature, and (iii) washing the nanoparticles obtained in step (ii), ultrasonically dispersing, and storing in cyclohexane to obtain luminescent nanomaterial.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing rod-shaped carbon dioxide cation doped sodium yttrium fluoride: ytterbium(III), erbium (III) luminescent nanomaterial.
   ADVANTAGE - The nanomaterial: has strong up-conversion luminescence intensity and large fluorescence intensity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for rod-shaped carbon dioxide cation doped sodium yttrium fluoride: ytterbium(III), erbium (III) luminescent nanomaterial obtained by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), precursor of carbon dioxide cation is cobalt(II) chloride; and precursor of the lanthanide rare earth ion is rare earth hydrochloride. The precursor of the lanthanide rare earth ion is yttrium(III) chloride, ytterbium(III) chloride and erbium(III) chloride. The molar ratio between yttrium(3+), ytterbium(3+), erbium(3+) and carbon dioxide cation is 30-70:15-20:2-5:10-40. The volume ratio between oleic acid and octadecene is 8-10:10-15. In step (iii), doping amount of carbon dioxide cation is 10-40%. The molar ratio between yttrium(3+) and sodium hydroxide is 1:3-9, and molar ratio between yttrium(3+) and ammonium fluoride is 1:5-15. Preferred Method: In step (ii), reaction is carried out at 300 degrees C for 1 hour after removing methanol.

TI Selective gas separation of produced gas from viscous oil recovery, for fuel, involves passing produced gas stream comprising e.g. methane through graphene-based material for selectively adsorbing hydrosulfide and/or carbon dioxide.
AU YAZDI A Z
   DUNN J A
   ESMAEILI P
AE IMPERIAL OIL RESOURCES LTD (IMOI-C)
GA 201946203C
AB    NOVELTY - Selective gas separation of produced gas from hydrocarbon recovery from underground reservoirs, involves providing produced gas stream (102) comprising 20-60 mol% carbon dioxide, 30-70 mol% methane, 0-0.4 mol% hydrogen sulfide, and 0.1-2 mol% 2C hydrocarbon from in-situ viscous oil recovery process, providing graphene-based material (104) and passing produced gas through graphene-based material for selectively adsorbing hydrosulfide and/or carbon dioxide from produced gas stream with graphene-based material and forming flue gas stream (106) comprising 50 mol% or more combined hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide and treated gas stream (108) comprising 75-95 mol% methane.
   USE - Selective gas e.g. hydrogen sulfide and/or carbon dioxide separation of produced gas stream from in-situ viscous oil recovery used for fuels and chemical feedstock.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively removes the hydrogen sulfide and/or carbon dioxide from produced gas streams, even at low temperature.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of Selective gas separation of produced gas from hydrocarbon recovery.
   Produced gas stream (102)
   Graphene-based material (104)
   Flue gas stream (106)
   Treated gas stream (108)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The graphene-based material comprises graphene oxide and is derivative of graphene nanomaterial and is in the form of sheet, ribbon, cap, quantum dot, solid powder, film, flake, gel-like material or porous structure and is derived from carbon source with crystallinity of 100%, preferably 10-20%, carbon nanotubes, graphite, coke, coal, asphalt, asphaltene, and/or woody biomass. The graphene-based material is chemically doped with adatoms, or functionalized with metallic or non-metallic functional groups to increase surface adsorption properties and selectivity and it is used as bed with total mass of 0.01-1 g/cm3 of produced gas stream. Preferred Property: The graphene-based material has lateral size of 5 nm-20 microns, and thickness of 1-100 nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred processes: The in-situ viscous oil recovery process is steam-assisted gravity drainage method (SAGD), cyclic steam stimulation (CSS), steam flooding (SF), solvent-assisted steam-assisted gravity damage (SA-SAGD), liquid addition to steam for enhanced recovery (LASER), solvent-dominated recovery process (SDRPs), vapor extraction (VAPEX), cyclic solvent-dominated recovery process (CSDRPs), cyclic solvent process (CSP), or steam flooding. The produced gas stream is passed through hydrogen sulfide scrubber to reduce an amount of hydrogen sulfide in produced gas stream. The treated gas stream is used as fuel to generate steam by using once through heat recovery generator to produce flue gas comprising hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and nitrogen. The flue gas is sequestered in underground formulation and is introduced to primary separation cell (PSC) beneath layer, middling stream, or floatation process within froth treatment bitumen extraction process. The treated gas stream and flue gas stream is injected to hydrocarbon reservoir as non-condensable gas and desorbing hydrogen sulfide and/or carbon dioxide from graphene-based material using steam and regenerating graphene-based material for subsequent adsorption cycle.

PN DE102017005678-A1; WO2018228717-A1
TI Production of gas product comprising carbon monoxide involves electrolyzing carbon dioxide to obtain raw gas comprising carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, and condensing formed mixture to obtain gaseous fraction enriched in carbon monoxide.
AU HENTSCHEL B
   PESCHEL A
AE LINDE AG (LINM-C)
GA 2018A1485W
AB    NOVELTY - Production (100) of gas product (H) comprising carbon monoxide involves electrolyzing (10) carbon dioxide to obtain a raw gas (A) comprising carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, condensing a feed mixture (C) formed using portion(s) of the raw gas to obtain a liquid fraction (E) enriched in carbon dioxide and depleted in carbon monoxide, and a gaseous fraction (G) enriched in carbon monoxide and depleted in carbon dioxide, and condensing the gaseous fraction (G) to obtain gas product (H) comprising carbon monoxide.
   USE - Production of gas product comprising carbon monoxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple, easy and economical production of gas product comprising carbon monoxide with improved yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for production plant for gas product (H) comprising carbon monoxide, which comprises an electrolysis unit, a unit adapted to form the feed mixture (C), a unit adapted to condense the feed mixture (C), and a unit adapted to condense the gaseous fraction (G).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the production plant for gas product comprising carbon monoxide.
   Electrolysis process (10)
   Heat exchanger (40)
   Production of gas product comprising carbon monoxide (100)
   Raw gas (A)
   Feed mixture (C)
   Liquid fraction (E)
   Gaseous fraction (G)
   Gas product (H)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The raw gas comprises 0-50% hydrogen, 10-70% carbon monoxide, and 30-90% carbon dioxide. The gaseous fraction (G) comprises 0-70% hydrogen, 30-90% carbon monoxide, and 10-40% carbon dioxide. Preferred Process: The gas product (H) is formed by purifying the gaseous fraction (G). The purification process involves membrane separation, adsorption, absorption, distillation, and/or carbon dioxide freezing process. The portion(s) of carbon dioxide contained in the liquid fraction (E) and/or portion(s) of carbon dioxide remaining in the gaseous fraction (G) is introduced into the electrolysis and/or to the condensation. The condensation of the feed mixture (C) is performed at a condensation pressure level of 10-80 bar and at a condensation temperature level of -70 degrees C to -20 degrees C, using a heat exchanger (40), preferably plate heat exchanger comprising stainless steel or aluminum and/or tube-in-tube heat exchanger. The electrolysis is performed at the condensing pressure level or at a pressure level below the condensation pressure level. The electrolysis is performed in the form of high-temperature electrolysis using solid oxide electrolysis cells and/or low-temperature co-electrolysis using a liquid electrolyte.

AU YATES S

AE HONEYWELL INT INC (HONE-C)

GA 2018869243
AB    NOVELTY - Environmental control system (S1) (100), comprises: a carbon dioxide source (101); a compressor (102) downstream of the carbon dioxide source; a Sabatier reactor (103) downstream of the compressor, where the Sabatier reactor produces methane and water; a water separator downstream of the Sabatier reactor, where the water separator produces methane and water; a pyrolysis assembly downstream of the water separator and upstream of the compressor, where the pyrolysis assembly includes a pre-form that adheres carbon and has a surface area/volume ratio of at least 20 cm-1, and the pyrolysis assembly produces hydrogen; and an oxygen generating assembly (OGA) (105) downstream of the water separator and upstream of the compressor, where the OGA produces hydrogen and oxygen.
   USE - The system is useful in a gravity free environment or a deep space vehicle (all claimed) for recovering oxygen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) environmental control system (S2) comprising the carbon dioxide source, the compressor downstream of the carbon dioxide source, the Sabatier reactor downstream of the compressor, where the Sabatier reactor reacts carbon dioxide with hydrogen, the water separator downstream of the Sabatier reactor, where the water separator separates hydrocarbons from water and the hydrocarbons include methane and ethane, the pyrolysis assembly downstream of the water separator and upstream of the compressor, where the pyrolysis assembly includes a pre-form that adheres carbon and the pyrolysis assembly pyrolyzes methane, and OGA downstream of the water separator and upstream of the compressor, where the OGA electrolyzes water; and
   (2) environmental control system (S3), comprising the carbon dioxide source, the compressor downstream of the carbon dioxide source, the Sabatier reactor downstream of the compressor, where the Sabatier reactor reacts carbon dioxide with hydrogen to produce methane and water, the water separator downstream of the Sabatier reactor, where the water separator separates hydrocarbons from water and the hydrocarbons include methane, the pyrolysis assembly downstream of the water separator and upstream of the compressor, where the pyrolysis assembly pyrolyzes methane to produce carbon and hydrogen and the pyrolysis assembly includes a pre-form that adheres carbon, and OGA downstream of the water separator and upstream of the compressor, where the OGA electrolyzes water to hydrogen and oxygen.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of environmental control system.
   Environmental control system (100)
   Carbon dioxide source (101)
   Compressor (102)
   Sabatier reactor (103)
   Oxygen generating assembly (105)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: In the system (S1): the compressor receives hydrogen from the pyrolysis assembly and the OGA; the Sabatier reactor receives carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide source and receives hydrogen from the OGA; the pyrolysis assembly receives methane from the water separator; and the carbon dioxide source is a carbon dioxide removal assembly. In the system (S2): the pre-form comprises carbon fibers; the carbon fibers are tightly configured into criss-crossed layers; the carbon fibers are loosely and randomly arranged; the pre-form includes alternating tight regions and loose regions of carbon fibers; the Sabatier reactor produces methane and water; and the pyrolysis assembly produces carbon, hydrogen, acetylene and benzene. In the system (S3): the Sabatier reactor operates at 400 degrees C and 1 atm; the pyrolysis assembly operates at 1100 degrees C and 20-100 torr; the pre-form is removable from the pyrolysis assembly once the pre-form is loaded with carbon; and oxygen produced by the OGA is routed to an environment for occupants.

PN CN112707427-A
TI Method for processing calcium carbonate, involves selecting and weighing limestone, filtering impurity, preparing calcium carbonate by carbon dioxide, collecting and processing by-products, and collecting, drying and storing product.
AU LUO H
AE LUO H (LUOH-Individual)
GA 202151558K
AB    NOVELTY - A method for processing calcium carbonate, involves S1, selecting and weighing limestone, S2, filtering the impurity by limestone, S3, preparing calcium carbonate by carbon dioxide, S4, collecting and processing by-products, and S5, collecting, drying and storing the product.
   USE - Method for processing calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The calcium carbonate processing method ensures that the output of by-products in the processing process is reduced, and the output of calcium carbonate is increased through the screening treatment of limestone, thus the by-products are collected, processed and reduced to reduce pollution emissions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step S1 includes the selection of limestone, and limestone with a relatively flat surface and a whiter color is selected for the preparation of calcium carbonate. In the step S2, the selected limestone is preliminarily pulverized, and preliminarily filtered through a filter screen, and a relatively small and flat limestone is selected. In the step S3, the limestone obtained by the preliminary screening is added into the reaction kettle, while carbon dioxide is introduced to carry out the reaction, and the reaction temperature is controlled to 60 degrees C during the reaction. The collection of the by-products in the step S4 is carried out by cleaning the residue at the bottom of the reaction kettle, and at the same time, the collected by-products are subjected to a reduction reaction treatment to reduce usable substances.

AU HONG H

   TAO J
   HU Z
   ZHANG Y
   ZUO J
   WANG B
   DAI R
   WANG J
AE ASYMCHEM LIFE SCI & TECHNOLOGY TIANJIN (ASYM-Non-standard)
   ASYMCHEM LIFE SCI & TECHNOLOGY TIANJIN C (ASYM-Non-standard)
   ASYMCHEM LIFE SCI TIANJIN CO LTD (ASYM-Non-standard)
GA 202083028X
AB    NOVELTY - Continuously synthesizing phospholipid-4-naphthoic acid comprises dispersing liquid of 4-naphthoic acid and carbon dioxide to form a gas-liquid mixture with a small increment of 50nm to 5 mm as the reaction material, continuously inputing reaction raw materials into a fixed bed reactor for catalytic conversion reaction, and continuously discharging piperidine-4-carboxylic acid toluene.
   USE - The phospholipid-4-naphthoic acid is useful for in synthesis of sedative drugs or antiarrhythmic drugs (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: saves cost; and is safe.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gas-liquid ratio of the dispersion process is 1:1-100. The particle size of the small droplets in the reaction raw material is 100-5000 mu m. In the catalytic hydrogenation reaction, the fixed bed reactor is loaded with a catalyst, and the catalyst is ruthenium/carbon, palladium/carbon, rhodium/carbon and ruthenium/aluminum oxide. The grain diameter of the catalyst is 0.1-5 mm. The organic solvent is ethanol, water, methanol and dichloromethane. Preferred Method: In the catalytic hydrogenation reaction, the reaction temperature is 80-160 degrees C, the reaction pressure is 4.0-5.0 MPa, the volumetric space velocity is 0.2-1.0/hour, the bed pressure drop of the fixed bed reactor is less than 0.2 MPa/m. The dispersion method is atomization method, jet method, venturi method or collisional convection method, the fixed bed reactor is axially adiabatic fixed reaction bed, radially adiabatic fixed reaction bed or a tube fixed bed. The method further comprises performing dispersion on the ethyl pyridine-4-carboxylate, the hydrogen gas and the organic solvent.

   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has simple preparation method, convenient operation, high catalyzing efficiency, low cost and can be recycled.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparing the catalyst which involves calcining indium metal-organic framework material in oxygen-containing atmosphere at 300-400 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The indium source and organic ligand carry out hydro-thermal reaction in which indium source is dissolved in organic solvent, preferably acetic acid and organic ligand is 4,4'-bipyridyl and/or 2,2'-biphenyl dicarboxylic acid in molar ratio of 1-3:1-5. Amount of organic ligand is 1-2 times theoretical amount. Preferred Parameters: The temperature of hydro-thermal reaction is 120-180 degrees C and time of hydro-thermal reaction is 10-30 hours. Calcining time is 2-4 hours. Pressure of mixed gas is 50-90 bar. Amine methylation reaction temperature is 150-210 degrees C. Preferred Components: The oxygen-containing atmosphere is oxygen, air, or mixed gas of air and gas. Amount of amine is 0.5-5 mol.%. Volume ratio of carbon dioxide to hydrogen is 1:2-4.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amine is aliphatic amine, aliphatic sec.-amine, aromatic amine, preferably sec.-amine of formula R1-CH(R2)-NH-R3. Reaction system is added with reaction agent chosen from N-((trifluoromethyl)sulfonyl)-1,1,1-trifluoromethane sulfonamide in ratio with amine of 1:5-15.R1-R3=H, alkyl, cycloalkyl, phenyl or heteroaryl in which greater than or equal to 2 groups are cyclized into cyclic group and has substituent containing alkyl or halo.

   USE - A method for preparing nickel-neodymium co-doped ordered mesoporous alumina material for methane-carbon dioxide reforming reaction (claimed).

Preferred Components: The molar ratio of triblock copolymer P123 and aluminum isopropoxide is 0.014-0.017:1. The volume-to-mass ratio of concentrated nitric acid to triblock copolymer P123 is 1.6-1.8 mL/g. The volume ratio of anhydrous ethanol to concentrated nitric acid is 12-15:1. The molar ratio of nickel nitrate hexahydrate and aluminum isopropoxide is 0.02-0.06:1. The molar ratio of nickel nitrate hexahydrate and neodymium nitrate hexahydrate is 1-8.5:1.

AU JO H Y
   KIM J S
AE UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
GA 2019277431
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering metal ions from red mud, comprises (a) irradiating red mud with microwaves, (b) dispersing red mud irradiated with microwaves into water, (c) separating the extract and the precipitate by filtering the dispersion, and (d) contacting the extract obtained in the step (c) with carbon dioxide to recover the carbonate of the metal ion, and recovering the iron by magnetic separation of precipitate obtained in the step (c).
   USE - The method is useful for recovering metal ions from red mud.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (d) is carried out by adjusting the pH of the extract to greater than or equal to 8 and contacting with carbon dioxide. Preferred Components: The red mud in the step (a) is present in slurry form. The red mud of the step (a) is present in the form of mixed powder of red mud dry powder and ammonium salt. The ammonium salt includes ammonium chloride, ammonium sulfate, ammonium nitrate, ammonium carbonate and/or ammonium acetate. The ammonium salt is mixed in amount of 10-100 pts. wt. based on 100 pts. wt. of red mud dry powder.

   PAN J

GA 202210564W
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing carbomer polymer involves mixing                cross-linking agent allyl sucrose ether, acrylic                acid monomer, initiator and solvent uniformly, and                obtaining carbomer polymer by cross-linking                polymerization under the condition of supercritical                carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for synthesizing carbomer polymer used                for preparing vaccine (claimed) and animal vaccine                adjuvant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method synthesizes carbomer polymer, which                has simple and easy method, complete system                reaction, stable reaction, high purity, uniform                polymerization degree distribution, good safety and                good adjuvant performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The initiator is a                  peroxide type initiator, an azo type initiator or                  a redox type initiator. The peroxide type                  initiator is an organic peroxide or inorganic                  peroxide, preferably ammonium persulfate. The                  organic peroxide is selected from benzoyl                  peroxide, lauroyl peroxide, tert-butyl                  hydroperoxide, methyl ethyl ketone peroxide,                  tert-butyl oxybenzoate, cyclohexanone peroxide                  sulfate and diisopropyl peroxydicarbonate, or its                  combination. The inorganic peroxide is selected                  from hydrogen peroxide, potassium persulfate,                  sodium persulfate and ammonium persulfate, or its                  combination. The azo type initiator is selected                  from azobisisobutyronitrile,                  azobisisoheptanenitrile, dimethyl                  azobisisobutyrate, and azobisisobutylamidine                  hydrochloride, or its combination, preferably                  azobisisobutyronitrile or azobisisobutylamidine                  hydrochloride. The oxidant in redox type                  initiator is selected from hydrogen peroxide,                  hydroperoxide, persulfate, dialkyl peroxide and                  diacyl peroxide, or its combination, and reducing                  agent is selected from ferrous sulfate, sodium                  sulfite, amine, aromatic amine, oxalic acid,                  sugar, thiol and naphthenate, and its                  combination. The solvent is selected from                  benzene, cyclohexane, ethyl acetate, deionized                  water and its combination, preferably ethyl                  acetate, deionized water or cyclohexane. The                  redox system initiator is selected from                  tert-butyl hydroperoxide/sodium metabisulfite,                  benzoyl peroxide/sucrose, tert-butyl                  hydroperoxide/sodium metabisulfite, benzoyl                  peroxide/N,N-dimethyl sulfite aniline, ammonium                  persulfate/sodium bisulfite, potassium                  persulfate/sodium bisulfite, hydrogen                  peroxide/tartaric acid, ammonium                  persulfate/ferrous sulfate, hydrogen                  peroxide/ferrous chloride,                  persulfate/hypophosphite, benzoyl                  peroxide/N,N-diethylaniline, benzoyl                  peroxide/ferrous pyrophosphate,                  persulfate/mercaptan, cumene                  hydroperoxide/ferrous chloride or potassium                  persulfate/ferrous chloride, preferably                  N,N-diethylhydroxylamine/sodium persulfate,                  hydroxydicumyl peroxide/tetraethylenepentamine,                  or potassium persulfate/potassium sulfite/                  thiourea dioxide. The initiator is selected from                  azodiisobutylamidine hydrochloride, ammonium                  persulfate, N,N-diethylhydroxylamine/sodium                  persulfate, ammonium                  persulfate/N,N-diethylhydroxylamine, or                  combinations of potassium persulfate/potassium                  sulfite/thiourea dioxide, iron persulfate/sodium                  bisulfite, hydroxydicumyl peroxide/tetraethylene                  pentaamine, preferably azodiisobutylamidine                  hydrochloride, ammonium persulfate,                  N,N-diethylhydroxylamine/sodium persulfate,                  potassium persulfate/potassium sulfite/thiourea                  dioxide. Preferred Conditions: The molar                  percentage of acrylic acid monomer in total                  amount of the crosslinking agent allyl sucrose                  ether and acrylic acid monomer is 40-100% and it                  is not 100%. The amount of initiator is 0.5-15                  wt.% of acrylic monomer. The amount of solvent is                  100-700 wt.% of acrylic monomer. The                  cross-linking polymerization reaction is                  performed at a pressure of 7.2-30MPa and                  temperature of 31.06-60degreesC for 1-24                  hours.

TI Processing high-quality fruit juice involves filling nitrogen, carbon dioxide, and other inert gas to discharge air in processing environment and crushing, juicing, filtering, storing, and sterilizing.
AU KE G
AE KE G (KEGG-Individual)
GA 2021306142
AB    NOVELTY - Processing high-quality fruit juice involves utilizing nitrogen, carbon dioxide, and other inert gas to discharge air in a processing environment, crushing, juicing, filtering, storing, and sterilizing. The juice is not contacted with air in the processing process to obtain the juice of high quality.
   USE - Method for processing high-quality fruit juice.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare the high-quality fruit juice rich in vitamin C, superoxide dismutase (SOD), and another easy oxidation component of fruit. The nutrient content can be better retained and the quality is greatly improved.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Process: The one way of realizing a non-air environment is: Cleaning, crushing, juicing, filtering, and sterilizing are performed. The filling mechanical device is installed separately or collectively in a relatively sealed environment, in which inert gasses such as nitrogen and carbon dioxide are continuously injected into each environment. The air is exhausted, and the screened fruits (prickly pear, sea buckthorn, etc.) are placed in a non-air environment for cleaning, crushing, juicing, filtering, sterilizing, filling, and so on, according to the specific process to obtain the juice of high quality. Another way to achieve a non-air environment is to continuously introduce inert gasses such as nitrogen and carbon dioxide through a gas pipeline to crush, inside the machinery such as juice extraction, filtration, storage, sterilization, and filling (the parts that are prone to air leakage can be appropriately improved and sealed as much as possible), try to exhaust the air in the processing environment, and make the juice as far as possible without contact with air Processing (processing equipment can be more or less, depending on the specific process), Mto obtain the juice of high quality.

   ADVANTAGE - The basic electrocatalyst has high current                density and high ethylene selectivity. The method                simple.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                manufacturing basic electrocatalyst electrode,                comprising (a) applying a mixed solution containing                a basic compound and copper particles onto a                substrate, and (b) forming a catalyst layer by                heat-treating the applied substrate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The basic compounds                  are potassium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide,                  lithium hydroxide, rubidium hydroxide, caesium                  hydroxide, francium hydroxide, beryllium                  hydroxide, calcium hydroxide, magnesium                  hydroxide, strontium hydroxide, barium hydroxide                  and/or radium hydroxide. The element                  coated or doped on the surface of the catalyst                  particle has a hydroxide form. The basic                  electrocatalyst has a nanoporous structure. The                  mixed solution further comprises a halogen                  material. The halogen material includes potassium                  iodide, potassium chloride, sodium chloride,                  sodium iodide, sodium bromide and/or potassium                  bromide. The copper particles include copper,                  copper(I) oxide, copper(II) oxide and/or                  copper(II) hydroxide. The copper particles have                  average particle diameter of 1 nm to 100 mu m.                  The substrate is an anion exchange membrane.                  Preferred Method: The method further comprises                  forming a gas diffusion layer on the catalyst                  layer.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The copper particles                  are supported on a carbon-based support. The                  carbon-based support includes carbon black,                  carbon nanotubes, graphene, carbon nanofibers                  and/or graphitized carbon black.

AB    NOVELTY - Method for preventing or mitigating corrosion on a metallic surface exposed to a supercritical fluid, involves adding a corrosion inhibitor composition to a supercritical fluid comprising a supercritical carbon dioxide, and contacting the supercritical fluid with a metallic surface, where the corrosion inhibitor composition comprises a corrosion inhibitor that has a solubility of greater than 5000 ppm in the supercritical fluid at 48.9 degrees C and 2200 psi.
   USE - The method is used for preventing or mitigating corrosion on a metallic surface exposed to a supercritical fluid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The supercritical fluid is contacted with the metallic surface during a carbon dioxide capturing and sequestering operation. The corrosion inhibitor composition is added to a pipeline originating from a carbon dioxide capture facility, an injection well tubular, or their combinations. The corrosion inhibitor composition is added to the supercritical fluid in an amount of 500-5000 ppm based on the total volume of the supercritical fluid. Preferred Components: The supercritical fluid further comprises water. The supercritical fluid further comprises a component of natural gas, nitrogen, nitrogen dioxide, sulfur dioxide, oxygen, dimethyl ether, or their combinations. The corrosion inhibitor migrates to water condensed on the metallic surface. The corrosion inhibitor comprises 2-mercaptoethanol, thioglycolic acid, or their combinations. The corrosion inhibitor composition further comprises a solvent, which is water, methanol, or their combinations. The metallic surface comprises iron, nickel, copper, aluminum, carbide, an alloy, or their combinations. The metallic surface comprises carbon steel. The corrosion inhibitor composition comprises 10-95 vol.% corrosion inhibitor. The supercritical carbon dioxide is present in an amount of 90 vol.% or more in the supercritical fluid, based on the total volume of the supercritical fluid. Preferred Properties: The corrosion inhibitor has a solubility of 10000-100000 ppm or less in the supercritical fluid at 48.9 degrees C and 2200 psi. The metallic surface has a corrosion rate of 0.15 mm/year or less.

AU SHEPELEV I I

AE UNIV KRASY STATE AGRARIAN (UYKY-Non-standard)
GA 202051606A
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to methods of cleaning exhaust gases of rotary furnaces of alumina production. For purification of gases it is proposed to use multi-stage cleaning in dust chamber, cyclones, electric filters and further "wet" cleaning from fine dust in scrubber-electrostatic precipitators. Off-gases with carbon dioxide content of not less than 18.5%, cleaned from dust in scrubber-electrostatic precipitators, are then directed into carbonizers of hydrochemical production of aluminum hydroxide.
   USE - Technological processes.
   ADVANTAGE - Invention provides purification of atmospheric air from dust and carbon dioxide in emissions from rotary sintering furnaces, and their subsequent use for extraction of aluminum hydroxide from aluminate solutions of alumina production.

PT P
PN CN110204433-A
TI Synthesizing 3,4-difluoro-2-methylbenzoic acid involves reacting 2,3-difluorotoluene and anhydrous aluminum trichloride with carbon dioxide gas at room temperature, and after reaction is post-treated, and carrying out recrystallization.
AU ZHENG Z
   TIAN S
   XU G
AE UNIV HUNAN NORMAL (UYLH-C)
GA 2019785206
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing 3,4-difluoro-2-methylbenzoic acid involves reacting 2,3-difluorotoluene and anhydrous aluminum trichloride with carbon dioxide gas at room temperature, and after the reaction is post-treated, and carrying out recrystallization to obtain 3,4-difluoro-2-methylbenzoic acid.
   USE - Method for synthesizing 3,4-difluoro-2-methylbenzoic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to synthesize 3,4-difluoro-2-methylbenzoic acid is simple, economical and practical, small energy consumption, easy to operate, produces high yield, cost-efective, and great application prospect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The reaction temperature is 0-50 degrees C, and the optimum reaction temperature is 25-30 degrees C. The reaction liquid is slowly poured into ice water and continuously stirred, the precipitated white solid is suction filtered, and the white solid is dispersed in petroleum ether, stirred, and suction filtered. The filtrate is ready for use in the recovery of 2,3-difluorotoluene. The above filter cake is suspended in methanol, concentrated hydrochloric acid is added, stirred at 45 degrees C, and suction filtered. The methanol was distilled off under reduced pressure, water is added slowly, stirred for 20 minutes and then filtered, and then evaporated. The recrystallization solvent is a mixture of ethanol and water, where optimal volume ratio of ethanol and water is 1:4. The petroleum ether in the petroleum ether filtrate is distilled off under normal pressure, and 2,3-difluorotoluene is distilled off under reduced pressure to obtain 2,3-difluorotoluene.
DC E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.)

PN CN109576051-A
TI Extracting alumina waste reside from coix seed oil involves adding alumina slag to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, carrying out extraction at preset pressure and temperature, separating at preset pressure and temperature.
AU ZHOU F
   WANG X
   DONG J
AE JIANGXI YAOMING YANGHAI MEDICAL TECHNOLO (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201933465C
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting alumina waste reside from Coix seed oil involves adding 4-6 kg of alumina slag to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, carrying out extraction at a extraction pressure of 10-35 MPa at 30-60 degrees C, separating at a separation pressure of 5-10 MPa at30-60 degrees C, controlling the flow rate at 20-50 L/hour, and carrying out the extraction process for 0.5-3 hour/kettle.
   USE - Extracting alumina waste reside from Coix seed oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The centrifugation process involves subjecting the refined Coix seed oil with alumina into the centrifuge drum at a speed of 1150-1600 rpm and frequency of 40-60 Hz for 8-12 minutes, and removing the alumina filter residue containing the seed oil from the filter bag after centrifugation. The process further involves subjecting the 25% oil containing 5 kg alumina filter residue into the extraction device, placing neutral paper filter on the extraction filter plate to adjust the carbon dioxide pump frequency of the pressure pump, maintaining controlled pressure of 20 MPa, preferably 25 MPa for 20 L/hour, preferably 30 L/hour at 50 degrees C, separating filtrate at a column pressure of 5 MPa and at a column temperature of 43 degrees C, discharging the oil for every 15 minutes at a rate of 50-80 g/hour and after 120 minutes no oil was produced and the weight of Coix seed oil is 1.17 kg, preferably 1.20 kg.

PT P
PN CN109251193-A
TI Preparation of propylene carbonate by reacting propylene and oxidant in the presence of catalyst, and reacting resulting propylene oxide with carbon dioxide in the presence of another catalyst.
AU SHI C
   LIN M
   ZHU B
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC RES INST PETROLEUM PROCESSING (SNPC-C)
GA 201910439S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of propylene carbonate comprises reacting propylene and oxidant in the presence of first catalyst to obtain first reaction product comprising propylene oxide; and reacting propylene oxide in first reactant with carbon dioxide in the presence of second catalyst to obtain second reaction production comprising propylene carbonate. The first catalyst is titanium-silicon (TiSi) molecular sieve catalyst. The second catalyst is molecular sieve catalyst containing structure directing agent.
   USE - Method for preparing propylene carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is energy-saving and environment-friendly, and has high catalyst activity, mild reaction conditions and high selectivity of propylene carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The TiSi molecular sieve is MFI-type TiSi molecular sieve, MEL-type TiSi molecular sieve, BEA-type TiSi molecular sieve, MWW-type TiSi molecular sieve, MOR-type TiSi molecular sieve, TUN-type TiSi molecular sieve, and/or hexagonal TiSi molecular sieve, preferably TiSi molecular sieve TS-1 and/or TiSi molecular sieve HTS having hollow structure. The structure directing agent-containing molecular sieve catalyst is molecular sieve catalyst containing structure directing agent remaining in synthesis process. The molecular sieve is beta -molecular sieve, ZSM-5 molecular sieve, SAPO-34 molecular sieve, TiSi molecular sieve TS-1, silicalite-1 molecular sieve, MCM-22 molecular sieve, SBA-15 molecular sieve, aluminum phosphate (AlPO4)-11 molecular sieve, SAPO-102 molecular sieve, UZM-5 molecular sieve and/or TiSi molecular sieve HTS having hollow structure. The second catalyst comprises partially activated TiSi molecular sieve catalyst obtained by mixing TiSi molecular sieve with acid and optionally peroxide aqueous solution at 0-90 degrees C for 0.1-48 hours. The molar ratio of acid, peroxide, water and TiSi molecular sieve is 0.02-15:0-10:15-100:1. The weight ratio of activated TiSi molecular sieve catalyst to molecular sieve catalyst containing structure directing agent is 1:0.1-10, preferably 1:1-5. The acid is hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, phosphoric acid, nitric acid, perchloric acid, and/or 1-5C carboxylic acid. The peroxide is hydrogen peroxide, tert-butyl hydroperoxide, cumene hydroperoxide, and/or cyclohexyl hydrogen peroxide. The molar ratio of nitric acid, peroxide, water and titanium silicon molecular sieve is 0.1-10:0.01-5:20-80:1. After activation treatment, the absorption peak area of activated TiSi molecular sieve in 230-310 nm UV-visible spectrum is reduced by greater than or equal to 2%, preferably 3-6% and the pore volume of activated TiSi molecular sieve is reduced by more than 1%, preferably reduced by 2-5%, measured using static nitrogen adsorption. The TiSi molecular sieve is TiSi molecular sieve TS-1 having surface silicon-titanium that is not lower than bulk silicon-titanium ratio. The surface silicon-titanium ratio is determined by X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy, and the bulk silicon-titanium ratio is determined by X-ray fluorescence spectrometry. The ratio surface silicon-titanium ratio to bulk silicon-titanium ratio is greater than or equal to 1.2, preferably 1.5-4.5. The TiSi molecular sieve TS-1 is prepared by dispersing inorganic silicon source in aqueous solution containing titanium source and alkaline source template agent, and optionally replenishing water to obtain a dispersion; standing at 15-60 degrees C for 6-24 hours; and crystallizing at 80-150 degrees C for 6-72 hours, preferably at 130-140 degrees C for 6-8 hours, cooling to 70 degrees C for greater than or equal to 0.5 hour, preferably 1-5 hours, and heating to 120-200 degrees C, preferably 160-170 degrees C, and recrystallizing for 6-96 hours, preferably 12-20 hours. The molar ratio of silicon source, titanium source, alkaline source template agent and water in the dispersion is 100:0.5-8:5-30:100-2000. The inorganic silicon source is silicon dioxide. The alkaline source template agent contains nitrogen or is based on hydroxide when it contains no nitrogen. The titanium source is inorganic titanium salt and/or organic titanate. The inorganic titanium salt is titanium tetrachloride, titanium(IV) sulfate, and/or titanium oxychloride. The inorganic silicon source is silica gel and/or silica sol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The first reaction is performed at 20-80 degrees C and 0.1-5 MPa for 0.1-12 hours, preferably at 25-60 degrees C and 0.2-3.5 MPa for 0.2-6 hours, in the presence of solvent. The second reaction performed at 50-200 degrees C and 0.5-10 MPa for 0.2-30 hours, preferably at 70-160 degrees C and 0.8-6 MPa for 0.5-10 hours, in the presence of solvent or in the absence of water. Preferred Components: The molar ratio of propylene and oxidant is 1:0.1-5, preferably 1:0.2-1. The weight ratio of propylene to first catalyst is 0.1-100:1, preferably 0.2-10:1. The oxidant is hydrogen peroxide and/or organic peroxide. The organic peroxide is tert-butyl hydroperoxide, cyclohexyl hydroperoxide, cumene hydroperoxide, phenylethane peroxide, peroxyacetic acid, and/or peroxypropionic acid. The molar ratio of propylene and solvent is 1:1-20, preferably 1:2-10. The solvent used in the first reaction is methanol, ethanol, acetone, and/or water. The molar ratio of propylene oxide to carbon dioxide is 1:1-100, preferably 1:2-50. The weight ratio of propylene oxide to second catalyst is 1-100:1, preferably 2-50:1. The molar ratio of propylene oxide to solvent is 1:0.1-50, preferably 1:0.5-20. The solvent used in the second reaction is methylene dichloride, acetone, and/or dimethylsulfoxide. The alkaline source template agent is quat. ammonium hydroxide, aliphatic amine, and/or aliphatic alcohol amine, preferably tetrapropylammonium hydroxide. The organic titanate is of formula R4TiO4.R=2-4C alkyl.

PN CN108704660-A; CN108704660-B
TI Preparation of nitrogen vacancy-modified oxygen-rich titania composite material as photocatalyst in carbon dioxide photocatalytic reduction, involves taking tetrabutyl titanate, precipitating with cold water, and repeatedly washing.
AU SHE H
   ZHOU H
   WANG L
   LI L
AE UNIV NORTHWEST NORMAL (UNWN-C)
   UNIV NORTHWEST NORMAL (UNWN-C)
GA 2018861768
AB    NOVELTY - A nitrogen vacancy-modified oxygen-rich titania composite material is prepared by taking tetrabutyl titanate, precipitating with 0-5 degrees C cold water, repeatedly washing with deionized water, adding deionized water and hydrogen peroxide, mixing at 0-5 degrees C for 0.5-1 hours, adding graphitic carbon nitride, and heating at 40-50 degrees C for 3-4 hours.
   USE - Method for preparing nitrogen vacancy-modified oxygen-rich titania composite material as photocatalyst in carbon dioxide photocatalytic reduction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product has improved catalytic activity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The nitrogen vacancy-modified oxygen-rich titania composite material is prepared by taking tetrabutyl titanate, precipitating with 0-5 degrees C cold water, repeatedly washing with deionized water, adding 1:0.5-1:1 volume ratio of deionized water and 30 wt.% hydrogen peroxide, mixing at 0-5 degrees C for 0.5-1 hours, adding graphitic carbon nitride according to mass ratio of 1:10-1:100, and heating at 40-50 degrees C for 3-4 hours. Preferred Components: The mol ratio of tetrabutyl titanate to hydrogen peroxide is 1:5-1:30.

PN CN108408724-A
TI Preparing active carbon comprises pre-processing the raw materials, placing carbon dioxide atmosphere, adding aluminum hydroxide as activator, activating, cooling, placing in inorganic strong acid.
AU BAO Z
AE JURONG SHENGDA ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (JURO-Non-standard)
GA 201865941R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing active carbon comprises (i) pre-processing the raw materials, (ii) placing the pre-processed raw material into 70-90% carbon dioxide atmosphere, adding aluminum hydroxide as activator, and activating at 400-600 degrees C for 1-2 hours, and (iii) cooling the activated product to 290 degrees C, placing in inorganic strong acid for 1-2 hours.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing active carbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: materials used in the method can be recycled; is economical and suitable for large-scale industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the activation process described in the step (ii), potassium sulfate is added to react with carbon in the raw material to provide a carbon dioxide atmosphere to form potassium sulfide. The potassium sulfide reacts with the inorganic strong acid in the step (iii) to form hydrogen sulfide as the warming fuel in the step (ii).

AB    NOVELTY - Continuous production process of basic copper                carbonate based on ammonia circulation system,                comprises (1) granulating or pulverizing the copper                raw material to make into a powder state, (2)                performing ammonia dipping complexation reaction to                the copper powder, (3) filtering the leachate to                obtain a filtrate, adding liquid caustic soda or no                liquid caustic soda into the filtrate to form a                copper-ammonia complex solution, (4) the                copper-ammonia complex solution first exchanges                heat with the tower kettle liquid, and then enters                the stripping and deamination tower to perform the                copper-ammonia decomplexation and complexation                reaction, and decomposing into ammonia and carbon                dioxide, re-forming the ammonia leaching solution                required for the ammonia leaching complexation                reaction, and (5) processing the tower still liquid                after stripping and delamination, and (6) treating                the filtrate after the treatment of the tower                kettle liquid.
   USE - The method is useful for continuous production                of basic copper carbonate based on ammonia                circulating system and copper rust, and copper                green.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is continuous stable production,                low energy consumption, little waste water waste                gas characteristics, so the technique has important                economic benefit and social benefit, improves the                efficiency, reduces the energy consumption and                reduces the waste gas amount.

   FORREST B A
   ALLAM R J
AE 8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
GA 2022C1477R
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves passing gas stream (61)                i.e. turbine exhaust stream at first pressure and                first temperature through a recuperative heat                exchanger. The gas stream is cooled to second                temperature less than first temperature. The gas                stream is separated into first fraction and second                fraction. The gas stream fraction is compressed to                second pressure greater than the first pressure. PN CN216741876-U
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide Brayton power generation circulation compressor machine device for use in supercritical CO2 Brayton cycle power generation system, has compressor and turbine whose shafts are rigidly connected with clutch device.
AU YANG X
   WEI Z
   ZHANG S
   ZHAO L
   CHEN J
AE SHANGHAI CHAOLIN POWER TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022820561
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model model claims a supercritical CO2 Brayton power generation cycle compressor a compressor CO2 Brayton power generation circulation system, compressor is provided with a motor, further comprising a supercritical CO2 Brayton power generation cycle system of the turbine, wherein it further comprises a clutch device, the main shaft of the compressor and the main shaft of the turbine are rigidly connected with the clutch device. The utility model device is set in the middle of the compressor and the turbine, the clutch device can control whether the transmission shaft work between the turbine and the compressor when the system is started, the clutch device is disconnected, the turbine and the shaft of the compressor are not directly connected. At this time, the motor drives the compressor to do work. When the system operates normally or the turbine can be normally used, the clutch device is closed, the turbine is directly connected with the shaft of the compressor, the power of the output of the turbine is directly used for driving the compressor Thus, it not only solves the system starting problem, but also reduces the energy loss.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the main machine device of supercritical CO Brayton power generation circulation compressor to claim.

TI Purification of air of indoor environment involves illuminating aqueous medium comprising algal culture with light source, conveying air flow into medium, and maintaining culture in state of constant growth.
AU GENTILINI E
   GUZZONATO F
   DAL NEGRO A
   GOBBER C
AE ALOS SRL (ALOS-Non-standard)
GA 202279144S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing an aqueous medium comprising an algal culture and illuminating aqueous medium with a light source. An air flow is conveyed into aqueous medium, thus generates a air bubbles having an average diameter of less than 20 mm. An algal culture in a state of constant growth is maintained by replacing, with a periodic frequency, between 1 time every two weeks and 1 time every 8 weeks, an amount between 60 and 80 percentage, of the volume of aqueous medium comprising algal culture with a corresponding volume of fresh aqueous medium and maintaining the aqueous medium comprising algal culture at an average temperature between 15 and 27 degree C.
   USE - Method for purifying air of indoor environment, e.g. room of apartment or house, office, or gym.
   ADVANTAGE - The method allows to purify the air of an indoor and/or domestic environment by means of the abatement/solubilization of pollutants in the aqueous medium and photo-conversion of carbon dioxide into oxygen thanks to the photosynthesis process of the algal culture.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of the device for air purification of indoor and/or domestic environments.
   Lighting device (1)
   Light source (2)
   Diffuser case (3)
   Inlet opening (4)
   Outlet opening (5)

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
