PN IN202141038865-A
TI Instant torque power generation (ITPG) method for generating green, clean and renewable energy, involves rotating main shaft of alternator, where torque needed to rotate main shaft is developed using weights.
AU MAHADEVAN A S
AE MAHADEVAN A S (MAHA-Individual)
GA 202234360J
AB    NOVELTY - The ITPG method involves rotating the main shaft of an alternator i.e. permanent magnet generator (PMG) alternator. The torque needed to rotate the main shaft is developed of heavy weights. The heavy weights are attached to the main shaft to develop torque due to gravitational force. The assembly of the alternator with weights attached to the main shaft is inserted in a torque vessel turbine at 90 degrees downwards. The torque vessel turbine is the main portion and also acts as the turbine for ITPG. The torque vessel turbine is loaded with the alternator weight assembly to float due to the buoyant force in a water tank.
   USE - ITPG method for generating green, clean and renewable energy.
   ADVANTAGE - The process of ITPG does not generate carbon dioxide or any waste product or cause any health hazard. ITPG can be situated at any nearby place, thus lowering down the costs meted out for the installation of long power transmission lines and avoiding power loss as well. Unlimited electricity can be generated without the usage of any form of fuel. The process is renewable and sustainable, so there is no possibility of any exhaustion. The ITPG is capable of producing electricity as much as we require in large scale.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram illustrating the ITPG.

GA 202218834K

TI Preparing bio-spherical nano silicon dioxide useful in electronic packaging material, comprises e.g. adding carbonized rice hull into three-mouth flask reactor with controlled temperature, adding mixed solution of sodium hydroxide and sodium carbonate, mixing, heating, washing, and roasting.
AU TANG B
   ZHANG L
AE UNIV CHANGCHUN TECHNOLOGY (UYGL-C)
GA 2021E63800
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing bio-spherical nano silicon dioxide comprises (a) controlling the temperature, (b) adding 100 meshes carbonized rice hulls into the 500 ml three-mouth flask reactor, (c) adding certain amount of mixed solution of sodium hydroxide and sodium carbonate, (d) mixing uniformly, and heating the system to 80-110degrees Celsius, (e) stirring and reacting for 2-3 hours, (f) filtering and separating the product to obtain filter residue and white precipitate, (g) activating the filter residue to prepare activated carbon, (h) adding switch surfactant to the obtained filtrate, (i) treating the filtrated product with 4-28 wt.% sodium bicarbonate solution to convert the sodium bicarbonate into sodium carbonates, and dissolving the silicon dioxide, and (j) washing the obtained white precipitation with distilled water for 3-5 times, and roasting at 400-600degrees Celsius to obtain bio-spherical nano silicon dioxide.
   USE - The bio-based spherical nano silicon dioxide is useful in high-end application field e.g. electronic packaging material.
   ADVANTAGE - The bio-based spherical nano silicon dioxide has high quality utilization of carbonized rice hull. The method uses industrial waste gas containing carbon dioxide as raw material, and is green and environmentally friendly.

AU CHEN F
   ZHANG Y
   LIU Y

AE FUJIAN YONGFU ELECTRIC POWER DESIGN CO (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 2021D0153X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves generating high-pressure wet steam by a steam generator. The high pressure wet steam generated by the steam generator is divided into three paths. TI Regeneration of acid gas absorbent involves absorbing acid gas into amine-based acid gas absorbent to obtain absorbent, regenerating absorbent by adding modified zeolite catalyst, reusing regenerated acid gas absorbent, and recovering modified zeolite catalyst.
AU BAEK I H
   NAMSUNGCHAN
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 2021C0693R
AB    NOVELTY - Regeneration of an acid gas absorbent using a modified zeolite catalyst involves (i) absorbing an acid gas into an amine-based acid gas absorbent to obtain an absorbent absorbing the acid gas, (ii) regenerating the absorbent by adding 0.1-20 pts. wt. modified zeolite catalyst to the absorbent that has absorbed the acid gas and removing the acid gas at 40-82 degrees C, (iii) reusing the regenerated acid gas absorbent, and (iv) recovering the modified zeolite catalyst and reusing to regenerate the absorbent. The modified zeolite catalyst is desilicified in presence of HZSM-5 zeolite cetyltrimethylammonium bromide (CTAB) and sodium hydroxide to increase the mesopores. The Bronsted acid point is increased by ion exchange in the presence of ammonium nitrate. The acid gas is carbon dioxide. The sodium hydroxide is treated at a concentration of 0.5-0.7 M (HZSM-5 zeolite 20 mL/g).
   USE - Regeneration of acid gas absorbent using modified zeolite catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses the modified zeolite which promotes decomposition of carbamate bond to acid gas from the absorbent absorbing acid gas, efficiently removes acid gas from the absorbent, regenerates the absorbent with regenerated lower heat, and economically regenerates the absorbent with high efficiency, and recovers acid gas.

TI Flame-retardant resin composition useful in electric wire or cable, comprises resin component, metal hydrate comprising metal hydrate treated with fatty acid and metal hydrate treated with phosphoric acid ester, base resin chosen from ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer, and compatibilizer.
AU YAMAZAKI H
AE NIPPON POLYETHYLENE KK (NPPP-C)
GA 2021B1346U
AB    NOVELTY - Flame-retardant resin composition comprises a resin component, a metal hydrate comprising a metal hydrate treated with a fatty acid and a metal hydrate treated with a phosphoric acid ester, 90 %mass at least one base resin chosen from an ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer and an ethylene-(meth)acrylic acid ester copolymer and a compatibilizer containing a polyolefin resin modified with a polar group-containing compound and a polar group-containing terpolymer, where the content of metal hydrate per 100 parts mass of the total of the base resin and the compatibilizer is 120-250 parts mass.
   USE - The resin composition is useful in an electric wire or cable (claimed), such as power cables and optical fiber cables, and an external metal shielding layer of copper, aluminum and lead, or a water shielding layer formed by winding an aluminum tape, and as a coating layer of an internal semiconductor layer or an external semiconductor layer.
   ADVANTAGE - The resin composition has excellent carbon dioxide gas whitening resistance, molding processability, mechanical properties, mechanical characteristics, tensile strength, flame-retardance, durability, abrasion resistance, compatibility, fluidity, flexibility and heat resistance and is economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The resin composition further comprises 0.001-5 parts mass fatty acid amphoteric metal salt (preferred) with respect to a total of 100 parts mass of base resin and compatibilizer, 10-60 %mass polar group-containing ternary copolymer based on 100 %mass of the total amount of the compatibilizer, 40-90 %mass phosphate ester-treated metal hydrate in 100 %mass of the total of the fatty acid-treated metal hydrate and the phosphate ester-treated metal hydrate. The polyolefin resin modified with polar group-containing compound comprises a linear low-density polyethylene modified with maleic anhydride. The polar group-containing terpolymer comprises an ethylene-acrylic acid ester-maleic anhydride terpolymer. The metal hydrate comprises magnesium hydroxide treated with a fatty acid and magnesium hydroxide treated with a phosphoric acid ester. The fatty acid amphoteric metal salt comprises at least one member chosen from aluminum stearate and zinc stearate. Preferred Composition: The amount of fatty acid treated in the fatty acid-treated metal hydrate is 0.2-1.5 %mass with respect to 100 %mass of the metal hydrate before fatty acid treatment.
   EXAMPLE - Examples 1-9, comparative examples 1-8 and table 1-2 describes about production of a flame retardant resin composition.

PN CN113324251-A
TI Collaborative treatment method for hazardous waste kilns, involves producing exhaust gas by incinerator, cooling exhaust gas to for first time and recycling waste heat, discharging gas from quenching tower for disposal.
AU DONG C
   DING W
   WANG G
AE HEYUAN JINJIE ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (HEYU-Non-standard)
GA 2021A4065H
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves connecting a multi-valve grab bucket with a hydraulic rotary cutting type crusher that is connected with a horizontal hydraulic mixer. A hydraulic high density single piston solid pump unit is connected with a hydraulic shaftless spiral conveyor. A forced feeder is connected with a spray gun that is connected with an incinerator. A waste heat boiler is connected with a quench tower. The hydraulic rotary cutting type crusher is connected with horizontal hydraulic mixer that is connected with the hydraulic high density single piston solid pump unit. The exhaust gas produced by the incinerator in conjunction with incineration of hazardous waste enters the waste heat boiler through the exhaust gas discharge channel. The exhaust gas is cooled to 800 degrees C for the first time, and the waste heat is recycled. The exhaust gas enters the quenching tower, and the exhaust gas drops to below 200 degrees C within 1 second, and the gas is discharged from the quenching tower for disposal.
   USE - Collaborative treatment method for hazardous waste kilns.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has no bad influence to the safety of the surrounding natural ecological environment, replacing a portion of natural fossil, fuel, coal, and reducing the carbon dioxide emission.

PN CN113239621-A; CN113239621-B
TI Method for measuring PVT based on elastic network            regression algorithm, involves inputting obtained            result to elastic network regression model, and            inputting measured component parameter to model to            obtain crude oil PVT characteristic.
AU LIANG H
   DU L
   YANG H
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 202194760S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves placing (101) an                underground infrared spectrometer. The component                and relative content of crude oil is obtained (102)                in real time. The obtained result is input (103) to                the elastic network regression model. The measured                component parameter is input (104) to the model to                obtain the crude oil PVT characteristic. The alkane                specific content of each component, alkane with                carbon atom and specific content of carbon dioxide                are represented by linear relation in bubble point                pressure model.
   USE - Method for measuring PVT based on elastic                network regression algorithm.
   ADVANTAGE - The process of cross validation and                hyper-parameter adjusting in three models is used,                so as to ensure the accuracy and stability of the                model. The prediction of the PVT of crude oil                through input component data is realized.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the flow diagram                illustrating the process for measuring a PVT based                on an elastic network regression algorithm.                (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Step for placing an underground infrared                spectrometer (101)
   Step for obtaining component and relative                content of crude oil in real time (102)
   Step for inputting the obtained result to the                elastic network regression model (103)
   Step for inputting measured component                parameter to the model to obtain the crude oil PVT                characteristic (104)

PN JP2021103008-A
TI Air-factor estimation system for estimating air factor in combustion facility, has computer that calculates carbon monoxide radical accumulation brightness obtained by extracting brightness in visible area of carbon-oxygen radical radiation.
AU AKASAKA C
   KOMORI K
AE CHUBU DENRYOKU KK (CHUB-C)
GA 2021798314
AB    NOVELTY - The estimation system (1) has a camera (4) which images the flame (L) multiple times and acquires flame images (29) received by a computer (2). The computer calculates the ratio of the carbon monoxide (CO-O) radical accumulation luminance obtained by each extracting the luminance in the visible region of carbon dioxide radical emission of the flame, and the methylidyne (CH) radical accumulation brightness obtained by extracting luminance of CH radical emission. The air factor corresponding to the CH/CO-O is extracted based on the accumulation luminance. The camera and the computer are exhibiting portability. The combustion facility has an observation port (O) which visually recognizes the flame.
   USE - Estimation system for estimating the air factor in the combustion chamber of a gas turbine combustor. Used in an air factor control system and in unburned or misfire detection system (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The air-factor estimation system has a simple structure, and is more easily low-cost and provides a more highly accurate air factor estimation system that is not easily influenced by noise.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of the combustion furnace with which the air-factor estimation system is applied. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Estimation system (1)
   Computer (2)
   Camera (4)
   Flame images (29)
   Flame (L)
   Observation port (O)

TI Preparing beryllium copper master alloy by using            vacuum consumable electric arc smelting comprises e.g.            uniformly mixing copper powder, beryllium oxide powder            and graphite powder, adding paraffin powder, and            loading cylindrical green body.

   GUO Q
   ZENG Z
   PENG S
   XIE Y
   LIU R
   HUANG Y
   HUANG J
AE MINMETALS BERYLLIUM CO LTD (MINM-Non-standard)

GA 2021825956
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing beryllium copper master alloy by                using vacuum consumable electric arc smelting                comprises (1) taking copper powder, beryllium oxide                powder and graphite powder, where the weighing                amount of graphite powder is 1.005-1.015 times of                carbon equivalent needed by beryllium and carbon                dioxide by reducing beryllium oxide powder by                carbon thermal reduction method, (2) uniformly                mixing beryllium oxide powder, copper powder and                graphite powder, where coating process refers to                applying polyvinyl alcohol to surface of beryllium                oxide powder, (3) pressing mixed powder to obtain                plate blank, (4) rolling plate blank, and crushing                to obtain blank powder after obtaining fragments,                (5) annealing blank powder, adding paraffin powder,                and pressing to shape to obtain columnar green                body, (6) loading cylindrical green body in                graphite mold, and demolding to obtain working                electrode, and (7) placing working electrode into                vacuum consumable electric arc furnace.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing beryllium                copper master alloy by using vacuum consumable                electric arc smelting.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is easy to realize the preparation                of beryllium copper master alloy with high                beryllium content, high preparation success rate;                does not need crucible smelting, and special                pressurizing sintering furnace; has low production                cost; and is easy for industrial production. The                alloy has high beryllium content.

PN IN202141025567-A
TI Internet of things (IoT) based network system used for sustainable environment and physiological monitoring in industrial workplace, has safe node which is powered by rechargeable battery and consisted of four environmental sensor.
AU SHIVAPRASAD S
   KARTHIGADEVI K
   VENKATA P M
   NAVEEN K V
   LAKSHMI K C
   MANIKANDAN K
   MATHAN K
   SNEHA P
   DEEPTHI T
   JANARDHAN B V
   VIJAYA K V N S
AE SHIVAPRASAD S (SHIV-Individual)
   KARTHIGADEVI K (KART-Individual)
   VENKATA P M (VENK-Individual)
   NAVEEN K V (NAVE-Individual)
   LAKSHMI K C (LAKS-Individual)
   MANIKANDAN K (MANI-Individual)
   MATHAN K (MATH-Individual)
   SNEHA P (SNEH-Individual)
   DEEPTHI T (DEEP-Individual)
   JANARDHAN B V (JANA-Individual)
   VIJAYA K V N S (VIJA-Individual)
GA 202177185Q
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a safe node that comprises a power management unit, one MCU, a long range (LoRa) module, and four environmental sensors. The safe node is powered by a rechargeable battery, so as to regulate the battery voltage to a constant voltage. The MCU is Simblee with built-in. The health node is comprised of a power management unit, an MCU with BLE, and two physiological sensors. A low-power buck-boost converter is adopted to regulate the battery voltage at 3.3 V for the rest of the circuit. The long range (LoRa) module is RFM95 from HoperRF electronics. The LoRa is configured for transmission of long range data from the BAN to the remote gateway. Four environmental sensors are BME680 temperature sensor, SI1145 relative humidity sensor, SI1145 UV sensor and carbon dioxide sensor. The sensors are selected due to the high performance, high accuracy, and low power consumption.
   USE - Internet of things (IoT) based network system used for sustainable environment and physiological monitoring in industrial workplace.
   ADVANTAGE - The safe node comprises a power management unit, one MCU, a LoRa module, and four environmental sensors, so as to ensure the safety in industrial workplace.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the safe node.

TI Method of determination method based on water            vapor quality based on infrared spectroscopy, involves            providing water sample, and determining quality of            water vapor quality in fire power generator according            to comparison result.
AU WEI X
   SUN Y
   WANG Y
   XU H
   WANG H
   XIONG Y
   DU Y
   NIU B
AE CHINA DATANG CORP SCI & TECHNOLOGY (CDAT-C)
   DATANG SANMENXIA POWER CO LTD (DATA-Non-standard)
GA 2021762511
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves detecting the actual                hydrogen conductivity, formate concentration,                acetate concentration, and carbonate concentration                in the thermal power system of the firepower                generator set of the power plant. The water                samples, acetate, and carbonate theoretical                concentrations of each water sample is obtained                according to standard curves. The actual detection                concentration and theoretical concentration are                obtained. The absolute errors and relative errors                are calculated. The water sample is provided with                the condensed water, water supply, superheated                steam, reheat steam. The quality of water vapor                quality in the fire power generator is determined,                according to the comparison result.
   USE - Method of determination method based on water                vapor quality based on infrared spectroscopy.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is directly and accurately analyzed                and monitored the harmful ions such as carbon                dioxide and organic matter in the water vapor                quality during the operation process of the machine                set. The method is accurately judged the abnormal                reason of the water vapor quality, and a new way                for detecting the water vapor quality abnormity is                provided.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a water                vehicle quality decision monitoring equipment based                on infrared spectroscopy.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                method of determination method. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)

   5Check valve

TI Powder/particles of lithium ion conductive            material useful in lithium ion conductor for separator,            anode, cathode, battery, rechargeable battery and            ceramic solid ion conductor, lithium-air or            lithium-sulfur- and lithium polymer batteries.
AU SCHUMACHER J
   TREIS P
   DREWKE J
   SCHMITT H
   SAMSINGER R
   ROTERS A
   SCHNEIDER M
   SCHUHMACHER J
   JAMZENGGEROLF
AE SCHOTT AG (ZEIS-C)
   SCHOTT AG (ZEIS-C)
   SCHOTT AG (ZEIS-C)
GA 202169623V
AB    NOVELTY - Powder, particles of lithium ion conductive                material having conductivity of greater than or                equal to 10-5 S/cm, is claimed. The powder has                inorganic carbon content (total inorganic carbon                content (TIC)) of less than or equal to 0.4 wt.%                and/or organic carbon content (total organic carbon                content (TOC)) of less than or equal to 0.1 wt.%,                d50 particle size of 0.05-10 mu m, and particle                size distribution (d90/d10) of less than or equal                to 4.
   USE - The powder of lithium ion conductive material                is useful in lithium ion conductor for separator,                anode, cathode, battery and/or rechargeable battery                (all claimed), preferably ceramic solid ion                conductor, all-solid-state batteries, lithium-air                or lithium-sulfur batteries and lithium polymer                batteries.
   ADVANTAGE - The powder of lithium ion conductive material                has high conductivity, and maintains small contact                resistance to the electrode materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for
   (1) lithium ion conductor comprising the                powder of lithium ion conductive material;                and
   (2) producing powder, comprising (a) providing                a raw product using a hot process which maintains                temperatures at greater than or equal to 900                degrees C, and (b) communicating the raw product                with the exclusion of carbon dioxide sources and/or                with exclusion of organic carbon sources.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The powder comprises                  lithium oxide, where the quotient of the                  inorganic carbon content (wt.%) and the lithium                  oxide content (mol.%) is less than or equal to 80                  ppm mol.%, and/or the quotient of the organic                  carbon content (wt.%) and the lithium oxide                  content (mol.%) is less than or equal to 20 ppm                  mol.%. The powder has specific surface area of                  greater than or equal to 0.05 m2/g, moisture                  content of greater than or equal to 5 wt.%. The                  lithium ion conductive material comprises lithium                  lanthanum zirconate and/or lithium aluminum                  titanium phosphate. Preferred Method: The hot                  process is carried out by (i) melt, (ii) reactive                  sintering, (iii) calcination of sol-gel                  precursors and (iv) bottom-up synthesis in the                  pulsation reactor. The step (b) comprises (b1)                  crushing the obtained product using a hammer and                  chisel, (b2) communicating the obtained product                  with jaw crushers, ball and/or hammer mills, (b3)                  performing size reduction using ball, impact                  and/or planetary mills, and (b4) communicating                  the obtained product with the aid of opposed jet                  mills operated with process gases or steam, dry                  and/or wet ball mills, dry and/or wet agitator                  ball mills and/or by high-energy grinding in                  high-kinetic rotor ball mills.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI UV photolysis waste gas processing device, has spraying pipe net whose lower end is provided with KL catalytic area, where lower end of KL catalytic area is provided with circulating water tank and side of circulating water tank is provided with filter screen.
AU MO X
   HUO X
   ZHAO H
   WANG H
   CHEN Y

AE ZHUHAI HENGCHUANG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTI (ZHUH-Non-standard)
GA 202176632B
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a UV photolysis waste gas processing device, comprising an ozone destructor and an ISO oxidant adding device, the ozone destructor is connected with the ISO oxidant adding device through a conveying pipe, the upper end of the ozone destructor is provided with a demisting area; the lower end of the demisting area is movably connected with a spraying pipe net; the lower end of the spraying pipe net is provided with a KL catalytic area. The utility model claims an auxiliary device for common surgery, provided with a KL catalytic region, ISO oxidant adding device; spraying pipe network and so on; when using, the catalyst in the KL catalytic area and the oxidizer added in the ISO oxidant adding device are combined, so that the ozone is converted into carbon dioxide and water vapour; the ozone content in the exhaust emission is reduced; at the same time, it will not generate any harmful side product; then adding high precision nozzle used by the spraying pipe network, which can make the actual spraying more uniform, finally improving the processing reaction effect of the whole, bringing better use prospect.
